# Thread: Still Suffering from Headaches Daily - What Next?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mafixit — 2013-04-25T23:14:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/post-84817>

Been on BHRT with an anti-aging Doc who firmly believes in Dr. Dzugan's theories. I am on Day 131 of her protocol. Went to see her to try and get some relief from these chronic daily headaches, which everyone assumed was menopause/hormone related. Been through all the MRI's, CT Scans, MRA's MRV's, Vestibular Testing, etc. Everything is "Normal". Was tested for Epstein Barr, Lyme Disease, Valley Fever, etc. Had the Adrenal Stress test done. Cortisol was high, Melatonin was low, but nothing dramatic.

90 days of Migra-Eeze (prescribed by Mayo), 131 Days of BHRT and now 60 days of Cymbalta (nothing was working and I needed some pain relief, so I gave in to my PCP and started the Cymbalta). I still have chronic daily headaches. Last labs 2 weeks ago show hormones in balance, magnesium and zinc levels good, thyroid good. Anti-Aging doc is good at ordering labs and keeping track, and my insurance coverage is good. We are all stumped. Started to feel some relief on Week 4 of the Cymbalta, then this week (Week 6) the plugged was pulled and I am in Day 5 of my most recent headache / neck ache / back ache fiasco. It all hurts.

Diet is gluten-free, I do not eat food I was discovered to be allergic or intolerant to. Higher protein, low carbs. I take the recommended supplements/hormones given to me by my anti-aging doc. I do Yoga for my upper back, neck and shoulders most every day, do the exercises prescribed by my PT, have acupuncture once a week, etc. No relief. We are missing something, but what?

Any thoughts on where to go next? I am going on two years of this daily hell and just want to feel 50% better, at the very least. They (Mayo Clinic Headache Neurologist) diagnosed me with "chronic migraine", but none of the treatments they prescribed work, from the 3 days of outpatient IV infusion to the Arthrotec pain reliever to shots of Toridal to the daily dose of Cymbalta. there's is no point in any OTC pain relievers. Tic Tacs would help more.

I have an appt with another neurologist early next month for another opinion.

Karen from Arizona

### Shijin13 — 2013-04-25T23:26:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/post-84818>

Karen

I'm re-reading Dr Uzzi's Hormone book [https://www.uzzireissmd.com/book\_naturalhormone.html](https://www.uzzireissmd.com/book_naturalhormone.html)

one thing that caught my eye this time around - is that just b/c you're in "range" of the lab ranges, doesn't mean your in the range for you. I'd look back at your labs, and how you feel, talk w/your doc about increasing the dosing of your hormones.

since I don't know what your labs look like and I don't know your hormone doeses - I can't say what I'd look to adjust.

I know for me - I don't do well cycling my Pg - so I don't even though Dzugan want's me too. I'm also on a super HIGH DOSE of Pg for a woman who isn't menopausal. I'm taking 20% Pg gel 1ml in the am and 1 ml in the pm + 5% sublinigual. (though I think I'm headed there fast given how my cycle is behaving!)
my normal isn't like anyone elses, and neither is yours.

detailed tracking is key IMO, especially when considering youre trying to get rid of migraines.... you may be one of the few women who can't cycle Pg - otherwise you get migraines.

I know you've got a constant migraine - is there a point in your cycle where the migraine is less painful than other times? if so what does your dosing look like for your Hormones?

how's your Thyroid? etc???

I think its why I feel so crappy: tired, fat, swollen boobs...more so than I did yesterday.

I stopped maca this week but I'm sure that didnt help me

### mafixit — 2013-04-25T23:49:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/post-84822>

Thanks for the EMF tip - I found that the plug to my alarm clock under the head of my bed was emitting in the range of 60, so I no longer have a clock in my room. And actually, my smartmeter was installed around the time this all started to happen to me. The meter, however is on the other side of the house on the outside of a garage wall. It's the furthest away from the living space it could be. Now, my neighbor's meter is closer to me than my own.

As far as cycles, I really don't have one. I am peri or menopausal depending on who you talk to. My last period was 45 days ago and the one before that was probably 4 to 5 months before the last one. Thyroid labs are good. Anti-aging doc checks everything she can think of.

Hence my issue. I can't identify triggers or cycles. I keep a daily log of how I feel, but none of the puzzle pieces match up.

At this point I am on progesterone capsules, 100 mg at night, E1/E2 Compound cream 1 click in morning, 2 clicks at night, Testosterone cream, 3 clicks in AM, and 1/2 of a 25mg sublingual DHEA tablet. Doc tried 150mg time release Progesterone and it literally knocked me out the next day. Can't take those because they are like sleeping pills for me. I thought maybe the PG was causing the headache, but stopping it did not make any difference.

Karen from Arizona

Karen from Arizona

>
> Physicians are very expensive considering how much they do not know. Do the simple things first. I think you will be pleasantly happy.
>
> Also it you have wifi in the house, consider hardwiring to eliminate emf wifi polution.

Went to see her to try and get some relief from these chronic daily headaches, which everyone assumed was menopause/hormone related. >

They (Mayo Clinic Headache Neurologist) diagnosed me with "chronic migraine", but none of the treatments they prescribed work, from the 3 days of outpatient IV infusion to the Arthrotec pain reliever to shots of Toridal to the daily dose of Cymbalta. there's is no point in any OTC pain relievers. Karen,
Just because your doctor checked your thyroid does not mean it is OK. They typically use TSH as a determining test, and many experts agree that TSH is worse than useless. Far better to measure your body temp and chart it every day. You should be near 98.6 and you should NOT suffer from old hands or cold feet. If you have cold hands or cold feet, then you are hypothyroid. Prove this by making a [chart of your body temp](http://www.drrind.com/therapies/metabolic-temperature-graph). If you have symptoms, refer to this [book on the thyroid](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/)
Headaches and Migraines are a symptom of hypothyroid. [http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/long-and-pathetic/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/long-and-pathetic/)

Doctors typically overlook the obvious, and one obvious symptom of iodine deficiency is severe headaches. I suggest you get an iodine deficiency test here, and also get the bromide & fluoride testing:
[http://www.hakalalabs.com/testing.html](http://www.hakalalabs.com/testing.html)

Headaches are typical of iodine deficiency for many:
[Iodine Deficiency Leads to Headaches and Fatigue](http://cantstayoutofthekitchen.com/2012/08/31/iodine-deficiency-leads-to-headaches-and-fatigue/)

Dr. Hownenstein says, "Other Health Problems Iodine has proven value in treating headaches, keloids, and parotid duct stones."
[http://www.newswithviews.com/Howenstine/james37.htm](http://www.newswithviews.com/Howenstine/james37.htm)

> and patients with migraine headaches stop having them. Even better results can occur when iodine is combined with magnesium and selenium. Symptoms of iodine deficiency include muscle cramps, lymph gland congestion, proneness to weight gain, underweight, low blood pressure, cardiac arrhythmia, slow reflexes, **poor memory, fuzzy thinking,** fluid retention, **migraines,** constipation, depression, headaches, edema, myalgia and weakness; cold hands and feet, dry skin, and brittle nails; hair loss across body, head and eyebrows; breast, ovarian, thyroid, and skin cysts;
> [http://joannebrophy.com/programs/iodinetherapy.htm](http://joannebrophy.com/programs/iodinetherapy.htm)

There are millions of hits in Google for **IODINE HEADACHE**
Beware that when first starting on iodine supplements, most people have detox symptoms that typically pass away. One of these detox symptoms can be headaches. \`
The Dr. Brownstein Iodine Protocol has proven effective for iodine deficiency symptoms:
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout)

So bottom line: I think it is extremely worthwhile to test your iodine levels per the above link. NOT A BLOOD TEST !

Grizz

### Grizz — 2013-04-29T17:53:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/post-85138>

> [mafixit said:](/goto/post?id=85129)
>
> Yes, thank you so much for the tips. I will discuss with my hormone doc. I am sure she will agree to order the tests so they are covered under my insurance. I will update when I have the results. Also beginning the temp testing today.
>
> I cannot stand being on this Cymbalta. The side effects are uncomfortable (dizziness, increased anxiety, hot flashes, fatigue and vivid dreams).
>
> Karen from Arizona

Karen,

OMG, you are on Cymbalta? Before you attempt to quit Cymbalta, I suggest you Google "Cymbalta Withdrawal" to get some tips on how to do this. Cymbalta withdrawal symptoms are a "Living Hell" for many, far exceeding their worst pain or depression. Suicide is frequently said to be a symptom. I have a good friend suffering TERRIBLY from Cymbalta withdrawal.

IMO, this drug should be outlawed. Here are some things I've found:

> A neighbor called me for help with her anti-depressant/pain killing drug, Cymbalta. She said it was impossible to withdraw from it, and her insurance company won't pay for it anymore because of Obamacare. It costs $160 a month or more and she can't afford it.
>
> I have extensive medical information in my computer, and could not find anything for weaning from SSRI Drugs. So I made a huge effort to help her find an answer. What I found out was one HORROR STORY after another.
>
> Cymbalta withdrawall. This seems to be a drug from hell.... NASTY.
> People are reporting withdrawal symptoms of:
> "Debilitating" brain zaps,
> severe headaches,
> hot & cold sensation,
> brain confusion,
> vivid nightmares,
> intestinal twisting,
> suicidal thoughts
> chest pain,
> tingling,
> moodiness,
> hot flashes,
> dizziness,
> wild uncontrollable rages,
> "people said they have these brain zaps anywhere from a few weeks to a year."
> "Doctors won't help with withdrawal"
> Vertigo, and...
> I stopped looking into this nightmare, as there seemed to be no solution to be found except suffering for up to a year while slowly scaling back on dosage.

> Msg to my friend,
> ...UPDATE: Several months later...
> It's been a while and I've decided to update everyone on my situation.
> In January of 2009, I was finally down to 20 mg of Cymbalta daily (I started from 60mg and tapered down 10 mg every two weeks). Since there is no lower dose than 20mg, I asked my doctor for 10mg of Prozac. I took Prozac daily for about a month and did not experience any withdrawal symptoms at all! Finally I stopped taking Prozac completely, and am now living a happy, normal life.
> There is hope, people! Good luck to everyone who is still going through this horrible experience. [http://byee.hubpages.com/hub/Weaning-Off-Cymbalta-What-The-Doctors-Dont-Tell-You](http://byee.hubpages.com/hub/Weaning-Off-Cymbalta-What-The-Doctors-Dont-Tell-You)
> Save the above link, it has valuable references to other web sites.
>
> Also E-How.com has what I think is the best 3 step method here, by combining Prozac & Omega 3 Fish oil:
> [http://www.ehow.com/how\_5531409\_quit-cymbalta.html](http://www.ehow.com/how_5531409_quit-cymbalta.html)
> I think I'm done sending you tips. I'm praying for you to beat this, you can do this!

Grizz

### nunki — 2013-04-30T02:57:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/post-85170>

> [Joann said:](/goto/post?id=84825)
>
> Wow. seems you have looked into every avenue. I was going to ask if you have ever been in an accident, but then I see you have had mri's for the spine and neck? Did you have the headaches before meno? I would think back to when they started to see if anything changed before headaches started.
> I know this may sound to simple...one time I bought a new bra ...halter bra...which I later found caused me and my dd to have headaches. where the strap landed on my back caused awful headache.
> hope you find relief soon

I didnt see where you saw Joann's question. Accidents involving the neck and shoulders can cause headaches. Just having all of those muscles tighten up can trigger one for me. If the hormones, and fixing the EMF doesnt help, I would look for a good OA chiropractor. Nobody was able to fix my terrible headaches and pressure feeling (like someone was trying to pry my head off of my neck with a pry bar, lol). A couple of visits to him- getting OA adjusted and active release techniques to loosen up my tight neck muscles reduced my headaches dramatically. Good luck! So frustrating when no one can fix what the problem is!

### mafixit — 2013-04-30T16:38:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/post-85191>

> [nunki said:](/goto/post?id=85170)
>
> I didnt see where you saw Joann's question. Accidents involving the neck and shoulders can cause headaches. Just having all of those muscles tighten up can trigger one for me. If the hormones, and fixing the EMF doesnt help, I would look for a good OA chiropractor. Nobody was able to fix my terrible headaches and pressure feeling (like someone was trying to pry my head off of my neck with a pry bar, lol). A couple of visits to him- getting OA adjusted and active release techniques to loosen up my tight neck muscles reduced my headaches dramatically. Good luck! So frustrating when no one can fix what the problem is!

I have not had any accidents. This all started in March 2011 and has progressed over the last two years. It was intermittent and then became a daily issue. I tried chiro twice and it made things worse.

I do have a dehiscence of the superior semi-circular canal on the left side and most likely on the right side, discovered on a CT scan last December at the ER while I was being treated for a monster headache. This can cause some of my dizziness. I don't have any of the horrible symptoms of this at this time. Just some disequilibrium at times.

I am doing some exercises the physical therapist gave me to strengthen neck and back muscles and overall core strength. I have also taken up a great Yoga session from a website for neck and shoulders. When I am done with that session I feel so "stretched out' in a good way, but still the headaches persist. I feel it in my left shoulder blade, shoulder muscles and neck from the base of my neck to my shoulders. Then it creeps up the left side of my head behind my ear, into my temple and then finally across my forehead. Shoulders hurt to the touch. Trigger points at the base of my head hurt to the touch, especially left side. The left side of my face sometimes feels slightly numb from the jaw bone to the cheek - Like I am on the tail end of novocaine.

The more I do the recommended exercises, the more my left shoulder blade hurts. I have been chalking it up to weak muscles.

I am a mystery.

Karen from Arizona

### nunki — 2013-05-01T03:33:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/post-85229>

You have three muscles that come up and attach at the base of your skull. I tore the attachment for mine in a car accident. Your symptoms sound pretty much like mine. I had steroid shots in mine- gave me terrible vertigo. When it is flared up my face will go numb- really weird feeling, lol. Two and a half years later, I cannot lift weight above my head, hold my arm up above my head for any length of time, cannot stand weight on my head- like a towel turban etc. I have a knot that comes and goes right in that insertion point. It really sounds like you have in some way damaged those muscles. It could have been anything, even turning your head too quickly. Plain old, crack the neck chiro's will not help it. Look for one that specializes in an Atlas Orthogonal adjustment- it is not a "crack" the neck adjustment.

Dont give up, you have to keep trying things until you find what works. I also use an e-stim machine on mine, makes those muscles completely relax temporarily.

### Joann — 2013-05-01T09:28:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/post-85233>

Sounds so familiar to me its scary. I had an "adventure" in the 90's was looking up at my computer which the monitor was on top of way too high. suddenly, the back of my head and scalp felt tense like I had a hat on, then my eyelid started twitching. Woke up the next day with swelling on side of face on cheek near nose. I thought it was a tooth problem went to dentist, he didnt find anything but gave me anti's anyway. Then my collarbone felt like electric shock when touched. Went to docs, looked at me like I was nuts! finally had an mri and x rays of head which was negative. went to chiro worse thing! she cracked my neck .....My ears Hummed like a siren. went to the library brought home a bunch of books on diff therapies...shiatso etc. Tried to fix myself. Few years passed, oh I slept on a sofa with pillows propped since I couldnt sleep with my head and shoulders flat on a bed. Finally I found a chiro that did different techniques. After about a year I let her do something to my head/neck
Lying on my stomach, she took my head from behind and moved it in such a way that my collarbone didn't feel like the funny bone of an elbow. Before finding her I had other chiros that "adjusted" me which made my symptoms worse and even gave me new symptoms.....my legs trembling, dizzy, facial numbness.
Its many years later, I cannot look up at my tall son....my ears will hum, going to the dentist ....feel beat up, shoulder and neck acts up. I run to the freezer for ICE It helps a lot.
I also have the knot at the back of my head that comes and goes (recent) and problems with raising my arms over head or wearing a hat. If the therapy makes me feel worse then its the wrong therapy for me.

I did the specialist chiropractor thing - neuro-cranial therapy. Spent a bit of money on that last year- didn't help. I know what you mean about having chiro therapy and then feeling worse. My last trip to the crack back guy caused issues the next day - clogged ears, more headache, neck ache, etc.

I am in acupuncture therapy for my trigger points now, which are all bothering me, but especially in my neck and shoulders. It doesn't seem to help a whole bunch, but it's cheap because my insurance covers it, so I go once a week.

I called the Mayo Pain Clinic for treatment - they are scheduling trigger point injections and occipital nerve blocks, but I can't get in until June 25th.

Mostly now this feels like tension headaches and I get visual issues - like my eyes won't focus together or I have a different prescription in each eye. My shoulder joints are stiffening up and my shoulder muscles from neck to shoulder hurt to the touch. Have issues in my left shoulder blade too. This has all been attributed to migraine and anxiety. I don't believe it.

I get into some of these yoga positions and my right shoulder joint locks up and I have to get out slow

Still no real help from the Cymbalta.

Karen from Arizona

### mafixit — 2014-02-25T21:52:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-suffering-from-headaches-daily-what-next.6874/post-112152>

Update on me -

Still suffering. Tried more trigger point injections (no help), cervical epidural (never again - the steroid side affects were AWFUL), tried Indocin, more acupuncture, now am in to see a chiro specialist for Atlas adjustment - Atlas Orthogonal. 3 treatments in and no help.

Right now I am on nothing - no supplements, no BHRT, no drugs, nothing. I feel just a shitty on or off any meds, so I am off for now.

Gained a ton of weight back - lost 35 lbs before this all starting in 2011. Gained it all back over the past year.

I am seriously considering going a round with Dzugan. It's expensive, but I have spent so much money with the Mayo Clinic and other docs. I also switched to the University of Arizona Integrative Health Center for my primary care doc. They will look at the whole me, not just give me an anti-depressant and send me on my way. Within this practice I also get to meet with a nutritionist, a heath coach, a mind-body practioner and take yoga, tai chi, nutrition and other classes. The center was started by Dr. Andrew Weil. They just opened here in Phoenix last year.

I also saw a new neurologist outside of Mayo (he was the one who put me on the Indocin). The Indocin gave me worse headaches, so we pulled that and he wants me on Inderol. Last week when I saw him he made me feel like we were out of options, with the exception of occipital nerve blocks, which my insurance does not cover.

I know my body has changed again because my hot flashes are minimal, but my menstrual cycle is a joke. 130 days , 19 days 40 days, 77 days, etc. My breasts are tender and I am sleeping like crap again. My last cycle my ovaries were screaming so bad I was ready to go to the Urgent Care. Even if I do sleep through the night, I never feel rested.

So I have run full circle and am back to the beginning. My husband wants me back and I want to be back. I know some of you are using Dzugan. It is worth the $$$$?

Karen from AZ.

# Thread: Radon, how it becomes dangerous
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radon-how-it-becomes-dangerous.6876/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inger — 2013-04-26T10:56:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radon-how-it-becomes-dangerous.6876/post-84860>

So, I have been concerned about radon for long, as I live in a area where there are huge amounts in the ground at places. So it is common here to test for it if you have a basement.
My parents tested, and it come out there was no issues at all in the air inside the house even if deep basements, also not in the water that comes from own well 30 meter deep.

So I was reading about it more. I accidetially come across some great info reading a book about radiation from the government, I got it from the library. It says, radon has been a issue only in modern times..........
Here it comes why;
It became an issue when people wanted to save energy and have it warm inside They made their houses so airtight, the windows, walls.. everything the radon coming from the basement / earth had no way to escape and collected into the air inside the house. Only then radon became a issue. Even if you have big amounts radon in the ground, and you have a basement, as long as your house is not airtight the radon will escape all the time and it will not accumulate and be an issue for our health. My parents house that my dad built himself has full of "not airtight places" as it is still not finished..lol I also keep the windows open a lot, in the bedroom all night long and also otherwise I keep doors and windows open as much as I can.

I wanted to share this if someone is concerned about radon, do not be! Just air out your house often and there will be zero issues. This made me recognize one more thing we have made dangerous with our aims for comfort in modern times.... that was not dangerous to begin with, if we did not do all those unnatural stuff living in airtight overheated houses etc.

### cantweight — 2013-04-26T14:33:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radon-how-it-becomes-dangerous.6876/post-84869>

That is so good to know! When we moved over the summer they did a radon test on the house and while it was in normal range it seemed at the high end to me. I am now grateful for my crappy old windows that let all kinds of air in!

### Inger — 2013-04-26T14:45:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radon-how-it-becomes-dangerous.6876/post-84870>

Yes cantweight, let the air in!
To have the windows and doors open as much as it gets has lots of benefits.... not only zeroing the radon issue

This house probably have mold contamination too, but airing often helps this issue also! I keep lots of green plants in my rooms too. Very good for cleaning the air for toxins.

### lioness7 — 2013-04-26T17:13:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/radon-how-it-becomes-dangerous.6876/post-84885>

Yes Inger , you are so right in my opinion. We live in an older home that has quite a few "leaks". This never bothered me as I always felt that the slight extra cost of heating was well worth the advantage of having fresh air coming in all the time. Even during our coldest Canadian days, I always slept with the windows open and never worried about drafts. Unfortunately, most of the homes built today in North America are "shrink wrapped", requiring added fresh air heat recovery systems which are very costly. I always tell everyone, shut off your lights and open up your windows/doors instead.

# Thread: Paprika
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paprika.6886/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### GlenPDQ — 2013-04-27T03:40:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paprika.6886/post-84923>

Jack advises certain people use paprika for niacin I think, but it also contains the red-orange carotenoid _**beta cryptoxanthin**_. Studies mention that beta cryptoxanthin inhibits adipogenesis without the damaging side effects of drugs , lowers the risk of polyarthritis by 50%, and can be converted to vitamin A.
Red peppers (paprika), pumpkin, and citrus are good sources of this carotenoid.

Anyone know how much sweet red paprika makes an effective helping to protect against polyarthritis or reduce adipogenesis in humans?

# Thread: Always cloudy here-how to simulate morning sunshine artificially???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/always-cloudy-here-how-to-simulate-morning-sunshine-artificially.6895/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bennyha — 2013-04-27T18:58:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/always-cloudy-here-how-to-simulate-morning-sunshine-artificially.6895/post-84946>

Hello forum. Thanks to all who chimed in on my frequent urination post. I appreciate the info.

My next question is regarding the Adrenal Rx/Leptin Rx. It says the first thing to do is to stare at the sunshine when you get up in the morning. I live in the Chicago area, and it's literally cloudy at least 5 days a week. Plus I have a building in my way that blocks the sun.

My question- is there anyway to simulate that morning sunshine I am missing? Do they make some kind of light box/light bulbs that give off lights that mimic the sunlight? I really appreciate any insight anyone can give me. Thank you so much!

### Inger — 2013-04-27T20:39:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/always-cloudy-here-how-to-simulate-morning-sunshine-artificially.6895/post-84950>

Bennyha,
I stare into the sky one minute be it sun or not, every morning. Today there were lot of clouds and I still could feel how very bright the light was to my eyes.
Jack has said many times, it does not matter if cloudy. So just try to get somewhere where you can see the sky where the sun rises (east). There need to be no glass or glasses or anything between the sky and your eyes, this is important. I sometimes also just look out from my open bedroom window into the sky as it opens to east.
Also never use sunglasses at daytime, ever. This is really bad for our health! Try to get as much daylight in your eyes as possible. I have moved my computer in front of the window, and also a small table in right by the window in my small kitchen, if I need to do any work I do it in daylight now.

There is no way to replace sunlight with artificial light..whatever marketing they use. Natural light wins - always.

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-04-28T15:37:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/always-cloudy-here-how-to-simulate-morning-sunshine-artificially.6895/post-84991>

> [bennyha said:](/goto/post?id=84946)
>
> My question- is there anyway to simulate that morning sunshine I am missing? Do they make some kind of light box/light bulbs that give off lights that mimic the sunlight? I really appreciate any insight anyone can give me. Thank you so much!

Google 'Philips goLITE BLU energy light'

### bionaut — 2013-04-28T15:57:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/always-cloudy-here-how-to-simulate-morning-sunshine-artificially.6895/post-84993>

I own one of these. It outputs 450-470nm light so it will stimulate the SCN. You should bear in mind that even on cloudy days daylight can contain light in the blue spectrum. It works well as a tool to help get on track with the circadian rhythm if I am traveling or have had a crazy week. I just don't have the temperance to use it consistently because I enjoy the daytime much more.

### bennyha — 2013-04-28T16:47:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/always-cloudy-here-how-to-simulate-morning-sunshine-artificially.6895/post-84996>

Awesome guys thank you so much! This is such a great forum where everyone just tried helping one another. I have many more questions on grounding and EMF's as all that stuff is so confusing to me, but I will just address one thing at a time and go from there.

So as long as I go outside and stare toward the east when I wake up, even if blocked by buildings, this will be good enough, correct? And just for a couple minutes?

Thanks again guys I appreciate all the input!

Christi

### Patty Cakes — 2013-04-29T00:05:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-re-30-day-hormone-panel.6903/post-85054>

I used DIM to help get rid of excess estrogen so on that one I would say it would interfere. The only thing I know about maca is that is affects adrenals/cortisol so since it affects that hormone I would imagine it may have an affect on others. If it were me I wouldn't take either one until testing was complete.

### Hope — 2013-04-29T03:40:50+0300

### jenaf — 2013-04-29T05:23:09+0300

in re of the hormone panel, i wouldnt take anything before just to get an accurate baseline

"I want you to know that you're getting better.
I don't care what everyone else has been saying.
You're getting better.
They're the ones who are getting worse.
They don't like what you've been doing, understandably.
They wouldn't be saying what they've been saying, if they didn't want you to stop.
People want you to be the way they are, and to stay that way.

Make it on your own, the way you've been doing."

Thanks for sharing Glen ....... everyone looks at me with shock when I say I eat high fat and medium protein and low carb. They always think I have said it backwards........

### prAna303 — 2013-05-02T11:40:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-high-fat-low-carb-diet-explosion-happening-in-sweden.6935/post-85290>

Yeah! This is the big thing in this country today. Because i live here everybody is talking about diets but have no clue what is happening. They start to eat LCHF and gets almost fanatics, just like aerobics did in the middle of the 80Â´. ItÂ´s quite funny that many say that they have started to eat like me today and when i scratch the surface they still gorge in strange things. Look at many cookbooks about paleo and LCHF they still make "candyfood" with less candystuff, still they need to feed their urge / obsession.

For sure itÂ´s great that many start to demand more natural things but in the big they are lost. Still everyone thinks that itÂ´s all about eating right and train hard because the Swede is known for hard work and results.

Do i need to say that many or almost everyone is in love with their gadgets (iPhone, iPad and iThingeverything) and would break down if they lost their smartphone.

Quote: I donÂ´t know what i would do if i lost it, i mean my whole life is on that thing (heard at cafÃ© last nite).

Most think that i go to extreme with my life but itÂ´s the opposite.

### caroline — 2013-05-02T12:00:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-high-fat-low-carb-diet-explosion-happening-in-sweden.6935/post-85291>

I took the train to Sydney today .... everyone has an ipad or is on their phone ...no exceptions....everyone seems so dead somehow.....

### Inger — 2013-05-02T16:04:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-high-fat-low-carb-diet-explosion-happening-in-sweden.6935/post-85299>

Peter.. I live in Finland and here are also this movement but the media is scaring people of it. There are stories telling how people got sick and how dangerous it is. And I think exactly like you, LCHF is not the only important thing when the choices are crappy.. and no one speaks about seasonality either... or circadian biology... it is all so superficial and I feel sad about it. But ok a small step in the right direction for sure.  But mostly people have no interest to dig deeper.. that is so weird.

### prAna303 — 2013-05-02T20:29:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-high-fat-low-carb-diet-explosion-happening-in-sweden.6935/post-85327>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=85299)
>
> But mostly people have no interest to dig deeper.. that is so weird.

ItÂ´s so simple... Why do some hard thinking by yourself when you can be feed by others. We learn early from school that everything is invented, done and all old theories are laws. You as a person can not question a teacher / book anymore because you donÂ´t have the right education. I meet this attitude everyday. The only thing one can do is to listen deeply to whatÂ´s happening around and within us, brake the rules and become a new Einstein today. Sure, many will not listen BUT WHO CARES?

I think we are going to see more and more of these quickfixes for everyday now. Pure simulations of the thing we call life, desperate ways of getting that fix. Like junkies we crave the source of life, we take drugs to get high, eat to get a kick and train to get something back of what we used to be, like a working body.

And in the big bowl of crazy some will make a living out of it, think lifecoaches and other strange things. This is why so many can be famous because of a way of eating / living.

# Thread: I guess we all have a new disease...Orthorexia!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-we-all-have-a-new-disease-orthorexia.6938/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### RayB — 2013-05-02T16:25:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-we-all-have-a-new-disease-orthorexia.6938/post-85300>

The food police have now stepped in with a new label. Next step....a patent on a mind altering drug.
I am already a HYPERchondriac.

[http://beforeitsnews.com/alternativ...lth-conscious-are-now-extremists-2635136.html](http://beforeitsnews.com/alternative/2013/05/orthorexia-the-health-conscious-are-now-extremists-2635136.html)

### nonchalant — 2013-05-02T16:51:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-we-all-have-a-new-disease-orthorexia.6938/post-85305>

Actually, the guy has a point. "â€œI monitor a lot of my clientsâ€™ weight, and if they are dropping really fast, and it seems they are going to extremes, youâ€™ve got to question them and say, â€˜Letâ€™s make sure you are staying healthy,â€™â€￾ said Volney."

I know of several people who have dug down deep and by sheer awsome willpower, lost quite a bit of weight, using government guidelines. They soon became sick, however, with bone problems, mental problems, heart problems, etc. It's so SAD. I think government guidelines should be labeled "extreme".

### RayB — 2013-05-02T17:02:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-we-all-have-a-new-disease-orthorexia.6938/post-85307>

I am kinda one of those people. Lost a good bit of weight, although not very fast, but using "conventional" guidelines. Now I am trying to undo the damage done.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-05-02T18:37:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-we-all-have-a-new-disease-orthorexia.6938/post-85314>

Its unfortunate that so many individuals got to extremes to lose the weight and neglect eating important fats. I suspect that's why so many people blame loss of carbohydrates for the extreme symptoms when in actuality they screwed themselves on the fat intake along with calorie restriction.

By the way, I noticed that my creaky shoulder joints are lots better now. It took awhile after losing alot of weight for the skeletal system to adapt or remodel or whatever it does.

### nunki — 2013-06-03T15:59:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-we-all-have-a-new-disease-orthorexia.6938/post-87373>

I was all with him until he had to lump people into the group "Mollify Your Conscience by Demonizing the Truth â€“ Ask the Experts â€“ the Griping, Whining, Conniving Zionists." Hmm...Seems a little too bigoted for me. He would have had a valid point if it had been "the Griping, Whining, Conniving junk food peddlers".

Not even sure what this had to do with the so called "eating disorder" part, or if it was the start of another article. But, definitely disturbing.

### Hemming — 2013-06-03T16:28:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-guess-we-all-have-a-new-disease-orthorexia.6938/post-87379>

>
> Its unfortunate that so many individuals got to extremes to lose the weight and neglect eating important fats. I suspect that's why so many people blame loss of carbohydrates for the extreme symptoms when in actuality they screwed themselves on the fat intake along with calorie restriction.
>
> By the way, I noticed that my creaky shoulder joints are lots better now. It took awhile after losing alot of weight for the skeletal system to adapt or remodel or whatever it does.

That's very interesting. I lost way too much weight via calorie restriction despite being slightly underweight to begin with. I'm now at 116lbs, 5'9'' and 26y male. I'm trying to regain weight and get my body back in shape.
Did you also go through a phase where your entire body deteriorated and can you elaborate on how you eat and how much?

Are you are a healthcare provider or practitioner?

We want to support you in integrating the protocols and prescriptions created by Dr. Kruse into your practice, collaborate together to support patients, and support each other in helping our patients achieve health and wellness.

As a Platinum Klub member, you will receive:

Access to a private forum on [JackKruse.com](http://JackKruse.com) where you can collaborate with other practitioners or doctors and Dr. Kruse to brainstorm on patient care, lab dilemmas, and other relevant topics in a private setting

Live monthly Q&As in Google Hangout around topics relevant to being a healthcare provider

Advanced notice of Continuing Education that will be given by Dr. Kruse around Optimizing Health, Reversing Disease and Clinical Case Management

A Listing in the Epi-Paleo Practitioners section of JackKruse.com that will include your info about your clinic, your picture, credentials, address and methods of contact so that members can find you.

Sign up [here](http://http://www.jackkruse.com/platinum/)!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/victoria-bc-vitamin-d-disease-prevention-symposium-may-22nd-free.6944/post-85340>

[http://victoriavitdsym.eventbrite.ca/#](http://victoriavitdsym.eventbrite.ca/#)
FREE Donations to Grassrootshealth.com are accepted but not required to attend

Event Details

The Victoria Vitamin D â€“ Disease Prevention Symposium is a FREE event designed to help educate healthcare professionals and the general public to create awareness about Vitamin D (The Sunshine Vitamin) and its many benefits related to health and wellness.

FEATURING:

Dr. Robert P. Heaney, M.D.

Professor of Medicine, Creighton University, Nebraska. Leading vitamin D researcher and author of over 400 original research papers on nutrition and vitamin D. Dr. Heaney will present an overview of the latest papers on the relationship between optimal vitamin D and disease prevention.

Carole A. Baggerly

Breast cancer survivor and director of [www.grassrootshealth.net](http://www.grassrootshealth.net), an organization dedicated to increasing awareness of vitamin D benefits, especially breast cancer reduction. Carole will focus on vitamin D, breast cancer prevention and children's health.

Dr. Marc Sorenson, EdD

Founder of the Sunlight Institute ([www.sunlightinstitute.org](http://www.sunlightinstitute.org)) and author of the book â€œVitamin D3 and Solar Power for Optimal Healthâ€￾. Marc will present â€œthe best source of vitamin D.â€￾ Learn scientific facts about the benefits and risks of sunlight.

My hubby's cholesterol keeps going down. I don't believe his diet is the problem. He got Crohn's in remission but got a leaky gut. What is the fastest way to increase his cholesterol?
He eats a lot of fat .....

**APRIL 2013**

**Total cholesterol 123
HDL 28
LDL 44
Triglycerides 255**

Vitamin D \* 55

**DECEMBER 2012**

Total cholesterol 121
HDL 37
LDL 67
Triglycerides 85

Vitamin D 51

**AUGUST 2012**

Total cholesterol 178
HDL 41
LDL 77
Triglycerides 301

Vitamin D \* 35

He is doing well but I worry about the cholesterol numbers. Presently, he stopped taking everything that he was taking for lowering triglycerides. That is what started the slide in the fall.
Hopefully, that will help bring his cholesterol back up.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-05-06T20:33:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hypertrophied-mitochondria.6984/post-85529>

I came across some terms while reading about a study on Ad Libitum feeding versus Every-Other-Day feeding of mice with and without exercise. One of the terms was " hypertrophied mitochondria". Hypertrophied mitochondria looked more oval/round, if I understand correctly, as opposed to long-and-slim. Anybody know which type would be optimal? The EOD mice were provided less food on the "fasting" days so they weren't starving on those days.

# Thread: I'm now below pre-baby weight!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-now-below-pre-baby-weight.6986/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Ruthie — 2013-05-07T03:58:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-now-below-pre-baby-weight.6986/post-85553>

Hi everyone,

I wanted to share that since starting the JK protocols on Dec. 1, 2012, I have lost 26 lbs! I started at 6 months postpartum and 153 lbs; I am now down to 127 lbs, which is a couple of pounds below where I was when I got pregnant. (I'm 5'3.) I could stand to lose about 5 more lbs and my mummy tummy needs some work, but I feel good again in clothes!

Prior to starting the Leptin Rx, I was totally frustrated at my inability to budge the baby weight. I had basically been at the same weight since a couple of weeks after the birth. Other paleo sources had me scared that if I cut carbs my thyroid or milk supply would suffer. I am still successfully breastfeeding my 10-mo-old and my thyroid is doing great!

If I find the time, I want to write more in depth about my issues, how they have improved, and what I still need to work on. (maybe start a journal) But I wanted to go ahead and share this one thing to encourage anyone else.

Cheers,
Ruthie

### caroline — 2013-05-07T04:32:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-now-below-pre-baby-weight.6986/post-85554>

Ruthie - congratulations on your success! - such great news to share with us ....please do start a journal ...we would love to hear more of your story and keep us posted on how you are doing......

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-07T16:05:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-now-below-pre-baby-weight.6986/post-85567>

Congrats Ruthie!! Great testimony!

### sooperb — 2014-03-11T00:20:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-now-below-pre-baby-weight.6986/post-114619>

Having a chuckle at the thread title, I had this mental image of someone who now weighs less than when they were a baby. I'm quite glad I was wrong

### SeaHorse — 2013-05-07T15:20:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/enlightened-doctor-in-the-uk.6987/post-85564>

I think there a quite a few people here who have come through the GAPS program (I was one of them) she has a lot of great information in her books. The timeframe she operates with is like Weston Price...the last few hundred years of "traditional" food culture. What has opened my eyes reading JK's material is that we are better off thinking in terms of (tens of) thousands of years of evolution and changing environments...that's where some of the best answers lie in figuring out our modern health issues.

Still....she is light years ahead of most doctors in her understanding of the gut/brain connection.

### caroline — 2013-05-08T02:24:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/enlightened-doctor-in-the-uk.6987/post-85603>

### bonsaiz — 2013-05-13T10:23:19+0300

# Thread: Sleep, ya big dummy! No sleep = propensity for developing Cancer
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-ya-big-dummy-no-sleep-propensity-for-developing-cancer.7007/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-05-09T21:07:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-ya-big-dummy-no-sleep-propensity-for-developing-cancer.7007/post-85745>

[http://www.lef.org/news/LefDailyNew...twitter&utm\_medium=social&utm\_campaign=normal](http://www.lef.org/news/LefDailyNews.htm?NewsID=19348&Section=Disease&utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=normal)

"Melatonin, the hormone produced by the pineal gland and responsible for regulating our sleep-wake cycles, is widely thought to be at the heart of this link between sleep and cancer. In the lab, researchers have found that higher melatonin levels suppress tumor growth; levels close to those found in people with too much exposure to artificial light seem to promote more aggressive tumor growth in cancer cells."

Dr Kruse in [http://www.jackkruse.com/why-do-we-sleep/](http://www.jackkruse.com/why-do-we-sleep/)
tells us the importance of sleep and cancer.

> It appears that sleep directly effects the chronic diseases of aging and likely plays a role in cancer development.

Follow this link to the blog index and scroll down to the category "sleep" for the other blog post pearls of wisdom regarding sleep.
[http://www.jackkruse.com/blog-index/](http://www.jackkruse.com/blog-index/)

Anybody still think sleep is not important to optimal health?

# Thread: Little buzzing buggers - conquered?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/little-buzzing-buggers-conquered.7008/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### diane — 2013-05-09T22:06:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/little-buzzing-buggers-conquered.7008/post-85752>

Yes, mosquitos! Had the most curious thing happen on the weekend. When there are mosquitos, I usually feel them biting me before I even notice they're out. Yes, I'm the one that's always covered in bites - after camping have counted 100 bites on my legs alone.

We were outside Sunday evening, and I could hear them buzzing and actually caught a couple. And I didn't get one bit. Huh?? Felt a bit weird, but so exciting! Or maybe I'm just too sour for the pesky little buggers. LOL. Am wondering what's made a difference - so I can keep it up! I hate bug spray - but I'm sure someone here will have a great natural repellent idea!

Or maybe I won't need it.....

### agatha — 2013-05-10T17:00:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/little-buzzing-buggers-conquered.7008/post-85789>

That's very convenient if they little blighters don't like you any more. I have heard others say this too after going low carb.

### cantweight — 2013-05-10T18:29:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/little-buzzing-buggers-conquered.7008/post-85791>

I always thought it had something to do with sweat or how much CO2 your body puts off....my daughter and i rarely get bit while my husband and sons get eaten up. Would love to know the answer..glad those suckers are leaving you be!!!

### Dextery — 2013-05-10T23:24:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/little-buzzing-buggers-conquered.7008/post-85808>

> [diane said:](/goto/post?id=85752)
>
> Yes, mosquitos! Had the most curious thing happen on the weekend. When there are mosquitos, I usually feel them biting me before I even notice they're out. Yes, I'm the one that's always covered in bites - after camping have counted 100 bites on my legs alone.
>
> We were outside Sunday evening, and I could hear them buzzing and actually caught a couple. And I didn't get one bit. Huh?? Felt a bit weird, but so exciting! Or maybe I'm just too sour for the pesky little buggers. LOL. Am wondering what's made a difference - so I can keep it up! I hate bug spray - but I'm sure someone here will have a great natural repellent idea!
>
> Or maybe I won't need it.....
>

I suspect the reason is the consumption of copious amounts of coherent spring water. But I certainly don't know why the critters don't like your blood now. Maybe it is your now smooth skin.

I recall that mosquitos are attracted by exhalation of carbon dioxide as Cantweight said and maybe the water has cut down on CO2 exhalation.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-05-11T08:31:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/little-buzzing-buggers-conquered.7008/post-85830>

Ketones repel mosquitos!

[http://www.telegraph.co.uk/science/...t-smelling-sweat-keeps-mosquitoes-at-bay.html](http://www.telegraph.co.uk/science/science-news/6142961/Sweet-smelling-sweat-keeps-mosquitoes-at-bay.html)
Sweet smelling sweat keeps mosquitoes at bay - Telegraph
[www.telegraph.co.uk](http://www.telegraph.co.uk) › Science › Science News‎
by Richard Gray - in 64 Google+ circles
Sep 5, 2009 – Now scientists have discovered key differences in the body odours ... The researchers found that these compounds, known as ketones, repel mosquitoes. ... New repellent could stop mosquitoes by confusing their smell ...

For what its worth, the other day a female coworker told me "you smell good!"
I don't wear cologne or a fragrance at work, so maybe she was detecting some of those fruity ketones. I had some fun with her and said its the cologne I use called Midnight Pasture.

### SeaHorse — 2013-05-11T15:26:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/little-buzzing-buggers-conquered.7008/post-85837>

Another good reason to stay ketosis during the summer! On a related note....I found that the first couple of bug bites of the season did not send me into a huge over reaction...a little redness, a little itching and then they both faded.This is a HUGE difference for me as I was often driven into a giant state of craziness by my bug bite reactions plus I, like you Diane, was the original bug magnet in my family. For the last few years I've been wearing an overshirt and hat that have the bug repellant impregnated in the fabric...not ideal but it made me get through the summers without insanity.

# Thread: Thyroid Lab Questions, Please Help!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-lab-questions-please-help.7015/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### saben — 2013-05-11T02:02:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-lab-questions-please-help.7015/post-85816>

Hi,
I have Hashimoto's and have been working to feel better-non-flouridated water, Blue-light blocking glasses at night, EMF mitigation, diet, progesterone, DHEA, Mitochondrial RX...I've seen progress but still struggling with energy. I'm currently on 1 grain of Naturethroid and 5 mcg of Cytomel. I doubled my dose of both to see if I felt better and I think I do. I checked in with my doctor who did not feel comfortable increasing my dose of either medication without checking my current thyroid functioning. Just received an email with my results:
T3, Free 2.6 Range 2.3-4.2 pg/ml
TSH 0.9 Range .40-4.50 miu/l
T4, Free (Thyroxine Free Direct) 0.9 Range .8-1.8 ng/dl, with a note saying the current lot of Free T4 reagent available from the manufacturer produce results that are approximately 9% higher than previous reagent lots. Please interpret these results accordingly.

What are your thoughts on my thyroid function? What would be good numbers for labs? My doctor seems to think my results are normal, but I still feel better on a double dose of both medications. Which medication would be more important for optimal labs and feeling optimal?

Thanks.

### JanSz — 2013-05-11T02:25:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-lab-questions-please-help.7015/post-85817>

> [saben said:](/goto/post?id=85816)
>
> Hi,
> **I have Hashimoto's** and have been working to feel better-non-flouridated water, Blue-light blocking glasses at night, EMF mitigation, diet, progesterone, DHEA, Mitochondrial RX...I've seen progress but still struggling with energy. I'm currently on 1 grain of Naturethroid and 5 mcg of Cytomel. I doubled my dose of both to see if I felt better and I think I do. I checked in with my doctor who did not feel comfortable increasing my dose of either medication without checking my current thyroid functioning. Just received an email with my results:
> T3, Free 2.6 Range 2.3-4.2 pg/ml
> TSH 0.9 Range .40-4.50 miu/l
> T4, Free (Thyroxine Free Direct) 0.9 Range .8-1.8 ng/dl, with a note saying the current lot of Free T4 reagent available from the manufacturer produce results that are approximately 9% higher than previous reagent lots. Please interpret these results accordingly.
>
> What are your thoughts on my thyroid function? What would be good numbers for labs? My doctor seems to think my results are normal, but I still feel better on a double dose of both medications. Which medication would be more important for optimal labs and feeling optimal?
>
> Thanks.

To see if you are using the T4 contained in Naturethroid you would have to know your RT3 and your inflammation status.

Having hashi (autoimmune problem)
it is good idea to not eat nightshades.

Are you man or woman?

Basically either should not use progesterone but specially men.

Progesterone is very important hormone and you want a lots of it
but
exhaust first potential of

pregnenolone and DHEA supplementation.

I am partial to pills of
pregnenolone-MLM
and
DHEA-MLM

\-------

Before supplementing with thyroid hormones directly
it is good idea to prepare background
so
thyroid hormones may do their good work.

As a minimum good cortisol-AM

but also iodine and all micronutrients that can be tested using Spectracell.
Search for Spectracell here:

[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508)

Take some time looking that place over.

///

# Thread: When to take meds for thyroid test
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-to-take-meds-for-thyroid-test.7020/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nuttmegs17 — 2013-05-11T16:47:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-to-take-meds-for-thyroid-test.7020/post-85838>

Hey all

I have been taking my meds then waiting 5 to 6 hours before the blood draw. Googling however I'm seeing many suggest taking the blood test fasted or at least before the test ( so no active drug in your system). What's correct? I'm now concerned that all this time my levels may be more off than I think Perhaps my ft3and ft4 are lower than I thought..

Sent from my iPad using Tapatalk HD

### freshveggies — 2013-05-11T17:24:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-to-take-meds-for-thyroid-test.7020/post-85840>

that is how I do it, ---fasting no drugs, unless the doctors asks otherwise.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-05-12T00:45:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-to-take-meds-for-thyroid-test.7020/post-85861>

I have always had the blood drawn first thing in the morning, fasted, and no meds before. You want to especially do this if you are on a natural thyroid med. If you are on T3 ONLY the results may differ a bit.

Your levels of FT3 and FT4 are probably lower.

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-12T01:39:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-to-take-meds-for-thyroid-test.7020/post-85863>

Interesting Patty - Dzugan wants me to take my meds in the am before getting bloodwork...

I wonder what would happen If I took meds then waited several hrs before getting labs???????

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-05-12T02:17:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-to-take-meds-for-thyroid-test.7020/post-85865>

Well you are on armour which has t3 which is incredible short lasting compared to the
T4. So that's my concern....I think it does matter. If you take t3. Then take the test your t3 is going to look higher than if it has been out of your system for several hours

My new doc whom I saw this morning likes to take the test fasted before meds. It was a great consult and she gave me a script to get an ultrasound on my thyroid to check for nodules. Surprisingly I've never had one but have been curious. Insurance will cover that at least I agreed with almost everything she said Of course she is out of network.

One thing she said that surprised me : she said based on my labs I'm HYPER. I know I'm on too much cytomel and not enough T4 but I don't know that I can categorize myself as hyper based on my TSH bc it's depressed from the cytomel. I want to cut back on the cytomel but not until someone can address my low ft4. She uses over the thyroid meds to treat but I want to stick to scripts she'll work with whatever though. I see my reg doc next sat so am curious what his take will be. I'm so fracken confused though! She is ordering a month long saliva female hormone test which I'm excited about. I'm gonna take a break from the bhrt while I do it and see what's going on

Sent from my iPad using Tapatalk HD

### Grizz — 2013-05-12T12:00:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-to-take-meds-for-thyroid-test.7020/post-85890>

FYI,

For those suffering thyroid problems, this is an extremely valuable website & book:
[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/site-map/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/site-map/)

Also, thyroid problems are the result of iodine deficiency. Many with thyroid problems fully recover when taking the Dr. Brownstein Iodine Protocol. Details here:
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout)

For women, the thyroid, uterus, ovaries, brain & breasts battle over what little iodine you get in your diet, and one or more of these organs gets shorted resulting in disease... typically painful lumps & bumps in the breasts, PCOS, uterus problems or thyroid problems. This is the reason why women suffer from thyroid problems far more then men do and at earlier ages. Thyroid is usually the first to go from iodine deficiency because it is so highly dependent on iodine. Symptoms are swollen thyroid, nodules, hypothyroid and for those who are also grossly deficient in selenium - hyperthyroid. Doctors should be treating thyroid problems with iodine, but they won't because it will destroy their income. The thyroid can get permanently damaged from iodine deficiency, and in these cases, thyroid meds may be needed for life. Yet the need for iodine is still present for the brain, breasts, uterus & ovaries. Common symptoms of iodine deficiency is Brain Fog, headaches, & feeling stupid. A [great new book on this subject](http://www.amazon.com/The-Iodine-Crisis-About-Wreck/dp/098603200X) is **"The Iodine Crises: What you don't know about iodine can ruin your life."**

Men also suffer from iodine deficiency, but not nearly so much as women, and much later in life. The brain fog, & thyroid problems are similar for men, followed by prostate & testes problems. In men, libido, hormonal & dysfunction problems fade away with iodine supplements.

List of iodine deficiency symptoms:
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Deficiency](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Deficiency)

Please join with us at the [iodine thread,](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?943-The-iodine-Thread!!!/page57)
Grizz

### yewwei.tan — 2013-11-22T11:43:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee.7032/post-101328>

Dave Asprey's take on coffee is definitely optimised toward ensuring a toxin-free brew, and he's even admitted that his decaf is pretty lacking on the taste department as a result. I've never tried his coffee though.

Regarding MCT oil, coconut oil is just MCT + some other types of fat. ie: MCTs are a part of coconut oil. Just think of pure MCT as concentrated coconut oil (6 times stronger in regular MCT). So you could say that you need to consume 6 times less MCT to achieve the same ketogenic effect as coconut oil. So you should add MCT if you're after the extra brain-boosting effects.

Relatedly, Ben Greenfield recently did [a podcast](http://www.bengreenfieldfitness.com/2013/11/263-5-ways-to-stay-awake-without-coffee-why-you-shouldnt-mix-carbs-and-fats-how-to-build-extreme-speed/) (with [a transcript here](http://www.bengreenfieldfitness.com/2013/11/episode-263-full-transcript/)) talking about "5 ways to stay awake without coffee", whereby he mentions decaf vs caffeinated coffee. Greenfield essentially does caffeinated coffee for 6 weeks, and then decaf for 2 weeks to re-sensitise himself back to the effects of caffeine as well as to mitigate some of the negative effects. He also talks about how green tea contains L-tyrosine, which gives your brain a boost, but without the sleep disruption (so Inger may be correct to grab the tea instead!)

Personally, no coffee for me, since I didn't like where my BUN/Creatinine ratio was going. I ended up limiting most teas pretty heavily as well, and ended up doing bitter melon tea as a substitute.

Sidenote for Aussies: Nature Pacific Coconut oil from Fiji is amazing! Got myself a 20L pail a month ago for $228, or $11.40/L (that's inclusive of shipping costs from Queenslands) -- [http://www.ebay.com.au/itm/CERTIFIE...98?pt=LH\_DefaultDomain\_15&hash=item27d116107a](http://www.ebay.com.au/itm/CERTIFIED-ORGANIC-Extra-Virgin-Coconut-Oil-20-litres-/171011608698?pt=LH_DefaultDomain_15&hash=item27d116107a)

### caroline — 2013-11-23T02:55:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee.7032/post-101424>

Yew ..... have you tried Nutiva coconut oil? Do you find the pail easy enough to handle?

### elvey90 — 2013-11-23T03:57:22+0200

### caroline — 2013-11-23T06:21:34+0200

### yewwei.tan — 2013-11-23T10:17:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coffee.7032/post-101461>

@caroline -- Don't know why I never thought of that. I buy so many things from iherb, but never took a look at coconut oil. I should give it a shot pretty soon.

@jude -- the breakdown of 100g of coconut oil is as follows:

1\. In 100g of coconut oil, there is about 90g of saturated fat, with the other 10g being mono and polyunsaturated fat

2\. Of that 90g of saturated fat, about 65g of that are medium-chain triglycerides (MCTs)

3\. Of that 65g of MCTs, only about 15g are caprylic acid (C8) and capric acid (C10). These are thought to be the "most ketogenic" MCTs

4\. A good MCT oil only consists of C8 and C10. This is where the "6x more powerful than coconut oil" claim comes from -- it would take 100g of coconut oil to get "the same ketogenic effect" as 15g of pure MCT oil.

5\. Between C8 and C10, C8 is "more ketogenic". There is usually only about 5-7g of C8 in coconut oil.

6\. Dave Asprey sells this thing called 'Brain Octane' which is nothing but C8, which is 1/18th the composition of coconut oil, and hence he claims that it's "18x more potent than coconut oil"

There are benefits to having to take less of an oil:
\- Less chance of diarrhea due to MCT overconsumption
\- Easier compliance for some folks

Personally, I can take 4 tablespoons of pure MCT and do just fine

Hope that helps!

The thyroid is not the only organ disrupted by iodine deficiency. Women especially suffer most from iodine deficiency with severe problems with the breast, ovaries, uterus, thyroid & brain. All of these organs are in competition for what little iodine we get in our food.
\* Thyroid - Hypo, Hyper, Swelling & Nodules & cancer
\* Breast - Fibrocystic Breast Disease = painful lumps, bumps & cancer
\* Ovaries - Cysts, swelling, fibroids & cancer
\* Uterus - Fibroids, swelling, endometriosis & cancer
\* Brain - headaches, brain fog, feeling stupid & cancer

I was interested in how I would respond to iodine because I haven't had a thyroid since 1986. So, yes, I was on all the co-factors. I soon found that Lugol's was cheaper, and iodine from the chemist was cheaper still. I stuck with the protocol for 8 months, I think. I never recognised any die-off, and I was up to 50mg/day very quickly. I dropped the protocol after about 8 months or so, only taking iodine occasionally. I haven't really had any since I've been eating seafood regularly.

### caroline — 2013-05-16T08:22:56+0300

### Grizz — 2013-05-16T17:04:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-on-iodine-thyroid-supplementation.7054/post-86205>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=86195)
>
> Corriander - That is what I am still so confused about. I asked JK about iodine at a consult and he said as long as I was eating lots of seafood I was fine - but that doesn't seem to be the prevailing wisdom here.....but he was looking at all my lab results.....

Caroline,
Normal lab tests cannot measure iodine levels in your system. If you want an accurate test, you must do the Iodine Loading Test, which measures the amount of iodine absorbed into your body compared to the amount excreted in urine. It works like this: You take a 50mg iodine pill, then collect your urine for the next 24 hours. The amount of iodine in your urine is measured. If there is NO iodine, your body absorbed it all, indicates you are severely deficient. If all the iodine is in your urine, then your are iodine-sufficient. You should also do the fluoride & bromide test while doing the iodine loading test.
Complete details here:
[http://www.hakalalabs.com/testing.html](http://www.hakalalabs.com/testing.html)

You can also do the iodine skin patch test that provides a ballpark estimate of your iodine deficiency. If the stain on your skin is gone in less than a few hours, then you are severely deficient. When I first did this test, the stain was gone in just 3 minutes ! Use Lugols 5% iodine for this test. This cannot measure iodine sufficiency, but is only useful to estimate a ball park deficiency.
[http://www.utopiasilver.com/iodinetranscript.htm](http://www.utopiasilver.com/iodinetranscript.htm)

The remaining indications of iodine deficiency are the symptoms. If you have any of these symptoms, then iodine deficiency can be assumed.
\* Brain related = Brain Fog, headaches very common,
\* Thyroid related = Hypothyroid, swollen thyroid, nodules, most common
\* Breast realated = lumps, bumps, pain, cancer
\* Ovary related = PCOS
\* Uterus related = Fibroids, severe painful cycles
\* Prostate problems

If in doubt, just do the iodine urine loading test,
Grizz

MIT engineers have transformed bacterial cells into living calculators that can compute logarithms, divide, and take square roots, using three or fewer genetic parts.

# Thread: Converting thyroid meds input needed
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/converting-thyroid-meds-input-needed.7105/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nuttmegs17 — 2013-05-19T05:07:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/converting-thyroid-meds-input-needed.7105/post-86456>

Hello

I am currently in 1.5 grain armour plus 10 mcg cytomel and got my recent labs back

My fr4 is now .84 ( range is .89-1.77)
My ft3 is 4.2 (2.0 -4.4)

I saw my doc today and he thinks its best (and I agree) to switch me to a cytomel synthroid combo. However since i got out of the appt and looked at conversion charts I'm questioning a few things..

If you take what I was on before it equals roughly 57 mcg t4 to around 23 mcg t3 YET

He prescribed me 50 mcg synthroid and kept me at 10mcg cytomel...as my starting dose tomorrow

I get that he wants to go low and slow. However as my t4 is now below range I can't help thinking decreasing my t4 isn't the answer. As my t4 has gone down these past few months I have felt more and more fatigued. I understand t4 converts tomt3 but he said if I check in within a week they will just increase my T3 ( and as i am technically on less t3 with the new medsI can see that might need to be increased as well. I'm just concerned that he isn't concerned by my low ft4. He said as long as it doesn't dip below .60 I'm fine!!!!! I thought it should be in the upper third of the range?

What should I ask him to putt me on as far as the cytomel synthroid combo? I expected to start at least as 75mcg with some amount of t3. Thoughts? I'm so confused and terrified of how I will feel this week as I already feel like crap.

Sent from my iPad using Tapatalk HD

### Patty Cakes — 2013-05-19T20:11:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/converting-thyroid-meds-input-needed.7105/post-86477>

I think this is one of those situations where you have to try a particular dose and see how it goes for you and then you may have to go up. It really depends on your body's ability to convert (as you said). The dose of 10mcg of Cytomel seems quite low to me. I wish I could tell you what would work because I really feel for your situation and fatigue. I went a year untreated, then treated myself and found the proper dose. Then after trying many things like no meds (to see if maybe I really didn't need them) to a lesser dose, I have resinged myself to the fact that I want to feel good and don't care that I take the meds. Experimentation has proven to me what I personally need.

I was on 3 grains of NP Thyroid (like Armour) taken twice daily and switched to all Cytomel at 30mcg taken in three doses (2 doses 12 and 1 dose of 6mcg). I ratcheted up from there based on my symptoms of fatigue, heart rate, temp, BP. I usually took the last three readings every couple of hours the first few months to make sure I wasn't going hyper. I ended up at 75mcg for a few months then actually went down because it seemed like a lot and I felt the same on 60mcg. Stayed there for a year and now I am currently in the process of switching back to NP Thyroid for my afternoon and keeping the T3 in the morning. I going back I am on 25mcg of T3 in the morning and 1 1/2 grains NP Thyroid in the afternoon. Not sure if this is the ideal dosing but so far so good.

My body does convert a little T4 into FT3 but it's such an individual thing. I hope you are able to figure your dose out. I'm sure it has been mentioned a zillion times before but make sure your adrenals are good and your iron is optimal. If these two things aren't in place your body may not be able to handle your proper dose of any of the thyroid meds. You will feel hyper and fatigued at the same time or for some people they just feel hyper.

Overall I think you need a bit more Cytomel to start. I know nothing about synthetic T4 although I had heard on a podcast the the T4 in Armour is synthetic combined with a porcine T3. I don't know if this is true as Armour didn't work for me at all which is why I switched to NP Thyroid as my natural med.

### Grizz — 2013-05-20T00:16:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/converting-thyroid-meds-input-needed.7105/post-86489>

Our bible, "Stop The Thyroid Madness" says NO WAY to synthroid:
[http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/synthroid/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/synthroid/)

Grizz

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-05-20T01:35:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/converting-thyroid-meds-input-needed.7105/post-86490>

Hello grizz. I'm very familiar with STTM and think they do great work i have learned a ton from them but also feel they can be a bit fanatical. Not everyone responds to natural thyroid. Some even get worse if it happens to set off antibodies or if the fillers cause a reaction. I think that attitude can be just as dangerous as an uninformed endo who ONLY uses synthroid and refuses to try anything else.

There are lots of options and combos and everyone is different I never did well on natural thyroid and gave it 2years before deciding to switch. Because its in a set ratio i finally hit a wall where it was impossible to move forward. For every story where someone says "I was on synthetics and switched to natural and it finally worked"there are just as many who have the same experience going from natural to synthetics. I'm not using just synthroid I'm using both synthroid and cytomel. I'm not sure yet it's the answer but its worth a try

Patty cakes. Thanks for your answer. I think you are right about the trial and error. I will try and be patient . Just sick and tired of being sick and tired. Yesterday was a real low for me as I was so lethargic and tired and I decided to go with 75mcg synthroid but keep the cytomel the same. I plan to call my doc tomorrow and explain why and hope they are okay with it I am sure it's not my final dose but hopefully ill feel better in a week or so and we can adjust from there

Sent from my iPad using Tapatalk HD
