# Thread: my thyroid is waking up
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-thyroid-is-waking-up.7114/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### agatha — 2013-05-20T23:16:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-thyroid-is-waking-up.7114/post-86560>

Hi there y'all
I have been taking thyroxine for some years and have been having to gradually increase my dose. However, in the last few weeks I have been having hyperthyroid symptoms and my TSH is now 0.4 (lowest end of normal range) - I have reduced my dose of thyroxine. About six weeks ago I changed two things - i started eating fish 3-4 times a week and making a little seaweed drink using about a teaspoon of dried hijiki about 3 days a week. I also started using an earthing sheet at night.

Do any of you have any thoughts about whether it is the increased iodine or the earthing which is more likely to have woken up my thyroid gland?

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-21T01:45:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-thyroid-is-waking-up.7114/post-86565>

I know my thyroid perked up w/ the increase of iodine last summer and its kept on trucking.....

### nuttmegs17 — 2013-05-21T04:35:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-thyroid-is-waking-up.7114/post-86577>

Great to hear this! Could be iodine and the cofactors for sure!

Sent from my iPad using Tapatalk HD

### agatha — 2013-05-21T22:19:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-thyroid-is-waking-up.7114/post-86612>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=86565)
>
> I know my thyroid perked up w/ the increase of iodine last summer and its kept on trucking.....

Thanks for these replies. I am a bit nervous about the iodine as I know some people think it can stir up Hashimoto's. I didn't have the Hashimotos antibodies when i got tested a few years ago but I don't want to create a problem. Have you been OK, Shijin since you started with the iodine?

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-21T22:49:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-thyroid-is-waking-up.7114/post-86614>

> [agatha said:](/goto/post?id=86612)
>
> Thanks for these replies. I am a bit nervous about the iodine as I know some people think it can stir up Hashimoto's. I didn't have the Hashimotos antibodies when i got tested a few years ago but I don't want to create a problem. Have you been OK, Shijin since you started with the iodine?

no issues as far as I know - we haven't tested AB - since I tested negative for 4 consecutive years all I know is I crave it. I'm eating seaweed as my main veggie at every meal; my kids are doing the same thing - especially dd - who we just discovered is hypo - at age 5!

I also put the Lugols in our water - I never could understand why I was drinking so much water - until I realized my kids preferred my water w/the 5% lugols in it over their water w/the 2% lugols in it.. I took about 6mths to work up to the 5% lugols - and did the same for the kids...

### Grizz — 2013-05-21T23:07:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-thyroid-is-waking-up.7114/post-86618>

Agatha,
Iodine requires Selenium - they are co-supplements. One without the other can cause problems with the thyroid... primarily hashi. This is why the Dr. Brownstein Iodine Protocol specifies selenium as one of the co-supplements.
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout)

For more on the connection between selenium & the thyroid, see these articles:
[https://docs.google.com/document/d/...5jXDmOp0HwpdBS6VY/edit#heading=h.q6cw9ojckwx1](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1_q9ixGwZFon9yidvmZ0IFbp3Vj5jXDmOp0HwpdBS6VY/edit#heading=h.q6cw9ojckwx1)

[Hashi & Selenium Articles](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1_q9ixGwZFon9yidvmZ0IFbp3Vj5jXDmOp0HwpdBS6VY/edit#heading=h.8eltjvzb6ets)

Grizz

### Meg — 2013-05-22T01:29:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-thyroid-is-waking-up.7114/post-86624>

Yeah Agatha! I know it feels good to see some change in the right direction. I am hypo and have had to back down on meds twice since February due to hyper symptoms and labs (TSH .006!). I can only attribute it to one thing, GROUNDING! That was my only change. How do you feel on the grounding sheets? I tried to use them but they made me feel bad- too much EMF in my environment I guess, made me like a like an antenae. Good luck moving forward.

### agatha — 2013-05-22T17:09:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-thyroid-is-waking-up.7114/post-86670>

Thanks for these helpful replies - I think I will continue cautiously with the seaweed and see what happens but I can see from your links Grizz that I need to make sure my selenium is replete - do you think Brazil nuts will do the job - I prefer food to supplements?
Meg, I am pleased with the earthing sheet - I sleep better and my husband's blood pressure which was high has reduced - but we live in a leafy location with not too much EMF. I did have a weird reaction the first few nights but that has settled now.
I am so grateful for you all and Doc K - your knowledge, wisdom and helpfulness. I would be entirely in the dark if I had to rely on my GP for all my medical info.

### Grizz — 2013-05-23T00:46:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-thyroid-is-waking-up.7114/post-86686>

Agatha,

Wife & I have been using 3 Brazil nuts a day for our selenium. You can also eat a can of sardines for your selenium.
I don't know if it meets the dosage requirement made by Dr. Brownstein, but it works for us.

Grizz

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-smart-meter-pilot-being-started-in-my-community.7127/post-86626>

Okay all you hunter-gatherers of information, I would like to suggest using this thread as a clearinghouse of links for Smart Meters and their effects.

I live in a small community, and they are in the process of initiating a smart meter pilot project. 1500 homes have been selected to receive smart meters, and I would like to write an article for the paper so that people can make an informed decision. As a side-note, I need hard data. I live in a community with a disproportionate number of PhDs. We've been ranked as one of the "smartest" communities in the nation. These people are gonna need the most sciency-stuff I can get my hands on.

I would like to see this pilot fail and I'm hoping you can help!

Thank you!

# Thread: Twist DHEA cream- is it any good?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/twist-dhea-cream-is-it-any-good.7128/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread

### Meg — 2013-05-22T01:48:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/twist-dhea-cream-is-it-any-good.7128/post-86627>

I've been meaning to chime in about this for awhile now. I have seen crazy good results from using this cream that Dr. K mentioned awhile back on a webinar. But beware- a little goes a long way, at least it did for me. Using the recommended dosage (which is two pumps per day) I went from 170 to 942, so I had to back down some. I was on 350 mg of oral 7 keto dhea before - and only had a dhea of 172! After using Twist I felt a noticable difference in my energy and sleep. If anyone is looking for a way to boost DHEA, I recomend that you try this and report back!

### fitness@home — 2013-07-30T04:03:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/twist-dhea-cream-is-it-any-good.7128/post-91147>

Reviving thread. This is an off label use for the product and I am not encouraging or recommending it. Just reporting my N=1.

Lately I've been getting an increase in compliments on my skin. What I started initially was after I rubbed the Twist DHEA cream on my arms, I would use what was left on my face. There wasn't much on my finger tips by that point.

After a few weeks of this and nothing freaky happening , I started using just a tiny dab on my cheekbones and the area around my eyes (crows feet). Not saying that this is the only reason for the improvement in the appearance of my skin... I have been working on other things too.

Just wanted to share.

### fitness@home — 2013-09-25T18:13:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/twist-dhea-cream-is-it-any-good.7128/post-96084>

A couple of months have passed. I’m stopping back to report on the results of using the DHEA cream on my face. I use it on the skin around the eyes and a tiny bit on my eyelids.

Over the past couple of years I noted a crepey look to the skin on my eyelids. When I would lift my eyebrows, there would be a distinct upside down “V” shape in the skin wrinkling (thanks Mom). She has had two eyelid lift surgeries in the past 15 years (and both were covered by insurance…not considered cosmetic).

I am pleased to report that the crepey appearance has improved and the “V” has faded significantly! The skin looks more plumped and hydrated. I can’t say conclusively that it is the topical application of DHEA cream but it certainly hasn’t had a negative effect.

**Clarification: this is not me!** It is just the closest picture I found showing what I described above.

# Thread: A truly great story regarding Autism and one determined mother
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-truly-great-story-regarding-autism-and-one-determined-mother.7139/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-05-23T07:35:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-truly-great-story-regarding-autism-and-one-determined-mother.7139/post-86708>

[http://www.naturalnews.com/040437\_a...105479\_6702468\_599624996727810#fd5ea9bc422414](http://www.naturalnews.com/040437_autism_special_education_genius.html?fb_comment_id=fbc_599181640105479_6702468_599624996727810#fd5ea9bc422414)

I have never heard of this Autistic genius up til now. His journey with the help of a very persistent mother is truly remarkable. A thought by this mother changed this kids entire world. If we seek our own guidance with reason, we can truly do remarkable things.

Our world is changed by this kids achievements. Pretty good results for a kid that was diagnosed with Autism at age two and his mother was told he would never be able to tie his own shoes.

### Inger — 2013-05-23T07:59:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-truly-great-story-regarding-autism-and-one-determined-mother.7139/post-86711>

### caroline — 2013-05-23T10:46:26+0300

# Thread: The effect of the smell of chocolate on the brain.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-effect-of-the-smell-of-chocolate-on-the-brain.7142/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-05-23T19:09:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-effect-of-the-smell-of-chocolate-on-the-brain.7142/post-86734>

This was posted on Facebook by a lady who identifies herself as a mental health counselor in Switzerland who pretty much mirrors Dr. K on a lot of things....CT and EMFs and a lot more.

She wrote this today regarding studys done to test various smells and then measured brain activity....especially about the smell of chocolate. Also she talks about what compounds are contained in chocolate and what they do for the body.

One question I have is.....chocolate contains the compound **Theobromine** 200mg in 100 grams of chocolate. Grizz has written about how important it is to rid the body of bromine, chlorides, fluorides and Aluminum. Does **Theobromine** compound doing anything to us that we do not want?

On the good side, chocolate contains a lot of resveratrol & a lot of other antioxidants good for the body.

Perhaps someone who has sleep issues prior to retiring for the night can open a bar of Lindt and smell the aroma and assist the sleep cycle.

I especially like Lindt 95% available at my Wal Mart for $1.68 for a 4 oz bar. I also like raw cacao nibs in Greek Yogurt that I get from nutsonline.com

The following is a translation by BING from the original German so it is a little disjointed at times.

Therapiezentrum Steinfels

Had a technical explanation just once again a B.s. attack and here:
The scent of chocolate can affect brain activity, which resulted in an experiment of the London neuro psychologist Neil Martin. "No other smell that we tested had such strong effects of chocolate". Together with colleagues, he had analyzed the effect of different fragrances in a study on the people. The subjects sat here in special rooms with neutral smell air, wore blindfold and earmuffs, so that they could focus on the sense of smell. While they were confronted with various flavours, their brain activity was measured. "Chocolate turned out in our odor experiment as a star", emphasizes Martin. Smell of coffee or garlic smell, not the aroma of
Peppermint, nor that of baked beans could cause such effects like chocolate in the brain. As "pleasant, comfortable and very relaxing", the "nose" felt the chocolate scent. "It seems at the same time to relax the brain and to attract, but also mentally awake to make," portrays Martin.

The aroma of chocolate increased at the same time the alpha and Beta activity of brain waves (see: frequency of EEG). "Alpha is often relaxed, but watchful adults found, beta again with people who operate a kind of mental arithmetic", explains Martin, who themselves can not explain mentioned effects of chocolate. Perhaps, the emotional Center in the brain is activated. But that in turn would open the question why not other pleasant scents trigger such effects.

It remains also a mystery why the smell of coffee and rotting meat showed no effects. "Just from these two odors we expected, that they can do a lot", says Martin.

Finally, American neuro-psychologists found that in some people the pleasure of chocolate can even lead to addiction. In subjects that are quickly fill with chocolate, the researchers found increased brain activity in areas associated with addiction in conjunction. The subjects who consumed chocolate just enjoyable, the region of the brain showed no activity.

Cocoa contains the following psychoactive substances (per 100 g): caffeine: 68 milligrams; Theobromine (similar effect as caffeine, blood vessels widen and stimulated the heart muscle): 200 milligrams. Smallest amounts of biogenic amines increase blood pressure increase and affect the functions of the brain. In addition, 1 milligram phenylethylamine causes the release of dopamine in the brain. Anandamide occurs only in smallest quantities and is a hemp plants occurring THC-like noise substance produced by the body itself. The release of anandamide occurs when stress and pain, therefore increases the feeling of well-being. There are only small amounts of chocolate to prove. Contains antioxidant chocolate polyphenols, which include anti-inflammatory and cancer-inhibiting effect.

American scientists have recently discovered that ground cocoa and unsweetened baking chocolate contain high amounts of Resveratrol (also in red wine and grape juice), preventing a highly active, secondary plant substance that protects the cells and the formation of free radicals. (Translated by Bing)

yet again Science explains why I LOVE CHOCOLATE.... nom nom nom.... now where did I place my 100% chocolate.... oh wait - there's some nibs....

### SeaHorse — 2013-05-27T15:35:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-effect-of-the-smell-of-chocolate-on-the-brain.7142/post-86939>

Recently I've been reading a bit about chocolate and Parkinsons....if you google the two together you come up with some interesting contradictions...some saying there are componds that are damaging and others saying the polyphenals are a benefit.

Because my mother had Parkinsons and chocolate (even without sugar) triggers a "more, more, more" in my brain, I'm going to stay away. I will use it as a mild stimulant instead of caffein if I have to driving long distances (i.e. 12 hour trips).

# Thread: Long Term Use of BCP and Recovery
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-term-use-of-bcp-and-recovery.7143/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mafixit — 2013-05-23T22:38:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-term-use-of-bcp-and-recovery.7143/post-86742>

After being on BC Pills since I was 16 - I am 49 now - I let them go late last year and moved to BHRT. It's been a bad downhill ride ever since.

I am wondering if:

A. I have done damage to my system that cannot be reversed.
B. How long it will take for my body to adjust to not having the artificial crap in my system.

Ever since I stopped, I have felt worse and worse by the day. It's been 6 months. I am under the care of a hormone doc. I see her in a couple of weeks for more labs, so I will have more of an answer of where I am at and why I still feel like crap all over.

I have advised my young adult daughter to stay away from artificial hormones, knowing what I know now. At least I can save her

Karen from Arizona

Karen from Arizona

What jumps out at me are the following:
D3 level needs to be above 70 ng/ml The 10000IU daily will eventually get you there 70 to 120 target.
Even though you are supplementing D3, be sure to get at least 20 min a day of full sun on as much of your skin as possible to recharge your body with photons from the sun. We all need the photons due to our lack of sun due to sun phobia in the past. My dermatologist tells me I am going to die of melanoma if I don't use sunscreen. Funny thing is the last 3 checkups have shown no basal cell or squamous spots. I did have one lesion excised two years a little after I found Dr. K.
Triglycerides need to come down to under 50 and 40-35 is even better.
HDL needs to go up as high as you can get it Over 80 This can get achieved by taking Slo-Niacin 1000 to 2000mg per day split morning and evening. This is the only product that does not get me a flush response. All the so called no flush products make me flush.
Dhea-s needs to be higher. Dr K has mentioned this DHEA cream and others have said it raised their levels very well. Twist DHEA cream. [http://www.amazon.com/Health2Go-Inc...TF8&qid=1369420269&sr=1-3&keywords=dhea+cream](http://www.amazon.com/Health2Go-Inc-1-7-OZ-pump/dp/B007OUXDF8/ref=sr_1_3?s=hpc&ie=UTF8&qid=1369420269&sr=1-3&keywords=dhea+cream)

[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7128-Twist-DHEA-cream-is-it-any-good&highlight=twist](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7128-Twist-DHEA-cream-is-it-any-good&highlight=twist)

Also, you also need to do a NMR Lipid profile test which will give you the amount of LDL particles you have in your blood. And it will tell you how many of your total LDL particles are the small dense particles that promote Atherosclerosis - 10% or under is ideal.

Jansz can give you insight on your preg and testosterone levels.

For an exercise program, once a day, take 5 minutes and run all out for 20 seconds, rest 10 seconds, then run all out for 20 sec and rest 10 sec...repeat for 8 cycles and then quit. I now do 10 cycles for 6 min out of my day. Not too much to ask. These are called Tabata exercises.

Stay on the epi-paleo RX and eat the 50 grams of protein in the morning...within 30 min of getting up....with adequate fat. You will not need to eat lunch and then lots of salad and fish or meat for dinner. Use a lot of coconut oil at this time of year to cook with. In winter, use primarily animal fats.

Drink at least a gallon of non fluoridated water a day. You will be amazed at the amount of energy you get from the water after a week or so...so much so that your hunger hormone...ghrelin...will tell you that you are not hungry.

You will feel much better and your labs will improve dramatically over time.

Again you've gotten great info from Dex!

DHEA, Testosterone, and pregnenolone are in the toilet - I'm assuming this is your Husband/SO Kathy. He needs to start eating Epi-paleo, following the Leptin reset, limiting artificial light at night and emf.

in addition, he can raise his DHEA, Testosterone levels by Laying out in the sun with as much skin exposed as possible - limit the sunscreen - but he'll need to build up time given his low vitamin D. He can also raise his testosterone levels by CTing! get him in a cold TUB ASAP! or at least start face dunks.

he's got a serious case of pregnenolone steal, as a result of a circadian mismatch. which is impacting all of his hormones and his Vit D! He really needs to pay attention to the circadian cycles.

His Free T3: rT3 ratio is 23 - which is good, but for the rT3 value you want it as low as possible and his is in the mid range.

ONe thing you can do is consider supplementing w/DHEA as well as Pregnenolone. both of which you can buy on Amazon, and LEF.org without a prescription.
a good starting point would be 100mg of DHEA and btwn 100-200mg of Pregnenolone and retest in a month or so...

as JanSz says "I'm not a Doctor" but I have figure out how to help myself!

Kathy's (lucky) husband Paul;

Hope you guys are doing good, live long and prosper.
\-------------------------------------------------------

You will get lots of good advice here.
So I will assume that you are following what dr Jack Kruse is preaching.
\----------------

Now is summer, easy to get sun exposure.
Do not do it until your vit D is tested and is highly over 50, better 100.
It may not happen this summer.

Depending on your skin, but specially when light skinned, easily burned, red head, it may be a good idea to get (melanotan II) injections.
(Melanotan II), MT-II, MT-2
can be bought over internet, google

(Melanotan 2) or MT 2
not to be confused with (Melanotan I)

Melanotan II (Listeni/mɛˈlÃ¦nɵtÃ¦n/) developed at the University of Arizona is a synthetic analog of the naturally occurring melanocortin peptide hormone alpha-melanocyte stimulating hormone (α-MSH)
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Melanotan\_II](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Melanotan_II)

Sometimes it also have aphrodisiac effects (so be prepared, near your hubby, for oxytocim making if it would work that way)
\==================================

Steroid Hormone Panel is trashed badly,
but what sticks out is cortisol

7-9 am 1.05 (0.27 – 1.18 mcg/dl)
11am-1pm 2.23 (0.10-0.41)
3-5pm 0.07 (0.05-0.27)
10pm-12am 0.06 (0.03-0.14)

1.05+2.23+0.07+0.06=3.41

1.18+0.41+0.27+0.14=2

There is twice as much cortisol than it should be,
actually there is 3.41 but it should be about 2.

That is very important.
You must first figure out why that is.
You must first find out how much cortisol you are actually making (and discarding in urine over 24 hr).

Depending on your resources and inclination you may want to just go to LabCorp and get:

Cortisol, Urinary Free (24-hour) Test Number: 004432 CPT Code: 82530

But collecting urine 24 hr is a chore, and when you already have a sample, it would be a good idea to put it to better use.

[http://www.tahomaclinic.com/services/](http://www.tahomaclinic.com/services/)
[http://meridianvalleylab.com/](http://meridianvalleylab.com/)

test 4100 Comprehensive ULTIMATE (includes Oxtocin, Melatonin, and hGH)

[http://meridianvalleylab.com/wp-content/uploads/2012/11/CompULTIMATE\_sample.pdf](http://meridianvalleylab.com/wp-content/uploads/2012/11/CompULTIMATE_sample.pdf)

\==================

Depending on what you may want to do, at the minimum go to LabCorp and see how much cortisol you are making over 24 hrs.
but that test at Meridian valley (dr Johnathan Wright) is the best when trying to figure what to do.

Assuming that the alert is premature, and you are not excreting excessive amounts of cortisol,
we will go to "normal" program of raising your steroid hormone panel.
That will include
raising your currently very low
pregnenolone
progesterone
testosterone
DHEAs

Keep in mind that when you take a one drop of blood, and measure all major steroid hormones within that drop of blood,
the biggest part by weight is taken by DHEAs

DHEAs part is 95.4% of them all

and
it happens that your blood test says
DHEA sulfate 103 (25-240 mcg/dL)

This part (25-240 mcg/dL)
is misleading and worthless,
you want DHEAs level of young 20-25 yo Adonis
and that is about 500
so DHEAs needs to be raised 5x

practically there is very severe deficiency of major hormone.
\--------------------------------------

Also Totaltestosterone needs to be much higher
the aim is to have
BioAvailableTestosterone=575.0 ng/dL
instead of
Testo, Bio 90.8 L (110.0 – 575.0 ng/dL)(this is test done at QuestDiagnostics)
other labs have different values

there is three more items that need to be reviewed/addressed
PSA---- prostate (state unknown)
Hgb, Hct, RBC, iron, ferritin ---- blood (state unknown)
SHBG -- Sex Hormone Globulin (currently high)

You may want to check the missing items first or start testosterone supplementation and fill in the blanks latter

One more, fertility, is it important to preserve?
If fertility is important, do not come close to testosterone supplementation unless you have ample stash of Ovidrel(rHCG) or HCG in your possession, so it will last you at least 6 months.

Another one,
testosterone supplementation will increase your estrogens, that may force you to use Aromatase Inhibitors (Anastrozole=Arimidex)
because you have low progesterone
Progesterone <0.5 (<1.4 ng/mL)
and will be waiting for cortisol status

I will wait for response on all those questions, before proceeding with

testosterone supplementation
pregnenolone-->progesterone--->cortisol/aldosterone management
\-----------

Note:
you may be dealing with major mando pregnenolone steal to produce huge amounts of cortisol

This situation may be (partially) rectified by applying good advice from dr Jack Kruse (read up on that, very important)
I am bringing this up because you will be supplementing with DHEA (I prefer DHEA-MLM)
and
at first you will need a lots of DHEA pills but latter (hopefully) less, when implementations of dr Kruses stuff kicks in.

\-------------

[http://www.amazon.com/Micronized-Ta...36&sr=8-2-spell&keywords=dhea+Allergy+reseach](http://www.amazon.com/Micronized-Tablets-Allergy-Research-Group/dp/B004BZJCCC/ref=sr_1_sc_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1369415136&sr=8-2-spell&keywords=dhea+Allergy+reseach)
DHEA 50 mg Micronized Lipid Matrix 60 Scored Tablets by Allergy Research Group

Use 4 pills/day, two at wakeup and the other two 4 hrs latter
goal
DHEA sulfate 490 mcg/dL on blood test (so, titrate dose size, do blood test month latter)

\----------------
look at this post:
[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508&viewfull=1#post81508](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508&viewfull=1#post81508)

Get
Spectracell

(EMFA) by Genova Diagnostics

and/or

Test ID: FAPCP
Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

but if you want to deal with another doc, but get superior information
call 888 320 8338
dr Patricia Kane
for referral to your local participating doctor and ask for
Red Blood Cell Fatty Acid test from Kennedy Krieger Laboratory
with interpretation
(this may be overkill at this time)

\----------------------------------------------------------

Do not eat fish or krill oils

Do not eat Proscar or Avodart, be careful with Saw Palmetto.

Do not do tests that measure omega6/omega3 index or O6/O3 ratio

...

latter

### [email redacted] — 2013-05-24T18:39:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-for-a-49-year-old-female.7154/post-86799>

Iâ€™m a 49 year old female. I currently weigh 187 pounds. I have been overweight most of my life. Morbidly Obese for 11 years. With in the last two years have lost 90 pounds (the hard way). In high school (1980-81) I had my appendix removed. 1982 I was in an accident where a fell out of a moving truck as it tipped over. (Didnâ€™t realize that affected me until I went to a naturopathic doctor and reading, recently, Dr. Kruse article about concussions.) In 1986 I had a thyroid storm (over active thyroid) I did not choose removal surgery. I had asthma 2-3 years agoâ€¦..no longer have it (thatâ€™s when I started to research about health and made dietary changes).
Currently, I get up and go to the bathroom at night (at least one time)â€¦.on occasion I sleep through the night. I realize Iâ€™m dehydrated on the cellular level. I wake up between 4-5:30 am. Usually get to bed by 9 pm.

**Saliva Test:**

DHEA 7-9am **307** 71-640 pg/mL
DHEA cortisol ratio **472** 115-1,188
7-9am **0.65** 0.27-1.18 mcg/dL
11am-1pm **0.28** 0.10-0.41
3pm-5pm **0.06** 0.05-0.27
10pm-12am **0.06** 0.03-0.14

HDL **59**
Triglycerides **54**
Cardio CRP (R) **1.0**
Glucose **75**
Hemoglobin A1c **5.3** <5.7 decreased risk of diabetes
Vitamin D **35**
T4, (thyroxine) total **7.4** 4.5-12.00 mcg/dL
T4 Free **1.1** 0.8-1.8 ng/dL
T3 Free **2.4** 2.3-4.2 pg/mL
Reverse T3 **18** 7.24 ng/dL
Estrone **60**
Dihydrotestosterone **<5 L** 5-46 ng/dL
Pregnenolone **41** Premenopausal 7-188 ng/dL
Progesterone **27.0**
**
Testosterone free, bio**
Testo total **39** 2-45 ng/dL
Testo free **2.7** 0.2-5.0 ng/ml
Testo bio **5.5** 0.5 -8.5 ng/dl
Sex hormone binding globulin **62** 17-124 nmol/L
Albumin, serum **4.4** 3.6-5.1 g/dl

Currently taking 10,000 IU's of Vitamin D. Need to get re-tested.

Thanks for your help! Kathy

### caroline — 2013-05-24T23:46:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-for-a-49-year-old-female.7154/post-86807>

Hi Kathy! I am sure that Dex will chime in on your labs as soon as he has time - he is great at reading labs and also JanZ.

Are you doing the Leptin reset? Light cycles are extremely important and drinking lots of non- fluoridated water. What about your EMF exposure? All the details really matter a lot!

Have you started C/T yet - that would help tremendously ... and how is your sleep?

I know it is alot to take on board but ...baby steps. Keep reading and re reading the blogs and eventually you start to connect the dots. It is great that you both got your labs done and super that you are doing it together!

Keep reading and asking questions and we will all try to help. If you are 100% committed and follow the protocols you will get there .... but it does take time .....We are all on the same path but everyone's journey is very different.

We are all looking for our own n=1 - it takes time and this journey is not linear - but there are lots of amazing successes.

Kathy, I will refer you to the post I did here: [http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7150-My-first-labs](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7150-My-first-labs) Much of it applies to you.

Do the NMR test for your LDL particle number and particle size. You will get back from the lab total particle LDL number and a particle number for your small dense LDL. That will show as small LDL-P and then divide small dense LDL-P number by your total LDL-P particle number and that gives you your percentage.

Your DHEA should be pushed up to the upper limit. The Twist cream seems to work well for folks.

Triglycerides need to come down.

Vitamin D3 needs to be 70-120 with D3 supplement plus sun. One thing on the other thread I did not mention is Dr. K wants us to use more sunlight in the summer because of the longer days thus less supplementation.

Janz can help on the hormones.

HDL needs to go up. Slo-Niacin.

Epi-paleo diet combined with the LEPTIN rx will dramatically change your labs and how you feel. Drinking at least a gallon of non fluroridated water a day. will assist you in how you feel also. Try to get most of that done by 2pm so you go to bed with less potential to fill up your bladder during the night....causing you to wake up.
Your skin will become smooth and silky to the touch after a couple of weeks drinking that much non fluoridated water. Your desire for food will abate once you start using the water that gets inside your cells as a fuel source.

And you will be down to 140 lbs in less than six months provided you stay strict epi-paleo. Nothing in a box to be eaten. No grains or sugar ever. And no artificial sweeteners.

You have a roadmap given to you by Dr K. Determine your goals and objectives and let nothing stand in your way of achievement. You are totally responsible for your own condition.

KAthy you've got great advise from Dex & Audrey!

Your Free T3: rT3 ratio is 13.3 - you want that ABOVE 20 - when its above 20 it means your thyroid is converting T4 to T3 and your not making rT3 - which can bind to the same receptor sites in your cells - limiting their ability to function properly -

you state you're - premenopausal - at what point in your cycle did you have these labs taken - that will help us figure out what your hormone panel looks like. additionally next time you get labs I'd get the Estradiol(Sensitive) - and a full estrogen panel. In order to deterimine your Pg:E2 - we need the estrdiol

you're pregnenolone is low - which isn't surprising given your freeT3:rT3 ratio - you've got some pregnenolone steal going on. which is also impacting your vitamin D levels.

focus on eating Epi-paleo following the Leptin prescription format BAB - lunch 4hrs later - dinner 4hrs later & At least 4 hrs prior to going to bed. limit artificial light at night, and limit your exposures to EMF

and of course ask questions....

# Thread: Oldest person living today is 121 years old
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oldest-person-living-today-is-121-years-old.7159/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-05-26T02:09:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oldest-person-living-today-is-121-years-old.7159/post-86863>

[http://www.treehugger.com/green-food/worlds-oldest-person-found-thriving-in-the-amazon.html](http://www.treehugger.com/green-food/worlds-oldest-person-found-thriving-in-the-amazon.html)

Badger on this forum asked about living on the equator. Apparently for some it works well for longevity in the Amazon jungle.

"Pereira credits her long-life to an active, healthy lifestyle, in addition to a diet rich in locally grown meats, fruits, and vegetables gathered in the forests around her home -- free of the extra salt, sugar, and preservatives so commonly found in foods around the world. Her all-natural diet, along with frequent walks around town, has allowed Pereira to thrive while others, many years her junior, do not."

What I take from this is eat your foods in season, eat real food, eat foods grown locally, Her food is probably not GMOs and she probably had a grandmother that passed along good genes from the same type of lifestyle.

And notice...No gray hair!!!

### caroline — 2013-05-26T03:28:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oldest-person-living-today-is-121-years-old.7159/post-86867>

I want to be a tree hugger at 121 too! .... to think I used to make fun of tree huggers!

### Danco3636 — 2013-05-26T03:56:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oldest-person-living-today-is-121-years-old.7159/post-86869>

Very nice find.... some dots to connect there.....

### badger — 2013-06-17T01:39:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oldest-person-living-today-is-121-years-old.7159/post-88195>

Good find Dexter. There's a place in Ecuador called Vilcabamba where the elders claim to be oftentimes well over 100 years old. The Wikipedia page for Vilcabamba tells of how researchers eventually found that the self-proclaimed centenarians were exaggerating their ages, and " the older they grew, the greater their exaggerations became". The older my granddad gets the more he exaggerates things too.... When I was in Vilcabamba I asked an older man how he felt about his home changing and becoming such a hotbed for american and european new-agers and vegans looking for the eternal spring... He said he didn't mind one bit because now he gets to tell people he's really old and takes pictures with lots of sexy chicas! Haha

Looks like this woman from the Amazon is legit though.. And besides, anyway you slice it, the people here are able to do quite a lot of work well into the autumn of life.

In the towns and cities things have rapidly changed, as they have nearly all over the world. People and young kids especially seem to subsist on nothing but baked sweets, ice cream, candy, rice, potatoes, plantains, corn, some chicken and pork, and coca-cola ! Ecuador is particularly in need of some culinary re-claiming in terms of what the land can and will sustain. To think that the climate would support the multitude of vegetables, animals, spices, and fruits people make good use of in Southeast Asia... I mean, comparing Thai food to Ecuadorian food is enough to make one wonder, what happened here? Oh yeah, SE asia managed to avoid being completely trashed and traumatized by colonization... that's right. Getting off topic... or am I? Re-claiming our ancestral biology, and cultural traditions that support it... what this site is all about.

# Thread: Bluefish ceviche
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bluefish-ceviche.7160/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dali Dula — 2013-05-26T05:48:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bluefish-ceviche.7160/post-86876>

Ingredients: 1 ocean full of bluefish.
Balsamic vinegar or lemon juice
Salt and pepper
Prep: Drive to the south shore of Long Island with a surfcasting rod 2 hrs before sunset.
Wade into the water barefoot up to your waist (water temp 50f)
Catch as many bluefish as you can before sun sets (2)
Fillet bluefish immediately. cut into bite size pieces. Toss with Balsamic or lemon ,salt, and pepper.
Watch sunset while you sit on beach and eat fish.

I had this dish the other night and recommend it highly.

### Inger — 2013-05-26T07:42:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bluefish-ceviche.7160/post-86880>

Oh man.. can I come with you and eat? This sounds like heaven to me.. so fresh.. so simple. Now, this is food that makes me feel amazing!

### caroline — 2013-05-26T08:33:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bluefish-ceviche.7160/post-86883>

It does indeed sound like heaven .....appreciating the simple, wonderful things in life ...can't get any better if you ask me.....

### Shijin13 — 2013-05-26T16:31:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bluefish-ceviche.7160/post-86888>

Dali

I'm sharing this recipe w/DH he love bluefish and its really cheap at our local international market...

# Thread: Some research on meditation/mindfulness and telomeres
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-research-on-meditation-mindfulness-and-telomeres.7161/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-05-26T22:25:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-research-on-meditation-mindfulness-and-telomeres.7161/post-86895>

I just came across some intriguing information through one of Dave Asprey's blogs, phase2minds.com. [http://mindbrain.ucdavis.edu/labs/Saron/pdf/Jacobs et al 2010 Psychoneuroendocrinology.pdf](http://mindbrain.ucdavis.edu/labs/Saron/pdf/Jacobs%20et%20al%202010%20Psychoneuroendocrinology.pdf)

There are a lot of additional cites within this study that seem interesting as well, and when I come across something interesting like this I normally look into everything cited as well. I don't have the time for that today, unfortunately, need to finish up this module and complete a genetics exam then start applying to dozens of places for a new job so I can start buying more/higher quality fish, lol! From what I understand from **briefly** skimming this, is that stress, depression, psychological problems and trauma can affect telomeres.... BUT so can eustress and 'using' stress, so can meditation, mindfulness, and gratitude. This is really getting me excited to start meditating again, although I really enjoy it more from a spiritual and psychological point of view.

As indicated by one of my recent posts, I was looking for the "best epi-paleo bang for my buck" since I'm terribly low on cash right now - I think I meditation may have to be included in there. I still have anxiety problems as it is (much better than 15 months ago), and sometimes I'm not so confidant in the control of my thoughts - meditation should help. By the way, does anyone have any insights on some things I've experienced/have been experiencing? Sometimes when I lay down to sleep, typically only when I lay on my back, right as I start drifting off it literally feels as if I'm going to 'pop out of body' and have an out of body experience. I've read up on OBE's and astral projection, and I know it can be achieved, whether it is something biological, something happening on the quantum level, or spiritually, I don't know, but it unfortunately frightens me at night. I get vibrations and a sudden airy/light feeling, I feel as though I don't weigh a single pound and that my consciousness is literally going to pop out of my body. This started after I got back into lucid dreaming recently. I also experience some odd sensations such as these once and awhile when meditating or listening to binaural audio... any insight would be helpful, as would any meditation techniques offering more of a calming effect. Thanks! Hope you enjoy the study!

### Inger — 2013-05-27T09:11:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-research-on-meditation-mindfulness-and-telomeres.7161/post-86934>

BPC777,
IDK but I personally am careful with "weird" meditations.. I do not do them. I have seen where this stuff have lead my brother and others and that is not a place I want to go. I do other stuff, like I cut out my thoughts and just concentrate on listening.. to all the different sounds around me, 100%. Or I concentrate on scents.. only that. Or I just look.. at the sky.. at the green trees and flowers.. I just am there and think nothing, I just enjoy the present. Very close to earth, nothing esoteric. To me the reality is very important. I can take a cup.. touch it and look at its color.. and just say to myself, this is a cup. It is white. And I touch it and feel it and it feels so good. It is so real. I really need reality because of all the weird religiosity I was raised in.. it was making me nauseous. Maybe try to go outside, sit on the ground or stay with bare feet, and just suck in the nature.. its sounds.. and scents.. anything. Makes my mind so very relaxed! Singing is great too.. it helps mood issues a lot! I sing so much alone..lol I put in so much feeling and it is very healing and makes me happy!

Coffee makes me anxious if I have too much or am stressed... not good at all. More than one cup and I get to feel it...

# Thread: Losing my poison ivy allergy?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-my-poison-ivy-allergy.7163/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2013-05-27T02:59:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-my-poison-ivy-allergy.7163/post-86914>

The ivy was slowly creeping up my porch; I knew I had to deal with it. But I hadn't wanted to get any of my garden tools infected with the horrid sap. I was sitting, staring at it a week ago, and on impulse I reached over (carefully using my left hand) and plucked it. What courage! How decisive! Was I nutz?? I have to avoid gloves that may have touched poison ivy long ago, or risk hives. Forget deliberately touching it! But I have noticed, over the past year, a reduced allergy reaction to lots of my previous triggers -- foods, cats, pollen, etc etc etc.

After about 10 minutes I lost courage, and rinsed my hand with water. No soap, however. The next day, I was just fine. I couldn't believe it! That morning, I deliberately walked through a patch of young (short) poison ivy with just my flip flops on. And no washing, until that night. Ok, the next day I did have a watery pimple thingie, on the top of my left foot. And my right hand also had one, near the site of a bromine bump. But that's it!!!!! And I'm fine now, those two pimples are healed. No red swollen patches of skin, no unbearable itching. And I can tell poison ivy from Virginia Creeper or other plants in an instant.

This is amazing to me. I had no idea that one could become un-allergic to this scourge. Was it CT? Seafood? Water? Grounding? Thanks, Jack!

### Inger — 2013-05-27T08:09:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-my-poison-ivy-allergy.7163/post-86929>

How amazing Sally...!!! I am totally confident when the body heals we lose every single allergy! I think it is all the stuff together that makes a big difference..  this is such a wonderful road to travel, full of nice surprises  It just gets better and better!

Now when thinking about it.. I used to itch in the night from stinging nettles.. so it disrupted my sleep. I stepped on stinging nettles as I did not see them in the pasture a week or so ago.. I thought.. argh! now I lose my sleep this night for sure! But my feet didn't itch at all that night, I forgot about the whole thing! I am curious to see if I want itch at all this summer.. I always step on those bastards running around here barefeet. I do not mind the minor pain but the itching in the night was a nuisance. Maybe I am healing too like you!  And Sally.. my second bromide bump is fully healed by now too. But I have been careful not taking any Iodine.. and have also cut back on the seaweeds too. Just taking a little. Now I need to increase their use again me thinks.

### nonchalant — 2013-05-27T16:05:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/losing-my-poison-ivy-allergy.7163/post-86940>

Inger, my bromide bump is still with me, but I very seldom put iodine on my skin. I either don't think about it, or I'm going to be visiting people, and don't want strange dark marks on my skin. Vanity! I have put iodine on the bump a couple times, and it did reduce in size. That's it, I'll go put some on right now.

# Thread: ? on preg/dhea supplementation and sleep/cold intolerance
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-preg-dhea-supplementation-and-sleep-cold-intolerance.7164/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bennyha — 2013-05-27T17:16:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-preg-dhea-supplementation-and-sleep-cold-intolerance.7164/post-86944>

Hello all. I have another question I was hoping you fine members could help me with. I started taking preg and dhea on my own last week, and upped iodine to 50 mg. On my last saliva cortisol draw, I was around midrange with morning cortisol and midrange with dhea. Also have low body temps and pulse rates (as low as 95 degrees some days with 40 beats per minute heart rate measured last week). I am taking these hoping the preg to increase my morning cortisol.

I have been taking 150 mg preg and 50 mg dhea at 6 a.m and again at 10 a.m. for the past 5 days. I have also in this time upped the iodine to 50 mg total (25 mg @ breakfast and 25 mg @ lunch and taking required co-factors as well). I have noticed a few good things, such as less brain fog, a little more energy, and my temperatures in the morning are 98.6 and my pulse has been 66 beats per min the past 3 mornings. This is great as sometimes my pulse is as low as 40 bpm last week.

On the other side, I have been experiencing extreme sensitivity to coldness. My hands/feet/toes are ice cold all day long. They feel as if I have been submerged in ice for hours they are that cold to the touch. Also, my sleep had been good, sleeping from 10 pm to 5:30 a.m. each morning and still waking multiple times to pee. A few questions.

1\. Why do I have the sudden increase in extreme cold sensitivity to my hands/feet/nose? Is it just because my T3 is flatlined with a very high TSH (so basically "hypothyroidism" using standard definition)? Or is this another sign of adrenal fatigue?

2\. Why is my sleep returning to my adrenal fatigue days? I was getting up each day with the sun naturally (around 5:30 each morning since summer has arrived) but the past few days I have been getting up at 4:00 a.m. and feel wide awake and cannot get back to sleep for anything. Is the increase in preg and dhea causing adrenal fatigue like symptoms to me again?

It's weird, because my temperatures are getting up to 98.6, but I still feel extremely cold down to my core. Also, if I go outside for as little as 5 minutes in 50 degree temperature, I feel extremely cold for hours after coming back inside and my temp goes back down to 97.6 when I take my temp an hour later. It's like I can't adapt to anything even remotely cold. A few years ago, I could be very comfortable in shorts and t-shirt in temperature in the 50's.

Thanks for any help you may be able to give me.

### Dextery — 2013-05-27T21:46:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-preg-dhea-supplementation-and-sleep-cold-intolerance.7164/post-86960>

Your degree of coldness you feel and having a heart rate in the 40s and a high TSH indicate...as you suggest..hypothyroidism. The iodine may have perked up your thyroid the past three days to increase your heart rate.

The iodine supplementation may help you with your thyroid function.

But you need to get a thyroid panel done to include the following: Free T3, Free T4, reverse T3, & TSH. And you need to find a Doc that will understand the principles outlines in the following website:
Stopthethyroidmadness.com which will give you some guidelines as to what to look for. Docs that are open to the info there are few and far between as many have found out.

And you really need to read/re-read Brain Gut 12 [http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-12-dare-to-disagree/](http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-12-dare-to-disagree/)

Adrenal fatigue is a brain problem...not a adrenal gland problem.

And you really need to be drinking at least a gallon of non-fluoridated water a day.

### bennyha — 2013-05-28T02:18:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-preg-dhea-supplementation-and-sleep-cold-intolerance.7164/post-86981>

Thanks for your reply Dex. Yeah I have been following Epi-Paleo diet/Leptin Reset principles for over a year now. I have read and re-read all of the blogs many, many times. I don't currently do CT (but did in the past), and have even very low carb for a few years now. I developed a thyroid problem in the past 18 months, interesting enough when I started going low carb. I think Jack said in a previous post that if you aren't cold adapted (which I am defintely not) and you eat low carb, you run into problems.

I have gotten all those test you recommended above.

Free T4 (blood spot)...............1.3.......................0.7-2.5 ng/dL
Free T3 (blood spot)...............2.5.......................2.5-6.5 pg/ml
TSH (blood spot)....................3.5...................... .0.5-3.0 uU/ml
TPO (blood spot)....................25....................... .0-150 Iu/ml

Other labs I had done at Lab Corp in February. I don't have the exact results, but I did a full iron and ferritin lab and they all came back optimal. I did an HS-CRP, and it came back 0.4.
I also did another Free T3, and this time it came back lower and out of range low again, at 2.2.
I also took a Reverse T3 test, and although it came back in range, my free t3: rev t3 ratio (the one where its "suppose" to be above 20) came back at 11.7.

I don't have the funds nor the trust in a conventional Dr. I tried one in the past and it was a horrible waste of money. I started taking Armour once, but felt like shit and quit after a month. That was almost a year ago. I now know my RT3 problem was causing the T4 to turn into RT3, making me feel like shit and with a puffy face on Armour.

I feel like my adrenal fatigue problems has gotten better since those tests almost a year ago for cortisol. I worked with a holistic doctor for a few months and he said I was filled with metals and microorganisms. I worked over a 5 month period to rid of them, so I'm hoping that could bring down my RT3 as well.

I guess I'm basically wondering if I should try natural thyroid again and see if that helps me this time around.

Whats the prevailing thought around here on thyroid medication? Is it a long term need, or a temporary patch on my way to recovery that can help me warm up and start the hormone pathways?

### bennyha — 2013-05-28T02:20:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-preg-dhea-supplementation-and-sleep-cold-intolerance.7164/post-86982>

Also, I drink around 80 oz of spring water a day, and I think I urinate 20x or more a day, so I don't know how much good that it's even doing for me as I'm obviously peeing it right back out

### Huck — 2013-05-28T02:44:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-preg-dhea-supplementation-and-sleep-cold-intolerance.7164/post-86984>

The one thing that seems to help me the most is getting lots of sun. I started the epi-paleo diet about 7 months before good sun (above 50 degrees), and I think that was key. The sun never affected me this way before I went on epi-paleo.

First, make sure your vitamin d is not tanked. When mine was real low, nothing else I did seemed to matter. Once I got it up to 50-60 with supplementation, I found that I could spend hours in the sun and not burn, just tan. I spent 6 hours in the sun today doing hard work (digging post holes) and feel the best I have felt in weeks. Grounding and at least 2 hours a day in the sun seems to do it for me. Gardening is great because you ground when you pull weeds and get sun at the same time.

So you might try an experiment. Try getting at least 2 hours of sun a day for 30 or 60 days and then retest. Out of all the things I have tried, photons help me the most.

### JanSz — 2013-05-28T03:54:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-preg-dhea-supplementation-and-sleep-cold-intolerance.7164/post-86994>

I use Poland Spring water for cooking and Pellegrino for drinking.

There is something different in Pellegrino. Very satisfying.
I can drink half bottle or more an hour before bed time and it does not make me pee more often.

Exposure to cold is known to increase peeing frequency, but that does not last.
Latter it reduces peeing.

The other thing cold does, it makes you feel cold when in the water, but afterward thyroid must kicks in, I feel like furnace.
Think of more frequent but shorter sessions in cold water.

................

If so, possibly you have a bladder infection?

That is not pretty, I had it after Green Laser Prostate surgery. Can't get out of the house for a walk.
Forget about holding any job.

..

# Thread: Surgery help!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/surgery-help.7169/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### juliebgood — 2013-05-27T21:49:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/surgery-help.7169/post-86961>

Hi, folks! I'm a long-time off-and-on lurker, occasional commenter, so you probably wouldn't know me, but I'm hoping people will help me out regardless, as this is the best place I can think of to come for help. As an absolute last resort, I'm having my first major surgery in a month, breast reduction surgery, and I'm really freaked out about taking antibiotics and healing well after surgery for optimal results, especially the healing well part.

If anybody has suggestions for things I should be doing pre and post-surgery to minimize the damage from antibiotics, prevent scarring, and generally heal well, I'd be eternally grateful.

Any help would be appreciated - thanks in advance.

PS: The thing is, if somebody were asking \*me\* this question, I bet I'd be able to rattle off a few obvious suggestions, but when you yourself are in a critical situation, the emotional turmoil can short-circuit your ability to think  And besides, the internet is way cleverer than I, so I'd be foolish not to ask!

### Dextery — 2013-05-27T21:59:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/surgery-help.7169/post-86962>

First be sure your serum Vit D3 level is pushing 100ng/ml For elective surgery, Dr. K always pumps up his surgery patients prior to the table. You can take 50000IU daily til surgery and this will pump up your immune system so that your need for antibiotics will be minimal.

Second, Get cold packs, ice packs and ice your breasts as much as you can possibly do for as long as you can do prior to surgery. Hopefully you will have a month to do this prior. The outcomes of surgery are always better...short recovery time in hospital and much quicker healing. Dr K always has his patients ice the area the incisions are to be made prior to surgery.

And do as much cold thermogenesis on your entire body every day as much as you can prior to surgery to prepare your body for the insult that you are undergoing.

### juliebgood — 2013-05-27T22:11:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/surgery-help.7169/post-86965>

Thanks, Dextery! I'm supplementing D3 now and soaking up the sun, also CT-ing a while every other day or so, but it sounds like I should really ramp up both.

Do you know what it is about spot CT-ing the incision area that makes it heal better? I can understand doing that \*after\* surgery to aid healing, but why you would do it for days and weeks beforehand confuses me.

### Dextery — 2013-05-27T22:26:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/surgery-help.7169/post-86966>

Nope. Can't tell you why pre surgery spot CT works. I only know that JK does it with great outcomes for his patients. And yes, do ramp up the CT daily...perhaps 2x daily and get your D3 level up NOW.

Post surgery CT is also fabulous also for healing. You will amaze your surgeon how well you do post op.

Here is the answer to pre-op spot CT. [http://www.jackkruse.com/peri-operative-optimal-surgical-considerations/](http://www.jackkruse.com/peri-operative-optimal-surgical-considerations/)

### Adria — 2013-05-28T22:46:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/surgery-help.7169/post-87026>

Use ice every day before, concentrating where the surgeon will cut. Do this as many times per day as possible. Eat strict epi-paleo. Get sunlight and ground yourself whenever possible. When you wake in the recovery ask for ice bag. I had brain surgery and they didn't even have an ice bag available in ICU but the nurse made one makeshift. Keep icing the incisions every day afterwards. After the wounds were completely closed I used coconut oil on the incision to help minimize scars.

Take care and hope all goes wonderful for you! I have a friend who had the breast reduction surgery and she is extremely happy with the results.

### juliebgood — 2013-05-29T03:52:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/surgery-help.7169/post-87050>

Dextry, you are a god. Thank you so much for that link. I had no idea Jack had done such a post; he's been so prolific it's hard keeping up with the man! That post should be invaluable. Thanks so much again!

### juliebgood — 2013-05-29T03:55:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/surgery-help.7169/post-87051>

Thanks so much, Adria! They all sound like great suggestions. I really need to start with the ice packs, and also generally ramp up the rest of what I'm doing. That's pretty neat that you were spot CT-ing after brain surgery!

# Thread: Still Not Sure If I'm Leptin Resistant
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-not-sure-if-im-leptin-resistant.7172/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-05-28T06:00:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-not-sure-if-im-leptin-resistant.7172/post-86999>

**MODS PLEASE DELETE - POSTED IN THE WRONG AREA**

Was just reading Jack's epic blog post on leptin resistance:
[http://www.jackkruse.com/the-leptin-rx-faqs/](http://www.jackkruse.com/the-leptin-rx-faqs/)

Just reading the section on determining if you're leptin resistant where he says:

> How do I determine if I am leptin resistant? Remember, you can be LR (leptin resistant) if youâ€™re fat or skinny. If youâ€™re overweight by more than 30lbs, it is a lock you have some degree of LR. If youâ€™re underweight by 20 lbs, you are likely LR, too. If you had an eating disorder, youâ€™re likely suffering from a serious leptin issue.
>
> The easiest test is to look in the mirror. The mirror does not lie and it is really cheap. For those people who still canâ€™t be sure after peeking in the mirror, you can order some blood tests. My favorite is the HS CRP (highly sensitive C-Reactive protein) and the reverse T3 tests (but there are others). They are accurate in over 90% of cases.
>
> My next favorite test is the salivary cortisol test, along with at least two of the following: HDL level, serum ferritin, homocysteine, fasting triglycerides, fasting insulin, or post-prandial glucose readings done every 15 minutes for one hour after eating. (Read my Hormone 101 blog for details.)

He doesn't really give details on what to look for when ordering tests.

I'm not overweight - Male, 190 around 15% bf

I've had some of those tests and they come back normal. My HDL is 87, my fasting triglycerides are 75 and fasting insulin is 87.

I'm a firefighter so this may account for some of my issues (mainly sleep).

For the last 9 months my sleep has been terrible with waking multiple times per night, sometimes hard to go back to sleep but usually not.

I do crossfit about 5 times per week along with a paleo diet.

In addition to poor sleep, it seems like I'm always hungry (sometimes I give in sometimes I don't but I pretty don't eat processed sugar) and I have been having odd muscle pains (but I think that might be because of the poor sleep).

My energy has been terrible since the poor sleep but it was never great even before my sleep tanked even with eating strict paleo.

So what else can I do to figure out if I'm leptin resistant or should I just go for the reset considering the info I have provided.

If I do go for the reset there is one main problem that I may have a hard time following: No exercise.

At the fire station we sometimes go about and play football for roughly 3 hrs/week total. Not tackling but quite a bit of sprinting.

I would really lose face if I sat out.

**Thanks for any input you have.**

John

# Thread: A few questions before I (re)Start

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1

### fireforlunch — 2013-05-28T17:18:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-questions-before-i-re-start.7175/post-87014>

I guess I should have mentioned this in my first thread but totally forgot.

I mentioned in my other thread that my sleep really went to crap about 9 months ago.

What I forgot to mention is that I tried the Leptin Rx 9 months ago and did it for roughly 3 weeks.

That's when my sleep tanked and the only thing I could figure was that it had something to do with the low carb and or high fat of the Rx.

I finally gave up and noticed some relief but it has never been the same since.

Basically when I gave up because I would wake up in the middle of the night and have a really hard time going back to sleep, sometimes I wouldn't be able to go back to sleep at at all after 3 or 4 hours of sleep.

Since I quit the Reset I now wake up several times during the night but usually don't have much trouble going back to sleep.

I'm concerned that if I try this again that the sleep will get worse again.

I should also mention that I've tried nearly everything for sleep - melatonin more than anything with no real improvements.

I just ordered 2 supplements from Amazon that Greg Everett (buddy of Robb Wolf) said has helped him:
Holy Basil and Gaba but maybe I should send those back.

One more question:

If I give this another go is regular cured bacon okay or does it need to be uncured?

I ask because sugar is in the ingredients list of the cured but it's not enough be even a gram per serving.

At this time I just can't afford the price of uncured bacon.

Thank you so much for your input.

John

# Thread: For Dextery...a question about Slo-Niacin please
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-dextery-a-question-about-slo-niacin-please.7178/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 23
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jude — 2013-05-29T02:09:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-dextery-a-question-about-slo-niacin-please.7178/post-87042>

Dextery, you mentioned in a post about the use of Slo Niacin to raise HDL. Just wondering if it really does work and if you've had any success with it? My HDL is rising oh so slowly..... thanks Jude.

### Dextery — 2013-05-29T05:35:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-dextery-a-question-about-slo-niacin-please.7178/post-87056>

Slo Niacin which is B-3 will raise HDL. I take 2000mg/day 1000 am/1000mg pm. Over two years my HDL has gone from 45 to 95ng/dl. But at the same time I started pretty strict paleo with few carbs. Result was the rise in HDL, but also I had a corresponding rise in LDL calculated and a rise in Total cholesterol...which I don't care about. I also saw my smdense LDL particles go from about 70% to 10% small dense and Triglycerides went down from over 100 to 30.

So the paleo diet and now epi-paleo had drastically changed my lipids for the better and I know the niacin has been helpful. Way back a long time ago, I read about Slo Niacin raising HDL in normal paleo people. I think I read about Niacin in the blog posting of Dr Grace Cathome at [http://www.drbganimalpharm.blogspot.com/search/label/Niacin](http://www.drbganimalpharm.blogspot.com/search/label/Niacin) This is a search for niacin on her blog. Everything you ever wanted to know about niacin and lipids.

Amazon.com has the best deal if you buy two for freight prepaid: [http://www.amazon.com/Slo-Niacin-Co...TF8&qid=1369793375&sr=8-1&keywords=slo+niacin](http://www.amazon.com/Slo-Niacin-Controlled-Release-Niacin-Tablets/dp/B000H208B2/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1369793375&sr=8-1&keywords=slo+niacin)

Dr K in Quantum Biology 4 said this about Niacin:

> Niacin: This is also why niacin works in these diseases because it is a ketone mimic drug. Why? Niacin also known as vitamin B3, nicotinic acid and vitamin PP, is an organic compound with the formula C6H5NO2 and one of the 40 to 80 essential human nutrients. In fact, when niacin deficiency is present it is one of 5 vitamins that causes a pandemic disease condition called pellagra. But it can be used to bypass a “broken” cytochrome 1 when mis-folding (due to a chronic ATP deficiency, think EMF 7). Why is understanding this hardcore biochemistry important? It underpins why understanding precision that QED requires is paramount in semiconduction. Without the proper protein folding you lose quantum tunneling of electrons and you lose the ability slowly to remain an omnivore. This is where QED takes us……….Vitamin B3 can help activate PPP when we are broken for some reason when we are ketotic from a dietary stand point using vast amounts of coherent water as I laid out in the quantum electron blog post. The PPP is responsible for making the chemical rings found in all human hormones. They become unstable as well when intracellular water is lost because cholesterol is a polar molecule as well and does well in a lipid and water environment to act. Its action at receptor sites is critically important in how the hormones work in the nucleus to turn on and off our epigenome.
>
> Yes people, niacin is a precursor to NAD+/NADH (cytochrome 1) and NADP+/NADPH (the magic of the PPP), which play essential metabolic roles in all living cells as I showed in EMF 4. Niacin cannot be directly converted to nicotinamide, but both compounds could be converted to NAD and NADP in vivo and this information is very valuable when you have liver leptin resistance, metabolic syndrome, or epilepsy. It also is important for those with liver viral diseases like hepatitis B, C, D, and E. These patients also tend to to well on niacin and a high fat diet for these reasons

### Dextery — 2013-05-30T03:41:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-dextery-a-question-about-slo-niacin-please.7178/post-87117>

The amount of each of these is so small, I will take the benefits that have been dramatic for me and not worry about the other stuff. hypomellose is some kind of sugar also and I don't eat any white sugar and only sugar in the occasional piece of fruit.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-05-30T04:39:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-dextery-a-question-about-slo-niacin-please.7178/post-87121>

Dextery, since B3 is a ketone mimetic compound, can it actually spark the production of ketones? I know there are ketogenic amino acids (leucine/lysine I believe), I was wondering if there were any other products/compounds that enhance ketogenesis and can create a deeper state of ketosis/increase carb allowance.

### Dextery — 2013-05-30T06:08:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-dextery-a-question-about-slo-niacin-please.7178/post-87123>

I do not know of any other compound that brings about ketosis...... other than coconut oil, butter, MCT oil, and olive oil eaten in the absence of carbs. I am not sure there is any less deep or more deep ketosis. You are either in or out....meaning you are burning either ketone bodies or glucose to fuel the brain and body.

I don't really get the need for carbs in working out. Seafood, meat and fat will fuel the body and brain quite well. I know the gurus stress carb loading, carb matching to activity for quick fuel but it spikes blood sugar and thus insulin. YoYoing BS and insulin can lead to Cardiac Arrest and/or Cancer. That is the last thing I want to do. The pancreas is overworked by SAD and I want to have as many Beta cells available right up to my last days. And that to me means, maintain a higher than mainstream thought on total cholesterol, smD LDL at about 10% of total LDL particles, low triglycerides and very high HDL with a hsCRP under .5 and good sleep, and a pretty strict ketogenic diet. If I can keep these numbers, I figure I might be around for a few more years.

People who think they need carbs look really buff on the outside, but look inside at the scarring on and in the heart. No thanks.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-05-30T07:13:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-dextery-a-question-about-slo-niacin-please.7178/post-87125>

Thanks for the input! I didn't realize olive oil would have an impact as well, I had a hunch butter did, though, since it contains butyric acid. What I mean by deeper ketosis is I believe you technically need a reading of 0.5millimols of ketones to be "in ketosis", but you can get much higher as many people get up to 4.0 from fasting. Ben Greenfield in his current keto-experiment is aiming for 1.0-1.5. I don't believe carbs are necessary whatsoever, but I don't know how to implement a fully strict keto diet right now and build muscle - I need to gain weight and I'm shooting for like 15-20lbs. So far I've been cycling in and out of ketosis, refraining from carb loads for a minimum of 5ish days and only after I workout. The reason I stated that I was wondering about a higher carb allowance was because I was thinking of doing "mini carb loads" after lifting weights, but not enough to keep me out of ketosis, especially if I take other measures (mct's/sct's/keto amino's/fasting/etc.). I was also thinking how that could play with excess protein. For instance, I consume an **Ultra-High Fat**, VLC, moderate protein diet and on days I lift/martial arts intensely, I "refeed" on extra protein (about 1.3g/lb, from what I've seen that's the max you can utilize), and although there is a possibility of gluconeogenesis, the mct's/sct's/amino's keep me fueled with ketones.

I too am opting for recovery, longevity, and well-being. I would rather have a decent but strong looking body that is functionally strong and efficient (movement patterns/breathing), be able to concentrate like no other, have stable energy, etc.etc.etc. than be 6% body fat and..... nothing else, lol.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-05-30T07:14:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-dextery-a-question-about-slo-niacin-please.7178/post-87126>

That being said, I don't want the weight I want/need to gain to be fat, I'd rather have it be mostly muscle and have a solid 165-175 frame with 12-14% body fat.... that's entirely acceptable.

### Dextery — 2013-05-30T16:59:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-dextery-a-question-about-slo-niacin-please.7178/post-87135>

### Dextery — 2013-05-30T18:34:04+0300

### Dextery — 2013-06-05T18:03:26+0300

Remember there are different tastes among the different coconut brands. Some I did not care for but it has been so long ago, I do not remember which were somewhat distasteful. I get the Vitacost.com private label 54oz for $21.69. I suspect Vitacost has Nutiva private label for them as the taste to me is identical.

Just cut the 500mg Slo Niacin in half and use it. I do not know what Dr K would say about why your hubby flushes or why anyone flushes.

The good news is the flushing abates after the body becomes accustomed to the niacin.

Just received labs for SO. 50 yr old male. 5'2". 155lbs. Used to do alot of weightlifting.
Very nervous because of the Lipoprotein Profile, NMR results. Doc wants to start meds.

LDL Particle Number by NMR 2397 range <1000 nmol/L

HDL Particle Number 28.7 >=30.5 umol/L

Small LDL Particle Number 1285 <=527 nmol/L

LDL Particle Size 20.8 >20.5 nm

Large VLDL Particle Number 3.7 <=2.7 nmol/L

Large HDL Particle Number <0.7 >=4.8 umol/L

VLDL Size 48.2 <=46.6 nm

HDL Size <8.3 >=9.2 nm

LP Insulin Resistance Score 71 <=45

Glucose 99 70-90 mg/dL

Testosterone 358 300-890 ng/dL

SHBG 19 11-80 nmol/L

Testosterone, Bioavailable 236 131-682 ng/dL

Testosterone, Free Calculation 83 47-244 pg/mL

Testosterone, Percentage Free 2.3 1.6-2.9 %

HS CRP 0.8 0.0-3.0 mg/L

Vit D3, 25-Hydroxy 46 ng/mL
Vit D2, 25-Hydroxy <5 ng/mL
Vit D, 25-Hydroxy 46 ng/mL

Cholesterol 222 0-199 mg/dL
Triglyceride 141 0-149 mg/dL
HDL Cholesterol 35 40-100 mg/dL
Non-HDL Cholesterol 187 0-130 mg/dL
LDL, Calculated 159 0-129 mg/dL
Cholesterol/HDL Ratio 6.3 1.0-4.5

Would like to know about options for raising Testosterone/how to lower LDL small particle/
raise HDL.
Thanks for all of your expertise.

# Thread: Dr John Cannell, Vitamin D Council, speaks on Vit D3 and Autism
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-john-cannell-vitamin-d-council-speaks-on-vit-d3-and-autism.7187/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-05-29T18:56:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-john-cannell-vitamin-d-council-speaks-on-vit-d3-and-autism.7187/post-87085>

[http://www.vitamindcouncil.org/blog/grassrootshealth-webinar-series-vitamin-d-and-autism/](http://www.vitamindcouncil.org/blog/grassrootshealth-webinar-series-vitamin-d-and-autism/)

This is a lecture done in conjunction with [www.grassrootshealth.net](http://www.grassrootshealth.net) ......a place you can get your Vitamin D3 level checked without a doctor's order.

Autism: Very interesting that Dr Cannell points out that there is a correlation between date of birth, urban or rural living. Children born in March seem to have higher incidence of autism. Urban children have more incidence as do children of dark skin people who have migrated to more northern climates...less sun.

If the environment was the cause of autism, we would have seen the outbreak would have occurred in the 1950s when many of us could see the air we were breathing...especially smog in the Los Angeles basin and many of the lakes and streams were dieing and the ocean was very suspect due to effluent run off.

He talks about prenatal supplementation and post natal breast feeding. Breast milk does not contain much D3 unless the mother was supplementing. He does talk about specific doses.

There is much, much more.

# Thread: 9 Food Practices banned in Europe that are not banned in the US
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/9-food-practices-banned-in-europe-that-are-not-banned-in-the-us.7189/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-05-29T19:24:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/9-food-practices-banned-in-europe-that-are-not-banned-in-the-us.7189/post-87088>

[http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2013/05/29/dangerous-food-practices.aspx](http://articles.mercola.com/sites/articles/archive/2013/05/29/dangerous-food-practices.aspx)

The one practice that really jumped out at me is the following #8

8\. Water Fluoridation

"Many do not realize that fluoride is a drug that is available only with a prescription. Yet it's added to municipal water supplies used by more than 180 million Americans, including infants and the elderly without any attention to personalized dosing or potential interactions.

Swallowing fluoride has been shown to cause weakened bones, bone cancer, hyperactivity and/or lethargy, lowered thyroid function, lowered IQ, dementia, kidney issue, arthritis and more, while studies have failed to show benefits for preventing cavities when taken internally. Cities around the US spend millions adding fluoride to communal water supplies each year, yet most European countries do not fluoridate their water."

If you have not switched to non fluoridated spring water or distilled water...what are you waiting for?
