# Thread: Direct contamination of foods with fluoride based pesticides and fumigants
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/direct-contamination-of-foods-with-fluoride-based-pesticides-and-fumigants.7191/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-05-29T23:24:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/direct-contamination-of-foods-with-fluoride-based-pesticides-and-fumigants.7191/post-87094>

[http://www.antigmofoods.com/2013/05/common-food-items-could-contain-180.html?m=1](http://www.antigmofoods.com/2013/05/common-food-items-could-contain-180.html?m=1)

Just when I thought I had eliminated fluoride from my life by drinking non fluoridated water, I now find out fumigants and pesticides contain compounds of fluoride. There was a local story that a producer of vegetables was marketing it as "organic" and the produce contained all sorts of residues of pesticides.

When something is labeled "organic" how do we the consumers know? I suppose the reputation of Publix, Safeway, Albertson's, Piggly Wiggly, Vons, Sprouts, WinCo, etc comes into play. Do you trust them? Do you trust the agri-business produce farmers to be ethical? All the more reason to know your farmer....if you can.

### nonchalant — 2013-05-29T23:52:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/direct-contamination-of-foods-with-fluoride-based-pesticides-and-fumigants.7191/post-87099>

Also, these veggies get sprayed with tap water regularly as they sit in the store display cases. More fluoride & chloride.

### nunki — 2013-05-30T14:10:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/direct-contamination-of-foods-with-fluoride-based-pesticides-and-fumigants.7191/post-87128>

Non chalant- not true for WF's. All water used in my store is processed through (I believe) a reverse osmosis system. It is definitely not straight tap water. Make sure what you are buying is certified organic- not just saying they are organic. It is a huge process for a farm to become certified organic. Not saying that dishonest ones slip by, but it is your responsibility as a consumer to do your research. i would never buy from a farm that doesnt welcome tours.

of course, I am a huge proponent of growing as much of your own food as possible.

### nonchalant — 2013-05-30T15:58:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/direct-contamination-of-foods-with-fluoride-based-pesticides-and-fumigants.7191/post-87129>

Thank, nunki for the clarification. I should have known that Whole Foods would treat their veggies special.
My rabbits like kale and other greens and carrots from the grocery store, but they go nuts when I give them something straight from my garden, like worm-eaten cabbage leaves or beet greens. Or weeds/branches from the yard. I think it might be that the extra electrons haven't dissipated yet.

### judy32 — 2013-07-03T08:10:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/direct-contamination-of-foods-with-fluoride-based-pesticides-and-fumigants.7191/post-89429>

Also, these fresh vegetables get applied with tap the water consistently as they sit in the shop showcases. More fluoride & chloride.

# Thread: Bloating dependent purely on volume of food/drink
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-dependent-purely-on-volume-of-food-drink.7229/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2013-06-03T14:32:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-dependent-purely-on-volume-of-food-drink.7229/post-87368>

Hi guys,

I have had this problem for a year and a half now. I get a distended abdomen every time I eat or drink anything. That contents of the food/drink don't matter it is purely volume. I can say this as I have tried various elimination diets, etc and the bloating happen when just drinking any type of water.

Any idea what could cause my abdomen to extend so much from small amounts of food? Any medical condition? Any tips?

The distension is specifically in the middle left of the abdomen. I also have various other problems that I think are caused by this such as really bad lethargy/fatigue, dizziness, etc. I have been to the docs and had all kinds of tests but they were no help.

Any help appreciated guys.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-dependent-purely-on-volume-of-food-drink.7230/post-87380>

I have something similar at times. My hypothesis is the CT seems to do phase one liver detox but to get phase two to kick in, I have to do coffee enemas. Also, keiffer, kombucha, Effective Microorganisms, and cultured vegies. However, these very things also cause phase one detox. And the coffee enemas seem to be the only thing to improve--move it on out. Okay, I may have something totally different, but it sounds very similar to me. In fact, last winter when I lived in a tipi with no heat, I had a real bad case of this problem. It was TOO MUCH phase one from constantly being in the cold. We are all treading on thin ice (pardon the pun) with all these new paleo hack things--so the answers aren't always out there. This is the closest I have come to. All I know is the coffee enemas work and they work awesomely, improving digestion and all those toxic sypmtoms. I think coffee enemas are excellent combined with CT. seanb4

There are things you can do to convert the bacteria in your digestive track to good bacteria. There are products that go into the digestive tract called polysaccarides that will ferment and will assist your gut to change its make up to good bacteria. Examples of these are potato starch, burdock root, artichokes, chicory, pectin & jicama (jicama is delicious cold with lemon juice and salt...available most grocery stores. These are very fibrous and ferment in the gut. In the beginning, you will have tremendous amounts of odoriferous gas from the fermentation.

I would send you to Dr Art Ayers website [http://coolinginflammation.blogspot.com/](http://coolinginflammation.blogspot.com/) for healing gut flora. He is not posting a lot anymore but his information is very good.

Another site to have a look at [http://digestivehealthinstitute.org/](http://digestivehealthinstitute.org/) Norm Robillard has written a lot on digestive matters and how to heal the gut.

Have you ever had a stool test to ACTUALLY KNOW what is going on in the gut??? You can ask this question out here til you are blue in the face, but until you test you will never really know. People can give you advice, but that is all it is - advice based on "their" experiences. You have to test and if you have a functional med doc to work with on treating, all the better. I have had parasites 2x now, h-pylori, unknown yeast, disbyosis, vibrio (cholera family), etc. Do you think I would have ever known this had I not tested? Some of these were serious and had to be treated via antibiotics. My gut is trashed and until I get some of theses under control, it will remain that way. You have to know, then treat, then treat some more and in the process start feeding the good bacteria. Poor gut health is poor health period!

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-06T20:35:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-dependent-purely-on-volume-of-food-drink.7230/post-87645>

I completely agree with vkiernan... you NEED to get tests run. I remember a podcast with Dave Asprey and the founder of Ubiome (I believe) and he spoke about how he didn't even know until he started checking that he had H.Pylori or something similar to that, and that once he got it under control he felt drastically better and would have never imagined that as "bulletproof" as he was, he had "performance robbing gut pathogens" growing inside of him. You really can't be certain of what's going on without getting it checked out. Just search up some Functional Medicine Practitioners, Alternative Care Practitioners, or maybe even a Naturopath. You don't have to agree with all their treatments, just get some tests run and you can easily come back here and people will help you sort it out.

Also, in terms of probiotics, just because you took some probiotics does not mean that you don't have poor gut bacteria or that they won't help. It entirely depends on the strains of bacteria. Dave Asprey has some good thoughts on this, most "mainstream" probiotic strains aren't really helpful, and just like our genes, we all have extremely unique gut biomes... you have to get very specific strains and from the top sources - that's normally expensive and hard to find.

### seanb4 — 2013-06-06T21:12:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bloating-dependent-purely-on-volume-of-food-drink.7230/post-87649>

@vkiernan and bigPapaChakra777

Yea I will get some tests done as soon as I can get the money but that could be years away yet, I am dead broke. I have an awful feeling I will spend the money and it will come back "fine" as have various other tests.

### vkiernan — 2013-06-07T01:45:04+0300

If you are having that much bloat, I doubt it will come back fine. The test I have been using is a stool test by Genova Diagnostics/Metametrix. They have reduced the price thru your practitioner. I'm thinking the initial full test is now about $200 and the repeats are $99. This is an awesome test that will tell you things you never imagined. Just make sure you aren't taking things like ox bile at that time (quit taking it a few days ahead) that could inhibit the real results. Ask your practitioner about what not to be doing. I sure hope you are able to do this test. I know what a trashed gut feels like and it is no fun! On the bright side, I would say I am making progress and the dollars spent have not been wasted.

# Thread: EMF's of Kindle Fire vs Ipad
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-of-kindle-fire-vs-ipad.7232/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Meg — 2013-06-03T21:05:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-of-kindle-fire-vs-ipad.7232/post-87397>

Please help I am so ignorant here- technology is not my strong suit and I do not have a device to test EMF's as of yet.

I need a traveling handy device (think small) that can skype and get email and I can read off of. I have heard some folks say the Ipad is a bad EMF'er, has anyone tested the Kindles to see what they are like? Do you have any other suggestions for technology used for travel and how to minimize EMF's?

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-06-04T15:04:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-of-kindle-fire-vs-ipad.7232/post-87439>

> [Meg said:](/goto/post?id=87397)
>
> Please help I am so ignorant here- technology is not my strong suit and I do not have a device to test EMF's as of yet.
>
> I need a traveling handy device (think small) that can skype and get email and I can read off of. I have heard some folks say the Ipad is a bad EMF'er, has anyone tested the Kindles to see what they are like? Do you have any other suggestions for technology used for travel and how to minimize EMF's?

Both iPad and Kindle Fire are very similar and equally bad IMHO re: EMF... I sold both of mine and went back to using a second generation Kindle, which gives off less EMF and only when you turn a page. Unfortunately, you can't Skype or email with this device...reading only.

### kathylu — 2013-06-04T15:42:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-of-kindle-fire-vs-ipad.7232/post-87443>

Interesting. I used my Trifield meter to measure my sister's Kindle Fire recently. There was basically no detectable field (slight blip above zero volts, no RF and no magnetic field. Just my observation.

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-06-04T17:51:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-of-kindle-fire-vs-ipad.7232/post-87456>

One other important thing (for me) that I forgot to mention... both the iPad and Kindle Fire require WiFi to effectively operate. Since I ditched the WiFi at home, it didn't make much sense to continue to own these devices. The amount of EMF is a matter of degree...both units do yield a measurable reading on a sensitive meter (such as a 8GHz meter...the Trifield is not as sensitive in this range), and I chose to have as little as possible. On the other hand, most certainly these devices are less 'smoggy' than a cell phone. As they say, you pays your money and you takes your chance. ;-)

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-06-04T18:19:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-of-kindle-fire-vs-ipad.7232/post-87459>

Back to the future ... the best bet for traveling is probably a laptop. In the EMF bootcamp, we learned that Apple products seem to have considerably more EMF than non-Apple laptops.

When using the laptop, have a fully charged battery so you can use it with the power cord unplugged.

Make sure the wi-fi is disabled, and use an ethernet cable to connect to the internet.

Finally, follow the info on this video to ground your laptop. [http://jackkruse.com/watch/grounding-your-computer/](http://jackkruse.com/watch/grounding-your-computer/)

### OysterRx — 2015-03-23T05:21:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emfs-of-kindle-fire-vs-ipad.7232/post-159254>

zhoooom.. time warp forward 2 years!

what about the aspects re: the light spectrum? Do kindle screens shine blue light in the same capacity as an iPad/iPhone? My bodily experience tells me no. anyone else?

I have recently had labs done and have noticed improvement in some areas and deterioration in others......I could use some help figuring it out.

Jan 2013 / May 2013 / Ref Range
Progesterone 24 / 41.9 / 5.3-86.0 nmol/L (luteal)
Estradiol 139 / 381 / 161-774 nmol/L (luteal)
DHEAS 3.2 / 8 / 2-10 umol/L
FBG 5.1 / 6.3 / 3.6-5.5 mmol/L
B12 460 / 817 / 156-698 pmol/L
HSCRP <0.3 / <0.3 / mg/L
25 OH Vit D 122 / 184 / nmol/L
TSH 2.08 / 1.39 / 0.3-5.50 mu/L
Fasting Insulin 46 / 68 / <95 pmol/L
Testosterone 2.0 / 0.7 / 0.5-2.6 nmol/L
SHBG 81.3 / 56 / 18-114 nmol/L
Calc BioA Test 0.5 / 0.2 / <1.0 nmol/L

I am taking progesterone (orally and vaginally), DHEA (orally) and pregnenalone. My vitamin d, DHEA, TSH and b12 have all improved, but my blood glucose, fasting insulin and testosterone are worse. Can anyone offer me any insights? BTW, these are Canadian tests so values might be different.

Current E2 conversion 381 = 103.786

Initial Pg:E2 ratio = 172.67
Current Pg:E2 ratio is 128.86

# Thread: Antibody wakes up T-cells to make cancer vanish
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antibody-wakes-up-t-cells-to-make-cancer-vanish.7241/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-06-04T21:04:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antibody-wakes-up-t-cells-to-make-cancer-vanish.7241/post-87465>

[http://www.newscientist.com/article/dn23646-antibody-wakes-up-tcells-to-make-cancer-vanish.html](http://www.newscientist.com/article/dn23646-antibody-wakes-up-tcells-to-make-cancer-vanish.html)

"Despite evolving throughout a person's life to control and destroy life-threatening intruders, the immune system is regularly outfoxed by cancer. This is often because tumour cells find ways to camouflage themselves from the immune system.

"Results presented this week at the annual meeting of the American Society of Clinical Oncology in Chicago show how three antibodies can blow cancer's cover.

"Cancer cells should normally be spotted by T-cells â€“ immune cells that recognise and destroy foreign material in the body. But tumour cells evolve a way of hiding themselves from T-cells by sprouting a surface molecule called a ligand. The ligand binds to and activates a receptor on the T-cell called PD-1. When PD-1 is activated the T-cell fails to recognise the cancer cell as foreign, fooling the immune system into mistaking tumours for normal tissue."

Most of us know that having a Vitamin D3 level pushing 100ng/dl is an excellent way to be sure our T-cells are active and strong to seek out and destroy invading bacteria and virus.

But this is the first I have heard of cancer cells masking themselves from our T-Cells rendering the T-Cells impotent.

Researchers have found three drugs that can unmask the cancer cells and make the T cells recognize the cancer and kill it.

And it is wonderful that researchers can engineer T-cells to recognize cancer cells as foreign in the body.

But what I want to know is how can we ordinary people live our lives knowing that we never trigger the cancer manifestation in the first place in our lives?
And what can we specifically do to our T-Cells to render PD-1 non operative on our T Cell receptors?

We have the road map in Dr K's writings to optimal health so that hopefully we do not express our genes to manifest cancer.

But can we increase our D3 levels to 200 or 300ng/ml to really activate our T- cells. Are higher levels better. I know Dr Cannell did deliberately raise his level to 400ng/ml just to see if there were any toxic effects and there were none for him.

Or is there nothing we can do about the PD-1 receptor?

### GlenPDQ — 2013-06-04T22:49:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antibody-wakes-up-t-cells-to-make-cancer-vanish.7241/post-87470>

Wouldn't bromelain, papain, and actinidin strip off some outer layer mucus/proteins from cancer cells so our white cells recognize them?

### Grizz — 2013-06-05T13:38:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antibody-wakes-up-t-cells-to-make-cancer-vanish.7241/post-87518>

The facts are totally different. Our bodies have cancer cells most of the time, and our immune system quickly kills them off **IF we are healthy.** Being healthy means NOT iodine deficient. Our immune system uses iodine to kill off cancer cells by a process called apoptosis, and those who are iodine deficient become cancer prone. It is that simple.

To find out if you are iodine deficient, you can take the [Iodine Loading Test](http://www.hakalalabs.com/testing.html). Please include the bromide & fluoride test with it. You can also take the Lugols iodine skin patch test to see if you are severely deficient ( ball park & not accurate ) This skin test cannot determine if you are sufficient, but it does give a good indication of severe deficiency. When the patch is gone in less than 1 hour, you are deficient. In my case, the patch was gone in just 3 minutes when I first started iodine. It now lasts 8 hours or more.
[http://www.utopiasilver.com/iodinetranscript.htm](http://www.utopiasilver.com/iodinetranscript.htm)

This gets even better. Even if you already have advanced stages of cancer, the Dr. Brownstein Iodine Protocol destroys it all. Yes, a simple iodine protocol cures cancer. For complete details, see my recent post:
[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?943-The-iodine-Thread!!!&p=87389&viewfull=1#post87389](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?943-The-iodine-Thread!!!&p=87389&viewfull=1#post87389)

The most striking example is our neighbor who was sent to home hospice to die with advanced cancer of the uterus & vagina. This cancer had spread all over and doctors gave up. She started the [Dr. Brownstein Iodine Protoco](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout)l and 9 months later she is totally cancer free. More striking cases in my own neighborhood include 2 cures of Fibrocystic Breast Disease in less than one month. Fibrocystic breasts are a pre-cancer condition. And for now, another neighbor has reduced her breast cancer by 90% in only 1 month with iodine painting on the breast & the iodine protocol. At this rate she might be cancer free before year end.

Complete references on iodine & cancer, see chapter Cancer:
[http://goo.gl/G4dLP](http://goo.gl/G4dLP)

Scientists are spinning their wheels & barking up the wrong tree attempting to re-invent the wheel. The medical industry consistently refuses to support iodine as a cure for common iodine deficiency disease, which includes cancers.
[http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references](http://tinyurl.com/iodine-references)

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-05T14:11:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-fix-cortisol-levels.7246/post-87523>

Won't have tests for another 2 weeks but I can't imagine that my cortisol is normal considering the poor sleep for the last several months.

I'm following the Leptin Rx but wondering what else can be done to fix the cortisol issues that I'm not already doing?

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-05T16:33:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-fix-cortisol-levels.7246/post-87542>

since you're a shift worker - you're cortisol levels are gonna be crazy. no if ands or butts about it... I say that from experience. I used to work swings/graves - through college, and on into my first job on & off for 4yrs, depending on the situation.

that said- what you're going to need to do is before bed - focus on oxytocin therapy to help you get a quick release of the melatonin at night. when you wake up immediately go out side and look at the sunrise - then oxytocin therapy and BAB. (this will also help your wife too )

also you need to see what you can do about getting on a regular schedule....

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-05T16:45:56+0300

Looking up oxytocin therapy (have no idea what that is lol).

I thought someone asked in one of my threads (can't find the question now for some reason) if I have ever had any concussions.

I have had two in my life - first when I was 15 at football practice - not to bad but I had some forgetfulness.

The second was 13 yrs ago when I was 22. I had a mountain bike accident (was wearing a helmet) but I don't remember anything.

Apparently before I went to the hospital I was asking the same questions over and over again and acting like an idiot. I have no memory of any of that.

Could this play a part in any of my troubles?

# Thread: Monkfish liver

**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dali Dula — 2013-06-05T14:37:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/monkfish-liver.7247/post-87524>

I came upon what I was told was Monkfish liver at the asian market. I never really know what I get at the market because no one speaks english there and i don't speak chinese. Any way this fish liver is large. I don't know the size of the fish it came from. It smelled very fishy, but I am an adventurous eater, so I cut it into bite size pieces and sauteed it with garlic, olive oil and some curry spice. Amazing delicious. Tender, no strong taste and I gotta believe it's super nutritious being an organ of fish. If you come across this, don't pass it up. It only cost $2.99/lb

# Thread: MCT vs LCT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mct-vs-lct.7249/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LRichard12 — 2013-06-05T17:26:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mct-vs-lct.7249/post-87557>

In Dr. Volek's 'The Art and Science of Low Carbohydrate Performance' he discourages the use of MCT's and favors high MUFAs (as does Dr. Rosedale in 'The Rosedale Diet'). He doesn't really give a reason why (at least not that I'm aware of). The only thing that I can come up with is that MCTs bypass the whole mitochondrial process and are quickly converted into ketones, whereby LCTs are eventually converted to Ketones after a long process. This may not allow for your body to learn how to process LCTs. What do you think?

Also if one eats a meal consisting of mostly (say 90%) MCT's and a little LCT (10%), would not the LCT be put aside and maybe even stored because the MCTs take precedence? Not sure, just an uneducated thought. What do you think?

# Thread: Waking up super early and BAB timing question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waking-up-super-early-and-bab-timing-question.7250/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-06-05T22:56:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waking-up-super-early-and-bab-timing-question.7250/post-87578>

As I've posted in a few threads before my sleep has been really jacked.

In addition to waking up sometimes around 1am and 3am last night (this morning) I woke up at 4:30am (which is common) and tried to go back to sleep but couldn't.

After laying there for 30 minutes I got up and made my BAB.

This happens a lot, but sometimes I am able to go back to sleep but often not.

JK says to eat the BAB within 30 minutes of waking.

So what's best trying to go back to sleep (successful about half the time) and getting some more sleep or just getting up and making my BAB?

Thanks for your input.

### bennyha — 2013-06-06T03:11:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waking-up-super-early-and-bab-timing-question.7250/post-87589>

I had this problem forever as well and still do sometimes. In the summer it's not so bad since the sun rises here at 5:20 or so in the morning right now. I used to always get up and just make my BAB and eat within 30 minutes of waking. I would always wake up between 3:00 and 4:00, and after laying in bed for awhile I would get frustrated then just get up for the day. This went on for a year or so.

For the past 6 months, I patiently lay in bed and wait for the sun to rise. I have all the power in my room shut off at nights, so I have no clock to tell. I have a tiny spot at the edge of my curtains that lets in the smallest amount of light when the sun starts to rise. When I see this, I get up and stare at the sun for 10 minutes with bare feet exposed to the concrete. I make my breakfast in advance the night before, so then I come in and start feasting.

I can't tell you how much this has helped me get my sleeping cycle back in order. I put on my blue blockers at night and lay in bed by 9:30 each night. Sometimes it sucks, being in bed that early. But being unhealthy sucks even more. So my take-and someone else may do it differently-but I would wait until you know the sun is up, go stare at it for 10 minutes, then go and eat.

Hope it helps man

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-06T04:22:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waking-up-super-early-and-bab-timing-question.7250/post-87593>

Thanks, i will give that a go first thing

> [bennyha said:](/goto/post?id=87589)
>
> I had this problem forever as well and still do sometimes. In the summer it's not so bad since the sun rises here at 5:20 or so in the morning right now. I used to always get up and just make my BAB and eat within 30 minutes of waking. I would always wake up between 3:00 and 4:00, and after laying in bed for awhile I would get frustrated then just get up for the day. This went on for a year or so.
>
> For the past 6 months, I patiently lay in bed and wait for the sun to rise. I have all the power in my room shut off at nights, so I have no clock to tell. I have a tiny spot at the edge of my curtains that lets in the smallest amount of light when the sun starts to rise. When I see this, I get up and stare at the sun for 10 minutes with bare feet exposed to the concrete. I make my breakfast in advance the night before, so then I come in and start feasting.
>
> I can't tell you how much this has helped me get my sleeping cycle back in order. I put on my blue blockers at night and lay in bed by 9:30 each night. Sometimes it sucks, being in bed that early. But being unhealthy sucks even more. So my take-and someone else may do it differently-but I would wait until you know the sun is up, go stare at it for 10 minutes, then go and eat.
>
> Hope it helps man

### Inger — 2013-06-06T06:28:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waking-up-super-early-and-bab-timing-question.7250/post-87598>

Bennyha made great suggestions! That is exactly how I do too. It works

### caroline — 2013-06-06T10:20:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waking-up-super-early-and-bab-timing-question.7250/post-87603>

yep .... great suggestions....

### cmegan — 2013-06-06T15:30:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waking-up-super-early-and-bab-timing-question.7250/post-87615>

Are you sleeping with an eye mask? That has helped me quite a bit over the past year...Brookstone sells nice ones...I recommend using one that is colored black, makes a difference in the light that may filter through the mask...when I wake too early I just lie in bed with my eye mask on and it really helps...

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-06T18:15:23+0300

# Thread: Low blood sugar?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-blood-sugar.7257/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2013-06-06T20:03:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-blood-sugar.7257/post-87640>

My 3 youngest kids recently had a pediatrician visit and had some routine blood work done. Their doctor called me because she is a bit concerned about their blood sugar. They all had low blood sugar - 59, 55, and 46. Any thoughts on why their blood sugar would be so low?

### HoneyChild — 2013-06-06T21:49:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-blood-sugar.7257/post-87655>

Liver leptin resistance? Your liver needs leptin in order to increase blood sugar using gluconeogensis/glucagon.

> When the liver is working well and not leptin resistant what does it do? Remember the 60/40 split of calories from a meal we spoke of earlier? The liver should use the stored 60% of those calories to feed the body fuel when we are not eating like during a fast or during sleep. This is done by raising the hormone glucagon to make fuel from the energy depots in the liver. This is how the body works to supply fuel it needs when we can’t eat or won’t eat. The liver is a fuel bank account for use in a rainy day when we are leptin sensitive. This pathway way does not work well at all when we are leptin resistant because the liver is spewing out excess fuel for storage instead of use.

[http://www.jackkruse.com/leptin-part-deux-the-liver/](http://www.jackkruse.com/leptin-part-deux-the-liver/)

### [email redacted] — 2013-06-06T22:02:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-blood-sugar.7257/post-87656>

I highly doubt the kids are leptin resistent. Especially the youngest. They don't eat sugar or grains at all. A mostly paleo diet, but we do eat dairy.

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-07T02:14:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-blood-sugar.7257/post-87663>

> [Trisha said:](/goto/post?id=87656)
>
> I highly doubt the kids are leptin resistent. Especially the youngest. They don't eat sugar or grains at all. A mostly paleo diet, but we do eat dairy.

Epigenetically I think it could be possible they're LR if you were LR when you were pregnant w/them... the difference you should be able to reverse most of the epigenetics with proper diet, and circadian cycles and seasonal eating.

### Cwave — 2013-06-07T04:14:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-blood-sugar.7257/post-87667>

I wouldn't worry too hard yet. These are low for an adult. For kids 45 - 75 may be ok, according to Wikipedia, see hypoglycemia. There is some disagreement (405-75!), so you look for symptoms: shaky, irritable, cognitively impaired. Of course, the younger, the harder to tell what's normal. If they eat paleo extremely low carb & are in or close to ketosis, they may be burning fat quite happily.
Usually when one gets weird lab results, the Dr just repeats the test, & blood glucose is a 30 second max test. Perhaps you already know thatglucose readings often vary 10-15 points in the time it takes to test? You could get most anyone with a glucose meter to check for you. Or Just buy one, i think they're about $15.

I won't be getting my reverse T3 done till next week but I was just looking at a couple of things from two months ago.

HS-CRP - .18
Vitamin D - 96

I've been assuming I'm leptin resistant because every time I try low carb and high fat I feel more sluggish than normal and never seem to get any leaner and I still deal with carb cravings and a feeling like I'm always hungry.

As you maybe have seen from other posts, my sleep has been really crappy for the last number of months.

Reading Jacks book last night he said a high hs-crp and low vitamin d are sure signs of Leptin Resistance.

Obviously the HS-CRP could be better but .18 doesn't seem terrible.

Any thoughts.

The biggest thing for me is lack of energy/sleep - if I could get that worked out I'd be very encouraged.

Thanks for your input

# Thread: An interesting read...

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-10T02:08:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-interesting-read.7320/post-87873>

[http://archaeonova.blogspot.com/2013/04/myths-of-paleo-part-one.html](http://archaeonova.blogspot.com/2013/04/myths-of-paleo-part-one.html)

Just recently read this, in fact, I'm about to go and re-read it. For those of you who are familiar with most/a lot of Dr. Kruse' blogs, this may not be entirely new to you. I was using it as a reference in my opinion about vegetables and carbs over at the Bulletproof Exec Forum. It delves into our evolution from what Dr. Kruse would call our "aquatic ape ancestors," and why starches make absolutely no sense in the human diet. The author goes on to cite how many times starches and legumes were actually applied in various ways other than a nutritional staple (using them for shelter and such). She is obviously an advocate of an ultra-high fat, very low carb, moderate-moderately low protein diet.

This actually inspired me even more now to tap into the PPP. I have been also following a keto-oriented thread over at MDA and saw how quickly some people could bounce back into ketosis after a "binge" or they maintained ketosis even after increasing protein. Kind of gives merit to the whole "protein loading" idea, especially when used in combination with MCTs.

# Thread: Should I get this CardioScan
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-get-this-cardioscan.7323/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-06-10T16:47:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-get-this-cardioscan.7323/post-87915>

Tomorrow I have bloodwork scheduled at the prompting of my cardiologist that will look at the large particle vs small particle of my LDL because.

She wants to do it because my LDL is in the 280's.

But my HDL is in the 80's and my triglycerides are 94.

In two weeks when I go to get the results of the LDL bloodwork she wants me to do a CaredioScan that will show a picture of any plaque in my arteries.

It's not covered by insurance so it's going to cost me $50 out of pocket.

Is this necessary?

I'd rather put the money towards something like a cortisol test or a test that JK recommends.

Thanks for your input.

### nonchalant — 2013-06-10T16:55:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-get-this-cardioscan.7323/post-87917>

I think the particle size test is not necessary, but it is good because it will make your doctor feel better about your not taking statins.
If the particles are nice and fluffy, as they will be because of what your HDL & trig levels indicate, I'm not sure that a scan would be necessary.
Personally I'd like to have a scan, to reassure my relatives that are shocked at the amount of animal fat I consume. But I also would rather use the funds on a more useful test.

### nonchalant — 2013-06-10T16:59:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-get-this-cardioscan.7323/post-87918>

Wait, a CardioScan is a type of CT scan? Sounds like a lot of EMF...
Perhaps the cardiologist is being pressured to make more use out of the CT machine.

### Dextery — 2013-06-10T23:06:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-get-this-cardioscan.7323/post-87939>

I'm with nonchalant on the emfs from the ct scan. You are 35 and in good shape as a firefighter should be. Heart calcification should not be a problem at your age. Is it just your LDL number? But that said a heart CT scan for $50 is very cheap.

The blood work will let you know where you are at. The NMR test will tell you your total LDL particle number(LDL-P)and your sm dense LDL particle number and then you will know that...hopefully the sm dense are 10% or less of the total LDL particle number.
And the LDL-C of 280s doesn't matter if they are all large bouyant.
The number that jumps out at me is your triglycerides. That number needs to be down in the 40s. With the NMR you will also find out your HDL-C number and that should be around 80 or above....the higher the better.

Be prepared for a particle number to be much bigger than the less than 1000 nmol/L number reference range indicated. And be prepared for a total cholesterol to be much higher than the 200mg/dl reference range.

Our paleo/epi-paleo diet does raise total cholesterol and HDL and LDL but the fat we eat...especially the saturated fat coconut oil & MCTs are good for the body.

I would bet she is going to freak out and tell you she is very concerned with your blood work. LOL.

Post your results.

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-11T03:09:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-get-this-cardioscan.7323/post-87953>

Thank you for posting, I feel good about holding off.

I will get the cardio scan yearly at work when I turn 40.

What is the difference between HDL and HDL-C?

My HDL is 87

> [Dextery said:](/goto/post?id=87939)
>
> I'm with nonchalant on the emfs from the ct scan. You are 35 and in good shape as a firefighter should be. Heart calcification should not be a problem at your age. Is it just your LDL number? But that said a heart CT scan for $50 is very cheap.
>
> The blood work will let you know where you are at. The NMR test will tell you your total LDL particle number(LDL-P)and your sm dense LDL particle number and then you will know that...hopefully the sm dense are 10% or less of the total LDL particle number.
> And the LDL-C of 280s doesn't matter if they are all large bouyant.
> The number that jumps out at me is your triglycerides. That number needs to be down in the 40s. With the NMR you will also find out your HDL-C number and that should be around 80 or above....the higher the better.
>
> Be prepared for a particle number to be much bigger than the less than 1000 nmol/L number reference range indicated. And be prepared for a total cholesterol to be much higher than the 200mg/dl reference range.
>
> Our paleo/epi-paleo diet does raise total cholesterol and HDL and LDL but the fat we eat...especially the saturated fat coconut oil & MCTs are good for the body.
>
> I would bet she is going to freak out and tell you she is very concerned with your blood work. LOL.
>
> Post your results.

### Dextery — 2013-06-11T03:39:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-get-this-cardioscan.7323/post-87955>

HDL-C just means HDL Calculated They are the same. HDL-C is what the NMR test reports for total HDL.

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-11T17:03:44+0300

# Thread: Leaky Gut/AI - Chicken and Pork?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-ai-chicken-and-pork.7326/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kposs — 2013-06-10T18:24:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-ai-chicken-and-pork.7326/post-87924>

Hi,

I don't actually have a diagnosed AI, but I definitely have leaky gut and systemic inflammation from chronic NSAID use. I did so well not eating eggs (almost 40 point drop in HSCRP - yes, it was THAT high) that I'm currently doing a super strict Epi-Paleo Leaky Gut/AI protocol (no eggs, soy, dairy, grains, nuts, legumes, nightshades, poultry, pork, chocolate, processed meats, seeds, etc.) for 6 weeks (to start) until my next blood tests.

I've searched, but I cannot find a definitive answer about chicken and pork and any reasoning as to why they might be good or bad for leaky gut/AI. Obviously not epi-paleo to start, but just wondering for sake of variety down the road.

thanks!

### Dextery — 2013-06-10T18:51:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-ai-chicken-and-pork.7326/post-87929>

Dr K has said fowl is fowl and he does not eat fowl. Most of us eat bacon, ham, pork chops etc. But if you had a hsCRP that is supposed to be under .5 mg/L stay with the epi-paleo nutrient dense seafoods in the order Dr. K outlined.

You can use copious amounts of turmeric as a gut healing agent. Buy it in bulk for about $8 a pound rather than buying capsules.

Make bone broth to also heal the gut.

Looking forward to your next report on hsCRP and blood tests.

### mh586 — 2013-06-12T06:34:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-ai-chicken-and-pork.7326/post-87988>

The argument against pork and poultry it the high omega 6 fat content, specifically when grain fed. PUFAs increase intestinal permeability (i.e. cause leaky gut) while saturated fat improves it.

# Thread: Cycling
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cycling.7334/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### WuLingZhi — 2013-06-12T11:38:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cycling.7334/post-87989>

Aloha all, first post. I'm a recreational cyclist, and I've been riding for about a year and a half, with sometime off during a collarbone fracture. I live in hawaii, so I also surf a good bit too. I've been eating a mixture of epi, and bulletproof the past two to three months, and feel great. My weekly average is around 150-200 miles with some heavy climbing. My questions are concerned with what I need to keep my performance up with some increased training. Ie, carb intake, protein, supplements etc. I have been eating under 75gm of carbs, and around 150gm of protein a day. Bulletproof coffee and the mct oil. I take a mixture of supplements, mostly Chinese adaptogens, as I'm a masters degree student in his last year for acupuncture, and integrative medicine. I also take the normal stacks of b vitamins, bcaa, magnesium, iodine, iron, etc. I know my cortisol levels are good, considering I'm a student trying to study for board exams, and doing chronic cardio. Hormone panels are all normal as well. I'm 29, 6'2" and around 200 lbs. Basically I've looked through the threads and found some interesting stuff from Barry, but a lot of it is very anecdotal. Any other cyclists out there? If so what are you guys eating? I'm very interested in the PPP, but either I've missed a detailed regime on how to access it, or it's not available, thanks!

### receo — 2013-10-04T10:17:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cycling.7334/post-96859>

Cycling are best to burn your legs fat and strong your lega bone.I also take the normal stacks of b vitamins, bcaa, magnesium, iodine, iron, etc. I know my cortisol levels are good, considering I'm a student trying to study for board exams, and doing chronic cardio.

### MeghanK — 2013-10-04T19:15:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cycling.7334/post-96879>

I do a little higher carb closer to 100g a day.... and train 2-3 hours a day mixture of run, cycle, strength, cross-fit etc... but I'm also female and many studies are coming out that females might need to be higher carb.... I would make yourself an N=1.... try higher carb 150-250g for 2-3 weeks keeping your cycling regime the same (do you have a power meter?)....and throw in a "test" course for time... then do 2-3 weeks of low carb same routine... I would do the low carb one first since you have been pretty low carb .... then compare your power/HR/ stress/ HRV/ feeling of fatigue in general etc.....
What do you eat to get your 75g carbs?
Do you take Creatine?

### Renfred — 2013-10-11T07:04:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cycling.7334/post-97501>

Cycling is great to lose unwanted weight, boost stamina and increase coordination. Cycling build strength, tone muscles and improves heart health. Cycling prevent from obesity, hypertension, heart disease and certain types of cancer.
[click here](http://www.grayslakechiropractors.com/libertyville-chiropractor-2/)

### Ricky — 2013-11-09T18:32:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cycling.7334/post-100273>

Cycling is my favorite activity. Its an exercise that has many benefits. Cycling is best cardio exercise that provides us fitness. It makes legs,bones and muscles strong.

# Thread: Saccharomyces on a GI Fx test anyone?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saccharomyces-on-a-gi-fx-test-anyone.7353/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-06-14T15:35:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saccharomyces-on-a-gi-fx-test-anyone.7353/post-88092>

Hi all,

have any of you ever had Saccharomyces sp. show up on your GI Fx test while you are also supplementing with S. boulardii??

And if so, did your MD tell you to treat that yeast.....? Mine did but it doesn't make sense to me.

Why'd I want to kill or root out something I'm also populating with?

### melrito — 2013-06-15T22:18:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saccharomyces-on-a-gi-fx-test-anyone.7353/post-88127>

**s. boulardii**

> [Hope said:](/goto/post?id=88092)
>
> Hi all,
>
> have any of you ever had Saccharomyces sp. show up on your GI Fx test while you are also supplementing with S. boulardii??
>
> And if so, did your MD tell you to treat that yeast.....? Mine did but it doesn't make sense to me.
>
> Why'd I want to kill or root out something I'm also populating with?

Finding s. boulardii on a Gi Fx test doesn't mean you have to treat yeast.... since s. boulardii is considered a beneficial yeast (hence the supplementation with it). Bad yeast such as candida albicans is generally considered an opportunistic pathogen - meaning that until you fix the environment (fixyour gut), the yeast will continue to proliferate. Supplementing with s. boulardii is actually beneficial against bad yeast, as it can inhibit the reproduction of yeast - at least that is the case with candida albicans. If you choose to treat yeast (if you have a yeast overgrowth), just be aware that you will need to do some major gut flora rebuilding afterward.

### Hope — 2013-07-12T17:24:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saccharomyces-on-a-gi-fx-test-anyone.7353/post-89955>

yeah...thanks Melrito!

# Thread: For those questioning mainstream science
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-those-questioning-mainstream-science.7362/>
**Subforum:** Factor X
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### SeaHorse — 2013-06-16T13:35:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/for-those-questioning-mainstream-science.7362/post-88156>

I've been getting updates from the Thunderbolt project and this meeting was announced recently. It is good to realize that many of the sciences have their outliers and questioners. Gerald Pollack (the water guy) will be at this meeting too.

ON THE NATURAL PHILOSOPHY ALLIANCE AND ITS ANNUAL MEETING:

World-Wide Science Think Tank Returns to the University of Maryland July 2013 to Address Topics Outside the Mainstream

College Park, Maryland (May 15, 2013) - The Natural Philosophy Alliance (NPA), a world-wide think tank, is holding its 20th Annual Conference at the University of Maryland, College Park, from July 10 -13, 2013, in conjunction with the Conference on Future Energy (COFE). This yearâ€™s conference will feature over 70 authors on various scientific topics including presentations on Relativity, Structures, Gravity, the Big Bang, Expansion Tectonics, Electric Universe, and Science Philosophy. The NPA will also be presenting their annual Sagnac Award, given to the best work in science outside the mainstream. This is the third year in which the NPA networked with other conferences, having held earlier events with the Electric Universe group and the annual Tesla Tech conference.

This yearâ€™s NPA conference will feature prominent members Dr. Bill Lucas, Dr. Cynthia Whitney, Dr. Gerald Pollack, and Wal Thornhill. Dr. Lucas, Dr. Whitney, and Dr. Pollack will be talking about their ground-breaking books including â€œThe Universal Force" (Volume 1) by Dr. Lucas, "Algebraic Chemistry: Applications and Origins" by Dr. Cynthia Whitney, and â€œThe Fourth Phase of Waterâ€￾ by Dr. Gerald Pollack.

Dr. Whitney will deliver the annual John Chappell Lecture in honor of NPA founder Dr. John Chappell. Stephen Smith, managing editor of the popular Thunderbolts PIcture of the Day, will give a presentation on â€œMars: The Great Desertâ€￾ in 3-D, and David Talbott, director of the Thunderbolts Project, will speak on "Exposing the Myths of Settled Science."

This yearâ€™s Sagnac Award will be going to Wal Thornhill, a pioneer of the Electric Universe paradigm. NPA members consider the Sagnac Award to be the "Nobel Prize" for scientists working outside the mainstream.

On Thursday, there will be an outside demonstration by Dr. Max Fomitchev-Zamilov of cavitation-induced fusion. Also, on Saturday afternoon, there will be a panel discussion on the NPA and the future of science.

About the Natural Philosophy Alliance (NPA) - The Natural Philosophy Alliance (NPA) is a world-wide scientific organization whose members have one thing in common: they have intensely studied mainstream theories in the sciences, including relativity, the big bang, and plate tectonics. Many members have taken a critical position on standard theoretical assumptions. The NPA's goal is to seek truth in science through observation, experiment, and critical reasoning. The organization invites members to vigorously evaluate competing theories, enabling the most compelling ideas to grow and flourish through the unfettered pursuit of scientific knowledge. See more at [http://www.worldnpa.org](http://www.worldnpa.org).

About the Sagnac Award - The Natural Philosophy Alliance (NPA), a world-wide think-tank and scientific organization chooses one or more scientists each year to receive a Lifetime Achievement Award for their groundbreaking work outside the mainstream of science. The Award is named after Georges Sagnac, a French physicist who lent his name to the Sagnac effect, a discovery that made possible both interferometers and ring laser gyroscopes developed since the 1970s.

About the Thunderbolts Project - An interdisciplinary collaboration of accredited scientists and independent researchers established in 2004. Its prime mission is to explore the Electric Universe paradigm. Recent discoveries in the sciences have placed a spotlight on the electromagnetic force in nature, from quantum worlds and biological systems to planetary, stellar, and galactic domains. More information at [http://www.thunderbolts.info](http://www.thunderbolts.info)

Natural Philosophy Alliance
Tel: 310-991-5744
[[email redacted]](mailto:[email redacted])
Mikamar Publishing

16871 SE 80th Pl
Milwaukie, OR
97267
US

# Thread: Successes? Changes?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/successes-changes.7366/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-06-17T05:59:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/successes-changes.7366/post-88216>

I thought maybe we could update what successes we are having ..... what changes we are noticing....
How we are doing with epi-paleo ......

What things/changes are we loving? What are we struggling with?

How has your diet progressed? Are you doing anything different now that is making things easier/better?

How is spring/summer affecting you? are you loving it or are you wishing for the cold weather to come back?

Are friends and family noticing and paying more attention to the changes that they see in you now that we have been doing this for awhile?

and most of all ...... do you feel vital and in love with life????

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-17T10:04:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/successes-changes.7366/post-88221>

Great idea for a thread, I may as well chime in.
I have been noticing a few things recently, and it was ironically after I started eating more fish - even canned and farmed fish. For the first time ever I have been getting tan easily and without any signs of sun burn. I always got tan in the summer, but it would take all summer and I would end up getting extremely sun burned. One time I even got water bubbles/blisters on my shoulder. I also was never good in the heat - I would get over heated and nauseous very quickly. I would get headaches and become dizzy. Well, so far this summer I have been getting tan easier and quicker. I haven't even gotten close to sun burn, not even slight irritation to my skin. My fiance told me multiple times I have a slight glow to me. I have noticed the dark circles under my eyes are fading. They are still evident, but I can tell that they are slowly dissipating. I have very little acne nowadays. We'll see how it goes tomorrow and this up coming week because I ate out today and ate quite a bit of dairy - not grass-fed or raw, but maybe okay quality as it was imported from Greece, lol.

My tolerance for the heat has also increased. I have always enjoyed the cold/winter as a child and teenager, and I still do. The thing is, when I was young, I wasn't able to tolerate heat for a very long time. It seems as though I'm now developing a tolerance to the heat as well as to freezing cold temperatures. Today in Chicago it was humid, and I could feel that it was humid, I perceived humidity, yet I stood there unaffected. I never get sick anymore either.

The things I'm struggling with are few and far between, but they are rather tough things. I may in fact have to make a post about it. Firstly, I'm continuing my battle with anxiety. I get anxious from the most random situations and thoughts. It's almost as if I have no control over it. I must say that since adopting a paleo, and now 'bulletproof/epi-paleo' diet, it has gotten much, much better. But I still battle with it. In fact, at my fiances graduation, the sky was making me anxious. Yes, the damn sky. The reason is that for sometime after attempting to lucid dream, I've come close to having "out of body experiences", and well, the graduation took place out on the open varsity football field. I was her family that I had not really developed a relationship with yet (her grandparents), I was having memories of when I went to that highschool before things went bad with family/illnesses and I moved and I was just having flashbacks almost, then the sky.... It was just so wide and far reaching and vast. I felt like an ant compared to the vastness of the Earth. It brought me back memories of an LSD trip when I was looking at the orange sky in Chicago at a music festival and me and my friend were conversing at how seemingly insignificant we were to the world. All of this just put so much anxiety on me I started gripping the metal seats with all my might and battled through the event. I got anxious today, too, at a comedy gig (Bill Maher). I think that was from cross contamination in my food, possibly (gluten).

I don't mind summer or winter or any season that will be coming in the future. They all have their perks. Especially now that I've read so much on these blogs. Summer and Spring offer a great time for me to take advantage of the photoelectric effect and vitamin D. Also, because of those two effects, I can eat starches without as much consequence and have some more liberal food choices. I can use the increased calories and carbs to pack on muscle as well. Winter and Fall will be great for CT and sleep. I also love Fall, Halloween and the changes in the colors of all that permeates in nature around me - it's the most beautiful season visually in my opinion. I have some questions though. Am I not creating a mismatch this summer by starting CT although it is a warm month and I'm eating starches 1x a week? Would it be wise to stop CT? To stop the cyclical ketogenic dieting and stay strictly ketogenic, or strictly eat starch?

All I can say is I'm aaaalovin' life right now regardless of my uphill battle. I plan on taking everything I learned to the next level. I plan on tapping into the PPP for martial arts performance and competing in my first BJJ tournament and I'm aiming at taking first! I have a wrestling background anyhow so I'm not necessarily overly ambitious, lol. I also plan on doing some serious biohacking. I want to create a blog to track my adventures into the PPP and track things with a blood or breathe ketone meter and get some DEXA scans for body fat and bone density. I then want to get my genome and/or exome sequenced as well as my lineage and heritablity of certain alleles making me prone to disease. I can then alter my epi-paleo diet to a more personalized template. I believe that epi-paleo should be just that - a template. The future is definitely in genetics/epigenetics. I know that I come frome Europe - I'm Polish, Russian, and Romanian. I also live in the midwest of the USA. Taking both of those into consideration, I bet my diet should differ from a Hispanic person living off the cost of Spain, regardless of our aquatic ape ancestry.

Well, that was kind of long winded, but I'm very optimistic towards my future and towards the information I will compile from the members here and the blog as well as Dr. Kruse's book. I just re-downloaded it and I'm going to purchase the paper back as well. I also just finished downloading Mastering Leptin 3rd Edition. So I've got some things to learn and apply!

BigPapa .....You are an extraordinary young man..... I always love reading your posts. You are so dialed in to life - just amazing. Thanks for sharing all that with us!

### Trinity — 2013-06-18T05:02:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/successes-changes.7366/post-88307>

Hi guys,

I'm still a work in progress, but I just got back labs that show I'm finally getting the estrogen dominance under control. Took 300mg of progesterone to get there, but my E/P ratio was 250! Yipee!

PS: Big Papa, can I ask why you find the prospect of an OBE anxiety producing? I am curious because I have had lucid dreams my entire life and started spontaneously having OBE's a couple years ago. Having suffered from "sleep paralysis" my entire life, I had probably been on the OBE threshold dozens, if not hundreds, of times throughout most of my life. However, it wasn't until I read Robert Monroe's "Journey's Out of the Body" that I realized that the paralysis and vibrations are "symptoms" of an oncoming OBE. Ironically, it was while reading that book that I had my first OBE and I have had dozens since then. After the first few experiences, I found the OBE separation of conscious awareness from my body very freeing and exhilarating.

\--Trinity

### Inger — 2013-06-18T07:23:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/successes-changes.7366/post-88327>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=88216)
>
> How has your diet progressed? Are you doing anything different now that is making things easier/better?
>
> How is spring/summer affecting you? are you loving it or are you wishing for the cold weather to come back?
>
> Are friends and family noticing and paying more attention to the changes that they see in you now that we have been doing this for awhile?
>
> and most of all ...... do you feel vital and in love with life????

I do oil pulling again.. I am loving it! Then I drink this golden fluid.. no idea if that helps.. but I do feel amazing... and i do wild green smoothies too. My diet is always good as always.  I eat less now. Weird.
I do feel vital and in love with life..huh
I do not wish for any other season than what is present. Why? That would be very stupid. Every season is here for a reason. That is why modern human suffers so much, she does not want to go with the seasons... so I have learned myself to love any weather, sun, rain, snow.. we need them all. When it is too much sun I am worried, because the pasture does not grow new grass for the sheep. It needs to rain! If it would be only summer.. I would have to be scared of dangerous bugs and stuff, like malaria and such. Now here is nothing to be scared of, I can enjoy the nature 100%. The winter takes care of many dangerous things. And I love winter because of the "wintersleep" thing.. that is time to turn inside.. and cool down. I love that. But I do love summer too, I love it all!

Oh.. and I can do 3 pullups! I tested and I was quite proud!

### caroline — 2013-06-18T09:17:14+0300

You make so many valid points as usual Inger ......... congrats on the pullups! Could you do any at all before?

### Destiny — 2013-06-18T14:13:51+0300

### Inger — 2013-06-18T20:25:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/successes-changes.7366/post-88359>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=88332)
>
> You make so many valid points as usual Inger ......... congrats on the pullups! Could you do any at all before?
> Is this the first time you have noticed you are eating less? Is it a seasonal thing Inger do you think? or is it .... In the spring a young woman's fancy turns to thoughts of love????

Audrey, I could do one pullup.. and then I once in a while have done one, and then I could do two, and now I could do three! I really do not exercise, but I think it is the sunshine and me repairing the fences around the pasture? My arms are pretty muscular now!
I do have had days with a little less eating but not like this.. now I have had it for a week or more.. I am just satiated with less.. maybe it is the love..  but it took a unexpected turn today. I will write in my journal.

Oh, and what else, I have quit drinking coffee every morning! I just take one cup occasionally instead. I really limit it. And it feels great.

# Thread: Bio Hacking-What to ask for

**Posts:** 9

### LRichard12 — 2013-06-17T14:30:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bio-hacking-what-to-ask-for.7368/post-88231>

Dear all;

I have posted this as a question for Dr. Kruse, but would also appreciate any advice from others if yo're so inclined;

I know you can't practice med online, but could you help me to talk to my doc. He knows nothing about thyroid, depression, leptin, etc. but is willing to write any prescription or do any blood work I ask for (good doctor ehe).

What tests should I request for my problem.

Any suggested method of treatment I could discuss with my doctor.

Again (Symptom Reminder); I'm hungry, have gained 10 lbs (since beginning the Leptin RX w/huge amounts of coconut oil), tired, irritated, depressed, antisocial, no motivation, poor work ethic (unless I get a proper cup of coffee-which has stopped working) no libido, chronic constipation (going on for nearly 2 years-barley responds to laxatives anymore), muscle weakness, heart palpitations, been getting cold lately, and sometimes really warm, again no energy, gaining back weight after loosing 100lbs on low calorie ketoginic diet (Atkins style-modified to paleo with less protein more fat).

Last labs came back showing Ferritin at 417 (range: 30-400), Total T3 was low at 0.51 (1.3-3.1 range), TSH was 1.55 mIU/L (0.27-4.2), Free T4 17.0 pmol/L (12-22), blood cortisol was 694.9 nmol/L (no range-sry) fasting.

Supplements: 2000+ mg biomagnesium, 10000mg tumeric, Artichoke extract, Neem leaf powder, digestive enzyme (full spec, w/peppermint oil), Oxbile, Full spec probiotics, 30 grams FOS, antioxandant support supp, no-flush niacin, folic acid, b complex, multivit, omega 3 fish oil supp, 5000iu vit D3, 1200 vit E, 150mg selenium, vit c w/bilberry extract, BioPQQ (w. Lcarnitine, LCarnosine, TMG, NAC, etc.), Reservation, coQ10, Rholdia extract, Kelp, 1g cinnamon w/200mcg chromium, and Alpha lipolic acid. Also add Himalayan pink sea salt to my food (5+ grams daily). Nearly 100% of my protein comes from Wild salmon, and my fat from coconut oil (very large amounts). Carbs are minimal coming from coconut flour, raw cocoa powder, avocados, salad greens, mushrooms, fiber supplements, etc.

How should I approach my doctor?

Thanks for your help!

In CHRIST,
Lucas Richard

### HoneyChild — 2013-06-17T17:15:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bio-hacking-what-to-ask-for.7368/post-88245>

I don't have much to suggest but a lot of people find folic acid difficult to take because of certain genetic issues. Methylfolate (Metafolin by Solgar) is the best. Many people also avoid B-complexes with folic acid.

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-18T23:21:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bio-hacking-what-to-ask-for.7368/post-88373>

> [LRichard12 said:](/goto/post?id=88231)
>
> Dear all;
>
> I have posted this as a question for Dr. Kruse, but would also appreciate any advice from others if yo're so inclined;
>
> I know you can't practice med online, but could you help me to talk to my doc. He knows nothing about thyroid, depression, leptin, etc. but is willing to write any prescription or do any blood work I ask for (good doctor ehe).
>
> What tests should I request for my problem.
>
> Any suggested method of treatment I could discuss with my doctor.
>
> Again (Symptom Reminder); I'm hungry, have gained 10 lbs (since beginning the Leptin RX w/huge amounts of coconut oil), tired, irritated, depressed, antisocial, no motivation, poor work ethic (unless I get a proper cup of coffee-which has stopped working) no libido, chronic constipation (going on for nearly 2 years-barley responds to laxatives anymore), muscle weakness, heart palpitations, been getting cold lately, and sometimes really warm, again no energy, gaining back weight after loosing 100lbs on low calorie ketoginic diet (Atkins style-modified to paleo with less protein more fat).
>
> Last labs came back showing Ferritin at 417 (range: 30-400), Total T3 was low at 0.51 (1.3-3.1 range), TSH was 1.55 mIU/L (0.27-4.2), Free T4 17.0 pmol/L (12-22), blood cortisol was 694.9 nmol/L (no range-sry) fasting.
>
> Supplements: 2000+ mg biomagnesium, 10000mg tumeric, Artichoke extract, Neem leaf powder, digestive enzyme (full spec, w/peppermint oil), Oxbile, Full spec probiotics, 30 grams FOS, antioxandant support supp, no-flush niacin, folic acid, b complex, multivit, omega 3 fish oil supp, 5000iu vit D3, 1200 vit E, 150mg selenium, vit c w/bilberry extract, BioPQQ (w. Lcarnitine, LCarnosine, TMG, NAC, etc.), Reservation, coQ10, Rholdia extract, Kelp, 1g cinnamon w/200mcg chromium, and Alpha lipolic acid. Also add Himalayan pink sea salt to my food (5+ grams daily). Nearly 100% of my protein comes from Wild salmon, and my fat from coconut oil (very large amounts). Carbs are minimal coming from coconut flour, raw cocoa powder, avocados, salad greens, mushrooms, fiber supplements, etc.
>
> How should I approach my doctor?
>
> Thanks for your help!
>
> In CHRIST,
> Lucas Richard

Richard

How long have you been doing the LR? weight gain while on it can be a sign of hormonal imbalances. Additionally, your chronic constipation tells me you're dehydrated. as for all the supplements - is there any specific reason why you're taking all the supplement? Supplements have their place - but you may not need all your taking. Additionally, you're eating salmon which is a good seafood. If you can you really want to eat shellfish - oysters, clams, mussles, shrimps, crabs, etc...

as for labs here's where you want to start you need a lipid panel, a full thyroid panel, a full hormone panel, CBC, Metabolic panel, cortisol, dhea-s IGF-1, shbg, pregnenolone, Vit D3 - 25 Hydroxy and Magnesium RBC.

Take the list in below - tell you doc you want to get a base line of labs - to see where your at. and then you want to retest, as you continue to improve your health, to see where your numbers are. your labs are a METRIC of your health, when you look at them together! this lets you take ownership and responsibility for your health. Your doc is going to help you figure out how to read the labs - and it may be an education for both of you.

I've listed the labs my Doc Just ran for me, along w/ the CPT Codes and Test #s for LABCORP.

Berkley heart lab, or a NMR, or a VAP - these are cholesterol labs - that look at the small subfractions of the LDL (Berkley is the new standard -HsCRP, HbA1C are included in the lab along w/a bunch of other good stuff) here's a link that you can take in to your doc - to explain why you want a berkley [http://www.bhlinc.com/patients/why-berkeley/](http://www.bhlinc.com/patients/why-berkeley/)

Fasting blood glucose Test # 001818, CPT Code 82947
HsCRP
HbA1C
CBC Test # 005009, CPT Code 85025
Comp Metabolic Panel Test # 322000, CPT code 80053
Thyroid:
TSH, Test # 004259, CPT Code 84443
Free T4, Test # 001974, CPT code 84439
Free T3, Test # 010389, CTP Code 84481
RT3, Test # 070104, CPT 84482
Progestrone, Test # 004312, CPT Code 84144
Estradiol, Sensitive, Test # 140244, CPT Code 82670
SHGB Test #082016 CPT Code: 84270
Testosterone Free Direct, & serum with Total Testosterone: Test # 140103, CPT Codes 84402, 84403
Prolactin Test # 004465, CPT code 84146
cortisol Test # 004051, CPT Code 82533
DHEA-S Test # 004020, CPT Code 82627
IGF-1 Test #010636, CPT Code 84305
RBC magnesium Test # 080283, CPT Code 83735
Vitamin D25 Hyrdoxy Test # 081950, CPT code 82306
Serotonin Test# 120204, CPT Code 84260 (requires frozen specimens)
Pregnenolone Test# 140707, CPT Code 84140 (requires Frozen Specimens)

let me know if you have any questions...

I would also verify with your insurance if some more unusual tests are covered on your insurance plan since it might be cheaper to get them on your own. It happened to me that my Homocysteine test was not covered. I had to pay the lab out of pocket what they charged my insurance - a really inflated rate of $228.00. If I went to get it privately I would have paid only $70.00.

> Berkley heart lab, or a NMR, or a VAP - these are cholesterol labs - that look at the small subfractions of the LDL (Berkley is the new standard -HsCRP, HbA1C are included in the lab along w/a bunch of other good stuff) here's a link that you can take in to your doc - to explain why you want a berkley [http://www.bhlinc.com/patients/why-berkeley/](http://www.bhlinc.com/patients/why-berkeley/)
>
>
> Fasting blood glucose Test # 001818, CPT Code 82947
> HsCRP
> HbA1C
> CBC Test # 005009, CPT Code 85025
> Comp Metabolic Panel Test # 322000, CPT code 80053
> Thyroid:
> TSH, Test # 004259, CPT Code 84443
> Free T4, Test # 001974, CPT code 84439
> Free T3, Test # 010389, CTP Code 84481
> RT3, Test # 070104, CPT 84482
> Progestrone, Test # 004312, CPT Code 84144
> Estradiol, Sensitive, Test # 140244, CPT Code 82670
> SHGB Test #082016 CPT Code: 84270
> Testosterone Free Direct, & serum with Total Testosterone: Test # 140103, CPT Codes 84402, 84403
> Prolactin Test # 004465, CPT code 84146
> cortisol Test # 004051, CPT Code 82533
> DHEA-S Test # 004020, CPT Code 82627
> IGF-1 Test #010636, CPT Code 84305
> RBC magnesium Test # 080283, CPT Code 83735
> Vitamin D25 Hyrdoxy Test # 081950, CPT code 82306
> Serotonin Test# 120204, CPT Code 84260 (requires frozen specimens)
> Pregnenolone Test# 140707, CPT Code 84140 (requires Frozen Specimens)
>
> let me know if you have any questions...

Dear Shjin,

Thank you very much for your answer! I will use this a s a basis for talking to my doctor.

I have been doing LR for about 3 weeks now. I too suspect hormonal imbalances and do look forward to getting these tests done.

As for dehydration; I drink ample if not copious amounts of water during the day and do urinate a lot. 99% of the water that I drink is filtered. I even carry a water bottle with a built in filter. I do ensure that I get an adequate potassium/magnesium/sodium (from sea salt) intake. I have had this constipation now for 2 years and it gets progressively worse. My fatigue, heart palpitations, low motivation, and depression began about around early 2010. The constipation started in late 2010, and really got bad in 2011.

My supplemental regimen is a result of reading up on hypothyroidism (which I'm sure is part of my problem), adrenal fatigue, leptin rsistance, leaky gut, etc. My goal through supplementation is to fight inflammation (which is part of my problem), support my thyroid, fight my chronic constipation, improve energy levels, and aid in weight loss.

Thanks again for the lab suggestions! I will see about getting these done asap. I can't get an appointment with the endocrinologist until the end of July, but can see if the regular GP will run these labs for me.

Thanks again for your help!

### LRichard12 — 2013-06-19T16:37:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bio-hacking-what-to-ask-for.7368/post-88422>

> [Destiny said:](/goto/post?id=88410)
>
> I would also verify with your insurance if some more unusual tests are covered on your insurance plan since it might be cheaper to get them on your own. It happened to me that my Homocysteine test was not covered. I had to pay the lab out of pocket what they charged my insurance - a really inflated rate of $228.00. If I went to get it privately I would have paid only $70.00.

Thank you for the warning!

# Thread: N=1; One Week BioHack at the beach
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/n-1-one-week-biohack-at-the-beach.7370/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2013-06-17T19:16:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/n-1-one-week-biohack-at-the-beach.7370/post-88254>

So last week - we went down to New Bern NC - about 45mins from the beach. Cell Signals where we were staying were intermittent. I turned off the Wi-Fi in our unit for the entire duration. We spent 4 out of 5 days at the beach. talk about battery recharge. by day two of playing in the ocean, the whole family's need for water had increased tremendously, as did salt. both Chaos and Havoc took naps every day - and I'm not talking a 3-45min snooze. I'm talking 1-2hrs worth of sleeping! by the end of the week, dh and I were waking up before them! my cystic acne cleared up after the first day of just walking in the ocean! we ate fresh seafood every day for at least one meal. There were some days, that food held no appeal. any food we ate was light. we had a long drive back on Saturday to reality. Sunday, DH and I were so energized - we knocked out a bunch of work we've been ignoring in the house for much. Its like we had more energy than we knew what to do with.

I feel so refreshed. I'm back at work, and I've got a ton of stuff to clear through, and I'm not even stressed about it. all those electrons from the beach helped reset me for this week... looking fwd to my next trip to the beach for some camping in mid September....

### nonchalant — 2013-06-17T22:50:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/n-1-one-week-biohack-at-the-beach.7370/post-88272>

Sounds like a marvelous vacation, Gretchen. The ocean really seems to kick skin problems to the curb, but you might see them return in a few weeks, unfortunately.
Mmmm, a week at the beach... sounds like heaven. Glad you and your family were able to seize the opportunity!

### fitness@home — 2013-06-17T23:21:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/n-1-one-week-biohack-at-the-beach.7370/post-88276>

Your vacation sounds wonderful Gretchen. A little envy going on here

Isn't it amazing how it recharges your batteries?

### Hope — 2013-06-18T01:48:34+0300

Gretchen, that is awesome!! Thanks for sharing...may I ask....did you get sun burned at all? The kids? Did you have any kind of protection? Like shade? I realize it's your n=1 but I wonder......

SO excited for you!

### caroline — 2013-06-18T02:41:49+0300

### Huck — 2013-06-19T17:30:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/n-1-one-week-biohack-at-the-beach.7370/post-88431>

When I lived in N VA, I used to make several trips a year to the Outer Banks. In talking with some of my friends, they all shared the same experience.

It takes a couple of days to relax and by the end of the week, life is good. No arguing, no fighting, just relaxed good fun. But once you get to Fredericksburg on the trip home, watch out. Traffic is a nightmare with people weaving in and out at 80 mph. You white knuckle the rest of the trip. By the time you finally make it home, a good argument usually erupts.

One of the best things I ever did was get the heck out of DC. The money is seductive, but the stress is unbearable.

### Trey625 — 2013-06-19T17:39:53+0300

I agree Huck...Im planning my escape...my youngest daughter has 1 year left in high school then im getting outta this area...wanna head to the country within driving distance to the ocean...that is my dream : )
