# Thread: Women: How to Increase DHEA w/o Increasing Testosterone
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-how-to-increase-dhea-w-o-increasing-testosterone.7376/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Trinity — 2013-06-18T03:32:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-how-to-increase-dhea-w-o-increasing-testosterone.7376/post-88287>

Hi Guys,

Last time I posted for help on labs, JansZ suggested that I try the MLM DHEA and pregnenolone from nutricology. Thanks JansZ! The cortisol is still a work in progress (trying to complete a wean off Cortef), but I have seen substantial progress in my DHEA level. The issue now seems to be that it's converting into too much testosterone. Here are the results of 2 salivary DHEA / testosterone tests. For reference, my DHEA level was at the very bottom of the reference range prior to trying the MLM formulation of DHEA.

TEST 1: 4/21/13 (50mg MLM DHEA, 100mg MLM pregnenolone, 25 mg 7-keto DHEA)

DHEAS (saliva): 12.5 ng/ml (2-23)
Testosterone (saliva): 360 pg/ml (16-55) yikes!

After this test, I reduced DHEA to 37.5 mg, pregnenolone to 75 mg, and eliminated the 7-keto. The results ~6 weeks later were:

TEST 2: 6/4/13 (37.5 mg MLM DHEA, 75 mg MLM pregnenolone )

DHEAS (saliva): 11.2 ng/ml (2-23)
Testosterone (saliva): 236 pg/ml (16-55) reduced, but still too high

I probably need to reduce the DHEA further because it is converting into too much testosterone, but after finally seeing progress I also don't want to tank my DHEA again. How does a woman actually get DHEA to the top of the reference range while maintaining a normal T level? I realize that 7-keto is an option for women, but after having followed some of the posts on this forum it appears like it may not be very effective at raising DHEA on labs. For this reason, I've stuck primarily to regular DHEA. I am thinking maybe I should reduce DHEA to 25mg for a month or so and retest. Any thoughts?

Incidentally, in spite of extremely high T levels for a couple of months, I have not observed any major side effects yet (excess hair growth, acne, ect).

Thanks,
Trinity

### JanSz — 2013-06-18T20:04:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/women-how-to-increase-dhea-w-o-increasing-testosterone.7376/post-88357>

> [Trinity said:](/goto/post?id=88287)
>
> Hi Guys,
>
> **Last time I posted for help on labs,** JansZ suggested that I try the MLM DHEA and pregnenolone from nutricology. Thanks JansZ! The cortisol is still a work in progress (trying to complete a wean off Cortef), but I have seen substantial progress in my DHEA level. The issue now seems to be that it's converting into too much testosterone. Here are the results of 2 salivary DHEA / testosterone tests. For reference, my DHEA level was at the very bottom of the reference range prior to trying the MLM formulation of DHEA.
>
> TEST 1: 4/21/13 (50mg MLM DHEA, 100mg MLM pregnenolone, 25 mg 7-keto DHEA)
>
> DHEAS (saliva): 12.5 ng/ml (2-23)
> Testosterone (saliva): 360 pg/ml (16-55) yikes!
>
> After this test, I reduced DHEA to 37.5 mg, pregnenolone to 75 mg, and eliminated the 7-keto. The results ~6 weeks later were:
>
> TEST 2: 6/4/13 (37.5 mg MLM DHEA, 75 mg MLM pregnenolone )
>
> DHEAS (saliva): 11.2 ng/ml (2-23)
> Testosterone (saliva): 236 pg/ml (16-55) reduced, but still too high
>
> I probably need to reduce the DHEA further because it is converting into too much testosterone, but after finally seeing progress I also don't want to tank my DHEA again. How does a woman actually get DHEA to the top of the reference range while maintaining a normal T level? I realize that 7-keto is an option for women, but after having followed some of the posts on this forum it appears like it may not be very effective at raising DHEA on labs. For this reason, I've stuck primarily to regular DHEA. I am thinking maybe I should reduce DHEA to 25mg for a month or so and retest. Any thoughts?
>
> **Incidentally, in spite** of extremely high T levels for a couple of months, **I have not observed any major side effects yet (excess hair growth, acne, ect).**
>
> Thanks,
> Trinity

Consider staying on just one thread, it will be easier to review past communications.
If you choose to do that,
please copy and re-post here our communications from other places.

Consider doing mostly blood and urine analysis.
Reserve saliva for cortisol (if that, but good to get initial impression and latter, fine tuning)).

You may want to do those tests at LabCorp.
Ask your doctor for script for items that you want to review,
you may want to pick them from list here:
[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508)

If indeed you have high testosterone, may want to investigate for
Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS)
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PCOS](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PCOS)

The testosterone may also raise when taking medications that are
5α-reductase inhibitors
Aromatase inhibitors
In
that case testosterone may raise because it is (not used) not down-metabolized, (so it is not because you are making too much of it)

.....

# Thread: Vince Flynn Dies from Prostate Cancer
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vince-flynn-dies-from-prostate-cancer.7397/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2013-06-19T17:46:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vince-flynn-dies-from-prostate-cancer.7397/post-88433>

I was late to the party and just started reading Vince Flynn this year. I was hooked on the 1st book I read and have read every book of his I can get my hands on.

I had read he had cancer, but had no idea how serious it was. He died this morning of stage 3 metastatic prostate cancer at the age of 47. A little over a year ago, Flynn had said his outlook was good.

> Minnesota author Vince Flynn has died after a long battle with prostate cancer.
>
> WCCO-TV has learned that Flynn died Wednesday morning at United Hospital in St. Paul.
>
> Flynn has authored 15 novels centered around the character of Mitch Rapp, an undercover CIA agent. The majority of those novels have made it to the New York Times bestseller list.

[http://minnesota.cbslocal.com/2013/06/19/mn-author-vince-flynn-dies-at-47/](http://minnesota.cbslocal.com/2013/06/19/mn-author-vince-flynn-dies-at-47/)

The article said his treatment included hormone therapy, diet (no red meat), and exercise. In another article, he talked about new drugs, but wasn't specific.

### CarolMorris — 2013-06-19T18:27:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vince-flynn-dies-from-prostate-cancer.7397/post-88434>

Very sorry to hear this. I love his novels.

# Thread: Risks of Long Haul Flights and how to mitigate
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/risks-of-long-haul-flights-and-how-to-mitigate.7401/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2013-06-19T22:14:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/risks-of-long-haul-flights-and-how-to-mitigate.7401/post-88446>

Hi,

I heard on a recent podcast that when you travel 20 hours on a long haul flight then it takes 20 days to normalise the body and that during that period you are in a pre-diabetic state.

Is that true? What are the dangers of long haul and what steps can I take to protect myself, wife and three young children.

I know Dr. Kruse has a protocol that he follows that includes pre-flight supplementation, grounding and post flight activities but I cannot find the exact details and I am also interested in what works for other people.

Last time we flew UK to Australia we spent 1hr during second part of the journey showering, taking spa and sauna and it made a huge difference. I have never been able to sleep on these flights due to noise and general discomfort factor (being 6ft 4") despite cocktail of Valerian root and Melatonin tablets plus own plump neck pillow. At least I know now that watching films 16+ hours on the IPAD is not a good idea! And taking own supply of structured Spring water is warranted plus fasting!

Would for example a portable Schumann resonance device be a good idea? - If it is good enough for NASA cosmonauts should be good enough for me.

Any other advice most welcome and thanks in advance.

### Hope — 2013-06-19T23:54:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/risks-of-long-haul-flights-and-how-to-mitigate.7401/post-88449>

hi. I haven't flown 20 hours yet. I also haven't been able to take any of my own water, but maybe that is different where you are....so here are a few observations, i.e. my n=1

Lots of grounding is great, before and after, and drinking and exposing yourself to daylight right away, if your destination is in daylight when you get there at least. Also, sleeping in darkness before - being super strict - I believe has helped me have the best (i.e. least jet lag) ever, as well as seeing about putting bare feet on the metals on the bottom of the seat in front (or underneath you if you can reach) while in flight. This appeared to have made a huge difference for me even on a shorter flight (2 hours) I took earlier this year, though I had also gotten two hours of great sun each day beforehand, along with the other stuff. But I often would get headaches from flying or was really tired in mid-flight - well that time, I was actually able to plan a lot of things while on the plane, writing them down and thinking straight and clear - was amazing and the 2 hours felt like 5 minutes. Drinking extra water before and after travel is also good....and doing CT at destination before and after you leave.

Also I took a lot of extra Ubiquinol which was great, I think. Perhaps you can exchange back rubs, even with clothes on, and try to get real air if you are staying over (ie. going outside in layover cities) if there is time.

I also take extra iodine to help with the bad air and germs etc.

And I love using some lavender essential oil to help with relaxing during flight and after...it's amazing. I had the best sleep after a recent trip though I didn't sleep a second on the flight, and did watch some movies when not chatting with my interesting neighbors from Egypt.

I think in general, when you do the various Jack Kruse protocols anyway, before and after your trip bc it's a lifestyle, it helps a lot.

Congrats to finding this place and transforming your health!! I have ONLY been following all this since last fall and was amazed at the difference it's made in how I felt with the jet lag coming and going too. So great.

I have never fasted before but I do bring my own food now (smoked salmon, canned sardines, smoked mackerel have worked great, and then some nuts, and for veggies, I have taken sauerkraut still warm in a small food thermos, or any other veggie like broccoli or cauliflower). I may pick at their vegetables if there is a meal but that's it. I happily (though maybe I shouldn't be happy) share the goodies they give out with neighbors. And I only drink the water they have when it comes out the bottle that I can see them pour. Otherwise it's tap and awfully chlorinated and whatever else. The beauty of having done the Leptin Rx and still not ever snacking has made traveling so much easier for me food wise. I don't feel like I have to worry about maybe getting hungry. My body is now so used to the three meals a day (which I still do, maybe some day down to two?) and I don't get hungry in between, it's very freeing....when I used to be making sure I had a snack just in case I might get hungry so I wouldn't need airport or plane food.

IF you can, I'd also suggest a magnetico sleep pad (for when you are at home). It's the most amazing thing I have invested in and totally worth it. It will help you tremendously coming back....at least I believe it helped me. IN fact, I now so strongly believe in it, I consider it more important than any supplement. At least if you live in a high EMF area, as I do. So on a recent transcontinental trip with 6 hours time difference, I came back, and slept around 8 hours each night, through the night, from about 9 to 10pm at night to 5-6:30am in the morning. Never experienced such greatness before. I was still tired during the day a few days, but no weird sleeping stuff. Though of course adjusting from a Westward flight always seems easier...

As to the Schumann generator I have no idea, I don't own one, yet.

I too am looking forward to hearing what others on this amazing board have to share.

Good luck and happy travels


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Darwin's Doubt The evolution debate is about to undergo a paradigm shift.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/darwins-doubt-the-evolution-debate-is-about-to-undergo-a-paradigm-shift.7404/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-06-20T03:13:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/darwins-doubt-the-evolution-debate-is-about-to-undergo-a-paradigm-shift.7404/post-88461>

I heard this author, Dr Stephen Meyer, interviewed today on talk radio. He appears to be blending some intelligent design into the fabric of the Cambrian Explosion of life.
I did not hear anything about life springing from in the cold depths of the ocean. But the interview was only about 20 min. Intelligent design does not really appeal to me, but I am open...but I don't think we will ever have proof positive of God or supreme power that orchestrated the origin of life over the Millennium of time.

[http://www.stephencmeyer.org/news/](http://www.stephencmeyer.org/news/)

"Darwin's Origin of Species launched a revolution whose scientific, cultural and spiritual effects are still with us. Now a new revolution is on the horizon. On June 18, the HarperOne imprint of HarperCollins will publish Darwin's Doubt: The Explosive Origin of Animal Life and the Case for Intelligent Design, by Stephen C. Meyer.

"For now but not for long, exclusively at DarwinsDoubt.com, you can preorder your copy, receiving a steep discount, free shipping and four free digital books!

"Dr. Meyer is the Cambridge University-trained leader and founder of the ID movement. His previous book, Signature in the Cell, caught the attention of scholars who previously had nothing friendly to say about ID. Even distinguished atheist philosopher Thomas Nagel admitted that it confronted him with a "fiendishly difficult problem." But Signature in the Cell considered only the mystery of the origin of life. Dr. Meyer's new book expands the scope of the case for ID to the entire sweep of life's history.

"In some ways his book resembles Darwin's Origin, with its methodical style, summarizing and synthesizing arguments and evidence in an unprecedentedly rigorous and powerful way. Or since the idea of intelligent design stretches back to classical philosophy, waiting only to be restated definitively in contemporary scientific terms, it might best be compared to Richard Dawkins's The Blind Watchmaker, which performed a similar service for Darwinian theory.

"Meyer begins with what Darwin himself regarded as a troubling enigma, a subject of doubt and even some scientific distress. It is a mystery from which subsequent generations of Darwinists have sought to distract the public's attention. Some 530 million years ago, in the event called the Cambrian explosion, there sprang suddenly into existence the majority of animal body plans (phyla) that have existed on Earth. The shallow seas of the Cambrian period abruptly teemed with diverse, exotic animals.

"Evolutionary biologists and paleontologists have struggled to explain this epic event. Dr. Meyer takes his readers on a journey through scientific history, starting with the discovery of the Burgess Shale by Charles Walcott in 1909. He shows how failed attempts to give a satisfying Darwinian explanation of the Cambrian explosion have opened the door to increasingly profound questions, posed by evolutionary biologists themselves, leading to a far greater mystery: the origin of the biological information necessary to build the animals of the Cambrian and all the living creatures that have existed on Earth.

"Here is a sweeping account, stunningly illustrated with gorgeous color photos, of the frontiers of the scientific critique of Darwinism and the case for ID. Exacting and thorough, yet remarkably accessible to the thoughtful lay reader, Darwin's Doubt introduces us to the challenges to Darwinism based on the study of combinatorial inflation, protein science, population genetics, developmental biology, epigenetic information, and more.

"Meyer explains how post-Darwinian alternatives and adaptions of Darwin's theory -- including self-organizational models, evo-devo, neutral or nonadaptive evolution, natural genetic engineering, and others -- fall short as well. He demonstrates that the weaknesses of orthodox evolutionary theory, when flipped over head-to-foot, are precisely the positive indications that point most persuasively to intelligent design.

"Evolutionary biologists studying gene regulatory networks and fossil discontinuity, among other fields, have come tantalizingly close to reaching this conclusion themselves.

"The Cambrian event, fundamentally, represents an information explosion, the first but not the last in the history of life. As no book has done before, Darwin's Doubt spells out the implications of this fact. Dr. Meyer stands on the verge of turning the evolution debate in an entirely new direction, compelling critics of the theory of intelligent design, at last, to respond substantively and in detail. The book will be a game-changer, for science and culture alike."

### GlenPDQ — 2013-06-20T22:20:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/darwins-doubt-the-evolution-debate-is-about-to-undergo-a-paradigm-shift.7404/post-88520>

Intelligent design would include extraterrestrial terraformers as well as a supreme being.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-20T22:43:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/darwins-doubt-the-evolution-debate-is-about-to-undergo-a-paradigm-shift.7404/post-88522>

^True, indeed. Can you say, Annunakki? Lol. This intrigues me because I wanted to get Darwin's works, and now when I have the funds I may actually do that and then purchase this mans books and compare the information. I think people are eventually going to start coming to strikingly similar beliefs, although there will be minor details. For instance, we have quantum theories, extra dimensions and string theory, the holographic universe, simulation theory, and then there are different Buddhist and Hindu beliefs (even among the practitioners, many different sects exist). A lot of them have similarities though. One of which is interconnectivity and consciousness merely creating itself whilst at the same time subjectively viewing itself. Gregg Braden has a lot of interesting work on this. As of right now I pretty much believe that we are all connected and very similar to a shattered mirror.... The shards are all 'individuals', but they are part of the same mirror. I view everyone of us as a shard of that mirror, an individual that is really just part of the whole - whether it is some universal consciousness, the universe, God, Brahma, part of a simulation, etc. Fascinating stuff though, I believe more so than physiology/nutrition and the like, although you can kind of connect them and make all that a spiritual practice as well.

**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LRichard12 — 2013-06-20T21:43:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-organic-cocoa.7414/post-88517>

I am currently doing the leptin reset. I eat 180 grams of salmon in the morning now with a mixture containing; 50grams of raw unsweetened cocoa powder, 50grams of coconut oil, 1 gram of cinnamon (w/200mcg chrommium (I open the capsule and dump it in)), 15-20grams of F.O.S., 1 tsp of matcha, some sugar free soluable fiber powder, and 19grams of fresh pesticide free raspberries.

Is the cocoa powder a good addition to the Epi-Paleo style of eating, and is it okay for the Leptin Rx?

### Inger — 2013-06-20T21:52:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-organic-cocoa.7414/post-88519>

I do think it is? Especially if raw and good quality. I cannot tolerate heated cocoa very well, even when it is organic and good brand (green and blacks 100% organic cocoa powder). IDK what it is but it pushes me too much somehow. Makes my armpits smell too. But raw organic cocoa is different. I can eat that without feeling "pushed" (it does taste a bit different than roasted). No idea why? I have tested this many times BTW.

### Hope — 2013-06-20T23:55:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-organic-cocoa.7414/post-88530>

The Optimal cookbook by Dr K has at least one recipe in it with raw cocoa powder...and he wouldn't put it in if it's bad, I'm sure.

Inger, that is amazing, your observation....

### Anna — 2013-07-15T12:31:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-organic-cocoa.7414/post-90136>

Not too sure in which part of the world you all are from and how this varies, but here in Australia, cocoa is a completely different product to RAW CACAO. Cocoa is a processed product waaay further down the line of manufacturing from it's orginal source, the cacao bean, with all sorts of sugars and refinements added. So in this case, do you mean Cocoa or Cacao?

**Posts:** 10

### Martin — 2013-06-21T14:12:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fructose-avoid-it.7424/post-88572>

[http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2013/06/130619164437.htm](http://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2013/06/130619164437.htm)

### Hope — 2013-06-21T20:53:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fructose-avoid-it.7424/post-88606>

whoa...nothing really new there but one thing...look at the two groups they studied, and what they ate:

Both diets had the same amount of fat, carbohydrate and protein, but the sources were different, Kavanagh said. The high-fructose group's diet was made from flour, butter, pork fat, eggs and fructose (the main ingredient in corn syrup), similar to what many people eat, while the control group's diet was made from healthy complex carbohydrates and soy protein.

I wonder what would happen if they studied folks on an epipaleo, light cycle minding diet/lifestyle...

### GlenPDQ — 2013-06-22T01:42:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fructose-avoid-it.7424/post-88631>

Is it just me or would the experiment have been more meaningful if both test groups ate the same thing except for fructose which should have replaced some of the carbs in that one group? Otherwise, in a knee jerk reaction, people will say it was the butter, eggs, and pork fat that caused the damage.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-22T03:06:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fructose-avoid-it.7424/post-88633>

[http://www.jci.org/articles/view/37385](http://www.jci.org/articles/view/37385) - fructose, but not glucose sweetened beverages leads to increased visceral adiposity and lipids and decreases insulin sensitivity in obese/overweight humans

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-22T03:09:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fructose-avoid-it.7424/post-88634>

I don't know if fructose is the villain it really is though. I'm not saying it ISN'T, but I've gotta look deeper into this because some people actually say fructose may be beneficial. Dave Asprey made some article about how fructose makes people fat AND stupid (bad combo), but then there is the work of Ray Peat and Ben Greenfield has shown some research that consuming _small_ amounts of fructose throughout the day did something beneficial - I don't remember it either had something to do with lipid/insulin metabolism or the actual metabolism of the body.

I'm also wondering about carbs naturally found in animal/fish sources (from organs/shellfish), I wonder if those even count compared to carbs from starches and starchy veggies.

### MeghanK — 2013-06-22T17:58:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fructose-avoid-it.7424/post-88664>

[http://thesmarterscienceofslim.com/...ructose-natures-energy-payroll-deduction-plan](http://thesmarterscienceofslim.com/community/#/discussion/2310/what-too-much-fruit-can-be-bad-for-me-fructose-natures-energy-payroll-deduction-plan)[http://thesmarterscienceofslim.com/community/#/discussion/2310/what-too-much-fruit-can-be-bad-for-me-fructose-natures-energy-payroll-deduction-plan](http://thesmarterscienceofslim.com/community/#/discussion/2310/what-too-much-fruit-can-be-bad-for-me-fructose-natures-energy-payroll-deduction-plan)

### x_danny_x — 2013-06-23T07:00:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fructose-avoid-it.7424/post-88714>

the more i read about fructose the more it is a deadly poison. specifically high fructose corn syrup.

### MeghanK — 2013-06-23T18:23:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fructose-avoid-it.7424/post-88746>

Dr. Jack,
Do you eat any fruits? Which ones and in what quantities?

### Albert83BCN — 2013-06-23T19:13:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fructose-avoid-it.7424/post-88752>

My bet is that fructose is detrimental when isolated from the fruit and put into manufactured food on epic amounts we could never be supposed to get. But frankly I doubt it might pose any problem to eat the occasional fructose on real fruit, that seems to be pretty weird from an evolutionary view. I believe we are supposed to pick a fruit when we see it on a tree and thrive on it! But come to think about it... when could that be feasible? When fruit is available, mostly in hot climates and when the season allows for it. That way, the way nature allowed it to be, you can sync to that seasonal availability of food and had an excess sugars that can take you out of winter mode and allow for reproduction. Not really a problem because any excess will wear out as winter comes back. You will even have some stored fat that you can use to cold adapt and survive the winter. I don't think we have a sweet taste affinity for things just by chance.

I think that 'the dose makes the poison' might apply to fructose more than most other substances we know about.

Just trying to connect some ideas from me, Jack and others.

### Anna — 2013-07-15T12:27:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fructose-avoid-it.7424/post-90135>

Love it @Albert83BCN - exactly - eating a whole piece of fruit, with all it's fibre, micro and macro nutrients allows the body to successfully break down and use fructose. Equally if not more important is the consumption of protein - protein must be consumed for the body to be able to breakdown and use this natural fructose correctly.

# Thread: Bright natural light during the day....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bright-natural-light-during-the-day.7427/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5

### Hope — 2013-06-21T15:57:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bright-natural-light-during-the-day.7427/post-88577>

So I am doing some research on vitamin D and came across an old blog post [http://www.jackkruse.com/what-can-you-find-about-your-own-health/](http://www.jackkruse.com/what-can-you-find-about-your-own-health/)

Reading through comments I found this fascinating gem by Jack....especially the part about working/living in semi-darkness or artificial light....I feel without even thinking about all the science behind it, I sleep much better on days when I got to be outside in bright light all day, like on weekends....when I was growing up, I always wanted to be outside as much as possible, even doing my homework outside on our balcony. I never knew then that I was influencing this. I just knew I HAD to be outside.

It's cool to be able to read this now and understand why this works so much better when controlled via light than carbs....bc food is only 10%, light is 45-50%....

Here's the quote:

I personally advocate increasing exposure to AM sunlight and max out the darkness at sunset by sleeping with an eye mask. Why? This is precisely how light levels control the circadian rhythm for cortisol in utero and why I use the light cycles in how I live after the Leptin Rx is completed. I am not a big advocate of using carbs to do it. Its too messy and does not work as well as the light sensors in our retina and lateral geniculate nucleus. This maneuver helps us reset the circadian cortisol cycle by allowing for optimal cortisol levels during daybreak and low cortisol at night. This also allows for optimal leptin function too because cortisol and leptin are supposed to oppose each other when one is high and the other low. **It is also key to avoid living and working in semidarkness during the day**. I have a radiologist patient whose cortisol could not be solved by four other doctors without steroid replacement. When I told him about the light cycle trick he was able to get off all exogenous steroids in 3 weeks and now is feeling pretty solid.

# Thread: Migraines and Magnesium
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/migraines-and-magnesium.7432/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-06-21T21:43:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/migraines-and-magnesium.7432/post-88613>

Thanks to the Hormone 102, I think I have figured out why, when I used to get migraines (and thankfully I now rarely do) monthly, coinciding with the start of a cycle, my then-local MD's infusions of magnesium worked (and drugs like Advil or Imitrex did nothing) to relieve the pain....along with drinking lots of water and the fact that the IV itself came in a lot of water. I wish that MD would have tested my hormones, DHEA, IL-6...back then. But oh well. It all makes so much sense now.....

I really hope some day, all MDs will be required to train in the kinds of things Dr Kruse is teaching us all here. Although I guess the Pharma companies won't allow it because they would lose so much business. Sigh....

Here is the quote:

These mechanisms are mediated by the hormone DHEA which is one of the major antioxident in the brain and correlates directly with effective sleep by lowering IL-6 levels. Higher IL-6 levels are seen in people with altered adrenal stress index testing and those with altered melatonin cycles. When melatonin cycles are altered it creates an imbalance in Magnesium-calcium optimization inside the cells of the brain. The result is **dehydration** and a change in the density of water in our CSF. This is very similar to what we see in pseudotumor cerebri. **It also may help you understand what causes a hangover headache and why re-hydration seems to help.** When the Mag-Ca balance is off it tells the astute clinician that melatonin and serotonin balance is also off in the brain gut axis. All the quantum physiology in this post has a common tie to the neural immune and endocrine systems and many of the diseases that still perplex modern healthcare.

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-21T22:21:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/migraines-and-magnesium.7432/post-88619>

I've found the same thing Hope... chocolate cravings are an indication for me that I'm low on Mg. I tanked up at the beach last week... and I felt great... but 1 day after returning home, I wasn't getting enough Mag to keep me from getting a migraine... then throw in the artificial light & the stress I came back too that is why I had 3 migraines this week.

I'm much more relaxed today... and just rolling with it...drinking lots of water, got some afternoon sun (not as much as I'd like) I'll be spending the evening outside having dinner and celebrating the Solstice... no complaints when you let nature nurture you....

Hey Gretchen - Thanks for the tip about chocolate cravings and low magnesium!
I've been addicted to 'mocha's' lately (quite literally) - I just can't seem to get enough of them even though it is a migraine risk with the chocolate AND caffeine.
But I did not know that the craving could indicate low magnesium.
I know it is really hard to get magnesium levels up. I know that CT in the ocean can really help but it is not always practical.

I have lost so many work days due to migraines the last few years. I'm lucky to be in a union that protects my job but still - you want to be worth something to your employer.

I have a frustrating dilemma: My headaches are caused almost exclusively from the stimulant medication that I take for - so called - "ADHD". For me it works as a great antidepressant.
So I have a choice - migraines or depression.
Anyway, thanks for the tip - I'm going to make magnesium a priority.

I would make the magnesium "magnesium malate" and get rid of aluminum at the same time... to this repertoire I would also add sublingual hydroxy b12, molybdenum @1000 mg/day in 3 divided doses and sublingual pantathene - in a high EMF environment, calcium is effluxing thus letting in any glutamates you might be eating - this includes meat that is cooked - the high EMF turns into blue light which acts like a carb, which uses 28 molecules of mag to break down one molecule of sugar (or carb or blue light which is what a carb turns into) - meat also uses up zinc/activated b6 (P5P) thus less is left to make BH4 which is required for neurotransmitters - not only that, but whatever crappy gut flora you have is generating lots of ammonia which then uses up BH4 to process the ammonia - so meat+bugs=less BH4=less dopamine/serotonin -

The molybdenum helps process the ammonia also - I'm also a huge fan of Life Extension's Super K formula -

My sister in-law can stop a migraine in 2 minutes using magnesium and b12, for what it's worth...

So, lower the toxic load of the bugs by reducing your EMF which feeds the bugs - take probiotics to balance your gut flora - I would make the protein count by making it all seafood - naturally I would use lots of coconut oil and bone broths -

Getting rid of the bugs is a huge redox-sucker IMHO - and zinc lozengers are fantastic for you if you're not eating/digesting lots of oysters...

Penny, I just wanted to thank you sincerely for this post. My wife has been struggling with debilitating migraines the last 10 years and I have been trying my best to figure out what was happening to her, to no avail, until I tried your suggestions!

Our life went from borderline hell (8month breasfeeding child, no sleep, migraines... ugh), to a happy place in a few days. THANK YOU.

The migraines are now way less frequent/powerful and we seem to be able to control them with heavy doses of magnesium malata and magnesium bicarbonate + some of the supplements you mentioned -

p5p (1 a day)
25mg zinc gluconate (1 a day)
2-4pills 1500mg mag malate per day
magnesuim bicarboate mixed in throughout the day,.
cod liver oil, 2Ts day
home made sourkraut all week
lowering extra glutamate foods (soy sauce, dried shitake, fish sauce,etc)
CT soon.

I've also started implementing Kruse's ideas as heavily as is doable. We currently have a 8month son, my wife is breastfeeding, and we live in central Los Angeles. 5G/wifi signals are everywhere.

**I'm wondering if you had a minute to recommend any other steps she could take?**

I am ordering hydroxy b12, pantethine and molybdenum now.

Little background:
My wife has struggled with poor health the last 10 years, been stuck in a blue light room designing in front of a computer. She developed psoriasis,
debilitating migraines (she would take exedrin for years). She HATES cold water and has never been able to participate in sports due to scoliosis. She was also stuffed with grains and dairy as a kid and developed ear infections and was pumped full of antibiotics throughout her childhood. Joy.

Now she eats a home made paleo diet (4+) years - evolving to epi-paleo the last 2months, and her health is improving heavily. But the migraines have persisted, so here I am, looking for the next step beyond the food... I've switched all the lightbulbs to red incadencent, installed Iris on the computers and we got our Uvex glasses. Oysters were found fresh at Lax-C downtown LA... And we're considering moving closer to the ocean if we can afford it.

My wife is off Irish decent, red hair, fair skin with frekles. Her redox is without a doubt, probably quite poor... but improving.

Thanks in advance for any help,

Gabriel + Amanda
LA,

# Thread: FAA may relax rules for using devices below 10K ft
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faa-may-relax-rules-for-using-devices-below-10k-ft.7435/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-06-21T22:01:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faa-may-relax-rules-for-using-devices-below-10k-ft.7435/post-88616>

[http://www.techhive.com/article/204...oking-to-relax-in-flight-electronics-use.html](http://www.techhive.com/article/2042669/the-faas-finally-looking-to-relax-in-flight-electronics-use.html)

They say:

The basic argument against using your smartphone during landing and takeoff is that all those devices whizzing and whirring in the cabin could interfere with the airplaneâ€™s navigation systems. But that rationale was shown to be false in a late 2011 article by Nick Bilton at The New York Times . Bilton visited EMT Labs, an independent testing facility, to see what kind of interference our gadgets were putting out that could help bring down a plane. The result? Nada.

....yeah, but they should also check the impact all that stuff has on the really important players..us!! Going backwards......going....gone....

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-21T22:26:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faa-may-relax-rules-for-using-devices-below-10k-ft.7435/post-88620>

Hope - so many people use these devices anyway during take off... once the flight attendant sits down - look around people pull them out and start playing on their iphone/ipad/kindlefire etc.... its been happening for the past 10 years...

the horse is out of the barn, its a bit late to close the barn doors - so to speak. The question - how do you mitigate your risk when you fly? the answer may be choosing not to fly... or moving to where you want to spend the majority of your time... etc... or you may push the mitochondrial rx hard to make recovery from the situation easier..

### Hope — 2013-06-21T22:32:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faa-may-relax-rules-for-using-devices-below-10k-ft.7435/post-88621>

Yeah, I know, i hate it all....what can you do. I really fly to Europe bc it's the best way. Would LOVE to live there......but no such luck for now. Otherwise, not much need to fly longer trips...

I just think it's so silly...it's all about the flight, not the people....

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-21T22:35:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faa-may-relax-rules-for-using-devices-below-10k-ft.7435/post-88623>

Hope you can always cruise across the pond...

### Hope — 2013-06-21T23:27:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faa-may-relax-rules-for-using-devices-below-10k-ft.7435/post-88625>

Ha, that would be a dream....though I don't know if there are any epipaleo friendly cruises?? Not sure I could bring all my food and keep it in a tiny cabin. LOL. But I have thought about it. It is, after all, how it all used to be....

### lioness7 — 2013-06-22T00:20:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faa-may-relax-rules-for-using-devices-below-10k-ft.7435/post-88627>

Not quite sure what their fuss is all about. I travelled to Europe recently and read in one of those on board magazines that most flights will have wifi soon but you have to pay for it. So, my question is .....does it really have that much of an impact on the planes's navigations system?

A bit off topic...Being a Canadian that has travelled to the States many times, less frequently lately as at our airport, there is a separate U.S. check.......and man, do they ever make you feel like a criminal. I wonder often how good the border patrolling is or if it is just intimidation. Upon arriving home once, I discovered five .22 bullets in my CARRY ON, (DH used it to go hunting a while before) and this was after an x ray and hand search..no one noticed going there or coming back. I almost fainted thinking of the potential repercussions. Yet at the same time, we both were called back by border patrol when some genius noticed that my passport last name had a space and his did not....haha, worry about the space whilst I walk on board with bullets you did not detect despite your searches......makes me laugh hilariously!!!!!!! I doubt wifi interference is an issue. They just had to figure out a system from which they can profit like they have recently charging extra for isle and window seats. But then again, I know zero about airplane navigation. Just wondering......

Yes, very true....I haven't not followed rules, mostly bc I only have an iPhone and don't use it much....I do think it's a gimmick perhaps bc tests showed it doesn't interfere. As to the border patrol...hm.....that is so weird. I have had beets taken away, sliced and in a glass, fermented. I was upset for sure. And you 'got through' with that. Perhaps they thought it was something else? Who knows.

### caroline — 2013-06-22T03:18:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/faa-may-relax-rules-for-using-devices-below-10k-ft.7435/post-88636>

**How much is balance a part of achieving optimal athletic abilities?**

[http://www.mymadmethods.com/articles/mobility-articles/1269-balance-is-power](http://www.mymadmethods.com/articles/mobility-articles/1269-balance-is-power)

For the recreational athelete as well as elite athletes...we have some on this forum...this balance training seems to accelerate the abilities of everyone in only 8 weeks. He says runners run faster!! Now who would not want that?

It seems like it is more like brain training rather than muscle training. It seems elite athletes emphasize muscle training to excel but what would happen if brain exercise training were incorporated into the mix?

Something more for Barry, Ben, Meghank, BigPapaChakra777 & newcomer erixsparhawk to consider in pushing the brain & body to the max in endurance competition.

Be sure to scroll down to the video demonstration.

Anyone ready for a trip to Park City, Utah?

Thanks for posting this, now I have to actually go
and read through it. If you haven't already, look into Z-Health, it's another aspect I'd like to engage myself in, it's more about training the neruomuscular pathways for proper movement and utilization of all muscle fibers and it has promising results.

### Dextery — 2013-06-23T03:00:15+0300

### colt — 2013-06-23T03:09:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-balance-a-part-of-achieving-optimal-fitness.7441/post-88697>

\*\*\*I am not convinced that innate abilities other than healthy and normal
nervous and musculoskeletal systems play a major role in the ability to
balance. The ability to balance and carry out any demanding motor skills
depend heavily on learning via optimal practice and mental attitude towards
the task. Some of the experienced gymnasts whom we tested were exceptional
in being able to assume balance rapidly, even with very narrow foot spacing
and with visual cues removed. Those who were not gymnasts but who had done
a great deal of ball balancing were far inferior to gymnasts and martial
artists in these tests. Some sedentary students were also superior to ball
balancers, but in their case their ability not to become anxious, muscularly
tense and apprehensive about the tests seemed to play some role in their
motor abilities in these tests.

There is no simple answer to your question about ability to rapidly manage
stabilisation, since each individual seems to rely on different strategies
and since one's childhood and youthful experiences play a significant role in
imparting balancing capabilities. Some of the most interesting research in
sports science is now taking place in motor control, but not too much of the
complex information emerging from some of the fascinating work there is
filtering down to the general world of sports training. Consequently, far
too many personally held beliefs and commercialised balancing systems and
schemes continue to dominate the world of fitness and rehabilitation.

So far, the research which is available seems to show that the balancing and
stabilisation abilities are highly sport specific, so that wobble boards and
physio balls do not appear to offer the competitive athlete any noticeable
advantage over balancing ability developed within the sport itself or in
other sports involving similar types of stabilisation. Maybe others have
come across some well-researched proof (and not anecdotal comments) that
balls and boards do offer some training advantages beyond basic early
rehabilitation. If so, please share it with us.

Dr Mel C Siff
Denver, USA

From a different site

Adequate has come to mean maximal to me over the years. If you first look at the response side of the equation,more than at the stimulus side,the only way to have a maximal response there is to have a maximal stimulus here. Maximal response means maximal recovery from the stimulus too. As soon as the stimulus becomes sub max in any of its parameters,then it emerges the need to fill the gap with timing of the next stim,recovery means,tempo,and similar.
Volume then can only be dictated by the ability of the system to engage in and dis-engage from maximal input/output situations.

Velocity!!

### colt — 2013-06-23T03:23:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-balance-a-part-of-achieving-optimal-fitness.7441/post-88699>

Want to improve on a marathon or tri? Get faster!!! Speed reserve

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-23T04:09:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-balance-a-part-of-achieving-optimal-fitness.7441/post-88705>

Mel C Siff is incredible, people should check out his books (Super Training, I believe is the title). That being said, you have to look at everything. People should look at Z-Health, I think the nervous system is just as important as the muscles themselves. In fact, look up Elliott Hulse or Strength Camp on youtube - he is a professional strongman and basically a philosopher - he does tons of work on his nervous system, grounding, bio-energetic releases, etc. and after incorporating his alternative approach, he is now stronger at a lighter body weight and he used to do steroids.... basically he got drastically stronger. Really I'm just saying that this style of balance training may be a great \*addition\* to other forms of training - especially for what I do - Jiu-Jitsu, balance and coordination along with patience and fundamental skill trump any strength, endurance, or maximal power/speed output. There's some powerlifter dude who started training with us shortly before I had to cancel my membership for a couple months (until this upcoming month) and he consistently went against our Master who probably weighed 30-40lbs less and doesn't lift weights or run - all he does is practice the sport, stretch, and do some almost yogic style activities and he dominates everyone in the gym (even has an undefeated amateur MMA record). I'd rather be coordinated, flexible, balanced, and have the reflexes of some crazy ninja cat than be super strong/fast, and this is coming from someone who still kind of has a sick addiction to building muscle/body building.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-23T04:11:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-balance-a-part-of-achieving-optimal-fitness.7441/post-88706>

And I think this style of 'Balance Training' may be different. It looks like the vibration plate of balance balls and what not, lo;l

### Dextery — 2013-06-23T04:18:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-is-balance-a-part-of-achieving-optimal-fitness.7441/post-88707>

It should not be too hard to rig a 14 foot 2 inch tow strap between two trees with a step up stool to test the theory on oneself. I am also thinking using the straps that truckers use to tie down loads on flatbeds.

BigPapa...I like your integrative approach...thinking outside the box from what everyone else is pursuing. Be strong but train like a cat.

# Thread: Better sense of smell
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-sense-of-smell.7456/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2013-06-24T17:16:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-sense-of-smell.7456/post-88807>

I've been noticing some improvement in my sense of smell. Food is becoming more vivid, and as a result, I'm becoming even more picky about what I eat. I never realized that coffee, as well as tea, had such flowery undertones. It's not exactly subtle. And I've been noticing the smell of my natural gas cookstove. It smells almost as strongly as a propane stove. I've been avoiding the unlit fumes lately. And if I come home in the evening, I can still smell the bacon I cooked that morning.

Perhaps I should try wine again, maybe I can appreciate it more now.
Or maybe I shouldn't!

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-24T18:15:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-sense-of-smell.7456/post-88818>

Interesting... In all this time I've never lost my sense of smell. my sniffer is very keen. almost overly sensitive. I've always been able to tell if some one has issues by their smell - I'm also extremely sensitive to perfume/cologn. I hate it and it usually overwhelms me to the point it causes a migraine. its quite interesting...

and yes, good coffee and tea have wonderful notes you can smell before tasting, and smell as you taste. same with good wine. even coconut oil and ghee have notes as well...

### Adria — 2013-07-02T16:44:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/better-sense-of-smell.7456/post-89377>

Pros and cons of this I know oh too well! Smelling everything and everyone, good and bad. Just like our dogs says my DH.  Especially can't stand the body smells of certain coworkers. And their lunches, prepacked microwave meals smell like chemicals. Holding my breath sharing the elevator.

What a different life we lead...

# Thread: Carbon DiOxide - Why It Matters & How It Effects Your Health
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbon-dioxide-why-it-matters-how-it-effects-your-health.7478/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kall1 — 2013-06-26T05:49:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbon-dioxide-why-it-matters-how-it-effects-your-health.7478/post-88954>

All too often when your Doctor pulls a Chem Panel they over look the CO2 levels if it's within normal limits. Just to dig into the science behind CO2 is rather amazing.

Having worked in the medical field for sometime now I've seen first hand the primary cause of death in a hospital. What? over ventilating someone in the ICU. The patients O2 sats are down so geee let's give them pure O2 via an ET tube at 10 L/m on a ventilator that pumps the air for them. Which is the wrong thing to do. Russians use pure CO2 for patients with low O2 sats. A friend of mine who's a Doctor in Germany will put a plastic bag over a patients head to raise their CO2 levels for 20 -30 mins, instant boost in overall health.

In fact Submarines pump CO2 into the environment at 20 x's the normal levels. But CO2 is the cause of green house gasses that lead to global warming right? Oh? Then how come Green Houses & nurseries pump high levels of CO2 into their crops? ummmm....... shoots the whole global warming out the window:

[http://www.infowars.com/co2-myth-busted-why-we-need-more-carbon-dioxide-to-grow-food-and-forests/](http://www.infowars.com/co2-myth-busted-why-we-need-more-carbon-dioxide-to-grow-food-and-forests/)

All living creatures know how to regulate their own CO2 levels - frogs burrow into the mud to keep levels higher in times of stress. Mice know to burrow into a tunnel to build up their levels to maintain health. Queen Bees live 47 x's longer than other bees - why? Her levels are 5 x's higher than outside the hive. It's estimated our current CO2 on Earth are hundreds of times LOWER than it was during Cave Man era. Another reason why we see so much disease than we did 20 years ago. Less CO2 = disease, low metabolism and so on.

CO2 affects hormones - why? Amines.... Makes measuring hormone levels nearly impossible. What's what? Not sure what that means?

Do you know where and how "TONIC" water got it's name? It'll floor you how this was started as a medical treatment. Simple Sparkling Water given to Pt's with TSA or Strokes - are not suffering the horrible downsides you would normally see. that was discovered in the later 1800's! Doctors are keeping it from patients.

Most patients post op in Germany are given doses of CO2 - brain health is greatly improved & less infections and more.......

What's missing in 100% of US HBOT centers? CO2 ! In Europe they inject CO2 into HBOT tubes - huge difference in the cures seen from this simple treatment.

I use a plastic bag over my head when I'm CT'ing to really boost the healing effects of both

Here's Dr Peat, PhD to explain it better than anyone I know.

CO2 & Your Health:

\[video=youtube;AZkGxrntmTE\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AZkGxrntmTE&feature=player\_embedded\[/video\]

Peat says chronic saturation of cells by CO2 or bicarbonate increases the number of mitochondria, increases the ratio of NADH to NAD, puts the tissues into a more chemically reduced environment, increases bone density, forces tissues to rehydrate, and increases thyroid and liver function. Would increasing the percentage of CO2 we breathe enhance the PPP pathway and increase the length of telomeres? I wanna know!

Peat speaks about blue-light radiation and wearing blue-light blockers and using red or orange lights to saturated your cells and increase function of mitochondria; he speaks of serotonin like Dr. Kruse speaks of dopamine and oxytocin; He talks about cellular regeneration (where Dr. Kruse talks about autophagy during sleep) during deep sleep; just so much stuff. **Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-help-dhea-or-melatonin.7480/post-88981>

What are the most important labs to be done? Hes going to docs next week and i want to send him with a list of labs to show his doctor otherwise he will prob just draw a CBC and BMP

### Shijin13 — 2013-06-26T17:16:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-help-dhea-or-melatonin.7480/post-88982>

> [Trey625 said:](/goto/post?id=88981)
>
> What are the most important labs to be done? Hes going to docs next week and i want to send him with a list of labs to show his doctor otherwise he will prob just draw a CBC and BMP

at minimum
Vitamin D (25 Hydroxy)
HsCRP
DHEA-S

If you can get the following (Check with Insurance to find out what labs they'll cover)
Berkley, NMR or VAP LIpid panel
Fasting blood glucose Test # 001818, CPT Code 82947
CBC Test # 005009, CPT Code 85025
Comp Metabolic Panel Test # 322000, CPT code 80053
Thyroid:
TSH, Test # 004259, CPT Code 84443
Free T4, Test # 001974, CPT code 84439
Free T3, Test # 010389, CTP Code 84481
RT3, Test # 070104, CPT 84482
Total T4 (Dzugan requested) Test # 001149 CPT Code 84436
Total T3 (Dzugan requested) Test # 002188, CPT code 84480
Progestrone, Test # 004312, CPT Code 84144
Estradiol, Sensitive, Test # 140244, CPT Code 82670
Total Estrogens Test# 004549, CPT Code 82672
SHGB Test #082016 CPT Code: 84270
Testosterone Free Direct, & serum with Total Testosterone: Test # 140103, CPT Codes 84402, 84403
Prolactin Test # 004465, CPT code 84146
cortisol Test # 004051, CPT Code 82533
DHEA-S Test # 004020, CPT Code 82627
IGF-1 Test #010636, CPT Code 84305
RBC magnesium Test # 080283, CPT Code 83735
Vitamin D25 Hyrdoxy Test # 081950, CPT code 82306
Serotonin Test# 120204, CPT Code 84260 (requires frozen specimens)
Pregnenolone Test# 140707, CPT Code 84140 (requires Frozen Specimens)

### Trey625 — 2013-06-26T17:45:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-help-dhea-or-melatonin.7480/post-88985>

Thanks so much for your help...this forum is amazing, everyone is so helpful and supportive of one another. Thank you!!!!

# Thread: My first time with ice on chest and cold water - Yikes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-time-with-ice-on-chest-and-cold-water-yikes.7485/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-06-26T23:24:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-time-with-ice-on-chest-and-cold-water-yikes.7485/post-89008>

I worked myself up to 60 minutes with about 25 lbs of ice on my stomach and chest.

According to JK's protocol you can add water to the tub once you get to that point.

Well today was the first time that I did it with water from the coldest setting out of the faucet with the ice on my chest.

O good Lord was it so much harder than just the ice on the chest.

I quit after 25 minutes.

The worst part is that the water wasn't even that cold - 78 degrees.

### nonchalant — 2013-06-27T00:53:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-time-with-ice-on-chest-and-cold-water-yikes.7485/post-89018>

Fire, the first time I went into the cold tub, it was in the 70's, and I stayed in 10 minutes. Or maybe it was 7 minutes.
Anyway, the tub is way different from ice packs, or face dunking. That said, it's the only way I go now. Last winter I stayed in cold rooms, so I was shivering on and off all day, but that had nowhere near the impact that CT in a tub has. Nothing else affects my hormones like the cold tub does.

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-27T15:07:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-time-with-ice-on-chest-and-cold-water-yikes.7485/post-89050>

Do u put ice on your chest and stomach as well as JK suggests while in the water?

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=89018)

> Fire, the first time I went into the cold tub, it was in the 70's, and I stayed in 10 minutes. Or maybe it was 7 minutes.
> Anyway, the tub is way different from ice packs, or face dunking. That said, it's the only way I go now. Last winter I stayed in cold rooms, so I was shivering on and off all day, but that had nowhere near the impact that CT in a tub has. Nothing else affects my hormones like the cold tub does.

### nonchalant — 2013-06-27T15:40:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-time-with-ice-on-chest-and-cold-water-yikes.7485/post-89051>

> [fireforlunch said:](/goto/post?id=89050)
>
> Thanks for posting.
>
> Do u put ice on your chest and stomach as well as JK suggests while in the water?

Never!!!
Oops, don't tell Jack...

I have iced my back many times, and feet and even my tummy a few times, but never my chest, and not while in the tub.
However, I do get in a tub that is just above freezing... so yeah, perhaps you could say I ice my chest. ;-)
Actually, it is my upper back that objects most strenuously to the cold. I use all the ice I buy to bring down the water temp.

### fireforlunch — 2013-06-27T15:58:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-time-with-ice-on-chest-and-cold-water-yikes.7485/post-89053>

How long did it take u to get to just above freezing water from 70 degrees for 10 minutes?

### nonchalant — 2013-06-27T16:22:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-first-time-with-ice-on-chest-and-cold-water-yikes.7485/post-89057>

I would guess ... about a year? But I stayed at 50-55F for a long time. I took a lot of convincing to try lower temps. Now when I get out of the tub it's always below 50F. And I stay in for at least an hour.

I would have to look at old posts to make sure, but I think it took me a month or two to get down into the 50's. I would get accustomed to a particular temp, then go down a couple degrees, and have to get used to that new temp. Ok, I'll admit it, I was a wimp!

### MeghanK — 2013-06-27T16:45:45+0300

# Thread: Source Field - What The Bleep We Don't Know & Should
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/source-field-what-the-bleep-we-dont-know-should.7492/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### kall1 — 2013-06-28T06:03:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/source-field-what-the-bleep-we-dont-know-should.7492/post-89113>

.

This video presentation may step on some toes - where did we come from? How did everything evolve? What is life? Plus - soooo much more....
The following video is an hour long segment of a 16 hour presentation over a 3 day period. Mind blowing research that no one has discussed before.

M-Water has undergone a subtle transformation through duplicating the forces of nature under ideal circumstances and the main difference between activated M-Water and any other type of water lies in the change of clusters of water molecules to a smaller seize, thus facilitating the transfer of water via so-called "aquaporin" channels into any cell and facilitating cellular hydration. (The discovery of aquaporin channels in cell membranes was awarded with a Nobel Prize in 2003.)

M-Water is sometimes referred to as “Primordial Water”. It is originally sourced from artesian springs in the Cascade Mountain range of Oregon. It is then subjected to a proprietary method of concussive and low-level electromagnetic forces resulting in a restructuring of the water molecules into smaller and more stable “clusters”. This “activated” or “energized” water forms crystals that show greater clarity when visualized at freezing temperatures using high precision microscopy. A study has shown M-Water to be readily available to cells and highly efficient at facilitating hydration and detoxification.

Independent clinical studies prove that drinking sufficient amounts of M-Activated Water™ created by adding small amounts of M-WaterÂ® to clean drinking water significantly increases cellular hydration within a few days and then stabilizes at higher levels over extended periods of time. In addition to increased energy and more available oxygen, drinking plenty of M-Activated Water™ results in: better absorption and utilization of nutrients, decreased inflammation, more coherent brain chemistry, a better functioning immune system, more efficient digestion and elimination, clearer thinking, more balanced emotions, improved athletics and sports and over all improved cellular chemistry.

M-WaterÂ® is a micro-structured water that contains high levels of stored energy and more available oxygen. Our patented M-Power Aqua TechnologyÂ® is utilized to transform ultra purified water using the revolutionary ElixÂ® system from MilliporeÂ® with the addition of small amounts of minerals from ancient clay sources. NMR O17 spectroscopic analysis proves that M-WaterÂ® contains much smaller H2O clusters than the same water that has not been transformed with Health Breakthroughs patent pending technology. Based on water memory transfer, ordinary clean drinking water is transformed with the addition of small amounts of M-WaterÂ®, with the stability of both M-WaterÂ® and the water it is added to – remaining stable with smaller H2O clusters for several months and even years.

These "ideal", smaller clusters of water molecules in M-Water also form a power grid of stored energy, which can be measured in (milli-)watts, a measure of stored energy. It also represents the donation of electrons and oxygen.

With the so-called Fenestra Analysing method (USA), using various parameters to test water in vitro and in vivo, it was found that the wattage of M-Water is hundreds of times higher than any other water ever tested; a direct reflection of the stored energy available to the body!

In 2004 a study was done, using the Fenestra Analysing method, with the conclusion that >90% of all people tested showed cellular dehydration, and that after drinking M-Water daily for a month, everyone who partook in the study, showed an increase in cellular hydration, the average being 22.3%.

In 2005 a follow-up study was done, this time over a six months period and with Core Water added. Again everyone who partook showed an increase in cellular hydration, the average being 35%, this time. M-Water is the only product to optimise cellular hydration, which is validated by clinical studies.

With the proven ability of M-Water to move freely into and out of cells this has to imply that M-Water plays a role in balancing the positive and negative charges of cell membranes.

The state of hydration also plays a major role with pH balance. Whereas the body fluids, like saliva, may show no sign of acidity, this is by no means a reflection on intra-cellular pH. Cells may be very acid, especially as a result of poor detoxification and subsequent accumulation of acid waste products.

Core Water essentially has the same properties as M-Water, but is 100 times more vibrationally potent. When the vibration of water is totally biocompatible, as in M- and Core Water, it has great organising power on cellular metabolism and hence will promote health and longevity. Increasing the strength of biocompatible vibration in Core Water has shown to dramatically increase cellular hydration (with all its metabolic benefits), when taken in conjunction with M-Water.

# Thread: The Best Bio-Hacking Gear?

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-06-30T19:35:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-best-bio-hacking-gear.7519/post-89246>

Hey everyone! Hope everyone's been doing well lately. I was just wondering if anyone knew of any interesting bio-hacker gear/tech that would be a wise purchase? I'm talking about anything that can help quantify various markers, somehow increase performance/well-being, etc. Personally one I would love to utilize is 23andMe, but I'll do that eventually.

I found two things that I'd love to purchase in the future, and maybe some people here would be interested in these devices:
[http://www.akersbiosciences.com/products/us/metron/index.php](http://www.akersbiosciences.com/products/us/metron/index.php) - this measures ketones released in your breath. Much more accurate than ketone test strips, although I believe that maybe if you're really keto-adapted you might not show a ton of ketones through your breath, but maybe I'm wrong. I'd love to get this to experiment with some ideas I have in my head

[http://www.akersbiosciences.com/products/us/vivo.php](http://www.akersbiosciences.com/products/us/vivo.php) - this is from the same company and measures oxidative stress through your breath. I think that'd be another great device to have. I haven't really gotten into tracking various bio-markers and what not yet, but even if I had/end up having the funds, I think I'd rather have something like this to quantify oxidative stress or something else to quantify fatigue rather than getting some cholesterol readings.

Does anybody else own anything or know of anything that would be neat for some lil bio-hacks? I already know a lot of people own/recommend magnetico sleep pads, that's why I didn't post that. Personally, before I'd even think about getting one of those, I'd want a better mattress. I was also thinking:
1\. GreenWave EMF filters
2\. Grounded Laptop
3\. Switch all bulbs for amber light bulbs
4\. RestWise: A piece of tech to 'quantify fatigue', but I think that can be used as a proxy for stress/sleep/overall health

Anybody else have any others!?

### Dali Dula — 2013-07-01T04:51:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-best-bio-hacking-gear.7519/post-89274>

Blood glucose meter. Cheap critical info. Do you or don't you have glucose metabolism issues? Take readings over a week's time, morning fasting, post prandial after different types of meals, post workout. Testing annually at check up is not good enough. I know this first hand.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-01T05:32:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-best-bio-hacking-gear.7519/post-89275>

I don't have any issues with blood glucose that I know of, by that I mean, all my appointments with pediatrics and my current health care practitioner state otherwise (from simple blood and urine analysis), but obviously it isn't the most accurate testing and I've never done any 'fasted' tests. I'm just going off that, the fact that I'm young, have no family history of diabetes, and so on. I feel as though measuring blood glucose personally would offer some good information as to how my body is processing the fuel I'm putting in there, so that'd be nice. It would also be cool to experiment with how I can change things up and quantify the effects, that's why I originally wanted a blood glucose and ketone monitoring system to keep track of that. I'm assuming you own a blood glucose monitor, can you recommend one?

red head lamp for taking care of tasks after dark w/out blue lights
battery operated alarm clock
100 gallon stock tank for CTing.
RO water
Spending as much time outside as possible regardless of the season

### salamander — 2013-07-01T19:51:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/left-side-of-head-clogged-after-ct.7527/post-89305>

Been doing CT for about a year and lately my left ear is stopped up and can't hear. Also sinuses on that side of head. Started after CT session and goes down a little and comes back every time I do CT now...for about a week. Also went through a 'detox' or cold recently when a lot of mucus was stuck in throat but seems like some of it stayed in head. Don't know how to make it go away. Even happened simply by putting ice on chest and abdomen and no water, so it's not water getting stuck in head. Really weird.

### beach bunny — 2013-07-07T03:50:16+0300

Even happened simply by putting ice on chest and abdomen and no water, so it's not water getting stuck in head. Really weird.

Hi Salamander,

I have huge issues with the sinus/brain/Eustachian tube of ear (right ear in my case). It may be that your immune system has still been unable to fully clear that infection. So when you do CT it stimulates your immune system to try to clear it from your head/ear.

It has recently been suggested to me to do some LYMPHATIC DRAINAGE to help move some of that inflammation out of the body. There are manual practitioners who can help move the lymph around to break it apart or they use special machines. Others use dry-brushing. Trampolines or Rebounders can be amazing for this (unless you have other structural issues that would prevent you from jumping/bouncing).

Not sure if that helps.

# Thread: Stroke has a new indicator!  The tongue!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stroke-has-a-new-indicator-the-tongue.7528/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-07-01T19:57:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stroke-has-a-new-indicator-the-tongue.7528/post-89306>

I have known about these indicators for years...but now a crooked tongue is another indicator.

Stroke has a new indicator! They say if you forward this to ten people, you stand a chance of saving one life. Will you send this along? Blood Clots/Stroke - They Now Have a Fourth Indicator, the Tongue:

During a BBQ, a woman stumbled and took a little fall - she assured everyone that she was fine (they offered to call paramedics) ...she said she had just tripped over a brick because of her new shoes.

They got her cleaned up and got her a new plate of food. While she appeared a bit shaken up, she went about enjoying herself the rest of the evening.

Her husband called later telling everyone that his wife had been taken to the hospital - (at 6:00 PM she passed away.) She had suffered a stroke at the BBQ. Had they known how to identify the signs of a stroke, perhaps she would be with us today. Some don't die. They end up in a helpless, hopeless condition instead.

It only takes a minute to read this.

A neurologist says that if he can get to a stroke victim within 3 hours he can totally reverse the effects of a stroke...totally. He said the trick was getting a stroke recognized, diagnosed, and then getting the patient medically cared for within 3 hours, which is tough.

\>>RECOGNIZING A STROKE<<

Thank God for the sense to remember the '3' steps, STR. Read and Learn!

Sometimes symptoms of a stroke are difficult to identify. Unfortunately, the lack of awareness spells disaster. The stroke victim may suffer severe brain damage when people nearby fail to recognize the symptoms of a stroke.

Now doctors say a bystander can recognize a stroke by asking three simple questions:

S \*Ask the individual to SMILE.

T \*Ask the person to TALK and SPEAK A SIMPLE SENTENCE (Coherently)
(i.e. Chicken Soup)

R \*Ask him or her to RAISE BOTH ARMS.

If he or she has trouble with ANY ONE of these tasks, call emergency number immediately and describe the symptoms to the dispatcher.

New Sign of a Stroke -------- Stick out Your Tongue

NOTE: Another 'sign' of a stroke is this: Ask the person to 'stick' out his tongue. If the tongue is

'crooked', if it goes to one side or the other that is also an indication of a stroke.

A cardiologist says if everyone who gets this e-mail sends it to 10 people; you can bet that at least one life will be saved.

### Hope — 2013-07-02T01:17:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stroke-has-a-new-indicator-the-tongue.7528/post-89327>

Fascinating. My MD has told me a crooked tongue may also be a sign of lyme issues.....

### colt — 2013-07-02T03:18:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stroke-has-a-new-indicator-the-tongue.7528/post-89338>

Doc correct me if this is wrong. I think neurology is huge here. The cortex fires down to the brainstem on the same side. Then from there the cranial nerves go out. The right cortex fires to the right brain stem then down the glossopharyngeal nerve to the tongue muscle. The tongue muscle is one of the only muscles that can actually push. So if the right side is not getting as much stimulation (as in a category I right in Chiro and Neuro terms) then it will not push as hard. This will cause the left to push more and the tongue will deviate to the right.
The palate is similar. It rises up. So the right side in this case would have less ability to rise. Looking at it statically we could see it lower if it has paresis or higher if it is atrophied. We look at the tongue all the time. It can tell what side of the brain is dominant. Thoughts?
