# Thread: More reason to be a beach bum!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-reason-to-be-a-beach-bum.7532/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martin — 2013-07-02T00:02:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-reason-to-be-a-beach-bum.7532/post-89320>

[http://www.dailymail.co.uk/sciencet...s-good-day-beach-theres-science-prove-it.html](http://www.dailymail.co.uk/sciencetech/article-2352159/Everyone-feels-good-day-beach-theres-science-prove-it.html)

### Hope — 2013-07-02T01:16:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-reason-to-be-a-beach-bum.7532/post-89326>

Thanks for sharing and I find it kind of funny....why? In Germany, they have been offering healing visits to the ocean for decades, where you spend several weeks at the ocean, being exposed to the air and water if possible and just being there is healing, though other modalities may be added like massage, other physical therapies like hydrotherapy based on Kneipp, a 19th century monk who healed himself by bathing and wading in ice cold water (an old method of CT I guess), as well as eating healthy foods like local fish and veggies etc. Though Americans of course consider Germany a socialist country, especially when it comes to healthcare. But many average people with maybe not great means have benefited from these at least three week long therapy sessions by the north shore. Even other European countries like at least Poland have offered this too. In fact, health insurance companies, which over there are not meant to be focused on profit, help pay for these visits if an MD considers it necessary, and people can qualify - last I heard - for these visits about every four years, and patients pay an affordable copay, like $40 a day. But even if not, folks just having 5-6 weeks yearly vacation where they can go up there themselves also helps...in pretty reasonable condos or private homes with rooms for rent, compared to here.

In fact, moms often accompany their sick kids, especially kids with respiratory and/or skin issues....I know a few that have done this even recently. It's a whole different way of looking to help heal people other than drugs and surgery...though of course it's different than living near the beach too which is the ideal. The downside of course is taxes are much higher and people make less money to begin with compared to the US. But they get to live in stone houses with less mold issues generally...i actually met a personal patient of Dr Dietrich Klinghardt today (who has mold issues and that gene issue Jack talks about in his lyme/mold blog from some time ago, and actually told me she sleeps not in her house but an empty church basement - with her husband who is also sick), the German MD in Seattle who talks a lot about EMF and mold and even he says people there seem healthier generally, though of course they have EMFs and eat a bunch of bad foods etc. but apparently less GMOs than here. Fascinating stuff.

But I digress. Just meant to thank you for this article. Always appreciate it

### caroline — 2013-07-02T05:02:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-reason-to-be-a-beach-bum.7532/post-89348>

Hi Martin ...... now you are taking my language!! If I had been any more relaxed this past summer I would have been comatose!

How are you anyway???? I am assuming you are running around outside in your speedos???

### Martin — 2013-07-02T15:28:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-reason-to-be-a-beach-bum.7532/post-89371>

Hi! Relaxed is good! I'm here, lurking away. Spending more time barefoot and stripped down while down in the garden. I really should take a picture - just for you. You're so appreciative of my talents!

### Martin — 2013-07-02T15:34:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-reason-to-be-a-beach-bum.7532/post-89372>

Hi Hope! The EU has some things going on over our profit-based system. It will change here, somehow. From below, certainly not from above!

I'm thinking of building a beach on my property here in the mountains!

Klinghardt is really researching this stuff. My mold doc in Atlanta studies under him often.

### caroline — 2013-07-03T06:12:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-reason-to-be-a-beach-bum.7532/post-89424>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lunch-ideas.7537/post-89396>

I'm good on Breakfast and Dinner but have a hard time figuring out what to eat for lunch. What do you all have for lunch?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lunch-ideas.7537/post-89397>

A can of sardines if I am out on the road....a can of sardines with some salad if I'm at home. Homemade tuna salad in an avocado.

### Dextery — 2013-07-02T22:08:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lunch-ideas.7537/post-89402>

### Pamela — 2013-07-02T22:09:10+0300

### GlenPDQ — 2013-07-03T01:29:13+0300

### caroline — 2013-07-03T05:24:52+0300

Usually pick up an avocado or some olives from the olive bar to go with it and a kombucha drink.

# Thread: Correct me if I'm wrong: Leptin
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/correct-me-if-im-wrong-leptin.7539/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-02T21:47:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/correct-me-if-im-wrong-leptin.7539/post-89400>

Hey everyone! I just wanted to make a quick post rehashing the things I've learned thus far, and hopefully the members here and/or Dr. Kruse can explain if I'm grasping the concepts. I have recently just finished every blog in the leptin series except for the one on Lyme disease and Osteoporosis, but I will do that tonight. I'm putting all my annotations into a binder so I can frequently refresh, and before I start the next series..... I'm looking through every single citation/video throughout the blogs and comments. So, I have a lot more learning to do on Leptin. I'm also planning on ordering Mastering Leptin (3rd Edition) in the coming weeks. Well, let's see if I at least have a brief understanding of leptin. \*not looking at notes, lol\*

As most of you know, leptin is the 'master hormone'. Resistance to leptin occurs before Insulin resistance, sometimes years before hand (I remember 5-7yrs being the example Dr. Kruse gave). Leptin is made from our white fat cells (but I've read it is also made to a certain degree by our muscles - Lyle McDonald's book on Bromocrpytine) and is essentially the connection from our fat stores to our brain, more specifically our hypothalamus. The hypothalamus controls our endocrine system, hence the important connection between leptin, our fat, and the connection that is sent to the hypothalamus. We receive leptin via our mothers colostrum (don't remember/understand how we receive it without breastfeeding, anyone care to elaborate?), and not being exposed to breastfeeding can literally change the methylation of our DNA. What is done prior to pregnancy, during pregnancy, and for the first six years of a child's life has drastic effects. This is getting off topic, though.... back to Leptin. Very thing, as well as very overweight people can and normally are Leptin resistant. You can check this with tests as well such as HS CRP, rT3, Vitamin D, o3/o6 ratio, and even your Leptin level (over 10 would be resistant). Leptin needs a minimum of four hours to bind to leptin receptors in the brain and other areas, hence why there is no snacking in the reset, and no eating after dinner/before bed. I know that Byron Richards of Mastering Leptin recommends 5-6hrs between meals - maybe that isn't necessary, maybe it is, I don't know. I've been doing just breakfast and dinner so no worries really, occasionally a snack of chocolate or an avocado and some cooked greens with butter after 4hrs from breakfast.

Leptin needs a minimum of four hours prior to bed to bind to its receptors in the hypothalamus. The blogs delving into this (Why The Leptin Reset Works blog) are new to me, and I haven't re-read them much yet. I know leptin has something to do with dopamine receptors (D2 I believe) and melatonin. This controls for pulsatile growth hormone secretion which can aids in fat burning/apoptosis, lean muscle gain, as well as autophagy. We need this surge in GH for our well-being. This got me thinking that one of the major reasons IFing may work, aside from refeeds, is that you're not eating at night thus giving your liver/pancreas/digestive system a break while allowing leptin to bind to its receptor and receiving proper GH secretions. Since leptin controls for all our energy in the body, it makes sense that there would be a blog on Osteoporosis because our exoskeleton requires massive amounts of energy to turnover. Leptin also controls fertility, and if one is LR, they won't get pregnant because the body will perceive a state of inadequate energy to sustain a life.

I'm still a bit rusty on how protein/fat loading in the morning essentially aids in regaining leptin sensitivity/signalling, but I know it has to do with the orexins, especially HCrT1 & 2. I know Ghrelin and these orexins, as well as other hormones, account for the food that we are eating and can essentially tell apart protein, fat, and carbs. We are also stimulating the vagus nerve which sends signals, stimulating these incretin gut hormones, priming us for digestion and assimilation. This is why eating breakfast every morning, along with no snacking, aids in leptin sensitivity and more energy and an easily sustainable appetite, because the ultradian rhythms of these gut hormones, namely ghrelin, all start working in unison and at similar times of the day. Hence why Dr. Kruse says it's surgery without a blade. We are using our vagus nerve and gut hormones to account for the food were eating, rather than light cycles and what not, we are using timing of food and macronutrients.

I'm beginning to understand how leptin plays an enormous role in the so called PPP, especially for athletics. Proper leptin functioning, as well as liver and thyroid, allow us to more readily and efficiently burn fat at the muscle level (peripheral leptin sensitivity) i.e. fatty acids and/or ketones, oh, and not to mention muscle is largely made up of intra-muscular triglycerides (IMTGs), hence more efficient use of fuel during exercise/competition. This also leads me to believe that fat would aid in muscle and strength increases even more than normal - Paul Jaminet in his book speaks about using sat/monounsaturated fats for muscle building, but this isn't even taking into account leptin sensitivity and more efficient use of fat by the muscles themselves. The proper orchestrated unison of the liver, thyroid, and leptin signalling also allows UCP1 and 3 to work better, so not only can we use fat as energy, use fat at the muscle, but we can also burn off calories as free heat rather than creating ATP from it. This is another reason why calories begin to lose significance. You partition them better with leptin and insulin sensitivity (glycogen and muscle vs new fat cells), use them better (as fuel), and we can even burn them as free heat!

There is a lot more, I know. I really want to memorize how exactly leptin works with the gut incretin hormones and allows the reset to work properly. Before I dig into the citations, I plan on re-reading Hormones 101, How/Why The Leptin Reset Works, and possibly the Leptin FAQS. I want all this knowledge ingrained in my brain so I can explain it to people, as well more easily understand how to utilize various protocols to my benefit. I remember a bit about pregnenolone steal syndrome - essentially our body is not properly using LDL's for sex steroid production and it is shunted towards cortisol production because our body will never sacrifice cortisol (which makes sense evolutionarily speaking), hence we get hypercortisolism (or is it hypo? lol) which leads to it's own problems such as increased truncal adipose mass, anxiety, etc.

So.... how am I doing so far? Looking at the citation list I compiled, I still have a LOT more to learn, lol.

# Thread: Hot foot bathhot shower  contraproductive
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lahelada — 2013-07-04T15:37:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89499>

Good morning.I seem to be fairly well cold adapted even though I only started something like 2 weeks ago. My question is this: I like cold, but I also like hot showers.I mean taking the two quite separated during the day.For the purpose of cleanliness hot feels better, for epsom foot baths I wonder if it gets absorbed better transdermally from hot wateras the exposure area is relatively small. Any experiences or guidelines?

### caroline — 2013-07-05T12:33:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89521>

Apparently epsom salts do dissolve okay in cold water but I tend to dissolve them first in a little warm water.

Are you only doing cold showers? you don't put ice in your bathtub and submerge? Most people will say \[I am one\] that cold showers are sooooo hard......

### Lahelada — 2013-07-05T16:38:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89531>

I am afraid it is the only option available at the moment. I only have a shower, so unfortunately immersion is not an option. I enjoy immersion more as well. Happy memories of swimming for "hours" in icy spring water. But I do use ice on my skin to cool myself down before hand so it is much more bearable to have the running cold water. I am okay with it on my front but directing the spray on my back costs me . Ice I can put on directly. Strange huh.
Thank you for answering my question about the epsom salts. I followed epsom salt
protocol of yore and panicked a bit afterwards.The next one I will do cold.

### MeghanK — 2013-07-05T17:11:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89536>

@Lahelada I have been doing a hot cold contrast shower... 20" cold, 10" hot.... cycling through for 5 minutes....
@caroline.... is doing a cold shower not as effective as submersion?
@ JACK...Do you have to shiver for CT to be effective?

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-06T02:34:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89561>

@MeghanK: I haven't been doing CT to the degree that many at these boards have been doing (will step it up soon), I just take cold showers and expose myself to the temperature it is outside no matter what in minimal clothing. I would argue from my experience and minimal reading into CT (yet) that total submersions would be a lot more effective than doing a cold shower, but you definitely still get benefits from taking cold showers - no doubt. Go look up the CT threads at MDA and one individual posts lots of studies to back up CT (because many were skeptical) and how even minimal exposure (contrast shower for a few minutes) increases your resting metabolism, burns fat, and activates BAT. That is also the reason shivering isn't _necessary_ , though it may have its own set of benefits, such as burning glycogen which also leads to beneficial hormonal effects.

### MeghanK — 2013-07-06T03:02:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89564>

Thank you BigPapa... how long do you do your showers? Do you do hot/cold contrast? Have you noticed a big difference.... I don't have weight to lose so I don't know how to determine if it is effective.....

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-06T04:11:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89568>

It really depends. I like to shower before work so I feel clean and what not and I normally only take a couple minutes in the shower in that case so I'm not running late. But any other time, I try and time myself and get a minimum of ten minutes, sometimes I've done them for a little longer. Sometimes I do contrast showers, for some reason there are some times that I feel in the mood for one, even though I really enjoy cold showers. These are purely my thoughts, but I've seen a lot of evidence for contrast showers/baths being equally as effective for fat burning, and I've heard of 'hot baths/thermogenesis' through Ben Greenfield for fat loss, and I know that our body adapts to regular exercise and we get less benefits, therefor a change in the regiment is needed.... Maybe the same thing happens with daily cold showers..? Maybe after you start accumulating more BAT your burning less fat simply because of the amount of BAT you have, and it would send your system a good shock by introducing a minute or two of hot water followed by cold again. Haven't looked into it enough to really know if that is effective or not.

In terms of weight/fat, I haven't noticed a difference, mainly because I'm already lean AND I'm trying to gain weight, and I don't have any calipers or anything to estimate my bodyfat, that would help because I would know if cold showers alone were helping me keep fat down while gaining muscle. I do notice benefits from cold showers alone, though, such as: (1) Increased alertness and 'mental energy' during and post-cold shower; (2) Only done the experiment once, but one time I took a ten minute cold shower right before lifting weights and all my lifts went up slightly and I was less fatigued (3) helps my back/neck pain and stiffness (4) Trying not to be explicit..... but it seems to get me in an erotic type mood shortly after, and it's not like I have some hormonal problems that would keep me from having a strong libido. So I see it as almost an aphrodisiac. I would really enjoy, umm, releasing some oxytocin with my fiance while taking a cold shower together, lol.

You should just track some things, go on quantified-mind.com or do brain aerobics and see how you perform after taking a cold shower, or even exercise directly after. I'd personally recommend cold showers to contrast showers, but again, maybe as I theorized up above an occasional contrast shower can burn more fat.

### caroline — 2013-07-06T04:40:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89570>

submersion is definitely better - I am going to listen to the C/T webinar again....I need a refresher!

Back to the epsom salts - Do you folks buy it at a reasonable price? I pay almost $6.00 for a smallish bag and the instructions are to use the whole bag for one bath.

long ago, on here, someone mentioned they were reusing the bath water the next day or two - what do you think? Is it still effective? the epsom salts I mean....

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-06T04:51:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89571>

@Caroline: I would also like to know how/where to get cheap epsom salts/magnesium flakes so I can start utilizing them. I'm going to look on this MDA thread, I believe I saw someone post something about where they get them for cheap, but I may be thinking about something else besides epsom salts. I'll post back when I find that thread.

In terms of CT/Contrast showers/"hot thermogenesis, everyone should look at this:
"Ben: Yeah, yeah. So your little icy cumberbun that you could wear to prom but they also, I found this this clothing company called Spartzy and its spartzy.com and what they do is they make this special fabric that’s a heated fabric and the idea behind this heated fabric they called it the T-X fabric material is it warms your muscles and by warming your muscles, it increases blood flow to the muscle and can step up metabolic rate in an area of localized blood flow and research has indeed shown that local heating that increases skeletal-muscle blood flow can also amplify fat oxidation, it can amplify carbohydrate oxidation so basically overall metabolism in whatever area is being heated and there’s a few different studies that have actually looked into what’s called the hemodynamic response to heat stress in in leg muscles and arm muscles and found that there there is indeed higher muscle metabolism when you heat up an area in that way. Its different when in response to cold so when you’re doing something like cold thermogenesis, you’re getting activation of like brown adipose tissue to generate heat to keep you warm and when you warm a muscle you get that increase in blood flow that steps up fat oxidation and carbohydrate oxidation just because of an increase in localized metabolism and this is probably why something like the protocol that we talked about at the Become Superhuman Conference a few months ago that we did where a guy named Ray Cronise was there, a NASA materials engineer and he was talking about how you get better fat loss results if you’re gonna use temperature for fat loss, **you get better results alternating hot and cold water** when you’re like taking a shower you know, 20 seconds cold to 10 seconds hot 10 times through, for example for a 5 minute shower. You get better fat loss results from doing something like that than if you were to do just a cold shower ‘cause you’re giving yourself the best of both worlds right. You increase blood flow, and then also the activation of the brown adipose tissue so ultimately, you know, this whole like hot bath thing especially if you were to follow it with like a cold shower, you could do that. You could also do something like wear one of these you know, cool fat-burner vests at work and then you know, maybe put on some of this this Spartzy fabric and you know, warm the muscle back up so, a few different ways you could play around temperature in that sense but you know, it’s kinda funny just because like you know, some people would do all this stuff and be like you know, whatever eat a hamburger for lunch and skip their workout for the day and it’s like we’re talking about the extra like 5% here. You know, granted in the case of Ray Cronise, he did show some data that indicated that even in the absence of exercise, doing this hot-cold contrast showers twice a day for 5-10 minutes, he was actually inducing 20-30 pound weight loss in some of the folks he was working with but again.."

Found that from this [http://www.bengreenfieldfitness.com/2013/06/episode-244-full-transcript/](http://www.bengreenfieldfitness.com/2013/06/episode-244-full-transcript/)

I'm thinking of just doing contrast showers now, with occasional full-blown cold showers before working out, then maybe occasionally throw in cold bath (can't afford ice baths, can throw in some pre-made ice, though) once or so a week for the 'cold adaptation' and activation of BAT and to deplete glycogen, enhance ketosis, etc.

### caroline — 2013-07-06T05:10:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89575>

Many people of this forum have noticed increased libido when doing C/T ....... just saying......

### Lahelada — 2013-07-06T15:42:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89596>

Glad my moment of panic sparked a lively conversation. I think I prefer to keep the hot and cold separate. I don't know why, it just feels right. If I had to find a reason then I would say that the effort involved to getting back to normal body temperature is what pulls fat out of the brown fat cells if you have them. Hot/ cold showers seem to short a space of time to make much difference. I mean the body does not feel sufficiently stressed to do something drastic like make BAT. In other words it is not the temperature introduced externally per se,but the duration of the "stress"and the work you give your body to get back to its preferred "normal" that instigates the BAT activation.
Being ergonomic by design in its use of energy, body can deal with lesser stress simply by shivering thermogenesis, which according to some studies I have read is not conducive to reactivating BAT, unlike non shivering thermogenesis. Just my few cents.

### caroline — 2013-07-07T11:11:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hot-foot-bathhot-shower-contraproductive.7551/post-89639>

I like your 2 cents ..... and I like your Einstein quote.........

# Thread: F.Lux .... do you also need the glasses?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/f-lux-do-you-also-need-the-glasses.7555/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MeghanK — 2013-07-05T04:31:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/f-lux-do-you-also-need-the-glasses.7555/post-89510>

If you have F.LUX on your computer... do you need the gunnar or orange glasses too?

### Melinda — 2013-07-05T06:04:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/f-lux-do-you-also-need-the-glasses.7555/post-89511>

You do when you get up to get more structured water from the refrigerator and the light blinds you! We unscrewed some of the refrig lights but it is surprising how many times I get zapped by forgetting that I need very low light as I move around the house. Over time I have gotten rid of the problems but it only takes one exposure. I put the glasses on about an hour before I want to go to sleep as they stupefy me in about 60 minutes and I don't have to worry about an unwanted exposure.

### Danco3636 — 2013-07-05T09:00:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/f-lux-do-you-also-need-the-glasses.7555/post-89512>

### caroline — 2013-07-05T11:45:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/f-lux-do-you-also-need-the-glasses.7555/post-89516>

### MeghanK — 2013-07-05T17:06:16+0300

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/f-lux-do-you-also-need-the-glasses.7555/post-89541>

I wear my glasses as soon as it starts to get dark.. in the winter time I' wear them as soon as I get home. I also wear them during the day when I'm at work b/c I work in a windowless office building and the only lighting is florescent bulbs and I get migraines by the end of the day if I don't wear them...

### Melinda — 2013-07-05T20:44:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/f-lux-do-you-also-need-the-glasses.7555/post-89548>

# Thread: MrPinkies lab results.. Thoughts?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mrpinkies-lab-results-thoughts.7562/>

**Posts:** 7

### MrPinkies — 2013-07-05T20:41:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mrpinkies-lab-results-thoughts.7562/post-89547>

Ok friends..

So.. They decided to run the tests that they wanted.. and my appointment is at the end of the month when we can "discuss" the other labs that I wanted and go over these results.. PLEASE if you think of something that I should have tested, let me know! I know the HSCRP and the reverse T3's are on my list already.. If anyone is curious, I am 125lbs lighter from 2010.. Even though in the last year I have lost a LOT of weight, I still feel that something isn't right just yet...

Here we go!

2013:

COMP Metabolic Panel:
ALB: 4.1 Albumin Healthy range: 3.9 to 5.0 g/dL
AP: 59 I'm not sure what this is.. but range is 27-120 Alkaline phosphatase?
BILIT: 0.7 Bilirubin Healthy range: 0.1 to 1.9 mg/dL
BUN: 11 BUN (blood urea nitrogen) Healthy range: 10 to 20 mg/dL
CAL: 9.3 Calcium Healthy range: 9.0 to 10.5 mg/dL
CD: 23 I'm not sure what this is.. but range is 20-30
CHL: 106 Chloride Healthy range: 98 to 106 mEq/L
CREAT: 0.74 Creatinine Healthy range: 0.5 to 1.1 mg/dL
GFRBF: 111 I'm not sure what this is.. but range is >60
GFROF: 92 I'm not sure what this is.. but range is >60
GLU: 99 Glucose? range 60-110
GOT: 18 I'm not sure what this is... but range is 15-41
GPT: 19 I'm not sure what this is... but range is 10-54
POT: 4.0 Potassium Healthy range: 3.7 to 5.2 mEq/L
PRO: 6.8 I'm not sure what this is... but range is 6.1-7.9 Protein?
SOD: 137 Sodium Healthy range: 135 to 145 mEq/L

Lipid profile:
CHD RISK: 2.8 I'm guessing that this is Total cholesterol/HDL ratio range <4.9
CHOL: 179 cholesterol <200 mg/dL (below 5.18 mmol/L)
HDL: 64 HDL cholesterol >40 mg/dL
LDL: 103 LDL cholesterol <129 mg/dL
TRIG: 60 Triglycerides Healthy range: 40 to 160 mg/dL

Complete Blood Count:
HCT: 42.3% Hematocrit 36.0-48.0%
HGB: 13.9 Hemoglobin 12 to 16 g/dL
MCHC: 32.8% Mean corpuscular hemoglobin concentration (MCHC) Normal range: 28% to 36%
MCV: 91 Mean corpuscular volume (MCV) Normal range: 80 to 100 femtoliters
PLT: 248,000 I'm guessing that this is Platelet count Normal range: 150,000 to 400,000 mL
RBC: 4.63 RBC (red blood cell) erythrocyte count Normal range: 4.2 to 5.9 million cmm
RDW: 14.7% Red cell distribution width (RDW or RCDW) Normal range: 11% to 15%
WBC: 7,600 WBC (white blood cell) leukocyte count Normal range: 4,300 to 10,800 cmm

To kinda compare from my last labs back in 2010:
TSH: 1.65 Thyroid-stimulating hormone Healthy range is 0.3 to 3
BUN: 12
CAL: 9.2
CD: 26
CHL: 101
CREAT: 0.60
GFRBF: 144
GFROF: 119
GLU: 88
POT: 3.7
SOD: 139
CHD RISK:3.3
CHOL: 197
HDL: 60
LDL: 118
TRIG: 96

Kate: Good changes Triglycerides down to 60 from 96. The lower the better.
HDL needs to go up to 70 or above. The higher the better. You can raise HDL by supplementing with niacin. Slo-Niacin is the product I use with minimal flushing. I take 500mg am and 500mg pm.

Bun/Creat ratio was 14 and now is 20 indicating you are hydrating well.

Suggested tests to run: NMR Lipid profile will give you the breakdown of your LDL total particles and then it will tell you if the majority of the particles are large bouyant that do no harm to the vascular system.

On the Thyroid side, TSH, T3, Free T3, T4, Reverse T3, high sensitivity Crp, Vit D3 serum level
Hopefully others will have more insight after the July 4 weekend.

### MrPinkies — 2013-07-07T19:46:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mrpinkies-lab-results-thoughts.7562/post-89666>

Thank for the eye opening insight, Dexter! I'm really happy about my triglycerides and cholesterol too.. I am curious why they didn't run the TSH like they did in 2010.. I will definitely ask for those tests.. I just feel like something is off..

### HoneyChild — 2013-07-08T12:57:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mrpinkies-lab-results-thoughts.7562/post-89694>

I've been reading up on magnesium lately and it looks as though most of us are deficient due to poor quality soils. Over the years the ref rage for RBC magnesium has been going down and down as is mentioned in the following link:

[http://gotmag.org/how-to-restore-magnesium/](http://gotmag.org/how-to-restore-magnesium/)

Look at the mag deficiency symptoms to see if any of them are familiar to you [http://gotmag.org/magnesium-deficiency-101/](http://gotmag.org/magnesium-deficiency-101/)

If you think this is relevant to you then I suggest you join the FB group: [https://www.facebook.com/groups/MagnesiumAdvocacy/](https://www.facebook.com/groups/MagnesiumAdvocacy/)

Good luck

### MrPinkies — 2013-07-08T20:34:07+0300

# Thread: Too high DHEA?

**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1

### Hope — 2013-07-05T23:35:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-high-dhea.7565/post-89557>

So I have been doing DHEA and Pregnenolone since April and it appears my DHEA is now above the top of the range.....as in 463 and the top is 325 in my range. My pregnenolone has nearly tripled from the mid thirties to 95,top is 188 so I have a ways to go.

Other levels as I remember are OK too, like D is in the mid 90s, HS CRP is still low at 0.2. Thyroid still or again at low normal range for T3 but no anitbods and no RT3 issues and I feel much better......I will share more details/labs later but am on the road and couldn't wait to ask on here....has anyone overshot their DHEA? Is that OK for some time? I sleep and feel like a rockstar - at least when at home (I struggled on vacation for some reason) when I have full darkness and my magnetico. I believe DHEA has something to do with it as well.

Would love to hear from anyone and will also reread the H 101 blog too....

# Thread: CT Newbie
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newbie.7567/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### beach bunny — 2013-07-06T06:00:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newbie.7567/post-89579>

I am hoping this really counts towards CTing because I am so excited about it...

I went camping 2 nights this week. Both mornings I had a huge BAB and did not get hungry until dinner!
The lake was still but really freezing both days.
First day was hot but not sunny so I barely made myself go in to the water to my knees.
Second day was extremely hot and sunny (35deg C) and I was able to get in and out of the lake up until my neck multiple times!

Although I think I got sun stroke from too much sun too quickly (also burned a bit), the combination of the hot sun (photoelectric) and cold lake water, as well as sleeping on the ground, breathing fresh air, no EMFs has left me feeling more energized than I have all YEAR!

Is this way of doing CT still beneficial?

### caroline — 2013-07-06T06:22:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newbie.7567/post-89582>

YES, YES, YES ...... that just sounds perfect! The more cold adapted you are and the more C/T - it seems we don't burn and get a lot darker. I had the most incredible sun tan this past summer and didn't burn once - even when in the sun for hours. In retrospect maybe that wasn't so smart - but I never did burn..... and I still am tanned even tho I am freezing!

### beach bunny — 2013-07-06T15:24:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newbie.7567/post-89594>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=89582)
>
> YES, YES, YES ...... that just sounds perfect! The more cold adapted you are and the more C/T - it seems we don't burn and get a lot darker. I had the most incredible sun tan this past summer and didn't burn once - even when in the sun for hours. In retrospect maybe that wasn't so smart - but I never did burn..... and I still am tanned even tho I am freezing!

Thank you so much Caroline!! I didn't realize one of the other CT perks was a better tan without burning haha! That is so awesome!

I am really far from cold adapted that is for sure, my thermostat is so out of whack! Even under that burning sun it took a while for my ice cold extremities to start to feel nice and warm. Throughout winter all my health issues prevented me from being able to shock my body with cold (except finishing with cold showers). So I have avoided the cold for as long as I can remember. I have always felt better in the warmer summer months/warmer climate until brain infections took over. Then I couldn't be in the sun either without feeling sick. But working on those too.

My top priority is to move somewhere coastal where I have access to ocean CT, sunshine and as much seafood as I can eat haha! In the meantime I'm still trying to do what I can to incorporate CT gradually into my life.

### caroline — 2013-07-07T11:16:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newbie.7567/post-89640>

Good Luck Beach Bunny with moving to a coastal location .... I hope you can make it happen sooner rather than later. As JK has said ......we really only have this moment .... no time is promised to us....... so pack your bikini an get moving. I know a great beach....

Why do you call yourself beach bunny? Is there a story there? \[that you can share  \]

### Hemming — 2013-07-07T13:35:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newbie.7567/post-89649>

FWIW, I've also noticed that I don't burn anymore and my tan does look nicer.

Something else I've noticed is that some of my "moles" (or whatever the right word is) that I wasn't born with have become lighter. I'm really curious to see if it continues and that they could almost go away.

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-07-07T18:32:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newbie.7567/post-89660>

That lake camping sounds wonderful! We did that, too a few weeks ago and I left feeling exhilarated. Three days of sleeping on the ground, being barefoot all day, lying in the sand under the sun, and super cold lake water is what I'd love to have access to all summer long. Since it's become hot where I live, my ability to do deep CT has decreased substantially. So I do what I can with what I have. Cold showers and baths. I add ice to my bathwater, as much as I can. I swim a lot with my kids so I air-dry off (usually in the wind, which always makes it feel colder!). I've noticed that this summer, I rarely need to apply sunscreen. Sometimes I just add a bit to my nose. This is the first summer I haven't had a single burn. I attribute that to all my cold spring soaking all winter long!

### beach bunny — 2013-07-08T02:27:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newbie.7567/post-89686>

@ Caroline: Omg you know whatâ€™s hilariousâ€¦ I tell my bf all the time that if our EMF infested condo ever burned down (be careful what you wish for right?) the only thing I would save is my BEACH BAG which is in the closet right by the front door! Hahaha!
(My bf used to call me bunny all the timeâ€¦and when I went backpacking through South America it evolved into beach bunny because I was doing so much beach hopping hehe.)

@ Hemming: That is really exciting about your â€œmolesâ€. It seems like it does wonders for skin health!

@Chrysanthemama: I know donâ€™t you love it! I canâ€™t wait to go back in a few weeks and I also wish I had access to the perfect natural oasis all the time. Iâ€™m so excited that you guys have noticed such a difference in the no burning.

Even though Iâ€™m nervous I think I need to step it up and really try to get into my tub. I wonâ€™t make the water too cold to start off. I will try it in the next week and see how I do.

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-07-08T02:43:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newbie.7567/post-89687>

Having had my own house, and the houses of my in-laws, and my husband's grandmother, and his uncle burn down, I can say that it's a remarkably freeing experience. We lost all our homes in a wildfire that burned through our town, and we were right in the crosshairs of there worst part of the burn.

We took our dogs, our bikes, our martial arts uniforms. And yes, my swim bag was right by the door, so I took that too.

### beach bunny — 2013-07-08T03:08:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newbie.7567/post-89688>

> [Chrysanthemama said:](/goto/post?id=89687)

>
> We took our dogs, our bikes, our martial arts uniforms. And yes, my swim bag was right by the door, so I took that too.

OMG Chrys! You just made all the hairs on my neck stand up. I cannot believe it... All of your homes gone just like that!
Is it awful that I want to be able to move so badly that I want this home to burn too? It's like what is wrong with me right?

### beach bunny — 2013-07-08T20:04:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newbie.7567/post-89707>

So I just attempted to do face dunking on my balcony for the first time! I used RO water with RO ice added to it. I did not measure the temperature of the water with a thermometer but I did not make it too cold just to see how I would feel.

I dunked my head a total of 3x for about 45 seconds each time. I have nerve issues (from viral/infection damage) and I felt the immediate tingling in the ones in my uterus from the cold shock. Then I felt the nerve in my right sciatica start to tingle and the sensation went all the way down my right leg (where there is another injury).

Now it has been about 15 minutes since the CT and I am feeling very sleepy and drowsy.
Maybe it is too much for my brain infections too fast

Dr. K is this dangerous for me to be doing?

### caroline — 2013-07-09T10:37:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newbie.7567/post-89749>

The one thing you could have done better Beach Bunny .....when you were camping - you should have gone fishing and then cooked your fresh fish on the Barbie ..... Now we are talking!

I just noticed that you are in Toronto ... I was born not far from sunny side!

Do you go up to Algonquin park? What about canoeing?

### beach bunny — 2013-07-09T15:41:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-newbie.7567/post-89764>

I have been to Algonquin yes. Have wanted to go back and canoe to one of the islands but physically not well enough for that exertion yet.
Our favourite park is Sandbanks because of the gorgeous white sand beach (closest thing I have found to Caribbean in Ontario haha).
We typically stay for longer than 2 nights and that's when we fish

# Thread: Desiccated Thyroid - Can You Share Your Experience?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/desiccated-thyroid-can-you-share-your-experience.7575/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-07-06T20:50:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/desiccated-thyroid-can-you-share-your-experience.7575/post-89611>

I talked to my primary Dr. briefly the other day and told her the situation with my thyroid (low T3 and High TSH).

She said she would probably want to put me on Synthroid but is open to Armour but we need to talk more - my appt is on Monday morning

I'm absolutely not going on Synthroid but will consider Armour or something similar.

I know there are several people here who do take a desiccated thyroid and was wondering if you could share your experience - good or bad.

thanks for your input

### Jonathin — 2013-07-07T00:42:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/desiccated-thyroid-can-you-share-your-experience.7575/post-89623>

Check your auto-immune status with thyroid antibodies tests.

Check reverse T3.

Keep checking TSH and T3 until the numbers get right or close to it.

If your doctor thinks, as most do, that Synthroid is a good treatment for lack of thyroid function I would be cautious. My doctor thought that getting TSH just below 4.5 was adequate (from 6.5) and I should see him again a year. I was self pay, ordered my own blood test and still got this kind of treatment.

For me, desiccated thyroid proved more effective at lowering TSH than straight T3 (cytomel) and I feel better. It has been a year of figuring this out along with a number of other issues. What works for me or anybody else though should not be a guide for what is going to work for you. Learn as much as you can as soon as you can.

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-07T03:39:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/desiccated-thyroid-can-you-share-your-experience.7575/post-89628>

Love my Armour - you couldn't pay me to touch synthroid. With Chaos having possible hypothyroidism - when we get to the pedatric endocrinologist - if she goes on meds it will be compounded Armour - or another NDT. If I won't use it, my kids won't use it..

### cantweight — 2013-07-07T06:17:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/desiccated-thyroid-can-you-share-your-experience.7575/post-89632>

I've been on 150mg of Armour for years....since about 2006. I was put on synthroid briefly by my GP, but holistic doc stopped it immediately. I have been happy with it, although still hopeful that I can get off it at some point.

2 of my kids also took very small amounts for a short period of time as well as cytomel and TG 100 supplement. Their thyroid function improved dramatically has has been stable for a few years.

### fireforlunch — 2013-07-07T16:59:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/desiccated-thyroid-can-you-share-your-experience.7575/post-89652>

Thank you both for posting.

This is exactky what i was hoping to hear.

Do either of have any ideas and convincing the dr if she is resistent to dessicated?

Thanks again

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=89628)
>
> Love my Armour - you couldn't pay me to touch synthroid. With Chaos having possible hypothyroidism - when we get to the pedatric endocrinologist - if she goes on meds it will be compounded Armour - or another NDT. If I won't use it, my kids won't use it..

### cantweight — 2013-07-07T18:57:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/desiccated-thyroid-can-you-share-your-experience.7575/post-89663>

> Thank u.
>
> Any ideas ob finding a holistic doc in a city besides just searching for a DO?

I believe you can search holistic board certified docs. Just be careful and take your time finding a good one....it seems these days there are some docs out there jumping on the holistic bandwagon.

The only opposition I have heard docs have about natural thyroid is that it has an issue with consistent dosing. This is very outdated information, and has not been a problem for some time now.

### fireforlunch — 2013-07-07T20:49:53+0300

I'm pretty sure she often uses a compounding pharmacy according to my mom so I will keep my fingers crossed.

> [cantweight said:](/goto/post?id=89663)
>
> I believe you can search holistic board certified docs. Just be careful and take your time finding a good one....it seems these days there are some docs out there jumping on the holistic bandwagon.
>

# Thread: When is weight gain or no weight loss more than just a leptin issue?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-is-weight-gain-or-no-weight-loss-more-than-just-a-leptin-issue.7594/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2013-07-10T04:16:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-is-weight-gain-or-no-weight-loss-more-than-just-a-leptin-issue.7594/post-89794>

I have been on the leptin reset for about 5 weeks now and I know that I have put on weight (no scales for me). I am making sure to eat my big arse breakfast usually within 30 minutes but sometimes it is a bit longer if I do not wake up earlier to take my thyroid meds. Keeping is low carb but how much, not sure since I am not tracking (eating disorder issue). I have not really started to workout yet, just some mowing and light walking and biking (trying to recover from an exercise addiction too).

I did just get 15 tubes of blood drawn for various test to see where a lot of the hormones are at. Thankfully I took my list of what blood tests I would like based on reading a bunch of Dr. Kruseâ€™s blogs and my doctor agreed to all of them so we will see where they are at and hopefully I got most of them. I did not do the saliva cortisol one yet. I can send out for that one but even if the cortisol is messed up, the basic leptin reset should fix that right? I will post my results when I get them and see where I need to do., if anything.

I am also trying the cold therogenisis right now. It is getting hot here and I do NOT do well in heat. I am hoping that the cold baths at night help me sleep better since we really do not like to use our window AC much.

I am going to continue this since I am pretty sure that I really really messed up my hormones due to years of chronic undereater and over exercising and how can it hurt.

Thanks

Nicole

### caroline — 2013-07-10T04:50:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-is-weight-gain-or-no-weight-loss-more-than-just-a-leptin-issue.7594/post-89797>

Welcome Nicole! Have you read the Leptin reset? it sounds like you are making a good start. You shouldn't be working out while on the reset - light walking is fine.
What is your EMF exposure? are you following the light cycles? are you drinking spring water?
Dr. Kruse's book is a great introduction to all of this - it makes it much easier to get started in my opinion. It is available in paper and kindle. The webinars are great too if you are able to join.

It sounds like you have a good Doc who is willing to be a partner with you and support you.
It is a good idea to get a diurnal cortisol ASI test done so you know your levels for hormones and cortisol and DHEA.
Limiting EMF's is huge as are grounding and paying attention to light cycles.

Keep reading and asking questions ... we are happy to help!

I just went back to check the leptin reset .....so none to very few carbs and 50 to 75 gr protein or more if needed.

### nicld — 2013-07-11T04:54:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-is-weight-gain-or-no-weight-loss-more-than-just-a-leptin-issue.7594/post-89860>

I am reading it right now and working on the EMF exposure. I do drink "ground" well water at home but not at work, I will start bringing my own water in. Not really doing any workouts per say, just mowing the lawn once a week. Summers seem so short here and I love doing it.

I am really trying hard to not stress about the weight gain and focus on the end goal of getting the hormones fixed.

Thanks

### caroline — 2013-07-11T06:16:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/when-is-weight-gain-or-no-weight-loss-more-than-just-a-leptin-issue.7594/post-89861>

# Thread: Foot swells after eating?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foot-swells-after-eating.7613/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Eve — 2013-07-12T04:00:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foot-swells-after-eating.7613/post-89918>

For the past couple of months, I've noticed my right foot mainly swells after eating. It comes down again maybe 1h or so later, but is prone to going back up. I'm on the Leptin Reset for 1.5 year and have Leaky gut (but it is slowly improving).
The only thing I've changed in the past couple of months is went on T3 only (previously I was on T3 and T4). Also, it is getting warmer here but it's not everyday. It's like 25 Celsius...I think that's 77F...and I'm not generally hot.
I drink 1 Gallon of spring water or slightly more each day and have since March? or around there.

What is going on? I'd like it to stop because before this started I bought some nice sandal wedges that I want to wear and can't get my fat feet into them!

Edit: I am also on Celebrex, a NSAID, which is my internal medicine doctor prescribed as my stomach was seriously inflamed (and nearly 8 months of eating nearly nothing but fish did not help at all)...It is helping (I take it with an acid blocker, which mitigates its wearing, long-term effects on the stomach lining), this is why I am not going off it yet. I looked up it's side effects and water retention/edema can be one of them....So maybe that explains it.

### Grizz — 2013-07-12T10:46:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/foot-swells-after-eating.7613/post-89934>

Eve,

I have 2 thoughts for you.
On your leaky gut I suggest you read the book, "Wheat Belly" by Dr. Davis.
[http://www.wheatbellyblog.com/](http://www.wheatbellyblog.com/)
Modern wheat is devastating to human health for many people, as detailed in the Dr. Davis book.

Do the Dr. Brownstein Iodine Protocol for stomach & thyroid.
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout)
Every cell in our body requires iodine, especially the thyroid, female organs & stomach. The toxins of bromide, fluoride & chlorine displace iodine from our cells causing health problems. Our thyroid cannot function properly on these toxins that have taken over our cell receptors intended for iodine. A [Pandoras Box of disease](http://www.thenutritionalhealingcenter.com/is-iodine-the-fountain-of-youth-part-one/) ([Part 2](http://www.thenutritionalhealingcenter.com/iodine-the-fountain-of-youth-part-two/#.UTGp-eyAuMw.twitter) )result from iodine deficiency. The book, [The Iodine Crises](http://www.amazon.com/The-Iodine-Crisis-About-Wreck/dp/098603200X/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1373614618&sr=8-1&keywords=iodine+crises) describes all of this.
Your need for thyroid medication could be reduced or eliminated entirely if you were getting enough iodine. Dr. Brownstein recommends 25 to 50mg a day of iodoral or Lugols with required co-supplements to overcome the toxins that surround us every day that are in our food, water, furniture, bedding. We live in a sea of these toxins.

Grizz

# Thread: High Progesterone in a female
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-progesterone-in-a-female.7618/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-07-12T17:30:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-progesterone-in-a-female.7618/post-89956>

I was wondering if anyone here has ever experienced high PG levels in the follicular phase of their cycle? I had a blood and US test yesterday, my day 10 and the PG level came back many times the normal, at 10. Estrogen levels were in the normal, low range for the F phase.

MD thinks I didn't have a real period this last cycle. I am confused.

I had higher than normal PG in F phase last October in a salivary hormone panel for a month....and was told it could be Endometriosis but my MD now doesn't think that.

\*\*I\*\* am wondering if it's because of several months of taking PG in the luteal phase that doesn't clear? I also have been on pregnenolone since March (200mg) and DHEA (50mg) since it was too low and I wonder if it's thats contributing in looking at the hormone cascade. Will stop the pregnenolone for a while and will recheck.....but I still wonder.

Have any of you other women ever experienced this? It seems really hard to find much info on higher PG than the usual E2 dominance. Ugh.

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-12T19:55:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-progesterone-in-a-female.7618/post-89973>

There are some women who aren't estrogen dominant. some of these woman tend to be tall, thing and don't have traditional symptoms. we talked about this in one of the BHRT Threads... I'll have to see if I can dig it up...

What do your other labs look like... you really need to see all the labs together to begin to piece this all together..

### Hope — 2013-07-12T23:05:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-progesterone-in-a-female.7618/post-89994>

Thanks, Gretchen. The other labs - DHEA is over the top at 463 (range up to 350 but MD says it's OK); pregnenolone is 93 now. T3 was 2.6 in late June, T4 .9 and TSH 3.5. It's always fluctuating up and down - I'm unable to get a smooth sailing thyroid dosage. Best T3 i have had was when I was off all thyroid meds for a month. Go figure. I have tried armour, compounded T3, levoxyl, nothing seems to help (and I always end up having to get off due to heart palpitations, like now, when I just tried upping the dose two weeks ago, ugh). RT3 has been fine the last 6 months so that is good. JAck said it's EMF related but I've been working hard at avoiding it, as much as possible in this bath. Love the magnetico.

Vitamin D in most recent labs in June was 91, did not do cholesterol then bc in April all was well, all in a good range, per the optimal ranges we know but will redo later this month. HS-CRP has been consistently below 0.2, that's how they report it for me, for the last 6 months. DHEA has been steadily rising to where it is now. I take DHEA and pregnenolone. Also the iodine protocol etc, epi-paleo etc.

I do take l-theanine or was regularly which I learned from Jack raises the dopamine and PG/E2 ratio, for that purpose. Not sure that contributed too. Such a bummer because with such a high level this early on the MD says I couldn't likely get pregnant bc the uterine lining is affected by all that progesterone even if all the other ducks are in a row. Sigh. More waiting but maybe it's a good thing....I can even out other issues more.

I WAS estrogen dominant last cycle, day 20, but it is getting better (but still E dom) so that's all weird. to me.

Thanks again, Gretchen....so appreciate it. Oh, and while tall, I'm definitely not thin. LOL. Though I am starting to wonder if I'm not losing a bit of fat and gaining a bit of muscle....as my clothes seem a bit looser, my belly looks a bit nicer (smaller LOL) but the scale is still at 150lbs (I'm 5'8'') and usually wear a size 10. But I still have huge thighs and butt. LOL.

# Thread: Question and observation of CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-and-observation-of-ct.7619/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2013-07-12T17:39:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-and-observation-of-ct.7619/post-89959>

I have been trying the CT for about a week now and each day it seems to be getting better, and I am amazed. I am so glad that I am trying this in the summer since I seem to get so hot and the cold water feels really good now. Last night, I did get to the point of having the coldest water filling the tub that I could get and actually thought it was not horrible. I also checked my internal temp one day before and after the CT and it actually went up. My usual temp is about 97.7 and I actually got up to 98.2. I will try to remember to check it every time I do CT and see what is says.

My question is; will this help with the overall body temperature regulation? In the summer I seem to be so sensitive to the heat and I have had a heat stroke and heat exhaustion in the past (summer not really my favorite time of the year because of it). Will it also make me better able to tolerate the cold too? As I sit here now, I am in a short sleeve shirt sitting at work with the AC blasting and trying real hard not to put on a sweatshirt.

Is it also ok to concentrate more on having the cold water on the lower body as that is where most of my excess fat it? Does one have to add the ice part to get the full effect or will some benefits still be seen just by using cold water?

It is going to be hot and humid here this weekend so I am actually looking forward to my cold baths.

Nicole

### nonchalant — 2013-07-12T18:11:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-and-observation-of-ct.7619/post-89968>

Hi Nicole! Glad you are getting into the CT. I used to suffer horribly in summers also. I think it was high inflammation. CT helps me tolerate high temps, as well as cold temps.

It seemed that my temperature tolerance was a very small band of degrees, a few years ago. But now I'll be fine, when others are complaining that it is too hot or too cold.

It's ok to concentrate the cold on the lower part of your body, but for the full effect, you need to get the upper body in for at least a while. Any cold water is good.

### nicld — 2013-07-13T18:46:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-and-observation-of-ct.7619/post-90056>

That is what I am hoping to get out of this is better body temperature regulation. It seems to be helping already. Last night I sat for 40 minutes in as cold as I could get my water and it felt pretty good. I did have my upper body out of the water most of the time then the last 5-10 put the whole body it. I was actually reading Dr. Kruse's book specifically the chapter on cold therapy as I was in the tub.

### nonchalant — 2013-07-13T19:02:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-and-observation-of-ct.7619/post-90059>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=90056)

> I was actually reading Dr. Kruse's book specifically the chapter on cold therapy as I was in the tub.

### Huck — 2013-07-12T17:59:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-lab-help-stumped.7621/post-89967>

I have been having prostate problems for several months and have been tracking some of my sex hormones to see if I can find any correlations.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

A few related labs from Winter, 2012:

Progesterone .2(.2-1.4)
Pregnenalone 47(<151)
SHBG 46.3(19.3-76.4)
DHT 32(30-85)
IGF1 195(75-212)​

I was on arimidex for 1 month in January to lower estradiol. It worked too well as estradiol went from high to a little lower than recommended. In 5 months, estradiol went back up and is now higher than it was originally. I'm finding conflicting info on estradiol and prostate cancer, so I'm not sure whether to try the arimidex again. It really messed with my head and sleep, so I would prefer not taking it again. It is a drug used to treat breast cancer.

Testosterone jumped way up in July (1 month). I think this was from spending a lot of time in sun. Not taking dhea, 7-keto, pregnenalone, testosterone. An interesting side note is my blood pressure dropped significantly during the same time period and I rarely need any medication to keep it in normal range.

When I took the arimidex, DHEA-S went to top of range, and when I stopped, DHEA-s dropped significantly.

I had thought there might be a relationship between dhea-s and PSA level, but my 7/12 lab is just the opposite. I often find that when I am having urinary problems, psa is higher, and vice versa.

The only supplement I am taking that might have a direct effect is progesterone cream. I have been using about 5 g every other day since January. I decided to stop it when I saw my most recent lab results.

So what other labs do I need to figure this out? I need to figure out why estradiol is high and dhea-s is low and what I can do to correct them. I have to make sure I don't take anything that pushes estradiol higher.

Update 1: Additional lab info

Lab on 2/25/13

Tot Chol 262(<200)
Triglicerides 74(<150)
HDL 71(>=40)
LDL 176(0-99) (Doc had me increase ALA in June)

Cortisol is low. RT3 is high. Tried taking cortisol and T3 without success. Decided that good eating and lots of sun would fix me over time.

Update 2: Supplements

Specific supplements I am currently taking to try to address problems.

-   Nettle Root - inhibits binding of testosterone to shbg
-   Saw Palmetto and Zinc - Prevent conversion of testosterone to DHT. Also blocks 5-alpha reductase, aromatase, and stimulates ... nitrous oxide.
-   L-arginine - ED
-   Beta Sitosterol - reduce prostate size

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-12T18:24:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-lab-help-stumped.7621/post-89970>

Huck

what does your Lipid panel look like? also have you had TSH, FREE T4, Free T3 and Reverse T3 run recently? You also might want to consider getting a 23& Me run - then using genetic genie see if you have a MTHFR or COMT Defect which could indicate a methylation and estrogen clearance issue.

Looking at the hormone cascade - it looks like the DHEA your making is getting turned into both Testosterone and Estrogen. You may just have a problem with estrogen clearance .

The following might be a good ask Jack: What does it mean when your DHEA-S is low and all your other steroid hormones are high?

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-07-12T19:51:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-lab-help-stumped.7621/post-89972>

As JanSz said in this post [http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthre...-TO-ESTROGEN-RATION-22-to-1&p=89950#post89950](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7609-PROGESTERONE-TO-ESTROGEN-RATION-22-to-1&p=89950#post89950) ....

the results of hormone creams do not register in serum tests.

Saliva or urine tests are more accurate to measure hormones when using creams and/or gels.

I have most of those, some current and some a couple years old. What I am focused on is anything prostate related. I have symptoms of prostate cancer and doctor recommended a biopsy. I think addressing the dhea/estradiol problem might be key to reducing inflammation and buying some time.

I can enroll in medicare Jan, 2014 and pay nothing for biopsy and any required treatment. Anything I do now will come mostly out-of-pocket as I have a high deductible and co-pay. Doc has me on a sulfur antibiotic that is tearing my stomach up. In the past, antibiotics would reduce symptoms, but not so much this time. She also had me on a colloidal silver tablet that worsened my symptoms, so I stopped that 2 weeks ago. In the past, whenever I have had severe symptoms, one of my steroid hormones has been high (testosterone, dhea-s. estradiol). It happened first when I was using a testosterone cream. It happened again later when I was taking pregnenalone and DHEA. And it happened again when I was taking medication to lower estradiol and my dhea-s went high.

I'm not making a decision based on money alone, but it is a factor. A biopsy will be about $4000 unless I can negotiate a lower price, and that is all out-of-pocket. Then, if I require treatment this year, I could easily be looking at another $5k-$10k. That's on top of insurance cost of over $8k/yr. When I retired, I never planned on such high health insurance costs. I've spent over $100k on insurance and have not been reimbursed for a single expense in 12 years. $100k down the drain.

Pregnenalone 47(<151)
is low
it suggest that you also have low progesterone.
Do not supplement directly with progesterone, specially when it is transdermal type.
Watch also
Cortisol,serum(7:30AM) aim it to be =20

Consider using
pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix 150mg tabs from Nutricology
and
DHEA Micronized Lipid Matrix 50mg tabs from Allergy Research

Use them at wakeup and about 11AM second dose.
Use enough to get:
pregnenolone,serum (do not worry about but monitor)
progesterone (1.4ng/mL up to 5ng/mL but may be higher)
DHEAs(350-490)
Cortisol,serum(7:30AM) aim it to be =20-21
\--------------

Estradiol,sensitive=~20pg/ml
further optimized by good nightly involuntary erections
that level of E2 and progesterone at 5ng/ml would put you at P/E2=~200
you may want to go higher if you are still missing on cortisol.

progesterone should control your E2 naturally and you may not need much of Arimidex.

that is high, binds most of testosterone, you will need to raise TotalTestosterone and control estrogens (all of them)

and
Fatty acids Analysis
follow ed up with optimization of defficiencies.

Save $$ on most supplementah herbs that you are taking.
They are also inhibiting 5aR
that is very hurfull.

3b-Adiol when low may be the reason for your prostate problesm

# Thread: Why does my sleep suck several hours after exercise
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-my-sleep-suck-several-hours-after-exercise.7622/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-07-12T20:03:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-my-sleep-suck-several-hours-after-exercise.7622/post-89975>

I've posted before about how my sleep hasn't been great over the last 10 months but it has improved somewhat over the last couple months.

I now think it's because while on the Reset i have not been working out.

Yesterday at work we played football (mainly lots of short sprints) from 4 to 6pm.

I pushed it to hard, not because I wanted the exercise but for the love of making a play.

It's hard not to, it's kind of addictive

Afterwards into the evening the body was very tired but my sleep was terrible.

I couldn't fall asleep until after 2am.

I originally thought that this had something to do with the high fat but my sleep is improved (not awesome but improved) on high fat with no exercise.

**Could this have something to do with the hypothyroidism?**

Thanks for your input.

BTW it has nothing to do with caffeine as I don't consume any in any form

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-12T21:53:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-my-sleep-suck-several-hours-after-exercise.7622/post-89987>

Increase of cortisol when it should be decreasing as part of the natural circadian cycle. However in your case as a shift worker - you don't have a "natural circadian cycle" yours is discombobulated. So any time you stress (exercise included) your body - it responds with a cortisol response and adrenalin - thus how you can perform at fires at 2am and why your sleep isn't great.

### fireforlunch — 2013-07-12T23:24:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-my-sleep-suck-several-hours-after-exercise.7622/post-89997>

I always appreciate your input.

That makes sense, nevertheless that brings up two questions:

1) why did it only really start when I started eating low carb/high fat?

2) why do the other guys at my work not really have this problem?

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=89987)
>
> Increase of cortisol when it should be decreasing as part of the natural circadian cycle. However in your case as a shift worker - you don't have a "natural circadian cycle" yours is discombobulated. So any time you stress (exercise included) your body - it responds with a cortisol response and adrenalin - thus how you can perform at fires at 2am and why your sleep isn't great.

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-13T03:05:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-my-sleep-suck-several-hours-after-exercise.7622/post-90008>

> [fireforlunch said:](/goto/post?id=89997)
>
> I always appreciate your input.
>
> That makes sense, nevertheless that brings up two questions:
>
> 1) why did it only really start when I started eating low carb/high fat?
>
> 2) why do the other guys at my work not really have this problem?

They Do, they just don't know it...

# Thread: IgG vs IgA food testing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/igg-vs-iga-food-testing.7623/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### vkiernan — 2013-07-12T20:58:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/igg-vs-iga-food-testing.7623/post-89978>

I have now had the Enterolab (IgA) and the ELISA (IgG) food testing. Eggs and dairy showed very strong reactions to on both so they are a no go. But, on the Enterolabs test, it showed a reaction to pork, chicken and tuna but the ELISA showed no reaction. I realize that these tests are from different perspectives within our bodies but now I'm not sure if I can eat those meats or not. My question to anyone out there is what is the real difference and how do I interpret?

Thanks for any input!

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-12T21:39:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/igg-vs-iga-food-testing.7623/post-89985>

Here's what I found:
"IgE, IgG, and IgA are acronyms for the three different kinds of antibodies produced by the immune system in allergic reactions to food Conventional allergy testing looks for IgE reactions only. These types of reactions typically occur immediately after contact with or ingestion of the allergen, and in some cases can cause serious, even fatal, health problems. Potential IgE reactions include swelling of the lips and tongue, hives, bloating, abdominal pain, or sudden diarrhea. These are the reactions that people usually think of when they hear the word allergy. However, IgE reactions can also be delayed, and can lead to many other symptoms not traditionally recognized as being caused by food allergies.

The problem with the standard food allergy tests, run by most allergists, is that **most food allergies are not IgE reactions, but are rather IgG reactions or sometimes IgA reactions**, such as in celiac disease. T**he IgG and IgA reactions usually show up hours or even days after ingestion of the allergen**. They are generally not nearly as dramatic as the more severe IgE reactions, and usually result in “mere” constipation, diarrhea, bloating, gas and abdominal pain. All three antibodies are important, and food allergy testing should include them or the cause of IBS may be missed."

I would say that for the foods you have an IgG response to, you should probably eliminate them. Same for the IgA responses. You're having a different response at a different IgG/IgA response depending on the food. some of which are causing a response to both IgG/IgA.

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-12T21:41:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/igg-vs-iga-food-testing.7623/post-89986>

here's a good article about food allergies, and sensitivities... [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2685801/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2685801/)

### vkiernan — 2013-07-12T22:14:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/igg-vs-iga-food-testing.7623/post-89990>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=89985)
>
> Here's what I found:
> "IgE, IgG, and IgA are acronyms for the three different kinds of antibodies produced by the immune system in allergic reactions to food Conventional allergy testing looks for IgE reactions only. These types of reactions typically occur immediately after contact with or ingestion of the allergen, and in some cases can cause serious, even fatal, health problems. Potential IgE reactions include swelling of the lips and tongue, hives, bloating, abdominal pain, or sudden diarrhea. These are the reactions that people usually think of when they hear the word allergy. However, IgE reactions can also be delayed, and can lead to many other symptoms not traditionally recognized as being caused by food allergies.
>
> The problem with the standard food allergy tests, run by most allergists, is that **most food allergies are not IgE reactions, but are rather IgG reactions or sometimes IgA reactions**, such as in celiac disease. T**he IgG and IgA reactions usually show up hours or even days after ingestion of the allergen**. They are generally not nearly as dramatic as the more severe IgE reactions, and usually result in “mere” constipation, diarrhea, bloating, gas and abdominal pain. All three antibodies are important, and food allergy testing should include them or the cause of IBS may be missed."
>
> I would say that for the foods you have an IgG response to, you should probably eliminate them. Same for the IgA responses. You're having a different response at a different IgG/IgA response depending on the food. some of which are causing a response to both IgG/IgA.

Thanks much! I had read that if you have an IgA response that you should "never" eat that food again and with the IgG, you might be able to later. Hoping that's not entirely true.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Question, is it normal to take so long afterward to warm up?

I take my cold bath before bed and cannot fall asleep due to trying to warm back up. My core does not feel cold, just the skin.

I am going to try and take it a bit earlier and see if that helps but any suggestions would be great.

Nicole

Thanks for the suggestions.

### nicld — 2013-07-15T23:08:37+0300

Would that still be effective and what if I cannot eat before the shower? I know that it says that one should not to CT on an empty stomach.

Thanks

### nonchalant — 2013-07-15T23:37:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-to-rewarm.7635/post-90176>

I don't usually eat before CT. In the beginning I was careful to eat first, but now I just hop in. I don't have any issues regulating my core body temp.

### CTforlife — 2013-07-16T02:33:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-to-rewarm.7635/post-90193>

The recommendation to eat before CT is for beginners. I still eat before CT, but that is how my schedule is set up. I suggest that you eat a high protein/fat meal before you CT in the evenings. When I first started CT it was in the evenings, and I would get sooo "cold" afterwards that I had to take a warm shower in fear of death. Now temperature is a variable to me like everything else in my environment, I adapt to survive.
Yes it is "normal" for a warm adapted mammal to "feel" cold after they CT.
I would consider either changing your schedule and CT in the morning, or become a fat burning furnace. It's really your choice.
It takes time to adapt to the cold, and it definitely takes a lot of time to EMBRACE the cold. The more details you have correct, the faster this "process" happens.
I also would highly suggest you eat a lot of omega 3 rich seafood before you CT. When I "adapted", I ate more salmon than you think was sane. But then again my hs crp and o3 to o6 ratio were worse than you have, and I don't know anything about you.
Improving the engine in all of your "cars" or cells, as smart people call them, is also a wise decision.
The closer your environment is to an actual winter, the faster you will adapt. Following the Leptin Rx and eating Epi-Paleo are some good starting points.

Time is valuable. The more time you "donate" to CT, the more time it "gives" back. After some time, you won't feel cold after arising from 50-55 degrees F water before going to bed. It will calm you down and make you sleepy after a while in the evenings. Change around the duration of your CT sessions until you don't "feel" cold afterwards. Soon enough, and you will EMBRACE the cold.
Sorry if this has spelling/grammatical errors, typed this out really quick.

It also is only really noticeable once I am in bed but not so much walking around.

It is so weird to me, the freeze baby, that I can sit in a tub of cold cold water and it feels good.
