# Thread: Motion sickness
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/motion-sickness.7636/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2013-07-14T18:21:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/motion-sickness.7636/post-90106>

The more I think of motion sickness, the more I think it may not be a malady. It seems to be a reasonable response to a disruption in our sense of direction.

I'm talking about the people who cannot read a book while in the back seat of a car. If they look out the window, however, they are fine. Or if they close their eyes, they are fine. Their brain cannot resolve the incongruity, however, between the stillness that they see (the open book) and the motion of the car (or perhaps the changes in direction). Same for airplane and boat movements. I'm not talking about stormy weather or turbulent movements, just gentle travel that bothers some of us.

Would better mitochondrial support help these people? Or is their response completely normal, and the only resolution is to deaden their sense of direction so that they perceive no incongruity? Are the rest of us, that do not experience nausea, missing a good innate sense of direction?

### fitness@home — 2013-07-14T19:36:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/motion-sickness.7636/post-90113>

I'd like to know the answer to this. As a child and teenager, I used to read in the car all the time without nausea. As an adult, it now gives me motion sickness.

### HoneyChild — 2013-07-14T19:39:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/motion-sickness.7636/post-90114>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/motion-sickness.7636/post-90115>

Ooh, love those endocannabinoids!
Perhaps when one eats foods with cannaboids (dairy, wheat) they experience problems with motion sickness?

### nonchalant — 2013-07-14T19:45:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/motion-sickness.7636/post-90116>

Excess cannaboids from foods means you aren't good at manufacturing your own?

# Thread: HELP: weight loss and low leptin levels
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-weight-loss-and-low-leptin-levels.7652/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Trinity — 2013-07-17T00:04:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-weight-loss-and-low-leptin-levels.7652/post-90244>

Hi guys,

I am posting this question on behalf of my partner. He is a 41 year old male with a history of Hashimotos thyroid disease diagnosed approximately 3 years ago. Although a healthy weight when younger, he has battled obesity for the past 10 years. At his highest, he weighed around ~247. He managed to loose nearly 40 pounds over the past year after finally getting his thyroid optimized with cytomel. That weight loss put him right around 207 pounds, but a DEXA scan revealed that he was still obese with a body fat of 27%. His weight loss stalled out around 207 pounds and since then he has had increasing difficulty maintaining his weight. Within the past 3 months, he has actually put back on 8-10 pounds. After telling him about Dr. K's website and the role of leptin, he convinced his endocrinologist to run a leptin level. We expected it to be high because he is still obese, but were surprised when it came back extremely low.

The reference range for adult males is given as 1.8 - 19.9 ng / mL. His level was 3.5 ng / mL. His endocrinologist felt that leptin level was way too low given his obese body fat level, but did not have any suggestions about how to improve the situation.

From my research into leptin, I suspect that his low leptin level is a result of his 40 pound weight loss. It is well documented that dieting lowers lepin levels and that this lowering likely places the body into a "starvation mode" that encourages a regain of lost weight. Even though he is still obese after a 40 lb weight loss, it appears that his remaining fat is not producing enough leptin to maintain normal metabolic function. Recently he has also started showing signs of adrenal fatigue and this has required him to lower the optimal cytomel dosage that he has been on without any problems for over a year. He does not have a prior history of adrenal fatigue and I suspect this is related to the leptin issue as well.

The question here is how do we raise his leptin and improve his hormonal function before:
1) he regains the 40 pounds that he lost
2) the low leptin "starvation mode" response leads to full blown adreanal fatigue and a further inability to tolerate his thyroid meds.

As far as I know, leptin injections are not available. Would cycloset be a good option for him? Or is there a better way to tackle this problem?

Thanks,
Trinity

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-17T00:07:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-weight-loss-and-low-leptin-levels.7652/post-90245>

I would say his best bet would be to follow the Leptin reset, focusing on circadian cycles (light/seasonal foods) as well as CT and grounding while eating Epipaleo.

### caroline — 2013-07-17T02:05:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-weight-loss-and-low-leptin-levels.7652/post-90253>

Great advice from Shijin.....has he checked the easy start guides on the home page?

### Anna — 2013-07-17T12:38:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-weight-loss-and-low-leptin-levels.7652/post-90272>

Hi Trinity, I would think the key here is 'inflammation'. Adrenal fatigue is a stress response to inflammation - I'm thinking that there is something he is consuming in his foods that is causing excessive inflammation............... you do not mention anything about foods he has been consuming - is he following Epipaleo, primal or the like? Has he eliminated wheat and perhaps even dairy? Is he consuming plenty of saturated fats and protein?........

### [email redacted] — 2013-08-22T07:39:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/got-my-results-back-and-it-is-enough-to-make-my-head-spin-help-please.7654/post-93394>

Are you sure it's only 1000mg of fish oil? Twice a day, it's still only barely above a teaspoon. But I can understand if you don't wanna overdo fish oil. Your Armour isn't quite replenishing your FT3. How are your symptoms? Feeling good? So so? I used to take Align. Doing anything for you? What in particular are your digestive or gut flora issues?

Your numbers look good in many areas. I can see that you have no hint of autoimmunity. So many times, whenever I see blood sugar issues or autoimmnity, I can tell it right away. You may have other issues but have none in those areas. Excellent blood sugar numbers overall and lipids.

One issue I have is your liver enzymes. Why are they elevated. Your Albumin also isn't quite as high as it should be. Are you recovering from fatty liver? Had past liver issues? That's the only metabolic issue I see but it's a minor one, especially if you had past issues.

You're supplementing iron because of low ferritin or anemia? I don't see anemia and your low-normal ferritin (and it isn't really that bad; it could possibly be optimal) could be due to your hypo.

Aside from that, I'd get your B12 up above 1000. 1000-1700 is the optimal level. The reference range is set by those who're deficient in B12.

# Thread: The search for DHEA led me to procaine...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-search-for-dhea-led-me-to-procaine.7661/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lahelada — 2013-07-18T19:53:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-search-for-dhea-led-me-to-procaine.7661/post-90339>

To make a long story short DHEA as you know it is almost impossible to come by here. However we do have it under the name prasterone. So far so good. However,it comes in a mix with procaine and B12 . Reading up on Procaine it seems it is the normal anesthetic and in a derivative a miracle "drug" from the sixties that was subsequently banned by the FDA for reasons of it being unapproved .
Limited studies show it to be more than interesting.
I am torn as to whether I will try it or not.
Composition of each tablet : cianocobalamina (VIT B12) 5.000mcg, sulfato sÃ³dico de prasterona (DHEA sodium sulphate) 20mg, clorhidrato de procaÃ­na/ procaine chlorhydrate 50mg,

Has anyone read more on it , tried it if they are not in the US or generally have an opinion?
Here are some wikis that do a good sum up of what the stuff is about.

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Procaine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Procaine)
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Procaine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Procaine)

# Thread: "Cold air " thermogenesis ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-air-thermogenesis.7665/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lahelada — 2013-07-19T21:20:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-air-thermogenesis.7665/post-90392>

We get very few really cold days here . For the next nine days we have a fantastic polar front coming in from the Antarctic with maybe just a very unlikely maybe of some snowflurries. So Ia m planning to make use of it to the max if it is worthwhile.
Do walks underdressed in thin clothes in the cold count for thermogenesis and reap the benefits?
Is exercising a good idea in the cold?
Any other ideas you have greatly appreciated. Thank you.

### Hemming — 2013-07-19T21:30:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-air-thermogenesis.7665/post-90393>

To just activate BAT there seems to be quite a lot of evidence that cold air is enough. Check out this link [https://www.coolfatburner.com/the-Science.htm#Is\_any\_of\_this\_backed\_up\_by\_science](https://www.coolfatburner.com/the-Science.htm#Is_any_of_this_backed_up_by_science) .

### Lahelada — 2013-07-19T21:41:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-air-thermogenesis.7665/post-90394>

I like ! Thank you Hemming for posting the link. Of all the CT sites I have seen this is not one of them.

### Dextery — 2013-07-19T21:43:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-air-thermogenesis.7665/post-90395>

Janz who lives in New Jersey said he was out strolling in the cold with only shorts and a light T shirt when he was stopped and questioned by a policeman as to his "odd" clothing in 35 F weather. LOL

### Hemming — 2013-07-19T22:43:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-air-thermogenesis.7665/post-90399>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=90394)
>
> I like ! Thank you Hemming for posting the link. Of all the CT sites I have seen this is not one of them.

No problem. You can wear a cap, gloves and warm socks while keeping the rest of the body more exposed to better handle the cold weather but still get a big exposure. In my own experience, and from what I've heard from others too, it's easy to deal with cold if your feet and hands are warm - I personally never wear a cap in the winter.

Maybe someone can comment on if any particular foods/supplements (in addition to bitter lemon extract) can increase the activation and recruitment of BAT.

### nonchalant — 2013-07-19T23:00:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-air-thermogenesis.7665/post-90403>

> [Dextery said:](/goto/post?id=90395)
>
> Janz who lives in New Jersey said he was out strolling in the cold with only shorts and a light T shirt when he was stopped and questioned by a policeman as to his "odd" clothing in 35 F weather. LOL

Sometimes you can find some late summer clothing that looks warm at first glance, but it is quite airy. Gauze-type fabric. I'll wear that during the winter. People have to be fairly close to see that my long-sleeved shirt (that I am wearing over a tank-top) is quite see-through and cool. People stare less if you have shorts on with a long-sleeved top.

### MeghanK — 2013-07-20T18:43:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-air-thermogenesis.7665/post-90444>

@ Hemming.....Bitter Lemon Extract and BAT activation?

### Hemming — 2013-07-20T18:49:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-air-thermogenesis.7665/post-90445>

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=90444)
>
> @ Hemming.....Bitter Lemon Extract and BAT activation?

Yes, I can't give you the scientific explanation but Jack has mentioned it in the CT FAQ and a quick google search showed some studies too.

### MeghanK — 2013-07-20T18:55:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-air-thermogenesis.7665/post-90446>

Is it a supplement you buy and take?

### Hemming — 2013-07-20T19:13:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-air-thermogenesis.7665/post-90448>

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=90446)
>
> Is it a supplement you buy and take?

No, I've tried it. I don't think it's available in Denmark. I think Ben Greenfield has recommended it for lowering blood glucose after meals.

### nonchalant — 2013-07-20T19:27:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-air-thermogenesis.7665/post-90449>

Are y'all talking about bitter melon?

### Hemming — 2013-07-20T20:27:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-air-thermogenesis.7665/post-90452>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=90449)
>
> Are y'all talking about bitter melon?

Sorry, I mixed up lemon and melon.

And to clarify - I've never tried it.

### nonchalant — 2013-07-20T20:40:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-air-thermogenesis.7665/post-90453>

I used it, early on. Chopped some off of the cucumber-like fruit and added it to my eggs. Tasted good, in a grassy, chlorophyll-like way. Not bitter, but strong-flavored. I used 3-4 of the melons while I was adapting to CT.

### Lahelada — 2013-07-21T15:22:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-air-thermogenesis.7665/post-90503>

Good morning. A windy day yesterday and I spent about 4 hours doing my errands in the cold. Great ! I finally wore a dark cotton T shirt with long sleeves ( winter colours) and loose jeans.
In the afternoon we went to the boat and I did a bit of sunbathing in the cold wind with exposed midriff .
CT is a problem for my partner. He goes out dressed like a polar explorer. When people see us together they give him poisonous looks enquiring why he does not find his gentleman side and give me one of his jackets . So far he is taking it well. The dog is fed up though . I took her to the park after dark with temp around 4 and halfway around she asked to go home. We normally do the full circle but when I told her the magic word "HOME" she bolted straight across the grass in the direction of home. Bwahaha.

No bittermelon here either. Before I knew that it would be good for me they had it as a vegetable but now it has disappeared from the chinese store. There was a study that it aided in BAT production from China and that it helps with not feeling the cold so much . For the latter I don't really need it.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-07-22T20:59:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/primo-water-from-walmart-ok.7671/post-90581>

Does the Primo water dispenser at WalMart remove fluoride? It has a sediment filter, carbon filter, and does Reverse Osmosis on the water. The higher the pressure that water is forced through an RO filter means more contaminants could be forced through.

Good Questions... Nonchalant or anyone else using Primo know?

# Thread: Why are the feet so difficult
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-the-feet-so-difficult.7680/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-07-22T22:19:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-the-feet-so-difficult.7680/post-90585>

When I first started in the water last week I only put my feet in and it was pretty difficult so I bailed after 10 minutes.

I thought my whole body would be as painful as the feet but that isn't the case.

I've seen some people say that they didn't put their feet in for 6 months.

Why do they seem to take the cold the worst?

Is it because they are farthest from the heart or is there some other reason?

### nonchalant — 2013-07-22T22:41:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-the-feet-so-difficult.7680/post-90587>

Very good question, FFL. Since our feet were made for strolling around in the cold water, feeling for shellfish, you would think they could tolerate the cold best.

I have been CT'ing for over a year and I still keep my toes out of the water, except for the first couple minutes. It might just be a habit, however. When I first started getting the water really cold, just above freezing, I would end up with whitish splotches on the soles of my feet. The calloused areas of my feet would freeze. The arch area was just fine, however. I don't see that freezing effect anymore.

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-22T22:46:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-the-feet-so-difficult.7680/post-90588>

Even when I deep CT I still wear 2-3pair of wool socks...

this Blog should give you some insight. [http://www.jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-6-bipedalism/](http://www.jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-6-bipedalism/)

### fireforlunch — 2013-07-22T23:01:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-the-feet-so-difficult.7680/post-90592>

Thank you, I remember reading that now but with all of JK's other stuff it slipped through the cracks of my brain

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=90588)
>
> Even when I deep CT I still wear 2-3pair of wool socks...
>
> this Blog should give you some insight. [http://www.jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-6-bipedalism/](http://www.jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-6-bipedalism/)

### CTforlife — 2013-07-23T22:25:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-the-feet-so-difficult.7680/post-90677>

Your feet and hands can take extra time to adapt to your new environment.

### fireforlunch — 2013-07-24T00:31:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-the-feet-so-difficult.7680/post-90681>

Right...

I am asking why

> [CTforlife said:](/goto/post?id=90677)
>
> Your feet and hands can take extra time to adapt to your new environment.

### CTforlife — 2013-07-24T00:59:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-the-feet-so-difficult.7680/post-90684>

Because you are a warm adapted mammal...

### Inger — 2013-07-24T09:13:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-the-feet-so-difficult.7680/post-90716>

Yeah.. it goes very slow! But now I recognize I need way less on my feet to keep them warm than the others in this house. Here is stone floor in my parents kitchen and living room. And it is cold! No floor heating. I still walk barefoot all the time. The others need socks and complain of cold feet, even if it is summer. My feet are always warm, or just fine. And I am not even doing much CT except of a short swim in the river every day years round and some face dunks in the morning! But I have been doing it for 2,5 years now.

### Nathan — 2013-11-15T05:41:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-the-feet-so-difficult.7680/post-100752>

My feet adapted pretty quickly to being in the water (I do around 52-55F). My problem is they stay cold for hours afterward. I don't CT my hands but they are bad too. Been driving to work with the windows down in the 40s lately and I'm fine except for my hands which get cold and achy and stay cold for a long time after. Just need to stick with it I guess

### Hemming — 2013-11-20T15:21:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-the-feet-so-difficult.7680/post-101101>

> [Nathan said:](/goto/post?id=100752)
>
> My feet adapted pretty quickly to being in the water (I do around 52-55F). My problem is they stay cold for hours afterward. I don't CT my hands but they are bad too. Been driving to work with the windows down in the 40s lately and I'm fine except for my hands which get cold and achy and stay cold for a long time after. Just need to stick with it I guess

I have the same problem. My feet are fine but hands are a pain. For me I simply think its partly due to low blood pressure and heart rate which makes it more difficult for the warm blood to get to my hands. Its quite annoying because I feel bad overall even though its only my hands which are cold.

### Shijin13 — 2013-11-20T15:40:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-the-feet-so-difficult.7680/post-101103>

I never put my hands in the water. Feet are fine - and as it gets colder I do wear two layers of wool socks.

### Hemming — 2013-11-20T16:19:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-the-feet-so-difficult.7680/post-101106>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=101103)
>
> I never put my hands in the water. Feet are fine - and as it gets colder I do wear two layers of wool socks.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-level-in-a-blood-test.7682/post-90594>

How accurate are these?

I had a doctors appointment about 2 weeks ago and asked for this blood test to be done and just got the results back.

It said that my level was 6.3 ng/mL and that normal levels are above 8 in females with a BMI over 25 (which I still have).

This test was done in the morning after fasting (not sure if that would make a difference).

I had been doing the reset for 6 weeks already and just starting doing CT.

Just got back from a mini vacation and I was able to stay with the eating program (for the most part) but did have some treats and the CT was just a quick cold shower (and boy I missed them). I know that I will have to start counting today and day one now due to this set back but that's ok. I did not screw up my leptin over night, I can't fix it over night but I can fix it.

I will be trying to scour the blogs and Dr. Kruse's site and see if I can find some more stuff on this but if anyone has any ideas on this, I would love to hear them.

# Thread: Grounding Revisited...Earthing Mats, Feet On Concrete, Feet on Grass...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-revisited-earthing-mats-feet-on-concrete-feet-on-grass.7684/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MeghanK — 2013-07-23T03:24:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-revisited-earthing-mats-feet-on-concrete-feet-on-grass.7684/post-90606>

Ok, I was trying to read through the grounding post with RayB/Anna discussing Earthing vs. Diet.... but it is a MONSTER post and I'm getting dizzy... so I will just ask....
1) **Earthing Mats-** What is the consensus these days? Dirty electricity or OK? I know NonChalant uses... Dr. Kruse not a fan? Better than nothing especially if living in an apartment in the city?
2) Can you **ground walking barefoot on concrete** or ONLY grass.... I have a park across the way from my apartment that I try to walk around the grass for at least 20 minutes a day but it would be convenient if I could walk on the cement barefoot and get the benefits since there is not much grass on the way to the store etc....
3) **How long** do you need to ground outside per day? meaning what is the minimum you can get away with?
4)What is the consensus on **EMF blocking or reducing bracelets?** Best Brand or Don't bother.....

### SeaHorse — 2013-07-23T17:01:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-revisited-earthing-mats-feet-on-concrete-feet-on-grass.7684/post-90640>

Meghan....here are my answers.

1\. I would not use these plugged into the wall....if you are in an apartment save your dollars and get a magnetico instead.
2\. Concrete does give you access to the earth resonance....grass is better, wet grass is even better and anything away from power lines, buildings, etc. is best.
3\. There is no minimum that you "can get away with" The idea is that we should be spending our whole lives grounded. So ANY time is better than no time. It is entirely individual as far as effects. There is no way to assign a time because of all the variables.
4\. I don't have experience with these, but I'm interested....I like the shungite stone that people were talking about earlier in the year. Still haven't purchased any though.

Just my 2 cents: Do what you can at the very highest level of your ability and experiment with your own responses. You won't get a "prescription" here because we are all different.

### MeghanK — 2013-07-23T18:14:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-revisited-earthing-mats-feet-on-concrete-feet-on-grass.7684/post-90661>

I know this is a lot of N= 1 but would love to hear others thoughts especially those who also live in cities/apartments/condos and or have used smart meters to test....
THANKS Sea Horse... never heard of the shungite stone.. can you tell me more about it..

# Thread: Nearly free CT baths
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nearly-free-ct-baths.7686/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-07-23T03:44:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nearly-free-ct-baths.7686/post-90608>

After driving 20 miles round trip to get 40 lbs of ice for $4 i figured there had to be a better way.

Finally the obvious became clear

A deep freeze and 4 1-gallon buckets is all u need. Actually a regular freezer will will work fine if u have the room.

Just fill your buckets of water and freeze them

Mine froze solid in less than 20 hours

Then fill your tub with water and put the buckets in the tub

The tub water will melt the ice in the bucket enough for a massive cub to come out.

My water out of the faucet was 78 degrees.

In 40 minutes my 4 massive cubes melted and my water was 55 degrees

No more buying ice and no more driving for ice - i luv it

### caroline — 2013-07-23T04:17:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nearly-free-ct-baths.7686/post-90612>

There was a joke around here - long ago ....our fridges and freezers are for ice and water - not food!

I just spent all day yesterday defrosting my freezer ...... my large baggies of water had leaked and everything in my freezer was frozen solid together! I have put off doing that for ages - finally - yesterday was the day! horrible job!

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-23T16:01:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nearly-free-ct-baths.7686/post-90631>

This reminds me. DH and I need to figure out how to get our 2nd deep freezer working....

### Lahelada — 2013-07-23T16:41:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nearly-free-ct-baths.7686/post-90638>

Great idea. I don't have the freezer space for the moment but will keep the idea in mind.
Caroline, I freeze PET water bottles to avoid the mess of leaky plastic bags.

### PrimalPam — 2013-07-24T02:27:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nearly-free-ct-baths.7686/post-90696>

I have two entire shelves in my freezer devoted to freezing ice for my baths. I use plastic containers of varying sizes and stack them on top of each other. Rubbermaid makes rectangle-shaped containers with lids that are BPA-free, and they are great for large blocks of ice. I combine those with smaller plastic containers, which melt faster. With those two shelves' worth of ice, I can get it down to 55 degrees on a hot day like today, or 50 degrees in the winter.

And yes, it's free!

# Thread: 2 months on leptin reset--few specific questions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-months-on-leptin-reset-few-specific-questions.7693/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### windycity88 — 2013-07-23T17:39:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-months-on-leptin-reset-few-specific-questions.7693/post-90648>

Hi all--

I've been on the leptin reset since early May, and I've seen a ton of positive signs. However, there are a few areas I'm looking to troubleshoot--and I'd appreciate any thoughts! Sorry if this is long--I'll give some background, then ask my questions.

Some background:

\- I started the leptin reset in May, 8 months after three HCG cycles and a diagnosis of Hashimoto's thyroiditis. (One symptom was persistent weight gain, even after HCG, despite very careful eating.) I gained 40 lbs as a kid, pretty much out of the blue, and have struggled with weight most of my life.

\- I've been paleo since 2008, auto-immune paleo (no eggs, nuts or seeds, nightshades, dairy, chocolate, alcohol) since November of 2012. I've been working on my adrenals (adaptogens, no caffeine since March 2013) and leaky gut (bone broth, L-Glutamine, NAC + zinc + C, DGL). I had a long, long exercise rest as part of healing my adrenals, and have also done a bunch of acupuncture, and I've seen some real improvements, including way more energy and focus in the morning.

The leptin reset was something I tried because my weight just kept climbing (10 lbs total), despite rigorous adherence to a low-carb, easy-on-the-gut AI paleo diet. Sometimes I could trace it to supplements--a probiotic that had traces of dairy, a Vitamin C powder that was derived from corn, and the weight gain would stop (but not reverse) when I removed it. Sometimes, it seemed arbitrary. Dr. Kruse's explanation of the hormone cascade made so much sense to me, and aligns with my experience of my own body.

I did the leptin reset throughout May, without cheating, and saw tons of improvements--dropped 5 of the 10 lbs I've gained, had tons more energy in the morning, improved sex drive. I then cheated for a few days on a vacation (went over the carb limit, did some snacking--no really unhealthy foods, though), and resumed the diet for four weeks in June. I re-lost the same 5 lbs (it had all come right back on), saw the color of my skin and nails improve.

Then, in early July, my weight started climbing again. I put back on the 5 lbs I had lost again, despite eating exactly the same things I'd been eating before (I work at home and cook all my food). So I thought maybe it was time to add in weight lifting and sprints. I also thought that perhaps it was time to switch to the "after leptin" diet--so, relatively smaller breakfast (25-30g protein) and bigger dinner. I also added in some fruit and starchy vegetables--which actually made me feel a lot better, probably since I've been very low carb since last fall. I've now been lifting for two weeks, and I added in sprinting yesterday. However, after two weeks of stability (which made me think the starchy vegetables--sweet potatoes, squash--and fruit were okay), my weight has started to climb again. I am still seeing tons of good signs--I sweat during exercise, my nails are growing like crazy, my weight is more stable rather than bouncing around, it's just headed in the wrong direction.

I sleep 8+ hours a night, get morning sun, avoid screens after dark, and go to bed when the sun goes down. I also have good relationships and fairly low stress in my life.

So, my questions:

1\. Is it common to gain weight before losing it on the leptin reset? With all the other good indicators, should I just keep going, or is the increased weight a sign I need to pay attention to?

2\. Was I wrong to switch to the "after leptin" diet? Should I stay on the initial "gigantic breakfast" reset as long as I need to lose weight, even when all the positive indicators are in place?

Obviously, I want to get these 10 pounds off now. But I'm more interested in restoring my long-term health, which I think will eventually lead to the weight loss. Any thoughts?

### caroline — 2013-07-24T02:49:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-months-on-leptin-reset-few-specific-questions.7693/post-90698>

Hi Windy.....you have been working at all this really hard! I don't know what I can offer - I want to think about this for awhile.

Have you checked for EMFs in your home? Did you listen to the EMF webinars with Michael Nuert? They were gold. Very important to see what your exposure is

What about water? Grounding? C/T? Drinking spring water is hugely important as is C/T to reduce inflammation
Grounding and getting sun everyday - also huge...
Do you have a smart meter? Do your neighbors?

Have you had labs done? A salivary ASI with diurnal cortisol would be really important to do..
Post your labs and someone will have a look as soon as they have some spare time.

Please keep readin Jack's blogs and asking questions.

### caroline — 2013-07-24T02:57:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2-months-on-leptin-reset-few-specific-questions.7693/post-90699>

BTW ....this isn't really about weightloss - it is about regaining your health. Something is obviously going on that you haven't addressed. Have you read the Hormone 101 blog? And we now have Hormone 102 that Jack just posted.
Have you read Jack's Book? It explains a lot in simple terms for those of us who don't have a science background.

It is a long but amazing journey to get to your optimal health....

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beverly-requesting-some-lab-help.7699/post-90701>

Hi Jack and members. I posted some of my information on the comments page of the Not so hard truth about hair loss article. I will repost here as well as my recent labs.

I am early 40's and female. Gallbladder out at 28 in 2000. 3 miscarriages in a year in 2010. I have 3 children. ACDF c6/c7 2012. For the past several years I have had hair loss that started in my late teens early twenties. I have felt somewhat unwell for the past few years also (a sore feeling in my chest (it feels like my heart to me), nausea, heartburn, difficulty swallowing) the only thing that showed up with those sx was the finding of a cold thyroid nodule 8 yrs ago.

And now after the birth of my last son 2 years ago I feel extremely tired. Like I get tired just walking to my elevator down the hall. Or doing simple things like showering. I have seen several dr's over the years but until now I have never really pushed to get any blood work done.

The week that I went in to have my blood work done (2 weeks ago) I also started getting sore finger joints and sore hip joints as well as a tingling feeling that is pretty general. Please have a look and let me know what you think of my labs and where I should start looking.
The only test I haven't done yet is a celiac panel because I'm doing a gluten challenge for a month first. My lab result is the one on top and the reference range for it is always right below it. Thank you.

### Beverly — 2013-07-24T18:54:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beverly.7705/post-90741>

Hi Dr K and members. I have been posting to Dr K on the not so hard truth about hair loss article and I am now posting my labs here. My name is Beverly. Caucasian female early 40's. 5'3 125 lbs. Haven't been feeling well for several years.

Diffuse hair loss since late teens. Nausea, fatigue, difficulty swallowing. Had a scope for the swallowing and a thyroid scan 8 yrs ago and I have 1 cold nodule. Gallbladder out in 2000 at 28. 3 miscarriages in a row in 2010. I also have 3 children. ACDF c6/c7 2012. My diet has always been your typical western high carb diet.

A few months ago I started getting dry red eyes. Then 2 weeks ago I started having finger joint pain and hip pain. I also still have fatigue nausea and difficulty swallowing too. I have to double swallow. I am doing a gluten challenge right now and in a month I will do a celiac panel.

Here are the lab tests I recently got back. My result is on top and the reference range is right below. Sorry it is so long. Please take a look and I would love to hear any suggestions.

# Thread: seasonal eating in brazil and thermogenesis without bathtub
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonal-eating-in-brazil-and-thermogenesis-without-bathtub.7713/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### paulineliden — 2013-07-25T13:40:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonal-eating-in-brazil-and-thermogenesis-without-bathtub.7713/post-90794>

hi eveyone! i am a 21 year old girl that has been eating a traditional diet since i discovered the work of weston a price about 4 years ago. i listened to jacks interview over at sean croxtons and from what i understood he said we should eat seasonally (and mostly try to be in ketosis?). i am from sweden but now i live in rio de janeiro, the seasonal available food there is much different from the one where i come from. should i follow the brazilian rhythm or should i follow the scandinavian one and go into ketosis in the winter months (despite the 90 degree heat in rio) and then in the summer months add apples, pears and berries (which are pretty much the fruits available where i come from), in brazil there is fruit and starches available all year round.

then i have a question about cold thermogenesis, i would really like to try it but i don't have a bathtub. i live near the sea but the temperature rarely goes down below 70 degrees. could i do it with cold water in the shower? even if it's not possible to get cold adapted that way could having cold showers from now on have any benefits?

/pauline

# Thread: Spot CT... ?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spot-ct.7715/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MeghanK — 2013-07-25T18:02:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spot-ct.7715/post-90810>

I tried to look this up because I'm sure it has been ask but I couldn't find it.... 2 questions...
1) Can you "spot" CT... meaning reduce fat in certain areas of your body by putting ice on those areas and trying to increase the BAT there?
2) Are there spots on the body that if you can't do a FULL tub CT, that you should focus on icing or putting ice packs on? (i.e. inner thigh, neck, back?)

Thanks,
Meghan

### Shijin13 — 2013-07-25T18:20:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spot-ct.7715/post-90816>

YES. you can spot CT. I spot CT my abdomen I also will spot CT my Neck/Back when I have a pinched nerve. CT where you can and when you can.

I'm on a conference call... when I get done... I'm going to go up to the CVS - and buy a couple of ice packs b/c my neck is in serious pain.. the cold feels so good...

### MeghanK — 2013-07-26T18:24:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spot-ct.7715/post-90924>

So what changes have you seen in your abdomen? How do you know if you are developing BAT....? How long do you "spot" CT for?

### AndrÃ©s — 2013-07-30T19:14:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spot-ct.7715/post-91174>

I didn't get to doing regular CT immersions yet (I hope to swim at the sea more often this winter) and my [DIY-ice-vest](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?5263-DIY-ice-vest-2-0) sessions are a little less frequent nowadays, something like once a week. I keep driving with full blast AC-on year round, though.

I don't think my belly has shrunk but I am not doing a low-carb enough (ketogenic) diet either. What I am quite positive is that after several months doing it my belly fat is generating heat of its own (I have the heat sensation afterwards) and I didn't shiver slightly until the 1 hour mark on winter.

### CTforlife — 2013-07-30T21:46:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spot-ct.7715/post-91186>

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=90924)
>
> So what changes have you seen in your abdomen? How do you know if you are developing BAT....? How long do you "spot" CT for?

CT for as long as you possibly can...

# Thread: back spasms wiki
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/back-spasms-wiki.7730/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jinron — 2013-07-26T21:15:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/back-spasms-wiki.7730/post-90940>

Hello respectful all .Here I am a newbie and junior member. Does anyone get back muscle spasms in the middle part of their lower back ? I get them every few months, typically after having done a yoga class or other stretching. The muscle spasm then takes 4-5 days to disappear and is quite painful, especially so when getting up from a period of sitting / lying down. I am always being told by specialists that I need to get more flexible - but every time I try to become more flexible my [back spasms wiki](http://www.backspasmadvice.com/types-of-lower-back-spasms-causes/)!No stretching and my back is basically ok - crazy I know. Does anyone have any similar experience and any ideas as to how to get more flexible without the back going into spasm after being stretched? Any thoughts really appreciated !
Many thanks,

### Hope — 2013-07-26T23:14:08+0300

# Thread: Made Goal Weight - Set New Goal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/made-goal-weight-set-new-goal.7750/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2013-07-30T02:02:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/made-goal-weight-set-new-goal.7750/post-91138>

I set a goal to lose 100 lbs 12 years ago. After 10 years, I still had 85 to go.

Since starting the leptin rx in October 2011, I have lost an additional 90 lbs for a grand total of 105.

My experience is that I lose easily when the sun is high in the sky and not so much the rest of the year. I still have a good 6-8 weeks of good sun this year that hopefully will get me down another 15 lbs (200 lbs). If I can reach 199, that will be the lowest weight since I was 19, 45 years ago.

### caroline — 2013-07-30T03:31:28+0300

### Beverly — 2013-07-30T04:37:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recommendations-on-which-labs-to-ask-for.7751/post-91153>

Hi everybody,

I have an appt and would like to discuss my previous labs with my dr tomorrow. Lyme has already been suggested as has certain Celiac tests so I will ask for those.

If Dr K or anyone else could hack my labs and recommend any other labs to ask for based on the previous labs I posted last week, I would really appreciate it.

Thanks.

### nicld — 2013-08-03T04:46:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recommendations-on-which-labs-to-ask-for.7751/post-91405>

How did it go?

Sorry did not have time to reply sooner but here is what I did.

I had my full thyroid done including reverse T3, leptin, female hormones, DHEA, Vitamin d and B and a bunch of others. I would make a list of tests that you want done and hand it to your doctor, that's what I did. I did tell him that I am working on my leptin reset and wanted these tests done to see where I am at and I am in charge of my health. I have heard of so many people whose doctors won't do some tests. One has to take charge and say that it is their health and their money to spend on the tests.

### Beverly — 2013-08-03T23:19:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recommendations-on-which-labs-to-ask-for.7751/post-91450>

Hi,

Thanks for asking. I actually had to postpone it until Tuesday. Great advice, I will make a list of the tests I would like. The derm that ran all my recent labwork is one of those dr's that only does tests when I specifically ask for them. He thought my last round of labs were "normal" so I am pretty sure he won't be open to any additional testing.

So I am seeing a different dr at a clinic that thinks outside the box. He thinks I am having a sensitivity to gluten. Good for you for being your own advocate. I am putting my health as priority as well and I will let you know how my appt goes on Tuesday. I will be asking for lyme testing as well as discussing my high cholesterol results. Let me know how your blood work turns out.

### nicld — 2013-08-04T04:44:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recommendations-on-which-labs-to-ask-for.7751/post-91466>

Just be firm in asking for what you want. I am so glad that I read a bunch of stuff from Dr. Kruse so I had some knowledge of what specific tests that I wanted and more importantly why. Not sure why doctors are so hesitant on allowing patients to gather information about what is going on in their body. I got back a bunch of my results and shows that I am hormonally off. Now the fun of trying to find a doctor that can help while I keep doing the LR and CT.

Good Luck

Nicole

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-04T16:24:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recommendations-on-which-labs-to-ask-for.7751/post-91490>

THe following are the labs I always ask for

Berkley or other Lipid panel
CBC Test # 005009, CPT Code 85025
Comp metabolic Panel Test # 322000, CPT code 80053
Thyroid:
TSH, Test # 004259, CPT Code 84443
Free T4, Test # 001974, CPT code 84439
Free T3, Test # 010389, CTP Code 84481
RT3, Test # 070104, CPT 84482
Total T4 (Dzugan requested) Test # 001149 CPT Code 84436
Total T3 (Dzugan requested) Test # 002188, CPT code 84480
Progestrone, Test # 004312, CPT Code 84144
Estradiol, Sensitive, Test # 140244, CPT Code 82670
Testosterone Free Direct, & serum with Total Testosterone: Test # 140103, CPT Codes 84402, 84403
Prolactin Test # 004465, CPT code 84146
cortisol Test # 004051, CPT Code 82533
DHEA-S Test # 004020, CPT Code 82627
IGF-1 Test #010636, CPT Code 84305
RBC magnesium Test # 080283, CPT Code 83735
Vitamin D25 Hyrdoxy Test # 081950, CPT code 82306
Serotonin Test# 120204, CPT Code 84260 (requires frozen specimens)
Pregnenolone Test# 140707, CPT Code 84140 (requires Frozen Specimens)

I have been lucky to always have had good and caring Doctors but I never knew the right questions to ask - even tho I am pretty sure I was a lot more informed than most people.
My present Doctor - at every appointment - always says he learns as much from me as I do from him. He is very time poor but is always interested to learn more from Dr. K. I am very lucky - but so is he

# Thread: Stronger Heartbeat - Annoying
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stronger-heartbeat-annoying.7801/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-08-04T18:59:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stronger-heartbeat-annoying.7801/post-91500>

Last week I noticed something that has got to be really annoying.

My heart seems to be beating stronger.

I really only notice it when I lay down at night but it is making it harder to go to sleep.

It's also probably about 10 bpm faster but I would attribute that to not working out - Used to be around 55bpm now it's around 64bmp.

I should also not that my last blood pressure (8/1/13) was 100/60 - I'm a male at 195 lbs - not sure if that means anything but I've never been that low.

Lastly, the thrush on my tongue seems to be stronger than ever.

Could these all be related or any thoughts at all on the reason for the strong heartbeat would be greatly appreciated

My last TSH was 3.96 down from 4.89

Thanks for your input

### GlenPDQ — 2013-08-04T19:25:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stronger-heartbeat-annoying.7801/post-91501>

The stronger heartbeat after going VLC used to almost drive me nuts, but I figured it was a good thing overall. Its been about 15 months since then and my heartbeat is not trying to imitate a sledgehammer any more. Better circulation should speed recovery time if its any consolation.

I have considered that the effect may be because the heart prefers saturated fats as fuel instead of carbs, and most VLC diets have extra fat, so it may be acting like a kid in a candy shoppe. Then the extra ketones being circulated might contribute to the effect. It might also be that the thryroid hormones, parathyroid hormones, magnesium & calcium are being produced/used in better ways. Calcium is used to contract a muscle and magnesium is used to relax it the way I understand it. Or could it be your body is settling into a new cortisol strategy? Oxytocin near bedtime might be beneficial. Is there a dog around you could pet for 15 minutes before bedtime?

A higher protein diet with gelatin, peanut butter, shellfish, & red meat, for example, will contain more arginine. It is a precursor of nitric oxide (NO), and NO relaxes the blood vessels which might contribute to a lower Blood Pressure. (Many individuals take arginine and citrulline to get this effect.)

### fireforlunch — 2013-08-04T19:31:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stronger-heartbeat-annoying.7801/post-91502>

Thank you for your input.

Interesting points.

I do have a dog that I can pet for 15 minutes before bed.

I don't think it has anything to do with improved cortisol as it's still pretty jacked do to getting up multiple times per night at work.

> [GlenPDQ said:](/goto/post?id=91501)
>
> The stronger heartbeat after going VLC used to almost drive me nuts, but I figured it was a good thing overall. Its been about 15 months since then and my heartbeat is not trying to imitate a sledgehammer any more. Better circulation should speed recovery time if its any consolation.
>
> I have considered that the effect may be because the heart prefers saturated fats as fuel instead of carbs, and most VLC diets have extra fat, so it may be acting like a kid in a candy shoppe. Then the extra ketones being circulated might contribute to the effect. It might also be that the thryroid hormones, parathyroid hormones, magnesium & calcium are being produced/used in better ways. Calcium is used to contract a muscle and magnesium is used to relax it the way I understand it. Or could it be your body is settling into a new cortisol strategy? Oxytocin near bedtime might be beneficial. Is there a dog around you could pet for 15 minutes before bedtime?

# Thread: In need of support and direction...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-need-of-support-and-direction.7802/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### K123 — 2013-08-05T01:44:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-need-of-support-and-direction.7802/post-91517>

Hi all, my first post here or on any primal related website. I will be 32 yrs old this month. Being an Indian I was brought up eating a mainly carb diet. And since I left home and struggled my way through uni I ate an even more poor diet of bad carbs like instant noodles and tonnes of rice. Also worked night shifts to support myself so messed up my circadian rhythm for a good three years. After which I have been through a lot of emotional stress with my church and in spite of having an MBA found that I had lost my confidence to go after any of my ambitions. So I haven't worked for the last 5 years almost. I was always a chubby kid but last year my father passed away after a lot unexpected struggle and I was the only one by his side and that stress made me put on another 10 kgs in two months. So right now, I am about 26 kgs over-weight for my height. I was diagnosed with PCOS about 6 years back but my guess is that I probably had PCOS for almost 12 years or so. It took a long journey to come to Jack's website. Started with the Shwartzbein Principle and then to the Harcombe Diet and Primal Blueprint and after much trial and error over the last two years I was finally led to Jack's site and knew it is the real deal. I think Jack is a real genius who the world will recognise only too late as always. Everytime I have tried to eat by his precepts I start losing weight and my feet stop hurting. Although I have been trying to eat primal, I noticed a few months back that I was getting some vitiligo/ white spots on my face. I realised that it was because of auto-immunity and I can't do eggs for breakfast anymore. I am basically struggling to actually start a leptin reset for an auto-immune disorder and to stick to it. I start but end up giving up in a week or so. The thought of sticking to it for 90 days seems so challenging. Although I know that a lot of my troubles - spots, facial hair, falling hair on my head, a lot of excess weight, heavy periods and pain in various parts of my body...I would definitely see improvement in all of it. I just need some support to start and stick with it. Also some advice on what a person with auto-immunity should do would help. I am confused about whether cooking peppers and tomatoes actually makes it safer for me or if I need to avoid it altogether. I know Dr.K said I should do a lot of CT so trying to get used to it. Sorry for this crazy long post but thought I should give the proper background to get relevant advice. Thank you in advance to all the heroes that have gone before me!

### nonchalant — 2013-08-05T02:05:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-need-of-support-and-direction.7802/post-91519>

Hi, K123, welcome! Do you eat seafood?

### caroline — 2013-08-05T02:19:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-need-of-support-and-direction.7802/post-91521>

Welcome K123 .....You have definitely come to the right place! I don't know a lot about autoimmune but there are lots of people here that do and they will help out as soon as they have some time.

Please try to move this to the journal section - maybe open a support ticket and ask them to do that. Journals are great because you get to track your progress and we can all offer some help and support you. It would be our great pleasure to do that and you can help us as well .....everyone's story impacts all of us..

You have done so well already inspite of all the negative stuff in your life.

In my opinion - the more simple your meals are - the easier it is. That is definitely how it is for me.
I am a little confused - I need to read your post again ..... are you doing the leptin reset?
If you are having a BAB first thing in the morning \[at least 50 +gr protein\] with lots of good fat ...then that will set you up for the day. If you do this - it becomes much easier. Your cravings will go away and you will slowly need less food later in the day. Sometimes you may need even more protein than 50 g for your BAB - I did.

Have you addressed your EMF exposure? and are you following light cycles? these are the most important things to take care of.

Have you listened to Jack's latest podcasts? they help alot to connect the dots in an easy to understand way. Are you able to see the webinars? Jack is very easy to listen to and they are great for getting to the point quickly for specific things.
Also - Jack's book is a great overview of all this and it is a great reference tool to have at hand.

There is a great deal to take onboard here .... so please keep reading and asking questions - we are here to help \[and we have all been there\]

I just re read your post.....
Please don't look at this as a 90 day challenge. You need to commit to this 100% and be fully engaged. This is a journey of a lifetime - I can promise you that for sure. You will get your life back - your head will clear - so many things will improve but it is going to take time. It took you a lifetime to get here ...so it is going to take more than 90 days to fix all this stuff. Personally - I can't imagine living any other way. I am having the time of my life trying to get my head around all this science and I feel great.

It is a very amazing thing to take control of your life and your health and that is what Dr. Kruse is teaching us. You need to become informed about all aspects of your health. You need to be in the driver's seat. It truly is the journey of a life time!

### K123 — 2013-08-05T12:43:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-need-of-support-and-direction.7802/post-91545>

I love seafood nonchalant! But it gets quite expensive in London, UK to find wild/ fresh water seafood. Ate Salmon fillets this morning but to get 50 grams of protein I had to eat 2 fillets which costs about $8.70 for breakfast alone. Can't do eggs anymore since I realise I am getting white spots on my skin which is a sign of auto-immune disorder vitiligo.

### K123 — 2013-08-05T13:16:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-need-of-support-and-direction.7802/post-91546>

Hi Caroline! Thank you so much for your time and effort  I couldn't find where to raise a support ticket so I just wrote to them on the contact us page and asked how to go about it. Still getting a hang of this website and the features. I apologise for my ramblings last night...I really wanted to just start writing and stop putting it off and therefore the lack of structure and clarity. To answer some of your questions :
1) I have tried to start the Leptin Reset a few times. I manage to stick to it for about a week or so and then end up cheating because I have a sweet tooth and still struggling with sugar metabolism symptoms...as in intense hunger at times and intense craving for sweet things. We tried the Whole 30 in November last year and saw some great results. Then came to read about Leptin reset this Feb and have been trying to really get going on the Leptin Reset. Usually I fail in about a week's time...when we have been so strict for a week and the weekend comes. So no, I can't say I am on Leptin reset yet...yesterday for example I ate the BAB but at night ended up eating chocolate cake!!!  So I am really trying to get back on the horse and stay there.
2) BAB: Like I mentioned I am having auto-immune issues showing up since I ate eggs for breakfast almost everyday. So then had to quit the eggs. Which means having to figure out some easy ways to eat breakfast...but wild seafood is really expensive here...not sure how it compares to the US. My breakfast salmon cost $8.70...is that about the same?
3) EMF: Hubby is a geek so of course our tiny London flat is filled with computers and a large TV and our flat has a lot of bright ceiling lighting and the tiny open plan kitchen has the washing machine and dishwasher and what not. And since I am trying to get back to work and work from home I am really surrounded by gadgets. DH says there is no point turning off the wi-fi because the neighbours wi-fi's are just as strong and all around us. Don't even know where to start.
4) Light Cycles: I tried it a little and even bought these tiny artificial candles for the night - [https://tinyurl.com/qh2wsdz](https://tinyurl.com/qh2wsdz). But I have to confess sleeping habits are all upside down right now.
5) Webinar: I did listen to the latest webinar that Gretchen did and Dr.K came in later. It was great. That is what made me want to actually join the forum.
I have been trying to battle this out alone for a while but I see I need people to be involved. To keep me on track. A friend of mine was doing something like weight-watchers and went to these weekly meetings and it really helped her to lose and to maintain her weight. Although she is still eating bread and what not. The reason I didn't want to do that is because I know that is not the path to optimal. I want to be the best I can be. But I also have just realised what a huge difference it makes to have others involved to spur you on. So that is why I have come to ask for help finally instead of struggling alone for months. I used to love biology in school and am quite into it even now. When my dad was hospitalised, his doctors kept asking me if I had a medical background because I could discuss things with them. So I absolutely love reading Dr.K's blogposts...although it is a LOT to take in. So I am totally with you on the fact that this needs to be for life, but I am thinking on the 90 day level because I am hoping in those 90 days I will get to the point like many others here have where your cravings are but dead. And your mind is clear enough that you can choose health and not feel enslaved by carbs  If you know what I mean??

### Lahelada — 2013-08-06T00:59:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-need-of-support-and-direction.7802/post-91589>

Hello KT123 ,

I am a newbie here myself so cannot offer too much good advice. I think though there might be some shopping tips I could give you. Salmon is out of my price range here too so I looked at alternatives. Pollock,haddock,mackerel is a perfect everyday fish. You might want to have a look at Aldi's and see if they have frozen pollock. They usually have good prizes. Failing that and if you have the time Borough fish market . I hear the fish there is half the supermarket price and fresh every day ,on top of that locally sourced .Good luck.

Edit: Had a nose around .Lookee here lookee here,good prices even for wild salmon [https://www.aldi.co.uk/en/product-range/frozen/fish-seafood/](https://www.aldi.co.uk/en/product-range/frozen/fish-seafood/)

### caroline — 2013-08-06T01:53:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-need-of-support-and-direction.7802/post-91593>

Hi K. .....You are not rambling at all!

Lets talk more about the BAB.....You need to eat alot of protein/fat within 30 minutes of waking. When I started I was 62K and I quite often ate around 80+g of protein for my BAB. You need to make sure you stuff alot down!

A lot of us here have/had eating disorders of one kind or another - me included - and please understand that obesity and anorexia are the same - only opposite ends of the spectrum. We are all in this together and we have Dr. K. shining his flashlite for us on this journey of a lifetime - we are very lucky...

Back to the BAB .....You need to eat that much regardless of how hungry \[or not\] you are .... adding in a lot of fat makes it easier. A lot of us find that seafood is just not filling enough for breakfast. I often throw a leg of lamb in the crockpot and eat that and maybe add in a can of sardines. Seafood is very expensive - so do what you can..... Dr. Kruse has said this many times. If you can afford oysters morning, noon and nite ....great - go for it....but most of us here can't so we do what we can. You can also have hamburgers for breakfast and I usually add some butter on top. It is easier to think - dinner for breakfast! Always, always think outside the box....be crazy and daring!

Honestly - without question ....if you eat enough protein and fat for breakfast your cravings will go away. I used to be hungry all the time and had terrible cravings but after starting the reset I shifted down to two meals a day - and absolutely no cravings. This happened for me in about 2-3 weeks - but it can take longer. I was very focused tho ...I hated all the food cravings ruling my life!

A lot of us have a BPC for breakfast .....Bullet proof coffee ...to get extra fat in. Green tea is fine too. I add a couple of TBSP of butter and coconut oil and sometimes pure cocoa. Sometimes I just eat a slab of butter - but then I have always loved butter

What else can I tell you?
lets just recap for a minute ....maybe re read the leptin reset to make sure that you haven't missed anything

\-Following light cycles is huge - get a pair of blue blocker glasses - no TV - limit computer time

Get lots of grounding - hug a tree - walk barefoot in the grass - swim in an ocean

C/T as often as possible - have you seen the easy start guide for C/T?

That is really all there is - don't over think it right now .... of course there is a ton to read and learn here - but just take baby steps....and ask lots and lots of questions.
I once asked Dr.K. what I thought was a very dopey question and he said - no its not dopey ....the dopey thing was not asking him sooner. That taught me a big lesson.

You are doing great ...have you listened to JK's podcasts \[home page\]? The last two or three tie lots of thing together.

PM me if you want.....

### caroline — 2013-08-06T02:02:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-need-of-support-and-direction.7802/post-91595>

A couple of other things - I just re read your last post! That is so great if you love biology and have an understanding...I don't have a scientific bone in my body and really struggle ....but I definitely get the overall picture

consider being a silver or gold member - of course you don't have to .....but think of this as an investment in your life and in your health and future.

All the webinars are so helpful and if you are a gold member the Q&A's are also included and you can ask questions.....

# Thread: Getting closer
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-closer.7837/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2013-08-09T19:02:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-closer.7837/post-91939>

Last round of hormone testing to be done this weekend. I have the 24 hour saliva testing tomorrow as well as the redoing the female hormone testing but on day 21 of my cycle like the test says to do (kinda irked that my doc did not know about this or ask). I am also doing an iodine test. Once I get these done I should have a pretty good picture of what is going on and why I am still gaining weight, not sleeping though the night, foggy head, tank on empty, no interest in stuff and low libido despite having been doing the Leptin reset and cold thermogenisis for a while now.

I know my thyroid is still mucked up. Even with being on 90 mg of Armour, my RT3 to T3 ratio is about 11 (yikes) and Free T3 is 2.3, bottom of the limit. My iron and ferritin are less then optimal, my B12 is less then optimal, my D3 is less then optimal, my DHEA is less then optimal, my leptin is low, my C-reactive is higher, Creatine is below lower limit, my BUN is high and my ALP in the liver profile is low abnormal.

I do have to find a doctor to help me with this since the doctor who did all of these test apparently thinks that everything is â€œin specâ€￾ so nothing is wrong (I am a quality engineer so saying that something is fine even if it is at the bottom of the limit it so WRONG!!!!). It would have been nice if the doctor that did my test was able to take a look at all of them and see the big picture and how everything is related but he did not and honestly I really did expect that he would. Maybe once I get this all figured out I will make another appointment with him and share what I have learned with him. I think there is only so much a GP can know but if I can have him maybe try to take a look a the big picture and see that something is wrong, that would be a step in the right direction.

Getting closer.

### MsYo — 2013-08-09T21:59:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-closer.7837/post-91960>

Congrats on getting your tests and moving in the right direction.

I wouldn't worry about sharing what you learn with him. Once you improve you may want to show him your improved self. Just prioritize and tackle your health issues. I just went to a new physician yesterday (thyroid/wellness). I have been on the waiting list for about 5 months and had to pay out of pocket. It was well worth it. Based on our conversation and my past trending labs, he ordered new labs and bloodwork. He even started me on Nature Throid. Many of the supplements he offers for adrenal/thyroid/health issues I am already aware of and/or taking. This is all because of information from this forum and learning through others.

### nicld — 2013-08-11T00:14:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-closer.7837/post-92053>

I think I will share what I learned with him so if he does see someone else like me it would make him think a bit deeper then he normally would. I am so grateful that he allowed me to get the tests ran that I wanted done, not many traditional doctors would.

I am listening to the "Finding a Doctor Bootcamp" webinar and it hits home with me.

Hoping in 2 weeks I will have the last of my tests results and have seen a doctor who is familiar with this and finally be making my way through the tunnel into the light.

# Thread: Are Hormones Responsible for Weight Problem?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-hormones-responsible-for-weight-problem.7850/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lyndylou — 2013-08-11T01:10:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-hormones-responsible-for-weight-problem.7850/post-92054>

I received a 20 minute video from Realdose.com wherein the speaker (an M.D. supposedly) says my difficulty losing weight is all related to hormones. He lists adiponectic, too much ghrelin, insulin imbalance and cortisol overload as the culprits. Obviously he's trying to sell me a bottle of his magical elixir for $69.00 for one month's supply, but there were parts that sounded very similar to the Leptiburn approach. His formula includes some very exotic ingredients, coffee beans from Costa Rica, something called Rhodeala and others I didn't catch.

I'm not writing you to put him down but rather to ask if the Leptiburn would enhance your Real Dose Weight Loss Formula or cancel it out. Can they be used jointly or again, are they at odds?

I've been pretty good about remembering my Leptiburn twice a day but my food intake is nowhere near what their print-out recommended. I've been on the Leptiburn for about a month and have seen no real changes, at least none that I can appreciate. I stay around 1200 calories a day, but haven't counted carbs at all. I've been keeping my fats fairly low but after losing 60 pounds in 6 months I hit a plateau that has lasted almost four months now. Then I started exercising, again something they say not do do until I'm no longer Leptin resistant.

So basically, I'm overwhelmed with too much information coming in, and not enough hard facts (in my mind at least) to know who to listen to, or what program to follow. The price is about the same for the Leptiburn as for the Realdose but I cannot afford both.

I'd appreciate it if you'd address these questions so I know which direction I need to go.

Thanks so much.

Lyndylou

# Thread: Coursera stuff
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coursera-stuff.7865/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Albert83BCN — 2013-08-11T20:21:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coursera-stuff.7865/post-92127>

Hi there people!

I recently discovered all that Coursera thing and the possibility to learn from online courses, cool thing!

I did a search on the forum and I have already seen that some of your are taking some of the courses (I saw some threads regarding one about epigenetics). I'm enrolled right now in two and I just wanted to share with you all as they might be of some interest to someone.

First one is by the University of Pittsburg - Nutrition and Physical Activity for Health
[https://www.coursera.org/course/nutritionforhealth](https://www.coursera.org/course/nutritionforhealth)

As an endurance runner, gym guy and nutrition enthusiast I couldn't help but enroll into this one! It's easy stuff and most people here I bet already know most of the concepts, some bad things is that it falls a lot into CW from the SAD, yet you can always learn something new (and challenge what you already know).

The second one by Duke University - Introductory Human Physiology is way more difficult, I think it could be easier but the material is not very well presented, they mix a lot of non-fundamental concepts without proper introductions (IMO) but well, there's the internet to look for 'dummies' sources of info so you can rule out the meaning of all this. The concepts by themselves seem very interesting and I think they can help undestand many things of the quilt for the poor laymen as I am.

[https://www.coursera.org/course/humanphysio](https://www.coursera.org/course/humanphysio)

There are many others that seem interesting! Once I finish one of the two, I'll keep looking for more to enroll!

### nonchalant — 2013-08-11T22:01:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coursera-stuff.7865/post-92146>

Albert, be sure to check out the forum for each class. You can get into some interesting discussions in those. I have squeaked in a few of Dr. K's ideas in a couple classes. Most people have typical responses, but in a Physics class I was in several people were interested in his ideas about quantum water and humans.

# Thread: Tips to prepare for a long flight?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-to-prepare-for-a-long-flight.7873/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-08-12T17:49:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-to-prepare-for-a-long-flight.7873/post-92234>

Hi All,

Long time, no post  Some of you may remember me and some not! I haven't posted in quite a while. The beginning of the summer I went as "off grid" as I possibly could and I've been totally reaping the rewards. I've been feeling the absolute BEST I have in years. I definitely still have a long way to go, but my belief in all that Jack has been saying about EMF has strenghened tenfold! Earthing, swimming and staying away from EMF is definitely where it's at for me. I've pretty much been living outside and limiting myself to about an hour or two per week of electronic use (aside from work, of course) and it has been glorious. Any time I use my laptop for more than a few minutes my entire right hand goes numb/tingly and ice coold. It's a definite sign for me that I am on the right track. I will try to write a post detailing all my improvements in the next few weeks, but for now I'm back asking for some help.

I found out a few weeks ago I have to fly across the country for work - Seattle to be exact. We decided to make it a family vacation and bring the hubby and kids along. I've been forcing myself away from electronics even moreso than before - handwriting things instead of typing them at work, basically anything I can do to limit it. I need to know what else we should do to prep for the flight though and we leave tomorrow! Nothing like a last minute request, right?

I've been popping the CoQ10 and trying to drink even more than I did before. I'm up to about 1.5 - 2 gallons per day. I know Jack mentioned what he did to prepare for his Santa Fe flight on a webinar, but I can't remember which one and I don't want to have the laptop on long enough to listen to ALL of them. I'm thinking it may have been the mito rx . . . does anyone know for sure so I can just listen to that one? Any other tips on what to do besides push the CoQ10 and water?

We'll only be spending 1 night in Seattle and then we're heading onto Olympic National Park where we'll be soaking up nature! I plan on tons of grounding and swimming in ice-cold lakes  So yes, the long flight has me very concerned, but I'm hoping to make up for it when we get there both in CT/nature, but also with family bonding time as we always find we grow and heal immensely on these family trips. That being said, I know the flight itself is not going to be the best for me, but stressing out over it will certainly not do me any good so I'm choosing to focus on the positive and benefits I WILL see!!

### nonchalant — 2013-08-12T19:58:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-to-prepare-for-a-long-flight.7873/post-92244>

Hi BJK, of course we remember you!!! Glad to hear you are better.
I think Jack also takes lots of magnesium. I'm looking for the post.

Post #46 of this thread: [http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthre...Auto-Immune-Castle-About-at-my-wits-end/page5](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7824-Hey-folks-I-m-the-unfortunate-King-of-Auto-Immune-Castle-About-at-my-wits-end/page5)

The Mitochondrial Rx.

### cinnamon — 2013-08-12T20:02:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-to-prepare-for-a-long-flight.7873/post-92245>

I remember he said he wears leather-soled shoes with no socks (he was laughing about going through the security checkpoint). He puts his bare feet on the metal footrest in front of his seat while on the plane and then he grounds as soon as possible after landing.

### BJK77 — 2013-08-12T20:38:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-to-prepare-for-a-long-flight.7873/post-92252>

Thanks for the tips guys!

Nonchalant - thanks so much for linking to that post! Such a recent communcation too, perfect! Looks like I am somewhat on the right track with the Ubiquinol. I know Jack said how much he took on one of the webinars and I'm thinking it was 1600-2400 mg. I'm going to find some time this afternoon to listen to the mito rx and see if that's where he said it. I need to increase the Mg too.

cinnamon - I was remembering the part about grounding using the metal footrest. I told my kids to plan on wearing flip flops to the airport . . . of course there were no complaints about that!

### caroline — 2013-08-13T01:48:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-to-prepare-for-a-long-flight.7873/post-92298>

Hi BJ .... so glad you are feeling a lot better ... and of course we remember you!! You are such a motivated and strong young woman .... have a wonderful family trip. Are you going to be sleeping under the stars? Don't forget - lots of oxy

I love how hard you have been working at all this - taking it all onboard and implementing it. You have a great attitude and an open mind and you are doing the research and figuring out what you need to do to improve your health. I applaud you.

I can't wait to hear what you have been up to - when you have time of course...
