# Thread: Healthy begginning
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healthy-begginning.7878/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raylynn — 2013-08-12T21:57:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healthy-begginning.7878/post-92274>

I started the leptin reset 8/3 and hope to continue to be healthy. I have reset before, about 2 years ago, but went downhill with a hospitalization for asthma and pneumonia. Lost my way until now. I have always eaten a BAB, but ate horribly the rest of the day and am now up about 30 pounds from 2 years ago. I will have surgery in one week and be home for 3 months. I am trying to get set so I can eat my best the next three months in hopes of healing faster.
I love and believe in Dr. Kruse's advice. I know it works, since I did it before. I felt the best I have ever felt as an adult at that time.
Since I started last week, I have begun waking up at least once a night sweating. I know Dr. Kruse says women will have a change in sweat patterns to begin with, even before losing weight. I hope this is what is happening to me. Also, I have begun having very vivid dreams. I did this last time, so I hope this means I am on the right road.
I have one question? I wonder if I am eating too much food. I eat til I am full, but afterwards feel too full. Should I slow down when I eat to let my brain catch up with my stomach? I hate that bloated feeling and want to feel full but not bloated.
Also, I am an operating room nurse, so don't get but 30 minutes for lunch, and it is hard to feel full in that short time.
Hope to continue to post on here after my surgery to keep me motivated.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-12T22:18:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healthy-begginning.7878/post-92277>

Hi Raylynn, welcome!
I rarely eat until full; I eat until I am disinterested. I'm not sure when I got to the point in which my body started telling me that. I just eat plenty of seafood and meat and fat, and at some point a switch seems to go off when I realize I have lost interest in eating and I start think about doing something else. It may be leptin, not sure about that. Even though I have lost interest, I may or may not finish eating. Depends on what it is. Usually I would eat the protein/fat first, then if I still wanted something, I would eat the veggies until I got bored.

You aren't eating too much, provided you are chowing down on animal/seafood fat and protein.

### caroline — 2013-08-13T01:06:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healthy-begginning.7878/post-92295>

Hi Raylynn.....I definitely do much better if I add in more fat and less protein - like you I would feel bloated and lethargic with too much protein. Have you tried having a BPC for breakfast? I usually have 2 and some raw meat and I am good to go. For me - I space my meal out over an hour or so - kinda gives my brain a chance to catch up!

Hi ,
I'm a 49 year old male , eating lchf for some time now , try to move natural as much as possible .
But have problems losing weight !

I had a hormone saliva test done , and the result show a PG to E2 ratio of 5.56 ?
Estradiol E2 is to high and Progesterone to low .
Testosteron is high .
Cortisol is also low ( Is this why I have a hard time starting the day )
DHEA is high .......is the immune system fighting something ??

Estradiol 11.20 pg/ml
Cortisol 3.9 ng/ml morning
1.9 ng/ml midday
2.4 ng/ml evening
DHEA 546.30 pg/ml
Estriol 32.80 pg/ml
Progesteron 72.40 pg/ml ( 0.0724 ng/ml ?? )
Testosteron 183,10 pg/ml

Would love to know what the test is saying ,

gr, Tony

shower in cold water
within 30 min from getting off bed, eat BAB, BigAssBreakfast, that is 50% of daily food
lunch
dinner, end of dinner 4 hrs before bed time
orgasm
sleep

# Thread: Falling off the CT wagon and getting back on
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/falling-off-the-ct-wagon-and-getting-back-on.7894/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ColdKnight — 2013-08-14T06:15:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/falling-off-the-ct-wagon-and-getting-back-on.7894/post-92470>

I was successfully CT'ing 7 days in a row at 50 degrees everything but hands in for 25 minutes. I would reheat in about 30 minutes. I noticed various benefits even with just 7 days under my belt. I was more tolerable of the cold, felt more energetic and smarter, and was more resistant to pain to name a few. I did spot icing with 20lbs of ice on my torso for 3 days or so before I built an outdoor tub and started the full immersions. Does anyone have experience with stopping the protocol and getting back on the wagon? Should I start with spot icing again to make sure I don't get cold uticarra before switching back to the immersions again? How long does it take to unadapt?

### nonchalant — 2013-08-14T15:48:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/falling-off-the-ct-wagon-and-getting-back-on.7894/post-92492>

No, just jump back in. Depending on how long it has been, you might take a couple baths before you are back to your normal reaction to the cold.

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-14T17:59:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/falling-off-the-ct-wagon-and-getting-back-on.7894/post-92505>

Sweet! I will report my findings soon  I forgot to mention it has been about 2 weeks since I stopped.

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-15T20:20:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/falling-off-the-ct-wagon-and-getting-back-on.7894/post-92653>

I'm so excited to find that after taking a break from CT for about 2.5 weeks, I was easily able to restart the full immersions. I feel like my thermoregulation was actually better than my last CT before the break. I do have to admit that I prepped myself pretty well by not eating sugar foods the day of, and working on keeping my o3 to o6 ratio along with magnesium optimal. I had two 3 egg yolk/cinnamon/vanilla/grassfed butter/cacao Bulletproof coffees spread out(one in morning, one midday,) 4 eggs fried over easy & bacon, and seared salmon with caramelized onions & carrots for dinner pre-CT. I went for 15 minutes as opposed to my usual 25 in 50-54 degree water.

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-15T20:21:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/falling-off-the-ct-wagon-and-getting-back-on.7894/post-92655>

Anyone else have experience with starts and stops? What is the longest time someone has went without and came back? How was the experience starting again?

### endless — 2013-08-15T20:24:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/falling-off-the-ct-wagon-and-getting-back-on.7894/post-92658>

I'm about to restart after a hiatus....and I've been craving it so I think re-entry is going to be just fine!

### cantweight — 2013-08-15T20:45:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/falling-off-the-ct-wagon-and-getting-back-on.7894/post-92661>

Ive started and stopped 100 times...selling a house, moving, renovating....for a million reasons I lost tub time. I always jumped back in to a full soak and often with a renewed vigor for colder temps and longer times. My body craves CT. As soon as my kids are back to school I am back in the tub....draggin my hubby with me....it was fascinating for me to watch the effects it had on him as I am fat and he is fit so very different experiences

### nicld — 2013-08-15T20:58:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/falling-off-the-ct-wagon-and-getting-back-on.7894/post-92663>

I would LOVE to get my hubby to try it but his response is H E Double Hockey sticks NO. Some of my healthy living is rubbing off on him but not enough and it just makes me kinda sad. Hate to say it, but I don't want to have to take care of him when his health is failing and mine is strong because of the food and lifestlye choices that he made. I want the both of use to grow old together and healthy. Maybe someday he'll get it and yes I have asked him to read some of the stuff I am reading but he declines.

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-16T01:05:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/falling-off-the-ct-wagon-and-getting-back-on.7894/post-92692>

Ahh yes nicld quite the quandary. I haven't found a wife yet and I would love for her to be into this. Sadly, I feel we striving for optimal are a minority of the population. Maybe though. It's probably best to have faith that things will go the way you want them to.

### cantweight — 2013-08-17T02:10:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/falling-off-the-ct-wagon-and-getting-back-on.7894/post-92874>

My hubs and kids thought I was nuts!

Hubs smiled and nodded as I went on and on and on about things I read in Dr. K's blog.

5 weeks later the twinkle in my eyes and spring in my step had them lining up to get in the tub!

I was shocked! Hubs ended up encouraging me to add more ice and up my frequency. He got on my case about EMF testing....I was speechless.

Just give it time

### caroline — 2013-08-17T02:20:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/falling-off-the-ct-wagon-and-getting-back-on.7894/post-92877>

As I have said before and found to be so true ..... when you walk the walk - you get to talk the talk......people are observing you - they really are paying attention ... make no mistake about that....

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ready-set-start-laughing-what-if-it-works-though.7896/post-92473>

Alright. So, I went to my Homeopath/ M.D. today, the venerable Dr. Abram Ber. I told him about Dr. Jack Kruse and his work. He said, "of course he's right; we've known for sometime that this signal based technology would result in adverse biological change". I queried,"then why I'm I doing this Homeopathy stuff?" He replied, "because of the loss of the brain/gut connection your immune system is shot, you have issues with bacteria/mold and intestinal permeability." He asked for Dr. Kruse's website. He just got back from a alternative medicine clinic in Montreal and gave me DefensAid. Apparently it was researched and formulated by some Russian Docs. Seems a little far out to me. Hey what the hell, I'll try anything once. Supposedly it's crushed gemstones in three pills. You take one every third evening and it's suppose to help your body with EMF.........I know.......I know.........You're all laughing at me now. Well, let me be the guinea pig who potentially wasted my money. BTW, I ordered the Magnetico......after speaking with Dr. Bonlie, I feel a lot better about that purchase!

Sounds like it could be a great idea - can't wait to hear more ...... you are sounding happier - that is a step in the right direction  Good luck with the magnetico! I hope it works for you......

(Hello, this was a BIG reveal for all of us!) Dr. Kruse made a special appearance at the end and joined us for a Q&A to boot.

Week Twoâ€™s call is **THIS THURSDAY, August 15, 2013.** When you sign up, weâ€™ll send you the link to watch last weekâ€™s call and access all the forms Gretchen shared to help you confirm your coverage, appeal your claims, plus templates to help you begin building your health history and tracking your symptoms (your docs will need this to justify many Rxâ€™s and treatments). Weâ€™ll also send you call-in info for this weekâ€™s call. I hope you all will plan to join us for this POWERFUL series!

[**As a Bronze member, you can register HERE for this event NOW for only $199!**](http://www.jackkruse.com/finding-a-doctor-bootcamp-2/)

\*\*All calls are on Thursdays at 7 pm CT; replays are Sundays at 6 pm CT.\*\*

# Thread: I so want to do another round
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-so-want-to-do-another-round.7902/>
**Subforum:** HCG Protocol
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2013-08-14T16:44:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-so-want-to-do-another-round.7902/post-92498>

I am uncomfortable with the weight that I have gained since my last round even though I have ate properly. With the hormones being off, my body just did not reset.

One side of my brain has it all planned out, what day to start on and how long to go to and even planted a garden that would support another round. The other logical part of my brain is saying no way, you are not hormonally well enough to do another round and have it be successful when you are done. If doing 3 rounds of HCG did not â€œfixâ€￾ the hormones why do another one until you are balanced. Maybe, I will not need to do another round.

Oh the struggles in my mind.

### caroline — 2013-08-15T07:10:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-so-want-to-do-another-round.7902/post-92605>

Dr. K. isn't a fan of HCG exactly ....I hope I am not putting words in his mouth....
You are doing epi-paleo right? and C/T Why don't you give that a chance and get your hormones straight first and then maybe......

I know we are all so impatient - me too. When I look back - it really only has been 10 or 11 months and huge changes have happened.....

### nicld — 2013-08-15T14:11:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-so-want-to-do-another-round.7902/post-92627>

I know that it is not recommended and knowing that my hormones are off any loss probably won't stick but the urge is still great. I am sticking to leptin reset and ct. I see a new doctor in about 10 days and hope he can help me fix myself. I have a feeling that I am estrogen dominant based on slot of the symptoms and tests. HCG won't fix that.

# Thread: Question for the ladies
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-ladies.7910/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2013-08-15T16:20:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-ladies.7910/post-92636>

I am finding the past two weeks that I am unable to sleep through the night when there were times in the past that I was. I have been waking up between 2 and 3 with hot feet and rarely get back to sleep. It just so happened to be my luteal phase of my cycle. I just started my cycle today and last night I did sleep a little better and handled the CT bath a little better. I am very curious to see if it gets better now that the cycle has started. I am still waiting for some more results of my hormonal testing but in doing more research and really analyzing my labs, I thinking I might be estrogen dominant and this would make sense if I have more issues after ovulation.

**Has anybody else noticed a change in sleep in correlation to their cycle? If they did and went on BIHT did it get better?**

Also, my cold tolerance is horrible right now. I have had to shorted the length of CT and increase the temp due to just not being able to rewarm before bed. I will be curious to see how it goes next week when my hormones shift.

The puzzle is coming together a little more each day.

Nicole

### HoneyChild — 2013-08-15T21:25:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-ladies.7910/post-92668>

Yes, I've posted before about how I wake up in the night feeling hot and also very thirsty and noticed that this occurred in the luteal phase. It's happened to me the last couple of nights about 2am although it hasn't been happening every month. When I got saliva testing done from ZRT I was low in both estrogen and progesterone and the PG:E2 ratio was 82.

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-15T22:24:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-ladies.7910/post-92676>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=92636)
>
> I am finding the past two weeks that I am unable to sleep through the night when there were times in the past that I was. I have been waking up between 2 and 3 with hot feet and rarely get back to sleep. It just so happened to be my luteal phase of my cycle. I just started my cycle today and last night I did sleep a little better and handled the CT bath a little better. I am very curious to see if it gets better now that the cycle has started. I am still waiting for some more results of my hormonal testing but in doing more research and really analyzing my labs, I thinking I might be estrogen dominant and this would make sense if I have more issues after ovulation.
>
> **Has anybody else noticed a change in sleep in correlation to their cycle? If they did and went on BIHT did it get better?**
>
> Also, my cold tolerance is horrible right now. I have had to shorted the length of CT and increase the temp due to just not being able to rewarm before bed. I will be curious to see how it goes next week when my hormones shift.
>
> The puzzle is coming together a little more each day.
>
> Nicole

We've had this discussion in the CT threads as well as several of the BHRT Threads. when my PG starts to drop I can't stand the cold. Remember Pg = Cold; why think hibernating pregnant bears. Estrogen attracts heat and dislikes cold.

As for sleep, for me my sleep issues arise when I'm in the last week of my cycle just before TOTM, and during TOTM + 5 days. for me BHRT has helped I'm sleeping better.. across the whole cycle... but as hormones aren't static there are days where I don't sleep as well as I could.

### nicld — 2013-08-16T16:50:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-ladies.7910/post-92775>

Thanks girls for letting me know I am not alone. This is the first time that I have been really looking into how I feel and what time of my cycle that I am in. Bad thing is my cycle has been getting shorter and shorter (much to the hubby's and my dislike). Every 25 days now when it used to be longer....another clue? I have 10 days until I see a new doc and I my latest round of hormone testing is going to be delivered to the lab today and should have results back next week.

I will have to check out the BHRT thread tonight during CT. Hoping I can tolerate it better then last week, that was brutal.

### HoneyChild — 2013-08-16T18:24:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-ladies.7910/post-92790>

My cycles have also been getting shorter, 25 days has become normal for me. I don't know how to interpret that, especially because I started quite a few supplements at the same time including maca which is supposed to boost progesterone. I thought that would lengthen my cycles but it hasn't.

### nicld — 2013-08-16T21:15:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-ladies.7910/post-92827>

I am seriously not happy about them shortening too. That means I am going to have almost 15 cycles a year and not 12. Not exactly my idea of fun. I wonder why the shorten too as we age (or that is my case at least).

### nonchalant — 2013-08-16T21:49:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-ladies.7910/post-92829>

What about synchronizing your cycles to the moon?

# Thread: Making all this affordable for us poor folk.....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-all-this-affordable-for-us-poor-folk.7917/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-08-16T03:05:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-all-this-affordable-for-us-poor-folk.7917/post-92717>

I am thinking maybe we should talk about this some more....post suggestions for keeping cost down.

I think it has likely all been said before - but maybe we need a refresher.....

So .....Gretchen is telling/teaching you how to keep your medical costs down - how to leverage your insurance company etc. finding great Docs and being informed.

I think we have forgotten \[a little\] How Dr. K. has said that food is just 10%......

So if we can't afford oysters every day or some of you don't even have access to fresh seafood - what to do?

Dr. K. has said this repeatedly - a can of sardines will do. He has never once said that you need to buy the most expensive seafood ... do what you can.

In my opinion - frozen seafood can be a bargain and often is flash frozen on ships - this is way better than getting fresh at a super market - you never have any idea how long it has been in transit.....

Liver is so cost effective, as well as heart and tongue. One of the paleo sites has some great looking recipes for tongue. I have never had it but I am definitely going to give it a go!

Also - great recipes for ground beef. Do you get emails from nom nom paleo? \[hope that is the right name\]
She just posted a great one the other day. Her recipes are great ....I have had cooking classes with a lot of her mentors and they are impeccable.

What prompted this you might ask? I find I am worried a lot less about food these days. If food is only 10%....we have a lot bigger fish to fry

Any suggestions? ideas? How do you feed a family on epi-paleo?

### caroline — 2013-08-16T03:21:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-all-this-affordable-for-us-poor-folk.7917/post-92723>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=92719)
>
> bingo..........

I like getting a "bingo" from you ..... I always feel like such a lite weight around here....

### Lahelada — 2013-08-16T16:30:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-all-this-affordable-for-us-poor-folk.7917/post-92774>

\- ask the butcher for bones for the dog
\- relearn old recipes from Victorian cookbooks like Mrs Beeton . Fish was a staple for breakfast even then and many recipes for the use of offal and cheap cuts . I suggest a bit more modern spicing though
\- what supplements do you really need ? Narrow it down. How much can you get from food ?
\- Fresh Fish.: look for ethnic stores that have a fish culture. Here it is Asian shops. Their prices are 20%-40% better than local fish shops. Londoners consider Borough fish market.

Thanks for bringing this up, Caroline.

### cantweight — 2013-08-16T17:41:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-all-this-affordable-for-us-poor-folk.7917/post-92780>

Sp appreciative of all ideas on this thread. I am one of those having trouble making it all work. There are so many things I want to purchase.....like magneticos.....that if I could tighten the food cost I might be able to.

My biggest challenge is that we are a family of 5. My kids are 13, 11, and 8 and they can put away some food! I love to see them devour a ton of fish.....but that fish is 9-32 dollars a pound!!

We get our beef from local farms it is better priced than grocery stores but not cheap 5-19 dollars per pound. Eggs from these farms are 4.75 a dozen. Bacon is 7.29 for 12 oz. You can see why I am struggling.

We have replaced our probiotics with raw milk....that is a big savings. And have also pared down the supplements to what really seems to help each one of us.

Bone broths, soups, and home made jerky are my next pursuits. School lunches are my nemesis hoping the broth and jerky will fit the bill!!!

Thanks for the thread Audrey  Asian shops are great idea Lahelada!

### Lahelada — 2013-08-16T17:56:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-all-this-affordable-for-us-poor-folk.7917/post-92783>

In the spirit of your signature  how about taking up fishing as a hobby ? N.VA has some amazing lake fish, not the same as ocean fish but free well almost. I don't know how feasible that is of course for you.

Your beef prices made me swallow hard. That is heavy duty and I can understand why you are struggling. My beef is from the only supermarket that guarantees grassfed beef and it ranges from $1 for soup meat to 10$$ for a fine steak.

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-16T20:34:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-all-this-affordable-for-us-poor-folk.7917/post-92820>

International markets are great. I get a 1lb bag of frozen mixed seafood (squid, cuttle fish, mussels etc) for any where from $2/lb to $4/lb - compare to Trader Joes where a similar bag -is $12/lb

Mackerel, blue fin, this week we bought skate - and made that. it was something like $3.99/lb

the international mkts also carry organ meats

Consider finding a Halal or Kosher butcher/market - better prices, and usually better foods.

look for local farmers and talk to them - see what things they offer people who buy from them directly.... you might be surprised...

and CANNED Sardines, Mackerel and oysters are essential...

### caroline — 2013-08-17T01:32:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-all-this-affordable-for-us-poor-folk.7917/post-92868>

A local fish shop here throws fish heads away - no one wants them! great for broth

A recent post of Dan's caught my eye - and, actually was part of the reason for this thread. Someone asked him what he was doing and eating on a daily basis. He is very committed to all of this and when he talked about food he said he doesn't always buy GF.

So - I thought - we would survive if we had regular beef once in a while or chicken once in a while - not the most optimum - but hey ...food is only 10% ....I think we need to give ourselves a break!

Maybe I am just lucky - I have eaten in some incredible restaurants and loved them ..... but ..... I also love meatloaf \[probably my favorite thing\] I adore a recipe from Paul Prudhomme ....and you guys could feel like you are in the Gulf!
I love meatballs and sloppy joes and kibbe etc. - I could go on and on.
It is very easy to make your own sausages - in fact JK had some great looking ones on FB awhile back that he said he made.
What about salmon cakes made with canned salmon? or crab cakes? and you could really feel like you are on the gulf!

I thought I had got over my food passion - maybe I need to re direct it!

Let's talk more .... what other ideas do you have?

what about sushi? If I need to go out for lunch .... i will have sushi - I love it and don't feel as tho I have committed a sin of some kind ..... we are in the driver's seat guys......

### caroline — 2013-08-17T01:38:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-all-this-affordable-for-us-poor-folk.7917/post-92869>

> [cantweight said:](/goto/post?id=92780)
>
> Sp appreciative of all ideas on this thread. I am one of those having trouble making it all work. There are so many things I want to purchase.....like magneticos.....that if I could tighten the food cost I might be able to.
>
> My biggest challenge is that we are a family of 5. My kids are 13, 11, and 8 and they can put away some food! I love to see them devour a ton of fish.....but that fish is 9-32 dollars a pound!!
>
> We get our beef from local farms it is better priced than grocery stores but not cheap 5-19 dollars per pound. Eggs from these farms are 4.75 a dozen. Bacon is 7.29 for 12 oz. You can see why I am struggling.
>
> We have replaced our probiotics with raw milk....that is a big savings. And have also pared down the supplements to what really seems to help each one of us.
>
> Bone broths, soups, and home made jerky are my next pursuits. School lunches are my nemesis hoping the broth and jerky will fit the bill!!!
>
> Thanks for the thread Audrey  Asian shops are great idea Lahelada!

This Nom Nom site I was talking about - she had a great Asian- or maybe Indian recipe for ground beef and served it in lettuce cups but also has a link to a flat bread recipe ...I will check it out and get back to you

### cantweight — 2013-08-18T19:12:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-all-this-affordable-for-us-poor-folk.7917/post-93042>

# Thread: Cinnamon and/or Apple Cider Vinegar?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MeghanK — 2013-08-16T04:53:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/post-92739>

Anyone use these if/when they eat carbs such as sweet potatoes or a starchier fruit? 1 tsp of cinnamon or 1 TBSP of Apple Cider vinegar... suppose to help with the insulin spike?

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-08-16T06:44:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/post-92753>

Yeah, use lot's of it. I believe Ceylon Cinnamon is the ideal form of cinnamon since it won't thin your blood like others - yet it still has beneficial compounds, especially in regards to insulin and blood glucose. Ben Greenfield said something about a few tbsp/shots of ACV before a meal helps the insulin spike of foods and helps you stay in ketosis, but I don't know of any studies that show that affect. In the 4-Hour Body, Tim Ferriss states that he and all his researchers never really found anything in the literature about vinegar and the glycemic and/or insulinogenic load of foods, yet Cinnamon and citric juices helped lower it when taken before a meal or used on a meal.

### MeghanK — 2013-08-16T17:18:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/post-92778>

Hmmm... I thought it was Cassia Cinnamon according to Tim Ferris.....

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-08-16T17:53:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/post-92782>

Typing "ceylon" in the forum search field revealed this thread.

[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6776-Blood-sugar-clarifications&highlight=ceylon](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6776-Blood-sugar-clarifications&highlight=ceylon)

### Raiken3712 — 2013-08-16T18:23:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/post-92789>

Is that true about Ceylon not helping with blood sugar? I think I have Ceylon...though I forget..its been awhile since I bought it.I've heard it has less coumarin too not none. Need some clarification cause now I'm confused.

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-16T18:55:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/post-92798>

Ya, and why should we use Ceylon then? For taste alone? There is no benefit beyond maybe some mere antioxidants? Quite the quandary.

### Danco3636 — 2013-08-16T19:56:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/post-92806>

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=92739)
>
> Anyone use these if/when they eat carbs such as sweet potatoes or a starchier fruit? 1 tsp of cinnamon or 1 TBSP of Apple Cider vinegar... suppose to help with the insulin spike?

Yes I do but I also don't do carbs..... I do: PPP - Epi Paleo - Keto
I take it in the morning first thing before a CT session...... that and Bitter Melon and a big cold class of Perrier water... Then a walk barefoot outside -shorts, no shirt in the sun (sunrise) for some grounding......
Then some Morning BP coffee...... and time with the wife enjoying some more outdoor time talking....

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-16T20:01:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/post-92808>

Sounds like what I just started doing. Although I've been sitting outside in the sun barefoot in shorts only with my BP coffee. If anyone hasn't tried Mark Sisson's idea about adding 3 egg yolks to a cooled down BP coffee, they really should consider. Its delicious. I add cacoa and Ceylon cinnamon too.

### MeghanK — 2013-08-16T21:06:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/post-92822>

[http://www.askmen.com/celebs/interview\_400/493c\_tim-ferriss-interview.html](http://www.askmen.com/celebs/interview_400/493c_tim-ferriss-interview.html)
I guess I should have specified... I have Saigon Cassia Cinnamon fresh ground from a local spice company.... Saigon is what Tim recommends...

3\. Cassia Cinnamon Cheat

Cinnamon helps keep blood sugars in check, which helps control weight gain. Both Cassia and Ceylon cinnamon help regulate glucose levels, but some studies show that Cassia does it better.

To determine the difference between these types of cinnamon, Ceylon sticks roll up from both sides like a scroll. Cassia looks more like bark and will often roll up from one side like a cigar. Use Cassia cinnamon in your coffee or even sprinkle it on a bowl of oatmeal or cottage cheese. Cassia cinnamon, it turns out, is the type you find in most coffee shops. Look for it at grocery stores for about $5. If unavailable, look for it at specialty food stores.

### MeghanK — 2013-08-16T21:07:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/post-92823>

Danco which do you use?

### Lahelada — 2013-08-16T21:09:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/post-92824>

Cassia in high amounts is to be avoided for its blood thinning qualities due its coumarin content which is according to some source 1000 times higher. The German "FDA" has done some studies on it I believe.

Re your use of lowering food GI that has been making the rounds since at least the South beach diet,came back via a facebook pos but hard data I have not seen. I would take it however for its antinflammatory properties which have hundreds of years of anecdotal evidence .

### MeghanK — 2013-08-16T21:13:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/post-92826>

Is 1 tsp a day considered a high amount?

### Lahelada — 2013-08-16T21:38:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/post-92828>

Yes,personally I think so. Have a read on this site. It is in German, but it refers to the studies done in Germany. Google translator does a decent enough job to make it understandable.
[http://www.bfr.bund.de/de/presseinformation/2012/26/cassia\_zimt\_mit\_hohen\_cumaringehalten\_nur\_massvoll\_verzehren-131683.html](http://www.bfr.bund.de/de/presseinformation/2012/26/cassia_zimt_mit_hohen_cumaringehalten_nur_massvoll_verzehren-131683.html)

### Danco3636 — 2013-08-16T23:32:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/post-92847>

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=92823)
>
> Danco which do you use?

just a ground cinnamon spice I got at Costco....

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-17T02:09:33+0300

### MeghanK — 2013-08-18T16:08:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-powder-instead-of-liver.7922/post-93025>

OH MY GOSH... I tried it this morning with L-Glutamine and Cinnamon (thinking the cinnamon would mask it) and EEEEWWWWW... any ideas?

### Danco3636 — 2013-08-18T16:31:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-powder-instead-of-liver.7922/post-93026>

I have my freezer stocked with grass-fed beef liver but I do take powdered liver as well if I am not cooking it.... Makes it easy when I am on the go or with limited time. I usually throw it in my BCAA's/ spurilina/ D ribose mix for a post strength workout drink before a meal.

### MeghanK — 2013-08-19T17:48:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-powder-instead-of-liver.7922/post-93113>

Don't you still taste it with just spurilina/D-Ribose? I also have Chorella Powder which I haven't figured out how to take because I can't stand the taste???

### cantweight — 2013-08-19T20:48:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-powder-instead-of-liver.7922/post-93131>

We get our chlorella in tabs and capsules. You could capsulate your powders?

Our spirulina is also in tabs...find it much easier to get down than powders.

I have a supplement called Energizing Iron from Integrative Therapeutics, Inc.
It is a capsule similar to a big fish oil pill. It contains liquid beef liver fractions, B12, iron, sodium, and trace minerals.

Maybe that would be easier for you to consume than the powder?

### Danco3636 — 2013-08-19T23:49:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-powder-instead-of-liver.7922/post-93150>

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=93113)
>
> Don't you still taste it with just spurilina/D-Ribose? I also have Chorella Powder which I haven't figured out how to take because I can't stand the taste???

I don't mind the taste.... and like spurilina.... I have it in powder form. My wife enjoys it too, not the liver and blends it in her smoothie for a "Big Green". I have acquired a taste for liver along with raw or cooked oysters. Love the stuff! That and "Bacon"! lol. Just had liver on the BBQ last night with curry powder on it for seasoning.... seared on the outside and a bit raw on the inside. It was a good creamy texture. I know it may sound awful but I liked it.....  and so does my dog..... She will live forever...... lol

The D-Ribose seems to give it a bit of a sweet taste.

### caroline — 2013-08-20T03:53:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-powder-instead-of-liver.7922/post-93181>

Have you tried making a chicken liver pate? I know not optimal - but the taste of chicken liver is very mild and in pate ...it is delicious. I just bought some beautiful chicken liver from a farmer - delicious!

The first time I ever had chicken liver - it was made by a friend. He sauteed it quickly with onions and celery and green peppers ...I was hooked!

Often the secret to eating something you think you don't like is to order it in a restaurant or have someone else make it for you.
....especially liver .... it can kinda gross you out a bit!

try liver and onions and cauli mash....

### caroline — 2013-08-20T04:10:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-powder-instead-of-liver.7922/post-93183>

I thought the recipe for chicken liver pate was here somewhere but I can't find it. It is very easy to make and so good.
If anyone is interested I am happy to post it.......
I make it sometimes now with lamb's liver but don't like it anywhere near as much - it is much stronger....I think you guys have calf's liver - that might be okay ...we can't get that here for some reason...

### cantweight — 2013-08-20T04:29:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-powder-instead-of-liver.7922/post-93185>

### caroline — 2013-08-20T04:48:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/liver-powder-instead-of-liver.7922/post-93189>

Christi - it would be great for kid's lunches maybe? why don't we start another thread?

### cantweight — 2013-08-23T23:56:45+0300

### MeghanK — 2013-08-24T17:55:57+0300

# Thread: Glass or Stainless Steel for water?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glass-or-stainless-steel-for-water.7924/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2013-08-16T18:31:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glass-or-stainless-steel-for-water.7924/post-92792>

I take a BPA free bottle to work and sometimes two...when I can find the lid...Anyway I've been contemplating switching in case the leeching is harming me.

Hopefully its not just placebo...but does anyone have any experience changing from using plastic to metal or glass and actually feeling an improvement in health. Its a hard plastic bottle..I wouldn't think it would leech much if at all...but I don't know for sure. I want a bottle or perhaps a pair of bottles that I can take to work and drink from for the 7 hours I'm there. 3:30-10:30 typically. A 20 ounce bottle like my current plastic ones wouldn't be bad. I've heard much about BPA free sometimes containing even worse substitutes, but I have no clue if thats the case with my bottles.

[http://www.academy.com/webapp/wcs/stores/servlet/Product\_10151\_10051\_26965\_-1?kwid=ps\_pla&mkid=PLA](http://www.academy.com/webapp/wcs/stores/servlet/Product_10151_10051_26965_-1?kwid=ps_pla&mkid=PLA)

So its 24 ounces...anyway I just said 20 because it doesn't actually show 24 on the measurements on the side. I guess cause thats at the top of the bottle.

I posted this in diet because it involves ingestion...even though its generally water. I guess it doesn't matter too much...the monster thread would be the other logical place.

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-16T18:42:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glass-or-stainless-steel-for-water.7924/post-92795>

I think water from glass definitely tastes better. I'm sure there are other things to worry about in plastic besides BPA. However it's made from petroleum, depending on how you look at that, it is a natural substance on earth. Maybe we worry about its effects on us more than we need to. I'm sure breathing in the fumes from burning gasoline isn't good for us, but is sticking your head over a fire of wood good for you?

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-16T18:43:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glass-or-stainless-steel-for-water.7924/post-92796>

I use a large old apple juice container. This holds a few liters.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-16T18:51:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glass-or-stainless-steel-for-water.7924/post-92797>

> [ColdKnight said:](/goto/post?id=92795)
>
> I'm sure there are other things to worry about in plastic besides BPA.

I agree. Just because BPA is the first toxic substance they have identified/admitted to, doesn't mean there are more. I believe plastics contain dozens of xenoestrogens.

We've been using glass canning jars. Cheap, but they occasionally break. And they sweat. And the lids have some sort of plastic film on them to prevent rusting.

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-16T18:59:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glass-or-stainless-steel-for-water.7924/post-92800>

nonchalant, You think they add more than just petrol to plastic?

### nonchalant — 2013-08-16T19:09:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glass-or-stainless-steel-for-water.7924/post-92803>

I'll see if I can find the article.

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-16T19:11:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glass-or-stainless-steel-for-water.7924/post-92804>

I use a 3 gallon bpa free plastic container for short term water storage( like 2-3 days). I also keep it cold so I'm hoping if there is stuff to leech it doesn't have enough time to do much. This might force me to look at huge glass containers or stainless steel. I can't see a problem with stainless.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-16T19:16:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glass-or-stainless-steel-for-water.7924/post-92805>

I found some glass carboys available at a local home brew supply store. I bought a 3 gallon one, because I'm wimpy. But the 5 and 6 gallon ones were about the same price. It has a rubber stopper. I also picked up a few 1 gallon glass jugs. Looks like I'm making moonshine!
Yes, I have been using two 7 gallon bpa free plastic containers for storage over a couple weeks. You do what you can.

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-16T20:03:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glass-or-stainless-steel-for-water.7924/post-92811>

Yes, I've had people call my apple cider jug moonshine. hehe. I hope I can find some huge glass ones like those.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glass-or-stainless-steel-for-water.7924/post-92908>

The stress involved in worrying about every little thing might be more detrimental than the actual things...No clue what kind of plastic a typical 5 gallon RO jug has....blah!!!

# Thread: Xanthelasma?  LDL turning to foam cells!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/xanthelasma-ldl-turning-to-foam-cells.7925/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HoneyChild — 2013-08-16T18:31:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/xanthelasma-ldl-turning-to-foam-cells.7925/post-92793>

I'm not sure when this started happening (maybe in the last week or so) but I've now got light orange patches on the skin around my eyes. After doing some research I came across the condition Xanthelasma, which is where oxidized LDL is taken up by macrophages and turned into foam cells. They then accumulate around the eyes to produce pretty horrible looking orange lumps. Mine are nowhere near as bad as the ones in the wiki page photo but they are in the same locations, only mine are flat.

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Xanthelasma](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Xanthelasma)
[http://www.gpnotebook.co.uk/simplepage.cfm?ID=422903818](http://www.gpnotebook.co.uk/simplepage.cfm?ID=422903818)

Any thoughts?

### Anna Dunn — 2026-02-02T00:35:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/xanthelasma-ldl-turning-to-foam-cells.7925/post-366221>

> [HoneyChild said:](/goto/post?id=92793)
>
> I'm not sure when this started happening (maybe in the last week or so) but I've now got light orange patches on the skin around my eyes. After doing some research I came across the condition Xanthelasma, which is where oxidized LDL is taken up by macrophages and turned into foam cells. They then accumulate around the eyes to produce pretty horrible looking orange lumps. Mine are nowhere near as bad as the ones in the wiki page photo but they are in the same locations, only mine are flat.
>
> [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Xanthelasma](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Xanthelasma)
> [http://www.gpnotebook.co.uk/simplepage.cfm?ID=422903818](http://www.gpnotebook.co.uk/simplepage.cfm?ID=422903818)
>
> Any thoughts?

I know his post is super old but if u r still on here, did u find out anything? My xanthelasma started about a yr after I swapped to a low oxalate diet and I started excreting them. My cholesterol more than tripled to a whopping 23mmol/l . After 2 years my cholesterol started coming down. Its now 14 but my x hasn't reduced.

# Thread: Crying over chopped veg
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crying-over-chopped-veg.7933/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2013-08-17T20:16:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crying-over-chopped-veg.7933/post-92952>

Has anyone else noticed that chopping onions doesn't bother them any more?

It hasn't been bothering me, but I have been using very sweet onions, 1015 variety. But the other day I chopped a couple from our garden, and they didn't bother me. I didn't have to turn on the fan, walk away from the chop block a few times, or even reach for the onion chopper device. No stinging, no tears.

I personally think, that for me, CT is responsible. A year ago I found that the bull nettle didn't sting after about 5 minutes, and earlier this year I found that poison ivy doesn't break me out any more. This onion thing seems to be along the same lines.

To test, I bought a cheap yellow onion from the market, and chopped it up. Easy peasy, no blood, no sweat, no tears.

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-17T21:50:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crying-over-chopped-veg.7933/post-92960>

I'll get back to you on this one in a week. I love caramelizing red sweet onions. YUM

### nonchalant — 2013-08-18T01:03:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crying-over-chopped-veg.7933/post-92974>

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-18T01:11:13+0300

.... They found that the most common emotion aroused by using Facebook is envy. Endlessly comparing themselves with peers who have doctored their photographs, amplified their achievements and plagiarised their bons mots can leave Facebookâ€™s users more than a little green-eyed.

[http://www.economist.com/news/scien...ork-seems-make-people-more-miserable-get-life](http://www.economist.com/news/science-and-technology/21583593-using-social-network-seems-make-people-more-miserable-get-life)

Question everything. Previously I have read that we are the collective sum of the 5 people that we are closest to. Bottom line ... if a person and/or technology is bad for us, we need to greatly reduce or eliminate the negative impact.

# Thread: Office ergonomics ... good article on using how technology disrupts our bodies
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/office-ergonomics-good-article-on-using-how-technology-disrupts-our-bodies.7947/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ATL_Paleo — 2013-08-19T23:07:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/office-ergonomics-good-article-on-using-how-technology-disrupts-our-bodies.7947/post-93141>

**Office ergonomics ... good article on how using technology disrupts our bodies**

Below are selected excerpts from a lengthy, but interesting article.

\-----
"Over the past six or seven years we have realized the consequences of people sitting too long, and it is an astonishing list." Specifically, he lists (in no particular order) diabetes, low productivity, hypertension, apathy, clinical depression, hyperlipidemia (elevated levels of lipids in the blood), low moods or mild depression, cardiovascular diseases, obesity, back problems, deep vein thrombosis and mental sluggishness.
\-----
Tablets, for instance, might at first glance seem to free people from their desks, but in fact people tend to place them flat on their desks and read them as if they were books, explains Dr. Alan Hedge, director of the Human Factors and Ergonomics Laboratory at Cornell University in Ithaca, N.Y. With a book, people will occasionally change posture as they turn the page, but with tablets they can remain hunched for long periods, he notes.

"Leaning forward doubles the compressive forces on the vertebrae in your lower back compared to leaning back," explains Hedge. "When leaning back 20 degrees in a lounge chair you are really relaxing and halving the compression. That is why we say 'sit back and relax,' not 'hunch forward and relax.' "

One result of hunching is a syndrome called iPad Neck -- chronic soreness of the back of the neck and upper shoulders. Hedge recommends propping up the tablet or putting it on a holder so you can read it with a straight neck.
\-----
Overuse of tablets may also interfere with getting a good night's sleep, says Dr. Mariana Figueiro, director of the Light and Health Program of the Lighting Research Center at the Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute in Troy, N.Y. Exposure to bright light in the evening will suppress the body's production of the sleep-aid chemical melatonin, making it harder to go to sleep.
\-----
Laptops, meanwhile, are considered non-ergonomic by nature, since there is no way to adjust the distance between the keyboard and screen. "Your hands want to be close to your chest, but your eyes want to be focused on something two feet in front of you," making an ergonomic posture unobtainable with a laptop, Hedge explains.

Using laptops on a desk, in place of a desktop, only compounds the problem, he adds. "Desks are usually about 30 inches high, which is a good height for writing by hand on paper but dreadful for typing, as it is too high unless you are taller than 6'2". Then you are adding the height of the laptop."
\-----
As for smartphones, the reliance on thumbs for texting has led to an upsurge of a condition variously called BlackBerry Thumb, Text Thumb, Nintendo Thumb or De Quervain Syndrome.
\-----
"The signal to stand up does something to the body that tunes it, controlling the blood pressure and circulation," says Vernikos. "Every 20 to 30 minutes you need to stand up. More often is fine, but doing it 20 times at once and saying you are done is not sufficient; you must do it throughout the day."

She bases her conclusions on research she did for NASA on the bodily effects of having no gravitational stress, such as what astronauts experience in free fall. Volunteers stayed in bed for a month; some of them got up to use a treadmill and others just got up. A third group just stayed in bed. The completely bedbound volunteers lost 25% of their aerobic capacity in just four days, Vernikos recalls.
\-----

[http://www.computerworld.com/s/arti...nomics\_?source=CTWNLE\_nlt\_thisweek\_2013-08-19](http://www.computerworld.com/s/article/9241435/Surprise_Mobile_devices_don_t_help_office_ergonomics_?source=CTWNLE_nlt_thisweek_2013-08-19)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Confusing DHEA labs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confusing-dhea-labs.7955/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2013-08-20T16:56:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confusing-dhea-labs.7955/post-93230>

Which is more accurate for checking DHEA levels, blood test or saliva?

I had a DHEA blood test done on 7/15 and I was taking about 25-50 mg of DHEA and I got a value of 233 ng/dl.

Just got the results back from a saliva DHEAS test and it read 32.3 ng/ml and I did not take any DHEA a day before the test or the day of the test and I had increased the supplementation to 50mg to 100mg based on my first test for the 2-3 week prior to the test.

The good range for the blood test was 102-1185 ng/dl.
The good range for the saliva test is 2-23 ng/ml based on age.

According to the blood test I am low but according to the saliva test I am too high and when I try to do the conversion to the same units is just does not make sense.

**Which one do I go with?**

Thanks

### JanSz — 2013-08-20T20:15:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confusing-dhea-labs.7955/post-93256>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=93230)
>
> Which is more accurate for checking DHEA levels, blood test or saliva?
>
> I had a DHEA blood test done on 7/15 and I was taking about 25-50 mg of DHEA and I got a value of 233 ng/dl.
>
> Just got the results back from a saliva DHEAS test and it read 32.3 ng/ml and I did not take any DHEA a day before the test or the day of the test and I had increased the supplementation to 50mg to 100mg based on my first test for the 2-3 week prior to the test.
>
> The good range for the blood test was 102-1185 ng/dl.
> The good range for the saliva test is 2-23 ng/ml based on age.
>
> According to the blood test I am low but according to the saliva test I am too high and when I try to do the conversion to the same units is just does not make sense.
>
> **Which one do I go with?**
>
> Thanks

This is pretty good question. Wish I had better answer.

when testing, DHEA=(free DHEA), DHEAs=DHEAs(storage molecule)--->Dehydroepiandrosterone sulfate
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dehydroepiandrosterone](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dehydroepiandrosterone)
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dehydroepiandrosterone\_sulfate](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dehydroepiandrosterone_sulfate)
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Steroid\_sulfatase](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Steroid_sulfatase)

> DHEA is produced from cholesterol through two cytochrome P450 enzymes. Cholesterol is converted to pregnenolone by the enzyme P450 scc (side chain cleavage); then another enzyme, CYP17A1, converts pregnenolone to 17α-hydroxypregnenolone and then to DHEA.

> Dehydroepiandrosterone sulfate is produced by the addition of a sulfate group, catalyzed by the **sulfotransferase enzymes SULT1A1 and SULT1E1**, which also produce estrone sulfate from estrone. DHEA sulfate can also be back-converted to DHEA through the action of steroid sulfatase

.

in blood we can test DHEA & DHEAs
in saliva we can test only DHEA (free DHEA)

proper balance between DHEA & DHEAs levels depends on actions of steroid sulfatase

Blood tests are commonly used.
I have seen number of answers as to applicability of saliva testing. The answer usually depends on author's inclinations.

High DHEA levels are sign of problem (DHEA is produced in the adrenal glands, the gonads, and the brain)(PCOS)
Most often after 20yo DHEAs levels are falling.

For whatever reason there is at least two groups of responses to given dose of supplemental DHEA.
Some (if not most), respond quickly to dose 25-50 mg of DHEA, (when checked by blood test)
To achieve desirable DHEAs, others need much more supplemental DHEA,
I use 300mg/day, dr Kruse was using 500mg/day during his hack.

**Until now I am using DHEA pills and monitoring DHEAs,serum.

Goal
men (350-490) μg/dL
women (275-400) μg/dL**

Goal for DHEAs levels come from lef.org
likely from dr Dzugan

.....................

DHEAs participation in properly functioning Steroid Hormone Panel is very high.
Cortisol(AM), cortisol is next.

When measuring only most notable Steroid Hormones and thaking it as 100%
DHEAs participation is 95%
Cortisol(AM) 4.25%
the 0.75% is for all other.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508)

//////

### JanSz — 2013-08-20T20:42:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confusing-dhea-labs.7955/post-93258>

Timely post by SueC :

[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?3941-7-Keto-Brand-matters/page5](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?3941-7-Keto-Brand-matters/page5)

> How to get the best results when monitoring DHEA therapy -
> There have been some questions lately about how to best monitor DHEA replacement therapy. And rightly so, whether using DHEA or 7 –keto DHEA, **topical or oral**, the selection of testing type is very important for accurate assessment of bioavailable hormone.

See more at: [http://www.zrtlab.com/professionals...-monitoring-dhea-therapy#sthash.zwJTD1mq.dpuf](http://www.zrtlab.com/professionals/zrt-blogs/entry/how-to-get-the-best-results-when-monitoring-dhea-therapy#sthash.zwJTD1mq.dpuf)

[http://www.zrtlab.com/professionals...the-best-results-when-monitoring-dhea-therapy](http://www.zrtlab.com/professionals/zrt-blogs/entry/how-to-get-the-best-results-when-monitoring-dhea-therapy)

////////

### nicld — 2013-08-20T21:11:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confusing-dhea-labs.7955/post-93260>

Very interesting but still confusing.

I am not on the 7-Keto DHEA but the test did show that my testosterone was a bit elevated and I am getting more acne. Don't know of the acne is from the DHEA or the estrogen dominance. I just might stop it for the week and see that the neuropath doc says about it when I see him and hope he can help. Oh and my cortisol levels are good too.

Thanks

### JanSz — 2013-08-21T00:29:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confusing-dhea-labs.7955/post-93286>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=93260)
>
> Very interesting but still confusing.
>
> I am not on the 7-Keto DHEA but the test did show that my testosterone was a bit elevated and I am getting more acne. Don't know of the acne is from the DHEA or the estrogen dominance. I just might stop it for the week and see that the neuropath doc says about it when I see him and hope he can help. Oh and my cortisol levels are good too.
>
> Thanks

I think that you have not made the right test, hence confusion.

> I had a DHEA blood test done on 7/15 and I was taking about 25-50 mg of DHEA and I got a value of 233 ng/dl.
>
> Just got the results back from a saliva DHEAS test and it read 32.3 ng/ml and I did not take any DHEA a day before the test or the day of the test and I had increased the supplementation to 50mg to 100mg based on my first test for the 2-3 week prior to the test.

You want to do
DHEAs, serum
test.

on LabCorp:

Dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA) Sulfate--Test Number: 004020 CPT Code: 82627

On that test you aim at (regardless of your age)(disregard laboratory provided range):
men (350-490) μg/dL
women (275-400) μg/dL

You may get more information here:

[http://www.lef.org/protocols/index.htm](http://www.lef.org/protocols/index.htm)

DHEA Restoration Therapy
[http://www.lef.org/protocols/metabolic\_health/dhea\_restoration\_01.htm](http://www.lef.org/protocols/metabolic_health/dhea_restoration_01.htm)

////////////////////////

Are you men or woman?
If woman, menstruating or not?

For menstruating woman, when working on steroid hormone panel, it is easier (on the beginning) to do blood draws on day 3-4 of menstruating cycle.

Latter on, one try to add blood draw also on days on maximal progesterone levels (19-21).

//

### nicld — 2013-08-21T20:32:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confusing-dhea-labs.7955/post-93363>

I did not have the blood one done by Labcorp but by my doctors office and it was sent to Quest Diagnostics. I looked up the test and it was the blood serum one so it should be good.

I am a woman who is still cycling. The saliva steroid panel was done at the max progesterone levels (and those are way low). The blood DHEA test was not done at the beginning of my cycle of at the max (doctor did not tell me that.....grrrrr). I wish that they would all use the same unit of measure too.

Until I see the doctor about this I have backed off of the DHEA. I just don't want to mess anything up and with getting two different readings, I need help.

When I need further testing I will be sure to check out Labcorp and see how the prices and the tests compare to the doctors office. There is a Labcorp 30 minutes from my work but if the price it the same, it would not be worth the extra gas money to go there if I can get the same test 5 minutes from work.

Thanks

# Thread: Herring and Mackerel?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/herring-and-mackerel.7957/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MeghanK — 2013-08-20T18:42:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/herring-and-mackerel.7957/post-93241>

Ben Greenfield talks a lot about eating herring, mackerel, and sardines everyday... I eat sardines almost everyday but didn't know the benefits of herring and or mackerel? Does Jack recommend this type of seafood? What are good sources? Is it alright to get canned versions?

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-20T19:04:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/herring-and-mackerel.7957/post-93246>

### Danco3636 — 2013-08-21T02:52:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-testosterone-question-for-you-pros.7958/post-93311>

Mine tanked after my vesactomy.... I dodn't know it for years but one day relized my labido was down, a bit of depression set in, my energy ans sleep was not good, etc. etc.
Started thinking how before I got my "V" I was a stallion...... My energy was always high, always active, raced and won triathlons, business was great because I was a go getter...... Researched, ran across HRT..... So I went and got tested.... my range was below the lower range of T levels for my age... My free was even lower and bioavalable was even lower..... And I was fit, had muscle, lean etc. I was doing all the right things already I was just having the above symptoms. So I was pretty close to optimizing what low levels I had....

After testing and recapping my life story and chain of events with my DR. We decided that it was a great possibility it was from getting cut..... So I first started with the gels to see how things would go and that way I have a bit of control.... Well it helped. It also jacked my wife's T levels up.... lol So we decided to do the pellets. We started with a smaller dose insert at first then tested after a while.... We then kept adding until it was in a good range that would stay in a good range for about 5 months.... He said I may have been able to go to 6 but we test every 5 and insert then as needed.

### fireforlunch — 2013-08-20T19:22:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-hypothyroid-cause-low-testosterone.7960/post-93247>

A little weary about taking testosterone for the rest of my life.

Not sure I really want to move forward with this.

When I saw the Endo yesterday he said I would rather not start you on 2 meds at once so since your testosterone is much worse than your thyroid lets start there then test your thyroid again in 6 weeks when we test your testosterone levels.

While reading online today I realized that The Hypothyroid could be causing the low testosterone.

See this article:

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10671947](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10671947)

Now I'm really nervous about administering this first injection today.

**If I do the testosterone for 6 weeks and my thyroid is still low could I in theory go off the testosterone and try armour and see if the thyroid fixes the low t?**

Thanks for your input

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-08-20T19:34:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-hypothyroid-cause-low-testosterone.7960/post-93249>

Hypothyroidism can impact many things. If I were in your shoes, I would address the thyroid first. And remember the thyroid and adrenal fatigue often go together.

Another consideration ... Dr. Kruse has said that HCG injections may be a preferable method of raising testosterone. After addressing your thyroid, you might want to research T injections vs. HCG injections.

# Thread: Copper & Allzheimer's  A Relationship. Do you have copper water pipes?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/copper-allzheimers-a-relationship-do-you-have-copper-water-pipes.7962/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-08-20T21:49:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/copper-allzheimers-a-relationship-do-you-have-copper-water-pipes.7962/post-93262>

[http://www.latimes.com/science/sciencenow/la-sci-copper-alzheimers-disease-20130819,0,5977613.story](http://www.latimes.com/science/sciencenow/la-sci-copper-alzheimers-disease-20130819,0,5977613.story)

As I was reading this account of alzheimer's & copper, I wonder what role the field plays in opening up the BBB to copper.

"New research finds that copper in amounts readily found in our drinking water, the foods we eat and the vitamin supplements we take likely plays a key role in initiating and fueling the abnormal protein build-up and brain inflammation that are hallmarks of Alzheimer's disease.

"While the mineral is important to healthy nerve conduction, hormone secretion and the growth of bones and connective tissue, a team of researchers from the University of Rochester Medical Center suggested that too much of it may be a bad thing, and they set about to explore copper's dark side.

"What they found, said neuroscientist Rashid Deane, is "pretty scary": A steady diet of copper, even at entirely allowable levels, breaks down the barrier that keeps unwanted toxins from entering the brain, and that it fuels an increase in production of beta-amyloid but impedes the performance of proteins that clear the stuff from the brain.

"On top of that, Deane's team found that copper accumulation in the brain causes inflammation in brain tissues. At low levels and for short durations, that may be a good sign that brain tissues are responding to the danger of excess beta-amyloid proteins and are trying to expel them, Deane said. In time, however, neuro-inflammation can overwhelm the brain and begin to damage cells, he added.

"Copper is found in a wide range of the foods we eat, including red meat, shellfish, nuts and many fruits and vegetables, as well as in many vitamin supplements. It also leaches from copper pipes into the water we drink. While we take in copper from foods, it is most readily absorbed into the bloodstream in its "free" form, say researchers -- when it is suspended in water.

"The research, which lay out the case against a long-suspected culprit in Alzheimer's disease, is published Monday in the journal PNAS.

"Deane said that, in the absence of effective treatments for Alzheimer's disease, his team's findings suggest a way to prevent the memory-robbing disorder or slow it once it has taken hold. One drug candidate currently in Phase 2 trials -- an agent that binds with copper molecules and escorts them out of the body -- might well do that, said the study's lead author. But even now, Deane said, consumers could be checking their vitamin supplements for copper and researching whether their water filters are equipped to remove copper from their drinking supply.

"The key will be striking the right balance between too much and too little copper consumption," Deane said. "Right now, we cannot say what the right level will be. But diet may one day play an important role in regulating this process."

"Deane's team worked with mice and with human brain cells that play a key role in forming the blood-brain barrier to detect the mechanisms by which copper might start, drive or worsen Alzheimer's disease. They noted that in the elderly, the blood-brain barrier becomes "leaky," letting in larger toxic molecules circulating the blood. So too does the concentration of copper in the brain's small blood vessels increase with age.

"Starting in the mouse-equivalent of young adulthood, they fed mice a regular diet, but gave half of them water that contained levels of copper equal to one-tenth the maximum allowed by the Environmental Protection Agency. The other half were given double-distilled water with a very low copper content -- less than 2% of that given to the high-copper group. In a second experiment, older mice bred for their propensity to develop beta-amyloid plaques were fed the copper-tainted water or the low-copper water.

"After three months, the effects were dramatic: In the mice who took in high doses of copper, the scientists observed that copper accumulated in the small blood vessels of the brain. Compared to mice on low doses of copper, the high-copper mice showed four times fewer levels of a protein called LRP1, which transports beta-amyloid and other debris out of the brain. They also showed abnormally high levels of beta-amyloid production and of neuro-inflammation.

"As they aged, the brains of mice who were fed high doses of copper had beta-amyloid levels on par with those of mice who drank low-copper water but had been bred to develop beta-amyloid at high rates.

Copyright Â© 2013, Los Angeles Times

### cantweight — 2013-09-12T00:27:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/copper-allzheimers-a-relationship-do-you-have-copper-water-pipes.7962/post-95647>

### nonchalant — 2013-09-12T02:11:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/copper-allzheimers-a-relationship-do-you-have-copper-water-pipes.7962/post-95661>

More likely it is the EMF that compromises the BBB, so the best long term solution would be to fix the EMF problem.

### Hope — 2013-09-12T03:05:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/copper-allzheimers-a-relationship-do-you-have-copper-water-pipes.7962/post-95663>

> [cantweight said:](/goto/post?id=95647)
>
> What binds copper to remove it? Just got some labs back and my copper is very high.

Cristi, copper is often elevated in folks with adrenal fatigue...it builds up and the body can't get rid of it all. Also has a lot of interplay with hormones; copper sort of acts in the body like estrogen, zinc (the other side of the see-saw) like PG4. So if you also have a E2 dominance, this is another reason...fascinating stuff. as you keep detoxing the copper should come down.

I think you are already doing a lot......eating protein is key so the Epi-paleo Rx = awesome. Vegetarians are often high in copper. It also ties in with mercury toxicity.....often we have hidden copper. There is a great book "Why am I always so tired" about copper toxicity...but the diet info isn't as good as what you know from here.....

Just my $0.02. I owe you a call. Would still love to meet. So busy decluttering and reorganizing the house. Found a local EMF woman who also does Feng Shui. A real building biologist, if she is indeed authentic. LOL. Husg to you ~

# Thread: Does DIM make you dim?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-dim-make-you-dim.7974/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2013-08-21T23:30:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-dim-make-you-dim.7974/post-93370>

I added DIM to my arsenal for the past week and I have noticed a very foggy feeling. I keep leaving the stove and oven on....I never do this! I also feel kind of disconnected and my libido is crashing and burning. I dont feel depressed or sad, just flat.

Has anyone had similar issues? Do you know if I should discontinue?

I never even got my hormone panel results....my doc closed up shop and left the building (yeah, thats the kind of luck I have..lol) Supposedly they will re-open in a new location and I can get them next week. I just started the DIM under the assumption it would help.

### motivatedhealthseeker — 2013-08-22T00:50:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-dim-make-you-dim.7974/post-93374>

Hi
I dont know about the flat effect but I get very nauseous after taking DIM to the point I have to vomit. Sometimes the effect is very intense and lingers and other times the feeling passes in 20 mins or so.

### fitness@home — 2013-08-22T02:32:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-dim-make-you-dim.7974/post-93379>

I took DIM for about six months last year. The only side effect I had was a slight headache until my body got used to it. Guess I was lucky.

# Thread: Best DHA source?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-dha-source.7975/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### psionic — 2013-08-22T15:30:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-dha-source.7975/post-93404>

Hi! I am new here and sorry if something related has been already posted.

I dont have to point out that Dr. Kruse stresses DHA as a most important factor for brain development and shellfish and crustaceans as a main and best source.

I want to ask if one can use other sources of omega-3, I mean (not intoxicated) fish oil caps, hemp seed (omega6mega3 is 3:1) or flax seed with almost identical results as seafood? The answer of this question is targeted mainly for people located in inlands where the access to shellfish and crustaceans is limited.

Thanks for suggestions

### Lahelada — 2013-08-22T15:58:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-dha-source.7975/post-93405>

Welcome . Fish oil is generally not recommended as it is difficult to source it fresh enough.The same goes for seeds. While hemp has a good O6 O3 relation you will need to make sure as well that the seeds have not been stored for a long time. The way to tell if seeds have gone to seed is if you grind them they release an odour of fish.
If you decide on using seeds you might be better of using flaxseed with a ratio of 4.5 to 1 as it is used in many diets so the turnover of it must be higher.

I am not a fish lover so I can't do strong tasting fish. While I do eat fresh fish the most effective fish for inlanders seems to be mackerel as it has a ratio of 11:1.
Have a look here.It is wikipedia but the values seem to be correct judging from two checks I made .

Furthermore before you supplement I would read this if you have not already done so.

[http://www.jackkruse.com/is-fish-oil-good-or-bad/](http://www.jackkruse.com/is-fish-oil-good-or-bad/)

### cjhf — 2013-08-22T18:12:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-dha-source.7975/post-93408>

If this will help anyone else I'll share info from my misguided path.
Question everything I am learning.

After many years of taking pharm. grade fish oil, having joined MLM firm to market product, attending conferences promoting its use, reading numerous references to the benefits I have
discontinued my dogma and stopped taking it.

Brain Gut 5 was recommended to me but I have many years invested in fish oil and still didn't get it.

More evidence with research articles and recordings by an unbiased engineer have convinced me to change my ways.
[http://www.brianpeskin.com/](http://www.brianpeskin.com/) In addition to the fish oil attack on his site I found other logical info useful and it has broadened my perspective.

I have not taken a different DHA supplement product at this point.
So far it is sardines, oysters, herring and salmon.

I've heard if you try eatting something 10 times you become tolerant. I am working up to some other recommended DHA sources.

I get canned sardines locally or online. There are many different spice combinations and added vegs to make them taste quite good.

I hope this will keep someone else from making the same mistake as me. Continuing to learn...the body is very adaptive and forgiving.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Fat around the Mid-Section from CT?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-around-the-mid-section-from-ct.7978/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 33
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MeghanK — 2013-08-22T21:15:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-around-the-mid-section-from-ct.7978/post-93421>

I am sure this has been brought up but not sure what to search for... won't the body preferentially lay down fat around the mid-section to insulate vital organs from hypothermia when you do CT daily? Or at least hold onto more fat because of the "cold"
Sorry if this is a stupid question....

### Hemming — 2013-08-22T21:47:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-around-the-mid-section-from-ct.7978/post-93423>

I think its a very good question because I've seen and measured that my waist has increased and weight has increased. Obviously my situation is a little different and some of the weight gain is not on my mid-section.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-08-22T22:39:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-around-the-mid-section-from-ct.7978/post-93431>

Good question, hopefully I'll find the direct answer to this, I believe I saw it in the comments of the CT series I'm currently digging through, but as far as I know this has to do entirely with your tissue fatty acids - you need to have slightly more o6 than o3 and you won't lay down fat around the abdomens. This fat is beneficial to species living in the oceans because they need the extra 'warmth' as well as fluidity (in their cells) to operate under the immense pressure and lack of heat. We don't necessarily need that. So yes, CT will lead to that IF your tissue fatty acid ratios are off. This is why Dr. Kruse doesn't necessarily demonize o6 fats.
