I haven't started CT heavily enough, only been doing cold showers for a long time and a couple cold baths in the past few weeks, but I plan on drastically upping it in the coming month since the colder weather will make the showers colder and I can have my room a lot colder (from about 68\* to 60\* degrees) and I'll continue to do my sprints/Foundation Training outside, regardless of how cold it is. If I start noticing a buildup of abdominal fat, especially lower abs (which is really the only area I hold ANY fat), I'll probably start adding in nut butters or something. I would just do CT and see how it goes. Add in o6's if you have to through nuts or other fats.

@Shijin: Have you been doing (even marginal amounts of) CT year round since you started your journey here? ### Shijin13 — 2013-08-23T19:43:26+0300

Should I still expect a detox this upcoming fall when I move and am capable of taking ice baths? Thanks!

I CT regularly all last year. this spring/summer - I chose not to CT to see what differences it made in how I felt/labs etc.

All last winter I was using a 100 gallon stock tank and CT 5-6days/week. My preferred temperature range was 45 degrees or below. CTing for me in 45 degrees or above water was harder, and I had to spend more time in the tank - upwards of 60minutes to see the same effects at the lower temps.

I built up tolerance where I could easily spend 20min in water at or below 32 degrees - but it took time to build up to that.

since the weather changed the past couple of week, and the decreasing light cycles I've been feeling the pull to get back in the tank... I'm craving cold..

### cinnamon — 2013-08-23T21:35:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-around-the-mid-section-from-ct.7978/post-93537>

I started doing CT last summer. I would stand in a cold shower hoping my skin would turn pink. I did this every 2 or 3 days, probably for at most about 10 minutes each time. I found that if I started with either the top of my head or my face, I could push the water colder and colder. I would keep going, letting the water hit my face until it was cold enough that it felt like a blizzard wind - numbing and tingling and taking my breath away. I would focus the water on my belly, because I had developed a pot belly in the previous couple of years that simply wouldn't go away. I would also focus on my bottom, hoping it might help the sagging cellulite that plagued me there.

I knew that this was no where near as extreme as those on the forum who reported lying for 30 to 60 minutes at a time in a tub with ice floating in the water. I figured it would be a good first step, and not too hard to implement. I was stunned and pleased to see that my pot belly was shrinking and my cellulite was disappearing. I was getting muscles in my arms and legs while doing no excercise except walking. My skin looked great!

I am sure that eating good fats (allowing absorption of fat-soluble nutrients that my body needed) increasing protein, and paying attention to circadian rhythm all helped with the improvement. But I am a firm believer that any CT is good!

After the start of the new year, when I decided to increase my seafood intake (which for many years had been zero). I noticed that CT was easier and I could let the water get quite cold (straight from the cold tap is 42 to 44 degrees F). I also noticed that my skin was turning pink while CTing and then retaining a wonderful rosy hue all the time. This summer I have been CTing almost everyday. Still almost always in the shower, but for probably 20 minutes or so at a time.

I should also mention that I think grounding has an impact on body comp. It was still deep winter here when Jack started blogging about grounding, so I started spending 30 minutes or so each day barefoot on the concrete floor in my basement. Literally within 2 days, I could sense a change in the shape of my butt! It felt somehow rounder? firmer? not sure how to describe it. When I do a lot of grounding I notice more muscle.

I am attaching a picture I took this morning. Unfortunately, I don't have a "before" shot. You'll just have to trust me that I would not have been wearing a bikini! LOL

### caroline — 2013-08-25T02:48:42+0300

### freesia — 2013-08-26T00:22:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-around-the-mid-section-from-ct.7978/post-93754>

My experience is similar to Gretchen's.
I've used CT 17 months solid now (3 - 6 x per week), but not quite as cold as Gretchen and other cold embracing peeps here get to! I live in a fairly moderate climate. Using lotsa ice I can get my baths to 40 F in Winter and 44 - 46 F through Summer.
NB I started leptin reset just a couple of weeks before beginning the CT protocol, so switching to a Keto\* diet may have contributed to my results.
Anyway...on topic...I noticed muscle definition in my arms and legs first, and a couple of inches loss around the waist after a couple of months. I was doing CT for it's antinflammatory & antioxidative effects and the little bit of body sculpting I noticed wasn't really on my radar, but it was interesting to see.

\*I was coming into winter so opted for 25 g carb with LReset.

### MeghanK — 2013-08-29T03:22:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-around-the-mid-section-from-ct.7978/post-94126>

**Practical Implications**,.... **I know many are not a fan of Stephan Guynet.. but thoughts?**

Understanding the underlying biology can often help us understand how best to apply techniques that are based on biological processes. Here are a few points of informed speculation that I'll offer to people who are interested in cold training for fat loss or performance.

1\. You don't have to lower your core temperature to activate brown fat. The brain activates brown fat in response to perceived threats to future core temperature, including a rapid drop in skin temperature. This is why cold showers probably activate brown fat strongly, even when the exposure time isn't sufficient to lower core temperature significantly.

2\. Simply revving up your brown fat for brief periods may not make much of a difference in calorie balance or fat loss. Like muscle tissue, what burns calories is using brown fat, not just having the capacity to use it. Resting metabolic rate for muscle and brown fat tissue are both low, despite both tissues having the capacity to use a huge amount of fuel when activated. You can have a highly trained non-shivering thermogenesis system, but if you don't use it for more than a few minutes a week, it may not make much difference in your body weight. Still, having a highly trained system does make you more resistant to cold stress, which may be advantageous, and may also have metabolic benefits. Also, it remains possible that brown fat activation has neuroendocrine effects on body fatness and metabolism that go beyond calorie use.

3\. The sensors that detect temperature for the purposes of activating brown fat are not, to my knowledge, located in brown fat itself (3). Some people have used ice packs for cold training. There's probably no advantage to putting ice on your upper back, where brown fat is located, relative to any other patch of skin on your torso. In fact, I might actually avoid putting ice on my upper back because the more you cool your brown fat, the slower uncoupling will proceed. Ice may also be a weaker stimulus than cold water due to the smaller surface area of skin it targets. Water doesn't actually have to be that cold to activate non-shivering thermogenesis, due to its high conductance.

4\. If you don't feel uncomfortable at all, it probably isn't doing anything. When the brain perceives a thermal threat, it initiates a coordinated physiological and behavioral program designed to oppose core temperature change. Part of that program includes activating brown fat, and part of it involves making you feel uncomfortable so you'll hurry up and put on a sweater or stop standing in a cold shower and wasting energy.

To illustrate that principle with an example, if you're overheated and you get into a cold shower, it feels good. Cold water doesn't activate your brown fat in that scenario, because it's helping you achieve thermal homeostasis, not posing a threat to it. Likewise, some people have found ways to feel more comfortable during cold training, such as keeping extremities warm. Since feeling uncomfortable is part of the program that the brain turns on when it perceives a cold threat, I wonder if these strategies make cold training less effective by reducing the degree of brown fat activation. You may not need to feel very uncomfortable to activate brown fat, but if you don't feel uncomfortable at all then I question whether you're having the desired effect.

I look forward to research on these questions so we can have more definitive answers. Any additional thoughts on cold training are welcome in the comments.

# Thread: Another DHEA question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-dhea-question.8006/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2013-08-23T23:20:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-dhea-question.8006/post-93551>

I started taking DHEA because in the readings that I was doing and the symptoms that I was having it seemed like I was low and supplementing would help. I had a blood serum DHEA that showed I was low on DHEA and I was on about 25-50mg a day so I upped the dose to 50 mg 2X a day. I just had a saliva test done and had stopped the DHEA 2 days before I had the test (from some stuff I read it should have been longer but the instructions did not say that). I got the saliva results back and it said that I was high on DHEA and that my testosterone was near the top. Still struggling to decide which one I should believe, but that is another post.

Since I started taking the DHEA I have steadily gained weight and the biggest thing is starting getting painful acne on my face. Since I do not know which test to believe and I have stopped the DHEA gradually. As soon as I reduced/stopped, the acne stopped, coincidence? I am just about to ovulate so we will see what the luteal phase brings. I see a doc on Monday and hope that he can make sense of what is going on and if I really need the DHEA.

Should note that my progesterone is tanked and the thyroid is tanked. I think that the low progesterone is dragging everything else down. I do not have PCOS either and my cortisol levels appear to be fine.

Until I get the progesterone fixed is trying to fix the others, specifically the DHEA just a waste of time?

Am I on the wrong kind of DHEA?

I am still doing the leptin reset and the CT but have been gaining weight and not sleeping great the whole time I was doing them.

Anybody have any insights to this?

Thanks

### fitness@home — 2013-08-24T00:29:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-dhea-question.8006/post-93554>

I think Shijin can answer your question best...

I am post menopausal and use Twist DHEA cream. My DHEA and Testosterone are both on the high end. Acne is not an issue...

### Trinity — 2013-08-24T02:34:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-dhea-question.8006/post-93569>

Hi Nicole,

You are lucky that your cortisol is still OK. That will make fixing your thyroid a lot easier! I would start with progesterone if I were you. Fixing the progesterone is key and is likely the cause of your poor sleep. Is your sleep worse the week or so before your period? My sleep tanks like clockwork on day 18 of my cycle (right when progesterone should be at its highest). With bioidentical progesteone its much better, but still not optimal. It has only been within the past couple of months that I've finally got my E ratio up around 200 (optimal is somewhere around 300). Everyone is a little different, but it has taken 300mg of progesterone just to bring it up to where its at now.

Trinity

### nicld — 2013-08-24T03:30:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-dhea-question.8006/post-93573>

Hi Trinity,

I was really surprised when I got the cortisol test back and it was pretty normal. 7.2 in the morning then drops off during the day like it should. I know about 7-8 years ago I fried the adrenals so at least that is one thing I don't have to worry about.

I see the doc on Monday, ovulation day so I am hoping that I get a script right away and the second half of the cycle will not be as bad. Right now, my ratio is 38 around my high day. No wonder I feel like crap. From what I have read, estrogen dominance wreaks havoc with the thyroid. Tanking the T3 and making the Rt3 to T3 ratio bad too (mine is 10.95 when 20 and above is better).

Thanks fitness@home. I just cannot handle the acne, it sucks.

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lahelada — 2013-08-24T15:33:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnesium-calcium-and-shark-fin-supplement.8008/post-93600>

As money has been tight recently I have not bought any new magnesium. Ideally I would like to put that off for another 2 weeks or so. I do still have a mag,calcium and shark fin mix from a time when I thought that was a good idea.
How bad would it be to take that before bedtime ? I think the lack of additional magnesium is affecting my sleep .
Alternatively I could do foot baths with Epsom salts, but I wonder if the absorption rate is enough.
(Magnesium oxide 470mg ,calcium carbonate 255mg)
Thank you.

### caroline — 2013-08-25T02:55:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnesium-calcium-and-shark-fin-supplement.8008/post-93661>

I don't feel that mag helps me sleep better.....I have been taking threonate in the morning and malate in the afternoon. Now it takes me forever to fall asleep - once I do - I sleep soundly. I think it is because the seasons are changing but I don't know what to do about it....

Well actually .... Brandy works - but that is such a bad idea....

### Lahelada — 2013-08-25T15:33:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnesium-calcium-and-shark-fin-supplement.8008/post-93694>

I do sleep better with mag. I did not take the mag at night after all but in the morning though. I slept well again last night 9 hours.
This explains pretty well what I was worried about with calcium acting as a stimulant. [http://www.metabolictyping.info/\_insom1.html](http://www.metabolictyping.info/_insom1.html) Well,next Friday I will try taking it at night as I don't want to disturb my pattern during the week .

# Thread: How do you feel about your body?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 44
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-08-25T10:53:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-93680>

Do you think that women worry too much about how their body looks......

I suppose, on one hand we do, but when we are overweight - it just feels so bad, and so wrong.
We want to look hot and sexy for the guys in our life - that goes without saying .... but in my opinion - that is only a very small part.

We need to feel good about ourselves ... for ourselves. It feels so awful when your clothes are too tight - you can't get your favorite pants done up and your shirt is gaping...... I think it is kind of humiliating.

Men can just let their belt out a notch and they are good to go. I don't think that men feel as uncomfortable in their skin as women do when they are obese.

I had a friend that was a very bright, articulate, witty, lovely woman .... she was very successful with her own company.....but....she was obese. It was such a disappointment to her. She always said - she could control so much in her life - she was so successful .....but her body defied her at every turn and she felt like such a failure in that area of her life.

Does a guy feel this way too? Does a guy care if he can't wear skinny jeans! BTW - guys look so hot in skinny jeans!

I know men get that their loved one gets really distressed and angry and unhappy over the shape/size of their body.........but honestly .......and in my opinion....it isn't vanity.....it is so much more.

We really want to be the woman on the outside that reflects how we feel about ourself on the inside.
We want to be happy and healthy and actively engaged in life. We want and deserve to feel great about ourself .... and, of course, a man deserves this too.

Does anyone else feel like this? How do the guys feel?

What are your observations? Do women expend too much energy/time worried about their shape that could be far better put to more productive use?

Having said all that ..... I think that it is amazing when a woman can embrace all her curves and just go for it. Is it because she has a wonderful, caring man that makes her feel like a princess and loves her unconditionally?

and having said that ....I had all of that and more and it wasn't enough ....I needed to love myself ......I needed to feel good about myself. It had nothing to do with someone else's "perfect". It had to do with reaching my own potential and giving myself the gift of confidence and vitality and loving myself....

Sorry .....I think I am really rambling here ......

I hope that some of the guys will give us their thoughts.........

### fitness@home — 2013-08-25T14:28:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-93687>

I can only speak for myself here. I have suffered from a strange affliction I like to call "Mirror Distortia" since my early teens. Over the years it didn't matter if I was underweight, normal weight, or overweight... My body's reflection never appeared to be good enough in my eyes.

Women are definitely too hard on themselves. I felt the worst about my body after the birth of each child...when that is a completely normal to time for a woman's body to have gained weight and change shape!

Another symptom of "Mirror Distotia" is when you look at old pictures of yourself and suddenly realize you actually were NOT as large as you thought. In fact, you appear to be very normal-sized...

We waste a lot of time being too hard on ourselves.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-08-25T14:31:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-93688>

When a person, regardless of their sex, relies on another person's opinion, it is a very slippery slope.

When a person asks the opposite sex for an opinion about body image, that is usually a no win situation for the person being asked. (aka - see point above)

Caroline ...... you provided the answer in your post ..... "I had all of that and more and it wasn't enough ....I needed to love myself ......I needed to feel good about myself. It had nothing to do with someone else's "perfect". It had to do with reaching my own potential and giving myself the gift of confidence and vitality and loving myself" .............. That is a profound and accurate view.

### Hemming — 2013-08-25T18:07:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-93707>

Yes, we definitely care about it, just look at all the guys working out in the gyms to impress the ladies with their guns. I completely agree with fitness@home, I have the same problem now of worrying too much about the mirror and my weight. When you truly love yourself you don't care about exactly how your body looks. I'm sadly not at that point right now but I'm trying to get there.

### Hope — 2013-08-25T20:36:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-93720>

This is a beautiful thread. I used to literally hate my body. Going through and dumping a lot of stuff to declutter and create a better space in our home, I came across an old calendar from 2000, where I'd write how I feel, what exercise I did etc. I literally wrote in there how I hate my body.....etc etc. I also always felt not good enough and then a guy I dated fed into the dysfunction....it's taken a lot of internal work and mental healing (largely just getting my brain healthier period, detoxified and just working better in general), and though I have a loving DH, I could never really hear his compliments, or even those of other people. I always rejected it. It wasn't until I really started to love and accept myself at a deep level - and this is still an ongoing process - that I could really hear it, believe it and tell myself. In fact just this week I had to chuckle when my dentist called me 'thin' (which at 150 and 5'8'' I am not really yet), but I could accept it. AND I can now look into the mirror and love my body. In fact I just did that this morning trying on clothes and seeing what I wanna keep. Surely an actual tan helps too. lol.

I now believe it doesn't matter what others say - it's all about what you believe about yourself. I've studied Taoism and Chinese Medicine some, which fascinates me.....one of the tenets of it is the Earth Element which correlates to the spleen, which makes blood, gives energy, key for immunity etc in Chinese Medicine. When you are not right with yourself, and specifically if you don't stand up for yourself and know deep down your own value, and beauty, you constantly look for it in the wrong places, or you can't accept it from other people....it has to come from inside. And when that doesn't happen, you waste a lot of energy and ultimately can feel miserable physically...all of which I did and suffered with. It's so so fascinating. And I see all kinds of truly beautiful people do it too - they have a beauty that nature gave them, but they can't see it, for whatever reason....I often think of Princess Diana with this. But anyway.

I know now that I don't need to look like a movie star to feel healthy, and beautiful and good enough.....and when I do get compliments, it's like icing on the cake, a reflection of what I already know. Like at Whole Foods I was picking out veggies and one of the helpers said the other day, "you look awesome today". And I was a bit shocked, because I was wearing an old skirt, a plain old white shirt. Nothing fancy at all. But I enjoyed it.

It's definitely a new thing for me, and I am sure without the various JK protocols, the magnetico sleep pad, iodine detox and almost nightly meditation, I might not have ever gotten 'here'. It sure took me long enough, considering I just turned 40. I used to be jealous of girls that had all the goods AND confidence at far younger ages.....but now I'm OK with it. I am on my own journey......and that's what it's all about

### Inger — 2013-08-25T22:11:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-93734>

Very beautiful said, Hope. Me too have suffered a lot with this. I always find the other more beautiful.. but when I am out in the wild I always feel so good. No issues with body image. And when I get enough rest and eat well and all this stuff. Then I feel so ok, even if I see I am far from perfect. It just does not matter..lol Right now I feel not so well, I miss my own privacy so much. I am far too close to others energies, that are not really building me up. This is hard for me but I need to find a way to cope. I just ned to breath once in a while, to find some lonely time. I do not even have time to read the posts here now. It will get easier when I get my first payment, now I live on others mercy and I need to be with them much as they wish so. Taking care of kids and stuff. Sure they are so lovely but still there is so much noise, kids that get fed crap is not alwasy so easy to handle for sure.. but it is not their fault. Poor kids. It hurts me to see.

Shortly said... when I have less stress and more peace in my life I feel so much better - and I look better too  I bet because I am a woman and we are so sensible beeings. IDK but all I crave is just... peace. Too much noise in the world today. Tomorrow I am going to take my bike and hide at the beach!

### nicld — 2013-08-25T22:20:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-93735>

Yes, yes, yes and yes. I am hoping someday to over come body image issues. I have got better since I saw Robin Woodall but each day is somewhat of a struggle. ### caroline — 2013-08-26T02:16:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-93772>

Thankyou all for sharing your thoughts and feelings.

There is something I haven't articulated yet .... I can't really work it out. Like fitness - I would look in a mirror and see an overweight person - no matter what size........

But - it is the Leptin reset that is the game changer here. Our brains are much healthier .... we are much healthier.
I now weigh less than I ever have .... but that totally is not the issue. It is the body composition that is so much better.
So - even tho I weigh less - I look so much healthier and my shape is so much better, and I am so much happier

The most important thing for me is - my outside matches how I feel on the inside. I can look at myself naked and think - hey - I look just fine.

So - our brain is now in the game ..... Dr. K. is probably thinking to himself......."it took you long enough"

Give us enough rope and we will eventually climb into the light ...........

### caroline — 2013-08-26T04:56:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-93792>

When I started this thread it was because of something that Jack had said in my journal....
I didn't mean for this to be about me so much....

I really want to know how an obese man feels .....is it anywhere near the same as a woman? or is it totally different?

When an obese man, or a thin man, wants to go to bed with a woman ....does he even think about his shape/size/weight?
Does it impact him at least a little ....or are they good to go?

I guess, as JK said in the sex webinar, a woman needs a symphony........ and I don't think a man does....

sorry if I am off track ..... but it all is about how we feel about ourselves - especially when our brain isn't optimal.

### prAna303 — 2013-08-26T20:30:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-93828>

Now we start to talk... Brains that share things other than just food.

Yes we do look at others with other eyes than before and i think that we have lost the feeling for being a complete human. On one side we have the ones only living for the body and the way they look and on the other side the ones that seek so called mindfulness. Today most care how they look but not in how they attract others, it is a constant catwalk but without the glamour. When did it go wrong?

If i look at myself i always hated to be present on pictures, there is not a single photo from my childhood and that makes me feel great. Today i know why i hated this so much, when we start to capture frames of our lifes we only get some small parts of what we are. I always thought i looked like a idiot because i am very physical in my prescence, a still frame is just boring and you get to look at meaningless details. I think we are not made up with a sense for frozen pictures, it is scaring because still means dead. Today everyone is snapping frames of themself eating dinner, taking a zip of java and documenting their exercise. Everything is about frames of the perfect life, this makes us unsure.

The mirrors are a man made utility, the only time we could see ourself before that was in the lake when it is still, did we get scared? Yes...

I always listen to the voice of my body, when i feel that i can not do my adventures in life something must change. The image in the mirror is not the reality. I helped one of my friends with her image of her body. She is an athlete at the highest level and she felt like shit about her body. A simple picture, photoshoped and she felt that she was amazing. The best thing was when i showed her a movie when she moves, then it clicked for her big time... She looks like a wild animal and that is beauty for me AND her now.

When we move with a purpose in life we always look wonderful.

Caroline, when you sit on the beach and look around. The things around you is so back to basic... If you look at men surfing it is the same as looking at to wild creatures fighting for the female attention. It is deep inside us.

So i think that if we try to get the both sides, body and mind we shine. When a body works with the mind in sync it is perfection, a single frame does not matter anymore, that is so boring.

### caroline — 2013-08-27T02:48:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-93858>

Peter .... as always ....you nailed it. Is that what Dr. Kruse has been saying all along? When body and mind work together we can shine.....that is perfection .....how perfect to be imperfect and love ourselves all the more.

There has been a huge revelation to me lately - just the last couple of days. I had coffee with a friend at the ocean - and after I left her I realised how excited I always am around her. We talk about the craziest, off the chart stuff - it is as if I come to life around her and I now realise I am really bored around a lot of my other friends. They are smart, lovely people but I am on a different wave length, a different path that they have no concept of and have no interest in....

### cantweight — 2013-08-27T05:27:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-93878>

I've always felt kind of weird with my body perception. I constantly forget that I am fat. When I dream I am always fit and healthy looking.... On multiple occasions I have written my weight on papers I had to fill out for Drs. as 1 something instead of 2 something. I am always shocked by pictures of myself. I look like someone swallowed me. Its kind of eerie the way I honestly forget the reality of my physical appearance.

I try not be hard on myself as I have learned too much to chastise myself for the state of my bod. I punished it enough when i was young....that really didn't serve us well. It is still in the process of cleaning up a huge mess, its has done right by me so far....I still have faith in it.

There are times when obesity smacks me upside the head. Amusement parks....Im so afraid of being over the weight limit, getting dressed for parties is hard when I hate the fat clothes I have to wear, and when other people look at me with that look....that look that says I know you sit on your couch all day, watch Oprah, and eat donuts. I hate that shit.....but I get over it....and eventually I am back to forgetting I am fat. Weird right?

### caroline — 2013-08-27T05:34:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-93880>

I totally get that ... I never felt fat on the inside - not until I looked in a mirror .... and then I couldn't understand how all that fat had attached itself to me!

You always hear stories of people who have lost weight and then regain it ....and people \[supposedly in the know\] would say it is because they don't see themselves as a thin person. What a stupid statement really.

I always thought of myself as a thin person - regardless of what my exterior looked like ....

### AnnieW — 2013-08-29T16:23:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-94157>

I like this thread but it makes me sad because of my self image. I am just gross. I'm 6' tall and almost 350 lbs. My shape is not feminine at all. I have a HUGE stomach and almost no butt or boobs. I am covered in stretch marks and have tons of body hair thanks to PCOS. I am sad to think that my skin is going to suck after I lose this weight. Since doing the leptin reset I feel pretty good inside with the exception of some GI issues which are related to me changing my diet. I am hopeful that I won't look that bad when I'm done. I'm also losing hair on my head. I hope the reset helps fix a lot of these hormonal issues.

### cantweight — 2013-08-29T16:36:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-94160>

Hang in there Annie...that good feeling you have inside will grow exponentially! You will notice things bit by bit that make you feel good....a change in skin tone, bright eyes, a healthier mouth, little things that will add up to something incredible. I am still very overweight...very very overweight. But the good feeling has spread, I am grateful to my body for healing from so much, my PCOS symptoms are minimal...the acne is less, even the hair is slower growing and seems lighter on my face...easier to keep it in its place so I dont feel like a goat anymore..lol.

My hair went from falling out to fantastic. My hairdresser couldnt believe it at my last cut. The grey is less visible for some odd reason and it is shiny and full. There is so much good to how I feel that the fat is just the last piece I am waiting for my body to figure out how to deal with.

The stretch marks will fade and Dr K has not had major issues with excess skin and I am hoping for the same. Put good thoughts in your head. Even if you dont believe them at first. Visualize your healthy, whole body and then do your best to see it through

### prAna303 — 2013-08-31T09:36:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-94449>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=94432)
>
> We all arrived here for different reasons ..... and look what we wound up with! Losing the weight etc. etc. is so secondary to everything else we are achieving. I hope one day we can make Dr. K. proud .... it must be so hard dragging us all kicking and screaming to a new reality ....which is really an old reality .... the laws of nature.

Yes, we all have different stories. I joined this place because it matched my own journey of optimal, not in the way of being sick or ill, just because many things Jack tells us has been natural to me my whole life. I have always been a outsider, weirdo and on my own trail. Obsessed i have been called but never ever gave up on my own inner voice, the one that i was born with. Looking thru so many places many of them touches things just a little bit, like making ripples on a lake but this place i feel that change is happening in a way that i feel is right. Perhaps this is the reason why so many come and go here? It is scary when your bubble is out in the open and you get advices that tears down every little thing you thought was right. I do not take it personal because i lived like this my whole life, i know it can be really tough to stand alone but i love it, times with others are around the corner the whole time. Time after time i stand alone but i KNOW soon company is here again, it is in my blueprint to find them. This is what i mean with resonation, it is not how a girl look, it is the complete human that walks past me that crashes in to me. I can see a model looking girl that everyone around me is drooling about and feel nothing, just because the shell and brains is out of sync. Take a beautiful mind and match it with a body of purpose, i see a reason to live, the body is the tool to do your thoughts, I AM IN LOVE.

This is the reason why my prefered taste in company is the ones that DO it and not ACTING it. In the kind of tribes i live with a common word is "posers", never trust the guy with shining gears...

So bottom line: For me looks tells a story, please never ever remove scars and other marks. If you get some of them, tell the reason why, it says that you LIVED. The only thing i feel matters is the sync between mind and body. Live with a purpose and you shine.

### caroline — 2013-08-31T10:08:02+0300

I have always been a outsider, weirdo and on my own trail. Obsessed i have been called but never ever gave up on my own inner voice, the one that i was born with. Looking thru so many places many of them touches things just a little bit, like making ripples on a lake but this place i feel that change is happening in a way that i feel is right. Perhaps this is the reason why so many come and go here? It is scary when your bubble is out in the open and you get advices that tears down every little thing you thought was right. I do not take it personal because i lived like this my whole life, i know it can be really tough to stand alone but i love it, times with others are around the corner the whole time. Time after time i stand alone but i KNOW soon company is here again, it is in my blueprint to find them. This is what i mean with resonation, it is not how a girl look, it is the complete human that walks past me that crashes in to me. I can see a model looking girl that everyone around me is drooling about and feel nothing, just because the shell and brains is out of sync. Take a beautiful mind and match it with a body of purpose, i see a reason to live, the body is the tool to do your thoughts, I AM IN LOVE.
>
> This is the reason why my prefered taste in company is the ones that DO it and not ACTING it. In the kind of tribes i live with a common word is "posers", never trust the guy with shining gears...
>
> So bottom line: For me looks tells a story, please never ever remove scars and other marks. If you get some of them, tell the reason why, it says that you LIVED. The only thing i feel matters is the sync between mind and body. Live with a purpose and you shine.

I loved this post. So beautiful.

### Inger — 2013-08-31T18:00:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-94489>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=94452)
>
> Take a beautiful mind and match it with a body of purpose...... that is what I am feeling more and more and could not articulate it until recently. I get so bored when I am around people with no purpose.
>
> I love when someone has a passion for something, anything --- then they have me at "hello"
>
> Passion vibrates thru your whole body ..... I think you can see someone's soul ....it is beyond exciting...
>
> It changes everything - it lights up the space you are in .... not a word needs to be spoken.
>
> My DH was a very quiet man ...but he was magic.......

and I loved this too Audrey.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Psychedelic Drugs Linked to Lower Risk of Mental Illness?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychedelic-drugs-linked-to-lower-risk-of-mental-illness.8021/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Optimalbound — 2013-08-25T12:52:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychedelic-drugs-linked-to-lower-risk-of-mental-illness.8021/post-93685>

An epidemiological study questions old dogma:

[http://www.healthline.com/health-news/mental-psychedelics-not-linked-to-mental-health-problems-082213](http://www.healthline.com/health-news/mental-psychedelics-not-linked-to-mental-health-problems-082213)

> Examining data on more than 130,000 Americans in the National Survey on Drug Use and Health, researchers from the Norwegian University of Science and Technology's Department of Neuroscience found that the use of psychedelic drugs not only doesnâ€™t contribute to mental illness, but also may actually have a protective effect against it.
>
> They found that people who used psilocybin or mescaline throughout their lives, as well as people who used LSD in the past year, had lower rates of serious psychological distress, outpatient mental health treatment, and prescriptions for psychiatric medications.
>
> â€œOther studies have found no evidence of health or social problems among people who had used psychedelics hundreds of times in legally protected religious ceremonies,â€￾ clinical psychologist PÃ¥l-Ã˜rjan Johansen said.

### Antonis — 2018-05-12T21:18:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychedelic-drugs-linked-to-lower-risk-of-mental-illness.8021/post-241087>

I want to know about this. I heard people saying that those drugs helped people when they tried them with a clear mind. They were talking about "the 3rd eye" and helping with stress.

### Jason Coates — 2018-05-14T13:58:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychedelic-drugs-linked-to-lower-risk-of-mental-illness.8021/post-241154>

One of the functional med Doctors on the Quantum Facebook Group insists that he has seen heavy Psilocybin usage cause damage to people. I would approach with caution - even including the microdosing. MAPS has plenty of research available and yet we are to be convinced. I would do my homework on this.

### Jason Coates — 2018-05-14T15:39:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychedelic-drugs-linked-to-lower-risk-of-mental-illness.8021/post-241164>

There is a shitload pro and anti psychedelics available - I have listened to many a podcast from experts from the pro-research groups etc. Quantum Biology #11 touches on reality iro quantum physics and LSD. I don't you will get Dr Kruse to comment beyond QED on this one. Perhaps spend some time keywording it and Jack Kruse on Google.

### Antonis — 2018-05-14T15:48:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychedelic-drugs-linked-to-lower-risk-of-mental-illness.8021/post-241165>

> [Jason Coates said:](/goto/post?id=241164)
>
> There is a shitload pro and anti psychedelics available - I have listened to many a podcast from experts from the pro-research groups etc. Quantum Biology #11 touches on reality iro quantum physics and LSD. I don't you will get Dr Kruse to comment beyond QED on this one. Perhaps spend some time keywording it and Jack Kruse on Google.

I have friends who tried it with low doses

### Jason Coates — 2018-05-14T16:18:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychedelic-drugs-linked-to-lower-risk-of-mental-illness.8021/post-241166>

I can understand why it may help for dysthymia and creativity. I have no experience with microdosing multiple times. As a member of various Facebook Forums around Psychedelics there often are people who report major problems as a result of heavy use of LSD, mushrooms, Peyote etc. These forums are worth exploring if you can get past the rubbish:like what movies and music to listen to whilst tripping etc. Reports on Erowid can be helpful. So too, their research etc.

### Antonis — 2018-05-14T16:35:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychedelic-drugs-linked-to-lower-risk-of-mental-illness.8021/post-241169>

> [Jason Coates said:](/goto/post?id=241166)
>
> I can understand why it may help for dysthymia and creativity. I have no experience with microdosing multiple times. As a member of various Facebook Forums around Psychedelics there often are people who report major problems as a result of heavy use of LSD, mushrooms, Peyote etc. These forums are worth exploring if you can get past the rubbish:like what movies and music to listen to whilst tripping etc. Reports on Erowid can be helpful. So too, their research etc.

I understand mate. I was just curious if somebody did already a research on these drugs and knows. I don't trust online bullshit. I really have an issue with my stress but I would never go to drungs than be in the sun and change my environment

### Jason Coates — 2018-05-14T17:18:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychedelic-drugs-linked-to-lower-risk-of-mental-illness.8021/post-241171>

By "research" do you mean personal experience? There are plenty of people here who have.
There was a TV documentary I saw where a man was taking epic doses of mushrooms for his migraines. He hated the trips, but it was the only thing that ever helped him. Consider that if you are going to level 4 or 5 once a month what it can do:
**Level 4**
Strong hallucinations, i.e. objects morphing into other objects. Destruction or multiple splitting of the ego. (Things start talking to ou, or you find that you are feeling contradictory things simultaneously). Some loss of reality. Time becomes meaningless. Out of body experiences and e.s.p. type phenomena. Blending of the senses.
**Level 5**
Total loss of visual connection with reality. The senses cease to function in the normal way. Total loss of ego. Merging with space, other objects, or the universe. The loss of reality becomes so severe that it defies explanation. The earlier levels are relatively easy to explain in terms of measurable changes in perception and thought patterns. This level is different in that the actual universe within which things are normally perceived, ceases to exist! Satori enlightenment (and other such labels). [https://www.shroomery.org/9067/What-do-the-different-trip-levels-Lvl-1-5-mean](https://www.shroomery.org/9067/What-do-the-different-trip-levels-Lvl-1-5-mean)

Most people don't go past level 3 and then only do it a few times in their lives. If they hit a 4 or a 5 it usually scares them off it. Microdosing is a relatively new phenomenon.

### Antonis — 2018-05-14T17:25:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychedelic-drugs-linked-to-lower-risk-of-mental-illness.8021/post-241172>

> [Jason Coates said:](/goto/post?id=241171)

> Strong hallucinations, i.e. objects morphing into other objects. Destruction or multiple splitting of the ego. (Things start talking to ou, or you find that you are feeling contradictory things simultaneously). Some loss of reality. Time becomes meaningless. Out of body experiences and e.s.p. type phenomena. > **Level 5**

The loss of reality becomes so severe that it defies explanation. The earlier levels are relatively easy to explain in terms of measurable changes in perception and thought patterns. This level is different in that the actual universe within which things are normally perceived, ceases to exist! Satori enlightenment (and other such labels). [https://www.shroomery.org/9067/What-do-the-different-trip-levels-Lvl-1-5-mean](https://www.shroomery.org/9067/What-do-the-different-trip-levels-Lvl-1-5-mean)

Yeah, elfs and this kind of drugs. Probably your brain cells are hyperactive and the work too much. You subconscious level aswell and all ypur memoriea mixed up. Thats why it may help one of he goes with the right attitude. DUNNO but it is scarry

# Thread: Trying the coldest CT to date
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-the-coldest-ct-to-date.8027/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2013-08-25T17:24:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-the-coldest-ct-to-date.8027/post-93704>

It is warm here and going to get HOT today so I am going to try my coldest CT to date. I have been making ice chunks for the past week by filling up ice cream buckets about half full (too full and the buckets break) and I have two milk jugs frozen too. I am gong to try for an hour but at least 45 minutes. I will probably do another warmer shorter one tonight like I did last night. If I am too hot going at bed time, I just don't sleep.

Wish me luck.

### nicld — 2013-08-25T19:33:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-the-coldest-ct-to-date.8027/post-93714>

Thanks I managed to stay in for an hour and when I got done most of my ice was melted and the skin was just about 50Â°. The skin was pretty pink except where the tummy roll and the low tatas covered the flesh. I stayed indoors and did the dishes to try and keep cold for a while. Now I outside in the 90Â° heat rewarming for a while.

Definitely will try to do this in the mornings on the weekends when I have plenty of time to rewarm. Just don't think I am adapted enough to do it at night and rewarm enough to get to sleep.

### Optimalbound — 2013-08-25T21:59:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-the-coldest-ct-to-date.8027/post-93732>

A full hour is very impressive. Well done!

### nicld — 2013-08-25T22:24:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-the-coldest-ct-to-date.8027/post-93737>

Its not bad when I am in it, just the rewarming in brutal right now. I know it may not be optimal but I watch Super skinny versus Super fat on my kindle. Makes the time just fly. The lower legs are still tingling so I am hoping it did something.

### freesia — 2013-08-26T00:28:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-the-coldest-ct-to-date.8027/post-93755>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=93737)
>
> Its not bad when I am in it, just the rewarming in brutal right now. I know it may not be optimal but I watch Super skinny versus Super fat on my kindle. Makes the time just fly. The lower legs are still tingling so I am hoping it did something.

Re-warming will become way less brutal. (Brutal is the perfect description by the way. LOL.)
Enjoy soaking up some sun afterwards while you still have it, I don't think it hinders progress.
You'll be sailing through CT **and** warm-up by the time Winter comes.
(You are doing so well!!)

### nicld — 2013-08-26T01:36:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-the-coldest-ct-to-date.8027/post-93764>

Thanks Freesia, I hope it gets better by winter, cause in the colder, longer CT's is brutal. I don't think I could have started this in the winter without having a heating blanket on the bed.

I will try to do a couple of colder ones this week since I can step outside when I am done and rewarm since the temps are supposed to be in the 90's and humid all week. I HATE the heat.

# Thread: Cashews?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cashews.8029/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MeghanK — 2013-08-25T19:25:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cashews.8029/post-93713>

I love raw cashews... I usually eat a few raw almonds and raw cashews as a night snack (I know snacks aren't Epi-Paleo but I need to gain 20lbs so they are for me).... anyway... are raw cashews OK?

### Jonathin — 2013-08-28T06:49:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cashews.8029/post-94016>

There are a number of iffy things about cashews. Seeds of all sorts have problems. Coconut manna or coconut based bark might be a better source of calories. Raw grassfed Cream is an option if milk is not a problem.

Food you love can get you into trouble.

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-08-28T16:22:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cashews.8029/post-94037>

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=93713)
>
> I love raw cashews... I usually eat a few raw almonds and raw cashews as a night snack (I know snacks aren't Epi-Paleo but I need to gain 20lbs so they are for me).... anyway... are raw cashews OK?

Google "cashews toxins" for some interesting reading...

### nonchalant — 2013-08-28T16:35:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cashews.8029/post-94042>

Yeah, I was so sad when Jack said "no" to cashews.

### cantweight — 2013-08-28T17:26:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cashews.8029/post-94049>

We stopped eating raw cashews after reading that the shells contain the same poison as poison ivy. That is why they are never shelled. Roasting is believed to kill the urushiol. But roasting oxidizes the fats. So basically there is no good way to eat cashews...bummer.

How about brazil nuts?

### Hope — 2013-08-28T23:50:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cashews.8029/post-94107>

Have you tried hazelnuts? Super expensive, but boy, amazing. Mixed with a little raw organic cocoa = my only treat. Super yummy.

### Grantmeliberty — 2013-09-02T09:27:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cashews.8029/post-94666>

The concern about eating "raw" cashews was something I encountered about 34 years ago, but didn't come to a conclusion until a few years ago. The "raw" cashews from the health food store are already heated enough to deactivate the toxins. Actually roasting them is unnecessary.

### Albert83BCN — 2013-09-02T10:15:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cashews.8029/post-94667>

Every single food has some issues if you look deep into it, just saying...

# Thread: Please help with my new lab results
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-with-my-new-lab-results.8048/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Flora — 2013-08-26T22:28:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-with-my-new-lab-results.8048/post-93836>

Have just received my lab results and am pretty worried - particularly by the thyroid tests.

The antibody tests have come back high (but I don't know how high as I haven't yet managed to find out what constitutes high) and I guess this means a Hashimotos diagnosis, despite normal TSH and free T4 and T3? (Reverse T3 has been done but will take a month for result, as it has to be done in Paris!)
The results came back directly to me and I haven't yet heard from my doctor - so no feedback yet.
Anyone know if there is any other reason for high antibodies?

A bit of background - I'm 58 and have followed JK for the past year, adopting epi-paleo (I do eat raw milk - mostly sheep or goats) and gradually incorporating as much as possible as I've followed the blog posts.
I did low-carb 6 months before that and lost 20 lbs but then weightloss stopped and fatigue worsened - not terrible all the time but definitely not right and lots of unexplained aches and pains too and sleep went downhill. Also put on 10 lbs very easily during spring when excessively stressed. But no other thyroid malfunction symptoms such as hairloss, cold, constipation - in fact hair is thick and virtually no grey and I'm usually hot rather than cold (especially at night).
Sleep has improved this summer since taking 50mg DHEA - also take Mg malate, niacin, occasional kelp if I've not eaten fish for a couple of days.
I think we are in a low EMF environment (but Tri-meter is on its way for testing) and I aim to do some grounding each day and observe light cycles (used blueblockers, candles and firelight last winter)

The thing that worries me most is that my Mum died of thyroid cancer 20 years ago - so I've had thyroid checked before but this is the first time antibodies have been done.
I live in very rural France (but am English), which adds to difficulties with doctor!

I last had some labs 9 months ago but (different) doctor wouldn't to Thyroid antibodies then - comparison levels of those available included below.

I maybe misguided - or not have enough info - but the rest of my labs seem to be ok - Vit D, cholesterol panel, DHEA, HS CRP, which are the basic ones Jack recommends for the Optimal reset.

I think my BUN/creatinine ratio may be shot, though - as it appears to be 33 ??? (and was at the last test too - but doc didn't comment. I only picked this up from reading this forum recently). Maybe I haven't worked it out correctly (lab doesn't do it) but it's rather worrying, isn't it?

Anyway - I'd be really grateful for any help / comments anyone can give.
I'm afraid the results are in French, but it's fairly easy to work out what's what!
Many thanks - Flora

# Thread: Fitbit? Jawbone?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitbit-jawbone.8053/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MeghanK — 2013-08-27T03:05:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitbit-jawbone.8053/post-93860>

I wanted to purchase one of these devices to track sleep and activity etc.... but was worried about the EMFs... would something like this emit EMF?
[Fitbit](http://www.fitbit.com/one?gclid=CJ7P3uCpnLkCFfE-Mgod7wsAjQ)

[Jawbone](https://jawbone.com/up)

Anyone use either? Likes? Dislikes?
Worth the $100 price?

Thanks!

### Danco3636 — 2013-08-27T03:39:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitbit-jawbone.8053/post-93863>

Blue tooth transmition. I keep mine off.

### MeghanK — 2013-08-27T17:28:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitbit-jawbone.8053/post-93907>

> [Danco3636 said:](/goto/post?id=93863)
>
> Blue tooth transmition. I keep mine off.

What? I'm confused.....

### MeghanK — 2013-08-27T17:29:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitbit-jawbone.8053/post-93908>

> [Danco3636 said:](/goto/post?id=93863)
>
> Blue tooth transmition. I keep mine off.

What? I'm confused.....

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-08-27T17:49:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitbit-jawbone.8053/post-93910>

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=93908)
>
> What? I'm confused.....

These devices connect wirelessly to your phone via Bluetooth, a wireless technology standard for exchanging data over short distances (using short-wavelength radio transmissions in the ISM band from 2400–2480 MHz) from fixed and mobile devices. In other words... EMF. EMF in the vicinity of your brain. Might not be a good idea. Hence, Dan keeps his off, as do I.

### caroline — 2013-08-28T02:43:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitbit-jawbone.8053/post-93978>

Is a Holter monitor \[to check heart rate\] a bad thing as well? My Doc wants me to wear one for a couple of days - just a precaution.... I keep putting it off...

### Nrelax11 — 2019-09-01T02:35:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitbit-jawbone.8053/post-276934>

My parents bought my girlfriend one recently. I wish they would have told me they were going to, so I could have told them not to. When we visited them in June, she wore it around them because we felt bad lol. Was th only time she wore it.

### malc0088 — 2019-09-04T20:40:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitbit-jawbone.8053/post-277147>

Trash for sure! I don't own one but see alot of people who wear them, and another concern with them is the color of light that is used to gather the biometric data via the wrist. A Lot of the ones i see are using a green light, though i have not measured the spectrum, looks like it might fall within the blue/green range that we should be mitigating. I measure HRV in the morning when i wake up and I use an old fashioned strap and my phone via bluetooth for 3 minutes.

# Thread: Tired after breakfast
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-after-breakfast.8059/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AnnieW — 2013-08-27T17:51:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-after-breakfast.8059/post-93912>

This morning around 6:45 I had my BAB which was two grass-fed beef patties, three eggs fried in coconut oil, several pieces of bacon, and a few spoonfuls of coconut manna. I'm not hungry at all and my body feels good, I just feel really tired. Yesterday I had the same thing happen but it was in the afternoon and I was nearly dozing off at my desk. It almost hits me out of the blue, like I just ate a huge sugar-laden meal. Any ideas?

### cantweight — 2013-08-27T18:10:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-after-breakfast.8059/post-93915>

It took my body a couple weeks to get use to low carb/high fat BABs. I believe it was my body lacking the ability to efficiently convert fat and protein to energy. I was a sugar burner, but denying the sugar gave my body no choice and it eventually caught on. Perhaps that is what you are seeing?

### RGP — 2013-08-27T20:10:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-after-breakfast.8059/post-93925>

> [AnnieW said:](/goto/post?id=93912)
>
> This morning around 6:45 I had my BAB which was two grass-fed beef patties, three eggs fried in coconut oil, several pieces of bacon, and a few spoonfuls of coconut manna. I'm not hungry at all and my body feels good, I just feel really tired. Yesterday I had the same thing happen but it was in the afternoon and I was nearly dozing off at my desk. It almost hits me out of the blue, like I just ate a huge sugar-laden meal. Any ideas?

Your breakfast is extremely acidic and so your pH level may be low. Check your pH levels and make sure that your drinking water is slightly alkaline to help buffer the acidic meals. Worked wonders for me.

Could be you have a CBS SNP and your sulfur levels are too high. I have multiple CBS SNP's and I can't do eggs.

Just a couple of thoughts.

### AnnieW — 2013-08-27T22:44:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-after-breakfast.8059/post-93944>

Thank you both so much for your replies. It wouldn't surprise me if it's my body just looking for sugar. I used to eat four breakfast biscuits and a large Coke from Chic-fil-a. I drink spring water so I'm not sure what the pH is.

RGP, what is CBS SNP? What can you do to raise the body's pH?

Thanks again!

### caroline — 2013-08-28T02:53:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-after-breakfast.8059/post-93980>

Hi Annie....I used to be really tired after BAB too. I upped the fat and lowered the protein. That really helped a lot.

### RGP — 2013-08-28T15:32:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-after-breakfast.8059/post-94026>

AnnieW:
CBS is the enzyme which catalyses the reaction which converts homocysteine into cystathionine (that's a mouthful). When one has a CBS SNP (single nucleotide polymorphism) or gene defect, the SNP can increase the enzyme activity (known as upregulation) of the CBS gene . If the gene is expressing or turned on (just because one has the defect doesn't mean its turned on), excess sulfur may increase CBS enzyme activity. A few causes may be high vitamin B6 supplementation, excess cortisol, excess sulfur in the diet. For more info about this, Google Dr. Amy Yasko's paper called "Methionine and Methylation: Chicken or the Egg".

You can obtain your gene information by doing a saliva test through 23andme.com ($99), then input your data for free at geneticgenie.com or for a small fee, go to mthfrsupport.com and receive a much more detailed report.

I follow the Epipaleo diet and I have active CBS SNP's. Therefore, I have to consume less meat/fish protein than I would like and watch the amount of sulfur obtained from food (meat protein, eggs, onions, garlic, etc).

I found that my spring water's pH level was around 5. So to raise my body's pH level, I raised the pH level of my drinking water to 7.5 - 8.0. I buffer my drinking water with magnesium bicarbonate (just Google to find recipe using seltzer water and milk of magnesia) or by mixing 2 parts sodium bicarbonate (baking soda) to 1 part potassium bicarbonate and measuring the pH level. I always measure the final pH because I don't want it too high. FYI: adding tea or coffee to your water will lower the pH, so the water one uses for these drinks will need to have a higher pH.

By only increasing the pH of my drinking water, my urine and saliva pH levels are in the ranges I want.

For more info on pH, check out the forum thread "The Iodine Thread". A poster named Grizz has provided alot of great info.

### AnnieW — 2013-08-28T18:41:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-after-breakfast.8059/post-94062>

This is very interesting. I have been in contact with Grizz as he is sharing some iodine info with me.

Thanks!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

### Flora — 2013-08-27T17:53:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-with-labs-2.8060/post-93913>

Hi
My last thread (yesterday) seems to have been locked down for some reason - so will try again and hopefully this time will be ok!

Please could anyone give me some feedback - I'm concerned about the Thyroid antibody results (first time I've been tested) - a) does this mean I'm definitely hypothyroid and b) do I need medication, or if I observe everything epi-paleo will I be able to manage without?
Is there anyone who has had similar results (high TG antibodies/ normal T3 and T4 levels) who might be able to give some feedback, please?

Also concerned about BUN/creatinine ratio of 33 (if I have calculated it correctly?) Could someone more technical possible check it for me - it doesn't look good.
And Iron levels seemed to have increased alot in last 8 months (previous tests on 3/12/12 included where I had them)

I've been following JK and the blog for almost a year and have started to see some improvements lately in sleep (50mg DHEA supplementation?) - but still no weightloss, nor much improved energy.
I live in rural France with very little EMFs in surroundings (edge of forest, end of line for power supply, nearest neighbour at 50m with no computer, leave alone wi-fi!). Have yet to check home environment but test meter in post. Water is from underground river out of the limestone hills, supplying just 80 households and minimally treated (def. no fluoride). Try to do some grounding each day and have spent time in the hot sunshine we've had all summer.

It occurs to me that some of the other results have improved since last time ( lipid profile) and vit D and HS-CRP are ok? Maybe the TGabs WERE worse and have now improved - I guess I just have to check again in 6 months?

Any help very gratefully received
Thanks, Flora

OK no replies so far - maybe I need to ask better or more specific questions.

My Pg is also above the norm (2.5 ng/ml N is 0.1 -0.2) - but I've been using transdermal progesterone cream for the last 10 years.

Vit D: 61
HDL: 83
DHEAs: 375.4
HS-CRP:0.9

The thyroid antibody results are very worrying to me, but I figure if I can fix the other things by upping my game and have a good cold winter with CT (currently on 10 min daily cold showers), less EMFs, correct light etc, maybe they will improve?
NB I'm not sure whether I should be asking for thyroxin or am better without it? My doc hasn't yet received my results (mine came by email, hers by post) - so no feedback there (but I don't have much faith seeing as she thinks eggs cause high cholesterol and wants to put me on statins and says I'm 'being trendy' asking for Vit D level! ).

Would very much appreciate any thoughts
Thanks
Flora

# Thread: From Fruit Bat to Great White
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-fruit-bat-to-great-white.8091/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AdaptogenicBeing — 2013-08-29T16:49:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-fruit-bat-to-great-white.8091/post-94163>

Greetings everyone. I wanted to drop in and talk about some of the success Ive had since finding dr K and this site.

A bit about me. Ive been dealing with an inflammatory issue every fall/winter for the past 11 yrs. My fingers and toes would swell to the point of looking like sausages. Ive always been a "biohacker" of sorts and after conventional medicine had very little to offer in my case the hacking came more from necessity than fun. I had been told it was chilblanes and that I didn't show signs of Reynaud's. Regardless it was very much affecting my ability to work and just have a half way enjoyable life.

This led me into the path of raw veganism. I had read much talk in the natural hygiene movement that condemned meat fish and other foods as being at the root of the decline of the human condition(much of this based on some wonky science and nothing more). I was sure at that time I had found my magic bullet and for 3.5 years I followed a raw vegan diet mostly centered around fruit and veggies. I wont lie it did make me feel awesome for a period of 8 months. This was followed by a very slow decline. No matter how much I ate I always had a sense of depravation and more importantly the inflammation never went away and at some points I can say it got worse.

Towards the end of last summer I began to show signs of adrenal fatigue and leaky gut. I was unsure as to what it was at the time. In one last attempt to make myself better through raw I did a juice feast spanning 3 months mostly based on fruit juice(please hold back your laughs). Not only was it fruit based but it was during the winter as well. This only made things WAY worse. I decide to give up veganism all together after hearing Dave Asprey on a podcast. I listened to that podcast 3 times and finally became convinced doing what I was doing wasn't working. Failed N=1. Get over it and try something new. During the time of playing with Bulletproof I was sticking to the cyclical keto diet and what do ya know? When I was on no carb days my swelling disappeared(this was late Feb) The swelling in my extremities would perk back up on my reefed days. Sometime later I read the blog post Why We May Need Carbs for Performance and that was my eureka moment! Keto was probably my answer.

My adrenal/stomach issues continued to get worse however. At first I thought that it was the consumption of too much red meat so I cut back on that and upped my leafy green consumption(at the time I had just begun to read Jacks blogs. I was not yet on the seafood wagon) I noticed that as I ate more greens my brain fog got worse ie I would forget where I was for a few seconds at a clip, racing heart. All of these insane issues and keep in mind Im 31.

To make a short story long: I went to doctors. One of the offices is one of the top in the boston area. I was told to go on stat drugs and was handed a card for a psychiatrist. This was all in my head of course. I asked for an ASI or some sort of hormone panel to be done. I was shot down. I then sought out a functional med doc. He gave me the ASI I wanted as well as a slew of other tests. My cortisol ended up being low. He said that every thing else in the ASI was fine. Since listening to Dr K's webinars I now question this as my doc didn't look at the ratios. He also made the statement "progesterone means almost nothing in men". So ill need to dig that info up and review.

Anyway I am following a combo of both leptin reset and EPCOT. I here recently introduced Resveratrol and D-ribose into the mix. My brain fog has vanished. I no longer wake @ 2am with night sweats. And as for doing CT: Ive been a fan of Wim Hof for years and have been fascinated by the Tibetan tummos since I was in highschool. So ya I do it. I love IT! I am very much looking forward to following seasonal eating as laid out by Dr K/nature this coming fall and winter. I may have finally come across the info that will put the pain in my feet and hands to rest.

Be Well
Chad

### caroline — 2013-08-30T02:17:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-fruit-bat-to-great-white.8091/post-94278>

Hi Chad .....thanks for sharing your story and your journey. Please start a journal in the journal section so we can follow your progress. Please read other journals as well especially Martin's \[now know as BIG HEAD\] and fitness@home's journal.

When everyone contributes - it helps everyone else too.....

Have you been observing light cycles? and the most important of all ...have you checked your EMF exposure?

Have you listened to Dr. K's podcasts? great info on EMF's ect. etc. and have you read his book? THE EPI-PALEO RX .... available on amazon...

### AdaptogenicBeing — 2013-08-30T02:32:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-fruit-bat-to-great-white.8091/post-94280>

Hi Caroline. I will start a journal soon. I haven't checked my emf exposure as I moved back in with my parents so Im not in my own dwelling. They seem to have no issue with me unplugging the wifi when they are not using it. They cant understand why and think Im paranoid . I have been militant about light cycles. Bed down with the sun and get up with it. Ive listened to the podcasts and about half the webinars. I will read the book at some point.

Thanks!

### caroline — 2013-08-30T02:43:37+0300

# Thread: Chocolate
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.8094/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lsaremi — 2013-08-29T17:47:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.8094/post-94183>

I have been making chocolate out of coconut oil coconut flakes, Stevia, and cacao powder.... Is this ok to have?

Sent from my iPhone using Tapatalk - [now Free](http://tapatalk.com/m/)

### Danco3636 — 2013-08-29T18:55:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.8094/post-94204>

I make a delicious creamy Chocolate treat.
I use as a base organic coconut butter. 1/2 a jar.
Mix in some extra MCT oil and or coconut oil to make it a bit more creamier.
I then add in a teaspoon or two of organic cocoa - I use the Bulletproof organic 100% cocoa.
I then add in a 1/2 teaspoon of Bulletproof fresh ground vanilla.
Mix it up real good with a spoon, chill and then let warm back up to room temperature.
Turns out delicious.... A few table spoons or so as a great chocolate treat.
I don't add a sweetener as I don't need it but I can see how a dash of Stivia would bring out a sweet taste to it as well.

\- Cheers!

### Danco3636 — 2013-08-29T18:56:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chocolate.8094/post-94207>

I make a delicious creamy Chocolate treat.
I use as a base organic coconut butter. 1/2 a jar.
Mix in some extra MCT oil and or coconut oil to make it a bit more creamier.
I then add in a teaspoon or two of organic cocoa - I use the Bulletproof organic 100% cocoa.
I then add in a 1/2 teaspoon of Bulletproof fresh ground vanilla.
Mix it up real good with a spoon, chill and then let warm back up to room temperature.
Turns out delicious.... A few table spoons or so as a great chocolate treat.
I don't add a sweetener as I don't need it but I can see how a dash of Stivia would bring out a sweet taste to it as well.

# Thread: Relationship between prolactin and GH.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relationship-between-prolactin-and-gh.8096/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mally — 2013-08-29T18:38:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relationship-between-prolactin-and-gh.8096/post-94201>

Can you all help me understand the relationship between prolactin and growth hormone?

From what I've read on the forum, Kruse seems to be saying that a messed up circadian rhythm cycle causes a lack of prolactin surge which causes a lack of GH secretion (and possibly cortisol secretion later on in the early AM).

But I've also read that the most common picture is high prolactin, high IGF1 indicating high GH), low AM cortisol and high fasting blood sugar.

And that makes sense because bromocriptine/Cycloset would reduce the high prolactin and thus reduce the high GH which would lower the fasting blood sugar and raise the AM cortisol.

But I can't make a lack of prolactin surge work with lowering high prolactin levels from the second scenario.

Does Cycloset lower growth hormone or increase it? What is the relationship?

What have I missed?

Mally

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-29T18:55:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relationship-between-prolactin-and-gh.8096/post-94205>

CT 7 gives a great explaination of the circadian hormone cascade... [http://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-7/](http://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-7/)

Cycloset - lowers prolactin, increase am cortisol, and am dopamine

### Mally — 2013-08-29T19:12:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relationship-between-prolactin-and-gh.8096/post-94209>

Yes, CT 7 is exactly what got me totally confused.

It is saying that the prolactin surge is what is responsible for the GH surge.

But Cycloset lowers prolactin, it doesn't increase it.

So how does that work? It seems like Cycloset should lower GH secretion (because it is too high already in those with high prolactin) yet CT 7 is saying the opposite, I think.

I've missed something, just not sure what!

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-29T19:17:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relationship-between-prolactin-and-gh.8096/post-94210>

> [Mally said:](/goto/post?id=94209)
>
> Yes, CT 7 is exactly what got me totally confused.
>
> It is saying that the prolactin surge is what is responsible for the GH surge.
>
> But Cycloset lowers prolactin, it doesn't increase it.
>
> So how does that work? It seems like Cycloset should lower GH secretion (because it is too high already in those with high prolactin) yet CT 7 is saying the opposite, I think.
>
> I've missed something, just not sure what!

IIRC GH has two peaks, one at night and one during the day during its particular hormone circadian cycle. but I'll have to go back and look.

I'll have to go to my personal wiki and see if I can find the info I've put together on cycloset - but it in increase both dopamine and cortisol.

### Mally — 2013-08-29T19:51:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/relationship-between-prolactin-and-gh.8096/post-94214>

Thank you Shijin13!

Also, supplements like mucuna are supposed to lower prolactin supposedly by increasing dopamine. This is similar to Cycloset as a dopamine agonist.

But these supplements also promote themselves as potent growth hormone releasers.

I'd just really like to know how both of those things (lower prolactin and higher GH) could be possible.
