# Thread: Labs/Tests to ask for tomorrow for my Hallucinogen Persisting Perceptual Disorder
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-tests-to-ask-for-tomorrow-for-my-hallucinogen-persisting-perceptual-disorder.8102/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-08-30T08:04:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-tests-to-ask-for-tomorrow-for-my-hallucinogen-persisting-perceptual-disorder.8102/post-94315>

Hey all, I have a really quick set of questions to ask. Awhile back I posted on the thread I created, "tDCS and My Existentialist State of Mind," about a lot of the symptoms I've been having. Everyone was very supportive and Dr. Kruse dropped some knowledge in there that I really appreciate. What I'm certain I have is **HPPD**, or, **Hallucinogen Persisting Perceptual Disorder** ([http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hallucinogen\_persisting\_perception\_disorder](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hallucinogen_persisting_perception_disorder)) caused from use of an anticholinergic deliriant drug that _may have caused_ acute anticholinergic syndrome - again, that's what I believe brought on HPPD which also causes me to have anxiety and bouts of depersonalization and maybe depression in addition to all the **trippy things I perceive** in my visual field. I'm an optimist/realist and I have also been researching a lot for the past 18 months, and I know that there are things such as neurogenesis, synaptogenesis, myelinogenesis, mitochondrial biogenesis, and so on, which all occur in our amazingly complex thermoplastic brains. Long story short, my family and I have very little money as I've posted before. It's hard enough for me to afford food, let alone getting extensive labs. Luckily, my mom (or maybe grandmother) called and set up an appointment with my grandmother's doctor who I have seen before for these problems, although I said they were occipital migraines rather than HPPD because I didn't want to come clean about my substance use at that point. He's pretty intelligent - in all honesty I think I probably know more bio-hacking information than he's heard of, and I know that's conceited coming from an 18 year old without a fully developed brain who has know medical training.... but he's a typical mainstream MD. He does believe in some alternative things, though, such as doing yoga and meditation as well as getting acupuncture. I believe if I get labs or ask for referrals to neurofeedback practitioners, and receive them through him, it is covered under my mothers insurance. **So, what should I ask for?
**
I know the VAP test can be used as a proxy for many other things, but my symptoms are all neurological/psychological/arising from the brain. Are there any tests that may be covered by insurance or discounted that I should get to monitor my brain? **Any tests that can serve as a proxy?** Maybe hormones associated with my circadian cycle/signalling? **Neurotransmitters**, since I've used substances like MDMA, MDA, and anticholinergics (DXM, diphenhydramine, and what I was laced with the day I suffered greatly - embalming fluid or PCP)?

**Any referrals I should ask for** besides some testing? I was thinking of asking for a referral to someone for a Functional Neurologic Examination, maybe a neurofeedback practitioner, maybe an EDMR based psychologist, but I don't know what else to ask for. My appointment is tomorrow at 2pm (Illinois time), so hopefully someone can give me some recommendations by tomorrow evening so I can ask. I plan on going to the library first and printing Dr. Kruse CT-6 post as well as some of my forum posts, in addition to some of Dave Asprey's stuff so he doesn't just look at me and think I'm crazier than I may be ;-)

Well, I understand if this is overwhelming and not worth answering, but I hope someone gives some input. Thanks in advance for ANY help I receive!

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-30T13:40:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-tests-to-ask-for-tomorrow-for-my-hallucinogen-persisting-perceptual-disorder.8102/post-94331>

Big Papa

I'm not entirely sure what labs might be of help to you today...

### gino — 2023-08-17T17:59:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-tests-to-ask-for-tomorrow-for-my-hallucinogen-persisting-perceptual-disorder.8102/post-325196>

thoughts on LDN (low-dose naltrexone) for HPPD?

# Thread: How long to notice effects of Armour
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-to-notice-effects-of-armour.8107/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-08-30T17:45:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-to-notice-effects-of-armour.8107/post-94350>

Found a wonderful doc who takes a more holistic approach and is willing to treat my hypothyroid with Armour.

She was willing to go off the numerous amount of labs that I've had in the past 3 months and then will do her own labs in a month.

I've been on it since Tuesday - 60mg once per day.

I was wondering how long does it take to feel the positive effects?

My recent thyroid labs are as follows:

TSH - 4.69
Free T3 - 1.9
Reverse T3 - 14.5
Free T4 - .8

thanks for your input

### cantweight — 2013-08-30T17:53:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-to-notice-effects-of-armour.8107/post-94352>

I felt effects from Armour very quickly....within 2-3 days. My hair showed changes in texture and color...it darkened. I felt a slight elevation in general....maybe mood and energy....just a slight overall better feeling. And the tightness in my throat went away.

If you are feeling nothing maybe your dose is not high enough. If I remember correctly....it was back in 2006....my holistic doc started me on a dose and we kept raising it until there was some improvement in symptoms, we also tested every 6 weeks. At one point we raised it and I had a rapid heart beat...we backed it down immediately and I have been at that dose ever since. 150mg daily.

### fireforlunch — 2013-08-30T18:18:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-to-notice-effects-of-armour.8107/post-94355>

I will see her again in a few weeks so we'll see how it goes

If I remember correctly....it was back in 2006....my holistic doc started me on a dose and we kept raising it until there was some improvement in symptoms, we also tested every 6 weeks. At one point we raised it and I had a rapid heart beat...we backed it down immediately and I have been at that dose ever since. 150mg daily.

### nicld — 2013-08-30T18:27:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-long-to-notice-effects-of-armour.8107/post-94358>

For me, 90 mg of Armour did not help even though I have been on it for 18 months. Just saw a new doc and took one look at my labs and saw that my RT3 was way to high and the Free T3 way to low meaning that Armour was just not getting converted properly. Just stated taking Cytomel 2X a day to help the T3 get converted along with iodine and selenium. If you are not feeling better ask about adding that too.

Good luck

>
> For me, 90 mg of Armour did not help even though I have been on it for 18 months. Just saw a new doc and took one look at my labs and saw that my RT3 was way to high and the Free T3 way to low meaning that Armour was just not getting converted properly. Just stated taking Cytomel 2X a day to help the T3 get converted along with iodine and selenium. If you are not feeling better ask about adding that too.
>
> Good luck


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Himalyan Salt Lamps?

Just like beeswax candles they release negative ions into the air. Negative ions, â€œare known to alleviate symptoms caused by allergies, sleep disorders, migraines headaches and depression. **Negative Ions also help alleviate common indoor air pollutants such as dust, mildew, humidity, and EMFs from TVs, computers, and other electronic devices.â€￾** TRUE????

### MeghanK — 2013-08-31T03:29:00+0300

Which brand is yours?
Dr. Kruse... are these things legit?

### MeghanK — 2013-08-31T03:29:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/himalayan-salt-lamps-shield-emfs.8109/post-94420>

Which brand is yours?
Dr. Kruse... are these things legit?

### MeghanK — 2013-08-31T03:33:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/himalayan-salt-lamps-shield-emfs.8109/post-94421>

There is absolutely no salt mine to be found anywhere in this Himalayan region, in fact the closest salt mines are in Pakistan.
**
Pink "Himalayan" salt contains fluoride and heavy metals.** IS THIS TRUE????

Heating salt crystals with the minimal amount of heat coming out of a lightbulb is going to release MINIMMAL negative ions if any.

An approaching or passing thunderstorm is going to be producing significantly more negative ions than a block of salt being heated by a lightbulb, in fact just outdoors generally should have more.

There is a pretty good chance that the heating has nothing to do with releasing ions and that you may in fact only generate negative ions (minimal) in the first few minutes of plugging it in/turning it on based on the photoelectric effect.

If heating salt in fact released negative ions, anytime you add salt to a boiling pot of water, cooked food containg ANY salt, microwaved food containg ANY salt, ate salt... you'd be releasing 'tons of negative ions!!!'. However, none of these situations produce appreciable (if any) amounts of negative ions.

Your shower (moving water) is likely to produce far more negative ions than a block of salt with a lamp in the middle. **THOUGHTS???**

### MeghanK — 2013-08-31T03:34:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/himalayan-salt-lamps-shield-emfs.8109/post-94422>

# Thread: How to make coconut oil palatable?

### AnnieW — 2013-08-30T23:03:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-coconut-oil-palatable.8112/post-94394>

I love cooking with CO and the energy it gives me. It's not the taste...I love the taste. I think it's a textural thing, and psychological because it's solid fat and for some reason it grosses me out. Is there anything I can do to make it more tasty? I've tried melting it and taking it that way, which isn't horrible but it's not great either. I've tried eating it solid out of the jar and it is almost gag inducing for me. I love the taste, it's just the texture. Any suggestions?

### nicld — 2013-08-30T23:32:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-coconut-oil-palatable.8112/post-94397>

Uhmmm mix it with unsweetened flaked coconut, stevia and cocoa powder. It is AWESOME. I call it my coconut crack.

I too have tried to eat it solid and it is a little off putting. Kinda just let it sit in the mouth and melt.

### caroline — 2013-08-31T03:44:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-coconut-oil-palatable.8112/post-94426>

### endless — 2013-08-31T05:56:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-coconut-oil-palatable.8112/post-94440>

Blend it into coffee or tea...

### PrimalPam — 2013-08-31T18:56:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-coconut-oil-palatable.8112/post-94496>

Since coconut oil liquefies at room temperature, try letting it get a little warmer and just put a spoonful in your mouth.

I put it in my coffee every day and don't even notice it.

How about just putting a couple of tbsp.'s on a salad?

### Grantmeliberty — 2013-09-02T08:39:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-coconut-oil-palatable.8112/post-94664>

Melting point for coconut oil is 78 degrees F. Depends upon how warm your house is. I have been using coconut oil for about 8 years. I love it.

### nicld — 2013-09-02T17:33:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-coconut-oil-palatable.8112/post-94684>

I put the solid stuff in my morning coffee too and now that it is cooler here it did go back to solid.

# Thread: Ready to exit this hormonal storm
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ready-to-exit-this-hormonal-storm.8113/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2013-08-30T23:29:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ready-to-exit-this-hormonal-storm.8113/post-94396>

Today I got my BHRT progesterone cream, the final piece of the balancing therapy.

I applied my first dose right away. I am just a day late but the doc said to start as soon as I get it and keep going to day 28 even though cycles have been 25 day.

I feel like I have been in the middle of a horrible storm and I WANT OUT. Sleep has been getting crappy again and energy is still low. I am hoping with all of the supplements and new meds that the seas will part.

Finally, I found a doctor that listens and says that I can get better, hallelujah!!!

He said that for some reason my body is NOT using the T4 to convert the T3 that I am getting from the 90 mg in the Armour that I am taking. He reduced the Armour and added cytomel 2X a day. He also let me get some BHRT cream for the low progesterone and bad Pg/E2 ratio.

So, now I have a plan to get balanced and here's what I am doing.

Armour - 60 mg in the morning
cytomel - 12.5 mg 2X a day
Progesterone 100 mg cream - 1 gram 2x a day
Selenium - 200 mcg a day
Iodine - Iodoral 12.5 mg 1x a day
Betaine HCL- 1200 mcg 2-3X a day
Fish oil -
Vitamin D - 8000 iu's a day
B12 - 3000ug with folic acid in it 1X a day
B complex
Iron - 65 mg 1X a day
Biotin
Probiotic
Digestive Enzyme
Bitter Melon - 1X before CT

Leptin Reset with my BAB everyday and that usually means some bacon.
CT almost everyday with long cold ones on the weekends.
Try to reduce the EMF's.
Blue Blocker glasses at night.
Rewarming and grounding in the sun.
Try to NOT bundle up so much and embrace the cold.
Eat seasonally which means eating a ton from my garden.
Besides BAB, eat when hungry and stop when full.
Eat more seafood and grass fed beef and pork. Hopefully I can get my hands on some more venison too.

Have to admit that I am kinda of scared that this will not work but I am trying to have faith that it will. I am sure that there may be some more bumps in the road and more tweeking. I have also learned that I am in charge of my health.

So hopefully I am finally on the road to optimal. Hang on, this might get bumby.

Nicole

# Thread: A new biomarker for aging emerges
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-biomarker-for-aging-emerges.8114/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JanSz — 2013-08-30T23:53:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-new-biomarker-for-aging-emerges.8114/post-94398>

in e-mail from Spectracell.

[http://www.spectracell.com/media/up...act2013intjepidmetabolic-markers-of-aging.pdf](http://www.spectracell.com/media/uploaded/2/0e2369161_1376149572_2067abstract2013intjepidmetabolic-markers-of-aging.pdf)

A new biomarker for aging emerges

In this large study, blood samples from over 6000 people were collected and analyzed for 280 different metabolic markers. When the researchers correlated biomarkers to clinical aging, they found that one biomarker - **glycosylated tryphtophan (C-glyTrp)** - emerged that was particularly interesting since it had not been previously identified as a marker of aging, yet it correlated well with both chronological age and age-related traits such as lung function and bone density. The authors stated that C-glyTrp "may point to novel molecular pathways involved in aging," especially since tryptophan metabolism has previously been linked to aging in animal studies.
(International Journal of Epidemiology, July 2013)

# Thread: Light bulbs

### AnnieW — 2013-08-31T02:37:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-bulbs.8116/post-94408>

Besides candlelight and amber bulbs, are there any other light sources which could be safely used in the home? Husband has put the kibosh on amber bulbs (surprise surprise) and wants to stick with neutral/white light.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-31T03:20:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-bulbs.8116/post-94418>

I've been buying 25 watt bulbs. If a fixture has 4 bulbs in it, I replace one with a 25 watt. Then in a month or so, I replace another. DH's night vision was really bad, but it seems to be improving with better nutrition. Since it is summer, I have been more lenient with light in the evening. In winter I will be much more strict.

Also, I use 60 watt yellow bug lights at the kitchen stove and in the refrigerator. Perhaps your husband, if he doesn't cook much, won't mind that. If I could find lower-wattage bug lights I'd snap them up pronto.

### AnnieW — 2013-08-31T18:38:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-bulbs.8116/post-94494>

Thanks for the suggestions. The good thing is my husband really doesn't like light (vampire) so he doesn't like bright bulbs at all. Most in the house are 40 watt. I need to get more anyway so I will see about buying the 25 watt. I have a huge fluorescent light in my kitchen that I'd rather do without. Unfortunately we rent so I can't go changing fixtures.

My husband only comes in the kitchen to forage for snacks and ask me what's for dinner so I don't think he really cares about kitchen lighting, LOL.

awake from 1am in the morning, try to relax but can't. Don't get up though until say 6 or 7am, will I be able to get into a circadian cycle having breakfast then, as obviously can't have breakfast as soon as I wake up at that time?

Thank you for any advice

### jib — 2013-08-31T17:39:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insomnia-and-breakfast.8120/post-94481>

The sleep blog may give you some relief. Also some of the magnesium supplements can help with sleeping if you are getting proper hydration.

### caroline — 2013-09-01T02:10:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insomnia-and-breakfast.8120/post-94535>

> [fatlass said:](/goto/post?id=94466)
>
> Hi, just trying to get going with all the information. Can I check, that as I suffer from severe insomnia, I can be
> awake from 1am in the morning, try to relax but can't. Don't get up though until say 6 or 7am, will I be able to get into a circadian cycle having breakfast then, as obviously can't have breakfast as soon as I wake up at that time?
>
> Thank you for any advice

Why can't you have breakfast at that time? You need to have a BAB within 30 minutes of rising. That is the critical part. That is what helps to reset your clock.

### Lahelada — 2013-09-01T02:18:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insomnia-and-breakfast.8120/post-94537>

This is purely my take from my experience. I also used to wake up earlier than what could be considered BAB time although not quite as extreme as 1am. What I would do is the following :

Get up, do not turn on any lights, stay away from lit areas if there are any and just try and stay awake for 20 mins or if you feel sleepy earlier so much the better.

Have the BAB when NEW daylight has reached you and you are still in bed. Don't stay up though,go back to bed however often it takes until you sleep always without light.

I also second taking magnesium and an Epsom salt bath /footbath one hour before bed. They are both magnesium based but I am thinking that it is better to have both delivery methods. Good luck, I know the despair of waking up when everyone just goes to bed.

### caroline — 2013-09-01T02:35:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insomnia-and-breakfast.8120/post-94543>

Great advise Lahelda .....BTW - I didn't mean to have a BAB at 1:00am. I think you said you get up at 6:00am?

### yorkiegirl — 2013-09-06T13:09:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insomnia-and-breakfast.8120/post-95015>

Thanks Lahelda, I use magnesium oil and epsom too, this does help relaxing me for sleep on a night too,which is great, it's just the waking up early bit. If I take magnesium then it doesn't have the same effect, if you know what I mean. I try and stay in the dark until sunrise. When I wake up early, I can never fall asleep again, I never feel sleepy. I know this is going to take some doing to sort out.
Caroline - yes if I get up at 6am/or 7am, I get my breakfast within 1/2 hour, and have been doing BAB for a few weeks now.

### Lahelada — 2013-09-07T03:37:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insomnia-and-breakfast.8120/post-95107>

Do I remember reading that doing light weight work helps with sleep or am I imagining that I read this somewhere here ?
I have been waking up at 4 am every day this week and it is getting a bit old . 5 hours of sleep and maybe one extra right before I need to get up is not my idea of "nice#

### Renfred — 2013-10-11T06:55:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insomnia-and-breakfast.8120/post-97500>

Hi Yorkiegirl,
Avoid taking naps in day timing and avoid over sleeping to deal with insomnia. Exercise regularly and early morning and create your sleep schedule. Avoid alcohol, smoking and caffeine and make your bedroom comfortable so you will get better sleep.

My husbandâ€™s 10-year old nephew has been found to have low cortisol. The doctor â€˜discoveredâ€™ he was entering very early puberty last year at age 9 and theyâ€™ve been running some tests since. So far, the low cortisol was the only thing the doctor found concerning at all and heâ€™s saying it isnâ€™t that big of a deal. I donâ€™t know what the exact number is for the cortisol. They are running some additional tests as well. I think one is called a stimulation test using DHEA?? I have never heard of that before, but heâ€™s supposed to have it done next week.

My husband has been desperately trying to convince his brother to change his sonâ€™s diet, have him follow the sun for sleep/wake times and ELIMINATE video games/TV. Heâ€™s also mentioned CT, but that went over like a ton of bricks! I'd love to have them order him a Magnetico too, but that will take a lot of convincing.

After starting school this year, his nephew has really started going downhill. Heâ€™s losing weight, looks pale with dark circles under his eyes, is complaining of lots of aches and pains and is just generally weak. His anxiety has kicked way up and heâ€™s a mess over going to school. The school has recently added wifi and ALL classrooms (even kindergarten) now have laptops for each student. A lot of the homework is now required to be done on the laptops too

Because the parents have seen such a recent decline in his health theyâ€™re finally starting to get a little more open to the idea of changing their fields. It has also helped that Iâ€™ve found such great improvements in the last several months by working so hard to change my own field. Since they seem to be ready and willing to listen now I want to give them some studies â€“ some concrete data they canâ€™t refute to show them how dangerous EMF and artificial light is, but these are not science minded people at all.

Does anyone know of any easy reads / articles/studies in layman terms that I could send to them? Iâ€™m looking for something just very simple, straightforward, to the point, yet convincing. Iâ€™m really desperate to help this little boy. Itâ€™s so frustrating when you feel like you have even part of an answer for someone, but you just donâ€™t know how to get it through to them!

# Thread: Egg Yolk Color?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/egg-yolk-color.8163/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MeghanK — 2013-09-06T03:50:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/egg-yolk-color.8163/post-94949>

I have bought pasture raised eggs from 3 different "vendors" from the Farmer's Market which have organic pature raised chicken eggs... and all three have different color yolks... one has really GOLD color, one has a medium yellow color and one was a pale color... DOES THIS MATTER? Should I be concerned? One more or less nutrient dense? I know you can artificially give chickens stuff to make the yolks more "yellow" but since these are "all natural" and pasture fed do you think they are equal nutritionally????

### Jonathin — 2013-09-06T04:22:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/egg-yolk-color.8163/post-94960>

Eggs should have deep orange yolks. The whites should be very gelatinous not watery at all. There is no nutritional standard for organic grown food. Birds should have access to bugs, worms, greens, weed seeds on top of a chemical free non-gmo feed. I am sure that if you visited the 3 vendors' farms it would be evident why the eggs were the way they were. I have seen lots of organic farms. Few have any thing that resembles a real pasture.

### nonchalant — 2013-09-06T05:08:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/egg-yolk-color.8163/post-94967>

I think it is the bugs that give the egg yolks such a great taste and color. I know when the grub worms are plentiful in early spring, my chickens could care less what food I scattered for them, they would run for the bugs when they were let out in the morning. And they learned to hang around anytime we worked in the garden, hoping for a handout. I miss my chickens since the city made me give them up. And I bet the june bugs will be plentiful next year!

### Dextery — 2013-09-06T05:42:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/egg-yolk-color.8163/post-94968>

Just be aware that a farmer's market vendor that is unscrupulous can purchase grain feed that has marigold leaves that will turn the egg yolks dark orange.

[http://poultry.purinamills.com/OURPRODUCTS/Products/Layena/default.aspx](http://poultry.purinamills.com/OURPRODUCTS/Products/Layena/default.aspx)

FEATURES BENEFITS
SunFreshÂ® Recipe Natural Plant Proteins
FREE of all animal proteins and fats for healthy, happy birds.
Superior Nutrition Great Appearance and Healthy Eggs
Consistent superior nutrition for great-looking birds with wholesome eggs.
Essential Amino Acids Strong Healthy Birds with Lots of Eggs
Enhanced with lysine and methionine to give birds the healthy nutrients they need to produce plenty of tasty eggs.
**Exclusive Level of Marigold Extract For Rich Yellow Yolks
A high level of xanthophyll, a natural coloring agent derived from marigolds, produces deep yellow egg yolks.**
Key Levels of Calcium and Manganese Strong Shells with Fewer Cracks
Top levels of calcium and manganese strengthen shells to increase the number of eggs that reach the kitchen.
Proprietary Level of Vitamin A Strong Reproduction and Overall Health
A high level of Vitamin A helps birds produce plenty of healthy chicks.
Key Level of Vitamin E Supports a Healthy Immune System
A high level of Vitamin E helps birds ward off disease.
Certified Natural Your Assurance of Quality – It’s Nutrition You Can Trust
Our guarantee that this is the very best feed providing safe, natural nutrition for poultry.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-09-06T16:41:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/egg-yolk-color.8163/post-95030>

I have seen different colored yolks within the same flock of hens. Perhaps some hens eat more of a certain food. Fast growing grass and green plants like spinach contain more carotenoids. Paprika powder and pumpkins seem to contain a good amount also. Try asking the local egg producer why his/her yolks are yellow instead of orange. I suspect a diet high in corn or a feed high in corn causes yellow yolks instead of orange yolks. Just my opinion.

### Grantmeliberty — 2013-09-08T08:28:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/egg-yolk-color.8163/post-95204>

Given that most corn is GM, I have taken corn out of my chicken feed. They get scraps and rolled barley and rolled oats.
For my cats and dogs, I am using Kirkland from Costco, the lamb and rice for the dogs, because the lamb is going to be grass fed, while the chicken will undoubtedly be corn fed and not as healthy.
I have a local source for grass fed beef which is pastured across the road from me. They are selling ground beef for about 4 dollars a pound, frozen.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-09-08T09:41:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/egg-yolk-color.8163/post-95210>

Since we're on the subject of chickens, I built a home made incubator 2 weeks ago out of an insulated Igloo cooler, a PC fan from the Salvation Army Store, 2 keyless one-bulb light fixtures with 25 watt bulbs, 15-volt cell phone charger cord also bought cheap from Salvation Army Store, bi-metal thermostat switch, digital temperature/humidity sensor bought on clearance at Lowes, and a 12 foot lamp cord. I am able to keep the incubator in a range between 98F and 100F and expect the test egg to hatch in 12 days. Fingers crossed. I put 2 green eggs in the incubator a couple of days ago to continue testing. My parents hatched some Transylvania Naked Neck crosses a week ago at their home. Naked Necks have the featherless necks caused by retinoic acid receptors in the chickens skin.

Trivia... Did you know the sex of the chick is determined by the hen, not the rooster?

# Thread: Question about kerosene/propane lighting
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-kerosene-propane-lighting.8170/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-09-07T19:09:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-kerosene-propane-lighting.8170/post-95144>

I've been trying to ask about this in the current blog comments - EE3, but I can't post any comments for some reason. I see others are having problems too, so apparently it's not on my side.

Jack mentioned in the comments that kerosene lighting is bad. I'm assuming just as bad as artificial lighting via electricity. We've been using propane lanterns at night since Jack brought up the issues with "off" circadian cycles. My salivary ASI has gotten better - not perfect or optimal just yet, but definitely much better than it was.

I would assume propane would be along the same lines as kerosene though . . . does anyone else use one of these or is everyone doing candles at sunset? We started out with beeswax candles, but found it to get very expensive and the flickering flame is not very conducive to reading which is what the entire family does once the sun goes down.

We were just looking at buying another lantern, but I don't want to do that if we're inadvertently causing more harm. I know all the local Amish families use propane lanterns and internal clocks seem to be pretty spot on. Thoughts?

### Shijin13 — 2013-09-07T20:26:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-kerosene-propane-lighting.8170/post-95149>

We're using candles - I've been saving the wax and re-melting it and putting a new wick in the wax and re-using them until they're done. I also wait to buy the candles when they're on-sale.

for reading we're using red head lamps - not idea but it works...

this am I was WIDE AWAKE at 615 couldn't go back to sleep - so I got up and started my day - went out on the porch to read.

### caroline — 2013-09-08T04:51:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-kerosene-propane-lighting.8170/post-95194>

My take home was .... that started our downward spiral ....but, God knows I could be wrong. I thought it was just less optimal......

I need to get something to read by ...I really want to not turn on the TV at all....my sleep has really gone to crap over the winter and I am sure that is why ....I started to watch more tv in the evening..

### BJK77 — 2013-09-08T15:23:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-kerosene-propane-lighting.8170/post-95224>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=95194)
>
> My take home was .... that started our downward spiral ....but, God knows I could be wrong. I thought it was just less optimal......
>
> I need to get something to read by ...I really want to not turn on the TV at all....my sleep has really gone to crap over the winter and I am sure that is why ....I started to watch more tv in the evening..

"Started our downward spiral" as in got us interested in and craving more light?? I'm hoping that's what was meant. We don't turn on TV at all in the evenings. In fact, it's rare if it's on for even two hours during the week in my house. Once in a great while the kids will ask to watch a special show or we'll watch a movie together, but it's never after sunset and happens very rarely even at that.

The propane lanterns work well for reading, playing board games, etc. and I'd like to continue using them, but of course not if they're not having the intended effect. Like I said though, my salivary ASI has improved!! Hoping that means it's ok!

# Thread: Really sad.....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-sad.8173/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2013-09-07T23:28:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-sad.8173/post-95162>

for one, I had a nice post all written up and bam, hit the spell check and it disappeared and was not in the auto save

I have been feeling off since about 2006. Now that I know way more about hormones then I did back then, I wanted to see what my labs looked like back then. I got the reports yesterday and it made me really sad and kinda pissed!!!!

Back then the doc I was seeing only did the TSH and that was 1.25 and was apparently was "in spec". She never did any other thyroid testing at this time. This was just before I was diagnosed with Celiac.

Then in July of 2007 she ran the Celiac tests and it was confirmed that I had Celiac. She redid the TSH and this time is was 1.51 but again, that was the only one.

Still was not feeling well and had to switch doctors and so had more testing done in Nov 2007. This time the TSH was 1.72 plus I finally got some more thyroid testing done. My free T4 was .9 and my free T3 1.8 and marked as low. Seriously, I was out and nothing was done about it. I was feeling like crap and she did not know enough to help me, just sad. My vitamin D was also low and had cortisol testing done and at this time it was pretty good, but not for long.

I still was not feeling well so I insisted on seeing an enochronologist, I thought this was going to be it. He would find something and I can start feeling better. it was also about this time that I had been burning the candle at both end and was getting burned, it was a rough time in my life. So in January of 2008 I saw the endo doc.

This time my FT3 was 2.7 pg/ml and the FT4 was 1.1 ng/dL. The big this was that my AM cortisol was .7 and the range was 4-24 ug/dL. Really, that was not a clue that something was off. He did all of my female hormones too but I do not know what time of the cycle that these were done in but I know he never said that I needed to have them at a certain time, nor does it say on the labs. Again, disappointing. And then there was the Vitamin D, which again came in low and was marked at low on the report. He never mentioned anything about this. All he said is that all the labs were within "range" so there was nothing that he could do except write me a script for a weight loss drug since that was my main complaint. I walked out of the office WITHOUT a script. I did NOT have an eating problem, I had a hormone problem.

Like I said, reading these lab reports, just makes me sad. Sad in that how many other people are told that every thing is "in spec" but they still feel like crap and no one can help them. Still a little peeved that I have been off for over 7 years and never got the help.

But now, I am way different. I am in control of my health and educated enough and strong enough to tell a doc what I think, why and what treatment I feel would work best for me.

So, now I am spreading the word about hormones and if one does not feel well, make sure that they educate them selves and to tell a doc what test they want done and to never stop.

I am done being sick and tired and am in control of my health thanks to all that I have learned from this sight. Dr. Kruse provided my with the information to save myself and to that I owe a big thanks.

### caroline — 2013-09-08T02:43:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-sad.8173/post-95173>

What a great post! Dr. K. has always said " when you get sick and tired of being sick and tired you" will start to make progress. He also always says ....never settle......and that is precisely what you are doing! well done you

### caroline — 2013-09-08T02:49:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-sad.8173/post-95174>

I just put in another support ticket ..... when we are trying to limit our computer time - this is making it very difficult...

### nicld — 2013-09-09T00:39:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-sad.8173/post-95276>

Yep, I got tired of being sick and tired and trying so hard to not gain weight.

# Thread: Finding a home and Cell towers
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-home-and-cell-towers.8174/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AdaptogenicBeing — 2013-09-08T00:53:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-home-and-cell-towers.8174/post-95166>

Howdy! Please forgive me if this was talked about in the EMF boot camp. I didn't become active on this site until a short while ago. My question is this: For those of you who have made moves to areas with lower EMF how did you approach the locations of cell towers. My thinking is that I should move to a small town that has decent cell service to help limit the possibility that one could end up on a mountain top up the street from me. I look back at homes up the mountains around me that I used to think were very cool. Now many of them sadly enough have towers just up above them.

### fitness@home — 2013-09-08T04:03:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-home-and-cell-towers.8174/post-95189>

I have an app on my iPad that finds all towers in the area. Was amazed at how close some were to the building I work in.

We went out today just driving around in the country areas of Magnolia, Stage Coach, Conroe, Montgomery, Cut n Shoot, New Waverly, Splendora, Huntsville, etc... Way out on some of these remote roads and there would be a tower sitting in the middle of the field. Another thing I noticed is there are usually two towers relatively close to each other.

It would be a bummer to buy some property and then have a neighbor lease land to a cell company for a tower .

### caroline — 2013-09-08T04:56:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-home-and-cell-towers.8174/post-95195>

> I have an app on my iPad that finds all towers in the area. Was amazed at how close some were to the building I work in.
>
> We went out today just driving around in the country areas of Magnolia, Stage Coach, Conroe, Montgomery, Cut n Shoot, New Waverly, Splendora, Huntsville, etc... Way out on some of these remote roads and there would be a tower sitting in the middle of the field. Another thing I noticed is there are usually two towers relatively close to each other.
>
> It would be a bummer to buy some property and then have a neighbor lease land to a cell company for a tower .

I wondered about that too. In one of the webinars or Q&As \[maybe the last one\] Jack talked about there being 2500 acres beside him with nothing on it ..... but how could you be sure that it will stay like that?

### Shijin13 — 2013-09-08T17:15:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-home-and-cell-towers.8174/post-95235>

Here's what I've been using to look for optimal places to live... [http://www.antennasearch.com/](http://www.antennasearch.com/)

what's interesting there are places we've considered as possibilities that have been scratched off the list due to the region having more antennas than where we currently live... and we've got a freaking satellite dish yard/communications hub less than 2 miles from our house....

### AdaptogenicBeing — 2013-09-08T17:36:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-home-and-cell-towers.8174/post-95238>

Wow!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

I checked my old address............................120 towers within 4 mile radius!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
Everything from cell tower to the air force base!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

My new address only has 6

### fitness@home — 2013-09-08T17:52:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-home-and-cell-towers.8174/post-95240>

> [AdaptogenicBeing said:](/goto/post?id=95238)

> I checked my old address............................120 towers within 4 mile radius!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
> Everything from cell tower to the air force base!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

> My new address only has 6

### cantweight — 2013-09-08T17:55:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-a-home-and-cell-towers.8174/post-95242>

That is wild! We went from 50 big towers to 1 big and 9 unregistered. The closest one over a mile away. From 177 antennae to 20. Also over a mile away. Crazy.

We did exactly what you said AB...looked for an area with shoddy cell service and little internet. We didn't want to be disconnected...not an option with a husband in IT....but I didn't want to pull up a dozen other peoples wireless networks in my bedroom like our old house.

Noticeably better, but whether or not it is enough....I guess time will tell....

# Thread: Transition to Epi-Paleo -  Early Wakeup & BAB

### hansel89 — 2013-09-08T13:34:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transition-to-epi-paleo-early-wakeup-bab.8176/post-95219>

Dear Dr Kruse & Other Members,

I am male, 24, and a recent university graduate.
I train 6 out 7 days doing rowing, weightlifting and yoga.
I've just recently transitioned from raw vegan to epi paleo and i am currently overweight weighing around 98 kg.
Since you advise working out in sunlight i intend to switch around my training hours and work out at 1 pm instead of at 4 am upon wake up.
I have a few questions i would be delighted to have your opinion on.

\* As i wake up everyday at 4 am it is still dark outside. Shall i wait do some light activities like meditation and have my breakfast as soon as the sun rises perhaps a couple of hours after?
\* For breakfast what is your opinion on having a drink consisting of ghee, mct oil, coffee, collagen protein, pure liquid vanilla, liquid stevia? I ask this since i prefer avoiding having to digest a more solid meal as well as time, energy efficiency and preferring a liquid meal in the mornings.
\* How about raw milk as snack during the day and for pre bed having either raw honey/ collagen protein with mct oil and krill oil ?

Thank you for reading and looking forward to hearing your suggestions.

# Thread: How to make an ULF antenna from a tree.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-an-ulf-antenna-from-a-tree.8177/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### CindyB — 2013-09-08T21:38:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-an-ulf-antenna-from-a-tree.8177/post-95260>

Just came across this very interesting copy of an article from Nature magazine (1978):

www-ee.stanford.edu/~acfs/ULF%20Tree%20Potentials.pdf‎

The graphic showing the time series of measurements taken from the tree simultaneous with traditional "20,000 turn steel-cored solenoids" ULF antennas at Stanford University is a very convincing piece of evidence for using a tree antenna. The paper includes instructions on making the antenna.

Watch out "Tree of Heaven" in my yard!

Other interesting snippets from the above paper:

"Finally, although it is not clear at present what effect induced ULF electric fields may have on the growth and other vital processes in a tree, the link between these ULF electric fields and geomagnetic field fluctuations suggests that some environment-related changes in trees could also be influenced by changes in geomagnetic activity. These changes may have a natural origin (for example, the changes that occur during a solar cycle) or they may be caused by a variety of human activities (by modern d.c.-powered mass transit systems, which can produce large amplitude ULF electromagnetic fields)."

"Presman (12) noted that electromagnetic fields usually have an adverse effect on living processes."

Where (12) is: Presman, A. S. Electromagnetic Fields and Life (transl. Sinclair, F. L., ed. Brown, F. A. Fr) 155 (Plenum, New York, 1970)

### prAna303 — 2013-09-08T21:47:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-make-an-ulf-antenna-from-a-tree.8177/post-95262>

Watch the complete sets from Dan A Davidson, do not remember where he talk about trees but a nice watch anyway... Can get a little goofy.

# Thread: Alternating currents/electromagnetic effects on human biology
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alternating-currents-electromagnetic-effects-on-human-biology.8185/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### digital — 2013-09-10T02:49:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alternating-currents-electromagnetic-effects-on-human-biology.8185/post-95380>

[http://cancerres.aacrjournals.org/content/64/9/3288.full.pdf](http://cancerres.aacrjournals.org/content/64/9/3288.full.pdf)

"low-frequency pulsed electric fields have been claimed to stimulate bone growth and accelerate fracture healing (3). However, as the frequency of the electric field
increases above 1 kHz, the stimulatory effect diminishes. Under these conditions, although a greater fraction of the fields penetrates the cells, due to the parallel resistor-capacitor nature of all biological membranes, the stimulatory power greatly diminishes as the alternating cell membrane hyper-depolarization cycles are integrated such
that the net effect is nulled. At very high frequencies (i.e., above many MHz), although the integration becomes even more effective, a completely different biological effect is observed. At these frequencies tissue heating becomes dominant due to dielectric losses. This effect becomes more intense as frequency, field intensity, or tissue dissipation factor increases"

# Thread: Garcinia Cambogia supplement for health and weight loss
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/garcinia-cambogia-supplement-for-health-and-weight-loss.8209/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Claudia — 2013-09-12T20:48:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/garcinia-cambogia-supplement-for-health-and-weight-loss.8209/post-95714>

Has anyone heard of this supplement and do you think it has any place in an Epi-Paleo diet as an added supplement?

It seems to help the liver process food better, helps with neurotransmitters (improving mood), curbs appetite, and is said to be good for leptin resistance.

I am skeptical but there are excellent weight loss claims as well.

### Claudia — 2013-09-12T21:09:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/garcinia-cambogia-supplement-for-health-and-weight-loss.8209/post-95717>

I just noticed the Garcinia Cambogia is in Dr. Mercola's weight loss supplement as well. It's one of several other ingredients.

### Albert83BCN — 2013-09-12T23:57:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/garcinia-cambogia-supplement-for-health-and-weight-loss.8209/post-95731>

It's in a lot of fat-burning formulas mainly targeted to bodybuilders, I'm pretty sure I must have used it sometime long ago in one of that products, since I only have found one fat burner that I can really tell that taking it makes a difference and does not have Garcinia in it, I'd say that GC its not a big deal, at least for weight loss.

In fact, If you look at [http://examine.com/supplements/Garcinia+Cambogia/](http://examine.com/supplements/Garcinia+Cambogia/) a site that is pretty reliable and unbiased, there seems to be little evidence as well, who knows if it still might have some benefits that are worth it, Examine.com did not report not much more in the other areas as well.

# Thread: Low sodium
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-sodium.8217/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2013-09-13T03:54:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-sodium.8217/post-95751>

I have had issues with sodium levels since my early 20's chronically low to the point of passing out from it.

I crave salt and feel like I ingest quite a bit, sea, Himalayan pink, etc. I cook with it, add it to my food, eat plenty of things meat and vegetable that come from the sea.

Where does the salt go? Why can't I seem to use it? Drs never seemed to care about it more than to mention it was low when they go over labs. Perhaps it is why I am so tired? How does one raise salt levels other....just eat more? Seems its not being used properly.

This one confuses me.

### nonchalant — 2013-09-13T04:40:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-sodium.8217/post-95754>

Dr. Kruse mentioned Cerebral Salt Wasting Syndrome, could that apply?

### caroline — 2013-09-13T05:08:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-sodium.8217/post-95757>

It must be connected to something else I would think....We still aren't smart enough are we....

### cantweight — 2013-09-13T14:25:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-sodium.8217/post-95765>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=95754)
>
> Dr. Kruse mentioned Cerebral Salt Wasting Syndrome, could that apply?

I checked it out non, but I dont have the symptoms. No polyuria, or polydipsia...my BUN/creatinine is 11 so not terribly dehydrated as would be expected.

I think I am going to hit up a doc that takes insurance and have her run labs...see if i can figure this out. I dont even know what I am looking for to be honest.

Low: sodium, mag, potassium, and calcium. Great copper, zinc, and phosphorus. Generally feel good, active mind, happy, centered, addressing field through grounding most days for at least and hour, moved to good area, limit exposure, magnetico is on its way right now( for my son, but I will lay there and do my school work all day!). I just have zero energy with a mind that wants to plan and do a million things. This salt thing has been going on for 20 years. From reading I gather there may be a connection between the lows and a hypersexual state I often experience...libido overdrive. Dr Kruse touched on where that can be a negative.

Yeah Audrey....this is totally over my head. Not as simple as just add salt it seems....wish this all came to me as easily as it seems to for others here...need an optimal brain!

### yewwei.tan — 2013-09-25T14:04:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/forums-all-back-up-and-running-yeah.8222/post-96059>

Bleh, someone screwed up the url encoding code. Some special characters are not being escaped properly, and that includes parens "(" and ")".

So for example, Hemming's log has the phrase, which when linked to from the forum, leads to the URL: [http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/show...ight-(from-the-other-end-of-the-scale)/page19](http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7553-Getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-\(from-the-other-end-of-the-scale\)/page19)

The correct URL is: [http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/show...weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale/page19](http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7553-Getting-back-to-a-normal-weight-from-the-other-end-of-the-scale/page19)

Note the absence of the "(" before "from" and the ")" after "scale".

Only something the programmers behind the site can fix unfortunately.

I had been using pretty much the same few passwords everywhere (as I know a lot of you do, too), and I didn't like the idea of hackers having my passwords and a linked email address that could fall into the wrong hands.
