# Thread: Starting with Neck Deep Immersion?  Achieve the benefits without any ice?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-with-neck-deep-immersion-achieve-the-benefits-without-any-ice.8380/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### tripaleo — 2013-10-08T22:46:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-with-neck-deep-immersion-achieve-the-benefits-without-any-ice.8380/post-97305>

I'm brand new to this fascinating world and have immediately jumped into self-experimentation with diet and CT. So quick background-have been eating intelligently Paleo for about 7 weeks and officially started CT training last week. There is a "Cold Plunge" at my gym with temps in the low 50s that I have used for the past year inconsistently on my own as a form of meditation but slowly built up to 5 minutes neck deep. In the past week, I have build up to 15-20 minutes and I've started drinking about 24 oz of ice water as soon as I get in.

Based on my personal situation, is there any risk of going straight for immersion?
Am I on the right track? I am planning on slowly building up to 45 minutes to do 2-3 times per week. This is for optimal health and performance, not weight loss.

Any suggestions or other forums/blogs you can link here?
What are people doing to warm up afterward?
Can you achieve the benefits with just the 50-55 degree water submerged like this without any ice?

### freesia — 2013-10-08T23:42:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-with-neck-deep-immersion-achieve-the-benefits-without-any-ice.8380/post-97307>

Hi, yes you're getting benefits!
Sounds like you're adapting really nicely at your own pace. 5 mins in low 50's at the start is plenty. Lots of people need to start closer to 70 F.
In my experience so long as you're not getting white or numb extremities, or suffering hypothermia it's safe to proceed. Proceed slowly with increasing the time like you planned to and I don't think you can go far wrong.

How are you doing at 15 - 20 mins? Any big muscle shivering afterwards? If and when this happens it's sending a message that Winter is here  and your body needs to activate more brown fat. It's after that the non shivering thermogenesis really starts to kick in and the whole re-warming process becomes very pleasant.
Some people exercise after. For a faster adaptation you probably want to keep your skin as cool as you can for as long as you can.
....In the beginning though if it's too uncomfortable or you've pushed it too far and your body needs help to warm then I think you can use some heat judiciously. This may slow adaptation slightly i.e. it signals your body there's not such an urgent need to get it's internal warming mechanism up to speed, but in the scheme of things it won't hurt it may just take a little longer. You'll keep falling forward because you're designed to.
Good luck.

### caroline — 2013-10-09T00:11:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/starting-with-neck-deep-immersion-achieve-the-benefits-without-any-ice.8380/post-97312>

Welcome Tripaleo .....it sounds like you are doing great. Freesia definitely knows what she is doing - she has made huge progress.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Tommie Copper Swoon?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tommie-copper-swoon.8381/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gracelind — 2013-10-09T03:20:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tommie-copper-swoon.8381/post-97317>

I have an autoimmune neuromuscular condition that has targeted my thighs causing severe atrophy of quadriceps. It's not true polymyositis--working Dx is inflammatory myopathy. With significant muscle wasting my balance is now terrible, so I rely for support on a cane, furniture, walls, whatever is close to hang on to. Two weeks ago I fell, badly twisting my knee and wrenching lower back muscles, right side.

In a what could it hurt?--my knee needs support--moment I ordered a Tommie Copper knee sleeve and T-shirt. When they arrived today I put the knee sleeve on first. After about half an hour I put on the T-shirt under a long sleeve cotton T and got ready to go shopping.

Within 10 minutes I suddenly had a flush that rose from my chest into my head, making me very lightheaded. Had to hold on to the kitchen counter to keep from falling. It was intense but only lasted a few seconds. Took the T-shirt off and it hasn't recurred.

The sensation is hard to describe--like a rush of energy but not in a good way. The closest would be that feeling right before fainting with a bit of warmth.

I'm in the mountains at 6400 ft in a rural community where I live part time, so it's not needing acclimate to altitude. Most neighbors do have wifi, as do I. Usually, two or three show up when I log on to my computer. After a major remodel most of our electrical is new. Power lines aare across the street, above ground due to winter snow (regulations against digging in the winter time even for repairs). Time to check out our field here.

Slight headache now. Not an experience I want to repeat. Luckily it happened at home and not in the car while driving.

Any thoughts appreciated.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: high platelet count & anemia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-platelet-count-anemia.8390/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### rlee314 — 2013-10-10T00:43:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-platelet-count-anemia.8390/post-97386>

Aloha all,
I need some help interpreting my almost 3 year old's lab work.
CBC
Ferritin 14 ref range 36-84 per my ND ref range for child 1-5yrs is 6-24
Iron 117 ref range 45-160 per my ND ref range is 50-100
TIBC 295 ref range 228-428 per my ND ref range is 250-350
Transferin Sat 40 ref fange 20-50%
WBC 8.6 ref range 6.0-17.0 per my ND 5.0-7.5
RBC 4.09 ref range 3.5-5.5 per my ND 4.2-4.9 male
hemoglobin 11.5 ref range 11.0-14.0 per my ND 14-15 male
hematocrit 34.6 ref range 33.0-39.0 per my ND 40-48 male
MCV 84.4 ref range 70-90 per my ND 82-89.9
MCH 28.1 ref range 22-32 per my ND 28-31.9
MCHC 33.3 ref range 31-37 per my ND 32-35
RDW 13.5 ref range 11-15 per my ND <13
Platelet count 495 ref range 204-405 per my ND 150-385x1000
Neutrophils 41 ref range 10-40
Lymphs 48 ref range 40-60 per my ND 24-44%
Monocytes 7 ref range 4-10 per my ND 0-7%
Eosinophils 3 ref range 0-6 per my ND 0-3%
Basophils 1 ref range 0-2
Neutrophils absolute 3.5 ref rangeg 0.5-8.0
Lymphs absolute 4.20 ref range 2.4-10.2
Monocytes absolute 0.60 ref range 0.2-1.7
Eosinophils absolute 0.20 ref range 0-1.1
Basophils absolute 0.00 ref range 0-0.4

mag RBC 3.9
Interesting thing about mag RBC--I had this tested for him end of June and it was 5.5 different lab was used.

ND's interpretation is high platelets can be due to anemia. There may be iron conversion issues. Low b12, folate, and b6. Possible ^^ lead toxicity. the elevated WBC count reflects viral or bacterial infection. she is suspecting he is low in Vit C. is diagnosing anemia--possible causes vitamin deficiencies b12, folate, b6 or GI inflammation. She ruled out polycythemia. She notes elevated lymphs can be due to infection/parasites/inflammation.
Suggested liver as supplement and 200mg/mag I already supplement mag orally and transdermally.

HELP!! thanks!
I also posted this question in the labs forum

# Thread: Finally, some bit of good news
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-some-bit-of-good-news.8391/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2013-10-10T04:12:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finally-some-bit-of-good-news.8391/post-97404>

I saw my neuropath last week and he did another thyroid tests to see how the lower dose of Armour and the addition of Cytomel was going.

My results were

T4, free 0.8 ng/dL (0.8 - 1.8)
T3, free 3.5 pg/mL (2.3 - 4.2)
T3, total 95 ng/dL (76-181)
T3, reverse LC/MS/MS 8 ng/dL (8-25)
TSH .29 mIU/L (.40-4.50)
My ratio is now 43.8

July readings
T4, free 1.0 ng/dL (0.8 - 1.8)
T3, free 2.3 pg/mL (2.3 - 4.2)
T3, total ??? ng/dL (76-181)
T3, reverse LC/MS/MS 21 ng/dL (8-25)
TSH 1.21 mIU/L (.40 - 4.5)
My ratio was 11

Finally something is getting better. I am on 60 mg of Armour and 25 ug of Cytomel 1/2 pill 2X a day. He suggests that I drop my afternoon Cytomel down to 1/4 in the afternoon. I have not had any issues with the new med combo (thank goodness). Good idea????

I am starting to feel better. Not nearly as cold as I use to be and starting to sleep better. The only thing that is not getting better is the weight is it is so frustrating.

I will keep doing CT and Leptin reset and now that fall is setting in, reducing the carbs more. I still have some foods from my garden that I am eating (organic English cucumbers are the best). Just starting working out again and that feels really good too.

Starting to slowly using Twist DHEA since my blood tests showed that these were low and the doc likes the blood tests for DHEA better then the saliva. I think I was on too much oral DHEA and it turned to testosterone and have me horrible acne.

Next set of tests will be sometime in December and probably the female hormone panel again and see how being on Progesterone is helping. Hopefully the pants will be fitting better by then otherwise it WILL be another round of HCG in January.

Getting tired now and hoping to sleep through the night tonight. But have some more EMF reading to do.

# Thread: Symptoms of Glaucoma
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-of-glaucoma.8393/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sienaflin — 2013-10-10T05:55:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symptoms-of-glaucoma.8393/post-97409>

I am experiencing some symptoms of Glaucoma that's why I hurriedly consult to my prescribe doctor, test has been conducted and luckily I am negative in Glaucoma.
According to [http://www.freeltcquotes.com/Glaucoma.aspx](http://www.freeltcquotes.com/Glaucoma.aspx), the symptoms of glaucoma are:

-   blurred vision
-   pain in or around the eye
-   seeing circles around lights
-   red eyes
-   eeling sick to your stomach and vomiting

Thanks God for Everything

# Thread: Gravity.....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gravity.8395/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-10-10T10:20:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gravity.8395/post-97417>

I saw this movie today......WOW..I loved this movie so much!

You already know that I am crazy about George Clooney ....And I love Sandra Bullock.

I thought this movie was so life affirming .....and certainly increases your heart rate!

I think this movie is amazing ....What do y'all think?

The last couple of 3D movies I have seen.....I get no 3D affect at all - really strange.
I have very little vision in one eye (lazy eye). I have seen 3D at Disney and at IMAX and it was fine
It seems like something has changed.....

### prAna303 — 2013-10-10T12:36:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gravity.8395/post-97423>

> The last couple of 3D movies I have seen.....I get no 3D affect at all - really strange.
> I have very little vision in one eye (lazy eye). I have seen 3D at Disney and at IMAX and it was fine
> It seems like something has changed.....

ItÂ´s because there is some big differences between "normal" 3D and IMAX...

IMAX will use dual projectors of highest quality.
IMAX have curved screen.
IMAX have glasses for large format.
IMAX often have uncompressed sound.

One can say that everything IMAX is more high end with better tools, that is also why a movie is so much more expensive.

From my point of view, i hate 3D because the art of making film is gone. No one used 3D and writes scripts for 3D, they take some scenes and make a lot of noice, effects. It is ok if you like Transformers but nasty if you would like some more details.

Will give it a try some day, the movie, i mean...

### veelansu — 2013-10-10T18:04:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gravity.8395/post-97434>

I haven't seen it yet, but will this weekend. I love space movies of any kind. When I was younger I wanted to be an astronaut. When I was in graduate school I even sent in an application to NASA. I guess that's why I work for a defense contractor now that works with space programs although I'm not on any of those programs. But I guess the proximity to it satisfies my childhood dreams. . Will let you know what I think of the movie after the weekend.

### prAna303 — 2013-10-10T18:17:17+0300

[http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0272152/?ref\_=sr\_1](http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0272152/?ref_=sr_1)

Quote:
Dr. Mark Powell: Uh, how is it that being a visitor from space, that you, uh, you look so much like me or, or anyone else from Earth?

Prot: Why is a soap bubble round?

Dr. Mark Powell: "Why is a soap bubble round?"

Prot: You know, for an educated person, Mark, you repeat things quite a bit. Are you aware of that? A soap bubble is round because it is the most energy-efficient configuration. Similarly, on your planet I look like you. On K-PAX I look like a K-PAXian.

Dr. Mark Powell: Prot, why did you want to come to our planet?

Prot: Well, I've been here many times before. But what brought me here first? I don't know. Pure curiosity, I guess. I'd never been to a Class BA-3 planet before.

Dr. Mark Powell: Class BA-3?

Prot: Early stage of evolution. Future uncertain.

# Thread: Pain Relievers . . . . Tooth Problems!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pain-relievers-tooth-problems.8398/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-10-10T21:27:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pain-relievers-tooth-problems.8398/post-97453>

I can't remember the last time I took any kind of OTC pain reliever, but I'm absolutely desperate now and willing to consider any suggestions. What do you all do? I've got a massive toothache. I've been posting a few questions to Jack on the tooth blog, but I've got to get this straightened out once and for all. In the meantime, I need to get some sleep and not have my mouth swollen and constantly throbbing. I used to use clove oil, but it's not cutting it AT ALL. I took a cold bath, put ice packs on my carotid arteries, took tons of FCLO, trying everything I can think of with no relief at all.

Here's my situation:

A few years ago I had several amalgams replaced. At least two of those teeth have been progressively bothering me more and more - very sensitive. The one is so sensitive it hurts just when I breathe in cool air or drink room temp water. I have a major bite problem too and initially my new dentist and I both thought the sensitivity was due to the bite problem because I was grinding my teeth at night.

I got fitted for a retainer last week and the teeth have gotten so much worse - presumably due to the extra pressure on them. I was worried there may be some kind of infection (they're both on the left side one directly on the top of the other) because I tend to get a lot of mucus in my throat and when the teeth are bothering me I feel like there's drainage inside my ear. I also can't eat ANY carbs (and by carbs I'm talking veggies like broccoli/cabbage, etc.). They get even more sensitive then.

My new dentist thinks this may all be due to inflammation from poor fillings. He explained that a lot of dentists have a high failure rate with composites because they treat them like amalgams when they're putting them in and they're two completely different materials and need to be handled differently. He has only ever worked with composites and says he has a very high success rate with his patients.

He does not do root canals. What he's suggesting is that I have him try to re-fill them. Given that there are two prominent ones and at least 1-2 other that are a little sore that I recently had redone all by the same dentist (actually his hygeniest did the filling), it makes me wonder if they weren't poorly done. I can't possibly need root canals in 3-4 teeth, right?! My only concern is that if he finds they are too big (and most are very deep already) to fill, he would extract them and Jack has said he thinks root canals are preferable over extractions.

Any advice or suggestions from anyone who has "been there" or had a similar experience or just wants to comment?

DH and I leave for Florida next Saturday for an anniversary trip and I really don't want to be in this much pain on vacation! I've resolved to at least stop wearing the retainer until after I figured this out. The bite problem is now the least of my worries.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

HDL is good
LDL is low
Trigs are High, but not bad, definitely want them lower
Bun/Creatine ratio is 26 - you're dehydrated

DHEA-S is low
Estradiol is high
SHBG is high (makes sense)
Your testosterone is high

you're missing Progesterone, Free T4, and Reverse T3

Its interesting about how high your Estradiol and Testosterone are - considering how low your LDL is. Are you on any BHRT?

Working on the dehydration-just got a magnetico and switched to the Primo water. Just upgraded to gold membership so I will check out the webinar with the lab info. Trying to convince family we need to nix the wi-fi.

Thanks for the advice. I take Vitamin K2 with D3 and eat seafood at least 5 times a week. I also take co-Q10, but it is ubiquinone. I will have labs done again in November so I can see if anything has changed.

It is really worthwhile to go back and watch old consults with Dr. K. I have a number of times - but not for quite awhile now.

You know how we get so much more out of a blog a year later ... the same thing happens if you have recorded your consults. There are subtle things that I had forgotten - or - I guess ...I appreciate now what he was really saying.......and goodness knows - he talks in pretty simple terms so we understand.
I guess I now appreciate all the nuances of what he was saying way back then - same message ...but I am seeing/hearing it thru different lenses.

This was quite a revelation this morning ......I had remembered Dr. K.talking to me about the pineal gland
and fake lite ...and there it all was - over a year ago!

# Thread: limiting computer time....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/limiting-computer-time.8438/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-10-14T09:27:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/limiting-computer-time.8438/post-97771>

Any tips to limiting computer time? I am getting worse not better at this......I love Mr. Google!

This is so vital for me ...and I am doing a lousy job at making this happen!

Any tips or tricks ........

Is the morning way better to be on line? or no difference?

It is so hard - I love being able to research anything and everything......

I wear my blueblockers. How much better is it to have a separate key board?

### prAna303 — 2013-10-14T10:25:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/limiting-computer-time.8438/post-97773>

> Is the morning way better to be on line? or no difference?

\- I use computers mostly daytime. The reason for me is that i feel that i have a chance to recover some of the damage made.

> It is so hard - I love being able to research anything and everything......

\- My take, do the research within yourself, think thru every question you have and then go look on the web. This way you can clear ut what is most important for you right now. The big problem with the web is that you spend hours just following traces and end up with ten more questions for each one you had. Look for answers in the "real" world also, sometimes i find clues in a cafÃ©, at a trail in the woods and so on.

> I wear my blueblockers. How much better is it to have a separate key board?

\- Always perfer a real keyboard. I also use a classic CRT monitor if i sit for a longer time, then i feel that i must go away much sooner. Stand up and do your computer time, makes you want to move much earlier.

I give you this little lecture by one of the greatest creators of today...

\[video=youtube;3QsTnxUTx7U\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3QsTnxUTx7U\[/video\]

### caroline — 2013-10-14T11:40:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/limiting-computer-time.8438/post-97777>

Thanks Peter .... you are so right - you google one thing and an hour later you are still at it!

Standing up is a great idea - I often start out that way but I eventually pull a stool over and make myself comfortable. I will stop doing that.

I will get a keyboard tomorrow.......what is a CRT monitor?

### Optimalbound — 2013-10-14T13:56:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/limiting-computer-time.8438/post-97782>

A CRT (Cathode Ray Tube) monitor is what we all used before the LCD screens came along, caroline.

You know, like these:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

From what I've read, CRTs emit quite a bit more EMF than LCDs, in general. But for all I know, there might be issues with different frequencies of the emission, that is perhaps more significant than the amount of emitted energy.

Not sure I follow your logic here, Peter. Are you saying that you are deliberately making your computing environment more harmful, in order to force yourself to spend less time one it?

My own plan is to make my own computer setup as low in emf as possible AND spend less time on it.

### prAna303 — 2013-10-14T13:57:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/limiting-computer-time.8438/post-97783>

> what is a CRT monitor?

The old fashioned ones...

### prAna303 — 2013-10-14T14:00:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/limiting-computer-time.8438/post-97784>

> Are you saying that you are deliberately making your computing environment more harmful, in order to force yourself to spend less time one it?

Not exactly but you have a point in that one... The thing with CRT is that after a while you feel the radiation on your skin and then inside you. I feel it after 30 minutes and then i stop. If you work with a LED or TFT you do not get the exact reaction, i do not. Others might do but we all are different.

### prAna303 — 2013-10-14T14:02:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/limiting-computer-time.8438/post-97785>

And of course, the quality of CRT in terms of colors and temperature are so much better .

I have always felt that TFT or LED look like crap.

If it is not beautifully presented i try to avoid it, a golden rule for me.

### Optimalbound — 2013-10-14T14:15:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/limiting-computer-time.8438/post-97786>

Thanks, Peter.

I am actually a bit surprised that there are still CRTs in working order, out there.

I remember in particular one CRT screen I had to use for a while, well over a decade ago. More than 15 minutes in front of it and I would start to feel nauseous.

I suspect older PS/2 type keyboards and mice generate less emf than the modern USB types, although I my guess would be that both types emit a lot less emf than the screens. On the other hand, they are closer to your body. I'll have a go at measuring what I have in my parts bin and report back if I find anything interesting.

### Lahelada — 2013-10-14T14:39:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/limiting-computer-time.8438/post-97788>

If you wear glasses no brand smart phones from China are great. They never do what is asked and you put them away after 10mins in disgust.
I would try for mornings only and definitely not connect on the beach. It might help with your sleep problems.

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-14T16:22:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/limiting-computer-time.8438/post-97794>

Like Peter I try to use the computer only during the day I also have a standing desk Which I love. I actually have found I'm much more productive in general with a standing desk - I get more done.

I have both a PC laptop and a macbook pro - I prefer the macbook pro w/the apple keyboard vs the PC w/the wired key board . I think crazy as it is. I think the PC actually is worse than the mac. just by feel.

I usually only hop on and off the mac on the weekends - but this weekend I had to write a report (got the results late on Friday) and the report is due tomorrow. I spent about 7 hrs on saturday & sunday each on the PC - and had very little re-connection time... I just felt off - even though I loaded up on water, and seafood (I had dinner with Misty - she was in DC for the weekend!) but just felt blah over all..

getting Chaos and Havoc ready to go spend the day hiking local nature centers so we can all re-connect properly (phone will not be with us)

I have found since returning to working form home - I use my cell phone much less. I use voip, skype, and google phone to make calls.

### BJK77 — 2013-10-14T21:37:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/limiting-computer-time.8438/post-97839>

I too limit my computer time to during the day only. On my 3 work days in the office, I have little choice but to use it quite a bit throughout the day, but I make sure to get up and take walks outside every hour just to get away from it for a bit.

When I'm working at home the other 2 days I sit outside as much as possible on my laptop.

Regardless of the day, I'm never on the computer past 6 PM. I just won't allow myself to do it! I also try to make myself earth for as many hours as I've been on the computer for the days I work at home and on the weekends. Unfortunately it's not possible on my office days.

I used to be quite addicted to Mr. Google too, but when you know better you do better . . . I think we've all heard that somewhere, right?! Just like I haven't had a banana in 2 years now, I will not allow myself to get too comfy with my computer these days. It's all about priorities and doing what you know is best.

# Thread: like a furnace!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-a-furnace.8445/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Julie5 — 2013-10-14T21:55:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-a-furnace.8445/post-97844>

I have been on the leptin RX for 2 weeks now. Not perfectly, but I'm getting there. After about a week into it I noticed that I am hot all the time (except my toes, which are always cold). Will this calm down in time? will my toes warm up? It's making sleep more of an issue. I wake up too hot during the night, but uncovering makes my arms cold.

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-15T00:18:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-a-furnace.8445/post-97853>

Great signs as you make forward progress....

### nicld — 2013-10-15T04:06:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-a-furnace.8445/post-97876>

> [Julie5 said:](/goto/post?id=97844)
>
> I have been on the leptin RX for 2 weeks now. Not perfectly, but I'm getting there. After about a week into it I noticed that I am hot all the time (except my toes, which are always cold). Will this calm down in time? will my toes warm up? It's making sleep more of an issue. I wake up too hot during the night, but uncovering makes my arms cold.

Good question. I too notice that I sometimes wake up in the middle of the night too hot and uncovering the arms gets me too cold. My feet are the first to get hot and I try to keep them uncovered too.

### cinnamon — 2013-10-15T04:23:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-a-furnace.8445/post-97878>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=97876)
>
> Good question. I too notice that I sometimes wake up in the middle of the night too hot and uncovering the arms gets me too cold. My feet are the first to get hot and I try to keep them uncovered too.

I seem to remember that Dr Kruse sleeps naked in the freezing cold. Some of us mere mortals are not fully cold adapted yet and still use sheets and blankets.

I find it helps to fold the bottom corner of the blanket up on an angle before I get in. If I get hot, I can get my feet to a cool spot easily by stretching down to that corner.

### Julie5 — 2013-10-15T18:58:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-a-furnace.8445/post-97910>

I"ve had to do that too: fold the blanket back a little. I have always had a problem with the backs of my arms being too cold, so years ago I took a pair of sock and cut off the tops, making arm warmers that I used just like leg warmers. I may have to do that again since even though the chest, back and shoulders are sweltering, the arms still get cold and prevent better sleep.

### Julie5 — 2013-10-15T18:59:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-a-furnace.8445/post-97911>

WEll, sleeping naked is out of the question for me if i want my hubby to leave me alone long enough to sleep!!!!

### cinnamon — 2013-10-15T19:28:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-a-furnace.8445/post-97912>

Julie5,

I was thinking about your question some more this morning. I remembered that I had a few weeks in December of last year where I was getting very hot an hour or two after getting into bed. I mean like sweating hot and very uncomfortable - very disturbing to sleep. At first, I thought maybe the effects of CT were turning on my "inner furnace" and that seemed like a good thing. I thought it would just be a transitional thing, like detox, and I figured I would push past it.

It was just before the Christmas holidays when I had been cleaning the house all day and was just exhausted from not having had a good night's sleep in three weeks. My DH ordered a pizza for dinner and, despite my misgivings, I gave in and ate that for dinner. Now, normally, in those days, I was having a vitamin K2 capsule at about 8 pm. I suppose because I was already eating bad food (and much too late) I skipped the K2 that night. When I went to bed, I slept much better - not waking in a sweat at all. The next day, I took the K2 as usual and the sweating happened right on schedule. I stopped taking the K2 at that point and over the next few days, my sleep got better and better and the overheating stopped.

Now, about the K2. I had been successfully using K2 for about 6 months already at that point. The difference was that I had been using the MK-4 formulation (menatrenone) and in early December, I hadn't been able to find MK-4 and had gotten a brand that used MK-7 (menaquinone) from natto beans. Shortly after I started using the MK-7, I noticed that I had a pain in one of my big toes when walking. I thought it was odd but figured it was temporary. The other thing I found was that after two or three weeks, I developed an odd mild pain in my mid-section. Difficult to describe - not strong, but definitely a feeling that something wasn't quite right. A couple of days before the pizza, I woke to find a small round bruise, about the diameter of a pencil, just to the left of my belly button. I was just about to give in and go see about this as it was disturbing. It was going to be right after the holidays, you know? And then the pizza thing happened. And I discovered the night sweats, the painful toe and the pain in the mid-section all completely disappeared when I stopped taking the MK-7. The symptoms were not linked in my mind until they disappeared together.

Now, this is all just my n=1. I know many people use MK-7 with no problems. For me, I only use MK-4 now.

I thought maybe my story might help you to consider if something else might be going on. Has anything changed for you before the night sweats started other than doing the leptin reset? Are you trying any new supplements?

cheers,
cinnamon

### BJK77 — 2013-10-15T22:50:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-a-furnace.8445/post-97918>

Hi Julie,

I had this happen to me too, but for me it was several months after starting the leptin rx. It would come and go for me and finally this Spring it seemed like it was gone for good . . . until recently when it's starting all over again

I have no idea why. I'm trying to look at things I'm doing differently, but I just can't pinpoint the cause. I lowered my thyroid meds (NT) based on labs and I did start eating a bit higher fat due to the changing season, but I've since backed down on the fat and no change in the overheating. I believe I do have a tooth infection now, so I don't know if that could be contributing . . . I imagine it could even be a combination of things going on.

In the past I used to think the overheating had to be hormonal, but part of me questions whether or not it isn't sometimes related to a detox effect.

### Julie5 — 2013-10-16T08:24:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-a-furnace.8445/post-97970>

Hmmm....interesting. I hadn't considered that it might be one of my supplements. When I went to see my doc after my labs, he prescribed a whole bunch of supps that I hadn't taken before. Maybe I will try eliminating one at a time for a few days each and see if the heat cools off. When my hubs says I'm hot, I don't want it to be because I'm sweating!

# Thread: Oyster question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-question.8446/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Julie5 — 2013-10-14T21:59:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-question.8446/post-97845>

So I really do love crab, shrimp, lobster and most fish, but I just can't get my head or tongue around oysters. At least the canned ones I got last week made me nauseous. Will frozen ones go mushy with thawing? What about scallops? Are they any better? Any ideas for preparing them so they are palatable?

### caroline — 2013-10-14T23:44:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-question.8446/post-97849>

I can't eat the canned ones either.... to me they are vile....I struggle with oysters now - I was okay before but now I just throw up...

### caroline — 2013-10-14T23:46:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-question.8446/post-97850>

A little off topic .... I just discovered frozen fresh Tuna at a new fish shop......it is so awesome for sashimi and so much cheaper and it was flash frozen when it was very fresh.

### JNV — 2013-10-15T00:11:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-question.8446/post-97852>

Would love to know why Dr. Kruse does not feel like Mercury is an issue by eating too much fish? Does anyone know? Also, how do I get RO water? He mentioned it is something I can buy, but I went to the store today and they didn't have it in the Boston area. Is it at a Whole Food perhaps?

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-15T00:27:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-question.8446/post-97854>

Smoked canned oysters - are my low budget oyster buy. Of course I grew up eating oysters for saturday and sunday lunch/dinner as we'd watch football. Even my kids love them... my family learned quite quickly if you want smoked oysters when Chaos and Havoc are in town - don't let them know you're eating them b/c they'll scarff them all up.

however, we try to hit up fresh oysters as often as possible. the local international market charges 5 for $2.00 which is reasonable. we shuck them ourselves, top w/horseraddish and lemon juice and down the hatch - just swallow them. no chewing. that's really the key.

### caroline — 2013-10-15T00:33:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-question.8446/post-97856>

It isn't the oysters I object to .... it is that artificial smoke thing going on ..... ugh...

### Julie5 — 2013-10-15T02:28:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-question.8446/post-97861>

**RO water**

" Also, how do I get RO water? He mentioned it is something I can buy, but I went to the store today and they didn't have it in the Boston area. Is it at a Whole Food perhaps?"

RO water was half our business for 35 years. RO stands for reverse osmosis. Basically, it's bottled water. If the bottle doesn't say reverse osmosis on it, then it isn't. You can pay for a bottled water service to deliver 5 gallon bottles to your home along with renting (or you can purchase) as water cooler. Most of the chubs of water you buy are either mineral water, meaning it hasn't been thru RO, but be sure to look b/c sometimes the minerals are added back in after going thru RO.

### nicld — 2013-10-15T04:10:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-question.8446/post-97877>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=97854)
>
> Smoked canned oysters - are my low budget oyster buy. Of course I grew up eating oysters for saturday and sunday lunch/dinner as we'd watch football. Even my kids love them... my family learned quite quickly if you want smoked oysters when Chaos and Havoc are in town - don't let them know you're eating them b/c they'll scarff them all up.
>
> however, we try to hit up fresh oysters as often as possible. the local international market charges 5 for $2.00 which is reasonable. we shuck them ourselves, top w/horseraddish and lemon juice and down the hatch - just swallow them. no chewing. that's really the key.

Wow, that is a great price. I don't even know where I can go in my area to get oysters and I know that they would not be that cheap. When I was out in Boston this summer I did notice that fresh seafood was way cheaper than beef so seafood the whole time was enjoyed.

# Thread: Some beginning labwork
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-beginning-labwork.8451/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rick — 2013-10-15T16:19:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-beginning-labwork.8451/post-97901>

Hello all,

Today I see Dana Cohen in Manhattan from the PPN but I wanted to ask if anyone on JK forum might have some insights as well: thyroid panel shows T3 at 4.2 out of 4.4 and T3 is 34 times > rT3 after scaling the units. HS-CRP is .71 mg/l but Pg/E2 ratio is fine but progesterone is quite low and the ratio is only good because estradiol is extremely low. Cortisol diurnal profile is generally the right shape curve and even tho' midnight is relatively elevated, all four samples are on the edge or below the shaded areas for low med & high (noon is way low).

I was expecting pregnenolone steal because T levels are low with long term hypogonadism but the low cortisol would suggest adrenal fatigue?

Yes Dr. Cohen will order a boatload of more labs to pin this down, but any comments or suggestions from here would be much appreciated!

Thanks, Rick H.

# Thread: Magnets or metal while CTing?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnets-or-metal-while-cting.8463/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Julie5 — 2013-10-16T08:35:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnets-or-metal-while-cting.8463/post-97971>

I thought I saw a post that talked about using magnets or metal while CTing. What's that all about? Or did I misread? Countdown to CT: 3 days.

### nicld — 2013-10-16T16:57:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnets-or-metal-while-cting.8463/post-97999>

I have seen that people bring a magnet with them and it might help with shivers. I bring my #250 pull one and set it beside my tub and I think it helps. I am pretty sure that it helps with my rewarming since it makes the legs tingle. That's just my N=1 though.

Good luck with the CT. Are you just going to get in a full tub or slowly fill? Now I just jump in and it still takes the breath away for about 2 minutes, then all good

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-16T16:58:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnets-or-metal-while-cting.8463/post-98001>

You don't want to use metal when CTing - it can cause issues for your skin etc.

but magnets can be beneficial...

### Julie5 — 2013-10-16T20:25:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnets-or-metal-while-cting.8463/post-98023>

Good luck with the CT. Are you just going to get in a full tub or slowly fill? Now I just jump in and it still takes the breath away for about 2 minutes, then all good \[/QUOTE\]

In a swimming situation I just jump in, but the first couple of times I think i need to go the slow route. Do you use a compression shirt?

### nicld — 2013-10-16T21:47:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnets-or-metal-while-cting.8463/post-98033>

No, I don't use a compression shirt. I might think about that this winter though. Most of my fat is in my thighs and booty so as long as they are under I think I am doing good plus I have a deep tub.

Oooh swimming would be fun. I might suggest getting neoprene shoes and gloves to wear. I know when my hands and feet get too cold, i'm done but that is my n=1.

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-16T21:53:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnets-or-metal-while-cting.8463/post-98036>

FWIW I started w/a compression shirt and by mid winter i was getting in the tank au nautral...

### nicld — 2013-10-16T23:10:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnets-or-metal-while-cting.8463/post-98038>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=98036)
>
> FWIW I started w/a compression shirt and by mid winter i was getting in the tank au nautral...

What is the benefit of a compression shirt? I have never used one since my water goes up to my tatas.

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-16T23:16:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnets-or-metal-while-cting.8463/post-98041>

IIRC it helps you tolerate the cold as you progress into the full body CT.

I started out CTing using compression gear and ice packs, then slowly transitioned to CT in ICE Water w/ compression gear, then to bikini, then au natural.

# Thread: eating while on the road?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-while-on-the-road.8465/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Julie5 — 2013-10-16T15:50:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-while-on-the-road.8465/post-97993>

I have always been a stay at home sort of person, but a couple of times a month I have to be gone for the whole day. I can't afford to always eat in restaurants, and in fact don't like restaurant food. Since being on the leptin RX, I am having a hard time figuring out what is really portable food without a microwave. Cold hamburger patties and fish don't float my boat.

While I was gone yesterday, I was in Costco and knew I had to have something since it was late afternoon and I hadn't eaten since 6:30 am. I ended up with cheese, tomatoes and macadamia nuts. It wasn't a rootbeer float, at least, but it wasn't optimal.

So what do the rest of you do when you are on the road or away from your kitchen?

### nicld — 2013-10-16T16:37:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-while-on-the-road.8465/post-97997>

When I travel for work or pleasure I always make some beef jerkey with. If you can find some good tuna packets, those might do in a pinch. I have a supplier that when I visit them, I always have to bring my own food since they have never once asked if anyone has food allergies. Either I brought my own food or I would not eat. Kinda why when I travel for work I make sure to find a hotel that has a hot breakfast with eggs so i can at least eat a good filling breakfast. And when I am in a real pinch I will get an Arby's roast beef with no bun.

### Gracelind — 2013-10-16T21:44:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-while-on-the-road.8465/post-98032>

About restaurant breakfast I've heard scrambled may be from dried egg powder or may have dried milk powder added. Better to order poached or sunny side up/over easy.

I'm sure it depends on the place--fastfood vs upscale hotel.

### caroline — 2013-10-16T23:28:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-while-on-the-road.8465/post-98043>

Do you take along coconut oil - for BPC? cans of sardines, coconut manna, dark chocolate, brazil nuts, nori sheets to roll up some canned salmon........

### SeaHorse — 2013-10-17T00:21:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-while-on-the-road.8465/post-98052>

You might check out some earlier threads on this forum....there were a lot of good ideas tossed around.

### nonchalant — 2013-10-17T02:11:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-while-on-the-road.8465/post-98060>

And there's always pork jerkey.

### BJK77 — 2013-10-17T16:47:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-while-on-the-road.8465/post-98156>

For day trips, I always make sure to have a can of sardines and/or smoked oysters with me. I check out the area where I'm traveling ahead of time too to see if there are any decent sushi restaurants - sashimi always works and most places will let me add a side of avocado. If there's a Whole Foods Market where you're traveling, they'll shuck fresh oysters for you or steam shrimp. Epic bars are another option. They taste fantastic and I always keep one in my purse for 'emergencies.'

For overnight travel I only stay in hotels with kitchens so I can pack a cooler and prepare my own food.

I travel often for work and I finally feel like I'm getting the hang of this!

### Julie5 — 2013-10-18T07:04:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-while-on-the-road.8465/post-98230>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=98043)
>
> Do you take along coconut oil - for BPC? cans of sardines, coconut manna, dark chocolate, brazil nuts, nori sheets to roll up some canned salmon........

Does the seaweed taste fishy? I'll gag if it does. And I'm not sure I can get past the sardine heads {shudder}. What is coconut manna?

# Thread: Robert O. Becker
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-o-becker.8469/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Awainer1 — 2013-10-16T19:16:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-o-becker.8469/post-98018>

Has anyone here read any of his books? I read Cross Currents and now I'm reading The Body Electric. This one is amazing and really explains a lot of the science that Jack talks. For example it explains how he discovered semiconduction using the Hall Effect. I never thought id be a science person but I am turning into one. He also talks about how much dogma he had to face in doing his work. I have a lot of time to read at work so I'm making sure to go slow, underline and really understand it. Fascinating stuff.

### Awainer1 — 2013-10-16T21:37:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-o-becker.8469/post-98030>

"When Becker found piezoelectric current in bone, we had a game changer moment in biology. \*The analogy I like to use is think about when Neil Armstrong landed on the moon and made his famous statement about mankind, and then visualize or make believe that no one \*bothered watching TV that day in 1969."

Lol and now this statement from emf 8 makes a lot more sense to me.

### NeilBB — 2013-10-16T21:49:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-o-becker.8469/post-98035>

You can walk into any Barnes and Noble today and buy a copy of "The Body Electric." It received lots of good reviews and is well respected. Yet the details of the science contained in there are: (1) crucial, (2) revolutionary, and (3) still completely ignored by relevant medical research communities. Becker's descriptions of the politics of the process of scientific research and its funding (also contained in the book) help explain why this is so.

Scientific paradigms have a life of their own and are incredibly difficult and slow to change. Truth is less important than "measured orderly stability" in social institutions. That is a fact, whether people want to face it or not. Revolutions in science are often socially, politically, and economically destabilizing and are therefore resisted with great fury by those in power. We should expect this.

I don't think Becker really set out to be a revolutionary. It seems that he was just a very intelligent and determined doctor/scientist who was unflinchingly committed to finding the truth, wherever it led him. This comes through in the book. I work in academic medicine and I can tell you that those qualities are exceedingly rare in organized medical science today. Any researcher exhibiting such qualities and proposing a view that fundamentally conflicted with orthodoxy would either be drummed out of the field or would be aggressively "mentored" into studying "more appropriate" scientific topics that fit within the current mold.

That is also why this brilliant and revolutionary website is run by a man who is in private medical practice and not formally affiliated with academic medicine. It just has to be that way today.

### Awainer1 — 2013-10-16T21:53:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/robert-o-becker.8469/post-98037>

Wow I just read all of the quantum bone blog after reading the first 130 pgs of "The Body Electric" and so many lightbulbs are turning on for me. Got to keep reading!

### SeaHorse — 2013-10-17T14:58:46+0300

> [beach bunny said:](/goto/post?id=98081)

> Sorry to sidetrack off post topic, but if you don't mind me asking why jaw surgery Sea Horse? I only ask b/c I've had serious jaw issues too.

Here's the quick story: Jaw injury when I was 19...lots of TMJ issues with no real understanding of how the disc was degenerating over the next thirty or so years. After early menopause (alarm bells ringing now) the disc and the bone started going south very fast. Two years ago I couldn't really open my mouth fully and was in a lot of pain and I started losing my bite due to the joint changing shape and hinging differently. So since starting JK protocols I've stopped my bone degeneration (YAY!!!) and now I'm in braces to straighten teeth and prepare for surgery next summer. I will be CTing like crazy all winter, minding the light, hormone support, doing all the good stuff and hopefully surgery and it's attending awfulness of emf, invasive metal, etc. etc....won't set me back too far. I really need to be able to chew properly and I can't which could be behind some of the gut issues I've had too (JK lightbulb moment in a consult)....I can't regain this ability without surgery and I hope I will be much healthier next summer to undergo surgery...there you have it, how about you bb?

So since starting JK protocols I've stopped my bone degeneration (YAY!!!) and now I'm in braces to straighten teeth and prepare for surgery next summer. Thank you for sharing Sea Horse, I can definitely sympathize! Iâ€™ve always had anterior open bite which wasnâ€™t a problem. I did have braces in Uni to straighten my teeth. Then an idiot dentist over-tightened my jaw during a cleaning and it LOCKED. Pain was relentless, I couldnâ€™t open my mouth, chew or speak for 6 months. My mother pureed all my meals. All the specialists said surgery only option but I met a TMJ specialist who saved my life. He used combination of techniques, mouth guard, cranial retainer, osteo work, chiro work, etc to get me functional. I was doing better than I had in years and just waiting to have funds to straighten teeth again. Then was in huge car crash, smashed my head, back to mouth guard etc etc. Once again surgeons are saying surgery the only way.... But my TMJ and lyme specialist are telling me it is last resort and theyâ€™re confident that I can avoid breaking bone.
Have you consulted with any specialists that work with jaw joints/discs etc without invasive methods? Or are they saying that it is your only option at this point? It really sounds like youâ€™re keeping on top of everything which is awesome!
Also recently has been mentioned to me that each of our teeth is connected to a particular brain circuit. And the unfortunate impact of braces can be to shut some of those down and that can lead to a host of other issues. So maybe when youâ€™re teeth are nice and straight you can have that checked out just to make sure those pathways are firing optimally
Sorry to ramble on, but as you can see this is such a huge part of my life haha

### Julie5 — 2013-10-16T20:21:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-wanted.8471/post-98022>

Shortly after finding Jack Kruse, i had bunch of labs done because of my serious insomnia. I'm now on a couple dozen supplements and a few prescriptions, all designed to boost my bod and ability to sleep. I sleep on a Nikken mag mattress, use amber goggles night and morning, shut off the power to our bedroom/bath, and will start CT this week. i have gone from sleeping just 4 hours a night to 5. That's progress, I suppose.

Will my sleep continue to improve and will I eventually be able to get off the sleep scripts if I keep up the leptin rx and ct?
I am desperate for good sleep!

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-16T20:29:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-wanted.8471/post-98025>

yes your sleep will improve as you balance Light, Water and Magnetism, by paying attention to the natural circadian cycles. It may take slow weaning to get off the sleep medications, though...

### nicld — 2013-10-16T21:41:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-wanted.8471/post-98031>

Yep, it should. Mine has but it has taken time and I am hoping that since winter is coming it will get better my making sure I minimize artifical night time light. I think it is working for the hubby too but he has never really had a problem falling asleep. Hang in there, it will get better.

### Hemming — 2013-10-16T21:48:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleep-wanted.8471/post-98034>

Your sleep will be better. But more so, you'll be sleepy in the evening when it gets dark and wake up in the morning when the sun rises basically following the circadian cycle.

# Thread: Quantum Physics, Quantum Biology, Quantum Thoughts & Quantum Actions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-physics-quantum-biology-quantum-thoughts-quantum-actions.8472/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shijin13 — 2013-10-16T21:12:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-physics-quantum-biology-quantum-thoughts-quantum-actions.8472/post-98028>

In searching for something to add back into my daily meditations, I've been re-reading a book that profoundly impacted me when I first read it back in 2006.

The book is called Mindlight -by Sliver Raven Wolf.

the following is an excerpt "...we have the capability of "clicking" matter on and off, thereby creating our own reality, and that everyone has this natural ability."

"One... means that in the still point of the void, that place that is not a place, in a time that is not a time, the zero point of the totality of the dynamics... there are none - which is one- united in nothing, yet bonded in everything, wholeness in the moment where there is no past nor present."

"Once we agree with the four parts of our selves "in here" (mental, physical, emotional, spiritual) then there is no barrier to "out there". We move smoothly into the power of the One, without any visible effort, without resistance, without emotional attachments, without negativity."

I found this is resonating where Jack is taking us. Thoughts can change your DNA. Where you live matters, how the field impacts us. But we haven't discussed how our if our thoughts can change DNA, our thoughts can also change the field. our DNA is part of the quantum world, so is the field (both native and non-native) So that means my thoughts can change it. As Silver Raven Wolf discusses how to make reality change through the thought of something either being present or not being present. She's changing her reality by saying the following about an item " its there, its not there, its there"
I find this fascinating. I plan on seeing if I can show a change in the field just by changing the reality with thought and measure with meters.

the book also inspired me to write down and begin practicing the following meditation which I chant whenever I find my self stressed about stuff to the point, it interrupts what I should be focused on in that given moment

"I am a Quantum Being
my thoughts, actions & beliefs
support my Quantumness
I am physically, emotionally,
mentally, & spiritually
in alignment with my
Quantum Being
My thoughts can change DNA
My thoughts influence my environment
My thoughts influence positive outcomes
for me & those around me
As a Quantum Being, I
control the present as I see it
and the future as I shape it.
The Energy of the Universe
is mine to use."

### caroline — 2013-10-16T23:32:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantum-physics-quantum-biology-quantum-thoughts-quantum-actions.8472/post-98044>

I love that Gretchen .... thanks for sharing.....I am desperate to make changes ....I am borrowing this!

### Rick — 2013-10-17T03:46:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/beginning-labwork.8475/post-98068>

Hello all,

Oct 15th I saw Dana Cohen in Manhattan from the PPN but I wanted to ask if anyone on JK forum might have some insights as well: thyroid panel shows T3 at 4.2 out of 4.4 and T3 is 34 times > rT3 after scaling the units. HS-CRP is .71 mg/l but Pg/E2 ratio is fine but progesterone is quite low and the ratio is only good because estradiol is extremely low. Cortisol diurnal profile is generally the right shape curve and even tho' midnight is relatively elevated, all four samples are on the edge or below the shaded areas for low med & high (noon is way low, below shaded curve).

I was expecting pregnenolone steal because T levels are low with long term hypogonadism but the low cortisol would suggest adrenal fatigue?

Yes Dr. Cohen will order a boatload of more labs to pin this down, but any comments or suggestions from here would be much appreciated!

Thanks, Rick H.

PS, any hackers out there, just let me know if u want to see all the exact numbers, I have the files from LEF.org and ZRT labs on request...

# Thread: Raw Animal Food. Detox or Bad Reaction?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-animal-food-detox-or-bad-reaction.8482/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2013-10-17T12:31:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-animal-food-detox-or-bad-reaction.8482/post-98122>

Hi guys,

I have been eating bits of raw mackerel recently with other food and it went okay. However Tuesday night I ate full raw meal for tea (makerel and sprats) and the following morning I ate the same meal again. Then roughly 5hrs later I felt sick, weak, and stronger heart palpitations than usual. It felt exactly like the "detox" I felt when fasting.
By the time tea came around I was sort of repulsed by the idea of eating raw, and was still feeling sick. I ate some cooked stuff anyway to get my calories up. Felt like crap rest of the night and awoke this morning with even less appitiate than usual and a headache. Forced myself to eat the same raw fish again but this time I did not enjoy the taste at all.

Is the raw fish causing detox? Has anyone else experienced detox from switching to raw foods? Did it happen this quick?

Is it the sudden lack of carbs? Doubtful, I have gone from moderate carb to zero before with no such symptoms.

Had the fish contained parasites/bacteria?

Sean.

### caroline — 2013-10-18T00:06:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-animal-food-detox-or-bad-reaction.8482/post-98180>

I think parasites can be a problem with fish.....I hope someone else can help you out here......

### Inger — 2013-10-18T10:21:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-animal-food-detox-or-bad-reaction.8482/post-98250>

Sean I can only speak for myself and my own experiences, but I never had such issues, and believe me I have been eating raw fish with even parasites in it! But, I do believe when the field is altered as Jack says... and the gut is not healthy... there can be issues. Say, if you have too little stomach acid, that cannot kill the parasites, and you get sick.

Try some wormwood, drink some very strong tea! Maybe 2 cups of it. Make your raw fish with lots of raw organic grated ginger and lemon juice / apple cider vinegar. These things helps with parasite issues. Eat safe fish/seafood with no parasites, or freeze them for minimum 2 weeks, very cold.

If your gut and stomach acid are not healthy you might need to be careful. And, the most important thing, avoid EMF! Ground a lot barefeet in the grass! Put icepacks on your abdomen, do CT, no fake light etc. Those all counts a lot when healing

### seanb4 — 2013-10-18T17:34:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-animal-food-detox-or-bad-reaction.8482/post-98273>

Okay so thursday night I was feeling less shit and had some cooked salmon. I only had a bit because my appatiet had dropped. Total calories for the day was probably <1000.

This morning was no longer feeling sick, bloating was slightly reduced (this was probably thanks to less volume going in my stomach) but still feeling even more tired than usual. I ate a whole mackerel minus head and tail this morning. Wasn't exactly tasty but not off putting either. It had been in the fridge for 3days. Anyone know roughly how many calories that is? Protein to fat ratio?
I don't feel nauseous at the moment so I think whatever it was has passed.

I will probably just have a bit of cooked salmon for tea again.
Does anyone know how much raw food affects your appetite? I have heard it lowers it somewhat which could be very useful to me.

@Inger
Yeah I was also thinking that raw paleo is probably fine for people with healthy guts but risky otherwise. I don't think I need wormwood as whatever happened seems to have passed. I am interested in using apple cider vinegar however at the moment I just want to see what results I get from just raw, ACV could mess up the experiment. Also does this not denature the proteins or something? Is this bad or not?

Would love a copy of your "cook" book, Thanks!

Wednesday I ate raw marrow, raw beef and raw beef fat. I felt nauseus and sick again but that seemed to go away however I am also experiencing a pain in my stomach that feels like something is stuck in there which hasn't gone away yet. It comes and goes usually when I am eating or when drinking water and at night and is quite painful.

It is difficult to describe like a pin point heaviness just 1 or 2 inches below my sternum. It woke me up last night/morning, was slightly present after breakfast, came back after I drunk some water, and is now present (I just ate).

Is this a parasite/bacteria?

Anyone else had something similar?

Could it be the fibre from the large chunks of beef I was bolting down is still stuck in my stomach? I was chewing the raw beef however it wasn't doing anything so I ended up swallow large chucks.

Any help is appreciated.

### seanb4 — 2013-10-27T20:12:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-animal-food-detox-or-bad-reaction.8482/post-99166>

### Inger — 2013-10-27T20:46:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raw-animal-food-detox-or-bad-reaction.8482/post-99171>

my labs
OK, here they are! I posted them in my journal and was told this would be the better place to put them.

Cholesterol ratio 2.8 (total 232, HDL 84, LDL 130)

Cardiac panel:
homocysteine 14.5
CRP hightly sens 2.44

T4 total 5.99

FSH 89
T3 total 85.7
DHEA 78.6

Testosterone 41.61
Free T .62
% Free T 1.5
Pregnenolone 32
Vit A .81
Retinyl Palitate .02
IGF-1 154
Estrone 93
Growth HOrmone .2
Reverse T3 9.6
Estradiol, ultra 18
2 MTHFR mutations
Melatonin and Cortisol: I don't have those numbers in front of me, but doc says they are very low.

deplin 15 mg
nattokinase 1500, 6 caps daily
5-HTP 50 mg, 5 nightly
B-6 100 mg , 2 nightly
baby aspirin
zinc 30, 1 daily
multi vitamin
omega 3 500 mg, 1 daily
cal-mag citramate, 3nightly
resveratrol, 250 mg, 1 daily

prescriptions:

pramipexole 1 mg for restless legs
quetiapine 100 mg for sleep
amytriptoline 10 mg, 1-5 for sleep

# Thread: deplin question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deplin-question.8491/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Julie5 — 2013-10-18T06:57:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deplin-question.8491/post-98225>

Is anyone else on deplin? Is there a cheaper yet still effective alternative?

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-10-18T14:33:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deplin-question.8491/post-98263>

Deplin is the Rx version of methyl folate. Google Kendal Stewart, MD for more info on methyl folate.

Methyl folate is available as an OTC supplement. Most versions are 400 to 800mcg whereas Rx version is 7.5 or 15mg.

Kendal Stewart promotes a supplement version at 5mg per cap. Watch this video; it briefly compares Rx to supplement version. [http://vimeo.com/55965134](http://vimeo.com/55965134)

If you Google, there are several other companies that offer supplement version in higher strength than 800mcg (0.8mg).

### Julie5 — 2013-10-18T16:15:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deplin-question.8491/post-98269>

Thanks! I'll give a read/listen.

### Julie5 — 2013-11-03T17:50:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ee-7.8502/post-99722>

I re-read this blog last night and printed out the stuff on menopause to give to my doc. Also, and not least, to remind ME of why I'm doing this. There are a variety of health issues, but it comes right down to feeling good and living life to the fullest.

I have a hard time with aspects of evolution. But strictly from a biologic and scientific point of view, it's fascinating and enlightening. OUr oldest son read some of what I forwarded to him because he has health issues that he shouldn't have at all at his age. When he read the stuff he said, "I feel like I have been lied to all my life." He's going in this week for the MTHFR and celiac tests. Where it goes from there, I don't know.

Jack must have an amazing ability to learn and absorb information, and he has a way of putting it all together in a way that is (mostly) digestible with a little effort on my part. What a blessing to have found him and his blog!!!

### caroline — 2013-11-03T23:33:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ee-7.8502/post-99736>

Julie - thank goodness your son is listening to you and reading....hopefully he can turn his titanic around.

Have you read the book that Jack recommended? Going somewhere: the truth about a life in science ....by Andrew Marino

It is something everyone should read .... and it is a pretty easy read. It is available on kindle for $10.00

I think this book would really blow your son's mind..... and yours too!

### caroline — 2013-11-03T23:36:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ee-7.8502/post-99737>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=99736)

# Thread: Great Film on Grounding
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-film-on-grounding.8505/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2013-10-19T16:29:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-film-on-grounding.8505/post-98330>

Grounded

> The movie tells the true tale of an Alaskan wildlife filmmakerâ€™s persistent curiosity and quest to test the claims of what appears to be an outrageously simple and â€œtoo good to be trueâ€ healing concept â€“ physical, bare skin contact with the Earth â€“ which may have been known by civilizations throughout history. New research has started to confirm the unexpected, that the surface of the Earth has healing power, like a gigantic treatment table. He introduces this concept to his fellow townsmen in Haines, Alaska (population 1,700), many of whom suffer from major pain or disabling conditions. Starting with his own pain relief, he witnesses and films a surprising and miraculous healing in the town generated by simply grounding people to the Earth, that is, reconnecting them to the Earthâ€™s healing energy. Even an orphaned moose calf named â€œKarenâ€ participates in the healing.
>
> The news of the townâ€™s response draws the attention of Apollo astronauts who walked on the moon, as well as doctors and scientists.
>
> This highly unusual and eye-opening movie premieres on many media platforms, beginning in October, 2013. It will change the way you look at the Earth beneath your feet! [http://www.kroschelfilms.com/ground-archive](http://www.kroschelfilms.com/ground-archive)

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jgwF0tpioTU](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jgwF0tpioTU)

### caroline — 2013-10-20T00:00:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/great-film-on-grounding.8505/post-98364>

My back got a little strained, but the CT has kept everything working and given me the energy to even contemplate this task....very happy to have this part of my life back...and very happy for you Gretchen...exercise is wonderful when it lines up this way.

Since 2 years I do not eat carbs at all, no fruits and from time to time I had dieting very strong combined with hard workout.
I am 1,69 m and my weight is 64 kg. ItÂ´s not to much, but it is for my opinion too much!!! I am hungry all day Long, but not so strong in the evening. I read the Epi - paleo book from Dr. Kruse and recognize I made many many mistakes.
Know I am here hope finding an answer because my great aim is to drop all the hormones and get back to my normal health like it was befor 5 years, befor taking the hormones. If I had startet the Paleo diet in former times - bevor I went to the docotor I am sure that I never had those Problems like yet! :-(

# Thread: Loose skin?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loose-skin.8527/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AnnieW — 2013-10-22T16:16:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loose-skin.8527/post-98571>

Hey guys, I'm just curious what you are doing for loose skin. There's a ton of stuff on the internet but it's difficult for me to sort through the fluff.

I have a body brush for dry brushing and I use coconut oil on my skin. When I can work up to CT I will do that. I'm having problems with urticaria right now so I'm trying to balance my omegas with diet.

Are there any supplements I should be taking to help with loose skin? Is there anything topical I should be doing besides the body brushing and CO? I'm sure I will have some loose skin because I've been overweight my whole life; I'm just trying to minimize it as best I can.

Thanks!

### Optimalbound — 2013-10-22T20:09:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loose-skin.8527/post-98584>

Here are some thoughts on the matter, that Dr. Kruse posted over at Mark's Daily Apple:

[http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread32345-83.html#post505009](http://www.marksdailyapple.com/forum/thread32345-83.html#post505009)

> Some one asked about loose skin post weight loss too......here are my thoughts.
> after my patients lose their weight we use a few things to tighten skin over about 12 months.....high dose Vit K2 and teprenone (Oral and topical), caprylic acid (oral and topical) and high dose dietary palm and coconut oil. It does amazing things to adipocytes and the skin once the weight is gone.
>
> We also add a couple of high dose supplements too....NAC and R-lipoic acid and resveratrol. This is not for a leptin reset.......this is when you are looking to lose the last twenty pounds after your weight loss.

### AnnieW — 2013-10-22T21:03:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loose-skin.8527/post-98590>

Thanks so much Optimalbound for posting that. I wonder if Dr. K will chime in and say how much of this stuff we should take, if it's even available to us without an Rx.

I'm just wondering what's considered "high dose" and where can we get topical caprylic acid and teprenone? I've seen the caplets of caprylic acid but not like a bottle of it or anything.

### caroline — 2013-10-23T00:08:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loose-skin.8527/post-98616>

C/T is invaluable for this. Have you started? It doesn't happen over nite - but it does happen....

### AnnieW — 2013-10-23T02:00:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loose-skin.8527/post-98654>

I've tried but because of the urticaria I have to work on the omegas before I can try again.

### caroline — 2013-10-23T02:14:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loose-skin.8527/post-98662>

> [AnnieW said:](/goto/post?id=98654)
>
> I've tried but because of the urticaria I have to work on the omegas before I can try again.

Are you doing the face dunks? Can you do C/T with warmer water - just to start and get used to it?

### AnnieW — 2013-10-23T05:24:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loose-skin.8527/post-98697>

I can do face dunks ok and I even take cool showers, which is something I've done for a while now. It's just the ice on skin which causes me problems.

### caroline — 2013-10-23T05:43:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loose-skin.8527/post-98699>

> [AnnieW said:](/goto/post?id=98697)
>
> I can do face dunks ok and I even take cool showers, which is something I've done for a while now. It's just the ice on skin which causes me problems.

Annie - how cold is your tap water? You shouldn't put ice on your skin starting out. Put a compression shirt on - a really tight one - it compresses fat cells .. And then put a bag of ice on top... Baby steps....you will get there - I am living proof ...and a lot of others here as well!

Changes definitely don't happen over nite ....but if you are committed and work the protocols as Jack outlines them - you will get there. Everyone gets impatient and it happens differently for everyone. What works great for me may not be so great for you or will work differently.

Everyone needs to find their own n=1

So - to be clear ....when you put ice on your skin - you get uticaria? That has probably happened to all of us!

Please read the reset guides again .... It is a lot to take in at first .....and we all want the fat gone yesterday..

### AnnieW — 2013-10-23T15:37:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loose-skin.8527/post-98720>

I don't have a compression shirt but I had another shirt that I tried, also I put a towel on over it. My skin got itchy and then I had slight bumps.

The tap water is pretty cold especially in the morning. I have never taken the temperature but I know it's not close to freezing, so it's probably not cold enough. I've always enjoyed taking cooler showers because I get hot easily and it keeps me from sweating. Also I have rosacea and the cooler temps help with preventing flare ups too.

Thanks Caroline for your continued support. The last thing I want is to look like a freak with a ton of loose skin when this weight is gone.

### caroline — 2013-10-23T23:18:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loose-skin.8527/post-98764>

Hi Annie..... Try a cold bath - you should do great. I just have a pool thermometer that I got for a couple of bucks.
I freeze bags of ice and then put them in the bath. Your tap water should be getting pretty cold now?

With C/T your body comp will change. Just a thought .....you aren't using those blue ice packs are you? I can't remember what they are called. They aren't good on your skin. Get bags of ice at a store if you have to or just freeze water in some large baggies.

Please let us know how you go.....

### caroline — 2013-10-23T23:21:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loose-skin.8527/post-98766>

Annie - have you read Dr. K.'s story? He lost 160lb and has no loose skin.....at least that is his story and he is sticking to it.....LOL.....

### AnnieW — 2013-10-24T02:13:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loose-skin.8527/post-98796>

That's pretty impressive! I'd still like to know the doses of those supplements he listed on MDA. I'm trying everything I can lol!

### caroline — 2013-10-24T04:11:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/loose-skin.8527/post-98810>

Honestly Annie ....C/T changes your body comp big time. And try doing a few kettle bell swings..
