# Thread: NBA stars find less playing time is more healthy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nba-stars-find-less-playing-time-is-more-healthy.8535/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ATL_Paleo — 2013-10-23T00:37:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nba-stars-find-less-playing-time-is-more-healthy.8535/post-98623>

[http://bleacherreport.com/articles/...tars-play-less-and-for-good-reason-heres-why?](http://bleacherreport.com/articles/1815776-in-todays-nba-even-the-stars-play-less-and-for-good-reason-heres-why?)

Interesting article on NBA players. A number of the protocols from the blog at being used by some athletes.
\------------------------------
More Rest, Better Recovery

One of the significant effects of deeper benches: allowing players to recover better game to game.

"Not only are you developing your team using your entire roster, but you're also giving your guys more time to rest," Cartwright said. "It's not a sprint, it's a marathon, and the healthier that you can stay during the course of a season, the better."

Clark added, "The less minutes played by these athletes will certainly increase their durability, resilience and longevity because it enhances recovery overall. When you play less minutes, you get less beat up."

Fratello said that like in Major League Baseball, where managers are more protective of their pitchers and use them in fewer innings, NBA coaches have made a more conscious effort to limit their players' minutes. He said that's even happening overseas, where he's been the head coach of the Ukraine national basketball team for the past three summers.

"Now, people are afraid to play guys 42, 44 minutes a night," he said. "It's just part of the times, part of the expanding of the staffs. There are that many more people involved, and you have that many more opinions. And they think this guy will last longer in the NBA if we cut down his minutes to 34, 36 minutes a night."

Along with the minutes decrease, players have the luxury of recuperating with advanced technology and treatment methods. Those aim to help players maintain their minutesâ€”even while lowerâ€”more consistently throughout the entire season. Cartwright recalled that when he played for the Bulls and New York Knicks in the 1980s and '90s, a player who hurt his knee would just apply some ice, take some aspirin and then put on a brace for the following game.

He said that's all "completely different today."

"We're smarter in how we train and play," Cartwright said. "It's so unbelievable with what's happening now with how players are being taken care of after the game with the trainers. You've got Jacuzzis, cold tubs, masseuses, oxygen tanks for the guys and cryotherapy that freezes the players. We didn't have any of that when I came into the league."

I just took my laptop in to get fixed. It seemed like the track pad is going. It was replaced 3 months ago.

The tech just called me and told me I had spilled something on it......something orange and sticky....like soda...

I have not spilled anything on it and certainly not soda! I am the only one that uses it. My grandsons haven't been over in ages and I still would not have soda here.

What else could it possibly be? Is it possible when they fixed it someone spilled something? It never seemed 100% when I got it back ...but nothing significant....I said nothing.

# Thread: Steel-reinforced concrete homes and EMF?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steel-reinforced-concrete-homes-and-emf.8542/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HoneyChild — 2013-10-23T19:10:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/steel-reinforced-concrete-homes-and-emf.8542/post-98745>

One of the houses we've been thinking of buying is built using the Dorran method of construction. Basically after the war thousands of these homes were built using "non-traditional construction" using concrete reinforced with steel. In the '80s problems were found with these homes including the corroding of the steel. As a result they're difficult to get mortgages on and so can be bought dirt cheap.

I'm just a bit worried that maybe the steel will cause problems with EMFs? It's in a very rural area, with only a few houses around. The mobile phone reception is really shitty (at least it was a few years ago when I was there) and I doubt there's any broadband.

# Thread: What happens when you take away a fat burners fat?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-happens-when-you-take-away-a-fat-burners-fat.8544/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2013-10-24T00:00:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-happens-when-you-take-away-a-fat-burners-fat.8544/post-98775>

Hypothetically, say there is this woman that does leptin rx, transitions to high fat....feels good on it, better than usual, but gains weight...like 13 lbs in a short period of time.

So she decides to drop dairy for the heck of it which takes her butter and HWC...no more bpc huge decrease in fat intake

Initially she feels quite good...drops that 13 lbs....fast and now she is exhausted....a crazy kind of exhausted.

Driving a car causes her arms to burn and feel like lead like she just did 50 dead lifts.

10 minutes of kicking a soccer ball around with her son and her thighs are burning and calves are seizing up.

Carrying a load of laundry upstairs leaves her breathless.

What gives???

### Jude — 2013-10-24T00:06:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-happens-when-you-take-away-a-fat-burners-fat.8544/post-98777>

I'm guessing here...dehydration would be a big factor I think...

### caroline — 2013-10-24T00:26:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-happens-when-you-take-away-a-fat-burners-fat.8544/post-98779>

I can see where dairy could be a big factor to eliminate for you.....can you up the co? Add in ghee, tallow etc?
I am eating more fat than ever ....and my weight is more stable and even down a little. I know that isn't what you want to hear. I have started buying GF rib eye steaks with a lot of fat on them.
Dr. K. Talked about eating pancetta today ....70% fat I think he said. What about pork belly?

Can you post what you are eating?

### caroline — 2013-10-24T00:29:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-happens-when-you-take-away-a-fat-burners-fat.8544/post-98780>

### caroline — 2013-10-24T00:31:25+0300

### cantweight — 2013-10-24T03:44:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-happens-when-you-take-away-a-fat-burners-fat.8544/post-98807>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=98779)
>
> I can see where dairy could be a big factor to eliminate for you.....can you up the co? Add in ghee, tallow etc?
> I am eating more fat than ever ....and my weight is more stable and even down a little. I know that isn't what you want to hear. I have started buying GF rib eye steaks with a lot of fat on them.
> Dr. K. Talked about eating pancetta today ....70% fat I think he said. What about pork belly?
>
> Can you post what you are eating?

I use ghee, lard, coconut oil. Coconut milk in my coffee.....I can add the fat back in....I was just gaining weight after upping fat, I cant afford to gain any weight. Up 30lbs in the last year. With fluctuations of 10-15 lbs several times.

I am eating fish, shrimp, beef, bacon, chicken. Spinach, broccoli, cauliflower, squash, sweet potato. I have had some apple and pomegranate lately too. Nuts sometimes,

Energy is frighteningly low. So much muscle fatigue. Weird.

### Inger — 2013-10-24T10:40:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-happens-when-you-take-away-a-fat-burners-fat.8544/post-98820>

I would ditch all carbs.... no veggies either, and NO fruit.

Jack also says, you need to go keto until you are healthy! the only thing I would do is wild greens, they are very healing. Whatever you find where you live. Make a smoothie or eat them just plain, a little will do because they are very powerful. And of course, seaweeds (but they are wild greens too..lol)

I would not do coconut milk either, do raw coconut oil instead. You can make a delicious coffee with just lots of raw coconut oil, a pinch of salt and then mix it into a nice fatty BPC. You do not need butter for that... or you could add ghee maybe. I always let my coffee cool a bit or put some ice cold water in it before I put the expensive raw coconut oil into it.. I just do not want stuff to get destroyed by heat

I never _ever_ eat chicken..... I would suggest not to do that. Also organic chickens get crap food like wheat and soy, believe me I have seen their feed as we have chickens too. And there is nothing for them to eat in colder months, even if they are pastured. Pastured does not mean they do not get added feed.

Go more raw, it always energizes me....

There is a lot to improve still, which is great, because that means there is much hope you will get way more energy soon  And remember, cutting out dairy does not mean you have to go lower in fat... there are so much nice fat out there to eat still! Do you ever eat lamb? Lambs fat is amazing too... and pretty easy to get, pastured.

I never forget the one member on the Zerocarbforum, his wife had Lyme and very bad. She was so sick all her hair fell out and she tried everything the doctors suggested. Nothing helped. She just got sicker and sicker. Then she started to eat Zero carb with her husband, they eat only pemmican, which is very high fat. Whole family does it together. And she got healthy again , she even got pregnant even ig her docs told her she will never be able to have kids, and have now to amazing and healthy boys, that have never eaten carbs, they just eat pemmican..lol You can see them on youtube if you google his youtube username, chachajoe.

If one would add raw fish and seaweed to that diet it would be the most perfect diet me thinks! Pemmican is also always made of raw, dried meat. Maybe you could try that?

I think... the more ill we are the stricter we have to go with our foods. We might need to go super strict. Super crazy strict! That is what I have had to do myself and I do not regret it
Because it is too sad to do not have energy when you go on in your life... I so wish I could give you some of mine, I have so much (almost like a fire burning inside..)! (((hugs)))

You can do it, CW! It is all going to get better

# Thread: Low T And Cortisol
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-t-and-cortisol.8545/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### dheat — 2013-10-24T02:59:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-t-and-cortisol.8545/post-98803>

Can low testosterone in a 50 year old man (251) cause low cortisol in the morning and afternoon?Thanks.

### Huck — 2013-10-24T15:08:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-t-and-cortisol.8545/post-98836>

> [dheat said:](/goto/post?id=98803)
>
> Can low testosterone in a 50 year old man (251) cause low cortisol in the morning and afternoon?Thanks.

Send jansz a pm and ask him to address your question. He will probably post a chart that shows how steroid hormones flow from ldl. If you have low testosterone and low cortisol, you probably also have low pregnenalone, high ldl, low dhea, and on and on. Be prepared to do lots of testing.

When you add 1 hormone, it is a precursor to other hormones, so you have to know what effect adding a hormone has both to that hormone and to hormones it is a precursor to. Again, that's where Jans chart will help.

I never had much success trying to treat a single hormone. I could raise that hormone, but always seemed to have a negative effect on some other hormone. I ended up taking a longer term approach. What has worked best for me is getting light cycles right, eating epi-paleo, ct, reducing emf, grounding, AND getting lots of direct sun on as much of my body as possible and walking.

### dheat — 2013-10-25T04:42:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/low-t-and-cortisol.8545/post-98973>

Thanks for the info Huck.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-10-25T04:49:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-just-came-in-26-year-old-male.8557/post-98976>

HS-CRP ideally is below 0.5 .... so your number is okay, but not optimal .... closer to zero, the better.

Free T3 and TSH numbers indicate possible hypothryoidism.

Triglycerides are pretty high ... how clean is your diet? Eating any grains?

Uric acid is high ... once again raises questions about your diet.

am cortisol is good or a bit high ... appears like you have good energy upon waking in the morning

Testosterone looks low ... I assume that is total .... free T is a more meaningful number

Finally .... anytime you post labs, please include the lab ranges .... your labs appear to be from Labcorp ... but as Dr. K says "I hate guessing".

# Thread: Catalyst Television in Oz debunking saturated far/cholesterol role in heart disease
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/catalyst-television-in-oz-debunking-saturated-far-cholesterol-role-in-heart-disease.8561/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-10-25T22:40:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/catalyst-television-in-oz-debunking-saturated-far-cholesterol-role-in-heart-disease.8561/post-99043>

[http://vimeo.com/77730824](http://vimeo.com/77730824) or this link if the vimeo won't play. [http://www.proteinpower.com/drmike/lipid-hypothesis/5257/](http://www.proteinpower.com/drmike/lipid-hypothesis/5257/) or this one [http://www.abc.net.au/catalyst/heartofthematter/](http://www.abc.net.au/catalyst/heartofthematter/)

An excellent Television broadcast to the masses regarding cholesterol & heart disease. Australian Health Foundation is under attack from the grassroots.

This written by Dr Michael Eades who was interviewed along with other physicians.

"Is cholesterol the villain it is made out to be by the medical authorities? Does too much cholesterol cause heart disease? Will it shorten your life? Should you avoid saturated fats to stay free from heart disease? The answers to these questions are not the ones most people would anticipate. The video below provides a different perspective.

About three months ago I got an email from a television producer in Australia informing me she was going to be in the US and would like to interview me. I consented, and the resultant TV show was just shown last night in Oz.

It may be the first time a major television network devoted a serious show to debunking the lipid hypothesis. As readers of the blog are all too aware, most take the opposite approach. Let’s hope this one starts a trend. It would be nice to have an investigative reporter go after a lot of these mainstream lipid guys. As you will see, they look pretty uncomfortable under intensive questioning. Unlike those of us on this side of the debate, they’re not used to being questioned as to the validity of their views."

### caroline — 2013-10-26T00:49:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/catalyst-television-in-oz-debunking-saturated-far-cholesterol-role-in-heart-disease.8561/post-99057>

### Nick — 2013-10-28T09:30:33+0200

# Thread: Mold/biotoxin issues and vacations....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mold-biotoxin-issues-and-vacations.8567/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hope — 2013-10-26T23:33:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mold-biotoxin-issues-and-vacations.8567/post-99096>

So I started studying mold issues, just got the big book and I'm so glad I found it. I don't know why I didn't get it a year ago when I first read the Lyme/Leptin blog but oh well.

But now I am wondering how in the world to go on vacations to new places. I was hoping DH and I could go down to FL or anywhere south for some sun etc. but now I'm not sure, especially after I just realized that our Outer Banks condo that we rented was likely having mold issues. Not only did it smell kinda bad (not too bad only because it was freshly renovated and painted, ugh and I feel like i smelled that more) but I had the worst sleep I have ever had, ever. Like unspeakably bad. I think I now know why. Another place I stayed at for a weekend in Asheville was so moldy, I nearly left the place but was too tired to start to look for anything different in between meetings, ugh.

So I wonder, anyone on here travel with mold issues? How do you figure out where to stay.....ugh. New buildings I guess. I have always been sensitive to smells (in fact I joke I have perfect smell, not perfect vision, as i seem to smell so much other people don't, like even the yucky chemical laundry detergent the neighbors use when their clothes are in the drier).

Would love for someone to respond.....anyone have a good place they can recommend, like even a name of a condo/hotel? There's gotta be some resource...and if not maybe I should start a website. "Lodge here if you have mold issues. Our indoor air is mold-free".

### SeaHorse — 2013-10-27T03:23:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mold-biotoxin-issues-and-vacations.8567/post-99135>

Hope...could you look at some eco- or green travel sites....a lot of them are into natural materials and would be low chemical....but I don't know if that would help you in moldy humid places.

### Hope — 2013-10-27T13:09:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mold-biotoxin-issues-and-vacations.8567/post-99144>

Thanks SeaHorse, yeah I was thinking that too. I did find Hyatt has a pure collection of rooms or something like that. I found eco travel in Costa Rica, but it's extremely pricey from what I have seen so far. That's the hard part. I have NONE of those issues anywhere I have ever stayed in Germany, Austria, Switzerland and even Northern Italy where I used to go. And Luxemburg. And Iceland. Even Poland.

For some reason it's much harder here. I will definitely also ask Dr Shoemaker. HE has a great chapter in his book about DC buildings and how many are WDB.....even the Pentagon may have issues, which made me think of Gretchen. He also shared about clients where the spouse has to 'travel ahead' to find a place that has no mold bf the sick spouse travels down as the potential exposure is so bad. Wow. Also mentioned how mold grows less in certain non-modern buildings....it's all so fascinating to me. It's so amazing. (And I used to hate biology in high school, though that was because we had a drill sergeant for a teacher. The few things I remember are Mendel's laws and the terrible things that happen when you're a drug addict or abort a child. It was good education to not do that.)

Strangely that is what I tried when I was on the Western coast of FL in Cedar Key this year, and all the hotels/condos I went into had a clear musty smell to me, so I wouldn't be going there....ugh.

But I'm not giving up. At all. I will keep looking and finding a place....it may take a long time....but it will be worth it. There is nothing more important than my health and my gut totally tells me that the biotin removal will be a big part for me....

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-27T15:30:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mold-biotoxin-issues-and-vacations.8567/post-99146>

Hope.. I think a lot has to do with construction, as well as proximity to water.

think about the different types of construction the use in Europe - vs what we use here in the states... some of the pre-fab framing already may have issues before new construction goes up.

I never had mold issues in Guam or Okinawa - and I attribute that to the construction materials rebarbed cinder block and cement houses - no carpeting, all ceramic tile floors. every floor had a drain in too - incase of a really bad typhoon.

DC has some serious issues down town - the only major water damage for the pentagon was from putting the fires out on 9/11. but considering there's a good portion that's underground - water is always a possibility... I can smell mold a mile away - As I've lived in a house with stachybacthrus mold...

Not sure how you find out if a location is mold free... but any tips are welcome here....

### Lava — 2013-10-27T16:57:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mold-biotoxin-issues-and-vacations.8567/post-99149>

I have found that most major hotel companies are on top of the mold thing due to lawsuits. The Hilton in Honolulu was a mess and they had to rebuild one building..I tend to stay away from places that are not up to date on mold and bedbugs etc….On Kwajalein the mold was only in the sheetrock buildings..The cement buildings were clean.

Living in Hawaii mold is a real issue and I ask before I stay…also mold mites are a real clue…tiny tiny creatures but they are there if you look hard enough..

### cantweight — 2013-10-27T17:12:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mold-biotoxin-issues-and-vacations.8567/post-99151>

Hope you are connecting so many dots for me....we lived in 2 new construction places in Ashburn one in 1999 and one in 2004 and both were cookie cutter homes thrown up in short periods of time...the whole home exposed to the elements during construction...so much wet wood! My basement in my last house always felt damp/cool/humid. Something i was shocked at when we moved into this older home was the dryness of basement, we all commented on it, realtors and inspectors alike. Mold hadn't crossed my mind but it makes perfect sense another reason we feel better here.

I am like you with the crazy sense of smell....I have often wondered if it was hormonal because during pregnancy was the first time my nose became hyper sensitive and now it always is. I drive my family nuts hunting down smells!

You mentioned the Outer Banks and it really made me think....our best stays there have always been at The Currituck Club. Many places I can remember feeling quite uneasy and not sleeping well, but the times we stayed there were wonderful. The currituck club is sound side and on higher ground than most places, it is somewhat newer construction and the homes have fiber cement siding (something I have been curious about in how it plays with EMF) the homes there never seem damp or musty to me which I had equated with them being newer but it may have more to with construction materials.

Can't wait to hear what Shoemaker says....There isnt a Lyme doc I havent read and he and Klinghardt are my faves hands down! Jemsek is good too I just find him too hyper focused on killing bad things and not strengthening good things, but his neuro understanding is awesome.

### Hope — 2013-10-27T19:27:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mold-biotoxin-issues-and-vacations.8567/post-99157>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=99146)
>
> Hope.. I think a lot has to do with construction, as well as proximity to water.
>
> think about the different types of construction the use in Europe - vs what we use here in the states... some of the pre-fab framing already may have issues before new construction goes up.
>
> I never had mold issues in Guam or Okinawa - and I attribute that to the construction materials rebarbed cinder block and cement houses - no carpeting, all ceramic tile floors. every floor had a drain in too - incase of a really bad typhoon.
>
> DC has some serious issues down town - the only major water damage for the pentagon was from putting the fires out on 9/11. but considering there's a good portion that's underground - water is always a possibility... I can smell mold a mile away - As I've lived in a house with stachybacthrus mold...
>
> Not sure how you find out if a location is mold free... but any tips are welcome here....

Hi Gretchen, so glad you are OK at the big P there  that is awesome.

As to construction, I have no clue!  Dr Shoemaker talked about an old house in MD he bought having no mold at all, and it was wood. I know in Germany, or Europe, most places I have been, it is not usual for a main home to be built from wood etc, it's all stone/cement etc. Maybe that helps, as well as the air - at least for Germany. No clue why....but it's totally fascinating, not to mention overwhelming.

### Hope — 2013-10-27T19:32:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mold-biotoxin-issues-and-vacations.8567/post-99159>

> [cantweight said:](/goto/post?id=99151)
>
> Hope you are connecting so many dots for me....we lived in 2 new construction places in Ashburn one in 1999 and one in 2004 and both were cookie cutter homes thrown up in short periods of time...the whole home exposed to the elements during construction...so much wet wood! My basement in my last house always felt damp/cool/humid. Something i was shocked at when we moved into this older home was the dryness of basement, we all commented on it, realtors and inspectors alike. Mold hadn't crossed my mind but it makes perfect sense another reason we feel better here.
>
> I am like you with the crazy sense of smell....I have often wondered if it was hormonal because during pregnancy was the first time my nose became hyper sensitive and now it always is. I drive my family nuts hunting down smells!
>
> You mentioned the Outer Banks and it really made me think....our best stays there have always been at The Currituck Club. Many places I can remember feeling quite uneasy and not sleeping well, but the times we stayed there were wonderful. The currituck club is sound side and on higher ground than most places, it is somewhat newer construction and the homes have fiber cement siding (something I have been curious about in how it plays with EMF) the homes there never seem damp or musty to me which I had equated with them being newer but it may have more to with construction materials.
>
> Can't wait to hear what Shoemaker says....There isnt a Lyme doc I havent read and he and Klinghardt are my faves hands down! Jemsek is good too I just find him too hyper focused on killing bad things and not strengthening good things, but his neuro understanding is awesome.

Cristi, that is so great about TCC. I will have to check them out for sure. I love OBX but wouldn't return to the summer rental we had. I read in Dr Shoemaker's book sensitive smell might be a sign of mold issues.....though it can be many other things. I can smell when the neighbor is smoking outside too. It sucks. And I barely have the window open, too, at times.

So so great you now have a basement that does not smell! Friends in the Shenandoah valley, I have two, when entering their houses - OMG it is so much drier and non-smelly than our place here, ugh. That and the non-EMF environment made me want to get out there stat. So we'll see....

I have found a website called The Pure Room, which sounds pretty good.....apparently they check hotels....but that's not everywhere.

I will let you know what I find out.....

### Hope — 2013-10-27T19:34:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mold-biotoxin-issues-and-vacations.8567/post-99160>

> [Lava said:](/goto/post?id=99149)
>
> I have found that most major hotel companies are on top of the mold thing due to lawsuits. The Hilton in Honolulu was a mess and they had to rebuild one building..I tend to stay away from places that are not up to date on mold and bedbugs etc….On Kwajalein the mold was only in the sheetrock buildings..The cement buildings were clean.
>
> Living in Hawaii mold is a real issue and I ask before I stay…also mold mites are a real clue…tiny tiny creatures but they are there if you look hard enough..

Lava that is fascinating. I didn't know HI had mold issues too, what with all the wind. I'm not sure all hotels are super clean in general, I guess there are varied levels of issues....in Dr S's book many organizations, like schools, even the EPA refuses to say there are mold issues and people have to take matters into their own hands. As we already are....but wow.

I bet HI is amazing though......you have all those great beaches here....

Nicole

### Cosmo — 2014-02-22T21:04:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-111720>

I personally sleep MUCH better now that I'm earthing - deeper and more complete it seems. With earthing, there may be a thing where it is more beneficial to put your own grounding rod in the ground outside your bedroom window rather than plug into house electrical grounding ports in the outlets in your home. I am still trying to uncover the full truth on that but for now, I have put my own stake in the ground out the back and sleep connected to it. It is awesome.

### scottyc — 2014-03-04T05:19:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-113283>

I met with an EMF consultant (Steve Magee) and he advised against the grounding sheets due to stray voltage. He said that while plugging directly into the ground is better than the wall socket, even the rods plugged into the ground can have stray voltage 'contamination' due to underground power lines. He said, that he doesn't even walk barefoot in his own home due to this 'stray voltage' issue.
Like Cosmo, I slept grounded in my last house, and noticed deeper sleep. However I think the stray voltage issue is something to keep in mind, depending on where you live and your proximity to underground power lines.

### Cosmo — 2014-03-04T07:34:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-113289>

> Like Cosmo, I slept grounded in my last house, and noticed deeper sleep. However I think the stray voltage issue is something to keep in mind, depending on where you live and your proximity to underground power lines.

That's awesome that you met steven magee - I've been watching his youtube channel for a while now. Lots of fascinating content there! I want to get to the bottom of the stray voltage controversy because it seems like a lot of people are getting benefits from simply plugging into the house electrical ground and yet I had so many sleepless nights when i did as such.

I actually am part of a discussion about that where there is a very interesting theory about grounding and restless sleeping. It has to do with getting earth energy from the K1 accupoint. If you want to read the full details the thread is on the earthing forum here: [http://earthingforum.com/does-your-ground-rod-have-stray-voltage-t8.html](http://earthingforum.com/does-your-ground-rod-have-stray-voltage-t8.html)
In fact, that whole thread is a discussion on stray voltage and how it affects earthing.

### Tanya — 2014-03-04T21:25:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-113413>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=99133)
>

>
> Also anyone use the auto set pad? I spend almost 10 hours a week in my car.
>
> Thanks
>
> Nicole

Nicole, I have and use just about everything that the Earthing website has to offer. When I read here about all that controversy, I immediately discontinued doing that. After awhile, I could really tell what a mistake it was on my part - I was in so much more pain again. So, the sheet, pillowcase, throw (I use it instead of the blanket) are back in my bed - connected to a rod in my backyard.

About the car seat - it was not doing anything for me while i kept it on the seat, but when I placed it on the car floor and kept one foot on it while driving I noticed a huge difference. I also tested myself with the body voltage meter and that was also helpful to see.

One more thing, I've had Magnetico for over 4 years now and I found it helpful, but it alone could not relief me of all that pain and inflammation I was dealing with until I started using the earthing products. Funny thing is, I don't have any choice but to use the outside grounding rod, when we tested our outlets, they were not grounded....

### sooperb — 2014-03-04T22:36:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-113430>

I've had the earthing half sheet for around 2 years, 18 months of that using the house earth via the socket and then I changed to a grounding rod sunk into my front garden. I also have a mat which I use under my computer and put my bare feet on. My experience is a positive one, before earthing my sleep was awful, after using it for a few weeks, it improved a great deal. Most mornings I had ankle pain on rising, this has disappeared completely.

There is an earthing thread on this forum somewhere, it was the reason I signed up, I only found the other stuff after a few visits when I decided to look around.

### [email redacted] — 2014-03-05T06:48:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-113514>

I use an earthing sheet actually a 'fitted' sheet on a queen bed (better than the half sheet). I think it has helped my sleep I notice also I 'like' my bed more kind of a subjective thing but it feels real. I stick the rod in the earth not the socket which I feel more comfortable doing. But one think which I thought was kind of 'funny' and maybe a bit deceptive on the part of the company................they have this 'tester' and supposedly when the sheet is grounded a green light come on on the tester. I used this and sure eneough on ANY other surface it does not light up but bingo on the sheet it does!! Well one day a few weeks ago the gardeners pulled the wire out of the rod by mistake it was completly disconnected from the rod BUT as a lark I thought let me test it now and would you believe it it lit up just as before. So that 'tester' is testing SOMETHING but NOT that it is grounded. Now I found that kind of deceptive as I say. I don't have an explanation of WHAT it is testing. Any ideas anyone?

### Cosmo — 2014-03-05T07:32:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-113517>

Pat, I would test the sheet more with it plugged in to the wall, unplugged, plugged into a self-put grounding rod, etc... Try to get to the bottom of it. Write down your results.

I would also contact the company and ask about that.

That is an oddity to be sure.

### [email redacted] — 2014-03-05T18:39:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-113614>

Well the thing is Cosmo it seems to test ONLY for the 'main wire' being attached (fastened) to the fastener on the sheet. If you unhook those things the light goes out. BUT what I find kind of deceptive is and maybe this was my mis-understanding is the implication is once that little green light goes on it is GROUNDED. I imagined it was some kind of measure of electrical exchange between the sheet and the ground and it is NOT! It is merely telling you it is connected (fastened) and then I wonder how does that work? Like what is the 'electrical exchange' that causes that little green light to go on. And then I'm thinking 'scam' like did they deliberatly do this just to create an 'impression' I don't really know what more testing I can do, if you have a grounding sheet and my impression is you do just unplug it either from the outlet or from the ground itself if that is what you have and then test it..............does the green light still go on? You're right I could contact the company and maybe I will I just don't 'enjoy' doing that kind of thing.

Hi Tanya, I had a similar experience. My back was much worse at the time, and I loved the way my ground sheet (attached to a rod in my garden) made me feel. I tried discontinuing it and discovered quickly that it was a mistake. Gradually my back got stronger and better, thanks to the EpiPaleo diet and water. Eventually I discovered that I could either take or leave the grounding sheet; I didn't need it so much. Later on, I think it was setting me back, at least while I was in the city, so I stopped using it here. I still use a ground sheet while in the country.

So perhaps a grounding sheet has both good and bad effects, and sometimes the good effects outweigh the bad. I know it was awesome for my back, at one point in my health journey.

### [email redacted] — 2014-03-06T18:09:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-113792>

I am now getting a bit confused by all this. But what I found is though the rod was definitly not grounded the green light still went on if the wire was 'fastened' to the sheet, if you unfastened it instantly goes off. ### Cosmo — 2014-03-06T20:42:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-113813>

Pat, the tester might be faulty.

But what Tanya said about body voltage is the answer.

Get a multimeter that can test AC voltage. You can see how this works here:
[How To Use A Multimeter To Test Body Voltage](http://earthingforum.com/how-use-multimeter-test-body-voltage-t10.html)

This will let you know if the earthing product is working and then you will have something to compare against when testing your current earthing tester.

### nonchalant — 2014-04-29T17:29:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/earthing-half-sheet.8572/post-123884>

@[Tanya](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/6641/), a couple nights ago a storm came through out in the country, and I made the decision to leave my grounding sheet attached to the ground rod. (Usually I hastily disconnect!) I noticed quite a bit of tingling that night, even before it started to rain. It wasn't on the same level as my first night on the sheet, but it was quite pleasant. I kept thinking about my grandfather, who said lightning was fertilizer for crops. I was sure soaking it up! And no, I didn't die after all.

Oh, btw, we got the largest hail I have personally seen that night. The stone DH retrieved was over 2 inches in diameter, and it sure was not the largest one. Nature must realize we need more energy.

She is on Ritalin to keep her from hurting herself and we really want to try to get her off of it now. Hope that this helps and I do know that this will not be a cure.

Nicole

Hey Nicole, started using the twin core with my 8 yr old son. He just has mild neuro issues, tourette type stuff....previosly treated for biotoxin crap

After using the magnetico he immediately broke out in a rash all over his thighs and developed an orange crust on his scalp primarily on the occipital lobe area. I called and spoke with Dr Bonlie and he was not surprised at all, I guess it is common, he suggested DMSA due to detox being from metals.

I dont think Jack is big fan of DMSA though I am not sure why and would love to know....

I have not given my little guy any and he is doing quite well. Energy has gone up, tics are rare, no crazy behavior or rage issues. Rash was clear within a week, scalp stuff continues

He also wakes most mornings and says that he didnt sleep at all, which is not true but maybe his sleep is not restful. His night terrors and bed wetting have been rare also.....a huge plus.

Granted we do a lot of stuff with him....he CTs on occasion, decent diet, RO water, grounding.....etc Hard to say what is the biggest help but a lot of the improvement has been post magnetico....cant wait to have one myself!

### Nick — 2013-10-28T08:59:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-pad-for-child.8576/post-99225>

>

Hi Nicole,

We have been using the 5G Magnetico on our 3yo's bed for about 4 months now. He has/had bad allergies and eczema. Definitely noticed some interesting effects on his sleeping patterns. His sleeping was always pretty poor due top itching at night, sleeping, waking, itching, crying then sleeping, waking itching and so on. With the magnetico he slept restlessly, so much so that my wife slept with him for about a month but it was a different pattern altogether. No itching and crying, just tossing and turning waking and sleeping. She spoke to Magnetico, who are great to chat with, and the advice was to put the Magnetico on the floor under the bed which drops the field to 2G until he is used to it then bring it back up to 5G.

So not awesome nights sleeps but a big improvement over the blood and guts and crying show we used to get during the night. We were able to get some great advice from Jack which we follow as best as we can with a 3yo, epi-paleo, spring water, no lights on at night, EMF at night as low as we can make it and minimal during the day. We have seen some really interesting results, we have won back chicken, beef, oysters, lamb, pork as well as other things that he was allergic to in a fairly short amount of time, plus his skin is much much better than it was, still a long way from perfect. We haven't had to use antibiotics, since we changed things around and we are using heaps less creams, its been a pretty big shift thanks to Jack!

We gave our 6yo a burst of 5 days on it and just thrived, had heaps of energy, so much so that we will soon buy one for him as well.

Hope this helps,

# Thread: Food intolerances affect intestines and NOT stomach?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2013-10-27T20:38:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99170>

Hi guys,

Eating elimination diets and super clean hasn't helped my stomach problems at all and I think this is because my stomach has a problem mechanically. All my problems start as soon as I consume anything, if food intolerances were an issue then I assume problems would only start when the food reaches the intestines (leaky gut). I don't think they would start in the stomach. Also water shouldn't cause any problems whatsoever with intolerances however with me it gives the same symptoms. This makes me believe it is not anything to do with WHAT I am eating but just anything making my stomach throw a fit.

An example: Say I drink some water after a light meal I can feel the water go down my oesophagus and then push hard to get from the end of my oesophagus into my stomach. As soon as this happens I get an uncomfortable, pressure like, pain in my stomach, this uncomfortable pain maintains for hours after a meal, depending on size. It's as though my stomach is really full and is struggling to fit in anything more. Afterwards I will most likely experience a feeling of food coming back up but with no burning or vomiting (yet?).

Is this true that food intolerances should only affect the intestines?
Does this information give anyone any ideas what could be wrong?

### caroline — 2013-10-27T22:07:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99176>

Sean - you are really struggling ... but trying to think it thru. Sorry - I have no knowledge about this.

I hope someone else will have something to offer.

Are you going to listen to the webinar with Dr. Jackson today?

### nicld — 2013-10-27T23:57:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99187>

I would highly recommend that you find a good GI doctor. They can do a barium study to see of there is something mechanically going on.

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-28T00:10:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99192>

Sean.. the more I read about your symptoms, the more I think there's a disconnect btwn your brain and gut via the vagus nerve. I'd first start w/CT6, then go back through the BG series... something in your environment is causing a disconnect btwn the two. initial gut reaction, wants me to look at light & non-native EMF - and your vitamin A cycles. But then there the bigger picture - other things could be causing this stress on your system thus resulting in the disconnect....

Do you have any recent labs? I know you're in the UK... and we need to help you figure out what's going on. I don't know if you can get your hands on one - but I'd see if you can get a metametrix/genova GI Effects stool test.

### Gracelind — 2013-10-28T04:47:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99211>

Hi Sean,

You sound like a good candidate for a barium swallow video exam. You drink a barium "shake" then the camera rolls while you swallow. You will also swallow a barium pill. This shows the passage of a bolus from your mouth down into your stomach. You may have a stricture or inflammation near the sphincter at the end of your esophagus, or possibly a hiatal hernia. It's important to check out the "mechanics" then you can work on cause for what is happening. I agree with the thought to go to a good GI doc.

Re: food intolerance I tested positive to sensitivity to foods such as avocado, cashews, celery (including celery salt), cranberries, spinach, parsley and others. Before the testing, with muscle weakness-caused swallowing problems I was often coughing and choking when eating to the point that 4 different docs were suggesting a feeding tube soon. Since avoiding those foods I rarely cough or choke, though due to muscle weakness have to eat slowly. So, at least in my case, food reactions begin from the moment it enters my mouth. It's not psychosomatic as I have reacted when the sensitive ingredient, especially celery, was hidden in the food and I had no way to know it was there.

Also, I have had reflux appear on the barium swallow video that did not burn or hurt. Actually, my last test had to be aborted due to aspiration of barium into lungs, but that's another issue.

Do think about the GI doc. Good luck!

### seanb4 — 2013-10-28T10:10:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99231>

@caroline I think webinars are for members only.

@nicld I have had a barium swallow test were I drank a small cup of fizzy drink with barium in it. The results came back normal. I did not expect this. I think maybe because it was only a small amount of liquid and it was all carbs that it wouldn't have upset my stomach as much and therefore the emptying times would have been only slightly longer than average and fell into the normal range. Maybe if I had the same test done but with an actual meal the results would be different?

@Shijin13 Well the thing that seemed to have started it all was when I took too many NSAIDs. Could this have caused a disconnect between the vagus nerve and the brain? Everything slowly went down hill from there. I am sure this is because the constant stresser in my life is my stomach. When I did the dry fast, my hair suddenly got 10x better, excema went away, skin felt good again, and I felt way more awake mentally. I am sure the stress caused by my stomach is why I feel so fatigued now but didn't feel fatigued after taking the NSAIDS.
I have been saving up for, and trying to get via the NHS the following tests: adrenal stress, T3 and rT3, comprehensive stool test, stomach ph test, estrogen and pregestorone. The waiting list is 4 months however and it is a real struggle to get the NHS to agree to doing tests. I'm slowly saving up money but it will be a while before I have enough.

@Gracelind I saw a gastroenteroligist before. First session the guy said he's going to try everything to figure out what's wrong with me and gave me some prokinetics and told me to see him again in 3 months. Prokinetics didn't work so I came back and this time it was a different doctor who just said sorry theres nothing we can do. Felt a bit short changed to be honest, what happened to the first doctor?
I find that interesting that the food intolerances don't only affect the intestines but like I said, I still don't think it's food intolerances in my case as water has the exact same affect as other foods.

### prAna303 — 2013-10-28T12:20:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99234>

I would consider two major things about how we consume energy today...

We talk alot about what and when we eat but not that much about how and where. If we still struggle with some things in life i think one should step it up one level. Consider how and where you consume your meals. Are you eating in a warm adapted place? Are you eating ungrounded? Do you eat in front of your computer, tv or anything else "modern"? If you have WiFi nearby, do you use a fork and spoon made of metals? Do you eat without stress and with this i mean stress from the fields you live in.

Many things can change but if we still do not try out to go back in our ways of doing things we cannot go forward also. The more we force feed us with energy killing elements the more we have to use ancient methods i think.

Just my take, do question everything you do with yourself every single moment.

### nicld — 2013-10-28T15:34:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99242>

Looked into SIBO, called my GI doc, got on antibiotics for a week (along with probiotics) and stayed away from almost all carbs and the gut has got better.

At least you know there is not something structurally wrong then.

Hope you find some help cause GI issues SUCK and just drain all the joy and happiness out of you, literally. From what I understand most of the good feeling hormone seratonin in the body is produced in the gut.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99297>

### nicld — 2013-10-29T15:26:54+0200

Hey Sean so here is my N=1. I have had HORRIBLE GI symptoms for years. Like you, I felt as if the GI system would just stop and when it did, the pain would be horrible. I would bloat right up and not go for days. I could tell right where it seems like the intestines just stopped working (left top side). I was so terrified of it stopping that I was on magnesium and Mirilax at least 1X a day, sometimes 2. I was diagnosed with IBS years and years ago and felt like I was going to be on Mirilax the rest of my life, ughhhh.

Then in April, everything seem to fall apart. It seemed like anything I ate I got horrible GI pains. Finally my GI system is getting into the circadian rhythm that is described in CT 7 and it is awesome.

### Gracelind — 2013-10-30T00:24:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/food-intolerances-affect-intestines-and-not-stomach.8577/post-99390>

Sean,

This article on Gastroparesis has some of the issues Gretchen mentioned, including involvement of the vagus nerve. It does indicate possible cause may be from meds. [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gastroparesis](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gastroparesis)

FWIW my first barium swallow and endoscopy in 1992 showed no cause for the tightness around throat I felt. Most literature at that time said such a sensation was psychosomatic ... in your head. 10 years later the next one showed esophageal dysmotility due to muscle weakness. Sometimes we sense problems long before exams can pick them up.

I hope you find a solution--sounds very uncomfortable.

### Gracelind — 2013-10-28T04:04:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/webinar-with-dr-tim-jackson.8581/post-99207>

A major message--as with most physiology/metabolic/biochem issues--gut health is key. How to accomplish that is still not clear to me, especially since we all have different issues and conditions. Time to do some GI testing for a base level and then some rereading of Brain Gut and other of Jack's blogs.

I have done the Genova hormone test that Misty mentioned and it's so informative. Results are returned in graph form with text explanations. My doc gave me the physician's version with lots of good info. One of my low results was noted that it could be a result of COMT and ... bingo! According to 23andMe I do have some mutations there. Lots of work to do to improve that profile with my results highly catabolic (makes sense since I have a degenerative autoimmune condition.)

Looking forward to the bootcamp.

Tuesdays at 7 pm CT=Tuesdays at 8PM EST (New York)
Sundays at 6 pm CT=Sundays at 7 pm EST (New York)
\----------------------------------------------------------------
Tuesdays at 7 pm CT=Wednesday at 11AM (Sydney Australia)
Sundays at 6 pm CT=Monday at 10 AM (Sydney Australia)
\----------------------------------------------------------------

**Subforum:** Optimal Labs

### MsYo — 2013-10-28T23:51:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2013-lab-results-and-link-to-2011-2012-labs.8588/post-99293>

45 Year Old woman. Peri-Menopausal. Fibroids. Former Endurance Runner. 12+ years post gastric bypass. 40lbs overweight (37.5%BF). Currently lift weights after 5pm - no cardio. Leptin Reset in progress. Former HCG user.

**Supplements Using**
Lugol's 5% Solution 82-100mg daily
Selenium (200mg/2x's daily)
Vitamin D3 10000ius (slacked off dosing in summer hence the drop)
Pregnenolone (Nutricology/150mg-- just restarted)
Nature Throid (2 grains/T3 method)
Boulouke (just started)
Phosphodelserine PS (new)
ATP Cofactors
Cytozyme AD (adrenal fatigue)
Magnesium (citrate-- theronate just started)
SAM-e 800mg
Prescript Assist Probiotic
Rhodiola

[https://drive.google.com/file/d/0BzXWlxIV-zQoZlVkTlVxbVQyNG8/edit?usp=sharing](https://drive.google.com/file/d/0BzXWlxIV-zQoZlVkTlVxbVQyNG8/edit?usp=sharing)

Previous Lab info for reference/comparison
[http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/show...2011-Labs-versus-2012-Labs-Feedback-Requested](http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?4102-Comparison-of-2011-Labs-versus-2012-Labs-Feedback-Requested)

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-29T01:20:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2013-lab-results-and-link-to-2011-2012-labs.8588/post-99307>

Hey MsYo. I took a quick look - but I'll go back and look indepth tomorrow but here's what I saw

Vit D3 - is low
LDL - is low
HDL is good
TRIGs are good
your pregnenlone is low
looks like you're showing some positive signs of Hashis b/c you're showing positve antibodies- I was confused b/c you had two TSH's drawn - one was above 1 and one was below 1. This is where I need to go back and re-look

your PG & E2 looks good. Unfortunately I don't know how to calculate a Pg/E2 ratio from saliva... off to figure that out after this

your DHEA-S is decent... and your ASI is a bit off so you need to mind the light.

I think you've got an altered circadian cycle, that's impacting your Vitamin A production, add that to your low Pregnenolone and LDL - I think you've got some pregnenonlone steal going on - but you are making some sex hormones - Unless that's all BHRT. I'm drawing a blank as to if you were taking BHRT -

More to follow but there's first crack!

I use a digital scale that provides a decently accurate reading based on your age/gender/activity level. I weigh 217lbs with a large/muscular bone structure. If I'm dehydrated my weight/bf% goes up and the water % (scale measures that too) goes down.

### MsYo — 2013-10-29T17:39:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2013-lab-results-and-link-to-2011-2012-labs.8588/post-99353>

My vitamin d3 was 72 before the summer. I slacked off supplementation to see how it would respond. Likely my office job and minimal sunlight in addition to EMF exposure caused it to drop. I'm now supplementing 10000iu daily. I'm going for a blood draw for my hormones this week with my primary care. I do use progesterone/BHRT creams dosing day 12-25 of my cycle. My cycle is about 28-32 days normally. I also was dosing K2 twice a day but need to reorder. My HsCRP was .4 earlier in the year and this last measure was 1.1
My ASI has been challenged for some time now. Looking into the magnetico and other areas that can make an immediate impact.

### JanSz — 2013-12-12T02:03:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2013-lab-results-and-link-to-2011-2012-labs.8588/post-103176>

MsYo

I am trying to figure out your

preg
prog
cortisol

It looks like (not sure) that you are supplementing with progesterone and are still low in morning cortisol.
So you are looking for methods to increase cortisol.
Meditation or whatever would work.

Over lat three years I (73yo man) have had good results supplementing with pregnenolone Micronize Lipid Matrix (from Nutricology).
I use huge doses of pregnenolone. Much larger than what dr Tim Jackson mentioned recently in his booth-camp.
I increase preg doses until my morning cortisol(7:30AM) is at the top level.
I also observe free,serum Cortisol and aim at the top range.
That way I do not have a problem of having plenty of cortisol.

Doing that my progesterone is very high.
If I was a woman, that should please dr Kruse, per him, the more prog the better.

My current prog=2.9 that is over 2x of max laboratory range of 1.4 (for men of for woman in first half of cycle).
But I had it at 4.4 without any negative observable sides.
Again, I am doing it close to 3 years now.

If I read correctly, you are supplementing with progesterone in second half of cycle.
Likely not enough, and totally neglecting first half of cycle.
And you are supplementing with progesterone while pregnenolone may possibly do better job, plus, it may take care of a whole cycle.

If you are doing CT (like you should), there is a good possibility that you are washing of and wasting lot or most progesterone cream.

/////

### MsYo — 2013-12-12T18:31:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2013-lab-results-and-link-to-2011-2012-labs.8588/post-103222>

JanZ---

My morning cortisol is still low. I was using progesterone cream (50ml) on days 12-25 of my cycle and pregnenolone all month. It is likely my initial dose wasn't high enough 150-300mg (2tabs of nutricology). I am taking 600 mg daily. My CT has been lacking. I slacked off on the preg dosing prior to my labs in Aug/Sept. Maybe my pregnenolone is being directed to my dhea. (wild guess)

### JanSz — 2013-12-13T02:20:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2013-lab-results-and-link-to-2011-2012-labs.8588/post-103272>

> [MsYo said:](/goto/post?id=103222)
>
> JanZ---
>
> My morning cortisol is still low. I was using progesterone cream (50ml) on days 12-25 of my cycle and pregnenolone all month. It is likely my initial dose wasn't high enough 150-300mg (2tabs of nutricology). I am taking 600 mg daily. **My CT has been lacking.** I slacked off on the preg dosing prior to my labs in Aug/Sept. Maybe my pregnenolone is being directed to my dhea. (wild guess)

You should not stop using pregnenolone or DHEA pills for the sake of laboratory testing.

There is a minimal chance that you will increase DHEAs by supplementing with pregnenolone (of any type),
but by having low DHEAs you can waste lots of benefits that you would otherwise have from pregnenolone supplementation.
.
You should make sure that you have a high DHEAs (Women 275-400 μg/dL), close to 400 better.
.
.
You are menstruating woman.
Theoretically, in first half of cycle (say 15 days) you should have progesterone at least 1.5ng/mL
Going by my (I am man) personal experience, nothing bad is going to happen if you put high limit on progesterone:
in first 15 days do not exceed progesterone,serum~5ng/mL
keep increasing pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix from Nutricology until
1\. your cortisol,serum(7:30AM)=(~20)ug/dL
or
2.progesterone level reaches ~5ng/mL
At this time I would put maximum daily pregnenolone at 1200mg=8tabs, each 150mg

If that dose is still not giving you good cortisol, you may want to add
prometrium (micronized progesterone) 100mg per gelcap.
Prometrium may induce sleepiness, use at bed time

Again, establishing good DHEAs is first step.

I think that you should first work on first half of cycle.

\-------------------

Have you ever done 24hr urine cortisol?

\-------------------
Note that I am using pills, nothing in any creams.

Because our group of people spends lots of time in the water (CT), transdermal applications of anything have less chance to work.

Besides, lots of people, specially those who still have thyroid problems, have difficulty in benefiting from transdermal applications even if they do not wash as frequently as we are. Their skin is harder to penetrate.

///

You can get progesterone over internet.
Your very low iron is old story at least since 2011 as you posted on previous thread.
It is about time to take care of it.
Look at ingredients in
Thorne Methyl-Guard Plus, and possibly replace this with the other similar pills you are already taking.
Consider eating coconut oils by tablespoons rather than pills.
In my local Asian stores I have 16oz cans with fresh pre-shucked oysters.
They are only $9.50/can, To fill up that can I would need 3-3.5 dozens of live oysters.
I am eating them raw, no problems.
\-----------------------
day 21,22, desirable levels=
estradiol 140pg/mL
prog 14ng/mL

day 2-4, desirable levels=
estradiol 40 pg/mL
prog 1.5ng/mL
\-----------------------------
blood taken on day 21-22
desirable E2=140 you got 101
desirable progesterone=14 you got 10.4

When you will reduce pregnenoloneMLM by one pill, your progesterone will also drop.
But you are taking NatureGest progesterone cream applied once daily mornings
Consider doubling the dose.
Overall, now your p/e situation should be improved but is far from critical at this time of your menstrual cycle.
Consider checking where you are on day 2-4.

If you get Prometrium or similar, they come in 100mg and 200mg gelcaps.
You may try:
pregMLM(wakeup)+prometrium(bed time) as your basic two
and possibly additional prometrium on days 18-24, or NatureGest progesterone.
Overall you are attempting to aproximate progesterone curve on this chart:
[http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/c/c8/Hormones\_estradiol,\_progesterone,\_LH\_and\_FSH\_during\_menstrual\_cycle.svg](http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/c/c8/Hormones_estradiol%2C_progesterone%2C_LH_and_FSH_during_menstrual_cycle.svg)

Please clarify your DHEAs situation.

I have personal experience with two types of DHEA pills.
Looking at my blood test results, there is small difference between them.
So it may be important to keep the same brand.

I have a problem in deciding about your DHEAs status.

There is
DHEAs
and DHEA
you tested for DHEA,serum (and you are low on it, low within range).

Think of DHEAs ar analogus to TotalTestosterone
Think of DHEA as analogus to FreeTestosterone
Similar but different.

You are taking 100mg/day DHEA pills, that is sizeable dose (for a girl)
I am 165# man, I need to use 200mg/day now, but was using 400mg/day
\------------
Your zinc is low, if you implement my suggestion above, it may affect your testosterone and DHEA/DHEAs
Your
testosterone 34 (2-45)
FreeT 2.3 (0.1-6.4)

You could improve there.

My first choice would be to have DHEAs,serum test.
But with all above, you likely may increase your daily dose from 100 to (125-150)mg/day
and do tests latter.
\-------------------
I currently use
DHEA 50mg Micronized Lipid Matrix from Allergy research
previously I was using
100mg or 50mg DHEA from lef.org

I happen to be looking at iodine use now, and I noticed your comment earlier in this thread about having fibroids. I am NOT recommending iodine at all, but you should listen to this audio interview and what is interesting is that one of the groups most-helped by iodine supplementation is women with fibroids. It apparently also reduces cancer risk in such women substantially. It is however a pretty serious therapy and can disregulate thyroid in about 10% of patients. So it needs supervision. [http://www.xpeditionstv.com/V2/](http://www.xpeditionstv.com/V2/)

My B12 has been historically high. I am an energy supplement addict (recovering). I switched from SF Red Bull to 5 Hour Energy. My doctor also gave me a methylated B12 that seemed to work wonderfully for a couple of weeks then I tanked. I suspect that was the time my iron dropped. I am stopping all B12 and more effort to STOP 5hour now that I know that my fatigue is from the low iron. My cortisol seems to have risen as I sleep throughout the night and awake at sunrise every morning. The exercise has helped my sleep and stress levels. I suspect a touch of overtraining as I was using a coach to help me with body recomposition. I was adapting his calorie recommendation to (EPI PALEO) but 2 weeks ago he added fasting cardio and more cardio type strength training. I know this wasn't the right thing for me but rather than whine I attempted. I have since modified my workouts.

I stopped DHEA and pregnenolone about 6 weeks ago. I have ordered both again and also the Thorne. Should I use the Thorne with my B12 being so high already? I suspect my RNY/gastric bypass has created a problem for me. Any susggestions for where I can research how to fix this?

\-----------------------------------------------
Methyl-Guard Plus®
Three Capsules Contain:
B2 Riboflavin (as Riboflavin 5'-Phosphate Sodium) 90 mg

Folate (as L-5-Methyltetrahydrofolate from L-5-Methyltetrahydrofolic Acid, Glucosamine Salt) 3 mg
Vitamin B12 (as Methylcobalamin) 3 mg
TMG Betaine Anhydrous (Trimethylglycine) 1.8 g

# Thread: Now Everything Hurts - Whatever This Is, It Is Progressing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-everything-hurts-whatever-this-is-it-is-progressing.8593/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mafixit — 2013-10-29T15:42:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-everything-hurts-whatever-this-is-it-is-progressing.8593/post-99343>

I have been posting for about a year about my headaches and menopause issues, and now everything seems to have taken a turn in another direction and I am not sure where to go next. Mayo Clinic diagnosis is Fibromyalgia.

I am in menopause (very long, erratic cycles - am on around Day 80 now). I am on BHRT with my anti-aging doc. We have run many labs, the latest being a Nutra-Eval nutritional panel. I took the nutri-panel from Genova after cleaning out my system, and the first part of the nutritional panel showed a high need for magnesium, B-1 and Alpha-Lipoic Acid. I am waiting on the amino acid profile. I started Ortho Biotic on the advice of my hormone doctor and began to take Magnesium Taurate a few weeks ago. I feel shittier than ever. I have episodes of blurry vision, too, especially in fluorescent lighting.

My headaches are different now, not as intense, but still every day. Part of these I think are attributed to a compressed nerve on the left side at C5-C6 causing issues with my neck and left arm/shoulder. I have been in physical therapy, but it has not helped. My next stop for that is an epidural in November.

Now I have widespread pain and stiffness, especially in my elbows, forearms and hands, but just basically all over. If I stay still for more than a few seconds, I stiffen up like the tin man with no oil. I feel like I am losing strength in my arms. There are things twitching that shouldn't be. I feel these random vibrations under my skin, mostly in my thighs and girly parts. I wake up at 4:30 every morning, 15 minutes before my alarm with this feeling of bad anticipation and my anxiety level has been pretty bad of late. I have a really hard time concentrating on my work. My allergies are up after 1-1/2 years of not needing any sort of anti-histamine. I am a software support specialist by trade and work from home. I go through these small bouts of depression now, because everyday I wake up hoping that this will all get better and it never does.

Mayo Clinic's answer was anti-depressants, debilitating migraine meds and Cymbalta. According to their labs I should be a perfectly healthy 50 year old woman. None of their drugs worked and I weaned off of EVERYTHING. I started over with just my BHRT and a probiotic about a month ago. I went back on BHRT because my hot flashes were so bad I could not sleep or function. The hormones appear to help my headaches somewhat also. I do acupuncture once a week. Not sure if it helps, but it's cheap, so I do it. I had a massage last week and the therapist said my "connective tissues feel dehydrated".

My diet is fairly clean. I no longer consume gluten, which a test showed I have an intolerance to. To sum it up, I am on Estrogen, Progesterone, pro-biotics and magnesium taurate.

I am not sure where to go next, what to look for next. My hormone doc is very open to discussions with me on what I want to do or check or try. Any thoughts on my next move? It's been three years of suffering and trying different things to resolve this and I am worse than ever. It seems that the more I do to try to feel better, the worse I feel. All my blood and urine panels have always come up "normal" according to Mayo. I have an appointment with a new PCP outside the Mayo. They are too tunnel-visioned to help me, unless it involves taking a drug to mask the symptoms.

So my path right now is a new neurologist, new PCP, and the pain doc for the epidural. Again, my BHRT doc is open to thoughts or suggestions that I bring in, so, any thoughts on where to go/what to do or try next? I feel lost at this point.

Let me add that two weeks ago I had to have a molar pulled - it was the very back one on the bottom that had a root canal years ago. It was decayed under the tooth bridge it was holding on, so now I am missing two bottom teeth in the back.

Karen from Arizona

### MsYo — 2013-10-29T17:58:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-everything-hurts-whatever-this-is-it-is-progressing.8593/post-99356>

Do you have copies of your labs? "Normal" isn't always optimal as you know. Do you CT? Eat Epi-Paleo? Maybe post your labs in the Optimal Labs forum. I take SAM-e for my stiffness but upped the dose to 800mg to help me with moodiness. You own your destiny..... if your current physician isn't a match.... Keep looking. I suffer from adrenal fatigue which is similar to fibromyalgia I think. I was on a wait list for a doctor for 6 months and within the first month of revisiting the Leptin reset, emf reduction and some adrenal support I am improving. Keep the faith...... believe.... never give up.

### Melinda — 2013-10-29T20:25:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-everything-hurts-whatever-this-is-it-is-progressing.8593/post-99367>

Hi Karen,

I'm eager to hear what replies you get. I've been Kruse directionally accurate for a long time. I'm post meno and on bio hormones. I was feeling so great this last spring and summer as my sleep improved and we transitioned to much lower EMF, I threw myself at projects that were physical, re-tiling a few rooms. The oddest thing developed: the usual post exercise muscle soreness never left. I'm sore in large areas of my back and neck; my inner thighs are useless and it's hard to get up and down from a chair/car. But the morning stiffness is the worst, like rigor mortise. I'm trying trigger point massage and it helps but I do not get how this could happen out of the blue. We are all eating clean and drinking the same water but this only happened to me. Not sure where to go from here.
I know this is not enough info to get meaningful advice but I just wanted to share with you that you are not alone.

Also, from Arizona

### mafixit — 2013-10-29T20:43:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-everything-hurts-whatever-this-is-it-is-progressing.8593/post-99371>

I can pinpoint the day the pain started in my right elbow- July 4th of this year. Thought I had tennis elbow. That pain has moved into my right hand, and now my left hand and arm are also extremely stiff. Getting in and out of bed or the car is painful. All my toes hurt when I flex them and two on my right foot burn like they have carpet burn. I described it as you did - it feels like I worked out yesterday for the first time in a year.

Saw a doc for RA. Nope, not that. Saw something this morning on using Chlorophyll in your water to make yourself more alkaline as acidity can cause issues. Wondering if I should head out and look for the urine test to check my pH

What part of AZ are you in? The Valley or the High Country? PM me if you want to on who you see for your BHRT.

.

### Huck — 2013-10-30T13:50:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-everything-hurts-whatever-this-is-it-is-progressing.8593/post-99410>

Did you just eliminate gluten or grains?

I take glucosamine & chondroitin or else my entire body aches. As long as you aren't allergic to shellfish, it might be worth a try.

You might also really focus in on inflammation. LEF ([http://www.lef.org/index.htm](http://www.lef.org/index.htm)) has some really good articles. Fish oil, curcumin, C, resveratrol, etc. Did you have a [hs-crp](http://labtestsonline.org/understanding/analytes/hscrp/tab/test) lab done?

### mafixit — 2013-10-30T15:47:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-everything-hurts-whatever-this-is-it-is-progressing.8593/post-99419>

> [Huck said:](/goto/post?id=99410)
>
> Did you just eliminate gluten or grains?
>
> I take glucosamine & chondroitin or else my entire body aches. As long as you aren't allergic to shellfish, it might be worth a try.
>
> You might also really focus in on inflammation. LEF ([http://www.lef.org/index.htm](http://www.lef.org/index.htm)) has some really good articles. Fish oil, curcumin, C, resveratrol, etc. Did you have a [hs-crp](http://labtestsonline.org/understanding/analytes/hscrp/tab/test) lab done?

I have eliminated gluten and grains. I did have the hs-crp lab done, twice in the past year. The latest was 1.6 mg/L, which is better than the previous results. Lower range, but I feel worse. I am on fish oil with my supplement pack. I have not tried Resveratrol. My husband takes it. I do peruse LEF.org often. My BHRT doc has their magazines all over their office.

I did test for EMF's and found my clock radio that was plugged in under the head of my bed was atrociously bad. I removed it. That is all I found in the house, unless I stand directly in front of the microwave. My meter is on the other side of the house outside of the garage. It's the furthest away it could be.

I am incorporating the MELT Method into my daily routine. I have the roller and foam/rubber balls and am waiting for her book to arrive. I have scheduled a massage therapist to come in once a week. I had a good massage yesterday afternoon and felt good when he left, but woke up in the middle of the night with a horrible headaches. Headaches are waking me up at night along with the pain all over.

I did notice that when I am on a small dose (in the morning when I wake up) of Nature-Throid, I seem to sleep better, but that is the only difference I feel.

Karen from Arizona

### mafixit — 2013-10-31T15:36:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-everything-hurts-whatever-this-is-it-is-progressing.8593/post-99526>

The whole neighborhood is smart-metered, so yes. My bedroom is on that side of the house, but it is a wall, not a window and I did not measure any EMF's in my bedroom except for the clock radio. The microwave is built in, so it remains plugged. I did not do bootcamp.

Now I am feeling like I am choking, hard to breathe but I can take a deep breath. Chest and upper abdomen feels tight over the past week. Which of course pushes the anxiety level way up - Tightness came first, not anxiety, so it's not anxiety related at least initially. Not sure if it's hormone imbalance again, or stomach issues. Or all muscular from the pain in my back and neck from the compressed nerve. Oy Vey!

On Day 82 of my cycle, one of the longest time periods between periods. Maybe menopause is finally kicking in.

Karen from Arizona

### Grizz — 2013-10-31T17:48:26+0200

Karen,

I suggest you take the Dr. Brownstein Iodine Protocol.
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-Handout)

Especially focus on your Body pH with pH test strips of your urine. A healthy pH level is required to absorb minerals & vitamins. It should measure between 6.8 and 7.2. If you test low, use this 12 step list to make corrections:
[http://www.iconperformanceonline.com/?p=3678](http://www.iconperformanceonline.com/?p=3678)

All of your symptoms sound like iodine deficiency and abnormally high body acidic levels.
[http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-PH-Levels](http://tinyurl.com/Iodine-PH-Levels)

I also suggest taking 9mg of Boron daily. Boron is a miracle mineral for painful joints and a pandoras box of additional health problems.
[http://tinyurl.com/Boron-References](http://tinyurl.com/Boron-References)

Also be certain you are taking Vitamin D3 Geltabs, 10,000 IU daily and after 3 months, adjust your dosage to get D3 blood levels to 80ng/ml.

PS) Make sure your drinking water is free of Fluoride & stop drinking anything with sugar, high fructose corn syrup.

Hope this helps,
Grizz

pH levels are in normal range for the last 4 days. I bought the ION Balance Urine and Saliva Test Strips. I am checking with morning urine.

Waiting for answers on Iodine.

Now for some reason I have developed pretty bad acid reflux over the past week or so. My esophagus is burning, my chest is tight, I feel like I am choking (a lump in my throat) and I feel short of breath, even though I can breathe. Doc wants me on Protonics. It's been 4 days and they are not really helping. I went to the office and they did an EKG, just to make sure. It had a small "abnormality" on it but nothing he said they would keep me for, but they want me to see a cardiologist because the newest EKG "had a slight change" from the one I had done in January. Not sure what that means or what to think. Obviously they don't think it's anything urgent because they still have not called to schedule the consultation.

Any thoughts on this new symptom of acid? I have stopped all supplements for now to give my tummy a rest. They also had me on a liquid antacid, which is causing loose stools because of all the magnesium, I guess.

Karen from Arizona

> Effects of wheat on me:
