# Thread: Lambs heart
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lambs-heart.8601/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthman — 2013-10-29T20:42:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lambs-heart.8601/post-99370>

Have just eaten this raw and must admit was not a nice taste.
Anyone else tried it?

### caroline — 2013-10-29T22:16:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lambs-heart.8601/post-99385>

yep - not my fave....but not too bad! I make it like steak tartare.......

### Inger — 2013-10-30T07:18:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lambs-heart.8601/post-99400>

Oh yes. That is what I eat every day right now because they are "in season" here. I eat it for my meat, almost everything else I eat is from the sea right now. The hearts are very cheap, I got 1 organic lamb heart, 1 kidney and 1 liver for only 4 â‚¬ package. I got me 10 of them, going to freeze something too. This is so cheap food, and it is so good for me. I do not care too much what it tastes like. I usually add some organic balsamic vinegar from 100% grapes, and salt and raw onion and dried wild mushrooms. Makes it delicious! That was my breakfast today... with a huge slice of grass fed beef fat... bigger than my hand. That fat I just munched on raw and plain... tastes like puff pastry to me..yum!

You could try to slice it up in mouth pieces and fry on low heat just a tiny bit, so it still stays a bit raw, like medium. Just salt and pepper, maybe some curcumin to taste. I have tried that and the taste is great! Makes the hard fat around the heart melt a bit and get so yummy.

### healthman — 2013-10-30T13:03:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lambs-heart.8601/post-99408>

Thanks for the replies
I ended up cutting the rest of the heart into small pieces and swallowing whole with a mouthful of water. Same with liver until I get used to eating it raw will have to make do with swallowing small pieces down
I am now eating 3 raw oysters every day and that took some getting used to...But eat prawns, mussels, kelp, dulse seaweed and cockles daily without any problems

A few questions please

How much raw liver can you eat each week/day safely?
The same question regarding lambs heart?
Anyone tried raw kidney?

Many thanks

### veelansu — 2013-10-30T19:27:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lambs-heart.8601/post-99437>

I love raw oysters and raw scallops, they taste about the same. I have never eaten raw lamb heart. I am trying to find a place to buy these things.

### endless — 2013-12-07T06:56:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lambs-heart.8601/post-102678>

I agree with Inger...organ meat is an aquired taste, especially raw, but making it like a tartare or ceviche with added flavor helps! I think I will try your method of swallowing with water for the liver though....I love it cooked but raw it's a bit strong for me.

I think raw liver 2-3 times a week is plenty. I'm guessing 2oz or so at a time? Heart I think you could eat every day with no problems....
Raw kidneys? Nope. I can't stomach kidneys, they taste like pee to me

### Inger — 2013-12-07T10:22:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lambs-heart.8601/post-102693>

Just had a breakfast consisting of raw grassfed beef heart plain with just a few grains of salt (I actually like it better without salt), some thin slices of raw onion, and sliced raw grassfed beef fat plain. A wooden plate full... eaten as finger food. YUM!
I think too, heart you can safely eat every day. Liver you will feel when you have enough, so no worry. Some get loose stools when they have too much liver so maybe the body just do not absorb it when too much, IDK? It will just not appeal anymore. That is how it works for me at least. I eat liver in periods, sometimes a lot and sometimes no. I sometimes eat liver for dessert..lol plain, raw and sliced, so sweet like chocolate! Yesterday for dinner I had a big glass of raw fish head smoothie, then a glass of seaweed smoothie, and for dessert a cup of sliced raw liver.. and an hour later some Brazil nuts but they was not satisfying me and I felt I should rather have left them out so I made me some thin raw beef fat slices and that was sooo yummy! haha.. pretty weird menu

Raw kidneys are not my favorite either.. tastes like pee to me too! I think you could put them in water for a while to lessen the pee taste but I have not tried that. I eat them seldom and when - I usually hold my nose.

I think it can take some time to get used to a new way of eating, and new tastes, so never give up if it tastes weird to a beginning. It usually changes when you keep eating it and all of a sudden you like it

### SeaHorse — 2013-12-07T14:53:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lambs-heart.8601/post-102714>

I've found that pork kidneys taste the best raw to me....lamb is good slightly cooked only, or swallowed without really tasting. Any kind of heart still remains my favorite for eating raw...

# Thread: Altitude Training or Enhanced Oxygen Training with Cold
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/altitude-training-or-enhanced-oxygen-training-with-cold.8605/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ColinGorham — 2013-10-29T21:59:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/altitude-training-or-enhanced-oxygen-training-with-cold.8605/post-99382>

**Question: Altitude Training or Enhanced Oxygen Training with Cold?**

Question: I have an Altitude Machine which can simulate oxygen concentrations at 1) Sea Level to 21,000 ft or 2) Increase the Oxygen concentration of Ambient Air up to 40 percent. While peddling on the Elliptical & wearing an Ice Vest, I practice Intermittent Hypoxia, Hypoxic Workouts, or Exercise with Oxygen Training (EWOT). (Everything done simulating low altitude/ mildly hypoxic air. Always use a pulse oximeter, never go below 85 percent blood oxygen saturation) From the perspective of body re-composition, what exercise scenario would be optimal?

Note: Not asking for medical advice, I just want to hear Jack Kruse's general feeling about this from a theoretical perspective.

More Info:
Hypoxic Workouts (HW). HW involves riding a stationary bicycle or running on a treadmill three times per week while breathing hypoxic air. HW can substantially lower your blood oxygen saturation, which may stimulate additional production of red blood cells. Mountain climbers benefit from hypoxic workouts on inclined treadmill with backpack to simulate high-altitude hiking.

Intermittent Hypoxic Training (IHT). In IHT, you repeat short intervals (typically 5 min on, 5 min off) breathing moderately to highly hypoxic air (as little as 10% oxygen). Interval length and oxygen level are adjusted to achieve a target oxygen saturation in the blood, typically 85% to 90%. You measure blood oxygen saturation with a pulse oximeter.

EWOT. Exercising while breathing oxygen concentrations higher than ambient air.

### ColinGorham — 2013-10-29T22:01:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/altitude-training-or-enhanced-oxygen-training-with-cold.8605/post-99384>

I plan to add grounding to the protocol in the future.

### RickD — 2013-10-30T03:55:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/altitude-training-or-enhanced-oxygen-training-with-cold.8605/post-99397>

Colin,

I think I know what Jack will say, but since I own an altitude tent and have used it extensively for both training and mountaineering I figured I'd toss in my two cents as well. The standard way to use it is to sleep high, train low. For training I'd set it to about 8000 ft and the results were great. Living at Sea Level I had no altitude acclimation issues using that protocol. For Mountaineering I'd set it to 14000 ft and again had zero acclimation issues once on the mountain. For me if I increased the altitude further I had problems sleeping which defeats the purpose of using the tent.

To research your question further look to the mountaineering community. That's an activity that literally destroys your body no matter how good of shape your in. After 1-2 weeks above 14000 ft you literally start to waste away because your body simply can't recover from even mild exertion. My advice is then not to do intervals at altitude. Training is hard enough and any serious endurance athlete is always on the edge of becoming overtrained. Hammering out intervals with limited oxygen will just put you that much closer to being overtrained. And take it from me crossing that line is something you want to avoid at all costs.

I also wear an ice vest while doing some indoor interval work. Most times it's on my kayak ergometer and I find the cooling actually helps the interval performance. Not sure though this is really that helpful from a CT perspective.

### ColinGorham — 2013-10-31T17:08:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/altitude-training-or-enhanced-oxygen-training-with-cold.8605/post-99534>

Thank you Rick D for the great perspective on altitude training. I tend to agree. In my opinion, interval training (HIIT) while breathing hypoxic air is not necessary and potentially dangerous. HIIT on its own drops oxygen saturation quickly during the short intense burst section of the workout, and coupled with hypoxic air you could bring oxygen down too low to the point where you could pass out. Instead, I use Hypoxic Workouts (steady state cardio while breathing hypoxic air) or Intermittent Hypoxia (stead state cardio while intermittently breathing hypoxic air). From a cardiovascular & oxygen saturation standpoint, doing steady state cardio while breathing hypoxic air intermittently is somewhat similar to a HIIT workout.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

### [email redacted] — 2013-10-30T03:01:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-mushrooms.8607/post-99394>

I saw in a post that Jack highly recommended that Martin start eating oyster mushrooms this winter but I never did see why. Does anyone know why these would be important? I know that he is dealing with a biotoxin issue, so are these type of mushrooms helpful?

Any insight would be great. Thanks!

### freesia — 2013-10-30T03:30:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-mushrooms.8607/post-99396>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oyster-mushrooms.8607/post-99405>

# Thread: Antibiotics alternatives?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antibiotics-alternatives.8618/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sammy — 2013-10-31T08:27:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antibiotics-alternatives.8618/post-99498>

Hi all,
I have an infected blister on my thumb joint. Is there anything I can do apart from taking antibiotics to cure it?? I quite reluctant to take then as my gut already pretty bad.... am having exams now so Im praying it will not become worse.....
Thankyou hope someone can help

### Inger — 2013-10-31T08:51:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antibiotics-alternatives.8618/post-99500>

Sammy, I would personally not take antibiotics for that... Try to put on liquid Iodine! Like Lugol's. That should work  Clay can also be a good idea, or raw organic honey

### Sammy — 2013-10-31T10:02:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antibiotics-alternatives.8618/post-99504>

I am trying manuka honey... Hope it gets better! Unfortunately i dont have lugols iodine or clay

### Sammy — 2013-11-07T09:40:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antibiotics-alternatives.8618/post-100054>

Hi everyone. Just wanted to update the infection has nearly recovered. I didn"t end up taking antibiotics, even though when I posted this my blister was so badly infected(pus, thumb swelled to double the size, couldnt flex it without hurting!) thought i was going to get septicaemia or something!
Anyways, that night i tried adding honey( i used manuka honey with a umf of 15) and added turmeric to make a paste and applied it to my thumb. Wrapped a bandage around it(loosely to allow it to breathe) and went to bed with it. When i woke up the next day, i could flex my thumb more without it hurting as much. I"ve been doing this for the past week with great results.
Just wanted to share this with everyone

### nonchalant — 2013-11-07T16:11:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/antibiotics-alternatives.8618/post-100075>

Great, Sammy! You might also think about icing your hand. Put it in ice water for a while. Several times a day.

# Thread: Having labs drawn
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-labs-drawn.8627/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Bonniebelle — 2013-10-31T21:21:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-labs-drawn.8627/post-99552>

I am having labs drawn on Tuesday and according to the office manager the following is what is included in their ultimate new patient evaluation. This doctor does not accept insurance so it will cost me $680 of pocket, but does include detailed history and physical, labs, and treatment plan. This is the doctor my sisters go to, they both drive over 2 hrs to see her and say that she has helped them tremendously. Her main focus is preventative care and she uses both allopathic/conventional medicine and alternative/complementary therapies...she does things like IV glutathione, IV Vitamin C, chelation therapy, prolotherapy, breast thermography, diet and nutritional analysis, allergy testing and food sensitivity testing, environmental toxins assessment, vitamin/mineral/amino acid profiles, heavy metal assessment, GI analysis, genomic testing, hormone testing, and bioelectrical impedence analysis

**Option 1 Total cost = $680**

The following Blood work plus physical and treatment plan = $475

1\. Comprehensive Metabolic Panel- I guess this would be Chemistry profile and where the BUN/Creatinine ratio would be covered
2\. CBC with diff
3\. GGT- tells about liver function and bile ducts I think
4\. LDH- test to check for tissue damage of heart, liver, kidney, muscles, brain, blood cells, and lungs
5\. VAP - Comprehensive Lipid Profile, homocystein, HS-CRP
6\. Ferritin - Iron
7\. HGA1C - to evaluate blood sugar
8\. Vit D
9\. G6PD- A G6PD test measures the levels of glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase (G6PD), an enzyme (type of protein) in the blood.
10\. Lymphcyte subset - determines immune status
11\. Comprehensive Thyroid panel - includes reverse T3, TSH, Free T4, Free T3, Total T4, Thyroid antibodies (ATA and TPO)

I am adding in saliva testing to check hormones (progesterone, estradiol, DHEA, cortisol) = an extra $205

Is there anything else I should request that isn't covered by the above?

**Option 2. Total cost = around $820**

Another option is to go with the anti-aging doctor that I saw previously. He is also out of network, so not covered by insurance until I meet the deductible, then it covers 80%, but he is right down the street. The last time I saw him by the time I met the deductible a new year was starting so in essence insurance didn't cover any of it and I had to pay everything out of pocket even my labs. He does offer a new option where the lab will bill insurance directly. If insurance pays I would just have to pay $175 processing fee, if insurance covers nothing I would have to pay an additional $364 or less whatever insurance covers (so worst case scenario is $539 for labs if insurance won't cover anything). Since I have previously seen him, his follow up appointment fee is $275 for a 30-60 minute office visit. So total of labs/office visit would be around $820. He would be doing 24 hr. urine specimen for hormones and cortisol (because insurance won't cover saliva testing but should cover urine testing, blood work would be thyroid panel, HS-CRP, Vitamin D. This doctor's focus is really just hormone status. He previously specialized in internal medicine, then sports medicine, and now anti-aging/hormone status.

**I'm thinking that the best option is to go with the first doctor which covers more and costs less, even though I would be driving from Birmingham to Montgomery to see this doctor. After I get this initial testing and treatment plan maybe I can just go to my regular internist for followup testing which would be covered by insurance and only cost me a $25 co-pay. The internist just practices conventional medicine though, so not sure how open he would be to all of these tests.**

Thanks for any feedback on this! I want to make sure I'm covering all the labs that I need.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-10-31T21:47:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-labs-drawn.8627/post-99557>

Before making a decision, I highly suggest that you purchase the Hormones webinar. Lots of good info on how to select (and interview) a doc.

Also, making a selection based primarily on cost could be counter productive and more expensive in the long term. Would you select a surgeon for an important operation (assuming not an emergency) based on price??

Finally, you might find it more cost effective to buy your labs online at discount prices ... not sure if they would qualify towards your deductible. But I assume that most docs will add a markup to the price they charge for labs.

Choosing a doc is an important decision. Take your time, do your research, and then make a choice based on the doc you feel can best work with you as a partner.

### MsYo — 2013-10-31T22:06:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-labs-drawn.8627/post-99558>

hsCrp is recommended. It is a test for inflammation and very important.
The day you do hormone testing is important if you are still menstruating (day 3-4 or day 21 suggested). If you go to an in-network physician and the test are considered "wellness" testing that may be more cost effective. Review your benefits plan to see what works for you. I have both an in-network physician(anti-aging) and one out of network(physician/natural medicine/endo). As long as I go to a network lab, my insurance pays accordingly. I pay the doctor fees and apply to my expenses for the year.

Saliva testing for hormones is not the most accurate (per some info posted on the forum). I think blood serum was preferred. Check to be sure.

### Bonniebelle — 2013-10-31T22:25:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-labs-drawn.8627/post-99561>

@ ATL\_Paleo - Thanks for your reply. I'm not basing it just on cost...I was trying to look at what tests were covered and what the doctor's overall background was relative to the price, that is why I provided a little bit about their practice and what each option covered. If I was basing it just on cost I would have just gone to my internist, would only have to pay a $25 co pay for office visit and labs and call it a day, but I feel like he would not give me the in depth analysis that the other two doctors would provide, so that is why I am considering paying the costs out of pocket to get a more comprehensive treatment plan. So I guess I don't understand why you think I'm just basing the decision on cost. I'm basing it on much more than cost, but let's be honest, in today's economy the cost of medical care does play a role in decisions for those of us who do not have unlimited financial resources.

What discount online lab company do you recommend? For labs to be covered by insurance, it would have to be a lab that was considered in-network (We have Blue Cross Blue Shield of Alabama). I have looked at ordering the tests through Life Extension but I don't see any cost savings to doing it that way from what I can tell by looking at the listed cost of labs.

I guess I should have stated that I have met both the doctors. Doctor #1 met with our whole family for over 2 hrs and did not even charge anything when my mom was diagnosed with pancreatic cancer to help her with nutrition and discuss what the options were on alternative treatments besides just the conventional surgery, chemo, radiation for pancreatic cancer, plus my sisters have been seeing her for 10+ years. I like the fact that she specializes in preventative care and she went into medicine and particularly this type of practice because her father died at a young age from heart disease. Instead of just writing a prescription as bandaid therapy, she actually tries to look at the underlying problem instead of just symptoms. Doctor # 2 I saw twice about 1.5 to 2 years ago and while I liked him, he really just seemed focused on hormones. I quit seeing him due to a drastic change in my mother's condition- I just sort of put my health on the back burner while attending to her, plus my husband was pressuring me about the fact that seeing him was not covered by insurance and to see him several times a year was expensive. Both doctors were easy to talk to and I felt both listened to my concerns, but they each bring different things to the table. My husband is more open to me seeing a doctor that doesn't accept insurance now, so at least that won't be an issue this time, but at this time we still don't have unlimited funds to spend for medical care.

I guess my basic question should be.....are there any crucial lab tests that I need to ask about that aren't included above?

### Bonniebelle — 2013-10-31T22:39:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-labs-drawn.8627/post-99562>

@MsYo - Thanks for the recommendations.

HS CRP is included in both.

I am no longer menstruating... Date of last period was February 2013.

I don't think my insurance covers "wellness" testing. I will need to double check this though.

I thought saliva testing or 24 hr urine testing was preferred over serum for hormone testing due to fact that our hormones are cyclic and levels fluctuate through the day. Just googled it and found this out from Meridian Valley Lab (which I think is the company that Dr. Tim Jackson listed in resources in the Hormone Q& A.) but I will need to research this further.

[http://meridianvalleylab.com/about-hormone-testing](http://meridianvalleylab.com/about-hormone-testing)

From this article it sounds like urine testing is the most accurate, but I will have to research this further.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-10-31T23:29:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-labs-drawn.8627/post-99564>

The point I was trying to make is that finding a good alternative care doc is very challenging. Many people have tried to save money by using docs who were in-network. Sometimes this works, but many have found the doc who was right for them only accepted cash. Good to hear that your husband is now open to this possibility.

Perhaps Gretchen's Doctors Boot Camp can provide some assistance. The website has a special the bootcamp that expires today (Oct 31). [http://www.jackkruse.com/doctors-insurance-bootcamp-special-offer/](http://www.jackkruse.com/doctors-insurance-bootcamp-special-offer/)

I order my labs through Life Extension. Direct Labs also has very good pricing.

Regarding which labs, Dr. Kruse's blog on Optimizing Labs is a great place to start. [http://www.jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/](http://www.jackkruse.com/what-are-the-optimizing-labs/)

### Bonniebelle — 2013-10-31T23:37:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-labs-drawn.8627/post-99565>

@MsYo - I'm really not sure what is the most accurate...saliva vs. 24 hr urine vs serum. I'm seeing articles for pros and cons of each. Because of this conflicting info I called both doctor's office managers because I have no clue which route to go. I was a nurse, but my specialty was neonatal intensive care and I'm not sure which is the preferred method of testing these days. Doctor #1 prefers saliva testing because it looks at intracellular levels and shows more about what is actually bioavailable, she does not like to use serum for hormone levels due to the fluctuation of hormones through out the day and the fact that serum tests are a measure of extracellular levels. Doctor #2 uses both saliva and urine, he does not like serum for hormones either due to the fluctuations of hormones throughout the day. From what I am finding on the internet is sounds like each doctor has their own preference and that there are pros and cons for each of the methods of collection. I'm definitely open to hearing anyone else's point of view on this topic though

I felt much better after talking to both office managers. Both doctors are open to me having follow-up lab work done by my internist so that the lab work will definitely be covered by insurance when it is time to retest, that will relieve my husband! After thinking about this and talking to both offices my decision is to go with Doctor #1, just because I think she is more comprehensive and I really like the amount of time she puts into sitting down with each patient and how much research she puts into finding the underlying cause of a patient's symptoms. I feel much better about the decision after writing this all out and getting feedback. Thanks to all those who added input into this discussion! It really helped me think about one option over the other and what I felt "in my gut" was the right option for me. It will be worth it to spend the extra money for more in-depth initial testing. Thanks again!

### Bonniebelle — 2013-10-31T23:44:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/having-labs-drawn.8627/post-99566>

@ATL\_Paleo - thanks! You are right...finding a good doctor is very challenging! Thanks for the suggestions about the bootcamp, webinar and blogs, I will definitely look at those and thanks for the suggestion about the different lab companies too. I may look at that for follow up lab work if my in-network physician doesn't want to do some of the testing on follow-up. I just want to get initial testing done as soon as possible before I get too much further into the Leptin Rx so that I can see where I am starting from and be able to tell what progress has been made  Thanks again for your input and suggestions!

### caroline — 2013-11-04T08:25:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocker-prescription-glasses.8650/post-99784>

Has any one got blueblocker prescription glasses? I have to get new glasses so want to get this.

I was pretty confused....

do I just want a yellow tint? antifatigue? U.V. guard?

there are some new lenses called "blue control" they apparently have a bit of a blue tint and are for use with computers, ipads, iphone etc. The optometrist seemed to think they are a gimmick.

He isn't so sure about all of this - but is lovely - and will go with what I want ....but...what do I want???
I just asked him to pretend that he does agree with me!!

any help would be greatly appreciated..... thanx

Gretchen - I know you do ...I tried to pm you - but of course it still isn't working.....

### Lahelada — 2013-11-04T12:34:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocker-prescription-glasses.8650/post-99792>

We have acompany here that supposedly makes them but a quick google tells me that this would be a very uncommon thing. Failing everything ,Blueblocker do clip on sunglasses in varying sizes . That might be an option.

### Ralef — 2013-11-04T21:13:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocker-prescription-glasses.8650/post-99825>

i have blue blocker glasses from the optometrist. Usually they are used to help with retina diseases, if you have such a disease they are prescribed here in germany. I had paid it on my own. I'm wearing them at the computer at work and on my way home when its dark after sunset. i paid 600 € for them. manufactorers for the glasses are Multilens, Zeiss, Rodenstock and Essilor.

### Shijin13 — 2013-11-05T00:15:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocker-prescription-glasses.8650/post-99854>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=99784)
>
> Has any one got blueblocker prescription glasses? I have to get new glasses so want to get this.
>
> I was pretty confused....
>
> do I just want a yellow tint? antifatigue? U.V. guard?
>
> there are some new lenses called "blue control" they apparently have a bit of a blue tint and are for use with computers, ipads, iphone etc. The optometrist seemed to think they are a gimmick.
>
> He isn't so sure about all of this - but is lovely - and will go with what I want ....but...what do I want???
> I just asked him to pretend that he does agree with me!!
>
> any help would be greatly appreciated..... thanx
>
> Gretchen - I know you do ...I tried to pm you - but of course it still isn't working.....

Caroline

yes I have these here's what you want to ask for: Blue Blocker w/ Antireflective coating (pinkish orange tint is the color of the lenses - they're not amber - but they block all blue/violet light and some greens)

The provider didn't really stuff up it just used a non adjusted (for daylight savings) default. Perhaps with us having recently gone forward, and the Northern peeps about to go back it makes sense to use a default.

Yes I'll look after you both...I'm from the future so it's my duty!!

@caroline: i think the glasses are from essilor, i must search for the bill. The glasses work very well for me. First day it was strange and i have to get used to it, but after that i feel much better when i use them, especally because i have to use the computer much at work. Co-Workers finding it strange at first, but get used to it fast too. My optometrist is specialized on that glasses for eye diseases. Blue Blocking for prevention was new to him, but he listened and finds it interesting. the glasses are in a middle dark orange, i choose a good grade of blue blocking. They aren't t really dark like sunglasse. they are blocking the relevant range of blue lights.

Here is the website of my optometrist: [http://www.optikbaart.de/](http://www.optikbaart.de/). It is in german language. Look for "Kantenfilter", perhaps the site is helping you? Or look at the manufacturers site, i think they have information in english.

Sure you can ask more questions, i check internet sometimes daily, sometimes every other day.

### freesia — 2013-11-05T19:08:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocker-prescription-glasses.8650/post-99953>

No you don't need to know what time zone he's in - you just need the **start time** in the zone specified \- and the **current time**. Then, the difference is how many hours you have before it starts.

### Fish — 2014-12-02T05:21:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocker-prescription-glasses.8650/post-148990>

Since a mother works in IT, I can’t ignore my eye problems. I have to stay in the front of computer for more than 8 hours, and also feed my baby girl. After I checked the danger of blue light for human health ([http://www.taimeopt.com/dangers-of-blue-light-with-human-health/](http://www.taimeopt.com/dangers-of-blue-light-with-human-health/)), I chose T'aime Dinodon eyewear to block blue light when staring at a computer. It states to block 97% of blue light. Actually I haven’t seen the whole test process, I just refer to a video which uses a spectrometer to test how the glasses work to filter the blue light from a computer screen.

I really enjoy it. As for the anti-fatigue and UV guard you talked about, Dinodon is more than enough. Just hope it helps.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-heat-or-cool-a-building-when-you-could-heat-or-cool-a-person.8656/post-99881>

MIT wristband could make A/C obsolete ...

Wristify, as they call their device, is a thermoelectric bracelet that regulates the temperature of the person wearing it by subjecting their skin to alternating pulses of hot or cold, depending on whatâ€™s needed.

[http://www.wired.com/design/2013/10...dy-at-the-perfect-temperature-no-ac-required/](http://www.wired.com/design/2013/10/an-ingenious-wristband-that-keeps-your-body-at-the-perfect-temperature-no-ac-required/)

........ for some reason, all I can think of is EMF

### fitness@home — 2013-11-05T03:34:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-heat-or-cool-a-building-when-you-could-heat-or-cool-a-person.8656/post-99893>

### prAna303 — 2013-11-05T10:51:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-heat-or-cool-a-building-when-you-could-heat-or-cool-a-person.8656/post-99909>

When the chaos is rising we invent some perverse items, this is another one. Instead of letting the chaos decrease we do the opposite with brand new technology. Another solution to a problem that really do not exist.

### Martin — 2013-11-05T14:37:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-heat-or-cool-a-building-when-you-could-heat-or-cool-a-person.8656/post-99921>

> [prAna303 said:](/goto/post?id=99909)
>
> When the chaos is rising we invent some perverse items, this is another one. Instead of letting the chaos decrease we do the opposite with brand new technology. Another solution to a problem that really do not exist.

I love gadgets, always have. If I have a problem, I want a tool or a clever device to help me fix it, or better yet do it for me while I walk around with it in my pocket or around my neck.

But all that time I spend thinking, gaining knowledge, even buying some gizmo, is not actually _doing_ something from Nature, like CT, seasonal eating, circadian awareness, no technology, etc.

Rubber shoes, fancy cars, and life-size inflatable vinyl dolls don't hold a candle to the real thing. I know, I've tried them all!

The Head

### Hemming — 2013-11-05T15:29:02+0200

I recently [reported in the Kruse' CT forums](http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?8225-The-Cool-Fat-Burner-undergoes-indirect-calorimetry-guess-what-happened-%29) that the Cool Fat Burner boosted metabolism by 300% as verified by indirect calorimetry. (However, I put a _""_ in the title, which made the thread inaccessible, and IDK how to edit the title!)

Anyhow, I recently ran another formal self-experiment, and showed that I was able to [raise Adiponectin by 62%](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wfkM2NExE5Y) in around 2hrs. This is a HUGE confirmation of the power of cold thermogenesis!!!

You can check out info in the video or on the [blog entry](https://coolfatburner.com/Blog.htm#The_Cool_Fat_Burner_BOOSTS_adiponectin).

thanks
Eric

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-via-the-cool-fat-burner-vest-increases-adiponectin-by-62.8672/post-150271>

I'm in awe. WOW! The eighteen scoops of ice cream and the 60 lbs of ice cream in 6 weeks,notwithstanding, you've validated the last forty- four months of my life. I've simultaneously gained close to twenty five lb of muscle and lost almost ten lbs of fat for a 16 lb weight gain.I used the cold tub(not the vest) and skipped the junk food and probably 90% of the weightlifting.But still, this is something I can show my personal fitness clients as an alternative/complimentary success story, that reinforces my own, more"monastic/Spartan" modalities. Great work.

### Danco3636 — 2014-12-19T01:53:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-via-the-cool-fat-burner-vest-increases-adiponectin-by-62.8672/post-150569>

I am doing a 4 week trial myself with the CFB to see if it produces any spot reduction of fat. I am already lean - 6-8% BF but wee will see how it goes.
In the AM each day I do a short CT in my 50 degree F pool and then put the vest and shoulder packs on for 1 hour or more.... I later in the evening may wear it for 30 minutes.

### Butters — 2014-12-20T18:08:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-via-the-cool-fat-burner-vest-increases-adiponectin-by-62.8672/post-150679>

### Danco3636 — 2014-12-20T20:14:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-via-the-cool-fat-burner-vest-increases-adiponectin-by-62.8672/post-150699>

This morning dunk in my just below 50 degree F pool then CF burner and Gut buster on for one and half hours while in a 62 degree room.
Drank cold water pre.
Wrapped a neoprene wrap around the Gut Buster to make it tighter on stomach and back.
Added some extra, small ice packs on shoulders.
After 30-40 minutes slight shivering.
Did this while naked, doing some outside grounding and body movements.
Incorporated ice water face dunks as well.
Followed it up with green tea and coffee.
Done in a fasted state and stayed fasted for 3 or so hours after.
Then a Epi-Paleo Ketosis meal. Yes oysters where involved ➖

Most of the ice packs where melted or slushy when taken off.

This could be addicting.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-via-the-cool-fat-burner-vest-increases-adiponectin-by-62.8672/post-224861>

> [JoeT said:](/goto/post?id=223355)
>
> Eric, I'm looking to try to use cold to get my blood sugar, which is "normal", down to very low levels. (i.e: HBA1C below 4.7 ish? ). Do you have any idea as to whether the vest might be able to help me vs having to sit in a CT tub? Thx.

Back when I was constantly monitoring and measuring this stuff, my resting BGL was in the high 60's or low 70's. Even if I'd do one of my crazy refeeds, the BGL wouldn't go too high, and would return to normal within a few hrs, max. Granted, I was strategically doing those cheat meals, along w/ CT and exercise.

Regardless of the method (tub, vest, sparse clothing in cold environment, etc.), the changes to insulin sensitivity, glucose uptake, BGL and so on, appear to pretty constant per intensity level (non-shivering, pre-shivering, various levels of shivering), regardless of the method used to induce the particular level of cold.

# Thread: New labs option: Blueprint for Wellness
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-labs-option-blueprint-for-wellness.8677/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### scottconnerly — 2013-11-06T00:17:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-labs-option-blueprint-for-wellness.8677/post-99971>

I'd like to offer up another possible source for personal laboratory testing: Quest Diagnostics has started offering [direct pay personal lab tests](https://my.blueprintforwellness.com/direct/) nationwide.

I think the best test for us would be their Heart and Diabetes Screen. It includes

-   Total cholesterol, High Density Lipoprotein Cholesterol (HDL), calculated Low Density Lipoprotein Cholesterol (LDL), Triglycerides, calculated Cholesterol/HDL ratio
-   Diabetes (Pancreas): Glucose
-   Blood pressure
-   Body Mass Index (BMI)
-   Waist Circumference

If anybody tries them out, let me know how it goes, how it compares to the other labs services.

Cortisol levels high out of range = lots of stress ...... number one cause is your workouts

DHEA level is very low .... the rest of your hormone panels probably are horrible

Your symptoms and labs do not bode for a healthy future .... when you will make the required changes???

Megan - I use the following to help me understand the steroidal hormone chain. [http://fdnmanager.com/steroidal\_hormone\_balance.pdf](http://fdnmanager.com/steroidal_hormone_balance.pdf)

Jansz really hits it on the head. If you want to stop feeling fatigued - you're going to have to cut back on your exercise until your body heals. Everything you're doing exercise wise is nullifying all the efforts of following the LRrx, Adrenal Rx and eating epipaleo.

Many have told you this before... I think you need to really sit down - and identify what your goals are. Do you want to be an elite endurance athlete - with serious health issue? if so, keep doing what you're doing. Or Do you want to get your health back? If you want to get your health back, you might need to put your athletic goals to the side until you get your health back.. once its back, then you will probably want to slowly re-enter your training - and monitor closely w/labs - probably on a monthly basis to determine if you're sending your body back to where it was before. The reality is it will probably take a while to right this course. you not only have to deal with the stress of EMF and your environment, but the additional stress you're putting on your body through the excessive endurance training.

As Jack has said - the truth sucks - The question is you've got two options, which one are you going to choose? Stay the course of your current effort - and continue feeling fatigued and having issues or change the course and focus on healing yourself...

DHEA 69.8: 6am-9pm..... Reference Range 23.8-291.1

Get blood test for
DHEAs,serum

You may be low, take DHEA pills, aim at
Women 275-400 μg/dL
Men 350-490 μg/dL

I do not know your age. Do not be misled by laboratory range for DHEAs that is age adjusted.
If you are low on DHEAs, 50-100mg/day supplementation is not out of question for women, much more for men.
\--------------------------------
On day 2-4 of your menstrual cycle, get blood test for:
pregnenolone
progesterone

You must have at the minimum progesterone=1.5ng/mL or real close to it.
Otherwise, if pregnenolone is low, supplement with (Pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix from Nutricology).
\-------------------------------

Get 24hr urine test for cortisol, you may be high.

\-------------------------------
Creatinine 49.6 .... Reference Range 28-259

Lowish, hopefully because you are underweight, not enough proteins, high stomach acid.

> Low-Protein Diet
>
> Diets that are low in protein can cause low creatinine levels. Foods that are high in creatine phosphate include salmon, pork, tuna and beef, all of which are high in protein. Because these foods are not included in a low-protein diet, there is less creatine phosphate to break down, resulting in reduced creatinine levels.
>
>
> Pregnancy
>
> Pregnancy may cause low creatinine levels in the blood. Once the fetus starts developing in the womb, nutrients are transported from the mother to the baby via the umbilical cord. Pregnant women may have less creatine phosphate to break down due to the changes in the body. Reduced creatine phosphate breakdown results in reduced levels of creatinine. Once childbirth has occurred, creatinine levels should return to normal unless other causes of low creatinine levels exist.
>
> Decreased Muscle Mass
>
> Creatinine levels are usually higher in men because their muscle mass is higher than that of women. In people who have decreased muscle mass, creatinine levels may be low. Aging causes a natural decline in the amount of muscle mass in the body. Some medical conditions also cause wasting of the muscles and a reduction in muscle mass. Muscular dystrophy and myasthenia gravis are examples of diseases that can cause decreased muscle mass. Disabilities that limit movement can also cause reduced muscle mass due to lack of muscle use.
>
> Liver Disease
>
> Liver disease can contribute to a decline in serum creatinine levels. When liver disease becomes advanced, the organ stops producing as much creatinine. According to Dr. Kevin Pho, who is certified by the American Board of Internal Medicine, liver disease can also result in decreased muscle mass. Diseases that can affect the liver include fatty liver disease, hepatitis and metabolic disorders such as Wilson's disease and hemochromatosis.

### RickD — 2013-11-06T18:31:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-stage-of-adrenal-fatigue-i-feel-like-its-2-or-3-neurotransmitters.8678/post-99996>

Meghan,

I'm not going to tell you to quit training because I probably better than most understand how important it is to you. But from the symptoms you list above and your labs my guess is your results have not met your expectations so you need to do something different. One size does not fit all when it comes to training. Some elite triathletes, cyclists and Nordic skiers can thrive, at least in the short term, on 1500 hrs of training a year while others break down with more than 6-700hrs. My advice is take a break and get your health back then go out and get a coach who will work with you one on one to maximize your potential.

### prAna303 — 2013-11-07T10:39:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-stage-of-adrenal-fatigue-i-feel-like-its-2-or-3-neurotransmitters.8678/post-100057>

Ok, just my idea...

The problem is that i do not know you in person, i can not see you with my own eyes, i can not see your music if we put it that way.

I do meet a lot of elite persons, in sports, in dance, in music and in many other branches. I feel your problems on a daily basis and the big thing is that we are different persons with different triggers. With so many factors behind illness today one must find the things that makes your own life going down... Today we see people with great passion crash and burn because they can not get it right, we think alot, read alot, listen alot but mostly to factors made up by others, by golden rules. When did you have a chance to listen to your own inner voice?

The training is a trouble for you now, at the same time your passion. This combination is making your head hurt also and the chaos inside of your is rising, so you need to calm that storm. Every time i talk to someone who is on the same course as you i feel that deep inside there is a call for help but not the outside world help, a call for letting the master piece become alive. This is a problem today because rules of the modern world often point towards a locked up creature in a cage. It is time for you to come out now.

So my way would be like this:

First of all, before anything, start with fixing your conductor in life, feed your brain. Food is just a small part but right now for you quite important. Put yourself in places that promotes wild food, fish, fats and good water. Second, life is energy and everything around us is some kind of that BUT make shure you think in a different way... You are not a combustion engine, by training you add entropy in your unit, by living you add entropy and maximum entropy is dead. Food can change the rules a bit but the big things is how and where you place the unit YOU. If your passion is training right now, put it in places where you reconnect with nature. Make your runs in the woods, mountains... Make your swimming in the sea not in the gym. The list goes on...

How do you live your days? Are you working the white collar way? Do you use technology? Are you living in a family? The point is that when your signal is sending at a frequency, your life is doing itÂ´s music it must match the world you are living in and with.

Do you get analog vibrations? If your passion today is training, go get energy from seeing performing arts, blend in with that kind of persons, you vibrate together, get that magic resonance the old school way. Remember that life feeds on negative entropy and music, singing and dancing is just some way to get that.

Get yourself on a trip, a hike on your own. Your body will hurt and in that process things will break down, your mind will be confused and stressed BUT in a natural way. Energy is in the nature and you feed up. Remember that we need sun, water and magnetism as pillars of life... Fix these three legs and you can over time build the fourth, the fifth and so on. Now you can see your training as a natural way of life. I guess you have lost some of the three right now and first your brains hurts, then your big muscle functions and so on... When the three parts is filled with natural energy you start to see life as sublime, the details gets clear because your mind is working at light speed...

I think that when someone like you get the chance to get the fundation worked up you see how your training either is good or bad for you. To say what you should do in the state you are in is difficult because the world is full of noice, and alot of noice is also loads of entropy.

I hope you post some clues about yourself, not only labs but the things that you really are, like what makes your soundtrack of your life. The big changes is in the world you put yourself in, what kind of fields you act in.

For you i share my own passion with these words:

\- Emotion thru motion -

>

>
> Bottom line ... you already know the answers.
>
> Why wait for some person or some event to "force" you to make a decision??? EMPOWER yourself and make the decision yourself. This decision will be life changing in more ways than one. Consider it the next chapter of your life .... one that you have the power to write yourself.

You are right the Answers are NO...my dad wants me to go release my medical safety to him.... and I don't even think I look healthy.... I hate how skinny and sickly I look I want to be a strong athlete but I look like a cancer patient.....
I guess I'm afraid to EMPOWER myself because I feel like "myself" has let me down these past few years and that's why I'm in this hole....

### MeghanK — 2013-11-07T17:08:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-stage-of-adrenal-fatigue-i-feel-like-its-2-or-3-neurotransmitters.8678/post-100080>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=100047)
>
> I really worry that you will have a heart attack or something equally catastrophic - and do so much damage it will be difficult to rewind.....if not impossible.....I don't want that for you.
>
> Do you live close to your Mom and Dad? Siblings?

You sound like my therapist and parents... I am close to my parents... they are in the burbs and I in the city but I have gotten "Life360" for them on my phone so they can track me......
I have tried to read EMF #4 and all the blogs but I get COMPLETELY OVERWHELMED by the info and then get OCD about the details and will feel guilty if I don't try CT even though it's miserable for me.... etc.....

The sad thing is, I know I am so much more than an athlete... I am SOOOO FREAKING CREATIVE.... I make cards and scrapbooks and crafts that would take your breath away but the exercise and athlete thing took over..... I am an identical twin who was the fighter... born at 1.9lbs...and had to fight 3 months in a incubator to SURVIVE while my sister thrived so I have spent my life trying to "SURVIVE" and also be UNIQUE....we always got attention... made EVERY TEAM, MVP, every boyfriend we wanted.... I NEVER FAILED... until now.....NOW, my sister is married, pregnant...lives in the cutest house and is a teacher.... ME... no boyfriend, no friends.... in and out of jobs, currently working at Trader Joes... and the only thing I have to show for the last 10 years is winning marathons, half ironmans, races etc. but NO BALANCE OR LIFE and now skinny, destroyed hormones etc......

### Inger — 2013-11-07T17:44:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-stage-of-adrenal-fatigue-i-feel-like-its-2-or-3-neurotransmitters.8678/post-100086>

Maybe... Is there anything you can help them with? Instead of using so much time for sport, do something good for your loved ones instead... or anyone in need. The world is full of needs... I think it is that easy, we just need to change stuff... and magic happens . Just start today, decide today you will do it different. Start with small things and then go from there. Of course you will need discipline... but I think that you have

Something I read on Jacks facebook site some time ago - start to love yourself dearly from today, because you deserve it

> Do not be afraid to be bold about your love for you. We can only love others as much as we love ourselves. Become immune to shame. Just as the sun does not shine for certain trees and flowers, love has no boundaries for any of us. If you are not good enough for you, who are you really good for?

The world desperately needs leaders.. and to become one we must get healthy in our mind, body and spirit... we are all on our way here, only the direction counts, not where you are on the road in comparison to others. Never compare yourself to others, but only to you

### Inger — 2013-11-07T17:48:56+0200

### cantweight — 2013-11-07T18:16:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-stage-of-adrenal-fatigue-i-feel-like-its-2-or-3-neurotransmitters.8678/post-100092>

> ..... it's about CONTROL....I want to be strong NOT SKINNY.....sadly I'm just making myself weaker and my mind KNOWS it but I am not changing????

It's about control yet you are not the one in the control. If you want control take it...its there just waiting. The only power your negative thoughts have is the power you give them every time you think them. Be done. Move on. Want to do something hard? CT.

I have an eight yr old son with neuro issues that gets in the CT tank with his little goggles on and practices how long he can hold his breath under water. He is the CT rockstar in our house and he is so proud of himself.

Do something to make yourself feel proud. It is an upward spiral, just get it going....it is amazing

### nicld — 2013-11-07T18:42:58+0200

Jack said that there is no real difference between and anorexic and an obese, both have messed up hormones and head issues.

### MsYo — 2013-11-07T21:27:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-stage-of-adrenal-fatigue-i-feel-like-its-2-or-3-neurotransmitters.8678/post-100118>

This thread touches my heart closely. I am a recovering exercise addict. I was overweight and after gastric bypass I found a new joy in competitive running and the gym was my "high". Much like the document Gretchen provided, I didn't realize the damage until my performance began to decline. My family life, career, and finally exercise was a source of stress. I discovered this blog in 2011 after I ran the 2010 Chicago Marathon with no training (also, lost my job 30 days later on the eve of my birthday). I kept trying to do cardio and added HCG to try to get back to what I thought was "normal"--and I realize the damage that I continued. Sometimes we have to do what doesn't feel "normal" in order to get on track to optimal. Read the link Gretchen posted, it really does serve as a reflection of both extremes in Adrenal Fatigue and personality types.

### caroline — 2013-11-07T23:20:40+0200

So much wonderful advice here Meghan - from people that care about you...

Could you drop out for awhile? maybe move back home with your parents? be with the people that love and support you the most......reconnect with your sister. Their heart is breaking for you and they would love an opportunity to hug you every single day for awhile!

You could run barefoot thru the streets in the "Burb" and hug trees .......makes friends with a bunch of children and teach them some stuff - you have so much to offer...

# Thread: Nature-Throid and Sleep - Am I imagining this?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nature-throid-and-sleep-am-i-imagining-this.8680/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mafixit — 2013-11-06T19:19:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nature-throid-and-sleep-am-i-imagining-this.8680/post-100001>

My BHRT doc put me on a small dose of Nature-Throid even though my Thyroid Labs are "normal". I swear that when I am on this med, I sleep deeper. Is this possible? I am on 1/2 of the 37.5 mg dose 1x per day in the morning.

I can't explain it, but I just feel like I am in a heavier state of sleep. I still wake up once a night to go pee, but I fall right back to sleep. And I find myself waking up to the alarm at 4:45 a.m., which is unusual, and I am not as anxious when I do wake up. When I am not on this stuff, I tend to wake up 45 minutes before the alarm with anxiety. Am I on to something?

Nothing else has changed med-wise. Still on my BHRT.

Karen from Arizona

### Shijin13 — 2013-11-06T20:52:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nature-throid-and-sleep-am-i-imagining-this.8680/post-100009>

I've slept better since being on Armour that coupled w/BHRT has helped with my migraines as well...

For me, open enrollment comparing options started with figuring out where I could find the information to begin my comparison. Iâ€™ve put together a matrix you can download to help you compare your options as well. /www.yourpaleohealthcare.com/open-enrollment-comparing-options/

### Shijin13 — 2013-11-12T00:04:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/open-enrollment-is-in-full-swing-newest-blog.8683/post-100459>

### Destiny — 2013-11-12T13:14:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/open-enrollment-is-in-full-swing-newest-blog.8683/post-100495>

My employer offered only one plan for next year, down from 3 plans last year. So I only had to pick from this one plan or go on the exchange.

I picked my employer's plan even though my plan jumped from monthly premiums of $225 to $425. My deductible went from 2K to 6K and out of pocket went from 4K to 9K. This will be paid for by before the tax dollars.
The affordable exchange plan was $600 a month and deductible 12K. Not sure if there was any out of the pocket maximum. All must be paid by after the tax dollars.

While the employer's plan is expensive, it is still cheaper than the "affordable" health plan.

### JanSz — 2013-11-12T16:23:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/open-enrollment-is-in-full-swing-newest-blog.8683/post-100502>

...

### nonchalant — 2013-11-12T17:23:07+0200

> [Destiny said:](/goto/post?id=100495)
>
> My employer offered only one plan for next year, down from 3 plans last year. So I only had to pick from this one plan or go on the exchange.
>
> I picked my employer's plan even though my plan jumped from monthly premiums of $225 to $425. My deductible went from 2K to 6K and out of pocket went from 4K to 9K. This will be paid for by before the tax dollars.
> The affordable exchange plan was $600 a month and deductible 12K. Not sure if there was any out of the pocket maximum. All must be paid by after the tax dollars.
>
> While the employer's plan is expensive, it is still cheaper than the "affordable" health plan.

I think a lot of people are finding this out. usually by now we've heard about our open enrollment for my company - and we haven't as of yet. I'm wondering what my lovely plans are going to look like.

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=100511)

choosing from 3 crappy plans , or only having 1 plan - at a reasonable monthly premium is definitely better than what I've been seeing on the exchange and the insurance companies websites.

# Thread: Kidney health supplement?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-health-supplement.8696/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### laker — 2013-11-08T07:21:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-health-supplement.8696/post-100170>

Worried about my 1.3 creatinine in recent labs. 26 yr old male. 68 gfr. Is the gfr measured on its own or only calculated from the creatinine levels? Could this be low because of not being well hydrated before the blood work?
96 fasting blood sugar. Following Paleo diet now for 10 months

UPDATED \_\_

I was fasting before the blood work

non black, low body fat, don't lift weights

UA was negative

BUN wasn't tested it seems ( these labs were one due to follow up @ kaiser )

I will get my BUN tested asap though!

What does this mean

### caroline — 2013-11-08T07:56:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-health-supplement.8696/post-100172>

Hi Laker ... and welcome! It would be great if you could start a journal in the journal section ..... go to the top of this page and click on forum and then go down 5 sections and start a new thread in the journal section.

Dr. Kruse likes journals - he really needs a lot more context ...your lifestyle? other labs? your non native EMF exposure?

It is all very, very important.

Have you listened to Dr. Kruse's podcasts? there is a new one today that is excellent. You can find them on the home page.

There is also a great free webinar by Dr. Tim Jackson - that would be really helpfull for you I think.... and he is doing a whole bootcamp series - starting next week.

Have you read Dr. K's book? It is a great overview of all of this and a really helpful section on labs. It is available in kindle and paper.

### Jude — 2013-11-09T01:32:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-health-supplement.8696/post-100215>

> [laker said:](/goto/post?id=100170)
>
> Worried about my 1.3 creatinine in recent labs. 26 yr old male. 68 gfr. Is the gfr measured on its own or only calculated from the creatinine levels? Could this be low because of not being well hydrated before the blood work?
> 96 fasting blood sugar. Following paleo diet now for 10 months

Dear laker, perhaps you could post all your labs and ref ranges. JK says context is crucial and we need to look at everything. iirc the BUN/creatinine ratio gives a better picture of ones' hydration status, so in isolation yr value can look strange but must not be taken out of context
Caroline's advice is good...more info better advice....all the best.

### JanSz — 2013-11-09T02:09:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-health-supplement.8696/post-100224>

> [laker said:](/goto/post?id=100170)
>
> Worried about my 1.3 creatinine in recent labs. 26 yr old male. 68 gfr. Is the gfr measured on its own or only calculated from the creatinine levels? Could this be low because of not being well hydrated before the blood work?
> 96 fasting blood sugar. Following paleo diet now for 10 months

If you have very low body fat, lots of muscles, exercise heavily, supplement with Creatine monohydrate, eat lots of proteins, creatinine may go up, eGFR may go down and all that may be normal.
Also eGFR is calculated with correction for African Americans.
I just got my labs. I am white. Got
eGFR=85 if nonAfrican American
eGFR=98 if African American

I got above after I stopped eating Creatine monohydrate.
While eating Creatine monohydrate I had
eGFR=58 if nonAfrican American
eGFR=67 if African American

your lab:
Creatinine=1.3(0.76-1.27)
eGFR=68>59

\-----------------------------------

.....................

### laker — 2013-11-09T04:21:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-health-supplement.8696/post-100235>

thanks, non black, UA was negative, i don't supplement that, i do eat high protein diet, my BUN wasn't taken but ill be sure to get that check asap

TESTOSTERONE, TOTAL 249 - 836 ng/dL 705
SEXHORMONE BINDING GLOBULIN 14 - 48 nmol/L 37
TSH 0.35 - 4.00 mcIU/mL 2.86
FSH 1.4 - 18.1 mIU/mL 3.9

### caroline — 2013-11-09T01:53:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/silica.8704/post-100218>

I know we have talked about silica briefly - but it is so hard to find things these day.....

I bought some liquid silica yesterday .... The instructions are to put one tsp in a glass of water daily.

I really like the taste it gave to the water - it was very pleasant.

I paid $29.95 for 500ml. It is probably dirt cheap in the U.S.?

Is this a good idea?

### Hope — 2013-11-09T01:57:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/silica.8704/post-100219>

Hi Audrey, I haven't looked into it yet.....but a health fair I went to in October, they talked about it wrt aluminum....it also supposedly helps with aluminum removal? It's also great for hair and nails. I remember seeing lots of ads in Germany talking about it.

A quick google search brought up this: [http://www.naturalnews.com/034756\_silica\_anti-aging\_heavy\_metals.html](http://www.naturalnews.com/034756_silica_anti-aging_heavy_metals.html)

Good luck.

Can you import your own cheaper supplements from overseas maybe?

### caroline — 2013-11-09T02:14:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/silica.8704/post-100225>

Hi hope ....sometimes we can - but often things aren't allowed. If they do come into the country they can be horrendously expensive! I want to get some great lakes gelatin but it costs more than twice what two containers cost you...

The cost for a lot of supps that I would like to get would break the bank for sure! So I am just going with JK on this ....I am getting what I need from a steak!

### Hope — 2013-11-09T02:30:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/silica.8704/post-100226>

Ugh, that is hard. But, so great you have all you need )

### CTforlife — 2014-10-21T23:27:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/silica.8704/post-144637>

Is silica important? From a cellular depletion standpoint or does it just help? I heard it has effects with collagen so it sounds important to me?

### Penny — 2014-10-23T06:51:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/silica.8704/post-144809>

silica - also known as horsetail - chelates aluminum... Fiji water has the highest amount in a bottled water and in fact if you want to use it to remove aluminum, the protocol is one bottle of Fiji water per day for 3 months... safe, but kinda pricey -

For all you Aussies, iherb has horsetail (silica) for less than $6.00/bottle...
[http://www.iherb.com/Nature-s-Way-H...sc=0&lc=en-US&w=horsetail&rc=471&sr=null&ic=1](http://www.iherb.com/Nature-s-Way-Horsetail-Grass-440-mg-100-Capsules/1978#p=1&oos=1&disc=0&lc=en-US&w=horsetail&rc=471&sr=null&ic=1)

malic acid also chelates aluminum - take it with garlic and it binds even more crap

I take a magnesium malate every day -

### Lahelada — 2014-10-23T17:00:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/silica.8704/post-144853>

Penny,
Silica is not equivalent to horsetail,it is just more marketable than getting you to swallow a capsule of sand as we are so far removed from nature.It is a component of sands, just plain ole bird sand has a lot of it .
Horsetail is the plant that contains a lot of silica but is a diuretic....
I have taken it but thinking about how much it costs us to get the water to go where it needs I am thinking that horsetail should stay a medicinal herb for specific use not a daily supp.

### Lahelada — 2014-10-23T17:06:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/silica.8704/post-144855>

For Germans reading Kieselerde is the capsulaeed sand you cannask for in any Droguerie at a fraction of the cost.

### Penny — 2014-10-28T00:34:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/silica.8704/post-145353>

thanks for that - I think I'll just shut up about horsetail from now on... but I think Fiji water would be safe...

# Thread: Pandora's box is open and I need to shut it :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-is-open-and-i-need-to-shut-it.8715/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thenursisin — 2013-11-10T02:56:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-is-open-and-i-need-to-shut-it.8715/post-100299>

Hi everyone:

My name is Donna and I am a registered nurse for over 34 years. I have always been healthy and naturally thin. I have run marathons and half marathons and always have been active.

In the last 20 years, I have developed Hashimoto's disease after my second pregnancy. With that I have had a partial thyroidectomy due to a huge growth and and fear of cancer. Thankfully, no cancer. I have been on synthroid, developed anxiety and insomnia and was placed on Paxil. As time progressed I developed skin issues, asthma, GERD, and huge digestive issues with a special reaction to WHEAT but no celiac per blood tests and colonoscopy. I have developed IBS. I have developed rosacea. I have developed severe ragweed/mite allergies. I have gained 40 lbs. No matter how much I exercise, I can't lose weight. I also have been in alcohol recovery for 8 years.

I switched to Paleo lifestyle and felt better but feel huge cravings to sweets and chocolates. I wondered if I wasn't just switching addictions. After several Whole 30s I felt better but couldnt stick to the plan on a constant basis. After several months of large carb/wheat binge eating, I have raised the white flag. I am **DONE**. I can't eat like this anymore and feel like this anymore. I receive acupuncture twice a week and have switched to walking/nordic walking/elliptical training and weight training twice per week. No more running.

I have begun to read Jack Kruse and feel immediately drawn to his writings. Eureka!!!! I have stumbled upon a solution that might work for me. I want to get off of Paxil and lose the weight and feel like my pre Hashimotos self again. I have begun to take Probiotics for the last month and that has greatly helped me overall.

I need help and think this forum is a great start. Any advice would be greatly appreciated.....

### jib — 2013-11-10T03:32:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-is-open-and-i-need-to-shut-it.8715/post-100303>

Hi Donna, Welcome.

# Thread: Pandora's box is open and I need to shut it
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-is-open-and-i-need-to-shut-it.8717/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thenursisin — 2013-11-10T03:55:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-is-open-and-i-need-to-shut-it.8717/post-100304>

Hi,

I am Donna and am glad to have found this forum. I have had Hashimoto's Thyroiditis for over 25 years. I had a partial thyroidectomy in mid 90's after a pregnancy for fear of thyroid cancer. It turned out to be Hashimoto's. After this, I have developed anxiety and depression and been started on Paxil.
Since then, on Paxil and Synthroid all hell has broke loose! I have developed rosacea, IBS, GERD, asthma, severe ragweed/mite allergies, dental problems, joint pain etc...the worse of problem of them all is 40 lbs weight gain despite running 25 miles per week. I suspect I have a wheat allergy due to severe GI reactions when I eat it yet negative celiac panel.

I have done Paleo (Whole 30) yet crave carbs and go off the food plan and my health spirals. Insomnia is a chronic issue for me. I recently began bioidentical Progesterone and Estrogen to help with menopause symptoms. This has greatly helped my insomnia.

I stumbled upon Dr Kruse and bought the Epi Paleo book on Kindle this week. I have so many questions and don't know where to start. I am financially strapped so cannot afford the boot camp, unfortunately. Would love to hear from anyone who can direct me to the first step!

### caroline — 2013-11-10T04:19:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-is-open-and-i-need-to-shut-it.8717/post-100305>

Hi Donna- and welcome! I am so glad that you started a journal! Dr. Kruse needs context to help where he can. At least you have a head start with the terminology!!

If you go to the home page you will find the easy start guides. Start the leptin RX using the epi paleo template.

Start reading the blogs that pertain to your health first - there is a lot - but just start slowly!

Please listen to Dr. K.'s podcasts - you will find them on the home page. The podcasts give you a lot of information quickly and Dr. Kruse is very easy to listen to. The last one he did with Evan Brand is great.

Have you listened to the free webinar with Dr. Tim Jackson? That would be really important for you.

HAve you looked at your non native EMF exposure? That is critical for you - that needs to be at the top of your list. There are some great podcasts that Jack did on that.

Keep asking questions - we all try to help each other. There are other folks with your problems ....so it would be great to connect with them in their journals.

When you start the leptin R/X - especially pay attention to the BAB (big ass breakfast) ... It knocks out cravings pretty fast. - I promise!

You need to eat within 30 minutes upon rising and leave at least 4 hours between meals and 4 hours before going to bed and no snacking what so ever. Follow Jack's protocols exactly as written

Avoid all fake lite.....no lights on after sunset ....And wear blue blocking glasses when on a computer/iPad/iPhone/TV etc.

Take everything electrical out of where you sleep and cut the breaker if you can .... Really important.

Lastly - read the C/ T easy start guide....

### thenursisin — 2013-11-10T14:15:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-is-open-and-i-need-to-shut-it.8717/post-100312>

thank you Caroline:
I just read your story at the end of Jack's book....very hopeful for me as I can relate to your story the MOST. I actually came to find Jack Kruse by listening to Tim Jackson's webinar! I am trying to eat my BAB right now but only ate it 45 minutes into awakening. I pray and meditate for 45 mins every morning upon awakening but I need to switch the order of that with my eating first.

Looking into blue blockers. I fall asleep with a sound machine but I think you are saying get rid of it? I need the white noise to drift....

This site has offered me the most hope to date. Thank you for responding and I will peruse your recommendations.

Donna

### nicld — 2013-11-10T18:53:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-is-open-and-i-need-to-shut-it.8717/post-100332>

Welcome Donna,

Caroline (aka Audrey) is a wealth of information and well as many others on this site.

You can get some blue blockers off of Amazon for about $10 and they really help. I have so sleep with a sound machine too but have switched from having it run on electricity to battery. Don't know if it is better but I cannot sleep without it (need to drown the sound of the hubby out).

Like Caroline said, I would start reading one of the series of blogs like the CT ones or all the Brain Gut ones. There is so much information that it will make you head hurt.

Also, get outside and get some grounding in. Not sure where you live but where I live, winter is fast approaching and I can already tell that I need to figure out how to get some time outside.

Hang on, this is going to be a journey.

### thenursisin — 2013-11-10T19:20:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-is-open-and-i-need-to-shut-it.8717/post-100333>

Nicki,

Thanks so much....My head is spinning. And I am a master's prepared registered nurse (master of science in nursing)....but it's because I am sooooo anxious to learn so much, so fast. Because I KNOW this is the solution. Paleo was such a huge help. This takes it to the next level. It all makes so much sense to me (what I have pulled out thus far).

I am a high school nurse and our teens are just so sick..they are sleep deprived, dying from the SAD and depressed, anxious and suicidal. It is sooooooo alarming to watch the numbers increase. But the staff is just as sick. I live on the east coast an hour from NYC so the pace and expectations are GREAT. It is a very competitive and intense environment. Driving is like the Indy 500 around here

I teach sleep hygiene to my teens and parents because they are ridiculously sleep deprived trying to get into Ivy League schools....I am sharing this info with my hubs who is on scientific staff at Princeton University.....He is a bit skeptical but, I think I can convince him. He loves the Paleo food plan....

I think I found my home......thanks for the welcome

~Donna

### sooperb — 2013-11-10T20:48:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-is-open-and-i-need-to-shut-it.8717/post-100336>

Crumbs Donna, in an environment like that it's a wonder you don't just want to shoot yourself! I wish you luck, you're going to need it. Just kidding.

I have long thought that the only way to change things for the better is to start with the children and hope that they can then teach their children, the parents are just a waste of space, they don't know any better and they certainly aren't interested in changing. If you try you just get dogs abuse!

### Roman1 — 2013-11-11T19:24:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-if-i-am-leptin-sensitive-yet.8732/post-100434>

I am not sure if I am leptin sensitive ,want to continue HIIT exercise\\ heavy lifting.
I am 27yr old male ,underweight ,5.67 ft and ~100LB.
I began doing leptin reset on april or may of 2012 and some time afterwards added c.t... for some time.
Epi-paleo when it first appeared. 2 meals a day. .I am not cold adapted yet. I have a thyroid issue, probably hypothyroid.
Still looking for a doc who could do the needed tests.
I watch the circadian cycles: look at the sky in the morning, use glasses. Watch EMFs in my apartment.

SIGNS IT’S WORKING:
\-Change in your sweating pattern: probably less ,I think ,but hard to tell b\\c of the wheather...
\-Energy levels ,not sure but sometimes it feels like they rose a little bit however
my feet (legs?) still get so tired after some walk.
\-Hunger and cravings did disappear ,probably 6+ months ago ,but it seems like cravings have
somewhat reappeared ,after I began lowering my carbs, in preparation for winter?
I usually can hold off eating easily for 6+ hours.

\-When I awaken I am in hurry for the BAB but maybe I am a bit more refreshed ,if my sleep was good, which usually is not ...
\-Men: quicker weight loss?? I think my body 'looks' a little bit better than before I started, like by 1-2% ?
less bloated all the time however I have indigestion or something. Still somewhat skinny fat b\\c of the stress...

Are there any other signs? Any advice would be very appreciated.
Thank you!

EDIT:
Changes in appearance: hand nails got so much worse in presentation. Less dead skin on my feet.

what about non native EMF? at work?

### Roman1 — 2013-11-12T18:54:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-if-i-am-leptin-sensitive-yet.8732/post-100519>

Thanks. I do walk but it starts to hurt after a short while... esp. feet probably b\\c I have a Flat Feet condition ......
I sleep on a 5g magnetico SLEEP PAD if that counts but the mattress is an old 8"+. I did grounding before ,
not much now thanks to the increased stress outside which is rarely bearable. I do try to touch a tree but unsure if
I do it right or long enough.

I have posted yesterday but it has been deleted or not posted and now I can\`t remember it.
Fifth night was ruined by mosquitoes. Last night's sleep was better.

I do not have a smart meter, nor do the neighbours ,However, a lot of wifi signals everywhere.
There is a cell tower 120yard away from my bedroom. No electrical device plugged in at night. Computer in room.

I did work in the past for a short while but it was too demanding for me..... Now looking for a new one.
I want to try flyers home delivery job ,but I worry that I won\`t have enough strenth and such for it,
hence a good reason to begin HIIT exercise asap.

### caroline — 2013-11-12T23:47:19+0200
