### cantweight — 2013-11-12T20:58:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-labs-i-think-its-working.8751/post-100531>

In September my TSH was low .329 so I lowered my thyroid dose from 150 to 120 mg Armour. I gave it a month to settle and tested again. New labs below, I am thinking I will drop my dose again as TSH is still relatively low and have had several episodes of racing heart (my too much thyroid signal). I think this sucker is finally coming back to life. CT/iodine one two punch  Not sure about the antibodies though....any input on them and thoughts on lowering dosage? Thanks!

TSH .48 .40-4.50

T3Rev 13 8-25ng/dl

T3Total 89 76-181ng/dl

T3Free 3.0 2.3-4.2pg/ml

Thyroglobulin AB <20

Thyroid Peroxidase AB 14

### Shijin13 — 2013-11-12T21:40:50+0200

Christi

I'm decreasing my armour as well... it dropped into the hyper range its awesome when some things start to work... even if everything isn't working all in concert yet!!!

### nicld — 2013-11-13T00:09:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-labs-i-think-its-working.8751/post-100548>

Very interesting.

When my TSH was below normal (.29) I felt fine and was sleeping though the night. The doc knock my Cytomel down a bit based on the last round of tests and that seemed to cause all sorts of issues and I am only on 60mg of Armour. I stopped sleeping through the night, 45 minutes of CT was really hard to do without shivering (used to be able to do almost 2 hours no problem) and I was getting cold again.

I just upped the Cytomel back to where I was when I was feeling good and almost slept through the night last night for the first time in almost 2 weeks. Plus I am just feeling calmer. When the TSH was showing low, my ratios were really good I think (43 this time versus 11 before). I am planning on getting retested in Dec so I will be curious to see what they are then and how I feel.

I am forever grateful for all the learning that I have received on this site. Can’t wait to see how much more I will learn in the boot camp. I just have to remember to have my labs spreadsheet open to see how everything relates to my N=1.

# Thread: New Cholesterol Guidelines issued
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-cholesterol-guidelines-issued.8752/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ATL_Paleo — 2013-11-13T01:09:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-cholesterol-guidelines-issued.8752/post-100560>

On first glance, it appears the new guidelines have ditched the above 200 number for total cholesterol. One of the 4 guidelines replacing it is above 190 for LDL cholesterol. This seems to make a bit more sense. ..... But I agree, statins are still bad.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

[http://www.medscape.com/viewarticle/814152](http://www.medscape.com/viewarticle/814152)

The four major primary- and secondary-prevention patient groups who should be treated with statins were identified on the basis of randomized, controlled clinical trials showing that the benefit of treatment outweighed the risk of adverse events. The four treatment groups include:

Individuals with clinical atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease.

Individuals with LDL-cholesterol levels >190 mg/dL, such as those with familial hypercholesterolemia.

Individuals with diabetes aged 40 to 75 years old with LDL-cholesterol levels between 70 and 189 mg/dL and without evidence of atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease.

Individuals without evidence of cardiovascular disease or diabetes but who have LDL-cholesterol levels between 70 and 189 mg/dL and a 10-year risk of atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease >7.5%.

### Destiny — 2013-11-13T13:52:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-cholesterol-guidelines-issued.8752/post-100611>

> [ATL\_Paleo said:](/goto/post?id=100560)
>
> On first glance, it appears the new guidelines have ditched the above 200 number for total cholesterol. One of the 4 guidelines replacing it is above 190 for LDL cholesterol. This seems to make a bit more sense. ..... But I agree, statins are still bad.
>
> \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
> It sounds to me that there is even a bigger push towards statins with these guidelines.

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sjoshua — 2013-11-13T02:41:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prahlad-jani-the-yogi-who-hasnt-eaten-drank-for-70-years.8754/post-100577>

[http://www.techeblog.com/index.php/...ived-without-food-and-water-for-over-70-years](http://www.techeblog.com/index.php/tech-gadget/another-look-at-prahlad-jani-a-person-who-s-lived-without-food-and-water-for-over-70-years)

Anyone else familiar with this story? Pretty neat stuff... The epitome of the PPP?

I bet his cave is pretty awesome. Do you think he has the magnetico sleep pad, or just uses the earth pulse?

### Josh — 2014-03-29T02:20:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prahlad-jani-the-yogi-who-hasnt-eaten-drank-for-70-years.8754/post-118597>

This guy is beyond mind blowing......

> The Shivapuri Baba aka Swami Govindanath Bharati was born in a rich Brahmin household in the Indian state of Kerala in 1826. At a tender age of 18, he decided to abandon the world and seek for absolute truth. Here, Bennett is talking of the time when "the future Edward VII was a three-year old infant in Buckingham Palace while the great Indian saint Ramakrishna was ten years old." Govindananda Bharati followed the "path of Rtambhara Prajna or absolute realization of God beyond all forms and images." The writer explains that this stage is not reached until there is a progressive elimination of all that support and comfort the mind. He further explains that when all is stripped away, and the soul enters the absolute nakedness that is beyond understanding can it make the final act of the Pure Will whereby all distinctions disappear and all the Individual and the Absolute are made one.
>
> Although an Indian sage, the Shivapuri Baba spent over 40 years of his life in Nepal. There is an interesting account of how he hoodwinked the Ranas and entered a country virtually closed to the foreigners. On March 1926, Swami Govindanath first visited Nepal, accompanied by a horde of Indian Shadhus on their way to pay homage to Lord Pashupatinath. The Indian sage entered Nepal at a time when the country was virtually closed to foreigners and pilgrims alike. Only during the holy festival of Shivaratri, Hindu pilgrims were allowed to enter the country on the condition that they would leave within one week.
>
> Govindananda was highly fascinated by the serenity of the Pashupati temple complex and the jungles lying in the outskirts of the city. On the day of his eventual return to India, He luckily met a Wilkinson who had then become the British resident in Nepal. It was he who coaxed the Rana rulers to let the Indian sage settle in the jungle lying in the outskirts of the city- the place where he would shed his identity of Govindanath Bharati and emerge as the saintly old man of the Shivapuri forest, the Shivapuri Baba. **_He breathed his last in the same forest retreat in the Himalayan foothills. He died at the great age of 137 years old. The Shivapuri Baba was the first modern yogi to transplant the wisdom of the Indian sub-continent to the West. While on a visit to England, he had no fewer than 18 audiences with Queen Victoria, who was deeply influenced by his ability to make her see beyond "the veil of death._**" His stay in England was the longest part of his pilgrimage although he was equally popular across the Atlantic. While analyzing the immense popularity of Govindanath, Bennett rightly observes, "His excellent English, enlivened with the apt stories from the Mahabharata or Ramayana, or even, I suspect, sometimes invented by himself, his quick wit, and his profound wisdom; and no doubt, most of all, the irresistible attraction that the power of sanctity exerts upon even half-receptive people-combined to make him sought after by the hostesses of the end of the century."
>
> **_Bennett has listed a number of amusing anecdotes about the Shivapuri Baba. Notably, among them is his visit with the great 20th century dramatist, George Bernard Shaw. Although a learned philosopher, he harbored a deep contempt for saints and their world. Therefore, during his encounter with the Shivapuri Baba, he said, "You Indian saints are the most useless of men; you have no respect for time." The latter's reply was something like this, "It is you who are the slaves of time. I live in eternity." No wonder, the Baba was blessed with a very long life, which, after his awakening at the age of 50, he dedicated entirely to the spiritual welfare of others._**
>
> Another anecdote concerns the visit of Indian President Dr Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan on the occasion of King Mahendra's coronation. Having disembarked from the plane, he straightaway headed towards the retreat of the Shivapuri Baba, of whom he had heard so much. When Radhakrishnan queried about his teachings, which he beautifully summed up in a sentence "I teach three disciplines- spiritual, moral and physical."
>
> **_The Shivapuri Baba taught the principle of Swadharma, which believes that as humans we have three principle duties: first, physical duty towards our self. It mainly consists of maintaining body and mind through proper livelihood, including the obligation to help one's dependents to accomplish the same.
>
> The second is one's moral duty, consisting in remaining sensitive to the obligation to seek the truth 24 hours a day. And the third, spiritual duty, means the worship of the Divine. He felt certain that if we attend carefully to the first two duties for a decade, we will naturally become able to fulfill the third duty. Physical discipline, he noted, brings pleasure. Moral discipline gives us serenity. Spiritual discipline yields deep peace and ultimate happiness._**
>
> The Shivapuri Baba was dismissive of conventional yogic paths, because he saw in them potential distractions that might keep a person from performing the three duties. On closer inspection, his own sensible prescriptions are in fact a form of yoga. Only rare individuals can devote themselves directly to the pursuit of enlightenment. "All of Yoga, in a way, is preparation for our final hour on earth. It matters how we exit from life, because death is only a doorway to another state of existence."

[http://www.bennettbooks.org/store/m...de=BB&Product\_Code=LONGP-GM&Category\_Code=211](http://www.bennettbooks.org/store/merchant.mvc?Screen=PROD&Store_Code=BB&Product_Code=LONGP-GM&Category_Code=211)

# Thread: Squatting for elimination

**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### baudoman — 2013-11-13T04:17:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/squatting-for-elimination.8755/post-100594>

I'm very glad to have found a place where people agree with the notion of squatting to eliminate.
(As per Jack's reference to it in his Brain Gut 17 blog post)

I find it too sad every time I see a commercial for a constipation-relief product and think that there are millions of people suffering from something that may be solved in such a simple manner, if only they were aware of it and perhaps were advised to try it.
I think about toddlers growing up and the fact that they squat naturally to use the toilet and then suddenly we 'potty-train' them and force them to do it wrongly.

I think the world would be a better place if people feel true relief when they eliminate.

I'm looking for ideas on how can we make this happen - awareness of such an important method for relief?

# Thread: Fermented Vegetables
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-vegetables.8763/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### elvey90 — 2013-11-14T13:05:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-vegetables.8763/post-100697>

Hey All,

A newbie here on the Epi Paleo diet. In Brain Gut 6, Jack mentions to eat fermented vegetables, can anyone provide examples of acceptable fermented vegetables to choose from?
I'm looking at saukreut at the moment but not sure if that's ok to eat.

Cheers!

### Hemming — 2013-11-14T13:49:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-vegetables.8763/post-100698>

Sauerkraut is fine. Kimchii in general.

You can make your own fermented food from whatever you like. Currently I'm fermenting apples with chili, ginger, garlic, cinnamon and liquorice (one flavour in each jar).

### JoeBranca — 2013-11-14T21:03:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-vegetables.8763/post-100722>

Hemming, fermented apples sounds interesting. I'd like to try that while they're still in season. How are you doing it? Just slicing them up and adding salt in a jar?

### Hemming — 2013-11-14T21:09:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fermented-vegetables.8763/post-100724>

I sliced them up and put them in a bowl with salt. I put 1dl of sauerkraut juice in each jar, added the apples with around 1.5l of water.

I haven't tasted the apples yet but the fermentation is on track. The liquid blew out when I opened them the other day. Its not as bad anymore  The liquid tasts like apple cider including the carbonated feeling.

# Thread: Breakthrough in Quantum Computing - Qubit Lasts 39 Minutes at Room Temp
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breakthrough-in-quantum-computing-qubit-lasts-39-minutes-at-room-temp.8766/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cinnamon — 2013-11-15T02:28:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/breakthrough-in-quantum-computing-qubit-lasts-39-minutes-at-room-temp.8766/post-100741>

Fascinating article published today on advances in research toward building a silicon based quantum computer.

[http://www.cbc.ca/news/technology/qubit-record-moves-quantum-computing-forward-1.2426986](http://www.cbc.ca/news/technology/qubit-record-moves-quantum-computing-forward-1.2426986)

Excerpt:
**

"Researchers are trying to develop quantum computers because they have the potential for exponentially greater computing power than conventional computers. That is because conventional computers encode data as "bits," each of which is in one of two possible states, "0" or "1," while quantum computers encode data as "qubits" that can each be in multiple states simultaneously. That would allow them to perform multiple calculations as the same time.

The phenomenon of being in a "0" and "1" state simultaneously, known as "superposition," is possible due to the strange laws of quantum physics that apply only to very small particles such as atoms that are used to create qubits."​

**
As you may have guessed, qubits work better in the freezing cold....

# Thread: Concerns about coconut oil and DHT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/concerns-about-coconut-oil-and-dht.8767/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### QiGuy1997 — 2013-11-15T03:12:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/concerns-about-coconut-oil-and-dht.8767/post-100742>

I understand that coconut oil is a very healthy substance with it's saturated fats, ketogenic properties and antimicrobial properties. I'm really interested in beginning to get a couple of tablespoons of it throughout the day, however, I have a concern. The only thing keeping me from consuming any is a study I read a while back discussing the inhibitory properties of lauric acid (from coconut oil) on 5-alpha reductase, the enzyme that turns testosterone into the more active Dihydrotestosterone. I'm 16 so I know my hormonal system is quite different from the majority of the people on this forum. I don't want to mess with my hormonal levels at this age as I'm concerned about permanent effects resulting from hormonal imbalances. I'm hesitant to consume a substance which may lower my dht levels. Does anyone have any knowledge on this topic? Will taking coconut oil decrease the dht by a significant amount, or do the positive effects outweigh the slight hormonal effects? Thanks!

### caroline — 2013-11-15T03:23:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/concerns-about-coconut-oil-and-dht.8767/post-100744>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/concerns-about-coconut-oil-and-dht.8767/post-100757>

### Jonathin — 2013-11-15T20:11:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/concerns-about-coconut-oil-and-dht.8767/post-100780>

Interesting if this is a valid concern. Seems like people in the coconut-eating tropics would be affected. I have not hear of such an issue.

### nonchalant — 2013-11-15T22:07:54+0200

Yes, interesting. Does anyone know what time of year this experiment was run?

### QiGuy1997 — 2013-11-15T23:25:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/concerns-about-coconut-oil-and-dht.8767/post-100795>

Here's the study I was referencing in my original post [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19353546](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/19353546) It appears to have been done in April of '09

Anyone here try myopulse therapy for soft tissue injuries? Been messing with some achilles tendonosis and decided to try a new Chiropractor in town. After checking me out he recommended I give myopulse therapy a try. I agreed and let his tech hook me up. Unlike most therapy techniques I felt absolutely nothing during the treatment and was thinking this was a waste of time and money, but walking back to the office afterwards my achilles felt better than it had it weeks. Same thing after a second treatment. So far I'm impressed.

Researching it bit it seems to come right out Becker's work. Small amounts of currents in the microamp range are applied to the body at frequencies generally below 10hz giving the injured cells the energy they need to heal themselves. The link below provides more background

[http://www.healthydoctors.com/acusc...ope and myopulse explantion.htm#\_Toc457224424](http://www.healthydoctors.com/acuscope%20and%20myopulse/acuscope%20and%20myopulse%20explantion.htm#_Toc457224424)

### caroline — 2013-11-16T05:25:00+0200

I do go off the rails now and again ... I promise to do better ...

### Josh — 2014-10-04T03:20:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ee-9-quantum-sleep.8775/post-142745>

**"The pineal gland pays attention to light, dark, magnetic fields and to gravity." EE9**

Where does the gravity come from? Inside from protein tubules and water or outside from the interaction of larger things? Or is it the center of the vortex in the 3rd ventricle?

I am thinking that the better the redox and electron flow with higher magnetic fields, the less susceptible to outside gravitational forces and the stronger the endogenous ones will be from microtubules and the 3rd ventricle vortex. ### Josh — 2014-10-04T03:21:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ee-9-quantum-sleep.8775/post-142746>

[http://www.cell.com/current-biology/pdf/S0960-9822(08)00865-8.pdf](http://www.cell.com/current-biology/pdf/S0960-9822\(08\)00865-8.pdf)

> Collectively the daily, seasonal, lunar and tidal geophysical
> cycles regulate much of the temporal biology of life on
> Earth. The increasing isolation of human societies from
> these geophysical cycles, as a result of improved living
> conditions, high-quality nutrition and 24/7 working practices,
> have led many to believe that human biology functions
> independently of them. Yet recent studies have highlighted
> the dominant role that our circadian clock plays in
> the organisation of 24 hour patterns of behaviour and
> physiology. Preferred wake and sleep times are to a large
> extent driven by an endogenous temporal program that
> uses sunlight as an entraining cue. The alarm clock can
> drive human activity rhythms but has little direct effect
> on our endogenous 24 hour physiology. In many situations,
> our biology and our society appear to be in serious opposition,
> and the damaging consequences to our health
> under these circumstances are increasingly recognised.
> The seasons dominate the lives of non-equatorial species,
> and until recently, they also had a marked influence on
> much of human biology. Despite human isolation from seasonal
> changes in temperature, food and photoperiod in the
> industrialised nations, the seasons still appear to have
> a small, but significant, impact upon when individuals are
> born and many aspects of health. The seasonal changes
> that modulate our biology, and how these factors might interact
> with the social and metabolic status of the individual
> to drive seasonal effects, are still poorly understood. Lunar
> cycles had, and continue to have, an influence upon human
> culture, though despite a persistent belief that our mental
> health and other behaviours are modulated by the phase of
> the moon, there is no solid evidence that human biology is
> in any way regulated by the lunar cycle

### Josh — 2014-10-04T17:34:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ee-9-quantum-sleep.8775/post-142780>

[http://www.collective-evolution.com...didnt-sleep-like-you-are-were-doing-it-wrong/](http://www.collective-evolution.com/2014/10/02/your-ancestors-didnt-sleep-like-you-are-were-doing-it-wrong/)

# Thread: How much water do you drink a day?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-water-do-you-drink-a-day.8783/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2013-11-17T16:41:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-water-do-you-drink-a-day.8783/post-100906>

I drink about a litre (bottled, spring) I would say. I have roughly <100g protein, 40g carbs, the rest fat. The protein should require me to consume more water but the fat metabolises into water when it breaks down (hydrogen + oxygen) so would this offset the extra water for protein?

I am wandering if this is enough and what other people drink.

I am quite thirsty often but only in my mouth if that makes sense. My stomach reacts badly to anything (bloating/distension) so I am never thirsty in my stomach (if that makes sense). An example of this was I dry fasted for 7 days and never really felt truly like I wanted water, my mouth did, but overall I didn't. When I did become pretty damn dehydrated I drank some water, but after a few sips I had to force the rest down and my stomach bloated instantly, I could feel the water slushing around in my stomach 24hrs later.
I know this probably happens to everyone when dehydrated but it happens to me every time I eat or drink but just to a lesser extent.

Anyway I have been trying to get around this by taking a mouthful of water roughly every 10-15 mins and 2 hrs away from food. But I am now wondering if I am constantly mildly dehydrated and that is contributing to my symptoms (fatigue, dizziness, palpitations, bloating, excema, cold extremities, sunken eyes, constipation, etc).

So how much water do you drink in a day and how much do you get from food?

### Inger — 2013-11-17T16:57:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-water-do-you-drink-a-day.8783/post-100907>

You get way too little Sean. I drink maybe 3 liters/day. It varies a bit as I do not count it really. I eat mostly raw so my food is moist and juicy mostly, I probably get some water from food too.
If you are around EMF and drink only 1 liter it is pretty bad I guess

### seanb4 — 2013-11-17T17:23:47+0200

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-water-do-you-drink-a-day.8783/post-101008>

### Inger — 2013-11-19T13:30:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-much-water-do-you-drink-a-day.8783/post-101010>

I never count...lol But I guess around 5 times maybe? It varies... sometimes I go to pee even if I do not feel like I have too, before going out etc. I pee in the morning after raising, and in the evening before going to bed and a couple of times in between

### seanb4 — 2013-11-22T17:34:12+0200

Okay so the general consensus is between 2-4 litres.

Over the last couple of days I've upped my water intake to 2 litres. Whilst bloating has increased slightly throughout the day it goes back to usual levels by morning, I think the bloating is causing water retention which is relieved somewhat by the extra water intake.
My fatigue, energy, and digestion seemed to have improved slightly leading me to believe i was slightly dehydrated before.

Anybody have answers for my other questions in this thread?

# Thread: Where do you get your Seafood (Aussies)?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-get-your-seafood-aussies.8788/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### elvey90 — 2013-11-18T12:34:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-get-your-seafood-aussies.8788/post-100948>

Hey All,

This is more for Australians (I'm from Melbourne), and was wondering where do you get your seafood(s) from?
I find that to get wild caught you have to go to faraway places (aka dandenong markets) and in terms of costs even farmed grown fish are pretty expensive too (yea I'm on a budget).

I'm also currently getting grass fed beef from Aldi and/or a local butcher, I'm working towards removing fowl/poultry altogether from my diet and incorporate purely grass fed beef, some seasonal veggies (kale, cabbage, broccoli, bok choy & sauerkraut), plenty of seaweed (found a authentic Japanese grocer to get these), free range eggs, and health fats (coconut milk, avocado, coconut oil, ghee), and of course seafood.

So if anyone has any suggestions on where to get affordable seafood or even share what you do so that I can brainstorm some ideas, would be very much appreciated

Cheers!

### caroline — 2013-11-19T00:01:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-get-your-seafood-aussies.8788/post-100981>

Hi Elvey. I get GF beef from Aldi too. What about Lamb? I often get a leg of lamb and throw it in the crockpot overnite and BAB is ready and waiting for me!

Jack has always said "Just do the best you can" sardines are great - in olive oil and canned oysters, mackerel mussels etc. etc.

You can get fish heads etc. at a fish market very cheap and make stock. I get flash frozen sushi grade tuna - in 4oz pieces from a fish market where I am. I just let it thaw slightly and then slice very thinly for sashimi - I roll it in nori etc.

Shrimp \[prawns\] are really easy to get. You must have loads of wonderful fish markets in Melbourne.....

I also get small frozen fish from a chinese store and make a MHS

Pork is great too........If you listen to Jacks last podcasts he talk about this. Also bacon from Aldi - 1Kg for $5.00

### PaulG — 2013-11-19T00:03:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-get-your-seafood-aussies.8788/post-100982>

Yes, I am finding seafood is way more expensive than the UK.

We used to have wild caught Salmon steaks twice a week but not anymore.

However, I try to eat three or four times per week New Zealand green lipped mussels that sell frozen from IGA for $6.20 per kg. They have a very good nutritional profile, high in omega-3, iodine, iron, vitamin a, c, zinc, magnesium - look it up. I also bought a steamer and they take 10mins to cook.

I have also read that the nutritional value is highly degraded from freezing and cooking so supplementing with powder version may be the way to go although Dr. Kruse advocates whole foods over supplements.

Also is it possible for you to take up fishing as a hobby - thats my next move, if you get into sea fishing then you are combining earthing with photoelectric effect just stay out of the sun between 11am and 3pm to avoid the extreme UV.

### caroline — 2013-11-19T00:07:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-get-your-seafood-aussies.8788/post-100984>

Great ideas Paul! Someone - I think maybe Dali ...if I am remembering right ...... does C/T in the ocean and then throws a line in and catches his dinner! and then cooks at the beach.......great multi tasking!!!

Mussels are great and inexpensive. Did you see the seafood stew in the recipe section?

Duck is excellent too - Aldi has Dick now.....it is frozen. I don't worry too much about frozen. It is flash frozen and, in my opinion, much better than the fresh fish hanging around in a supermarket counter.

### elvey90 — 2013-11-19T01:22:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-get-your-seafood-aussies.8788/post-100988>

Thanks for all the suggestions, with purchasing beef and eggs etc it feels easy for me but with seafood totally new with this!
As of right now with my schedule I won't be able to take up fishing and undertake CT in the water (though that sounds like a really awesome idea and actually have considered moving down near the beach areas).

Just a quick question with Aldi brand bacon, aren't they filled with nitrate?

With regards to to frozen seafood, so from the readings, I'm getting the consensus that if you can't get wild caught seafood, it's better to get farmed, frozen, canned etc seafood which is better than getting no seafood at all. I guess I should also supplement with iodine and selenium to combat the mercury levels as well. Am I heading in the right direction?

Will definitely check out IGA and grab some mussels!

Cheers!

### Sammy — 2013-11-19T01:49:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-get-your-seafood-aussies.8788/post-100989>

You can get lots of seafood at Vic market. I normally buy sardines ($6.50/kg), they also sell salmon heads for $1-2 each. Lots of meat and cartilaginous bones. I use it in broth. You can get a kilo of frozen mussels (no shell) for $14. I buy half a dozen of oysters a week for $6. I once asked my butcher and he told me all beef in australia is grassfed. I am not sure whether this is true or not.

Don't drive yourself crazy .... Dr. K. has said that food is only 10% ...... you need to worry about the conditions of your existence much more! non native EMF?

### Gracelind — 2013-11-19T05:26:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-get-your-seafood-aussies.8788/post-101000>

In the U.S. bacon that claims to be cured "naturally" without sodium nitrate has usually been cured with celery salt--that has a very high concentration of nitrate.

Vital Choice from Alaska does ship to Australia, though I imagine shipping is pricy. I met the owners at a Weston Price Conference and the products we tasted were quite good. [http://www.vitalchoice.com](http://www.vitalchoice.com)

Fish such as salmon that is frozen immediately after catch may have more nutrients preserved than "fresh" fish that has taken several days to arrive at market. Same with frozen veggies and fruits. [http://www.vitalchoice.com/shop/pc/viewContent.asp?idpage=2#Flash-Frozen](http://www.vitalchoice.com/shop/pc/viewContent.asp?idpage=2#Flash-Frozen)

You have to be careful with canned fish--be sure it's packed by a canner with exceptional standards of hygiene that uses the best parts of the fish.

Bon appetit!

### yewwei.tan — 2013-11-21T23:38:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-get-your-seafood-aussies.8788/post-101278>

I live within the melbourne metro region, and there isn't any issue finding decent quality Fish and Shellfish. Just yesterday I went over to my usual market and picked up this guy:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/photo1-jpg.179/)

Pretty damn fresh NZ salmon harvested and shipped immediately. This one was about 5.3kg, for $15/kg, or $79 for the whole thing which is cheap enough for some great salmon. I immediately chopped it up, sliced up some sashimi, and seared some trimmed off parts of the belly for a quick snack.

In general, I tend to buy whole fish. Like yesterday, I picked up a pike for $4/kg, some cuttlefish for $6/kg, and a couple of jack mackerels at $4/kg. The yield (edible portion by weight) on most of these fish are 70-80% in my experience, which still puts them under $10/kg if you only count the flesh (but I use the heads too!) Sometimes I pick up the prawns too, and in general, you can get pretty decent Queensland prawns at $15/kg (frozen and cooked is just fine). This was all at Preston market, which isn't the best, but is plenty good for my needs.

So yeah, for seafood, making it affordable requires that you're willing to dive into the markets and get your hands dirty.

\---

For offal, again, I know a couple of butchers at the market. Personally, I just look for the quality of organ meats. As you know, they say that a stressed animal will never have a healthy liver, and so if you see spots, uneven colour, weird textures, etc ... I wouldn't buy the liver. That said, most butchers here tend to have decent liver, and I get mine from this stall at the market as well for $2/kg.

For meats, I tend to take Dave Asprey's advice and eat leaner cuts of meat when I'm not sure of the source (toxins usually get stored in the fat). So for beef, stuff like oyster blade, gravy beef (shins), certain cuts of chuck, rump, etc ... I don't usually get lots of pork, mainly because I've had skin issues before from fatty cuts of pork, and usually just stick to loin cuts and bones (bitter melon and pork soup is comfort food for me). Lamb in general doesn't seem to hurt me much, and I just get the stuff from the markets as well, usually $10/kg of lamb leg or loin chops.

\---

I rarely eat poultry nowadays, but the markets also give you easy access to pastured chicken, duck, and turkey on the cheap.

\---

I should also add that homebrand unsalted butter from woolies is usually from New Zealand, which usually means grass fed as well. (the salted one is from Australia though!)

### caroline — 2013-11-22T00:48:27+0200

You are a pro ..... what great information ^^^ - thanks! plus you seem to have QED nailed!!!

Thanks for the info

Yea I'm gonna spend this weekend at the closest market I can get to and find some seafood and some liver. But really there's pastured fed chicken? I thought even pasture fed chicken still eat grains and silage??

With regards to pork I've found a butcher close to me that sells them nitrate free and stated that they are partially pasture fed due to the nature of raising pork.

### Kiera2 — 2013-11-22T05:51:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-get-your-seafood-aussies.8788/post-101308>

elvey90, try making your own ghee. It's so easy, and a much cheaper option.
There are a lot of tutorials online, here's one I just found, and it looks good. [http://www.everydaymaven.com/2013/how-to-make-ghee/](http://www.everydaymaven.com/2013/how-to-make-ghee/)
I don't skim, I just stir a lot until the 'bits' have settled. Be careful not to burn them!

### elvey90 — 2013-11-22T06:24:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-get-your-seafood-aussies.8788/post-101310>

> [Coriander said:](/goto/post?id=101308)

> elvey90, try making your own ghee. It's so easy, and a much cheaper option.
> There are a lot of tutorials online, here's one I just found, and it looks good. [http://www.everydaymaven.com/2013/how-to-make-ghee/](http://www.everydaymaven.com/2013/how-to-make-ghee/)

### yewwei.tan — 2013-11-22T07:07:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-get-your-seafood-aussies.8788/post-101312>

> [elvey90 said:](/goto/post?id=101310)
>
> Good suggestion, was contemplating that but honestly where I'm at now, I have little patience for this kind of stuff haha, plus the convenience of having ghee right there and then. Yes something to work on but eventually it would be good to know how to make your own ghee.
>
> Cheers!

Agreed with coriander on making your own ghee. I do it too since butter seems to give me a problematic reaction, and usually melt down 10 sticks of butter at a time, getting about 2kg of ghee 40 minutes later. So yes, it is a substantial upfront investment (which you should never rush by turning up the fire, lest you burn the butter protein), but after that I usually have enough ghee to last 3-4 weeks.

You're right about so-called "pastured" chicken, they're raised on a farm and are free-roaming birds, but you won't really know if they truly are fed on just seeds, nuts and grubs. The only time whereby I saw a vendor ever advertise that was at a farmer's market, and the chicken wasn't cheap!

Good find regarding the pork, and hope that you find some success with all of the food sourcing.

### Kiera2 — 2013-11-22T09:07:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-do-you-get-your-seafood-aussies.8788/post-101323>

'Pastured' chicken doesn't mean they are pasture fed, it means they are free to roam in a pasture. Like at Taranaki Farm: [http://www.taranakifarm.com.au/produce/](http://www.taranakifarm.com.au/produce/) and Polyface Farm: [http://www.polyfacefarms.com/](http://www.polyfacefarms.com/)

# Thread: Labs in - Is it my field or what?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-in-is-it-my-field-or-what.8790/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HoneyChild — 2013-11-18T17:36:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/labs-in-is-it-my-field-or-what.8790/post-100964>

I did a basic blood test on Friday (cd22) and got the results today. I'm concerned about quite a few things, I know I should really do more testing but I was hoping that this test would at least give me an idea if my field is a major problem or not.

Basically I'm 31, have no libido and intercourse is painful. I get swollen toes from time to time and this summer developed xanthelasma around my eyes (basically little orange blobs under my skin). This is supposed to be related to oxLDL and heart disease!

I started leptin reset this time last year along with cold showers and occasional cold baths. I've always been naturally slim however I ended up losing more weight than I expected and now I think I'm underweight. Whenever I do cold baths I seem to lose even more which scares me and I stop doing them.

I also started having problems with waking up in the night hot and thirsty but this appears to have stopped since I cut back on the fruit about a month ago.

I do my best to keep technology to a minimum, I work from home, don't have wifi or cordless phones. My magnetic field at night is very low and I have a magnet sitting on the floor under my bed. I wear UVEX glasses in the evening.

The only supplements I take are maca and magnesium spray on my feet.

The problems with where I live are: live couple of flights up, city population about 1.2 million, nearest cell phone masts are about 1/4 mile away. We're desperate to move however I'm struggling to find the idea home away from other houses.

I have some other tests I did earlier in the year and will post them soon.

"Any type of cellular stress lowers our stores of ATP and of Magnesium (Mg) because they are coupled together by our ATPase enzyme as we saw in this post. This is classically seen in diabetes development as we mentioned there. Another interesting thing also occurs to the cell, that we clinicians can follow on our patients regular labs like a Chem 7. Their carbon dioxide (CO2) levels also fall because mitochondria are actively transfer tons of electrons to O2 to make more ATP to replace the deficit from the stress. This causes a simultaneous drop in CO2. The normal cellular response to ANY cellular stress however is rather telling to review for most lay people and MD’s because they may have forgot how that biochemistry works. The simultaneous fall in ATP and Mg stimulates the cell to make LDL cholesterol! Yes, you read that correctly folks, the supposedly bad LDL stuff, LOL! It is obviously not bad if the cell is making it for some reason. Let us look why the cell does this. You may want to reread this blog on cholesterol."

### Shijin13 — 2013-11-19T20:46:35+0200

The field affecting you may not be in your house/flat. it could be from your neighbors wifi, or from the cell transmission tower, or the electrical lines, or the radio waves ...

You talk about your LDL not converting. Its not converting for two reasons 1) your vitamin A cycle is off (you're body's not making Vitamin A from exposure to sunlight and from eating foods like oysters and liver) and your Free T3 isn't able to work properly to convert the LDL with Vitamin A to make pregnenelone - so you've got a classic case of pregnenenolne steal syndrome going on.

So you need to look at four things - 1)your vitamin A cycle - go back and re-read hormones 102. & 2) you need to figure out if your making Reverse T3 instead of Free T3. if you're making Reverse T3 it will compete and prohibit any Free T3 your body's making (you've got to find out what's happening and fix this). 3) T4 is converted in the Gut to T3. you've got some gut issues going on that's impacting your thyroid. 4) Problems with your thyroid can directly impact your cortisol, telling your body that it's stressed b/c you don't have enough Free T3 to help Vitamin A to convert LDL to your Sex hormones.

you're in a perpetual do-loop. I'd start there. you can supplement pregnenolone - to help move your forward -but if you don't know whats driving your pregneneelone steal you won't fix the issue...

just my 2 cents...

# Thread: Need some guidance! (anxiety causing PN)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-guidance-anxiety-causing-pn.8808/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Frankie L — 2013-11-20T03:52:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-guidance-anxiety-causing-pn.8808/post-101077>

Hi everyone!
I hope someone can help me with his issue. Here's a bit about me...

I am a 40 yr old athletic muscular woman. I work out 3-4 times a week. Eat plenty of greens and no gluten. I am (was) a vegetarian (4 Yrs) and eat low carb diet and and stay away from **red meat and chicken** and NO processed food., I eat fish. I don't drink alcohol or coffee or any other of the no no drinks. **I just started to eat red meat November 19!!!!**

Since January of this year, I've developed moderate anxiety to the point that my the tops of my feet are constantly numb and part of my hands too slightly. I developed anxiety from worrying about my lymphoma coming back, I had it 7 years ago and I am in remission.

I've had numerous blood tests and diagnostic testing that says the cancer is gone - ok I get that but my sympathetic response is not "getting it"....I have permanent numbness and I would like to reverse this. I have no pain, I can live with this but I would like to fix it. I don't believe is RX for anxiety, so when I need a little calmness I take St. John Wort. - that's it because the anxiety is tolerable. I can go weeks without feeling anxious then one day I wake up and boom, there's the SR kicking in for no reason - I think great, those goes my feet vibrating away. My feet and hands vibrate like I'm holding a cell phone! OMG it is really freaky!! What is the cause of this?

I just started taking B12, liquid magnesium, R-ALA and ACL and 2 tablespoons of Coconut oil and a probitoic. I just started to take sea vegetables (liquid iodine), barley grass and beta 3D glucan (for immune boost), Co q10 and selenium.

I understand that PN is caused by myelin deterioration, but my adrenals are going non stop although I am not mentally anxious. My body wants to go into SR first thing in the morning and it feels like I had 3 cups of coffee!! When I go to bed I can feel my hands tremor!!!!!

What would you suggest to return feeling in my feet and hands?
Is Paleo the solution?
Any thoughts would greatly be appreciated!

One other question, what is the vibration in my feet caused by? Is this the nerves firing up or the blood circulating away from the peripherals?

Thank you

Frankie L

### sooperb — 2013-11-23T19:13:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-guidance-anxiety-causing-pn.8808/post-101478>

Hi there. As someone who has endured anxiety attacks for years I sympathise with your plight. I, too, would class myself as someone who isn't a worrier or a stress head yet I have been plagued. I think there is a subconscious response that provokes an anxiety response to stimuli which kicks your adrenals into action and before you know it you are experiencing a panic/anxiety response. I have lost count of the number of different "symptoms" my body has experienced but I can relate to the numbness and the vibration. My anxiety seems to kick in mostly at night, I have palpitations and sweating and many times I had the vibration. The first time I awoke and felt it, I thought it must be my washing machine spinning, it was just that sort feeling, in the centre of my chest and down to my stomach area, I have seen it described as a cellphone type of buzzing. It was some time before I realised it was me and not the bed shaking! It added to my anxiety of course. I have experienced tingling around my jawline, I have lain in bed shaking as though I had a fever and I have had a buzzing sensation in my foot. Numbness and tingling in my labia at the start of a panic attack, the list is endless.

These days nearly all of those things have gone and I credit my earthing sheet with bringing about the change. I still get palps but these tend to be triggered by either alcohol (I'm not a slop head, 1-2 glasses can set them off) MSG, sugar overload or a combination. Knowing the cause is most of the battle and makes it easier to eliminate the effect. I sleep on the sheet every night, it is earthed actually out of the bedroom window and into the earth and I take it with me if I am away from home for more than one night. It might be worth a look for you too.

### caroline — 2013-11-23T21:27:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-guidance-anxiety-causing-pn.8808/post-101487>

I think MSG is huge and it is very hard, if not impossible, to avoid unless you only eat what you yourself make. I made some bone broth the other day and I added in a tiny bit of stock powder \[a brand I have used for years\] and I had a terrible night.

I am not willing to sacrifice a night's sleep for anything if I can help it!

### sooperb — 2013-11-23T22:14:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-guidance-anxiety-causing-pn.8808/post-101496>

I was aghast to find MSG in Bisto and stock cubes. Both seem to have removed it now but I stopped using either and chose organic. I am very careful, we cook pretty much everything from fresh or we check labels if we have to. Eating out is a lot trickier, I haven't dared have a Chinese takeaway for years, they use so much MSG. I have asked in the Indian restaurant if they use it but the proprietor, who we know quite well, half shrugged and half laughed so I am none the wiser. If he thinks I'm not happy he will say they don't use it just as he told me very seriously he would make sure there was less salt in my food. There wasn't of course.

I've watched Asian chefs cooking on TV programmes, they use a huge amount of salt, probably a throw back to when there wasn't much refrigeration. I'm not against salt per se but I'd stake money on it that they aren't using best additive free sea salt lol.

### caroline — 2013-11-24T00:55:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-some-guidance-anxiety-causing-pn.8808/post-101507>

Sooperb
.... Could you ask for some steamed veggies? And bring your own sea salt? Or some veggies sautÃ©ed in ghee?

### elvey90 — 2013-11-20T11:06:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/checked-labs-help-intrepreting-please.8813/post-101095>

Hey All,

Just got my labs in and was wondering if I can get a bit of help interpreting the results:

Also BOLD means it's elevated according to the prescribe ranges

Vitamin B12 & Folate:

Vitamin B12: 468 pmol/L (150-700) Increased since my last check by 114
R.C Folate: 715 nmol/L (> 630) Increased since my last check by 125

Serum/Plasma Biochemistry

Sodium: 139 mmol/L (136-146)
Potassium: 4.3 mmol/L (3.5-5.0)
Chloride: 102 mmol/L (95-110)
Bicarbonate: **35 mmol/L** (22-31)
Urea: **11.8 mmol/L** (2.3-7.6)
Est.GFR(ml/min): 84 per 1.73sqm (> 60)
Creatinine: 107 umol/L (60-110)
Total Bilbirubin: 7 umol/L (<20)
Ala. Aminotransferase (ALT): 23 U/L (<40)
Asp. Aminotransferase (AST): 20 U/L (5)
Alkaline Phophatase (ALP): 82 U/L (35-110)
Gamma Glutamyl Trans. (GGT): 19 U/L (<40)
Total Protein: 75 g/L (65-85)
Albumin: 46 g/L (38-50)
Globulin: 29 g/L (22-38)

Thyroid Function Test (Serum)

Free T4: 15.4 pmol/L (10.0-19.0)
TSH: 2.22 mIU/L (0.50-4.00)
Free T3: 4.5 pmol/L (3.5-6.5)

**Iron Studies**

Ferriton: **458** ug/L (20-300)
Iron: 13 umol/L (10-30)
Transferrin: 2.2 g/L (2.2-3.7)
Transferrin Sat: 24 % (13-47)

**Serum Glucose**

5.1 mmol/L (4.0-7.8) taken at 7:50PM

**Ultrasensitive C-Reactive Protein**

<0.10 mg/L (0.00 - 10.00)

That's it, please let me know what all of this means and possibly point me in the right direction. I'm looking at the Ultrasensensitive C-Reactive Protein and looks pretty low, meaning I should be doing the Leptin Rx?

Any help would be very much appreciated

Cheers!

### caroline — 2013-11-21T03:33:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/checked-labs-help-intrepreting-please.8813/post-101198>

I am crap at reading labs - but someone will look when they have a chance!

Did you do Vit D, hsCRP, rT3,

You probably will have to talk your Doc into hsCRP - he will think you don't know what you are talking about and just want to do a regular CRP

You can get rT3 thru Healthscope .... they have a functional medicine section. You can also get melatonin thru them.
Your Doc can order these - you have to pay - around $50.00 each if I remember right.
My Doc has requisition forms for them but if your Doc doesn't he can use regular requisition forms. You can call them and get all the info.
They also do a saliva ASI ..... I get mine thru ZRT labs \[canary club} in the U.S. basically same price - but ZRT will give you your results.

Your Doc can also order pregnenolone - extra charge. Just be aware it is batch tested so the results could take 6 weeks.

Jack's book has a great section on labs ... it is available from Amazon ... in kindle or paper

Tun off Video by 7 pm (F.Lux on computer can let you cheat a little) and mellow out until bed with enjoyable reading. Cut the Cable TV and do not listen to news, what good does it do you?

Yea I got flux installed on my comp and currently waiting for my blue blocking glasses to arrive. Why listen to the news when you can read a book


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: a heart warming story of a neurosurgeon.......
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-heart-warming-story-of-a-neurosurgeon.8825/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-11-21T09:42:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-heart-warming-story-of-a-neurosurgeon.8825/post-101230>

Last night on TV there was a beautiful story about a young girl....

Up untill the age of two she had been perfectly fine and then, all of a sudden, she developed acute epilepsy.

She was seizing up to 200 times a day. It was the most heartbreaking story of parents searching for answers for their beautiful little daughter.

They finally found a surgeon in the U.S. .... a Dr. Mathern at California Best hospital. He agreed to see her as soon as they could get there \[from Perth\]. He had answered their email the very next day.

When he examined her he said there was a window of opportunity and it is now. He removed part of her brain. The concern was she would lose the use of one side - if she survived surgery.

It all turned out wonderfully well....

Everyone during this story was so choked up with emotion.

The Doctor said something I have heard Dr. Kruse say .... "when someone is down - I have to extend my hand"

I have tears running down my face as I am typing this .......how lucky we are to have wonderful, talented, and caring Doctors.....

I am not sure that we say "thankyou" enough. we are always quick to criticize and we do need to question everything ...... but we do need to appreciate the legions of talented and caring professionals who look after us when we are down.

### caroline — 2013-11-21T09:50:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-heart-warming-story-of-a-neurosurgeon.8825/post-101231>

I forgot to add ..... it really freaked me out to see a neurosurgeon operating on a child's brain. All I could think of is this is what Dr. K. does on a weekly basis..... I am in awe....

Dr. K. said not long ago that he leaves part of himself on the table every single time ...... thank goodness for your meditation room and your cold tub and your family and the people who care about you .... that would be us ...

sorry about the soap box - I never see it coming..... strange...

### Nick — 2013-11-21T11:41:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-heart-warming-story-of-a-neurosurgeon.8825/post-101238>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=101230)
>
> I am not sure that we say "thankyou" enough.

Couldn't agree more! Life without this knowledge is a horrible thought. Thank You Jack for all you have done.

### HoneyChild — 2013-11-21T17:38:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-heart-warming-story-of-a-neurosurgeon.8825/post-101257>

Wouldn't a ketogenic diet have been better to try before surgery?

### caroline — 2013-11-22T01:28:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-heart-warming-story-of-a-neurosurgeon.8825/post-101284>

> [HoneyChild said:](/goto/post?id=101257)
>

# Thread: Femlae Hair loss
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/femlae-hair-loss.8833/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### motivatedhealthseeker — 2013-11-22T04:17:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/femlae-hair-loss.8833/post-101304>

hello Everyone!
Ive had gluten issues for a while and noticed in college that the hair on the crown of my head were less than the rest of the hair. I wasnt diagnosed with gluten issues until almost 7-8 yrs later. Now all of a sudden I noticed the one side of head, the front, and the crown is also thinning more. I had a lot of hair loss in the last 2-3 months. I thought it was due to extreme stress from losing a sibling but it continues still. I am on the iodine protocol and have been taking 500 mg of metformin. Can the metformin cause hair loss indirectly due to low b12 levels? Does iodine have anything to do with it? I also take 75 mcg of cytomel and have been for about 1.5 yrs. I am 39. I also had a pretty low P/E ratio and had been using progesterone cream..1/4 tsp of Dr Lees Progesterone cream at night. It prob wasnt enough to get to optimal but it was hard finding enough skin to get all that cream into as it was. Pills scare me. So not sure what is causing this hair loss but I am very concerned. I am getting my iron and TIBC, ferritin, b12, free t3, free t4, tsh, and a CMET soon. Does anyone have any suggestions as to what might be causing this and how to deal with it. The two things I am worried about that I have added only in the last couple of months is the metformin and iodine protocol. I take 50 mg iodoral along with 200 mg magnesium, 200 mg selenium, and bcofactors.
Thank you in advance!

### caroline — 2013-11-22T08:56:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/femlae-hair-loss.8833/post-101322>

Are you able to get Dr. Tim Jackson's bootcamp? have you listened to his free webinar? you really need the bootcamp if you can swing it. Dr.K & Misty were offering a plan to pay in two payments for it. Why did you start iodine? what does your Doctor think?
Dr. Kruse has always said that you can't out supplement a bad diet. Dr. Tim is going into excrutiating detail about starting with healing your gut first before you start supplementing willy nilly .... you can get serious detox and really set yourself back.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/femlae-hair-loss.8833/post-101375>

I am soooooo bummed out that I missed Dr Jackson's free webinar. I had tried to click the link but its not working. Is there anyyyy way to listen to it now? I am going to have to travel out of country for a month but will look into the bootcamp when I get back but yikes $399 is kinda steep. I started iodine because I thought it would be a good idea for general health and I used to get ovarian cysts. I started out slow and ramped up as per the protocol. I got my blood work back today and it seems iron is pretty low. I held it in optimal for a year and then it just tanked all of a sudden and the only thing different was the iodine and the metformin.
Serum iron 57 (35-155)
TIBC 290 (250-450)
iron sat 20%
ferritin 78 (15-150)
free t3 4.2 (2.0-4.4)
TSH .013
free t4 .06
I am on 75 mcg of cytomel
B12 1600 (211-946) HIGH I fasted for 3 days off the b12 but does this look like methylation issues?
Thanks in advance for any insight!

### nonchalant — 2013-11-22T18:13:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/femlae-hair-loss.8833/post-101378>

What do you eat, motivated?

### motivatedhealthseeker — 2013-11-22T21:06:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/femlae-hair-loss.8833/post-101390>

I eat a strict gluten free diet..4 eggs with 1 cup of egg beaters with mushroom, kale, spinach, green peppers, onions etc in it with 2 turkey sausages for bfast every day and then lunch and dinner usually consist of steamed cauliflower with some kind of fish or chicken or a turkey burger with no bun. Dessert when I have it consists of 1/2 avacado with raw cocoa powder with almond milk and 1/2 banana and a spoon of gluten free protein powder whipped into a pudding with some walnuts.I will eat blue corn chips as a snack sometimes with picante sauce and 1/4 avacado and maybe some sour cream if its available. I dont eat any kind of added sugar or any fruit except the 1/2 banana. I am surprised my glucose is the way it is. I need to work on going to bed on time but lately its been extremely hectic as I am trying to leave town for a bit.

### nonchalant — 2013-11-22T22:03:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/femlae-hair-loss.8833/post-101394>

I know what you mean about the gluten, when I dropped it I started to get my health back.

I stopped losing hair, and regrew it back when I started eating large amounts of animal fats and meat. Then I started adding lots of seafood and my skin gradually changed as well, becoming very soft yet tough. Are you in the northern hemisphere? Perhaps you should really increase the fat. Shellfish, beef, pork, even consider tossing the egg whites and substituting yolks. You will probably have to gradually add in fat; your body has to adjust to digesting it.

### caroline — 2013-11-23T02:10:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/femlae-hair-loss.8833/post-101419>

now if I eat anything with MSG I have huge problems ... like sausages etc.

ditch the corn chips!

Have you looked at the leptin reset using an epi paleo template?

Please think about becoming a silver or gold member .... you certainly don't have to. If you become a gold member you have access to everything \[all the webinars etc.\] at anytime that is convenient for you and as many times as you need to "get it" If you are a gold member you get all the live Q&A's that follow a webinar a few days later and they go for a couple of hours and you can ask Dr. K. anything! The bootcamps are much less for members.

Our understanding increases over time and I will go back and watch an old webinar and think...WTH....how did I miss that? or I just didn't have enough knowledge at the time to get it.
I probably have less money than anyone here \[retired\] but I look at it as an investment in my health and an optimum life. I want to go out with my boots on!! My Dh was sick for many years and wound up with vascular dementia ....I can't let that happen to me - it is heartbreaking.
so - to me - a gold membership is a necessity ..... I need to soak up as much of this as possible and I love learning all this stuff .... well - trying anyway! .... not to mention all the inspiring people here that motivate me every single day to do better.

Dr. K. is shining his flashlight for us - what a rare opportunity ... and I want to take full advantage of every single second.

sorry about the soap box........

Since going gluten free, I only have a few things I rotate out for my meals as there wasnt much time to think about how to eat and what to eat. I have looked at the leptin reset but I also had adrenal fatigue and was told not to limit too many carbs..was also doing the circadian rhythm method. ### Determined — 2013-11-25T00:22:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/femlae-hair-loss.8833/post-101542>

**Flying**

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=101538)
>
> I have to try to remember where Dr. K. talked about flying .......He drinks a ton of water and takes alot of coQ10 depending on how long he is flying.......
> I do think that you need bare feet. I understand the caankles! My last few flights were not pleasant to say the least!

I was just listening to this podcast (again) yesterday & took greater note as I fly twice a week - abt 2 -3 hrs at a time. Not good for us!

\- bare feet on the metal as the avionics are grounded
\- 2-400 mgs CoQ10 prior to trip
\- then same dose every hour during the flight
\- big mob ? 32 Ozs cold RO water - and then 16ozs every hour thereafter
\- CT

He talks a bit abt long haul flights and some ideas for reducing these effects & also Melatonin use

I love how I am always learning more w every listen to Jack - bit by bit it's going in even w/out a science geek brain - just takes a lot longer !! Haha

Hair transplant is an absolutely success if done by a good transplant surgeon. These days world seem to be flooded with doctors saying transplant. But I am sure if you look for someone good you’ll find one. I will suggest a hair transplant for following reasons:

• Safety of the procedure: With various advancements in technique and instrumentation, it is not much different from dental treatment. Only thing is that the process looks simple so many untrained & unethical people having started doing this. Beware of low-cost quacks.

# Thread: Insurance deadline tommorrow need help
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insurance-deadline-tommorrow-need-help.8835/>

### Raiken3712 — 2013-11-22T07:33:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/insurance-deadline-tommorrow-need-help.8835/post-101313>

I can't get insurance at Walgreens after November 22 and I think thats my best option.

I have no clue what to get.

I wish there were an easy way to link all my options so people could give me ideas and thoughts....

My choices are Aetna, BCBS, UnitedHealthcare Bronze, Bronze Plus, Silver, Gold and Platinum

Bronze has 2,750 Deductible while Platinum has none the costs are much higher though with it.

Also Platinum seems to need In Network providers and the places I want to go like

[http://paleophysiciansnetwork.com/doctors/TX/Irving/maximal-health](http://paleophysiciansnetwork.com/doctors/TX/Irving/maximal-health)

are not In Network.

I've heard sometimes you don't have to meet the deductible for certain things, but I'm not sure what...I really have no clue.

The price for Bronze plans is an average of 15 dollars every two weeks with the subsidy they give us.

The platinum which I'm sure is a horrible option for me is 126 dollars.

Should I just pick the cheapest to avoid the Obamacare tax?

Also for Dental I have Bronze at 1,000 annual maximum Deductible is 100...sounds low I don't understand this at all...nothing like the medical here.

Bronze price is around 4.00 per period.

Platinum is around 50 cents....with credits and no deductible or annual maximum...that actually sounds good...I have to missing something.

Aetna, UnitedHealthcare, Metlife, and DeltaCare USA Platinum are the choices.

Vision also doesn't sound that bad Bronze is from 1.00 per period to Platinum which is 3.60.

Bronze is Exams covered in full. Frame discount and lenses discount.

Gold is 10.00 copay for exams 200 frame allowance 10 dollar copay for lenses no platinum option.

Choices are VSP, Metlife, UnitedHealthcare

I should have done this sooner...I know, but if anyone has any input I really need it.

Thanks

I haven't wrangled in my junk eating habits...but I haven't had anything major happen healthwise..

I am constantly tired, my knees hurt from inflammation and a bicycle accident that never healed probably, and my lower back hurts occasionally.

I think I might be able to deal with most of my issues by starting to eat better and exercise, but I have to sign up for insurance because of Obamacare so I want to make the right choices.

# Thread: Conference: Transcriptional Regulation in Development
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/conference-transcriptional-regulation-in-development.8844/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gracelind — 2013-11-22T22:40:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/conference-transcriptional-regulation-in-development.8844/post-101397>

Hmmm . . . Don't see anything here about QED.
Plus, it's disconcerting if it's a novel event to "bring together researchers working in developmental biology with those studying the specific molecular and cellular mechanisms of transcriptional regulation."

Back in my more-naive days and before this journey on the medical self-education I never really wanted to have, I used to believe that scientific research was coherent and inclusive.

**Conference: Transcriptional Regulation in Development**
[http://www.cell-symposia-transcriptional-regulation.com/](http://www.cell-symposia-transcriptional-regulation.com/)

_It is clear that cell fate decisions and developmental processes both drive and are driven by changes in transcriptional regulation. Over the years, insights from developmental biology have fueled advances in our understanding of transcription and chromatin while studies of transcription mechanisms have provided powerful tools and concepts for understanding some of the molecular events underlying developmental biology. Our understanding of key concepts such as regulation by non-coding RNAs, histone modifications, pluripotency, differentiation, and epigenetics would likely be rather limited without the ongoing dialogue between these disciplines.

For this symposium, we aim to continue to bring together researchers working in developmental biology with those studying the specific molecular and cellular mechanisms of transcriptional regulation. We hope that this meeting will provide a forum for scientists to challenge assumptions about whether a seemingly important detail of enzymology is in fact biologically relevant and to discuss approaches to identifying molecular basis for interesting but poorly understood biological phenomena._

No QED mentioned here, either:

[http://www.cell-symposia-humanevolution.com/index.html](http://www.cell-symposia-humanevolution.com/index.html)

**Cell Symposium: Evolution of Modern Humans - From Bones to Genomes**
March 16 - 18, 2014, Hotel MeliÃ¡, Sitges, Spain

# Thread: Optimal/Protocol for Social Eating
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-protocol-for-social-eating.8847/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### elvey90 — 2013-11-23T03:53:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-protocol-for-social-eating.8847/post-101428>

Hey All,

On Dave Asprey's page he has a protocol on what to do if you 'accidentally' eat crappy foods (can't find the link someone please find it  ).
Was wondering what everyone does when they eat non Epi-Paleo foods when they go out on social gatherings, please feel free to share.

Just to make it easier to categorize, here's a list:

1) What you do before heading out
2) What you do during the social event
3) What you do after the social event

Please keep in mind that this is what you do if there isn't any or limited Epi-Paleo foods at the event.

What I personally do:

1) Before heading out:
\- If I don't feel like eating at all, I'll have a high protein and fat meal about 1hr before I have to head out, that curbs any hunger.
\- If I don't have time, I have a 1-2 cups of coconut milk mixed with raw/organic cacao powder and if I have Maca powder I add that in too.

2) During the social event:
\- Try and find Epi-Paleo foods
\- I used to bring a lemon, cut in half and squeeze into a cup then drink it without water before I eat, might get some funny looks
\- I don't eat at all and drink tea or water

3) After the social event:
\- If I ate I would drink 2 glasses of water with 1x lemon with manuka honey
\- Take Activated Charcoal (mentioned on Dave Asprey's site), as soon as the meal is consumed.

It'll be good to grab if anyone wants to share their protocol if you have one

### caroline — 2013-11-23T06:18:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-protocol-for-social-eating.8847/post-101440>

I always eat something before going out, even if only a BPC. It is really hard to make informed picks if you are really hungry.

A dinner party is hard....I still eat before hand and, if necessary, just move food around on a plate. Now I just bring something I can eat and share.

Anyway. - it isn't the end of the world.....people are more important than food. Just do what you need to do (eat) and move on.

But - for me - I can't tolerate gluten very well and definitely can't do MSG ..... And people get that.

Always call a restaurant the day before and explain your situation. A lot of people eat paleo these days .....

At my tiny farmers market - I asked for chicken liver and they told me it always sells out first because there are so many paleo people. I was pretty surprised!

We should never be afraid to ask for what we need .....that is just plain dumb!

The one thing I do find ...friends worry that I eat too much protein. They don't get the high fat, medium protein, low carb thing. But having said that - they think everyone needs some sugar......

When I was in central Asia travelling - I was asking for butter for my tea and everyone thought that was pretty cool. in the long run it was the vodka that got me...LOLl

### Inger — 2013-11-23T09:41:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-protocol-for-social-eating.8847/post-101455>

Ignore the food and put your interest and focus on people. Easy  No one will feel bad because everyone likes to be recognized

+

I bring my own food
or eat before I go
or pick up something from a shop on the way to eat
or eat only the stuff that is acceptable for me (you will mostly know before the event what kind of food there will be)

I never eat bad stuff. The only things I cheat with on such occasions are; HWC, raw cheeses, salad or some veggies, berries, nuts, cooked seafood and meat (I eat raw at home)

Help cleaning up, serving, etc. I always say I can be the waitress...lol Or look for the children when their parents are eating. There is always stuff/work to do on parties!

This is actually not an issue at all when we stop focusing so much on food and focus more on person = love

### prAna303 — 2013-11-23T09:58:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-protocol-for-social-eating.8847/post-101458>

> 1) What you do before heading out

I do nothing special, often the event is at night so food is not a real problem for me. Often i eat my last meal quite early which do not disturb my way of living. My days now, because it is winter here, eating is about 1000 grams of seafood / fatty fish, up to 500 grams of lard, some eggs and water from a stream in the mountains.

> 2) What you do during the social event

For me food has always been about fixing my offset, this i learned during the last eight years. So for me food does not mean anything at a party, i do not have feelings for it, so i do not look at that corner. For me the event is always about connecting, this is what i really feed off, human vibrations. When you connect with others, food and the cravings for it goes away. This is getting tougher and tougher today because of all this iPhone, smartphone updating illness that struck the world, but i always manage to attract a crowd that will resonate.

> 3) After the social event:

Depends, if it was a meaningful event, i suck up on the feelings, let them feed me. If it was a EMF hell, i go out in the wild and sleep outside. If it is summer i simply go freediving, to make the offset in me.

# Thread: Please help - having a real snafu calculating bun/creatinine ratio.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-having-a-real-snafu-calculating-bun-creatinine-ratio.8850/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Horse — 2013-11-23T07:08:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-having-a-real-snafu-calculating-bun-creatinine-ratio.8850/post-101443>

Would someone kindly help me out with this one thing? My Bun (Urea) is 6.7mmol/L (Ref 2.5 - 9.0) and my Creatinine is 55 umol/L.(Ref 40 - 100) I understand I need to have both readings in the same units so I converted the umols to mmols (won't put conversion here as I don't wish to pre-empt). I then thought it was a simple matter of dividing the Bun by the Creatinine to get the ratio; but I come up with ridiculous numbers!!

If someone is able to help; could you please spell out the steps of the calculation as well as the answer (ie the ratio) please? I'd be **so grateful.**Thank you.

### Horse — 2013-11-23T08:53:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-having-a-real-snafu-calculating-bun-creatinine-ratio.8850/post-101452>

PS. After 2 days of pulling my hair out with this; I've just realised that the reference range for Bun/Creatinine ratio (10-20:1) is different to the Urea/Creatinine ratio (as this ratio is in SI Units and is 40-100:1). Therefore JK's ideal of the ratio needing to be under 10 applies to the former. A ratio of under 40 would seem to be the ideal for the latter? I understand the actual calculation method (formula to arrive at the ratio) would be the same; but the ratios themselves don't correlate due to the fact that SI values are proportionately greater resulting in a higher comparative ratio. I hope this makes sense; but I would still appreciate the help please. I simply had not realised there'd be a complication with the ratio if using different units to the US. BTW I realised all of this from finally finding [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BUN-to-creatinine\_ratio](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BUN-to-creatinine_ratio) THANKS AGAIN!

### HoneyChild — 2013-11-23T12:19:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-having-a-real-snafu-calculating-bun-creatinine-ratio.8850/post-101466>

I used these calculators for my own results and hope that I did the correct thing...

[http://www.endmemo.com/medical/unitconvert/Creatinine.php](http://www.endmemo.com/medical/unitconvert/Creatinine.php)
[http://www.endmemo.com/medical/unitconvert/Urea\_nitrogen.php](http://www.endmemo.com/medical/unitconvert/Urea_nitrogen.php)

6.7mmol/L = 18.77 mg/dL
55 umol/L = 0.62 mg/dL

18.77 divided by 0.62 = 30.27 = seriously dehydrated!!

### Horse — 2013-11-24T00:13:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-having-a-real-snafu-calculating-bun-creatinine-ratio.8850/post-101504>

Thank you so much for that HoneyChild - so greatly appreciated. However, I have to confess my head is still done in on the matter. I'd posted further information quite close on the heels of my first query above; and I got a message that it had to be checked by a moderator before being posted but it never showed up. I can't understand why. It would have been great for you to see that further information before you replied...

Basically, the further information was that I'd just found in Wikipedia a sentence which really set the cat among the pigeons. It would be worth you taking a look. [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BUN-to-creatinine\_ratio](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BUN-to-creatinine_ratio) Basically, the sticking point is the second sentence of the first paragraph (containing the bolded words) - " The units of creatinine are also different (μmol/L), **and this value is termed the urea-to-creatinine ratio.** The ratio may be used to determine the cause of acute kidney injury or dehydration." So if this is correct, then converting the creatinine (in my case 55 umol/L) to mg/dL and then using that in the equation would be totally invalid. Wikipedia seems to be emphatically saying that my creatinine value of 55 IS ACTUALLY THE RATIO!!!?? (The range for the ratio in SI units is 40 - 100:1 whereas the range for the ratio in US Standard Units is 10 - 20:1). I understand that JK is saying the ideal would be for it to be under 10 in Standard Units. After having pulled my hair out for days only to come across this info has really confused me no end. What I'm saying is that your methodology seems to be spot on; but if Wikipedia is correct; we're seriously thwarting/deranging the result. I hope this makes sense.

On the other hand, if my ratio is indeed 30.27 which you kindly calculated for me, it's so terribly dismaying as I've been drinking around 3 litres of pure filtered water a day for years. I understand that it's the quality of the water which is the critical factor but it's dismaying nonetheless.

I really do hope this makes some sense to you (and anyone else seeing this) and that this post shows up successfully!
