### Ricardo — 2013-12-16T20:18:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnulin-pf.9120/post-103617>

I was taking quite a bit of cinnamon then I heard that it contains substances that can accumulate in the system so I looked for a better option. Now I take much less cinnamon and I have added Cinnulin PF. It is a water soluble extract that has proven to have some interesting benefits.

Cinnulin PF will lower fasting blood glucose and systolic blood pressure. It will also help increase lean body mass.

Its helping me to get through the Leptin RX reset.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2129164/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2129164/)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-learn-about-obesity-researchers-turn-to-grizzly-bears.9124/post-103655>

> But bears do know a thing or two about weight gain, and that makes them more like people than rats whose genes are manipulated to make them obese. In the weeks before hibernating, bears pack away enough apples, berries and salmon to put on 100 pounds or more under their brown fur. Their bad cholesterol jumps and blood pressure spikes. But unlike humans, their health doesn't suffer. Bears' arteries don't clog from the gorging, nor do the animals battle heart attacks or turn into diabetics.

[http://online.wsj.com/news/articles...4081632?mod=WSJ\_business\_LeftSecondHighlights](http://online.wsj.com/news/articles/SB10001424052702303330204579246003604081632?mod=WSJ_business_LeftSecondHighlights)

# Thread: Man's severed hand grafted to ankle after accident
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mans-severed-hand-grafted-to-ankle-after-accident.9130/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1

### HoneyChild — 2013-12-18T12:28:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mans-severed-hand-grafted-to-ankle-after-accident.9130/post-103734>

[http://www.theguardian.com/world/video/2013/dec/17/severed-hand-grafted-ankle-accident-video](http://www.theguardian.com/world/video/2013/dec/17/severed-hand-grafted-ankle-accident-video)

Doctors in China have saved a man's severed hand by grafting it onto his ankle. Xiao Wei lost his right hand in an accident at work, but could not have it reattached to his arm immediately. Instead the hand was kept alive by stitching it to Xiao's left ankle, enabling him to borrowing a blood supply from arteries in the leg

# Thread: Cortico Basal Degeneration
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortico-basal-degeneration.9132/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Flora — 2013-12-18T15:28:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortico-basal-degeneration.9132/post-103740>

My brother has just been diagnosed with CBD. He's gone from having slight neuropathy in his fingertips to losing the use of his left arm in the last 2 years. He also has sleep apnea and uses a CPAP machine. He's 63.

He was an Air Force (UK) pilot for 30+ years and flew every type of fighter jet -and clearly this is the end result. This Australian report on high-flying pilots is interesting [http://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/265036.php](http://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/265036.php)

I have sent him and his wife lots of specific blogs / threads / summaries from this site to read and I know they have already started to implement some things - turning off the wi-fi and removing the computer from the bedroom and eating more fish as a good start - and I've sent them coconut oil.
He is taking cod liver oil, B vitamins (presumably in a complex format) and an Ibuprofen tablet(!) each day - as suggested by their doctor and Neurologist.

Unfortunately I don't see him often as we live in different countries, but am desperate to get him to try CT, a ketogenic seafood diet, grounding etc. He's no longer working since a week ago-which is good because he no longer has to undergo the EMF soup of a train commute to the City each day and being attached to a computer all day.

I would just like to know if there is anything else I can suggest to them - am thinking that PQQ and COQ10 could be a good supplements? or if anyone has any experience of this neuro-degenerative disease or similar (eg Parkinson's) where they have seen improvements or a slowing of disease progress from adopting epi-paleo, as I know the prognosis is not good according to conventional medicine.

Dr Kruse - I would be so grateful for any general input from you...

Many thanks

# Thread: Green Tea
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-tea.9150/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### QiGuy1997 — 2013-12-21T03:28:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-tea.9150/post-103944>

Until about 4 months ago, I drink a few cups of tea everyday. However, I began doing research on tea and this led me to discontinuing my consumption of tea because of it's high content of aluminum and it's extremely high content of fluoride (far more than fluoridated water, in fact). When I discovered Dr. Kruse's work it simply reinforced my thoughts on fluoride; it's terrible. However, I noticed that Dr. Kruse still recommends large consumption of green tea. That's a heck of a lot of fluoride! Does anyone know how he's able to justify this large tea intake in terms of fluoride? Do the health benefits of green tea outweigh the large dose of fluoride? This seems rather unlikely. And what about the aluminum in it?

### nonchalant — 2013-12-21T17:07:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-tea.9150/post-103963>

I know there was a discussion previously about the fluoride content of green tea. About the aluminum, good point. I'll be sure to add some silica water with my green tea.

### QiGuy1997 — 2013-12-21T23:03:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-tea.9150/post-103988>

I drink bamboo tea as my source of aluminum chelating silica. I'm concerned, however, as a study showed that only oligomeric silica reduces aluminum absorption and monomeric silica has no effect. I'm not sure if the bamboo tea's silica is monomeric or oligomeric. Also, I can't seem to find the green tea/fluoride discussion, could you maybe link it? I'm hesitant to drink something so rich in fluoride without some strong reassurance first.

### caroline — 2013-12-21T23:12:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-tea.9150/post-103990>

I don't remember that discussion ........ that is great that you are researching and questioning ........

I hope others chime in.

### QiGuy1997 — 2013-12-22T00:55:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-tea.9150/post-104005>

Let's hope that someone can provide a reassuring response to the fluoride/aluminum issue. I want my tea back! xD

### caroline — 2013-12-22T01:37:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-tea.9150/post-104010>

maybe an "Ask Jack"?

### QiGuy1997 — 2013-12-23T05:55:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/green-tea.9150/post-104107>

### kennyjames — 2014-01-15T05:27:31+0200

# Thread: Overwhelming CT Experience
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overwhelming-ct-experience.9154/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chris Wyllie — 2013-12-21T18:33:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overwhelming-ct-experience.9154/post-103968>

Hello,
I've recently started the CT protocol. After reading the awesome hormonal, immune and neurological benefits of CT, I was eager to begin. Very ambitious, I jumped right into the cold tub, placing ice over my abdomen and back while wearing a compression shirt. My cold tubs have been roughly 45 minutes. I have willed myself to deal with the violent shivering that ensues for about an hour after the tub. I have a few concerns because after the last few CT sessions, I've experienced welts all over my body and feel as though my immune system has been run over by a truck. I recall Dave Asprey, the Bulletproof Exec falling asleep on cold ice bags, and similarly experiencing ice burns.
Questions
1\. Did I proceed to the cold tubs to quickly? Is more CT, the longer and colder the better? At what point do I need to be weary of potential harm?
2\. After experiencing the welts and swelling, I a concerned that rather than diminishing inflammation I have induced a massive increase in cytokines and inflammation based on shocking the body? Is this the case?
3\. Would CT still be recommended while battling an illness? Would the increase in cortisol, and immune system replenishment through the cold be a quick way to eradicate the illness?
4\. Is the case that more CT at this point would be effective in reducing the inflammation induced by initially overwhelming the body?
Thanks so much Jack for your time and willingness to answer each and every one of our questions. You're optimal productivity is truly amazing.

### Josh — 2013-12-21T19:14:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overwhelming-ct-experience.9154/post-103969>

In everything, gradient is important. This has been my life learning. Leap before you look works sometimes and makes for great stories, and the human body is a delicate dynamic system striving for homeostasis based on the available resources and conditions. Exceed its ability to adapt and it becomes less graceful. Consider standing before Mount Everest with no conditioning or climbing skill and saying what the hell...All this being said, I found that the gradient for CT has been very very very important. Dr. Kruse has detailed things to consider int the CT blogs, I believe 7 is a key. Also, the appendix of the book is a must read. I skipped the face, but spent a month with the abdominal ice pack. Burnt myself, worked on omega 3/6, etc.. I can now handle a tub on days when I am not tired. This is not the same thing as pushing yourself through a sports event even though you are not in shape. There also, you will hurt yourself. Gradient, the steady horse wins the race as one mentor cautioned me....

### SeaHorse — 2013-12-21T21:18:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overwhelming-ct-experience.9154/post-103978>

Cold uticaria (hives, welts) can be a sign of bad 03/06 ratio...something to look at before you head back into too much CT. There was a reason that the protocol involved incremental steps

### Chris Wyllie — 2013-12-21T22:36:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overwhelming-ct-experience.9154/post-103983>

Josh thanks for the valuable words of wisdom. Great advice and I will certainly go back and review CT-7.

### caroline — 2013-12-21T22:54:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overwhelming-ct-experience.9154/post-103985>

Yep ..... re read CT #7 really carefully. Dr. K. is very specific with his protocols. Sometimes we don't always know the exact reasons because he hasn't laid it out yet .....

What temperature are you doing? Are you measuring your body temp?

Jack is very specific about all of this ....... all lot of us learned the hard way...

Always remember that Jack is about 10 years ahead of us on his own journey ....so he has definitely been there!

We are always in so much of a hurry - I know I am ..... but trust in the process and never stop reading and asking questions! In our great hurry we often overlook really important things - or it doesn't quite sink in ..... and our brain isn't optimal......

Have you seen the C/T webinar?

### caroline — 2013-12-21T22:57:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overwhelming-ct-experience.9154/post-103986>

never, ever forget to trust your own instincts!

### nonchalant — 2013-12-22T03:13:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overwhelming-ct-experience.9154/post-104018>

Welcome Chris! Sounds like you have enthusiasm!

The Cold Thermogenesis Protocol blog, [http://www.jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/](http://www.jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/)
is what you need. It starts with face dunking only, and progresses.

My first ice bath was only for about 7 minutes, at 55F.

### nicld — 2013-12-23T01:04:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overwhelming-ct-experience.9154/post-104088>

Welcome Chris,

I would take it slower. I started in the summer so I could rewarm outside and started out slower. I did jump into the baths right away but it was summer and we do not have central AC so the colder baths at night helped me sleep. I have never done the ice with a compression shirt but that is my N=1 and my fat is in my butt and thighs.

I think Jack has said to get all the CT one can but is had to be what you can tolerate. Today, I am trying for 2 1/2 hours total today but some days I can barely get in 30 minutes. It does take time but so worth it.

Good luck,
Nicole

### Chris Wyllie — 2013-12-27T22:35:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/overwhelming-ct-experience.9154/post-104306>

Thanks Nicid,
I intend on becoming a Gold Member and viewing the webinars - specifically Deep CT - so that I can optimize my CT sessions. After my seventh cold tub, 30 minutes in a bath have filled with half-melted snow and cold water, I believe that I have reached a significant improvement in my cold adaptation. I felt a wave off exhilaration after the tub, and only shivered for around 15 minutes.
I usually engaged in some intense Qigong exercises, post-CT, to quickly turn the fire back on in the body. Boy are the tendons and ligaments supremely robust after a CT session.

# Thread: I cant do 40 degree cold bath or showers no more
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-do-40-degree-cold-bath-or-showers-no-more.9172/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### x_danny_x — 2013-12-23T20:13:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-do-40-degree-cold-bath-or-showers-no-more.9172/post-104134>

I have cold adaptive to the 50-55 degree water....I decided to go foward and cold adapt even further.....I could now hold my own for about 1 hour with 40-45 degree water...My body then suddenly could not handle it and I had to revert back to a higher temp of 48-50 degrees...I was doing 40 degree baths or showers for 3 months with no issue. The 40 degree gave the most benefit with energy, power, able to work out with heavier weights and put on some muscle.

I didnt get any shivering or problems. It just felt too cold....I couldnt go back.

### Danco3636 — 2013-12-23T21:23:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-do-40-degree-cold-bath-or-showers-no-more.9172/post-104142>

Interesting. 40 for an hour. That is crazy.

### nonchalant — 2013-12-24T02:33:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-do-40-degree-cold-bath-or-showers-no-more.9172/post-104164>

Danny, perhaps it is because the sun is less bright, giving you less energy each day? I know the feeling, you just can't do it anymore. You may need to find another source of energy before/during CT.

### x_danny_x — 2013-12-24T16:45:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-do-40-degree-cold-bath-or-showers-no-more.9172/post-104191>

the sun...never thought it would be an issue...its winter here in New York....so sun sets quite early.....what other energy sources do you have in mind?

### Shijin13 — 2013-12-24T16:57:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-cant-do-40-degree-cold-bath-or-showers-no-more.9172/post-104193>

Danny

at the colder temps - go for shorter sessions - you don't need the same "Time" in the tank to get the same benefits. I prefer the colder temps b/c I can get the same benefits at the warmer temps where I have to sit longer (I don't always have the time for 1hr in 50 deg water) but 5-15 mins depending on temp is do able!

see if backing off the time helps. we don't all need to be Deep CTing to get positive benefits!!!

### x_danny_x — 2013-12-24T22:17:55+0200

> Danny
>
> at the colder temps - go for shorter sessions - you don't need the same "Time" in the tank to get the same benefits. I prefer the colder temps b/c I can get the same benefits at the warmer temps where I have to sit longer (I don't always have the time for 1hr in 50 deg water) but 5-15 mins depending on temp is do able!

### Ricardo — 2013-12-23T22:11:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/circadian-soiftware.9173/post-104150>

This software works best if you input your coordinates. The city you live in (or closest to) could suffice. Then it creates a pattern of light frequencies ( which you can adjust ) so that after dark your computer screen emits less blue light frequency.

It seems to work well enough: I get drowsy within minutes of the screen emitting the warm orange light.

This might help,

[http://justgetflux.com/](http://justgetflux.com/)

PS: Free for Windows

### sooperb — 2014-01-05T01:27:41+0200

# Thread: Nausea
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cabiria — 2013-12-24T02:09:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104161>

Hi,
I've been having nausea for the past two weeks and it doesn't seem to improve. Initially it seemed to occur only after meals, but now it is throughout the day. Lately I've increased the amount of fat in my diet. However, few years ago I was diagnosed with gallbladder stones and I'm wondering whether this could have something to do with it. I don't have any abdominal pain...

### fitness@home — 2013-12-24T02:34:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104165>

Cabiria, do you use any type of digestive enzymes? Maybe your body needs Some help digesting the foods since you've increased the fat.

I don't tolerate nausea well. I'm not sure, why but a pinch of Celtic salt on my tongue always helps me when I have it.

### caroline — 2013-12-24T04:39:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104170>

Please give us some context ..... Have you seen a Doctor?

I got pancreatitis from gall stone problems. I was very sick and wound up in hospital....

I am certainly not saying that is your problem .....but wouldn't it be a good idea to be seen by a professional?

Why have you increased the fat in your diet? When I had gall bladder/gall stone issues I couldn't tolerate fat..

Please get looked at.....

I don't remember having any pain..... But I couldn't stop throwing up....

### cabiria — 2013-12-24T05:35:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104171>

Yes,I've started taking enzymes, but they don't seem to make a difference with the nausea...I've been on a gluten free diet for almost three years now and have increased the amount of saturated fat gradually. Now I'm eating mostly grain free and seasonal food, so my diet is better then ever. I've never been on a low fat diet before, but I was definitely eating a diet higher in carbs and more polyunsaturated fats...
Thank you for the tip on the Celtic salt, I will try it.

### cabiria — 2013-12-24T05:48:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104173>

Caroline, I have an appointment to see a gastroenterologist tomorrow morning. I increased the amount of fat in my diet as I was changing my diet from a high carb diet into a paleo diet. I didn't seem to have any trouble digesting the fat until now...
When I was diagnosed with the gallbladder stones, the doctor suggested removing the gallbladder, no other option was suggested...How did you solve your gall stone issues?

### Josh — 2013-12-24T07:44:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104177>

consider this at least for information and a perspective:

[http://www.healthbeyondhype.com/info/preparing-for-the-essential-liver-gallbladder-flush](http://www.healthbeyondhype.com/info/preparing-for-the-essential-liver-gallbladder-flush)

Doctors do not have a lot of alternatives. I have done dozens of these and passed stones 1 inch in diameter. This does not mean it is right for you, but it is worth considering before you lose an important organ.

Also, consider backing off on fatty foods until you sort it out as the fat stimulates more bile flow that may be blocked by stones.

### caroline — 2013-12-24T08:58:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104179>

I Had my gall bladder out years ago .... I was never given options. I would not do it now.

Have you seen Dr. K.'s blog post about your gallbladder? very important read before your appointment.

Dr. Kruse is teaching us to be informed about our own health, trust our instincts and to never, ever settle. If you need to get a second and third opinion .... do so.

Have you read Jack's book? ... The Epi Paleo RX .... important read for you to get a handle on all this. It is available in kindle and paper from Amazon.

My DH refused to have his gall bladder out and was bullied by lots of Docs about being so silly. He was very sick - but he cut all fat and ate lightly until he started feeling better \[and lost weight\] ..... he never did have it out!

I assume you are female? How old if I may ask? other health history? Dr. K. needs context. Have you thought of starting a journal?

If you go to the home page you will find a blog index and a search feature

The blog you want is CPC #3 Do you need a gall bladder

Please keep reading and asking questions and we will try to point you in the right direction

### caroline — 2013-12-24T09:11:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104180>

### cabiria — 2013-12-24T10:29:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104182>

Yes, I'm a female, 42 years old, 5'6", 120lbs. In terms of health history, I had shingles in my late 20s, 2 pregnancies (I was 36 and 39 years old), both C-section delivery. During the first one I developed gestational diabetes, which I controlled through diet only. Subsequently, I also discovered I had gallstones (no pain, but I had an ultrasound done when liver enzymes results were high at a regular checkup). At the end of my second pregnancy I developed Cholestasis of pregnancy. After I turned 40 I started having IBS symptoms, and I got interested in reading more about nutrition... From Weston Price, to "wheat belly", paleo community, Dr. JK, I started tweaking my diet. The biggest changes were removing the wheat from my diet and no longer using vegetable oils in cooking (just coconut oil, butter and lard). Removing the wheat solved both my IBS symptoms (now I only occasionally get the abdominal pain), and cleared a red rash I had on my thighs for years. Also, in the last 6 years, I've been having on and off tingling and numbness in my upper back, right side (same area where I had shingles). For the longest time I thought it was due to a pinched nerve because of my poor posture. Visits to a chiropractor seemed to solve them for a while. After reading Dr. JK blog on peripheral neuropathy, I started thinking that might be what the tingling on my back is, so I'm trying to improve further my diet by reducing the carbs more and increasing the amount of fat, but it appears I have to take care of my gallbladder stones first... However, if the nausea is due to the gallbladder, shouldn't it be only after meals, instead of throughout the day?

### caroline — 2013-12-24T11:07:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104183>

When I had Gall Bladder attacks - I would have pretty serious pain in the upper back ... I can't remember where exactly now - but it was a pretty traditional place for pain. I don't remember throwing up - but it was a very long time ago! I am pretty sure that the pain often happened in the middle of the nite as well.

But the pancreatitis that developed was awful - could not stop throwing up... and I was really sick for over a week and in the hospital.

At least tomorrow you will see a Doc and hopefully get some answers.

There is a newer book - Grain Brain by a Dr. Perlmutter that is supposed to be excellent. There was just a free Gluten summit a couple of weeks ago - Did you happen to catch that?

Have you had blood work done? Did you read Jack's gallbladder blog?

Please let us know how your appointment goes tomorrow.

Where are you located?

This is going to be a lousy Christmas for you .... so sorry ..... but at least you should get some answers tomorrow. Please try really hard to hang on to your gall bladder.

### cabiria — 2013-12-24T17:48:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104203>

Thank you for all your help! Yes, I did read Dr. JK blog on the gallbladder, and I'll just read it again now before the dr. appointment.
I'll also check out the references you mention - I haven't followed the gluten free summit.
Here's a bit more about my overall diet/environment:
0-18th - lived in a small city in Romania; food 100% seasonal, mostly home cooked from scratched (bread, deli meats, cheese were pretty much the only processed foods), very low EMF environment. However, I ate quite a lot of grains (mostly bread).
18th - 24th - moved to big city in Romania for college; food still 100% seasonal, but less good quality.
24th - 32 - moved to Virginia, US; very bad diet (mostly fast food, microwave food); stress (working and going to college in the same time); higher EMF (studying computers); fluctuating between sedentary life and bouts of stressful gym exercise. In my early 30s started to wise up about eating habits: started to eat organic, went to farmer's markets, but diet overall high in starches and wheat.
32 - 40 - moved to LA, CA; diet increasingly better: more and more home cooked from scratch; organic mostly and from farmer's markets or CSA, but didn't paid much attention to seasonal food; Still diet high in starches, moderate protein, probably high in polyunsaturated fats. High EMF (programmer, in front of the computer 8 to 10 hours a day).
40 - present - 95% non-processed food, gluten-free and organic/grass-fed. Increased the amount of fats (coconut oil, butter, lard). Recently started to pay attention to artificial light and currently taking a break from work (less EMF) and also started doing facial CT, but stopped because of current health issue.

The last blood work I had was last December, around this time. The labs were pretty much ok, except for the glucose which was on the higher end, and low for Vitamin D (31). I also did some more in depth labs at that time, and Sjongren's Syndrom marker came out positive. I did the 23andme testing to see if I have any methylation issues, but I didn't see anything on the report. I do have very dry skin. Family history of diabetes, high blood pressure, and Hashimoto's.

### Josh — 2013-12-24T19:07:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nausea.9176/post-104209>

In my experience cleaning out a stagnant liver and gallbladder bile system is about a 2 year job. The blockage at the gallbladder is the tip of the iceberg so to speak. Once the liver and possibly pancreas become toxic and inflamed due to the blockage which may be in the common bile duct where it affects the pancreas directly, all of this needs to be addressed. There are many good practitioners in LA. I can recommend Dr. Mark Flannery DC.

Caroline, dancing (and especially tango) was such a passion of mine, until I experienced the most brutal attack of RA. My legs suffered the most. Doctor's solution for me was knees replacement and ankle fusion, as there was nothing else that can be done for me to fix it. - To make a long story short - I proved them wrong, did some research and eventually got myself out of the wheelchair, to a walker, then used a cane. I am at the point now that I can do rebounding and walk with just a slight noticeable limp. I truly hope that dancing is still in my future once again. I am originally from the former Soviet Union, but I live now in the Northeast part of the USA.

JanSz, I most certainly need to have some testing done - it’s been awhile. But even without it I know that I still have lots and lots of inflammation in me, even though the sedimentation rate of 110 (lab range 0-10) dropped to 16 and my CRP of 2.5 (ref. 0 -1) also went down to 0.1 now!

I need help making out what this all means? I know there are some areas of interest, but I don't know where to start?

I have been on the Epi Paleo Rx for about 6 weeks, I have lost 20 lbs. I am using CT Protocol with about 60lbs of ice for about 50 minutes. I am 49 yrs. old and over weight at 6'5" I am 320 lbs. I started at 342lbs. I have dropped grains recently and noticed a great difference in the way that I feel. I am working with my wife's employers Onsite health clinic, they are wonderful and curious as to what I am doing. They are starting a wellness clinic after the first of the year and some of the tests that I asked them to run are in line with what they are going to do. But They didn't understand all of the tests and why I wanted them?

I know my HS CRP is very high and they are concerned about it! I would appreciate some feedback?

Glen

>
> > We are designed for cold environment and a marine diet and this is core foundational principle of every mammal left on this planet since the K-T event. We just have remained blind to it. Hence, this is why K-T is Factor X for all eutherian mammals left standing. Modern life has allowed us to stray very far from our real evolutionary blueprint. I am not talking about the paleo 1.0 book here either!
>

>
> > Food became my medicine not my entertainment. With this type of medicine, I believe, we all can regain our health. Without our health, does anything else really matter?
>
> \---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
>
> Your LUTEINIZING HORMONE (LH) is low while Total Testosterone is low.
> Your pituitary gland is not responding correctly, it should have raised your LH
> You need to do pituitary MRI.
> Either growth (usually benign) or you have been hit in the head.
>
> Eventually you will need to raise your Total Testosterone.
> With your hemoglobin=16, you will have to be careful about it, and monitor it closely when managing your testosterone. If Hgb would raise above 17, have phlebotomy, drop pint of blood.
>
> You can raise testosterone two ways.
> You have to start with HCG injections first. Say
> HCG-shot=300iu/EOD
> use
> BD Ultra-Fine II U-100 Insulin Syringes Short Needle 31g 3/10cc 5/16in
> Depending on condition of your testicles that may or may not raise your testosterone (sufficiently).
> Sufficient TotalTestosterone=>600ng/dL
> While on HCG your testicles and scrotum are protected, keep using it even when you will need additional direct testosterone supplementation.
>
> Testosterone metabolizes to DHT
> DHT is responsible for sexual side more than testosterone
>
> If with HCG use you achieve sufficient testosterone, you may leave it that way.
> If you need additional testosterone, choice of testosterone that you will use will depend on DHT.
> You want to have DHT~85 ng/dL or only little higher
> Most of testosterone can be delivered as injections, preferably use above syringe and testosterone Cypionate or Enanthate 200mg/mL
> If you need to raise DHT add small amounts of Androgel 1% 2-3x/week
> Overall you still want to have Totaltestosterone~600-1000
> Likely you will need to stay closer to 600 because of your hemoglobin being already highish.
> Actually Hgb=16 is ideal value.
>
> While supplementing testosterone you may also need to monitor
> estrogens and progesterone.
>
> Your LDL is highish.
> That may change when you adjust your DHEAs which is likely low at this time.
> You want to have DHEAs(350-490) μg/dL
> Use DHEA pills, as many as it takes, 400-500mg/day is top dose but often 100-200 is enough
>
> Your cortisol= 8 ug/di 4-22 is low, you want it at 22
>
> Following dr Jack Kruses's system should help you here, but you still may need to work on this area.
>
> You should monitor
> pregnenolone
> progesterone
> cortisol,serum(7:30AM, noon, 2:30PM)
>
> you want
> pregnenolone high within range
> progesterone>=1.4ng/mL
> cortisol(7:30AM)=22
>
> Likely your pregnenolone is low. 26 mg/mL
> Pregnenolone is metabolized from LDL with help of vitD, vitA, T3
>
> Your vit D is extremely low
> You want vitD(80-120)
>
> 2/day-->Super Selenium Complex, 200 mcg 100 capsules (lef.org)
> 1 dropperfull/day=48mg/day
> Lugol's Iodine Family Pack -- 6 (2 fl. oz.) bottles

> 3/day CarlsonVitamin K2 Menatetrenone 5 mg
>
> Many if not most people are low on stomach acid.
> 8 tabs with each meal Country Life Betaine Hydrochloride with Pepsin -- 600 mg - 250 Tablets

# Thread: Magnetico Questions
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-questions.9186/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2013-12-26T18:20:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-questions.9186/post-104256>

I recently emailed magnetico the following but have yet to get a response. Anyone else want to chip in some answers or even ask more questions?

Hi,
I am considering buying one of your sleep pads but this will be a very big investment for me and so I need to ask a few questions first...

1\. Would sleeping on a classic pad with a 4" mattress provide more or less the equivalent gauss as using a core pad with an 8" mattress?

2\. Does the size of the positive field increase with higher strength pads?

3\. Have you had feedback from clients with digestion issues? Whatever I eat or drink, even just spring water, my stomach bloats, becomes uncomfortably full, and I can feel liquid rise up through my oesophagus. I don't expect this to directly cure it but would be interested to hear how the pads have helped others with similar problems.

4\. If I lay the pad directly on the floor is it okay to be in the same room as it for the rest of the day? I spend most of my time on my bed using my laptop when I am in my house.

5\. My laptop is grounded and 2-3 feet away from my bed. Has anybody reported equipment malfunctioning at this distance?

6\. The total cost minus duties and taxes is $1,457.66 (Â£891), I also intend to use the Jack Kruse discount, for the core pad to be delivered to England. Can you give me an estimate on what the duties and taxes would cost?

Thanks for your time, Sean.

### seanb4 — 2014-01-07T11:04:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-questions.9186/post-104963>

The answers I got if anyone is interested:

1\. Would sleeping on a classic pad with a 4" mattress provide more or less the equivalent gauss as using a core pad with an 8" mattress?
There isn't too much gauss strength increase when using the Classic 5 gauss model. For each inch less than 8 inches,you would gain about 0.1-0.2 gauss
so using the classic model underneath a 4" mattress, the gauss strength would be about 5.5-5.6 gauss
With the 10 gauss Core model, the 10 gauss strength is achieved under an 11 inch mattress then for each inch less than 11 inches you would gain approx. 1/2 gauss.
Conversely, for each inch greater than 11 inch between your body and the magnets, you would decrease the gauss strength by 1/2 gauss per inch.

.

2\. Does the size of the positive field increase with higher strength pads?
The magnetic field from the edges of all sleep pad models is about 4 feet

3\. Have you had feedback from clients with digestion issues? Whatever I eat or drink, even just spring water, my stomach bloats, becomes uncomfortably full, and I can feel liquid rise up through my oesophagus. I don't expect this to directly cure it but would be interested to hear how the pads have helped others with similar problems.

I am not aware of anyone using our sleep pads specifically for digestion issues. If you would like to discuss, I would suggest calling and speaking with Dr. Bonlie

4\. If I lay the pad directly on the floor is it okay to be in the same room as it for the rest of the day? I spend most of my time on my bed using my laptop when I am in my house.

The return positive field from the edges of the sleep pad extend approx. 4 ft from the pad so you wouldn't want to be within this distance for long periods of time.
We don't recommend using a laptop when you are on the bed or again within the 4 ft as the magnetic field may erase information stored on the laptop.

5\. My laptop is grounded and 2-3 feet away from my bed. Has anybody reported equipment malfunctioning at this distance?
We have not specifically had anyone report any issues however we do not recommend having a laptop or computer on or near the sleep pad
6\. The total cost minus duties and taxes is $1,457.66 (Â£891), I also intend to use the Jack Kruse discount, for the core pad to be delivered to England. Can you give me an estimate on what the duties and taxes would cost?
We are not able to determine duty & taxes. What we have control over is the shipping cost. We would charge your credit card the pad price& shipping& insurance. Any other charges would be your responsibility to pay at time of delivery. You would have to check with your customs dept. to determine approx cost of all additional charges ( duty,taxes and any customs fees)

### Josh — 2014-01-07T23:34:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-questions.9186/post-105021>

It has to do with the direction of the vortex in the 3rd ventricle of the brain. The Magnetico reinforces the positive effects at night, but works against it in the daytime when the direction is reversed. More in the sleep webinar and I believe the blog post that came shortly thereafter.

### caroline — 2013-12-27T03:39:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/c-t-4-the-holy-trinity.9190/post-104289>

I have just been reading C/T #4 again ......This is one of my favorites

really worth a re visit ....... It kind of brings everything back into focus .... for me anyway

Especially when I am struggling with QM ........

I have also been re reading the October 2013 Q&A thread ........ that really is an epic thread.....

### Jonathin — 2013-12-27T22:18:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/c-t-4-the-holy-trinity.9190/post-104304>

After a year and a half, I have been re-reading all of Jack's blogs from the beginning. I had saved them as word docs and slightly altered the formatting for printing. I find that reading them slowly and underlining sentences that I find outstanding to be helpful. If it is a long blog I might put it down and pick it up again over two days never in a rush to finish. Jack's writing style is not always clear on the first read through but I don't move along until a paragraph makes sense. Also spending time with some of the cites and maybe looking up terms on wikipedia or google to flesh out a bit more about sometime \[e.g. SCN\]. Fortunately, winter is the time for me to do this. Working on the EMF series now.

Right now I have about 234 page doc of copy and pasted bits from the forum including some epic threads. Mostly Jack's statements with some others for context. I was think about making this available to anybody that wanted a copy. The problem is that it will not be finished.

I listened to the C/T webinar yesterday. . ... I was interested to see how Jack's style has changed this past couple of years.

# Thread: Did you overindulge over the holidays?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-12-28T02:07:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104315>

How did you do over the holidays? did you become intimately involved with a chocolate Santa???

I did have a banana and some cherries and a couple of peaches .... but really didn't want anything apart from what I would normally have. I just had no interest at all.....
I was going to make myself some paleo banana bread but couldn't be bothered, and anyway, the only reason I am interested in it is because of the huge slabs of butter I have on it!

I really don't like how I feel when I eat out of my norm.....

How did you manage? was it tough? what was your course of action?

So many things are much more important .........

### Jude — 2013-12-28T05:58:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104328>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=104315)
>
> How did you do over the holidays? did you become intimately involved with a chocolate Santa???
>
> I did have a banana and some cherries and a couple of peaches .... but really didn't want anything apart from what I would normally have. I just had no interest at all.....
> I was going to make myself some paleo banana bread but couldn't be bothered, and anyway, the only reason I am interested in it is because of the huge slabs of butter I have on it!
>
> I really don't like how I feel when I eat out of my norm.....
>
> How did you manage? was it tough? what was your course of action?
>
> So many things are much more important .........

I have been naughty, naughty, naughty. Went on a bender, ate everything I shouldn't ...but not in huge quantities!
Being on the lean end of the scale I decided to do an N=1.
And I have paid for it! The most noticeable thing for me was the tiredness, just didn't have my usual upbeat energy thing for those busy Christmas days..and that's the first time I've felt like that since starting 2 1/2 yrs ago. The bananas were ok, and so were all the delicious sweets but will not miss them at all.
Back on track today and already feel energised, so perhaps that's a good sign.
My dil's Boxing day spread would have made JK proud, so that's a major success.

### caroline — 2013-12-28T06:15:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104329>

I didn't even enjoy my banana! I used to love them. It is the loss of energy I hate too. Does your family eat epi paleo Jude?

### Jude — 2013-12-28T06:59:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104334>

Caroline we try to eat epi p.....that's the intention.

Have experienced a lovely little episode to relate to you!
Two days prior to Christmas, my dil,grandies and I were able to catch up with Nick and his family in Brisbane!!!
Was so great to meet a fellow Kruiser and his kruisers! To be actually able to talk face to face with someone who's read JK, understands heaps more and has implemented even more in their lives is like.............the best Christmas present ever!

This experience should be for all of us! Make plans now to meet up! It's exhilarating....Caroline I'm sure there must be someone near you? We're only 9 hrs away by train!

It was tremendously exciting for my dil to be able to meet other folk who are achieving such success with the JK protocols, and more specifically with regard to healing children. Nick is a great model and if iirc has a growing number of JK devotees amongst his friends and acquaintances....need a blog here Nick!

So we came away with firm intentions of going all out to get the magnetico for kids and us! Was surprised that the magnetico was much smaller in height than I imagined. Nick had lots of tips for us re use of same and was badgered with lots of questions. We are so grateful to Nick and Jacquie for hosting such a delicious epi paleo banquet, nourishing body, soul and spirit of fellow travellers with such kindness.
THE END ......
It was such a special time......................................

### caroline — 2013-12-28T07:08:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104335>

How awesome Jude. Nick is such a lovely guy. How did that happen?

It is funny you mention this .... I have been thinking of doing an outback trip...Cape York or some such. Never mind that I have never camped or anything remotely close. I wouldnt have a clue how to put up a tent etc!

Do you need help shearing some sheep??

I am going to pm you

### caroline — 2013-12-28T07:19:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104337>

I think we should organize our own meet and great!

Corriander, freesia, ?? Who else?

### caroline — 2013-12-28T07:21:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104338>

Soooooo .....Nick puts on a big feast for people passing thru??? Good to know!

### Jude — 2013-12-28T07:27:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104339>

Yup........................................................

it's 41.4 outside now and shearing in Feb much the same...........shearing isn't all it's cracked up to be!
...........meeting up somewhere a lot colder sounds really good to me!

### Inger — 2013-12-28T09:23:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104341>

Nope.. I had some 85 - 99 % chocolate and lots of raw organic nuts (hazelnuts, macadamias, pistazios, walnuts, almonds), but it was on purpose  and it did not make me feel bad at all. I also made a raw peppermint chocolate cake/pie with just a few dates and almonds + coconut oil and spices for crust and 85 % organic chocolate, raw egg yolk, coconut oil and peppermint oil for filling, that was crazy yummy! Family liked it too (at least some of them lol)

I do ate out of my regular meal times because of eating with others and ate without hunger etc. but so far I have had no ill effects really. Going back to no carbs after the holidays for sure but the few carbs from nuts and the VLC raw choco cake did not affect me short term - or maybe the fact that I have laughed so much this Christmas made up for the damage..lol

I have had so much energy... and not tired at all even if it is so warm and dark and rainy here all days. very weird, +4 degree C
and I have not had coffee either! I have not wanted to.. that is weird too!

### nicld — 2013-12-28T19:32:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104355>

I have not been too bad, some caramels and a gluten free Russian Tea cake cookie every other day. Had a bit more cheese than I should have and a little more wine then I should.

Did have some risotto with roasted duck the other night and it tasted oh so good. We went out saw The Phantom of the Opera afterwards and stayed out a bit too late and yesterday was rough.

Good thing is have been doing a lot of CT. Just have to make it through New Years Eve.

### Destiny — 2013-12-29T15:41:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/did-you-overindulge-over-the-holidays.9196/post-104394>

I did not have a problem to stay on the ketogenic diet. My family did the same since I am the one that cooks !

I was exposed to the CW treats from my friends but would not touch them. In addition, I was not even tempted.

### Nick — 2014-01-01T01:35:30+0200

# Thread: Another wifi consideration
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-wifi-consideration.9198/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lioness7 — 2013-12-28T05:51:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-wifi-consideration.9198/post-104327>

Just a heads up to anyone with a newer furnace or thermostat....we had a new thermostat installed in the fall. My "new " thermostat died after four years requiring a new replacement. The technician was pleased to inform me that it was wifi enabled as a factory preset. Gee golly thanks....the old one was not wifi though programmable. He thought it was great to be able to set the furnace or a/c while anywhere in the world through one's phone....I told him to disable the wifi and he did not know how and that I would have to figure it out myself which I did within a few moments. Please check any of your newer equipment. Most are pre set for wifi whether hooked up or not....fridges, freezers, washers, dryers, printers, gaming systems etc. Ridiculous! Thankfully still missed on the radar for a smart meter....they will have to deal with my dog or worse yet , me...;-) ( no opt out here yet) I am sure most of you are aware already but for those who are not ....

### caroline — 2013-12-28T06:23:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-wifi-consideration.9198/post-104330>

So - to deal with your dog will be bad ..... But to deal with you will be far worse! I love it! You are a force to be reckoned with..... Just ask your kids!

### lioness7 — 2013-12-28T06:58:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-wifi-consideration.9198/post-104333>

Caroline....luv you ! I figure if I don't feed the dog or the kids we will be a force with which to be reckoned..lol!!! my dh is just waiting for the confrontation and how I will manage it. ahhh...waiting for a bit of fun.

### caroline — 2013-12-28T07:14:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-wifi-consideration.9198/post-104336>

How is your family? I have never forgotten that video you sent me....

### lioness7 — 2013-12-28T22:00:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-wifi-consideration.9198/post-104366>

Thank you for asking Caroline ....they are all good and his highness is thriving (lots of lip and attitude/ treatment side effect due to growing up a tad quicker, though must admit a bit of genetics as well...but otherwise, so far no txt related side effects, though i would love a sock in his mouth every so often! switched schools for the last 4 to a non emf environment...no wifi etc...no phones allowed on the property (why do i have to pay for this ?)

I had an emf scan five yrs ago....we were fine...but this spring I re did it as a result of a new tower going up. my only issue was the three yr old wifi printer I never enabled and was shocked to find out out it was factory preset. the thermostat issue really drove me over the edge...wth! we have promoted ourselves to incomptency...just because we can do something , doesn't mean we should.

### caroline — 2013-12-28T22:54:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-wifi-consideration.9198/post-104374>

In Jack's EMF webinar - I think .... he talked about having old appliances rebuilt instead of buying new - exactly what you are talking about.

That is so interesting about your son's new school ..... so some people are actually getting it.... You must have interesting conversations with other parents there? You are in North TO - right?

I have told this story before about a woman I met with a daughter with a very serious genetic condition and her son who has lots of problems too - very bad asthma etc. He got very much worse when they changed to a school that is wified to the hilt. I gave her all kinds of stuff to read and links etc. I saw her the other day .... she is still taking him to the same Doc and he is still continually on antbiotics and she hasn't read anything. Altho, at the time, she told me she would do anything! She is obviously not a "Lioness"

I have a lot of trouble understanding that ......

Giving you a lot of lip??? sounds pretty life affirming to me!

### lioness7 — 2013-12-30T17:45:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-wifi-consideration.9198/post-104448>

Not sure if people are getting it. The school's policy is more related to distraction than the health dangers of emf. Most of our friends and family think we are making a big deal out of nothing..."why don't you have cordless phones? omg , how do you talk around the house?" (duh, on a 25Ft corded one or speaker phone) "Oh but the wifi is so cool, you can start your washing machine from work with the app!" (so what, most washers have had timers for years.) "Why haven't you fixed the broken GPS on your car? " ; "If it was all bad for us, why would it be allowed?" (at which point I usually drop the topic), etc, etc.........

Having very limited internet access for the last 10 years has certainly helped my kids to not become gaming addicts, social media teen freaks and make them use their time more wisely on the computer as well as to explore better ways to spend their free time. For now, they can only tether with their phones with a pre set GB limit, making them more aware of their unnecessary usage. One day I hope to be able to have hardwire access, but for now I don't.

I will also continue to repair my appliances so that I do not have to purchase new ones or negate the warranty if I have to rip out the wifi they are pre loaded with!

BTW....99 cents on kindle today: [http://emfhelp.net/emr-survival-guide/](http://emfhelp.net/emr-survival-guide/)

### nonchalant — 2013-12-30T18:15:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-wifi-consideration.9198/post-104449>

> [lioness7 said:](/goto/post?id=104448)
>
> Not sure if people are getting it. The school's policy is more related to distraction than the health dangers of emf. Most of our friends and family think we are making a big deal out of nothing..."why don't you have cordless phones? omg , how do you talk around the house?" (duh, on a 25Ft corded one or speaker phone) "Oh but the wifi is so cool, you can start your washing machine from work with the app!" (so what, most washers have had timers for years.) "Why haven't you fixed the broken GPS on your car? " ; "If it was all bad for us, why would it be allowed?" (at which point I usually drop the topic), etc, etc.........

Lioness, I had a dream a couple nights ago in which 3 of us were trying to convince a few other people not to become enslaved to a futuristic society that catered to their every food, fashion, and fun whim. It was sorta like a combination of the movies Matrix and Wall-e (if you watch movies). Trying to convince them with logic was extremely frustrating and futile. At the end of my dream my two cohorts had converted to the other side and I was on the run... Hey, I just love these convoluted, drama-like dreams I've been having, Dr. K!

# Thread: Interesting Study on Ketosis and DHA
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-study-on-ketosis-and-dha.9199/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### QiGuy1997 — 2013-12-28T09:00:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-study-on-ketosis-and-dha.9199/post-104340>

While doing some research on ketosis and free fatty acid metabolism, I came across this interesting study: [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/16125522](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/16125522)
Essentially, ketosis reduces DHA and other PUFAs in the fat and blood, and increases their presence in the brain and liver (by quite substantial amounts). Now, as far as I can tell, the increased accumulation of DHA and AA in the brain is a good thing. The decreased amount of PUFA in the fatty tissue and the blood would be considered very good by Ray Peat/Danny Roddy but I'm not sure whether Dr. Kruse would see this as a good thing or a bad thing. Lastly, I'm not sure if the vastly increased amounts of PUFAs (25x increase in AA and DHA) in liver triglycerides is a good thing or a bad thing. Do they have a role in improving liver function or are they just lingering around, creating lipid peroxides and what not? I'd love to hear the thoughts of this forum's members.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

I am thinking of starting to use maca & d ribose. But am concerned as i have a enlarged pituitary & low testosterone.
Could their be any complications from using these? Are their any known side effects?
Me & my partner are looking at starting a family so are their any other supplements that may help.

Many thanks

### caroline — 2013-12-29T22:39:32+0200

> I am thinking of starting to use maca & d ribose. But am concerned as i have a enlarged pituitary & low testosterone.
> Could their be any complications from using these? Are their any known side effects?

healthman, I love maca, and how it makes me feel, but there is so much conflicting information about it regarding its use for people who are hypothyroid like I am. Some sources say that it is OK to do it and some are warning against it. I stopped taking mine until I know for certain what the correct answer is.

# Thread: Making new Memories....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-new-memories.9214/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-12-30T05:37:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-new-memories.9214/post-104427>

I have been thinking about this ....Making new memories - a lot lately.

Dr. K. has said that as we age it gets harder and harder to make new memories .... so we have to work at it.

At face value - it doesn't seem like it would be that hard .... but it kinda is!

I have been trying really hard lately to do different things everyday. On the surface - it should be a piece of cake - especially if it is fun/enjoyable....... but it doesn't seem to be that simple.

I can easily revert to old habits - back to my comfort zone.

I was talking to a woman in the pool this morning about swimming all winter. She said "You just have to do - not think"

So - from now on - I am just going to get up and put my speedo on..... and then turn off my thinking machine .... and just do!

Are you making new memories? What is on your "to do" list???

Something tho - that I don't quite understand ..... If I had money I could do a ton of things - make a ton of new memories ......I wouldn't even hesitate to do the most crazy things.
But when it comes to my everyday routine ....... it is so much harder.

Some routine maybe is good for our general wellbeing? or is that just a safety net?

### Inger — 2013-12-30T08:24:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-new-memories.9214/post-104429>

I love routines in the morning and evening... I think they do me good. I always do the same thing, wake up.. lay a bit in bed and think and enjoy the sweet feeling... raise, cuddle my cat and feed her, drink lots of good water, do face dunks, eat a good breakfast... shower/CT, look into the sky, read something that gives me good vibes, and I am good to go. I love my mornings. In the evenings I am very reduced in every way.. and I go early to bed. Those two moments of the day are sacred to me. I think I need it for my well being. Help me stay clear in my head and thoughts too.

now in the holidays the house is full of religious people that makes quite a bit of noise, so I just lock me into my apartment mornings and evenings, I do not care about their "party"... lol And it helps so much! I do not get much stressed at all. I am learning to not care about others expectations or their demands. All day I play with the children. They are magic... even if they are hurt by their parents. The more they need unconditional love. So I make those memories... for me and for them. They are beautiful. I don't know... but it always happens somethings every day and that is what will be the memories and I want them to be beautiful so I do those beautiful things. Take the kids out and cuddle with the sheep... or bake some paleo treats with them. Hold them and touch them. Listen to their stories. Talk to them. Those memories are so much worth to me and I know they are to the kids too. I remember when I was a kid and my aunts and uncles... I LOVED them. Yeah... their daddies and moms are crazy fundamentalists and hurt their kids mentally and physically - the more they need a loving aunt! I do not care about the parents. Sorry. All they do is thinking I need to be saved by God. HA! ;-)
Life is full of challenges. I try to focus on the essentials. What difference can I do in this world right now?

I think... the memories comes from all this.... when I am 100% in the moment. I have many moments in my previous life I do not remember at all.. I bet it is the times when I was not focused and sucking in life. It is kind of sad.. like wasted life/time. So I have decided not to live like that anymore. Instead I do 100% what I do. And it feels very good! Like very much alive =)

hope this does not sound too confusing...lol

### caroline — 2013-12-30T09:46:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-new-memories.9214/post-104434>

I think you are so right Inger .... there is great comfort in morning and evening routines.

I do find tho, for me, I have to push myself out of my comfort zone or I can fritter too much time away. But having said that I think being still ... just "being" seems to be vitally important to me now ....... well - as long as it is outside.

It seems that I can sit at the ocean for hours - just sucking up all the wonderful energy .....or on my balcony listening to the birds and the ocean and the wind blowing thru the trees.

But - I do crave craziness and silliness and adventure and all the unexpected things/people that come my way when I leave my comfort zone ......

### caroline — 2013-12-30T09:51:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-new-memories.9214/post-104435>

The thing is Inger .... If I had money - I would jump on a plane and tell you" Happy New Year" in person!
I would give you the biggest hug! xo

### nonchalant — 2013-12-30T18:51:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-new-memories.9214/post-104454>

Speaking of memories, not Memories, I've been a bit concerned lately about other's forgetfulness. Just about all of my relatives, even some much younger than I, are forgetting things that occurred or were said just a few days ago. Or more important things from a few weeks ago. I know I'm getting better, because I can now remember where I placed just about anything, by thinking about it for a minute. I can usually remember where I put it, as well as what else I was doing/thinking as I put it there. But I also think my relatives are getting worse.

### Inger — 2013-12-30T18:56:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-new-memories.9214/post-104457>

Yes.. that would be amazing  maybe next year Audrey!
I am not sure if I will manage to stay up until midnight this year either though, last year I was at sleep at 10 PM...

### caroline — 2013-12-30T22:18:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-new-memories.9214/post-104469>

I can't manage to stay up that late ether anymore - the other nite I was in bed a t 8:30!

This year we have a trifecta of fireworks at the Opera House/coathanger. I will probably miss all of it!

# Thread: Cold showers question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tanya — 2013-12-30T17:31:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104447>

Here is my dilemma. Cold showers are the only option for me to do my C/T (at the present time, anyway), but I canâ€™t stand to wash my hair in cold water. So, when I try to do it using warm water in the beginning - I have a problem switching to a cold water later. So, I start with cold and then finish it with warm water for my hair, but doing that just does not feel right to me. I am curious to know how the rest of you, who also take cold showers, do it?

### nonchalant — 2013-12-30T18:20:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104450>

Hi Tanya. I think you will find it easier to wash your hair in the summertime, when the tap temperature is higher. I used to wash first with cold and end with slightly warm water. Perhaps you could continue through the winter and try it again in a few months?

BTW, I really like your signature.

### Inger — 2013-12-30T18:29:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104451>

I have to always wash my hair with warm water too, every second day. I use mud and with cold water only it does not get clean I think. So I shower with warm first and then after I am finished washing I turn shower to ice cold and shower my feet slowly moving the shower up on my legs.. the other foot.. then I do the same with my hands and move the shower up on my arms to the shoulders and then in the end I shower my head and face and whole bod, no issue at all. It is very nice way to do cold showers - instead of jump starting with the head... I think =)

Sometimes I wash my hair in the kitchen sink too. You could do this if you do not want warm water on your body.

### nonchalant — 2013-12-30T18:55:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104456>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=104451)
>
> So I shower with warm first and then after I am finished washing I turn shower to ice cold and shower my feet slowly moving the shower up on my legs.. the other foot.. then I do the same with my hands and move the shower up on my arms to the shoulders and then in the end I shower my head and face and whole bod, no issue at all. It is very nice way to do cold showers - instead of jump starting with the head... I think =)

I agree completely.

### caroline — 2013-12-30T22:32:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104471>

yep ..... kitchen sink for me too sometimes .....but I don't mind the cold shower either. It is hard at first - but mind over matter!. Actually I don't have a choice - I pulled the breaker on my hotwater tank! Only turn it on when my grandsons come over - and they want to have a bath. I cannot convince them that cold is good!

### Tanya — 2013-12-30T22:52:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104474>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=104451)
>
> I have to always wash my hair with warm water too, every second day. I use mud and with cold water only it does not get clean I think. So I shower with warm first and then after I am finished washing I turn shower to ice cold and shower my feet slowly moving the shower up on my legs.. the other foot.. then I do the same with my hands and move the shower up on my arms to the shoulders and then in the end I shower my head and face and whole bod, no issue at all. It is very nice way to do cold showers - instead of jump starting with the head... I think =)

Inger, thank you for these very practical tips - it might just work for me. Actually, I believe that it will definitely work for me. Anyone who can manage to get their skin as velvet has to be doing something right!!!

### Tanya — 2013-12-30T23:04:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104475>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=104450)
>
> Hi Tanya. I think you will find it easier to wash your hair in the summertime, when the tap temperature is higher. I used to wash first with cold and end with slightly warm water. Perhaps you could continue through the winter and try it again in a few months?
>
> BTW, I really like your signature.

nonchalant, I can't wait that long for the summer to arrive. I am already late into this game. Hopefully, what Inger shared should work for me....

Thank you for noticing my signature. There so many thought-provoking and inspiring ones compiled here. Makes me appreciate even more being here!

### Tanya — 2013-12-30T23:07:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104476>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=104471)
>
> yep ..... kitchen sink for me too sometimes .....but I don't mind the cold shower either. It is hard at first - but mind over matter!. Actually I don't have a choice - I pulled the breaker on my hotwater tank! Only turn it on when my grandsons come over - and they want to have a bath. I cannot convince them that cold is good!

caroline, for the sake of your grandsons - I hope that you will get it fixed soon.

### Flora — 2013-12-31T00:46:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104479>

Yep, I'm a cold shower gal too - have come to really love it now, but I always start warm and have a wash / shampoo hair first and then turn to cold gradually. I can now do it right over my head and back (hardest part!) but mostly concentrate over abdomen, legs, face and lower back. Sometimes I tell myself that I don't have to go cold today - but I always do because I know it makes me feel so good afterwards! Water is now at around 8 C / 45 F - feeling good and definitely noticing benefits.

### Tanya — 2013-12-31T19:45:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104515>

Flora, It is good to know that you are getting benefits from taking cold showers. Interesting that just like me you also have a difficulty with having it on your back. I wonder why that is happening? At any rate, I hope that eventually we both will be able to overcome that.

### caroline — 2013-12-31T23:41:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104526>

> [Tanya said:](/goto/post?id=104476)
>
> caroline, for the sake of your grandsons - I hope that you will get it fixed soon.

Tanya - you misunderstand .... I pulled my breaker on purpose - so I am not tempted! I only need hot water for dishes/washing up - so I just boil the kettle. It is only me and I don't cook - hardly ever! That is why raw is great!!

When I realised how long it was taking to heat up a whole tank of water - I decided to opt out!

sounds weird I guess ......

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-01-01T01:25:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104540>

I love my cold showers, but also find that washing my hair is less than pleasant in the cold water. Not so much because of the temperature on my own body, but because it seems like the shampoo/conditioner doesn't like the cold temps and my hair seems to get grossly tangled up. I have found that washing my hair in the sink is a good solution. Or I'll just wash my hair quickly at the start with lukewarm water. That seems to make it easier to transition to cold cold. But I also think there is value in hot and cold, so sometimes I'll just relish the cold water for as long as is reasonable (I live in the high desert, so water is an issue here), and then I'll turn on super hot water at the end and enjoy some back and forth thermogenesis.

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-01-01T01:27:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104542>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=104526)
>
> When I realised how long it was taking to heat up a whole tank of water - I decided to opt out! sounds weird I guess ......

That is awesome!

### Tanya — 2014-01-01T21:56:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104562>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=104526)
>
> Tanya - you misunderstand .... I pulled my breaker on purpose - so I am not tempted!

caroline, it is almost funny how much I misunderstood what you were describing earlier. I stand to being corrected. To tell you the truth, I actually thought that you were joking about the breaker (I think you have a great sense of humor and I always enjoy it, by the way). Now that I know that you did it on purpose, I am so impressed with your dedication to the “cause”.

### caroline — 2014-01-01T22:14:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104563>

Tanya - I don't know if it is dedication. When we first moved to OZ I was always floored by our electric bills! when I called about it they said it was the hot water tank - it was on demand - so every time we turned the hot water tap on - it started heating.

When I moved where I live now - for some reason - my neighbour was talking about the hot water and said not to leave it on .... just turn the breaker on 15 minutes before a shower and then turn it off after.

So - as usual with me .... I went one or two steps further....

It has done wonders for my electric bill - and I get to C/T ..... multi tasking!

Now you have me really wondering about what else I have said that people may have misunderstood???? I tend to post first and think later .... maybe that better be my NewYear's resolution!

### Tanya — 2014-01-01T22:28:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104566>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=104563)
>
> Now you have me really wondering about what else I have said that people may have misunderstood???? I tend to post first and think later .... maybe that better be my NewYear's resolution!

caroline, I am pretty sure that I am the lonely and very embarrassed person here who managed to misunderstand something that was stated quite clearly. So, please find something else for your New Year's resolution (if you really need anything at all). As I said before, I find all of your posts quite interesting. Happy 2014 to you!

### caroline — 2014-01-01T23:21:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-showers-question.9216/post-104568>

Tanya - absolutely no reason to be embarrassed .... I do tend to post first and think later and put my foot in my mouth!

I think I better work on my writing skills!

# Thread: The Sideritis (Greek Mountain Tea) question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sideritis-greek-mountain-tea-question.9222/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Eugenia — 2013-12-31T04:57:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-sideritis-greek-mountain-tea-question.9222/post-104493>

_Update: Could an admin delete this thread, it's duplicate. Thx!_

In the whole of Balkan, Turkey and Italy they always considered the mountain herbal tea from the Sideritis family one of the most healthful things one could have. I'm actually pretty surprised that the Paleo community hasn't tapped on to the specific herbal tea family, because research is abundant about it, and in my opinion, it shows higher protective results than that of green tea. For one, except the fact that it doesn't have caffeine in it (for some of us, caffeine sends us to the toilet), but most importantly, it doesn't accumulate fluoride. All modern caffeinated teas these days have more and more fluoride in them, as the environment becomes dirtier by the minute by the industry. Especially true for tea that comes from China. Sideritis tea has no fluoride in it. Here's the research, way too much of it to give specific links: [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=sideritis](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=sideritis)

I personally buy Greek Mountain Tea from my local Mediterranean store, but Amazon also carries it. Since it's a tissane-type tea (it's not minced as in industrial teas, but it's the whole, unprocessed tea plant that you buy) it has a more specific way to prepare it. I put up a tutorial with pictures here: [http://eugenia.queru.com/2007/04/21/greek-mountain-tea/](http://eugenia.queru.com/2007/04/21/greek-mountain-tea/)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Good WiFi Risk to Schoolchildren Review
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-wifi-risk-to-schoolchildren-review.9227/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh — 2013-12-31T18:52:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-wifi-risk-to-schoolchildren-review.9227/post-104512>

[http://rense.com/general96/trower.html](http://rense.com/general96/trower.html)

[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=z99\_SzoXZdY](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=z99_SzoXZdY)

### Hope — 2014-01-01T00:09:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-wifi-risk-to-schoolchildren-review.9227/post-104532>

Thanks so much for sharing this indeed!!!! It breaks my heart.

### Nick — 2014-01-01T01:26:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-wifi-risk-to-schoolchildren-review.9227/post-104541>

I felt ill reading this, it really is heartbreaking in so many ways. I have a 3 and 6yo who are going to a school that has just wired up every classroom with Wi-Fi and now have a test class that is iPad driven. I have planned to see the principal when school starts again about at least turning off the Wi-Fi when possible and turning the iPads onto Aeroplane mode to at least mitigate the effects a little. As well as giving him a copy of Andy Marinos book I will be adding this article to the collection.

Thanks for the link Josh, it is powerful reading, reaffirms everything Jack has been saying and is a sobering way to start the new year. Still no point having your head in the sand, best to meet these challenges face first.

### lioness7 — 2014-01-01T01:44:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/good-wifi-risk-to-schoolchildren-review.9227/post-104546>
