### CoolSwede — 2014-01-07T12:05:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/post-104969>

I talked to my GF the other day about pregnancies (we do not have any children yet, but plan to in the future) and she told me about a friend of hers who were very nauseated during the first 3 months of her pregnancy. That got me thinking:

\-Are humans the only mammals to get pregnancy nausea?
\-Why do we get it?
\-What are the causes? Remedies?
\-Isn't it counterintuitive that a mother throws up food when she is in most need of it?!

What are your experiences, ladies? Did you manage to eat high-fat/paleo/epi-paleo during your pregnancies of did you fall of the wagon due to nausea?

Thanks!

### fitness@home — 2014-01-07T15:50:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/post-104976>

Three preganacies.

1 - Tiny bit of nausea the first couple of months. Excitement over first preganacy helped me overlook it (Gained 30 lbs)

2 - OMG...very sick for 6.5 months with extreme nausea. Couldn't stand the smell of coffee or even something a simple as hearing/seeing bacon sizzle. (Gained 30 lbs the last 2 months). three years later during procedure for tubal ligation, surgeon told my Mom that I had "significant scarring" on my gall bladder...always wondered if this preganancy's horrible nausea was from gall bladder issues. BTW, this child came quickly, latched right on to my breast after birth, and was _extremely_ healthy!

3 - No nausea but did have "oofs" (tiny burps) the entire pregancy. After pregancy #2 this one was a piece of cake (gained 30 lbs).

Eating crackers and light things like soup helped. Both of these have a saltiness to thiem and for some reason, salt helps my tummy settle down when upset. I stayed away from any prescriptions and just toughed it out.

Another thing I noted was it was better for me to take the prenatal vitamins at midday, with a meal. Mornings I could not handle them.

### CoolSwede — 2014-01-08T13:44:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/post-105057>

Thanks Fitness for your reply. It still puzzles me why many (most?) pregnant women suffer from nausea. I just seems so...counterintuitive...

Edit: You mention salt. Could salt deficiency be one piece of the puzzle?!

### fitness@home — 2014-01-08T15:49:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/post-105066>

Increases or fluctuations in hormones? They are certainly all over the place during pregnancy.

I remember salivating much more, which for me is always related to nausea. There was also increased sensitivity to smells of foods. Things I used to love were no longer appealing.

The morning sickness likely led to dehydration in my case. Maybe that's why I craved the salt.

### HoneyChild — 2014-01-08T16:30:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/post-105069>

I remember Jack saying it was something to do with an upside down PG:E2 ratio. I've also read it's associated with elevated leptin. [http://beyondmorningsickness.blogspot.co.uk/2011/03/hyperemesis-gravidarum-related-to.html](http://beyondmorningsickness.blogspot.co.uk/2011/03/hyperemesis-gravidarum-related-to.html)

### JanSz — 2014-01-08T16:38:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/post-105070>

[http://www.allthingsmale.com/forum/...oung-male-I-call-bullshit&p=218649#post218649](http://www.allthingsmale.com/forum/showthread.php?23765-What-s-a-healthy-normal-testosterone-level-for-a-young-male-I-call-bullshit&p=218649#post218649)

> [Hugh\_E said:](/goto/post?id=0)
>
> Regarding low testosterone levels and unusual body shapes for people in the first world, I've been trying to draw people's attention to something that probably goes a long way towards explaining it: the medical use of artificial hormones during pregnancy. Many hormone-based treatments currently and historically being given to pregnant women would shut down testosterone production in an adult man if he were to be given the same. Most of these treatments tend to be given during "problem" pregnancies, where the mother's thought to be at risk of having a miscarriage or going into premature labour. This practice started shortly after WW2 with an artificial estrogen called DES. DES was pulled from the market in the 1970s, however it was replaced with progestins, which are a second type of female hormone (and, as is the case with estrogens, can suppress testosterone production in an adult man at doses below those commonly used for medical treatment in women). In high enough doses, corticosteroids can suppress testosterone production too.
>
> You might think, why is testosterone production important in an unborn baby?
>
> Although we have a sex-determining chromosome (the Y chromosome), it only has a few dozen functional genes on it, and basically all it does is instruct your undifferentiated gonads to turn into testicles (without it they'll turn into ovaries instead), plus providing some of the genes needed for spermatogenesis. That's all it does. All the genes that control sexual development are held elsewhere on your genome, so everyone has the full set of instructions for both sexes. By default you develop following the instructions for female development, but if there's testosterone (and its derivative DHT) present, then you'll develop as male instead. Going on what happens in a condition called 5-alpha reductase deficiency, DHT seems to be the more important of the two as far as genital development is concerned, whereas testosterone itself seems to be what drives masculinisation of the brain.
>
> So what happens when you expose a male fetus to testosterone blocking drugs? In theory at least, you'd end up with a baby who developed fully as male up until the time the drug started being administered, but (assuming the dose is high enough to shut down testosterone production) developing fully as female during the time the drug is being administered.
>
> I couldn't find anything in the publicly accessible medical literature dealing with this potential side effect of hormone treatment in pregnancy, however I did discover that there was this drug DES which had been widely used as a treatment for miscarriage and was also used to chemically castrate adult men with prostate cancer. The doses used for miscarriage prevention were considerably higher than those used for prostate cancer treatment (under the standard "Smith and Smith" dosing schedule, over 40 times higher during the later stages of the pregnancy). That made it virtually inevitable that it would have shut down fetal testosterone production for at least part of the pregnancy, making "DES sons" the ideal candidate for finding out what effect testosterone blocking drugs have on male development.
>
> For the last couple of years I've been trying to find out as much as I can about DES sons, both by joining a DES sons support group (that was originally set up as part of a research project into DES sons), and also by using a search engine to find discussions about DES in online forums. Just recently I've been given a link to some research conducted by the organisation Hhorages France, which basically confirms what I saw in the DES sons support group. In both groups, around a third of those exposed seem to have intersex-related genital abnormalities (several of the ones I've talked to personally were born quite badly intersexed and needed multiple surgeries during their childhood as a result). They also seem to have very high rates of endocrine problems and signs of having undergone "eunuchoid" development, indicating lifelong lower than normal male testosterone. The biggest gripe in the DES Sons support group seemed to be gender dysphoria though, about a third to a half of them seem to have ended up identifying as women. With Hhorages France, the focus is on severe mental illnesses and very high suicide rates. I guess France is one of these countries with a very macho culture that classifies gender dysphoria as a serious mental illness. It's not though, based on what I've seen, if you expose a male fetus to testosterone-blocking drugs during the second and third trimester (which is what often happens with these miscarriage treatments), then you'll end up with a person who looks male but has a brain that's predominantly developed as female, right down to having a need for female-typical hormone levels rather than male-typical ones.
>
> I'm wondering what to do next. The situation with DES is so bad that there must be people who knew at the time it was pulled from the US market in 1971 that something had gone terribly wrong there. Nonetheless, the public's been kept completely in the dark about, it and very few of those affected even know they were DES-exposed (after all, do you know what medication your mother was given during her pregnancy with you?).
>
> I get the impression that everyone looking at the DES disaster has assumed that, just because it's such a powerful estrogen, all the harm it caused was through estrogenic effects. The fact that it's also highly effective at blocking testosterone production seems to have been completely overlooked. All use of estrogens in pregnancy was ended shortly after DES was withdrawn, but progestins and other hormones with the ability to block testosterone production continue to be used in medicine to the present day. I think this is the reason why we're suddenly seeing so many stories in the news about men wanting to become women (and, based on what I've been told, DES appears to have sometimes created people in the opposite situation too - born female but identifying as male).
>
> What do people here think? Until a couple of years ago I was testing computer software, and now suddenly I've stumbled across what looks to be a colossal medical disaster that nobody wants to own up to. What's the correct procedure in such a situation? I was planning to petition the FDA, and here's what I've written so far:
>
> [https://www.change.org/en-GB/petiti...-pregnancy-can-cause-intersex-and-transgender](https://www.change.org/en-GB/petitions/the-us-food-and-drug-administration-fda-acknowledge-that-hormone-treatment-during-pregnancy-can-cause-intersex-and-transgender)
>
> It should have a covering letter though, and I'm not sure who at the FDA to address it to. What do people here suggest?

### CoolSwede — 2014-01-09T10:10:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/post-105137>

Interesting links, JanSz and HoneyChild. If it is caused by elevated leptin, then I assume that women with leptin resistance and/or obesity should be at greater risk. Sounds like the fenomenon might be neolithic. Are there any H/G societies with this issue?!

### Linz — 2014-01-09T11:47:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/post-105140>

I had moderate nausea, quite extreme tiredness and felt cold every evening working in a city office (with lots of natural light and before computer screens were introduced). During a week's skiing holiday nausea and tiredness disappeared completely from the first day and were much less when I returned to work. I had expected not to be able to ski everyday or to keep up with my party but I hardly knew I was pregnant. Leptin must have been doing the right thing up that cold snowy mountain.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-09T16:58:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/post-105168>

Interesting. I had no nausea, but I know my hormones weren't optimal because I had extreme tiredness and sleepiness also. I certainly had no "healthy glow" that everyone expected.
I had a slightly better pregnancy 17 months later, however, possibly because I didn't try to mall walk so much for exercise, and because I really pushed the (fluoridated) water. I suppose a lot of polluted water is better than a little polluted water. ;-) No nausea the second time either, but still tired and sleepy. I gained less weight, though.

### CoolSwede — 2014-01-09T17:07:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pregnancy-nausea.9275/post-105170>

CT and recreation = Hasta la vista, Leptin!

# Thread: Sumatran lamb curry
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sumatran-lamb-curry.9278/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Linz — 2014-01-07T21:00:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sumatran-lamb-curry.9278/post-105000>

A really special curry for a cold winter day.

Blend 5oz onions, 4 cloves garlic, 2 inch pice of ginger,(peeled and chopped), i red pepper (seeded, chopped) and 3 tbs water. Add 2tsp paprika, 1 tsp cayenne, 4 tsp ground coriander seeds, 2 tsp ground cumin, 1/2 tsp turmeric, black pepper, 1/2 tsp ground cardamom and 1/2 tsp ground cinnamon. Blend briefly. Add enough water to 14 oz thick coconut milk/cream to make 1 1/4 pints.

Heat 6 tbs of suitable oil - coconut, olive, duck etc and stir in blended paste. Stir for a few minutes until the paste is rich reddish brown and add 2 lbs cubed lamb shoulder or casserole lamb. Stir for a minute and then cover and cook gently for 10 mins, stirring occasionally. Add the coconut milk, 2 tbs thick tamarind paste and salt to taste. Stir and simmer slowly for about 45 minutes or until tender.

Serve with cauliflower or dark green veg - spinach or kale - and substitute the nightshades for something different if you need to.

### fitness@home — 2014-01-07T22:53:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sumatran-lamb-curry.9278/post-105016>

This sounds really good. I have everything on hand except the tamarind and lamb. Going to try this one - thanks for posting!

### Tanya — 2014-01-07T23:12:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sumatran-lamb-curry.9278/post-105018>

> [Linz said:](/goto/post?id=105000)
>
> Serve with cauliflower or dark green veg - spinach or kale - and substitute the nightshades for something different if you need to.

I wish I could have this., but eve if I substitute the nightshades, the curry itself may present a problem for me. I can't imagine making this dish without it though. Thank you for sharing your recipe.

# Thread: How do you brew your BPC?

### Hope — 2014-01-08T18:01:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-brew-your-bpc.9286/post-105078>

So I am thinking I need to try to get my DH off his Starbucks coffee habit and onto BPC. So I wonder, do any of you brew your coffee without a coffee machine but with some other device? If so, which?

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-08T19:50:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-brew-your-bpc.9286/post-105087>

I switched from a French press to a regular brewing machine when I have time and I don't need to make a large pot I use pour over with filter. I always use a filter now....

here's what the pour overs look like. [https://shoponline.melitta.com/items/COFFMKRSPROVER](https://shoponline.melitta.com/items/COFFMKRSPROVER)

I've got a travel one that made of silicone and collapsed - it goes in my luggage along w/preground beans, ghee, Coconut oil and braun hand blender

If we had the space I'd get the porcelain 6 cup coffee maker but since we don't I've got a regular drip brewer...

Off hand, from the list

you should not use them, eat sea food.
\-------------------------------

2\. Minerals Iodine, Iron, and Selenium

About iodine/iodide, rather than supplement with Lugol's dr Kruse encourages eating sea food, (oysters are #1) and on his recent podcast with Dave Asprey he specifically mention seaweed Arame.

At this time I am waiting for clarification on Arame:
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthre...th-Dave-Asprey&p=105067&viewfull=1#post105067](http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?9281-New-podcast-out-with-Dave-Asprey&p=105067&viewfull=1#post105067)

I need clarification about radiation from Fukushima.

\----------------------------
10\. DHEA/MELATONIN

No to melatonin, unless flying more than 3 hrs and changing time zones.

\----------------------------

8\. Multivitamin

Personally I do not use any multivitamins.
Instead I do Spectracell Micronutrient Analysis and follow my test results with targeted supplementation.

\---------------------------

Overall, the first priority is to modify and align life style:
CT
Food (Epi-Paleo-Rx),
Circadian Rhythm, BAB, (exposure to blue light after sunrise, no exposure to blue light after sundown)
minimize EMF exposure, control it (Magnetico)
Micronutrient supplementation

If the above does not make you happy,
hire medical professional (who's priorities are aligned with dr Jack Kruse's)

Gut analysis/optimization
Optimize micronutrients
Optimize Fatty Acids and Phospholipids
Optimize Steroid Hormone Panel
Optimize Thyroid Hormone Panel
All else

//
Depending on dedication, many of the above are good concepts for do it your self project.
Dr Kruse is a fantastic teacher plus he make available to us best expertise.
You should buy and study recent seminar with dr Tim Jackson.
Likely other are in the pipeline.
If you are at first reluctant to experiment on your own body, (n-1), at least that would give you a background to evaluate your doctor.

### Inger — 2014-01-09T09:39:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/oh-the-stupid-stop-just-stop-with-your-stupid-smart-phone.9294/post-105135>

The reason I am still in this house.. is mainly because my parents think technology is, kind of... Antichrist (among other things.. they have many rules..lol) and they happily listen to me when I talk about its dangers - for me this is a blessing! Every time I look for somewhere else to live I get freaked out about all the wifis and stuff surrounding any apartment or home that is close to neighbors. I have to wait for having enough money or the opportunity to live in a place those things are far from me! I feel very lucky I have this place and other "issues" feel rather small.. because I see what is coming
sure here are towers (a few km away) and smart meters too (have installed a filter on the house but IDK if it helps much), but no wifi's and not much technology either. Still, I feel a big difference being out in the wild / here
I am working from home now too. It is not a risk less thing I have jumped into but, it might be worth it. Means a little more time on the computer but I still think it is a better choice than working in the big city as I did before.... I feel this weird urge to not go in there (into the city) if I can avoid it. I just have to balance increased computer time with more time outside etc.

it is like everyone just HAVE to have those smart phones and toys

if being bombarded with radiation impacts our thinking and screws it.. it is pretty serious what is going on

# Thread: Nest Thermostat
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nest-thermostat.9321/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### motivatedhealthseeker — 2014-01-13T03:58:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nest-thermostat.9321/post-105514>

We have a Nest thermostat that can connect to wifi in the house to control temperature and can be controlled via cell phone if one is outside to change temp of house. This was bought before we realized what a hazard this could be. I usually have this feature turned off during the day and do not connect to wifi and I turn off wifi at night from the router. Is there still risk of getting the EMF from this type of a thermometer even when the connection to wifi feature is turned off? Is it still trying to send a signal? If yes, then is there a remedy for this without having to switch thermostats again?

### lioness7 — 2014-01-13T04:12:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nest-thermostat.9321/post-105515>

There should be a setting to disable wifi on the thermostat forever. check the manual. turning off your router does not disable equipment that is wifi factory preset from seeking out a connection.

### motivatedhealthseeker — 2014-01-16T08:08:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nest-thermostat.9321/post-105882>

Thank you lioness. I have it set to do no connect to wifi option but I think Dr K still thinks its not a good idea to have in the house. I wish I knew this before it was bought. It was suppose to save on energy for the long run but didnt realize the EMF part of the equation.

Hi,

I do not have much room in my bedroom and I spend a lot of my spare time in there (daytime). I have been really considering getting a magnetico sleep pad for my bed but have recently heard that the field the pad produces is actually bad for you during day time. I think this is due to earths magnetic direction flipping during daytime, or something.

So if I were to buy it then I would get positive effects at night but these would be cancelled out by the negative effects in day time.

Any body know how to mitigate this. Got any more precise information?

I have been wondering if you flip the mattress upside down during day time, will this simulate the earths magnetic direction flipping?

Thanks, guys.

### Linz — 2014-01-16T12:47:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-small-bedroom.9324/post-105890>

Sean, sorry to hear you are feeling down. I think the magnetico might not be the best investment if you haven't first corrected the circadian rhythms in your everyday life.

Don't stay in your bedroom most of the day. You need to be outside as much as possible when it is daylight (on grass not tarmac streets or pavements). perhaps you should get diurnal cortisol and melatonin labs done and I think DHEA. Honeychild posted details of labs that will do this in the UK and I think gps will order these for you if you pay for them. They just don't usually offer to do it because it is not available as routine on NHS. It would be much cheaper than a Magnetico and you would know for certain if you need to improve the circadian stuff - and you could ask for more specific help from the labs section here.

Lots of sympathy here. I spent 5 years chasing my tail while my health was disintegrating and it is no fun. It's also no fun when others offer advice but it too is in the wrong context so it doesn't work either. Many of the things I was doing should have been helping but I was missing the context so they weren't working, or they worked for a while and then stopped (ie I was much worse in the autumn as light levels changed but had no clue it was the light ). You are very lucky (even if you don't feel it! ) that you have all the amazing information here to help put everything together. Have you really convinced yourself yet that things like the wrong light and temperature and standing outside in barefeet can make a huge difference to your own circumstance? An experiment doing the labs before and after changes would give you a measurement of what was happening perhaps before you could feel gut changes (my personal experience is that gut motility is slow to improve - transition metals/vagus nerve/gut bacteria are all on my radar but lost in a deep black hole - but back to near normal sitting on a volcanic rock with feet in the sea).

Did you read Alex's journal? The lifestyle changes he has made for his health are extraordinary and they really tell us all how much we can do for ourselves just using nature. I started - and continue - MUCH smaller but the changes for the better are still happening. I would take Caroline and Inger's advice to get outside, right away from that small upstairs? room during the daytime before you spend a lot of money.

Willing you to find some positive improvements ...

### seanb4 — 2014-02-17T11:02:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-small-bedroom.9324/post-110760>

Another magnetico question. How far does the positive field come out from the sides of a single 10 gauss and how far does it extend from the top and the bottom?

I've heard 4 feet for the sides but I've also heard half the width of the mattress. Anyone know?

### Andrea — 2014-02-17T12:01:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-small-bedroom.9324/post-110766>

Magnetico sais that the field up to 4 feet to the side of a pad is harmful. That's where the field is turning back to the bottom of the pad. So you should avoid being closer than 4 feet sideways from a pad longer periods of time.

And as for how far it extends from the top/bottom... The only answer I've got from them is that with 10 and 20 gauss pads, you have the full strength of the field up to 11" from the pad. Whatever that means... I couldn't get a good reply though about what happens higher than that.
What I'm getting at is that the field will "end" and turn at some point. And I do wonder what then happens in the body in the area where the field turns. If you have a topper that's, say, 6" thick and then add your body's thickness - or width if you lie on your side - half of your body will be in one sort of field and the other half in a very different one.
This I haven't got an answer to.

### seanb4 — 2014-02-17T12:05:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-small-bedroom.9324/post-110767>

By top and bottom I mean looking at it from a top down, 2d perspective, if you get me. So not upwards or downwards.

### nonchalant — 2014-02-17T16:28:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-small-bedroom.9324/post-110808>

You could get a cheap ball compass and check it out. I found that the mattress affected the compass for several feet. I would guess 4 feet, yes.

### Josh — 2014-01-14T01:27:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/death-stats-from-20th-century.9332/post-105608>

Estimated major causes of death, 20th Century (1900-1999)
cause estimate totals
disease 4,130,265,400 72.03%
infectious 1,679,707,055
non-communicable (excl. cancer) 1,916,793,682
cancer 533,764,663

health complications 494,612,291 8.63%
pregnancy, labour, childbirth 435,186,847
nutritional deficiences 59,425,444

the natural world 131,460,286 2.29%
famine 100,794,176
natural disasters 23,948,396
animals 6,717,713

humanity 978,121,739 17.06%
ideology 141,663,126
war 130,727,000
murder 177,038,067
accidents 297,963,486
drugs 115,052,756
air pollution 115,677,304

total caused deaths 5,734,459,716
estimated total deaths in 20th century (our estimate) 5,700,990,774

"natural causes"? -33,468,942

[https://docs.google.com/spreadsheet/ccc?key=0Aqe2P9sYhZ2ndDFON0Zidmh0ZHc1WGU4NHMzQ1AtMVE#gid=44](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheet/ccc?key=0Aqe2P9sYhZ2ndDFON0Zidmh0ZHc1WGU4NHMzQ1AtMVE#gid=44)

\-more data

[http://www.informationisbeautiful.net/data/](http://www.informationisbeautiful.net/data/)

# Thread: CT and grounding effect...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-grounding-effect.9334/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Danco3636 — 2014-01-14T06:57:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-grounding-effect.9334/post-105617>

OK so it seems that CT is good not just for getting cold..... but also has a huge grounding effect. Water seems primordial in many ways so why not immersion for benefits?

With this I have been using water for a grounding effect and it seems to work great. Just washing my hands (I use water only, no soap) and running the cold water on my arms has a soothing grounding effect.... Why not right? The water that comes out should be grounded. At least at my place sense the water runs along pipes connected to my well pump and tank that sits on the ground slab. Anyway most piping makes it's way on the ground.....

So lately, and any given chance I rinse my hands and arms and splash my face with cold water. Anytime I go to the bathroom I rinse.... When I have been walking around with shoes for a long time I find time to rinse.... I especially enjoy washing the dishes by had as I feel it is a grounding effect. Even mild cold showers seems to have this effect.... Just being in water keeps me grounded....

Any one else notice this effect?
I am getting addicted to water, both drinking it and being in it.....
Perhaps this is why swimming (think Michael Phelps) is so great..... You are almost always grounded and eliminating most non native EMF's...?...?
My n=1 seems to point to getting wet often.

Any thoughts?

### Da-mo — 2014-01-14T07:35:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-grounding-effect.9334/post-105618>

I work in a power station so grounding is a very important thing for me given the feilds that Im around. Some areas have feilds exceeding 500uT!!! Couple that with lots of artificial light and microwave transmitters everywhere for remote control of the grid and shiftwork . . . Its a wonder im still alive

My CT consists of a 15 minute walk through the bush each night after dinner to a waterfall currently around 14C (just over 55F). Currently have worked up to immersion up to my neck for 25 minutes. I do that walk barefoot for the grounding but also consider the waterfall immersion as very effective for grounding.

I have to remain shod when at work and have often wondered about grounding during the working day - thanks for the idea about water rinsing, I will try it although the water in my work environment is possibly affected as well.

### Inger — 2014-01-14T20:38:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-grounding-effect.9334/post-105648>

I always wash dishes for hand too, since forever! Even if I have had a dishwasher, I did not used it. Now I know why... I always wash my dishes under running water too. I never have much dishes as I wash them right after use.

I loved to rinse my hands in cold water working as a waitress in the city full of EMF, it kind of gave me new energy! And I love to wash my feet under running water before I go to bed.. (if I do not shower before bed) and I love to shower, I often do twice a day (warm showers too) + a dip in the river.

I did not think about it like grounding before but sure it makes one feel good... yeah to get wet is nice - and rainy weather is nice too

### Jonathin — 2014-01-14T21:20:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-grounding-effect.9334/post-105657>

I know that people have talked about pools on another threat somewhere. I just happened to come across this company that created natural pools with no chemicals using bio-filtration. This could be a DYI but would be hard to achieve the nice results that are shown on the website.
\- [www.bionovanaturalpools.com](http://www.bionovanaturalpools.com)

### Da-mo — 2014-01-14T21:56:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-grounding-effect.9334/post-105659>

Jonathin, I've wondered about placing the plants in a sandbed and pumping water back from the bottom of the sandbed to the swimming area of the pool. Then you'd bet the benefit of a sand filter that never needs backwashing as the plant could consume the sediment.

Im all for ways to reduce/eliminate chlorine from the pool. Here in NZ they have a product for hot tubs that is a block that automatically leaches things into the water to control pH, total dissolved solids and bacteria. The biocide used is ionic silver and they last about a year. They dont have one for swimming pools yet but I think they are working on it. Not sure if Im allowed to post a product link but .. . . [http://www.simplesilver.co.nz/](http://www.simplesilver.co.nz/)

### caroline — 2014-01-14T22:09:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-grounding-effect.9334/post-105665>

I do that walk barefoot for the grounding but also consider the waterfall immersion as very effective for grounding.
>
> I have to remain shod when at work and have often wondered about grounding during the working day - thanks for the idea about water rinsing, I will try it although the water in my work environment is possibly affected as well.

### Da-mo — 2014-01-14T22:55:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-grounding-effect.9334/post-105668>

THanks for the suggestion Caroline - I'll look into it. We have to wear saftey footwear but I'll check how the grounding shoes work in principle and may be able to do something with anti-static straps so long as they arent a trip hazard

### caroline — 2014-01-14T22:57:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-grounding-effect.9334/post-105669>

It seems to me that someone here got grounding straps for their kids to wear to school......sorry - I don't remember who..

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-01-28T22:49:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-grounding-effect.9334/post-107223>

I do the majority of my CT in a natural spring-fed pool. The water is about 40-degree F during the winter, and stays stable even during really cold periods. I find that I much prefer this natural CT and grounding session outdoors and in contact with the ground and rocks and water, than doing CT in my bathtub at home. I come away feeling exhilarated and connected, and it has become a really spiritual ritual for me. When I get out of my bath tub I just feel like, well, yep, that was cold, but don't feel that zingy feeling I get after sitting in the canyon.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-28T22:55:58+0200

**Subforum:** Beginners Area

### margor_j — 2014-01-14T22:58:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pls-give-me-thoughts-on-dhea-pregnenolone.9341/post-105670>

Hi hi
iv discovered i can by pregnenolone in nz.. 4 years of doing salivary labs (sex hormones / cortisol) constantly shows me morbidly low cortisol in the day, and sky high at night. last test it was almost off the chart, but i had just come back from europe..
im about to do more labs, of the same. since iv started JK protocol, yes, my sleeps improved and body pain has reduced by about 95%, but this fatness is DOING my head in..

i think, knowing my history - pregnenolone, at least, is going to help, not hinder.. but i heard JK saying in an interview, DHEA is usually hand in hand with chronic insomnia.. and the more i read, the more confused i get  but im SO desperate to have a change in my fat.. its been 4 years of NOTHING working..

can i start on pregnenolone? i have used it before and had no adverse effects, no good ones either (story of my life, the last 4 years, ive tried it ALL and spent thousands and thousands and thousands of dollars..)

thoughts? i cant go wrong? im HOPING LIKE HELL it will help me at least lose... 5kg? that would be a start...

thanks guys..

desperate MG

Ok, I say it would not hurt but you NEED to get to the bottom of why the cortisol is shot. It finally clicked for me when my last test of tests showed that my pregnenolone was tanked, my thyroid labs were still wonky and the progesterone was just marginally better. I have major pregnenolone steal going on to keep the adrenals and cortisol levels happy. I am taking meds and supplements for all three of the hormones but it is not addressing why I need to. I plan on calling my GI doctor today to get some stool sampling done and see if I have an infection in my gut (ready Misty’s stuff) plus I see my hormone doctor about my last set of labs today. We will see if he thinks I am right in needing to address the adrenals.

Hate to say it but there is no quick fix if your hormones are so screwed up. I did 3 rounds of HCG in the past 1 Â½ years and I lost a bunch of weight on it but it did not stick due to the hormones being wacked.

It is a process and hang in, it will be a bumpy ride.

oh and it would be worth it to become a gold member for a bit and listen to all of the webinars and QA sessions. Tons of great information in there. I have downloaded most of the audio versions and put in on an mp3 player to listen to on my commute.

Nicole

id have no idea WHY my cortisol is shot.. but i am sleeping better with a darker room, wearing blue blocking shades at nite.. i try to look at the sunrise etc. but i do still use melatonin or my chinese sleep herbs to sleep at nite.. i know melatonins not the best, but when youve had 4 years of CHRONIC insomnia, then .... desperation means you cant always heed advice. however, most nites, ive gotten away with having only half a one..

i had very stressful jobs this past year, which kicks the coffee habit off again - it mite only be one a day, but i start to feel myself unravel with all the stress etc.. plus i had jobs that meant i was getting up anywhere from 3 -5am to go to work.. hello adrenal stress!

# Thread: Bedroom lighting solution - when the breaker is OFF
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bedroom-lighting-solution-when-the-breaker-is-off.9342/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KatieO — 2014-01-14T23:23:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bedroom-lighting-solution-when-the-breaker-is-off.9342/post-105676>

OK, so you've got the breaker off to your bedroom, and you're sleeping in complete darkness- great! But what happens when your 5yr old comes wandering in at 4am or you don't want to break your neck getting to the bathroom? I know I could have a candle, but I am not to be trusted with an open flame at 4am!

Here's what I did, and I hope this is helpful to someone...

Step 1- get go to IKEA and get a SUNNAN Led lamp:

[http://www.ikea.com/us/en/catalog/products/90154371/](http://www.ikea.com/us/en/catalog/products/90154371/)

Step 2- order some amber "correction gels" from Amazon:

[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0006Z7M4G/ref=wms\_ohs\_product?ie=UTF8&psc=1](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0006Z7M4G/ref=wms_ohs_product?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

Step 3- cut the gel to cover the bulb area of the lamp, and glue or tape it on!

Viola- a little bit of light by your bed, no outlet needed, and you won't burn the house down, plus no blue light.

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-14T23:30:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bedroom-lighting-solution-when-the-breaker-is-off.9342/post-105677>

or you could just use a red headlamp...that's what we use [http://www.amazon.com/Princeton-Tec...1389734961&sr=8-7&keywords=red+light+headlamp](http://www.amazon.com/Princeton-Tec-Remix-Headlamp-5mm/dp/B002HMTNUW/ref=sr_1_7?ie=UTF8&qid=1389734961&sr=8-7&keywords=red+light+headlamp)

### KatieO — 2014-01-14T23:59:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bedroom-lighting-solution-when-the-breaker-is-off.9342/post-105679>

Nice headlamp! I like that idea.
In our case, flashlights were hard to keep track of, so I like that this just sits right by the bed, and I know it's where I left it. But really, what I love about those lamps, is the rechargeable battery! When my daughter takes her panel out into the sun to charge, it really drives home the messages we are trying to send her about where energy comes from.

### caroline — 2014-01-15T00:00:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bedroom-lighting-solution-when-the-breaker-is-off.9342/post-105681>

My eyes must be better because I have no trouble seeing in the dark .... Then again - I don't have a five year old!

# Thread: Reverse or slow progression of Alzheimer's?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AnnieW — 2014-01-15T00:55:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/post-105689>

Hi friends,

We just lost my grandfather to Parkinson's in July. Now my almost 83 year old grandmother might possibly be in the early stages of Alzheimer's. The doctor wasn't clear and it's obvious we need a second opinion. I was wondering what, if anything, could be done to reverse or slow the progression if she is officially diagnosed? She is diabetic and has high blood pressure, plus she takes pain medicine for a back condition. She is not very active because of her back and leg issues. She told me today she wakes up and her hands are numb or a few hours. I just want to help her the best I can.

### fitness@home — 2014-01-15T02:12:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/post-105700>

I have read that adding coconut oil to the diet is helpful to those with Alzheimer's. Try Googling it for more information.

### SeaHorse — 2014-01-15T02:52:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/post-105704>

### AnnieW — 2014-01-15T03:34:00+0200

### nonchalant — 2014-01-15T16:49:29+0200

### caroline — 2014-01-15T22:10:21+0200

### Jude — 2014-01-15T23:14:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/post-105817>

One small thing to note is coconut oil can tend to give older folk diarrhea....know of friends who started their mum, a 90yo, on a tablespoon daily with that result. She was in a nursing home and the staff then banned it! Start slowly!

### AnnieW — 2014-01-15T23:37:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/post-105823>

Yes I found his posts Caroline, thanks for the suggestion. He has lots of knowledge so I will be following him. I am looking at ordering some MCT oil soon. My grandmother has LOTS of GI issues. I will be sure to tell my mother to start her slowly and add some into her tea maybe a teaspoonful at a time. Coconut oil even gives me diarrhea and I'm 31.

You know, I "fell off the wagon" a few months back, after losing 16 lbs in two weeks (what the hell???). I feel like total $hit. Nothing in life is ever a coincidence I think. I have been thinking about the way I have been living lately and if I keep it up I may not make it to 40. I think...are there limits? Is 350 lbs not enough? Back on the path to optimal for me. I made a deal with my grandma that she couldn't die until long after I have my first baby (that was when she almost died two years ago). I have been trying for three years with no luck. It's the weight and the PCOS and the massive block in my brain that is keeping me from being optimal. And of course, everyone around me is pregnant right now. Sometimes it bothers me but sometimes it doesn't because I know how horrible my health is. It would be very unjust and unfair for me to put myself or a baby through an obese pregnancy. Again, no coincidence. Anyway, I think God is saying to me...you are sucking right now and need to change. So I need to not just listen, but act on it. My poor grandparents' health was just so awful with both of them having diabetes and my grandfather suffering from Parkinson's for ten years, and now my grandma and her "possible" Alzheimer's. I just am feeling this pull again toward optimal and I need to go with it before my life passes me by and I have nothing left but health problems and empty arms.

Anyway, sorry for the side rant. Thanks for being a great community guys!

### caroline — 2014-01-16T00:28:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/post-105830>

Annie - Dr. Tim talked about becoming pregnant while being suboptimal. It really struck with me.

He said you are subjecting your child to a horrible childhood of poor health \[or even much worse\] and not being able to enjoy their childhood.

Dr. K. has said several times - that, as much as it pained him, he has had to tell patients during a consult that they shoudn't be having kids. very painful to hear I am sure.
He has also talked of the stats of having an autistic child - seemingly simple things like having to go into hospital with the flu during your third trimester....etc. - really stacks the deck against you.
Dr. K. told us of a very good friend of his who he had to tell that to and they didn't listen and the nightmare of their life.

This is totally none of my business - but have you thought of adopting and giving yourself and your grandma the gift of a gorgeous health baby that needs you...

### AnnieW — 2014-01-16T01:32:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/post-105839>

Yes I'm terrified of autism too. I know there is help out there for autistic children, but if me being suboptimal increases the risk of having a child who develops it of course I'm going to do whatever I can to prevent it from happening.

We are definitely open to adoption. The problem is that our finances are suboptimal too, but we are working on that as well.

My endocrinologist says I'm "just a kid" and that I still have time to conceive if I can get down to 200 lbs. I feel like PCOS is fighting against my clock though.

### caroline — 2014-01-16T06:58:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/post-105878>

Annie - when you have time read Mr. Pinkies journal....

Have you read dr. K' s stuff on autism? There is also a webinar on women and fat loss.....

### CoolSwede — 2014-01-16T11:23:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/post-105886>

Many elderly people have very low levels of B12 due to malnutrition, and B12 deficiency can mimic dementia and Alzheimer's. Just my 2 cents...

Edit:

> She told me today she wakes up and her hands are numb or a few hours

Tingling and numbness are MAJOR symptoms of B12 deficiency!

[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BvEizypoyO0](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BvEizypoyO0)

### AnnieW — 2014-01-16T14:44:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/post-105895>

Hi Caroline,

I have read Quantum Autism and it really opened my eyes. I have read MrPinkies' journal and I admire her so much for not only her personal weight loss success but how she has used the information on this site to help her family. I must have missed the fat loss webinar so I will look that one up today.

CoolSwede, thanks so much for that link. I would not be surprised if she is B12 deficient. Her diet has gotten better since living with my mom, but it still needs a lot of work.

### AndrÃ©s — 2014-01-22T16:11:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reverse-or-slow-progression-of-alzheimers.9343/post-106622>

> [CoolSwede said:](/goto/post?id=105886)
>
> Many elderly people have very low levels of B12 due to malnutrition, and B12 deficiency can mimic dementia and Alzheimer's. Just my 2 cents...

Not completely independent, there has been a clinical trial where they have stopped progression of dementia in those with high blood homocysteine levels with some high dose vitamin Bs regimen commented by [Dr. Kendrick](http://drmalcolmkendrick.org/2013/12/18/how-to-avoid-dementia/), [Dr. Briffa](http://www.drbriffa.com/2013/05/23/b-vitamin-therapy-looks-promising-for-staving-off-alzheimers-diseaselow-carb-diets-can-help-maintain-the-metabolism-after-weight-loss/?utm_source=feedly&utm_medium=feed&utm_campaign=Feed%3A+drbriffa%2FsOZf+%28Dr+Briffa%27s+Blog+-+A+Good+Look+at+Good+Health%29) and [AHN-Europe](http://www.anh-europe.org/ANH_Exclusive_Beat_Alzheimers_with_online_and_blood_tests_plus_high_dose_B_vitamins?utm_source=feedburner&utm_medium=feed&utm_campaign=Feed%3A+anh-europe%2Fnews+%28Alliance+for+Natural+Health+News%29).

# Thread: CT question - Shivering
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Da-mo — 2014-01-15T08:32:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105738>

I'm currently doing CT in a pool at the base of a waterfall immersed up to my neck at 14C for about 25 minutes.

I start shivering within 5 minutes of immersion and the worst of the shivering peaks during the 15 minute walk back from the waterfall and continues for 30minutes to an hour afterwards.

Should I be keeping the same duration, increasing duration or just going to the point where shivering is induced?

I do try to maximise the calorie burn and muscle workout from the shivering but wonder whether that should be the end point or not?

### nicld — 2014-01-15T16:40:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105760>

> [Da-mo said:](/goto/post?id=105738)
>
> I'm currently doing CT in a pool at the base of a waterfall immersed up to my neck at 14C for about 25 minutes.
>
> I start shivering within 5 minutes of immersion and the worst of the shivering peaks during the 15 minute walk back from the waterfall and continues for 30minutes to an hour afterwards.
>
> Should I be keeping the same duration, increasing duration or just going to the point where shivering is induced?
>
> I do try to maximise the calorie burn and muscle workout from the shivering but wonder whether that should be the end point or not?

Shivering afterwards in normal. I asked jack once about shivering while in CT and he said that it was not really good and that when that happens to him, he gets out and has some shrimp or coconut oil then get's back in and it helps. Once in a while I get a little shivering while doing CT but not too bad. When it does get bad I get out. That's my N=1 and I do ALOT of long cold cold CT and it has got way better over the past 6 months.

### Dali Dula — 2014-01-15T20:13:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105780>

> [Da-mo said:](/goto/post?id=105738)
>
> I'm currently doing CT in a pool at the base of a waterfall immersed up to my neck at 14C for about 25 minutes.
>
> I start shivering within 5 minutes of immersion and the worst of the shivering peaks during the 15 minute walk back from the waterfall and continues for 30minutes to an hour afterwards.
>
> Should I be keeping the same duration, increasing duration or just going to the point where shivering is induced?
>
> I do try to maximise the calorie burn and muscle workout from the shivering but wonder whether that should be the end point or not?

For the first year I did what you are doing and had the same experience. Now I stop full immersion when I begin to shiver whether that is just five minutes or longer. This also seems to prevent shivering post CT. Saves a lot of time as we are non functional during shivering.

### Da-mo — 2014-01-15T21:58:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105793>

Thanks for the responses. So if I understand correctly, I should end CT at the onset of shivering and over time the duration before that onset should increase?

I should probably say that the shivering is not uncontrollable - I can stop it at will if I want - just wonder whether I should . . . .

### caroline — 2014-01-15T22:03:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105795>

I really thought some shivering was a good thing .... am I wrong about that?

### nonchalant — 2014-01-16T03:45:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105849>

What kind of shivering are we talking about? Can you talk normally? Are they fairly intense, or almost unnoticeable tremors running along your spine?

### Josh — 2014-01-16T03:51:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105850>

If you have not already, read CT 6, 7 and the second half of 11 where someone talks about their process; and the appendix of the book if you have it. These will frame your inquiry.

### nicld — 2014-01-16T04:19:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105854>

I think the small shivers are fine at least I hope so since I get them. The real issue may be the whole body shakes where your core body temperature may be dropping. When I get the whole body shaking then its time to get out.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-16T04:24:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105856>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=105854)
>
> I think the small shivers are fine at least I hope so since I get them. The real issue may be the whole body shakes where your core body temperature may be dropping. When I get the whole body shaking then its time to get out.

Yes, I agree totally.

### Da-mo — 2014-01-16T07:27:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105880>

Seems like there is no definitive answer so I'll take it to the Ask Jack section after checking the CT blogs again . . . . .

Next silly question . . . . Risk of reperfusion injury

After I get out of the waterfall pool it is a 15 minute walk back to the car. Given that I am wet and cold and shivering, and I feel it more in my arms and legs and between my shoulder blades - I dont really feel cold around the torso otherwise . . . . .

What risk am I running or a reperfusion injury where because of the walking I am not circulating cold blood from my extremeties and limbs back through the heart and lungs? . . . . .

this is the part of my CT where I feel the coldest - for the first part of the walk, by the second half of the walk I'm feeling warmer again.

I may take this question to the Ask Jack section as well. I know he will stand outside in the buff for awhile after getting out of the lake - but maybe he doesnt move around so much while he does this.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-16T18:52:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105922>

No idea about the reperfusion injury, but I like the double meaning in the second line of your sig.

### Da-mo — 2014-01-16T23:16:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-question-shivering.9347/post-105963>

I hadn't thought of that until you mentioned it nonchalant  But yes, you are right!

I sometimes ask people, chocloate or vanilla, how many choices do you have . . . most say two. I say you have four, chocolate, vanilla, both or none . . . .

My father extended this even further. Once when he was in hospital he was given a menu so he could tick in the box what foods he wanted. What he wanted was not on the list, so he wrote on that list what he wanted, drew a small box next to it and put a check mark in the box. Blow me down if they didnt turn up with the breakfast he chose

BTW, we saw an eel in the waterfall pool where we CT last night . . . dont think wife or daughter will be going back anytime soon unless I make some sort of cage for them to sit in . . . .

### Josh — 2014-01-24T06:52:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/90-day-optimal-reset.9350/post-106878>

On Gerald Pollack and 4th Phase of Water stuff.

1\. The book is great and easy to read, but it takes time to digest.
2\. He actually does not talk about human cells much in the book. For this I recommend the following which include much of the material from the book and more:

[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JnGCMQ8TJ\_g](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JnGCMQ8TJ_g)

EU part 1

[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hqHWueBp23c](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hqHWueBp23c)

EU part 2

[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Y--L6BoH3Ug](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Y--L6BoH3Ug)

a lecture that covers the high points and gets to the point with the cell, but is not as comprehensive conceptually as the book. I watched it with my wife and she not only got it, but she got excited and started applying it everywhere. Not an everyday experience with her and physics....

[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=M3Sy1SZNebk](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=M3Sy1SZNebk)

with Ameer Rosic who asks some good and not so good questions, still much to be gathered on how to think about it.

Also the Thunderbolts Project Videos. All in all, if you watch them all, you get most of the book plus more related to biology. He is a good speaker and does not overwhelm. I would say that one could watch the videos and get what is needed to understand Dr. Kruse and go on.

You gotta love a man who readily says "we have no clue yet...." and "well, water has some potential for the future..."

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/90-day-optimal-reset.9350/post-107024>

# Thread: Hair falling out and dizziness

**Posts:** 25

### Eliza — 2014-01-16T10:32:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-falling-out-and-dizziness.9357/post-105885>

Hi all,

I've been on the leptin reset for about 2 weeks now and CT for about a week. I'm a 28 year old female. Since starting the reset I'm finding on occasion I'm quite dizzy despite putting salt on all my food. I've got bone broth cooking as we speak. My hair is falling out more than normal too and my skin is dry.

Nobody looks at me and says I'm overweight, but according to numerous BIA I have 38% fat mass. I feel very overweight.

Can someone tell me what might be causing the dizziness? And whether the leptin reset with CT will allow me to drop back to a much lower fat mass and reverse the insulin resistance?

My history: I'm being treated with metformin for mild insulin resistance. Other relevant labs I've had done so far (for the others I will try to get the Dr to agree to):
Vitamin D: 65nM/L (currently on supplementation)
Free T4: 14.4pmol/L
TSH: 1.8 mU/L
Free T3: 3.2 pmol/L
Testosterone: <0.5nmol/L
SHBG: 93 nmol/L
Free testosterone: <4
FAI: <0.5L
DHEAS: 4umol/L
ESR: 16mm/hr
Cholesterol: 6.9 mmol/L
HDL: 2.3mmol/L
LDL: 4.3 mmol/L
Trigs: 0.76mmol/L
Magnesium: 0.79mmol/L

Thank you so much for your help in advance. This is the first site I've seen in years that's offering real light at the end of the tunnel for so many people.

### caroline — 2014-01-16T12:05:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-falling-out-and-dizziness.9357/post-105887>

Hi Eliza and welcome! Sorry - I can't help with labs but someone will come along as soon as they have time.

Ask your Doc to do a hsCRP ..... that indicates how much inflammation you have in your body.

Have you had a saliva ASI done? you need to know your cortisol levels and DHEA and Pg/E2 etc.

There is a free 90 Day optimal reset starting soon. Please sign up on the home page. There is a yellow banner right at the top of the page where you can register. Dr. K. is going to cover all the basics and he will have guests - Dr. Jackson is one and Ameer Rosic etc.

Dr. Kruse and Misty have done a webinar about getting labs. You should get a link when you register.

Also - Dr. Tim Jackson did a free webinar about hormones - you can track that down on the home page.

Do you have Dr.K's book The Epi Paleo RX .... it has a great section on labs and gives you a great overview of all of this and what Dr. Kruse is all about.

What is your non native EMF exposure? at home? at work? this is critical.
Are you able to get outside for grounding and sunlight? Are you drinking spring water?

Please start a journal in the journal section - Dr. K. likes journals - he needs context. Tell us where you live...in a city? and more of your health history. A journal is great for keeping track and interacting with the rest of us!

There is also a section for posting labs - they might not get seen here quite as readily.

Please keep reading and asking questions......

This is a great journey to Optimal health - so glad that you have found Dr. K.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-16T18:45:19+0200

### HoneyChild — 2014-01-16T19:15:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-falling-out-and-dizziness.9357/post-105923>

You really ought to get a full thyroid panel done which includes antibodies and rT3 as this can be linked to hair loss.

Also, B12 deficiency can cause hair loss. I remember watching a vid about a women who went bald because of it. Unfortunately I can't seem to get the video to load but it used to be here

"Janette first appeared in the BBC InsideOut documentary in October 2006. Since then the NHS has forced her doctor to withdraw B12 replacement therapy on a number of occasions, and she tells of her struggles with having B12 and then having it taken away.

She has bravely agreed to be filmed without her usual wig, but her memory is playing up because it is so long since her last injection.

Janette also appears in the BBC Documentary, filmed 4 years earlier. In the meantime, she's had her treatment stopped on two occasions by the local health board."

[https://www.b12d.org/pernicious-anemia-and-hair-loss](https://www.b12d.org/pernicious-anemia-and-hair-loss)

### cantweight — 2014-01-16T19:27:07+0200

Hi Eliza, not sure what is causing your dissyness but in response to your second question about using the leptin reset/ct/etc to effect weight loss and basically resolve insulin resistance is that.... yes it will...and in a fashion completely belonging to you, which really means no one can predict how fast or slow that may take

Are you getting a Biochemistry and hormone tests as well?
Your Vit D is low at 65....my Aussie Dr likes it round 120.. you need Vit D3 and probably 10,000u for a couple of months and recheck.
DHEAS is low as well (you didn't include ref ranges)
Trigs ok
HDL is lower than LDL indicating a leaky gut..(which epi paleo covers)
Your T3 is a bit low.
Looks like ?pregnenolone steal syndrome...heaps on here about this..Hormones 101 to start.
Have fun!

### Eliza — 2014-01-20T09:15:38+0200

### caroline — 2014-01-20T11:33:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-falling-out-and-dizziness.9357/post-106272>

BPC = Bullet proof Coffee. You add a couple of TBSP of coconut oil and the same in GF butter to your coffee and blitz.
It makes it so much easier to get more fat in. You can do it to green tea too.
But this has to be in context - maybe you shouldn't be having coffee? if you have adrenal issues?

remember that gut issues and hormones are inter related. Everything is connected.

are you sure your fat mass index number is correct? If it is a scale you have at home - my understanding is they aren't all that accurate.

It doesn't sound right to me......

### caroline — 2014-01-20T11:45:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hair-falling-out-and-dizziness.9357/post-106273>

There are a fair few Aussies here! We have pretty specific issues here because of the donut hole in the ozone. Dr. Kruse goes into a lot of detail for us in that thread I mentioned.

There is a lot of stuff to get thru here and some of it is pretty tough going unless you have a science degree.

As Dr. K. has told me .... you don't have to understand Chinese to enjoy Chinese food.

Just make sure you are doing epi paleo and C/T ... and limiting your non native EMF exposure and getting a lot of sunshine and grounding. It is the concepts that you want to get a good grasp of and your understanding will improve down the track.
I read something now that I read a year or two ago and go ... so that's what he meant! It happens to all of us. Remember that Dr. K. is 8-10 years ahead of us,

Have you listened to the free podcasts? they are a huge help.......and the webinars and follow up Q&A....

# Thread: Ulcer Colitis & Crohn's Disease
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ulcer-colitis-crohns-disease.9358/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthman — 2014-01-16T12:23:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ulcer-colitis-crohns-disease.9358/post-105889>

Is a colonoscopy the only way to test for ulcer colitis & crohn's disease?

### sooperb — 2014-01-16T22:01:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ulcer-colitis-crohns-disease.9358/post-105945>

[http://www.nhs.uk/Conditions/Crohns-disease/Pages/Diagnosis.aspx](http://www.nhs.uk/Conditions/Crohns-disease/Pages/Diagnosis.aspx)

### Destiny — 2014-01-17T02:59:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ulcer-colitis-crohns-disease.9358/post-105977>

There is a test called fecal Calprotectin - Intestinal Inflammation Index. It might be a good first step before a colonoscopy.

[http://www.calprotectintest.com/](http://www.calprotectintest.com/)

### Eugenia — 2014-01-18T03:33:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ulcer-colitis-crohns-disease.9358/post-106084>

You don't have to be afraid of colonoscopy. As long as they put you under (not complete anesthesia, there's a middle path for it), you shouldn't feel a thing. In some countries they don't offer that though..

# Thread: Sighâ€¦â€¦.. I think I need a new hormone doctor
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sigh%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%C2%A6%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%C2%A6-i-think-i-need-a-new-hormone-doctor.9373/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2014-01-17T18:36:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sigh%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%C2%A6%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%C2%A6-i-think-i-need-a-new-hormone-doctor.9373/post-106032>

I went and saw him on Wednesday and he was a little bit helpful. He read my labs and noticed that I was low on magnesium which concerns me since I am on 3 forms of magnesium and the more expensive ones. I need to figure out how low magnesium relates to other hormones being off now.

My Albumin level (gm/dL) (main protein of plasma) is lower than the limits and not sure why. He asked if a ate protein and besides fat, that is the main food in my diet so it is not related to eating. Another one to figure out.

He said that it looks like I have some microplasma biofilms in my body since my M.pneumoniae Ab IgG and IgM came back positive. He suggested a supplement to try but I cannot remember but I need to dig deeper into what this is too. I need to find out why I have this which for me, means GI stool testing. I will be seeing which doctor to have it ordered from depending on if one ordering clinic gets a better discount than the other. I also showed Parvo antibodies (no current infection) and he is not sure how to deal with this either

And then there was the whole pregnenolone steal issue. I am 99% sure that I have this since my pregnenolone levels are in the basement, thyroid was low, dhea was low and Pg was low before supplementing. He had never really heard of this and when I described it, he was not really sure about it. He had nothing to offer me and did not think it was the adrenals even though I had told him about my crash many years ago and my years of compulsive exercise (which also stressed the adrenals). He did want to see me start exercising and see how I handle it. **_I am hesitant right now in this because if it is my adrenals that are wonky, wouldn't exercise make them work harder and be a less than ideal thing?_**

Basically, I think I have learned more than my doctor and he cannot longer help me if he does not really understand it and I get the feeling he really did not want to learn about it. He is not a bad doctor just not what I need right now.

Right now, I do not think that my GP would be able to help either since he did take a look at my tests and did not see the big picture of what was going on but he probably does not have the time to see the big picture since he is part of big medicine.

I have to do my melatonin test again this weekend and am still waiting for my 23 and me results. I will try getting the GI testing next week and then I think I should have enough information to see what the bleep is going on.

So, I will be patiently waiting for Dr. Tim to announce the plans for this year’s consults.

Oh and if anyone knows of a Dr Kruse protocol doctor in the Minneapolis MN area, let me know.

### ATL_Paleo — 2014-01-17T20:18:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sigh%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%C2%A6%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%C2%A6-i-think-i-need-a-new-hormone-doctor.9373/post-106038>

IIRC, magnesium is very difficult to measure through standard bloodwork.

The adrenals and thyroid are closedly linked. Thus, your intution about not exercising seems correct.

Low thyroid is often linked to adrenal issues. I would focus on the thyroid issue since other hormonal issues are difficult to resolve with thyroid and/or adrenal issues.

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-17T20:24:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sigh%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%C2%A6%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%C2%A6-i-think-i-need-a-new-hormone-doctor.9373/post-106041>

Nicole think about this: Sometimes, selecting a doctor who will at least work with you is better than not having a doctor to work with at all (especially if you want things paid for by your insurance company).

You can help educate him. and always follow your instincts - you know you best. tell him you worked out one day and felt like crap and keep on doing what works for you. if he runs labs and is willing to work with you... hang with him. you can always keep searching for another doc..... but for now you've got one that's at least working with you..

### MsYo — 2014-01-21T22:27:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sigh%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%C2%A6%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%C2%A6-i-think-i-need-a-new-hormone-doctor.9373/post-106498>

Having a doctor that is willing to work with you is better than one that won't. I have a primary doc that started me on BHRT while I was researching other doctors. I was on a 6month waiting list to see a new MD that specilized in natural medicine as well as MD. It is a slow process, but 6 months later I'm improving. I am hypo/adrenal fatigue and a former endurance runner. I started exercising but it is mainly strength training (I lift heavy) and I limit my cardio (for now). I added pregnenolone (300mgs) and DHEA. I also take cytozyme AD. Create a partnership with your doctor until you find a new one.

### JanSz — 2014-02-05T21:12:11+0200

If you want, post all available tests, and I will take a look at them.
Look also my posts on this thread, may give you some idea.
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/#post81508](http://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/#post81508)

Better yet,
you have already said that your
DHEAs, pregnenolone and progesterone are low.
Lets atm concentrate on just those three.
Eat two pills, one pill of each type (preg & DHEA) at wakeup, get blood testing on day 2-4 of your cycle, but have to be at least one month+ from the time you start those pills.
Test the three above and cortisol, preferably at LabCorp.

Nutricology/ Allergy Research Group Pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix 150mg
[http://www.amazon.com/Pregnenolone-Nutricology-Allergy-Research-Tabs/dp/B003PWJFJW/ref=sr\_1\_sc\_3?ie=UTF8&qid=1391627253&sr=8-3-spell&keywords=pegnenolone 150](http://www.amazon.com/Pregnenolone-Nutricology-Allergy-Research-Tabs/dp/B003PWJFJW/ref=sr_1_sc_3?ie=UTF8&qid=1391627253&sr=8-3-spell&keywords=pegnenolone+150)

Allergy Research Group - Dhea 50 Mg Micronized Lipid Matrix 60 Scored Tablets
[http://www.amazon.com/Allergy-Research-Group-Micronized-Tablets/dp/B004BZJCCC/ref=sr\_1\_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1391627349&sr=8-1&keywords=allergy DHEA 50](http://www.amazon.com/Allergy-Research-Group-Micronized-Tablets/dp/B004BZJCCC/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1391627349&sr=8-1&keywords=allergy+DHEA+50)

1 ------- Pregnenolone, Mass Spectrometry--Test Number: 140707 CPT Code: 84140
4 ------- Progesterone--Test Number: 004317 CPT Code: 84144
5 ------- Cortisol, Free, Serum, With CBG (Endocrine Sciences) Test Number: 500440 CPT Code: 82533; 84449
6 ------- Cortisol, AM & PM Test Number: 104000 CPT Code: 82533(x2) (7:30AM, 12PM)
8 ------- Dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA) Sulfate--Test Number: 004020 CPT Code: 82627

Post results on this thread, let me know when ready.

.

# Thread: Supplements for SLEEP!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements-for-sleep.9377/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### margor_j — 2014-01-17T23:23:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements-for-sleep.9377/post-106061>

another quick question...

iv had chronic insomnia for four years now. its improved lately - blacking out the room at nite, getting up with the sun, blu blocking glasses but.. yesterday Dr Tim Jackson told me (via email) that if im using melatonin, il never get better..which guts me, because.. iv been using melatonin. admittedly, im down to half a tab now (2.5mg?) - my phosphatidylserine has run out - but i also read that takes months to build up in your body, so it prob wasnt doing much anyway and its not cheap!
i also use 5HTP and L - Tryptophan - tho last nite, i went to bed with no melatonin and sure as sh\*t - the heart rate started to thump, the head was working over time .. i cracked and took half a melatonin at midnite, then in frustration took one of my chinese sleep herbals - which are AMAZING.. id prob be dead by now if i hadnt discovered them, lol.. but theyre running low so iv been trying not to use them.. (can only get in malaysia, so im waiting on a resupply... fingers crossed they come in the next week before i go away for a month but im not liking my chances)

i woke at 6.20 am, so prob 5 hours sleep.. i know this isnt ideal and all the 'experts' tell us how important sleep is, what it does etc etc and to get more of it, which i dont have a problem with, but what if you CANT get to sleep!!  im not sure if last nite was to do with full moon or not, i have noticed some connection - and i usually get this effect like every two months.. itll be one -3 nites and the i come good again.. i wonder because last nite, the melatonin wasnt enough.. also, i had a shocker of a day yesterday, absolutely wound up about the state of my body.. so maybe MAYBE that was some of it? when youre dreaming of lab tests and writing on here i imagine so, lol..

if i dont use melatonin im still afraid to go commando on sleep aids.. oh, i use FASTER EFT , when i remember.. it did seem to help me relax last nite, i jsut hate the nites when im so wound up theres no way i can sleep..

thanks guys! i really appreciate your suggestions, input, advice.. i thought i knew a lot, but JKs freaking amazing and i keep learning every day..

Mags

### caroline — 2014-01-18T00:02:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements-for-sleep.9377/post-106065>

meditation? JK talks about that a fair bit and there are some folks here who have given great links. Do you follow Josh's posts?

Have you tried C/T in the evening? many say it helps with sleep.......

### margor_j — 2014-01-18T00:08:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements-for-sleep.9377/post-106066>

yeah.. i think when you get to frustration factor, meditation doesnt do much for me..but i think the faster EFT helped for sure.
umm il look josh up! just new on here!

### caroline — 2014-01-18T00:16:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements-for-sleep.9377/post-106068>

### sooperb — 2014-01-21T13:37:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements-for-sleep.9377/post-106456>

I sympathise with your plight over sleep issues. I had a terrible night last night and haven't had an unbroken nights sleep for a long time. My issue is palpitations. Sometimes I can calm them down with relaxation techniques but sometimes it's pointless trying. Fortunately I don't have a busy head but my gut tends to rumble and pop and complain and I can feel my heartbeat in most of my of my arteries especially my neck and throat. It's very unpleasant and sleep is impossible.

I don't take anything mainly because I would prefer to solve the problem not mask it with pills. There's a herbal pill called Nytol in the UK which a lot of folk swear by, I don't know if it's available where you are.

### Lahelada — 2014-01-21T14:05:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements-for-sleep.9377/post-106458>

High dosage magnesium helped me recalibrate. I used melatonin but over time itloses effect and is actually counterproductive.
I sense that half the problem is your anxiety about not being able to sleep which after 4 years is more than understandable.
Can you take some time out ? I am thinking about a week or just a couple of days when you don't have to sleep if you cannot. During this time you can do anything at night as long as it is in the dark,listen to the radio,call into a late night show discussing the merits of growing radish above ground and the revolutionary socioeconomic aspects thereof , anything you fancy as long as it can be done in the dark . If you sleep one hour only so be it. At sunrise you go out and greet the sun and you stay awake for the day but without coffee or stimulants. You are allowed to go to bed at sunset as long as you have eaten 4 hours before. Rinse and repeat if you have the time.

Now I know that you are already doing a lot of this but the difference is that with this approach you are not doing it to sleep to function the next day. You are doing it to get a rythm back,to lose the sleep anxiety. Makes sense?

If you cannot take time out try this.Eat at least three hours before bedtime ( Cortisol output triples during eating ) If you go to bed and cannot sleep/switch off after 10 minutes get back up without any lights,walk about stay vertical and after 15 mins try to sleep again.Rinse and repeat.

### nicld — 2014-01-21T16:06:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/supplements-for-sleep.9377/post-106465>

I used to use melatonin all the time too. Reducing the night light and blue blockers have made a huge difference for me. The hubby would fall asleep within 5 minutes after his head hitting the pillow, me an hour to an hour and a half later I was sleeping then waking up a couple of hours later.

Need to stop the melatonin and be prepared for things to get worse before they get better but it can get better. Also need to figure out what is going on with those hormones. It is not a quick process, trust me, I’ve been at it for over 7 years now but it does get better. Oh and make sure to get CT in, that is huge.

havent done much CTing at nite before bed per se - i can do a cold face bath but an ice baths not so easy where i am.. for now.. hope to change that soon. i so miss my morning swims in the lake in the other town i was in! but i did them first thing in the morning, otherwise ppl see you and assume youre self harming cos WHY would anyone wanna swim in that lake?!?! (blah blah blah..)
7 years trying to heal hormones?!?! ugh, that gives me anxiety! lol. i guess iv been at it for 5 years so..

thanks guys

so for now, protein shakes along the lines of - heaps of coconut oil (give or take, around 3TBsp), coconut cream if i have it, protein powder, maybe some quality cocoa, maybe some avocado... i can usually get at least 7 hours off it, which ,when im working, is ideal!
