### healthman — 2014-01-28T21:47:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-ribose-question.9482/post-107211>

Can anyone advise on the best form of d ribose to get?
I see life extension and jarrow formulas offer powder, veggie caps and chewable tablets?
Any help appreciated
Thanks

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-28T21:52:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-ribose-question.9482/post-107213>

I use life extensions. I've had the best success with it... but other have had success with other stuff too

### Josh — 2014-01-28T22:01:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-ribose-question.9482/post-107214>

If one reads the label, most supplement companies are using Bioenergy s Ribose including Jarrow, NOW and LEF. I suspect that Nutrabulk which sells it for less is also, but they are not controlling the moisture as well so it comes clumpy. It is very hygroscopic. It gets a lot cheaper as you buy larger quantities. Bioenergy starts at 10kg boxes for wholesale accounts at 47/kg delivered.

[http://www.bioenergyribose.com/?utm...ret\_Billy&utm\_campaign=NI.com\_Website\_Jan2014](http://www.bioenergyribose.com/?utm_source=NutraIngredients.com&utm_medium=Leaderboard&utm_content=Secret_Billy&utm_campaign=NI.com_Website_Jan2014)

It is a sweetner like sugar that dissolves in water and just about anything including your mouth really well. No need to pay people to encapsulate it.

### Tanya — 2014-01-28T22:06:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-ribose-question.9482/post-107215>

I also used to buy Life extensions brand which I liked very much, but later I switched to Healthy Origins and I don't see any difference, except for the price. healthman, I think with the powder, you don't get any additives, it is much better absorbable, and you can have more flexibility as to where and how you want to use it. For traveling purposes, I would use chewable tablets. Just my opinion.

### persistence — 2014-03-12T05:39:22+0200

# Thread: Opening oyster shells the Mesolithic way
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opening-oyster-shells-the-mesolithic-way.9489/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-01-29T10:45:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opening-oyster-shells-the-mesolithic-way.9489/post-107275>

This is very short but to the point. It also has two really nice photos -- a massive blow-up of an oyster shell at the top of the page and oyster shells on a fire lower down.

> The experiments were carried out by archaeologists Esben Kannegaard from Jutland Museum and SÃ¸ren H. Andersen, professor emeritus from the University of Aarhus. â€œYou do not need to use force or any special technique to open an oyster, a brief warming is enough,â€￾ said the archaeologists in the latest issue of the journal _Skalk_.
>
> The background to the experiment was to explain the huge quantities of oyster, mussel and cockle shells found in famous midden sites which are known from ErtebÃ¸lleruten, where there are thick layers dated to c. 6000 years ago. These kitchen middens contain no evidence of tools required to open oyster shells, which would need to be be thin, strong and flexible. In addition, the shells show no damage or evidence of tool use but occasionally exhibit slight traces of exterior scorching. ...
>
> The amount of shells left behind also shows that oysters were not an insignificant supplement to general sustenance but a common food source for our ancestors.

[http://www.pasthorizonspr.com/index.php/archives/01/2014/opening-oysters-shells-the-mesolithic-way](http://www.pasthorizonspr.com/index.php/archives/01/2014/opening-oysters-shells-the-mesolithic-way)

### HoneyChild — 2014-01-29T13:42:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/opening-oyster-shells-the-mesolithic-way.9489/post-107296>

I remember watching a wildlife documentary where some monkeys had worked out how to open shellfish. They basically just hit the shells against a tree and eventually the mollusc got tired and the shell opened.

Although not all of the monkeys had the patience to do this.

I saw this quite a few years ago and can't remember what kind of monkeys they were or even what country they were in.

# Thread: some labs from 2 yrs ago and some clues perhaps
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-labs-from-2-yrs-ago-and-some-clues-perhaps.9501/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sandra — 2014-01-30T04:51:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/some-labs-from-2-yrs-ago-and-some-clues-perhaps.9501/post-107412>

I dug out some old labs from 2 years ago, pre-Paleo, still eating sugar and grains.

I can't work out how to format this any better. The figures in brackets are the ranges they said the results should be in. BOLD numbers are where they were not in the presented range.

Everything is in range except for HDL, and Iron Levels (TIBC and Ferritin). A follow-up iron test confirmed no loss via stool, so they wanted to give me supplements, and I said I wanted to try with diet. I didn't go back to test until last week - so still waiting on those results, but still feeling all the same symptoms of the tiredness, cold hands and feet, and just a bit under the weather.

11-Nov-11
aged 36
not preg

BLOOD COUNT

Haemoglobin............ 133 g/L (115-165)
Haematocrit............ 0.39 (0.35-0.47)
RCC ........................4.2 10\*12/L (3.9-5.6)
MCV ........................94 fL (80-100)

WCC ........................4.7 10\*9/L (3.5-12)
Neutrophils ................2.22 10\*9/L (1.5-8)
Lymphocytes............ 2 10\*9/L (1.0-4.0)
Monocytes ................0.35 10\*9/L (0-0.9)
Eosinophils ................0.06 10\*9/L (0-0.6)
Basophils ................0.06 10\*9/L (0-0.15)

Platelets ................206 10\*9/L (150-400)

CHOLESTEROL

Chol 5.3 mmol/L (3.9-5.5)
Trigs 1.3 mmol/L (0.6-2.0)
HDL **2.1** mmol/L (1.1-1.9)
LDL 2.6 mmol/L (0.0-4.0)
Tot Chol/HDL 2.5 (0-4.5)

THYROID FUNTION

TSH 1.6 mU/L (0.3-3.5)

ANAEMIA

Iron 19 umol/L (5-30)
TIBC **84** umol/L (45-72)
Trans Sat 23 % (20-55)
Ferritin **20** (30-150)

OCCULT BLOOD in reference to above iron deficiency
Occult Blood 1 negative
Occult Blood 2 negative
Occult Blood 3 negative

ROUTINE CHEMISTRY 11 Nov 2011, aged 36, not pregnant

Sodium 138 mmol/L 135-145
Potassium 4.4 mmol/L 3.5-5.5
Chloride 102 mmol/L 95-110
Bicarbonate 24 mmol/L 20-32
Anion Gap 12 mmol/L 5-15
Ca (Corr) 2.32 mmol/L 2.1-2.55
Phosphate 1.2 mmol/L 0.8-1.5
Urea 4.7 mmol/L 2.5-7
Urate 0.3 mmol/L 0.15-0.4
Creatinine 66 mmol/L 45-85
eGFR 88 mmol/L >59
Fast Glucose 4.8 mmol/L 3.6-6.0
Total Protein 69 g/L 64-81
Albumin 42 g/L 37-48
Globulin 27 g/L 20-39
T Bilirubin 8 umol/L 3-15
ALP 42 U/L 20-105
AST 20 U/L 10-35
ALT 20 U/L 5-30
GGT 7 U/L 5-35
LDH 151 U/L 120-250
Cholesterol 5.3 mmol/L 3.9-5.5
Triglyceride 1.3 mmol/L 0.6-2.0
Iron 19 umol/L 5-30
Haemolysis Index 2 0-40

They said the HDL was nothing to worry about, but the iron was a concern. So, if I am getting a lot of iron in my diet, where is it going, and why is it not being absorbed - gut issues??? I am loathe to just take supplements - I'd like to fix it.

# Thread: Hadza gut microbiome
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hadza-gut-microbiome.9503/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-01-30T09:07:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hadza-gut-microbiome.9503/post-107423>

> Anthropologist Jeff Leach hopes to test \[theories of disease\] by studying the microbiota of the Hadza, a community of hunter-gatherers in northern Tanzania whose lifestyle resembles that of humanity before the advent of agriculture. Leach, who's spending 2 years among the Hadza, also plans to adopt their lifestyle for a while to find out how it will change the microbes living in and on his own body. The first results of the study are expected later this year.

[http://news.sciencemag.org/biology/2014/01/slideshow-secrets-inside-guts-africas-hunter-gatherers](http://news.sciencemag.org/biology/2014/01/slideshow-secrets-inside-guts-africas-hunter-gatherers)

Here's his blog (which has a lot more):

[http://humanfoodproject.com/going-f...cquire-healthiest-gut-microbiome-world-heard/](http://humanfoodproject.com/going-feral-one-year-journey-acquire-healthiest-gut-microbiome-world-heard/)

### HoneyChild — 2014-01-30T14:58:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hadza-gut-microbiome.9503/post-107438>

But how much will the change in microbes be related to the diet vs lifestyle? This aspect usually gets overlooked, esp in the Paleo diet community.

### Gagnrad — 2014-01-30T15:50:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hadza-gut-microbiome.9503/post-107447>

Leach says in the blog post linked above:

> Excitingly, our work with the Hadzabe hunter-gatherers in Tanzania will allow us to compare our western selves to people who still hunt and gather the majority of their food, have limited access to western medications, are all born naturally and breastfed for 2+ years, live outside more or less 24/7, are covered in microbial-laden soil (natures blanket), and that have an intimate connection to a vast (natural) microbial world that we in the so-called developed world have moved away from.

I guess you could say that, in this phase of the experiment, he isn't trying to isolate any one factor -- if for no other reason than because it's not feasible to even try.

I suppose a lot of people in the Paleosphere think health is pretty much all about diet not just as regards the gut but in general. But you will find mentions of the downside of antibiotics and over-cleanliness (and stuff on movement, light, and a lot of other things) from people like Mark Sisson and Frank Forencich.

I think I heard Jack say recently on someone's podcast that he estimates diet as worth only 10% and other factors -- sleep, light and circadian rhythms; temperature; and EMF radiation exposure -- as worth 90%.

### Josh — 2014-02-01T22:48:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hadza-gut-microbiome.9503/post-107774>

If they do not include the larger helminths and their role in the microbiome, they are missing a lot. This is from personal experience. They have multiple effects at multiple trophic levels in the microbiota and interact in many ways with our own system doing the work of what might require 5-10 medications. There are costs and benefits and a key seems to be the number of larger helminths one carries. I doubt any human was helminth free before the 1930's. Consider the soil microbiome and the roles played by nematodes and worms. What is not seen with worms is how their secretions and signals influence other organisms and the immune system. Current probiotic research is like taking the soil out of your garden and throwing away anything you can see with the naked eye as unimportant.

# Thread: Ketogenic Mediterranean Diet
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketogenic-mediterranean-diet.9507/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-01-30T16:33:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketogenic-mediterranean-diet.9507/post-107456>

I stumbled on this looking for something else. I haven't had much of a look yet.

The site seems to be run by a doctor. But I'm not sure about some of this. For example:

> Very-low-carb ketogenic diets may have the potential to cause osteoporosis (thin, brittle bones), kidney stones, low blood pressure, constipation, gout, high uric acid in the blood, excessive loss of sodium and potassium in the urine, worsening of kidney disease, deficiency of calcium and vitamins A, B, C, and D, among other adverse effects.

I think "may" is acting as a weasel-word here. I mean that in the original sense of "weasel word" -- i.e., a word that sucks meaning out of another word -- so-called because of the line from the Shakspeare play:

> I can suck melancholy out of a song as a weasel sucks eggs.

So in that sentence "may" is sucking the meaning out of "potential", so that the sentence appears to be saying more than it really is. But have they this "potential" or not? Have one or more of the effects ever been observed? Where are the references?

And what does "among other adverse effects" mean, for goodness' sake? Say what they are, rather than hinting darkly! And is he saying these _unnamed_ adverse effects will occur or are these also something that _may_ be potential. IOW, things someone's said will happen with, perhaps, no evidence at all.

It's shoddy writing.

However, I think the idea of ketogenic, and low-carb, Mediterranean diets is an interesting one. After all "Mediterranean" usually indicates much use of seafood, fresh produce, olive oil, and some red wine. Take the excess carbs out and that could be a good diet.

So here's the link:

[http://advancedmediterranean.com/category/ketogenic-mediterranean-diet/](http://advancedmediterranean.com/category/ketogenic-mediterranean-diet/)

### caroline — 2014-01-30T22:42:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketogenic-mediterranean-diet.9507/post-107488>

Isn't this really dated thinking?

I am doing so well on VLCD and now with adding in much more fat and less protein - everything is falling into place ... my Doc couldn't be happier with my numbers.....

### Gagnrad — 2014-01-30T23:26:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketogenic-mediterranean-diet.9507/post-107496>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=107488)
>
> Isn't this really dated thinking?

What, Audrey? The bit I was complaining about? Yeah, I guess so … I'm not even sure how much "thinking" ever went into it.

What was in my mind was that a low-carb version of Mediterranean would really be pretty good. Mediterranean is, after all, all about high seafood and fresh produce. Take the grains and legumes out and it would pretty much be Epi-paleo. That's why I linked it. He might at any rate have some good recipes somewhere on that site.

> I am doing so well on VLCD and now with adding in much more fat and less protein - everything is falling into place ... my Doc couldn't be happier with my numbers.....

That's good.

I don't usually crunch numbers on these things, but I did record my day's food today. According to FitDay, I ate 2051 Kcal (74% fat, 18% protein, 8% carbs) today. I guess that's VLC. I'm surprised it didn't come to much in the way of calories: that was a large and satisfying breakfast, and a big salad with some salmon at lunch. For dinner I had some smoked duck, a load of boiled curly kale with plenty of ghee melted over it, a cup of broth, a big lump of pork rilletes, and a handful of macadamias. Not exactly starving oneself is it, yet it doesn't add up to much in energetic terms? I don't think a couple of thousand calories is very much for a 6-foot man, but I'm not at all hungry. I never ever am. I can't really remember what it's like to feel so.

Am I in ketosis? I suppose so. I tend to come pretty far to the right on the ketostix, but the meters don't seem to be around this side of the Atlantic, and I know the ketostix are not accurate. I'd guess I'm only _mildly_ in ketosis. But I'd prefer to eat in a more Paleo style than in a hardcore ketogenic style. The stuff Peter at Hyperlipid eats, for example, doesn't look like ordinary meals, and even he says he "drifts in and out" of ketosis. I don't want to push my protein down to 60 grams a day and start skipping a meal, or a day's meals, in addition -- which is what I guess anyone would have to do to get really deeply into ketosis.

### caroline — 2014-01-30T23:43:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketogenic-mediterranean-diet.9507/post-107502>

I used to not get into ketosis very easily - haven't measured in a long time..... can't find my ketostix! I am not eating very much in the way of carbs - I seem to have a thing for carrots tho!

I am not keeping track at all any more really ... my body seems to be functioning so much better with all the fat .... who would have thought? Dr. K. of course!

I am guessing that I am in Ketosis pretty good - I am definitely on a high. I love it! I seem to be attracting positive people like a magnet. It is so much fun every day .... what an adventure!

It sounds like you have your diet nailed ...... It feels great doesn't it?

I see what you mean about the med diet ..... are you eating seafood every day?

### Gagnrad — 2014-01-31T08:20:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketogenic-mediterranean-diet.9507/post-107557>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=107502)
>
> ..... are you eating seafood every day?

Pretty much, if it's only canned sardines. Often twice a day. Remembering Dr. K's nutrient demonstrations, I include shellfish, too … when I can get them. I had oysters a couple of times a while ago, but I haven't seen any in the fishmonger's for some time.

I suppose you'd get a really good choice of seafood in Australia.

### caroline — 2014-01-31T09:49:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ketogenic-mediterranean-diet.9507/post-107563>

I didn't. It sounds good.

Actually, Dr. K puts out so much stuff, I don't suppose I've seen the half of it. Plus he's scared me so much over sleep  (even more than the authors of _Lights Out_ had done) that I don't tend to watch video material in the evening, which is my only really free time.

I may have to buy his book.

# Thread: Cyclical ketogenic diets
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cyclical-ketogenic-diets.9523/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jason — 2014-01-31T16:29:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cyclical-ketogenic-diets.9523/post-107582>

Hi, everyone. This is my first post! Wanted to say I've found the blog to be great. I love the way it challenges my current line of thinking and everything follows in a very logical fashion. Currently, I've read through the leptin and CT series and I'm in the middle of [EMF 4](http://www.jackkruse.com/emf-4-why-might-you-need-carbs-for-performance/).

As I'm reading through EMF 4, it brought to mind cyclical ketogenic diets. I've done them off and on over the past two years and found they work quite well. Usually, my pattern for this kind of diet is to do 6-7 days of 30 grams of net carbs a day or less, followed by a large carb refeed on the 7th day that usually starts around mid afternoon and ends about half an hour after sunset. I don't count carbs but would imagine they run ~500 grams. They're mostly high glycemic so that the insulin spike has subsided by the time I need to go to sleep.

However, now that I've read the CT series and it's been cold where I am (southern Texas), I've done much longer periods of keto and feel great. I've also paired that with the beginning of the CT protocol and have graduated from face dunking to ice packs on a compression shirt.

This brings me to three questions:

1\. When the temperature fluctuates from cold to warm in the middle of a season like winter, do you adjust carb intake? For example, for the past week it's been consistently below 50 degrees all day, every day. However, today the low is only 59 and the high is 74. Tomorrow it will be even warmer.

2\. Do large carb reeds (like I described in my second paragraph) have any real place in the epi-Paleo diet? In Dr. K's [intermittent fasting and leptin blog](http://www.jackkruse.com/intermittent-fasting-and-leptin/) he seems to indicate that if you're leptin sensitive and working out (I lift weights 2-3 times a week), AMPk allows us to be very insulin sensitive and burn glucose for energy, however EMF 4 (which I'm still reading) indicates it's a poor way of recylcing ATP.

3\. Is it possible to "slingshot" between the [pentose phosphate pathway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pentose_phosphate_pathway) (PPP) mentioned in EMF 4 and the creatine phosphate system and Krebs cycle? For example, you're running on the PPP throughout the week, but do your one large carb refeed, tap into those other pathways during the refeed and in the next day or two you're back in the PPP?

### Cynthia Holmes — 2019-02-13T19:41:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cyclical-ketogenic-diets.9523/post-263104>

I just read your 2014 post on cyclical ketosis. Did anyone ever respond to your excellent questions? I’ve been on a ketogenic diet (without cycling in a higher carb day) for about a year and a half. At first I felt fantastic and quickly lost 35 pounds which I have kept off with no difficulty. But now I’m starting to feel sort of crappy, mostly emotionally. I can’t seem to get out of blue periods in which my ability to follow through with desired projects is compromised. In your experience, does cycling in a carb day help with this? I’ll probably just give it a try, but I’d like to understand a little of the science here. I read somewhere, maybe a Mercola post, that staying in ketosis long term may cause thyroid problems. The post didn’t specify what kind of problems but I’m guessing hypothyroidism: do you know? Grateful to hear anything about your experience.

> [Jason said:](/goto/post?id=107582)
>
> Hi, everyone. This is my first post! Wanted to say I've found the blog to be great. I love the way it challenges my current line of thinking and everything follows in a very logical fashion. Currently, I've read through the leptin and CT series and I'm in the middle of [EMF 4](http://www.jackkruse.com/emf-4-why-might-you-need-carbs-for-performance/).

Usually, my pattern for this kind of diet is to do 6-7 days of 30 grams of net carbs a day or less, followed by a large carb refeed on the 7th day that usually starts around mid afternoon and ends about half an hour after sunset. I don't count carbs but would imagine they run ~500 grams. They're mostly high glycemic so that the insulin spike has subsided by the time I need to go to sleep.
>
> However, now that I've read the CT series and it's been cold where I am (southern Texas), I've done much longer periods of keto and feel great. I've also paired that with the beginning of the CT protocol and have graduated from face dunking to ice packs on a compression shirt.
>
> This brings me to three questions:
>
> 1\. When the temperature fluctuates from cold to warm in the middle of a season like winter, do you adjust carb intake? For example, for the past week it's been consistently below 50 degrees all day, every day. However, today the low is only 59 and the high is 74. Tomorrow it will be even warmer.
>
> 2\. Do large carb reeds (like I described in my second paragraph) have any real place in the epi-Paleo diet? In Dr. K's [intermittent fasting and leptin blog](http://www.jackkruse.com/intermittent-fasting-and-leptin/) he seems to indicate that if you're leptin sensitive and working out (I lift weights 2-3 times a week), AMPk allows us to be very insulin sensitive and burn glucose for energy, however EMF 4 (which I'm still reading) indicates it's a poor way of recylcing ATP.
>
> 3\. Is it possible to "slingshot" between the [pentose phosphate pathway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pentose_phosphate_pathway) (PPP) mentioned in EMF 4 and the creatine phosphate system and Krebs cycle? For example, you're running on the PPP throughout the week, but do your one large carb refeed, tap into those other pathways during the refeed and in the next day or two you're back in the PPP?

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=\_pVbOuWLxVY](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_pVbOuWLxVY)

Luc Montagnier whom I last met when "And the Band Played On" was published back in the bad old days with ACT UP and everyone, who'd a thunk it....?

### Josh — 2014-02-01T22:41:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/really-enjoyed-this-answers-many-questions-for-me.9531/post-107773>

This suggests, as I suspect that we do not have any definitive technology for testing for the presence of infection. It does not mean that ELISA, PCR, stool tests, etc. are not useful, just that they are not definitive. This puts it back to the classic terrain or as Dr. K calls it "terroir". There are different names in different traditional systems. Pathogens have always been lumped into the category of the effect they are part of. Examples in Oriental Medicine include "hot", "cold" and "phlegm" that cause things such as "stagnation", deficiency, excess, etc.. Translation is never perfect and there are two groups fighting over the translated terms in Oriental Medicine. So much may be taken into consideration at a theoretical level that it makes what we are struggling with look not too bad. However, to actually practice it in a given context with a given individual is a different exercise. I have always felt that the root of the practice is to maintain as good of a resonant balance as possible based on the limits of the system one is working with. I have seen extraordinary examples of beings who can enhance their resonance or maintain it at a high level regularly. I am seeing the concepts behind why it is possible in the water and energy stuff. One of my Qi Gong teachers would have most beginning students stop standing in a high horse stance after 15-30 seconds because he said that was their limit to increase their vibration or resonance and if they exceeded it, they would lose rather than gain resonance. He could stand in a deep squat with his legs at 90 degrees and no muscular tension indefinitely.

The thing I wanted to ask is what I started to see about my skin. I never suffered by acne and after many months on keto epipaleo my skin looks just amazing, but I started to have acne on my upper back and now to skipped to neck and the skin on my face gets reddish and rashy time to time (must be connected with ct). I assume it is high estrogen sign, but not sure this would be something expecting. thanks!

I really look great and everyone is asking what the hell I do, i even mangaed to put on some weight where a woman would expect and I started to build again muscles. # Thread: Too big for my tub
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-big-for-my-tub.9546/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AnnieW — 2014-02-02T02:42:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-big-for-my-tub.9546/post-107837>

So I tried to get in the tub this evening and I couldn't fit. I'd like to think the tub runs small like most of my pants, haha. Actually it is small in that it's not very high. It's not even a foot deep. Anyway, will doing spot CT have the same benefits even though I can't do total submersion? I can definitely do cold showers and bags of ice for spot CT until I shrink enough to fit in the tub.

### caroline — 2014-02-02T02:49:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-big-for-my-tub.9546/post-107841>

Annie - take bags of ice in the tub and put on your torso and neck - that is what I do. Maybe consider an ice vest?

Are you taking part in the free 90 optimal reset? there is a FB group....

### AnnieW — 2014-02-02T03:56:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-big-for-my-tub.9546/post-107853>

So no water, just ice? Will the CT benefit my fat legs if the ice is only on my torso? I'm ok with cool showers and face dunks but I do need a thermometer to make sure I'm getting it low enough. How about just standing outside for a little while? I'd thought about leveraging our current weather right now in lieu of buying ice. Maybe that could help shrink things up a bit too.

I sure am taking part! That's why I attempted to shove my big ass in the tub. Should've brought a can of cooking spray into the bathroom. Lol!

### caroline — 2014-02-02T04:44:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-big-for-my-tub.9546/post-107857>

> [AnnieW said:](/goto/post?id=107853)
>
> So no water, just ice? Will the CT benefit my fat legs if the ice is only on my torso? I'm ok with cool showers and face dunks but I do need a thermometer to make sure I'm getting it low enough. How about just standing outside for a little while? I'd thought about leveraging our current weather right now in lieu of buying ice. Maybe that could help shrink things up a bit too.
>
> I sure am taking part! That's why I attempted to shove my big ass in the tub. Should've brought a can of cooking spray into the bathroom. Lol!

You are too cute!! LOL

No, no, no ...you need water! I freeze big bags of ice and then cover myself with them!

Where are you? do you have snow? what is the temp of your tap water? You don't need ice if your water is cold enough.

do it all! get outside in a bikini, Don't worry about a thermometer until you have a chance to get one. I got mine on ebay for under $5.00
You just have to go for it girl!

### Lahelada — 2014-02-02T10:50:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-big-for-my-tub.9546/post-107871>

What Caroline says is correct. However Dr Kruse does soemthing else and that is expose parts of his body to heat and the other half to cold using Fournier's law.
That may also be an option for you if you can use your tub safely. If not ice packs and cold showers are just fine. Be as cold as you dare as much time as you can . If you are in winter use the free cold as Caroline said.

[http://www.jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/](http://www.jackkruse.com/the-evolution-of-the-leptin-rx/)

### AnnieW — 2014-02-02T14:08:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-big-for-my-tub.9546/post-107888>

I'm in VA and we had some snow but since we've had some warmer days it's melting. It's going to get cold again soon though. Tap water is cold but I'm not sure how cold.

So I can do spot CT with ice and hang around outside and still receive the benefit without the tub for right now? I could try to get in there again but it is extremely uncomfortable being 6' tall and weighing 350. I might be able to get an inch of water in. A bikini? Holy balls Caroline you've lost it!

### nicld — 2014-02-02T18:37:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-big-for-my-tub.9546/post-107928>

Some members here have bought a stock tank and set that outside for CT. Is that an option where you live? Come spring that is what I am going to do when out tank actually unthaws from the ground.

Nicole

### caroline — 2014-02-02T22:34:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-big-for-my-tub.9546/post-107976>

Annie - do what you can do .... just start and keep working it! Maybe consider an icevest?

I have been known to lose it the odd time ...

### AnnieW — 2014-02-03T03:36:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/too-big-for-my-tub.9546/post-108038>

Unfortunately I can't do a stock tank. I'm going to look into the ice vest. Thanks for the help guys!

# Thread: Calcium RDA figure must be wrong
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calcium-rda-figure-must-be-wrong.9558/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gagnrad — 2014-02-02T20:47:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calcium-rda-figure-must-be-wrong.9558/post-107960>

Where did they get it from? This figure _has_ to be a totally broken suggestion.

I know that Professor Cordain has said so for some time.

One wonders if they originally thought something like "bones … ah, ha, _calcium_ and almost plucked a figure out of the air. And suggested one _independent of_ recommendations for D or K2 (admittedly, that hadn't be isolated yet, and it seems doctors in the West have still to catch up with the Japanese in realising its therapeutic value. Another ten to fifteen years?)

This seems to be one area where Weston Price was wrong -- a rare case where he was misled by the assumptions of the day, perhaps. Price seems to be basing his statements on what a guy called Sherman had said:

> The normal adult needs to receive from the foods eaten one-half to one gram of calcium or lime per day

[http://journeytoforever.org/farm\_library/price/price15.html](http://journeytoforever.org/farm_library/price/price15.html)

This is referenced to **SHERMAN, H. C. Chemistry of Foods and Nutrition. New York, Macmillan, 1933**.

I have a strong suspicion that once this figure had become imbedded in the textbooks, the USDA would have been happy to run with it, since you then "need" a fourth "food group" -- namely, dairy products -- it being difficult to get up to this levels without such and you thereby cater to the dairy industry. Hasn't it been said that the "four food groups" actually represent the "four food lobbies"?

I'm not dead against dairy -- I'd certainly be happy to eat a piece of goat's or ewe's milk cheese two or three times a week, although I've gone strict Paleo right now, since I have some minor skin issues and wonder whether they're connected to dairy produce. But the "recommended" three or four glasses of milk a day, or whatever it currently is? **I just don't buy it**. In fact I wonder if that much calcium, in the absence of sufficient K2, is actually dangerous.

Price seems to have made measurements of various minerals in the diets of the isolated populations that he visited. On the same page I already linked (Chapter 15: Characteristics of primitive and modernised dietaries) he asserts:

> In other words the foods of the native Eskimos contained 5.4 times as much calcium as the displacing foods of the white man, five times as much phosphorus, 1.5 times as much iron, 7.9 times as much magnesium, 1.8 times as much copper, 49.0 times as much iodine, and at least ten times that number of fat-soluble vitamins. …
>
> The ratio in the Swiss native diets to that in the displacing diet was for calcium, 3.7 fold; for phosphorus, 2.2 fold; for magnesium, 2.5 fold; for iron, 3.1 fold; and for the fat-soluble activators, at least ten fold. …
>
> The native diet of the New Zealand Maori provided an increase in the native foods over the displacing foods of the modernized whites of 6.2 fold for calcium, 6.9 fold for phosphorus, 23.4 fold for magnesium, 58.3 fold for iron; and the fatsoluble activators were increased at least ten fold. ...

The trouble here is that he is not giving absolute figures. These figures are based on so many times what's in the "displacing foods"

It's curious that the Swiss, who seem to have eaten plenty of cheese are stated to be taking in much less than the Eskimos or the Maori.

Further up the page the "typical American dietary" of Price's day is stated to:

> have a calcium intake ranging from 0.3 to 0.5 gram

If the "displacing foods" are supposed to contain 0.3 g of Ca, then the Maori, for example, were supposedly getting 0.3 x 6.2 = 1.86 g per day; the Swiss, 0.3 x 3.7 = 1.11 g.

I'm deep suspicious of these figures. I can't help wondering if Price, or his lab assistants, botched their assays.

I just don't believe that one can get a whopping great calcium intake on a Paleo diet. I crunched the numbers on what I had today just to see.

Breakfast: Manx cure kipper, 2 cups steamed Swiss chard served with a tbs olive oil, 1 avocado, coffee

Lunch: 4 oz tuna steak fried in olive oil, 2 oz crabmeat, steamed leeks and chicory with olive oil, 1 oz walnuts, 20 g 90% chocolate, one 4 fl oz glass Argentinian Malbec, 1 cup coffee

Dinner: 1 tsp cod liver oil, 1 tsp coconut oil, 2 oz cooked bacon, 1 duck egg fried in duck fat, 2 cups savoy cabbage served with 1 tsp ghee, and 1 oz desiccated coconut (as a pudding)

Now FitDay tells me that that lot is 2195 kcal (68% fat, 19% protein, 9% carbs, 4% alcohol).

Here's the thing: I take the RDAs with a grain of salt and don't worry about them, but FWIW this hits or exceeds the RDA for everything (OK, it says 93% for vitamin E and 85% for Potassium, but that's near enough), for everything, i say, except calcium. According to FitDay I only had 30% of the RDA for calcium.

Well, I'm sorry, the figure must be wrong.

### nonchalant — 2014-02-02T23:21:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calcium-rda-figure-must-be-wrong.9558/post-107990>

All that RDA stuff is from such old research. I imagine the true numbers for everything now is quite different, and also different if you are cold-adapted and eating animal meat/fat. And I imagine the number of "essential nutrients" is larger now, with all the EMF keeping our systems from optimally synthesizing/recycling amino acids, proteins, vitamins and such.

### caroline — 2014-02-02T23:25:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/calcium-rda-figure-must-be-wrong.9558/post-107992>

I love the sounds of what you had to eat!!


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

### Gagnrad — 2014-02-04T10:29:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-bake-your-bacon.9560/post-108271>

Golly, that would be low. That would be about 90 C. A "low oven" usually means something in the range 140 C to 170 C. Less than 100 would be the right at the bottom of the dial just a notch or two above plate-warming temperature.

### Dali Dula — 2014-02-04T15:06:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-bake-your-bacon.9560/post-108276>

Below 165 f is not going to cook. I bake bacon at 225 and it takes like an hour. It will not crisp at low temp. I prefer non crispy bacon. I finish it under broiler for DW who likes crispy.

### Gagnrad — 2014-02-04T15:52:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-bake-your-bacon.9560/post-108278>

[http://www.jamieoliver.com/recipes/pork-recipes/pork-belly-roast](http://www.jamieoliver.com/recipes/pork-recipes/pork-belly-roast)

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=108415)

That looks soooooo good - I am really hungry right now!

### Gagnrad — 2014-02-05T10:30:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-bake-your-bacon.9560/post-108437>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=108415)
>
> Thanks .....Have you tried it?

No. I know someone who used to cook a pork joint rather like that, though. She followed a recipe that said to score the skin like that, to rub it with oilve oil, and to start it at high temperature, then turn it down. It did work inasmuch as it did result in crisp crackling. Unfortunately, the recipe also said to poke garlic cloves in. Bad idea. Perhaps you were supposed to make deeper holes for those, but at any rate I guess they'd be stil be a bit too close to the surface. The garlic used to burn, which makes it taste bitter and smell nasty.

Did you ever read Anthony Bourdain's _Kitchen Confidential,_ Audrey?

[http://www.amazon.com/Kitchen-Confidential-Anthony-Bourdain/dp/1408845040/](http://www.amazon.com/Kitchen-Confidential-Anthony-Bourdain/dp/1408845040/)

(One you'd very much enjoy, I'd think.)

Anyway, Bourdain said as a French-trained chef he could always smell a Chinese restaurant streets away -- because they burn the garlic (since they cook it at high temperature in stir-fries) which is an absolute no-no in French cooking.

Bourdain's pretty Epi-Paleo himself in a way. A big seafood man. He said the best meal he ever had was in Japan -- course after course of really high-quality seafood, much of it raw.

### caroline — 2014-02-05T10:45:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-bake-your-bacon.9560/post-108439>

I don't think I have read that one ........

I guess we should have a meet and great in Japan! whaddayathink???

### nonchalant — 2014-02-05T16:53:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-bake-your-bacon.9560/post-108473>

I think 200F is what Jack means. I'm gently frying some bacon right now. It barely makes any noise, and yes, its a process that takes hours. I occasionally get up and turn it, and when some pieces start shrinking, I remove them and add more fresh bacon. I nibble all morning. Plz don't tell Jack! I got my laser CT thermometer and measured the temps. They range from 165F to 210F in various areas of the cast iron frying pan.

I suppose the correct way would be to save up all the cooked bacon and eat it for lunch, but I can never resist it.

### nonchalant — 2014-02-05T16:57:19+0200

### LieselK — 2018-02-22T20:05:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-bake-your-bacon.9560/post-235418>

This blew my mind.

Looks like I'll be baking the bacon while everyone else is gone from the house for the day... bwahahahaha... more (all) for me.

### drezy — 2018-02-22T20:42:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-bake-your-bacon.9560/post-235431>

> [LieselK said:](/goto/post?id=235418)
>
> This blew my mind.
>
> Looks like I'll be baking the bacon while everyone else is gone from the house for the day... bwahahahaha... more (all) for me.

Everyone knows that when it comes to bacon the question:

"Do you want the last piece?"

Is really:

# Thread: Smartphone Use at Night May Lower Productivity
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smartphone-use-at-night-may-lower-productivity.9566/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ATL_Paleo — 2014-02-03T00:40:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smartphone-use-at-night-may-lower-productivity.9566/post-108018>

[http://psychcentral.com/news/2014/01/23/smartphone-use-at-night-may-lower-productivity/64892.html](http://psychcentral.com/news/2014/01/23/smartphone-use-at-night-may-lower-productivity/64892.html)

The ubiquitous smartphone may ironically be counterproductive to work efficiency as a new study suggests use of the device for late night work lowers next day productivity.

In a pair of studies over a broad spectrum of U.S. workers, Michigan State Universityâ€™s Russell Johnson, Ph.D., and colleagues found that people who monitored their smart phones for business purposes after 9 p.m. were more tired and were less engaged the following day on the job.

â€œSmartphones are almost perfectly designed to disrupt sleep,â€￾ said Johnson â€” who acknowledges keeping his own smartphone at his bedside at night.

â€œBecause they keep us mentally engaged late into the evening, they make it hard to detach from work so we can relax and fall asleep.â€￾

### Hope — 2014-02-03T00:57:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smartphone-use-at-night-may-lower-productivity.9566/post-108021>

THanks for sharing!!!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smartphone-use-at-night-may-lower-productivity.9566/post-108022>

### nicld — 2014-02-03T22:41:49+0200

But just do it, it will help, trust me. Do you use a digital thermometer and how long do you leave it in? I have found that if I check the reading just after it beeps then leave it is for another minute I get two different temps.

I think I remember Jack saying that a lower body temp is not too bad but I cannot recall if he said how low.

### freesia — 2014-02-03T23:11:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/300-hypothyroid-symptoms.9575/post-108177>

> [Tanya said:](/goto/post?id=108161)
>
> I can relate to that. My temp. rarely goes above 95 F. Kind of hard to do CT with that, but knowing how important it is, I just have to do it...

I started with a really low basal temp and frequent blue fingernails and cold extremities in response to even a minor cold temperature change. The key is not how hard and fast you go but consistency.....always just a little colder or longer...even if you start with a 70 F bath.

And if you have a difficult warm up after one session then the progress you should be looking for the next day is NOT a further reduction in temp or duration - you'll put yourself off that way by making it too hard - but a slightly easier warm up. Pace yourself based on _your_ own responses - not someone else's - so you fall forward gently.

I switched to the digital thermometer a while back for convenience and it is close enough with all the previous readings that I was getting with the old one. Thank you for your suggestion - I will leave it on a little longer after it beeps tomorrow and see if it would make any difference.

### Tanya — 2014-02-04T16:57:58+0200

# Thread: Any techies out there?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-techies-out-there.9576/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### K123 — 2014-02-03T17:44:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-techies-out-there.9576/post-108117>

Hi guys
I want to specifically talk to people who's work involves working on computers all day long. I would like to learn how you balance your occupation with your health. I have bought blue blockers now so I use them in the evenings. My husband refuses to switch the wi-fi off because first he is not convinced about the impact of EMF on our health and secondly we live in flats and we get wi-fi from all the neighbouring flats and he doesn't think switching off ours it going to help in any way. Would love to learn from your experience.

### sooperb — 2014-02-03T18:54:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-techies-out-there.9576/post-108130>

I spend a lot of time at my computer. I bought an earthing mat that I use with my bare feet.

### Albert83BCN — 2014-02-03T19:12:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-techies-out-there.9576/post-108131>

I am a software engineer, blogger, smartphone addict and almost all-things IT related, and I spend almost all day long in front of the PC, bad bad. So far, I've not implemented many changes in that regard, just installed f.lux although I don't use it very often because honestly, it's a little bit unstable and once it crashed I forget to run it again, I don't believe it will be so effective as true blue light blockers, which I always swear I'll buy but in the end I forget...  I also have some supps for mitochondrial support as I'm also an endurance runner... so lots of stress to my body, and drink a lot of non fluoridated water in the hope of getting rid of the terror

I'm pretty sure I'll implement some more of the EMF Rx tips any time sooner or later, by the mean time will be interested to hear what more techies have to say

### nicld — 2014-02-03T20:12:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-techies-out-there.9576/post-108141>

> [K123 said:](/goto/post?id=108117)
>
> Hi guys
> I want to specifically talk to people who's work involves working on computers all day long. I would like to learn how you balance your occupation with your health. I have bought blue blockers now so I use them in the evenings. My husband refuses to switch the wi-fi off because first he is not convinced about the impact of EMF on our health and secondly we live in flats and we get wi-fi from all the neighbouring flats and he doesn't think switching off ours it going to help in any way. Would love to learn from your experience.

I have grounded both my work laptop and home laptop, turned off Wi-Fi at home (can't at work except on my computer) and I have 250# pull magnets under my desk and where I sit at home. We also live in the country with all buried powerlines so that helps.

The other thing I do is lots of cold cold CT. I try for 45-50 minutes 2X during the week and 4-5 hours on the weekends. Like Jack, I really like my job so I need to do what I can to offset the non-native EMF's that I get at work.

Next step might be to get a magnetico sleep pad. I got one for the youngest daughter but none for us yet since I need a king ($$$$$$). Blueblockers will help as well as trying to turn the lights down/off at night (hubby won't turn everything off).

Unplug your at night and keep the phones out of the bedroom. Every little bit should help. Just ask him to explain why people are getting sicker and fatter then 100 years ago and see if he can connect the dots. Technology has developed so rapidly that a human has not been able to adapt genetically.

I am reading a book called The Story of the Human Body by Daniel Liberman [http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00C8S9VCK/ref=kinw\_myk\_ro\_title](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B00C8S9VCK/ref=kinw_myk_ro_title) and it talks about how the human body has been able to adapt over the centuries to environmental changes but with all this new technology (including new foods) the body cannot adapt quick enough thus why we are getting so sick. Maybe get that book and see if he will read it but I cannot get my husband to read it yet so good luck.

### JoeBranca — 2014-02-03T21:30:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-techies-out-there.9576/post-108160>

I work IT/sysadmin at a university in Irvine, CA. I also have to work remotely on a regular basis. All of this meaning I'm on the computer way too often and work and commute through major EMF soup. Here are details on the items and habits I've implemented so far, based on what I think will help my situation, though not clear yet on which changes are the most or least effective:

At home:

\- I have everyone sleeping on magnetico pads now. My wife and I are on a 10gauss pad (I'll get another 10gauss when the opportunity is right), and our 3 year old sleeps on a 5g pad. Even the dogs sleep on a 5g often. Our water supply also "sleeps" on 5gauss ; )

\- No devices plugged in any of the bedrooms, in fact the circuits feeding most of the bedroom outlets are permanently off.

\- If I wake early enough I try to make time to take the dogs out for a nature hike along a local crest of hills, barefoot and in the morning sun. The preferred spot is where a lot of pine trees are along the path.

\- For post sunset lighting conditions, most light bulbs have been changed out for amber-ish "bug lights" from Walmart, low blue light LEDs, and a couple of himalayan salt lamps. Additionally I wear red blue blocking "safety" glasses made by Dewalt, which does a tremendous job of blocking out most blue as well as green light, though some slips through the sides now and then since they're not completely wrap around.

\- In the past year I eliminated Wi fi from the home and gone back to cat6 cable, also opting out of our smart meter for the extra fee, so there are no longer any RF sources inside the home.

\- 95% of the day I keep my cheap cell phone in airplane mode, and when I do have it on it is outside of the house but in earshot if it rings. All calls are taken outside on speaker phone, and kept short as necessary. No smart phones in the family

\- we've almost completely stopped watching blu rays or Netflix at night time. Our night time activities are more relaxing now and For reading I use a non-backlit Kindle 3 and a red light headlamp for light.

\- When I have to work from home, I try to "batch" my computer tasks around morning early daylight hours or near sunset, but many times I have to work later at night as well. So in addition to the safety glasses and f.lux, I also place a rectangular cutout red film over the laptop screen, the kind used for filtering theatrical lighting. I use USB connected keyboard and mouse to the laptop to keep some distance from it, though it is also a low voltage aluminum cased HP Spectre ultrabook it still picks up significant magnetic field readings near the motherboard. I also ground the laptop with the USB/ground wire concoction mentioned from the EMF webinar.

\- I turn off all circuit breakers at night except for the fridge. In the daytime I also keep the bedroom breakers off since our toddler takes afternoon naps and plays in her bedroom, etc.

At work:

(My office is shared, with fluorescent light, on the second floor of a building with antennas above me and Wi Fi router/repeater right outside in the hallway. There are also classrooms and science labs in the building.)

\- I have one salt lamp and one Citrine & Amethyst crystal "tree" on my desk near the computer, they are also between me and the wi fi router on the other side of the wall:
[http://www.ebay.com/itm/Citrine-Amethyst-Geode-Crystal-Cluster-Wire-Life-Tree-TWIN-SPIRIT-TREE-COOL-/300985264062](http://www.ebay.com/itm/Citrine-Amethyst-Geode-Crystal-Cluster-Wire-Life-Tree-TWIN-SPIRIT-TREE-COOL-/300985264062)
[http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B001892AX2/ref=oh\_details\_o05\_s00\_i00](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B001892AX2/ref=oh_details_o05_s00_i00)

\- I wear a black Tourmaline necklace, and carry an extra chunk of Amethyst crystal in the pocket

\- when I commute in my truck I have the salt lamp, a couple of magnets under the driver's seat, and a 44lb cast iron kettlebell in the back seat.

\- I drink up to 2 ice cold Gerolsteiners during the work day, as well as additional RO water I bring from home. Typically they've been sitting on top of magneticos overnight or longer

\- I do have servers and systems I manage in campus data centers, but like most in our organization I am able to limit my physical presence in those data centers with remote access tools. If I do have to spend a lot of time in the data center for some reason, I try to mitigate by taking extra CoQ10, Mg, glutamine, water, etc. And some relaxation breathing exercises with additional grounding outside when possible.

\- for lunch hours I don't eat lunch because of high fat/protein breakfasts, so I often just get outside and get moving, in the sun, barefoot and shirtless when possible. Either go to a large park off campus with lots of movnat potential, or to one of the local beaches which are only a 10 - 15 minute drive. I'll often try to swim in the cold Pacific Ocean for at least 20 minutes, do some mobility work on the sand or climb beachside cliffs, etc.

The biggest concerns still to address:

\- The smart meter mesh we live in our neighborhood. Though our smart meter is gone, our closest neighbor's meter is only an additional 20 - 30 feet away, and one of the neighborhood collector meters is at the end of the street a little over 100 yards away, likely most of the signals coming from the direction of the other side of our house. I don't have adequate measurement tools or knowledge of the right kind of materials to block /absorb some of this RF we are caught in without doing it wrong and deflecting EMF back at us. I'm considering ponying up the cash for professional consulting. I may also speak with my neighbor soon about the idea of opting out of the smart meter.

\- Our toddler's new bedroom also happens to have the breaker box mounted on the outside corner of the house where that room is. I arranged it so the bed is farthest from that corner, and a large dresser directly in front of where the magnetic field measures strongest. Once the magnetic field reader is moved away from that part of the wall and beyond the dressers, the milligauss readings drop off almost completely. Nevertheless, I am not sure if that's reassuring enough to not worry about trying to relocate the breaker box.

\- Need to test/get consult for dirty electricity in the home, and the integrity of the ground

\- The closest above ground high voltage power lines are 1 mile away from the home, the closest RF mobile tower is 1/2 mile away. I have not done enough measuring or research to know if these are big enough factors to consider moving, let alone the smart meter situation.

\- Eventually I want to work towards a schedule of working 1 day a week from home, but this is assuming the EMF situation is better at home than it is at work, without thorough testing for accurate comparison. I get the sense this is the case based on what I think I know about the EMF sources present in each environment.

### AnnieW — 2014-02-04T04:18:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-techies-out-there.9576/post-108245>

My husband is also uncooperative with my suggestions. He's a PC tech and refuses to turn off the router at night because the one we have is crappy and apparently it needs to be reconfigured any time he plugs it back in. He has a whole Cisco lab across from our bedroom. He also said since we live in a townhouse there is nothing we can do to completely rid ourselves of EMF, and that cell phones are more dangerous. I've basically stopped talking to him about this stuff. I'm thinking about wearing a foil helmet to bed, lol.

### nicld — 2014-02-04T04:52:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-techies-out-there.9576/post-108259>

> [JoeBranca said:](/goto/post?id=108160)
>
> I work IT/sysadmin at a university in Irvine, CA. I also have to work remotely on a regular basis. All of this meaning I'm on the computer way too often and work and commute through major EMF soup. Here are details on the items and habits I've implemented so far, based on what I think will help my situation, though not clear yet on which changes are the most or least effective:
>
> At home:
>
> \- I have everyone sleeping on magnetico pads now. My wife and I are on a 10gauss pad (I'll get another 10gauss when the opportunity is right), and our 3 year old sleeps on a 5g pad. Even the dogs sleep on a 5g often. Our water supply also "sleeps" on 5gauss ; )
>
> \- No devices plugged in any of the bedrooms, in fact the circuits feeding most of the bedroom outlets are permanently off.
>
> \- If I wake early enough I try to make time to take the dogs out for a nature hike along a local crest of hills, barefoot and in the morning sun. The preferred spot is where a lot of pine trees are along the path.
>
> \- For post sunset lighting conditions, most light bulbs have been changed out for amber-ish "bug lights" from Walmart, low blue light LEDs, and a couple of himalayan salt lamps. Additionally I wear red blue blocking "safety" glasses made by Dewalt, which does a tremendous job of blocking out most blue as well as green light, though some slips through the sides now and then since they're not completely wrap around.
>
> \- In the past year I eliminated Wi fi from the home and gone back to cat6 cable, also opting out of our smart meter for the extra fee, so there are no longer any RF sources inside the home.
>
> \- 95% of the day I keep my cheap cell phone in airplane mode, and when I do have it on it is outside of the house but in earshot if it rings. All calls are taken outside on speaker phone, and kept short as necessary. No smart phones in the family
>
> \- we've almost completely stopped watching blu rays or Netflix at night time. Our night time activities are more relaxing now and For reading I use a non-backlit Kindle 3 and a red light headlamp for light.
>
> \- When I have to work from home, I try to "batch" my computer tasks around morning early daylight hours or near sunset, but many times I have to work later at night as well. So in addition to the safety glasses and f.lux, I also place a rectangular cutout red film over the laptop screen, the kind used for filtering theatrical lighting. I use USB connected keyboard and mouse to the laptop to keep some distance from it, though it is also a low voltage aluminum cased HP Spectre ultrabook it still picks up significant magnetic field readings near the motherboard. I also ground the laptop with the USB/ground wire concoction mentioned from the EMF webinar.
>
> \- I turn off all circuit breakers at night except for the fridge. In the daytime I also keep the bedroom breakers off since our toddler takes afternoon naps and plays in her bedroom, etc.
>
>
> At work:
>
> (My office is shared, with fluorescent light, on the second floor of a building with antennas above me and Wi Fi router/repeater right outside in the hallway. There are also classrooms and science labs in the building.)
>
> \- I have one salt lamp and one Citrine & Amethyst crystal "tree" on my desk near the computer, they are also between me and the wi fi router on the other side of the wall:
> [http://www.ebay.com/itm/Citrine-Amethyst-Geode-Crystal-Cluster-Wire-Life-Tree-TWIN-SPIRIT-TREE-COOL-/300985264062](http://www.ebay.com/itm/Citrine-Amethyst-Geode-Crystal-Cluster-Wire-Life-Tree-TWIN-SPIRIT-TREE-COOL-/300985264062)
> [http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B001892AX2/ref=oh\_details\_o05\_s00\_i00](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B001892AX2/ref=oh_details_o05_s00_i00)
>
> \- I wear a black Tourmaline necklace, and carry an extra chunk of Amethyst crystal in the pocket
>
> \- when I commute in my truck I have the salt lamp, a couple of magnets under the driver's seat, and a 44lb cast iron kettlebell in the back seat.
>
> \- I drink up to 2 ice cold Gerolsteiners during the work day, as well as additional RO water I bring from home. Typically they've been sitting on top of magneticos overnight or longer
>
> \- I do have servers and systems I manage in campus data centers, but like most in our organization I am able to limit my physical presence in those data centers with remote access tools. If I do have to spend a lot of time in the data center for some reason, I try to mitigate by taking extra CoQ10, Mg, glutamine, water, etc. And some relaxation breathing exercises with additional grounding outside when possible.
>
> \- for lunch hours I don't eat lunch because of high fat/protein breakfasts, so I often just get outside and get moving, in the sun, barefoot and shirtless when possible. Either go to a large park off campus with lots of movnat potential, or to one of the local beaches which are only a 10 - 15 minute drive. I'll often try to swim in the cold Pacific Ocean for at least 20 minutes, do some mobility work on the sand or climb beachside cliffs, etc.
>
>
> The biggest concerns still to address:
>
> \- The smart meter mesh we live in our neighborhood. Though our smart meter is gone, our closest neighbor's meter is only an additional 20 - 30 feet away, and one of the neighborhood collector meters is at the end of the street a little over 100 yards away, likely most of the signals coming from the direction of the other side of our house. I don't have adequate measurement tools or knowledge of the right kind of materials to block /absorb some of this RF we are caught in without doing it wrong and deflecting EMF back at us. I'm considering ponying up the cash for professional consulting. I may also speak with my neighbor soon about the idea of opting out of the smart meter.
>
> \- Our toddler's new bedroom also happens to have the breaker box mounted on the outside corner of the house where that room is. I arranged it so the bed is farthest from that corner, and a large dresser directly in front of where the magnetic field measures strongest. Once the magnetic field reader is moved away from that part of the wall and beyond the dressers, the milligauss readings drop off almost completely. Nevertheless, I am not sure if that's reassuring enough to not worry about trying to relocate the breaker box.
>
> \- Need to test/get consult for dirty electricity in the home, and the integrity of the ground
>
> \- The closest above ground high voltage power lines are 1 mile away from the home, the closest RF mobile tower is 1/2 mile away. I have not done enough measuring or research to know if these are big enough factors to consider moving, let alone the smart meter situation.
>
> \- Eventually I want to work towards a schedule of working 1 day a week from home, but this is assuming the EMF situation is better at home than it is at work, without thorough testing for accurate comparison. I get the sense this is the case based on what I think I know about the EMF sources present in each environment.

Ok, that is impressive.

Do you feel that the black tourmaline and amethyst help? I have pendants made out of them and I think that the black tourmaline is starting to get cracks in it which I assume it means that is it taking in stuff.

Nicole

One cost about 500 € so they are not cheap. I have seen them on sites for electrosensitive people, there are quite a bit of them here.

### scubasteve — 2014-02-05T04:30:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-techies-out-there.9576/post-108395>

I work nightshift at an IT company and am around massive electrical fields from thousands of computers in a very close proximity. I know..... its not a great situation. I do LOADS of CT, I have an ice vest strapped to me most of the time at home, have been using sunlight for water charge separation post CT when possible, don't wear any warming clothes, just a T shirt and pants in the coldest weather. Drink RO water, eat strictly keto and eat fish eggs daily and eat a pack of sardines plus some oysters a few times a week.
I own a 10g magnetico and schumann resonator,additionally I have purchased a biomat to use post CT, I haven't tested this much yet so I don't vouch for it.

Was grounded at home through a wire running down a window but after renting emf sensors I found that the highway behind my house was outputting a lot of signals. I removed the grounding mat since I was worried signal would travel up the line.

Supplements
Ubiquinol, Acetyl L carnitine, Bitter Melon, Capsaicin, D Ribose, Magnesium Malate, Magnesium Threonate.

So how does all of this stack up... doing okay. Whenever I am off and on a day schedule every few months I feel amazing.

I used to live in a flat covered in multiple wifi and moved recently. One thing I noticed is that I am not nearly as thirsty and I do not feel a need to stress eat.

Have you all ever gone and stayed in a cabin out in the wilderness? A night out in the boonies with zero tech and you will probably feel like a million bucks in the morning. I used to think this was due to a much needed vacation but this blog has taught me otherwise.

If he needs to be convinced there are tons of educational blogs on this site, once you get the idea across that water is the primary energy medium in the body and that emf causes calcium efflux to dehydrate your cells you simply have to connect the dots. Jack has also stated that emf also breaks quantum tunneling in the mitochondria but this is a little bit tougher to educate new people on. Maybe you could just leave a copy of the body electric on the counter table and encourage him to read it. I wish you luck on convincing your husband to your cause and I can tell you that with a supportive significant other this journey gets way better.

### caroline — 2014-02-05T09:32:35+0200

It is a real eye opener..

### Shijin13 — 2014-02-05T19:17:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-techies-out-there.9576/post-108498>

Here's my set up. I've docked my macbook pro - its sitting on a wooden file cabinet, along with the wired modem and wired router. I have a himalayan salt lamp sitting next to all of these things. My Monitor is on my standing desk. My work PC is next to that, along with my work Monitor (those are only on when working) I ground my mbp and pc using the grounding USB - but due to wiring issues I've got to run an extension cord to ground them properly.... waiting for the electrician to come give us an estimate to properly ground the plugs in my office.

at night we turn off the breakers w/the exception to the fridge and deep freezer. Lights are turned off at sunset, and we use candles and red head lamps for light.

everyone has a 20gauss magnetico.
we're still trying to figure out the best solution for addressing the electrical transmission line that runs along the top of the house to the meter along the kids' bedroom. So far we haven't had success.... so we're trying to decide if we should just move the kids downstairs or if we should put up some mu metal. We know we'll be moving sometime in the future.... just plotting our escape route.

CT /ground/sun as much as possible. temp in the house is kept at 62 deg on the main level, but due to the single pane windows the rest of the house runs below 60 most of the time.

> \- When I have to work from home, I try to "batch" my computer tasks around morning early daylight hours or near sunset, but many times I have to work later at night as well. So in addition to the safety glasses and f.lux, I also place a rectangular cutout red film over the laptop screen, the kind used for filtering theatrical lighting. I use USB connected keyboard and mouse to the laptop to keep some distance from it, though it is also a low voltage aluminum cased HP Spectre ultrabook it still picks up significant magnetic field readings near the motherboard. I also ground the laptop with the USB/ground wire concoction mentioned from the EMF webinar.
>
> \- I turn off all circuit breakers at night except for the fridge. In the daytime I also keep the bedroom breakers off since our toddler takes afternoon naps and plays in her bedroom, etc.
>
>
> At work:
>
> (My office is shared, with fluorescent light, on the second floor of a building with antennas above me and Wi Fi router/repeater right outside in the hallway. There are also classrooms and science labs in the building.)
>
> \- I have one salt lamp and one Citrine & Amethyst crystal "tree" on my desk near the computer, they are also between me and the wi fi router on the other side of the wall:
> [http://www.ebay.com/itm/Citrine-Ame...Life-Tree-TWIN-SPIRIT-TREE-COOL-/300985264062](http://www.ebay.com/itm/Citrine-Amethyst-Geode-Crystal-Cluster-Wire-Life-Tree-TWIN-SPIRIT-TREE-COOL-/300985264062)
> [http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B001892AX2/ref=oh\_details\_o05\_s00\_i00](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B001892AX2/ref=oh_details_o05_s00_i00)
>
> \- I wear a black Tourmaline necklace, and carry an extra chunk of Amethyst crystal in the pocket
>
> \- when I commute in my truck I have the salt lamp, a couple of magnets under the driver's seat, and a 44lb cast iron kettlebell in the back seat.
>
> \- I drink up to 2 ice cold Gerolsteiners during the work day, as well as additional RO water I bring from home. Typically they've been sitting on top of magneticos overnight or longer
>
> \- I do have servers and systems I manage in campus data centers, but like most in our organization I am able to limit my physical presence in those data centers with remote access tools. If I do have to spend a lot of time in the data center for some reason, I try to mitigate by taking extra CoQ10, Mg, glutamine, water, etc. And some relaxation breathing exercises with additional grounding outside when possible.
>
> \- for lunch hours I don't eat lunch because of high fat/protein breakfasts, so I often just get outside and get moving, in the sun, barefoot and shirtless when possible. Either go to a large park off campus with lots of movnat potential, or to one of the local beaches which are only a 10 - 15 minute drive. I'll often try to swim in the cold Pacific Ocean for at least 20 minutes, do some mobility work on the sand or climb beachside cliffs, etc.
>
>
> The biggest concerns still to address:
>
> \- The smart meter mesh we live in our neighborhood. Though our smart meter is gone, our closest neighbor's meter is only an additional 20 - 30 feet away, and one of the neighborhood collector meters is at the end of the street a little over 100 yards away, likely most of the signals coming from the direction of the other side of our house. I don't have adequate measurement tools or knowledge of the right kind of materials to block /absorb some of this RF we are caught in without doing it wrong and deflecting EMF back at us. I'm considering ponying up the cash for professional consulting. I may also speak with my neighbor soon about the idea of opting out of the smart meter.
>
> \- Our toddler's new bedroom also happens to have the breaker box mounted on the outside corner of the house where that room is. I arranged it so the bed is farthest from that corner, and a large dresser directly in front of where the magnetic field measures strongest. Once the magnetic field reader is moved away from that part of the wall and beyond the dressers, the milligauss readings drop off almost completely. Nevertheless, I am not sure if that's reassuring enough to not worry about trying to relocate the breaker box.
>
> \- Need to test/get consult for dirty electricity in the home, and the integrity of the ground
>
> \- The closest above ground high voltage power lines are 1 mile away from the home, the closest RF mobile tower is 1/2 mile away. I have not done enough measuring or research to know if these are big enough factors to consider moving, let alone the smart meter situation.
>
> \- Eventually I want to work towards a schedule of working 1 day a week from home, but this is assuming the EMF situation is better at home than it is at work, without thorough testing for accurate comparison. I get the sense this is the case based on what I think I know about the EMF sources present in each environment.

only just saw this now but an excellent summary of things to look out for and engage in to mitigate risk

### Josh (Paleo Osteo) — 2015-01-05T08:24:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-techies-out-there.9576/post-151664>

> [Michele Wilson said:](/goto/post?id=142211)
>
> can anyone here tell me if looking at computers during the day (with their high blue light emittance) have a negative effect? I use blue blockers during the evenings, but at work I am surrounded by bright computers and sometimes get headaches. I specifically want to know if they affect circadian rhythm or melatonin production if you see the screens during daylight hours. I understand the amount of blue emitted is a higher proportion than natural sunlight, unless I am wrong?

you are correct.

# Thread: Sous Vide?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sous-vide.9589/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JoeBranca — 2014-02-04T01:12:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sous-vide.9589/post-108203>

The wife had the brilliant idea of us getting a sous vide for each other for Valentine's Day... brilliant assuming our tax return looks good : /

Looks like there are a couple of models in the $300 and $400 ranges. Any sous vide owners have input on if one model is better cost value than the other? We have a freezer full of roasts, steaks, lamb, etc. etc. Also if anyone has some memorable epi paleo sous vide experiences, I'm all ears!

### caroline — 2014-02-04T01:19:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sous-vide.9589/post-108205>

Do you follow Nom Nom Paleo? she uses one and has heaps of recipes.....

I am still not clear - is a sous vide way better than a crock pot?

### caroline — 2014-02-04T01:26:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sous-vide.9589/post-108208>

You are on Tim! great news!!!

### BJK77 — 2014-02-04T16:57:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sous-vide.9589/post-108286>

Anyone know if we should be concerned at all about cooking in plastic in the sous vide? Or are the temps low enough that it's not a concern?!

### Hemming — 2014-02-04T17:15:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sous-vide.9589/post-108288>

I bought a Vac Star immersion pump (I can only recommend it) which is much more flexible solution. You can use any container and the pump can handle up to 40l of water.

Regarding cooking in plastic, I think its a personal decision. The bags are not ordinary plastic bags but made specifically for sous vide. Whether that's 'good enough' must be an individual decision.

### AnnieW — 2014-02-04T17:56:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sous-vide.9589/post-108291>

Guys, there is a ton of sous vide info over on eGullet. It's a great forum.

### JoeBranca — 2014-02-11T06:09:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sous-vide.9589/post-109577>

after reading this comparison test of water circulators - [http://www.seriouseats.com/2013/12/sous-vide-circulator-review-sansaire-nomiku-anova.html](http://www.seriouseats.com/2013/12/sous-vide-circulator-review-sansaire-nomiku-anova.html)

I think we might just go for the Anova for $199. [http://anovaculinary.com/](http://anovaculinary.com/)

Looks like it works best with a large stainless steel pot. But I emailed the company asking about using it in a 7.25 qt dutch oven.

Yeah, I'm also wondering about cooking in plastic bagging as well.
